Table exploration.prospect {
  prospect_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the exploration prospect or target area. Primary key.']
  affected_stakeholder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.stakeholder. Business justification: Prospects require stakeholder engagement planning, social impact assessments, and FPIC determination. Mining operations track which communities/indigenous groups are affected by each prospect for cons']
  competent_person_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.competent_person. Business justification: Prospects reference the competent person responsible for resource classification and reporting. Currently prospect stores competent_person as a denormalized string. Adding competent_person_id FK to th']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Prospects are cost accumulation points in exploration. Finance assigns cost centres to prospects for expenditure tracking, budget control, and AISC reporting by prospect.']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: Each prospect requires an emergency response plan for exploration activities (remote location, medical emergency, environmental incident). Safety management system requirement and regulatory expectati']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_permit. Business justification: Prospects require environmental permits for exploration activities (clearing, drilling, camp establishment). Permit conditions govern prospect-level operations. Critical for regulatory compliance and ']
  primary_commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Prospects target specific commodities (iron ore, copper, lithium) - fundamental to exploration strategy, resource classification, and statutory reporting. Normalizes existing primary_commodity text fi']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Prospects transition to profit centres when moving to production. P&L reporting, segment reporting, and JORC reserve attribution require linking prospects to profit centres for financial planning.']
  responsible_geologist_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Prospects require assigned geologists for exploration planning, resource estimation sign-off, and accountability. Critical for workforce planning and competency management. Removes denormalized text f']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the exploration license or mining tenement under which this prospect is located.']
  area_hectares decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total surface area of the prospect or area of interest, measured in hectares.']
  centroid_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the prospect centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum).']
  centroid_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the prospect centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum).']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Name and specification of the coordinate reference system used for local grid coordinates (e.g., MGA Zone 50, UTM Zone 31N).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this prospect record was first created in the system.']
  cutoff_grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Minimum economic grade threshold used for resource estimation and prospect evaluation, expressed as a percentage.']
  discovery_date date [note: 'Date when the prospect was first identified or discovered through exploration activities.']
  drill_hole_count varchar [note: 'Total number of drill holes completed within the prospect area to date.']
  environmental_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of environmental impact assessment and regulatory clearance for exploration activities in the prospect area.']
  estimated_grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Current estimated average grade of the primary commodity in the prospect, expressed as a percentage. Confidential business data.']
  estimated_tonnage_mt decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current estimated total tonnage of mineralized material in the prospect, expressed in million tonnes. Confidential business data.']
  exploration_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of exploration maturity for the prospect, ranging from early-stage grassroots to advanced feasibility studies.']
  geological_confidence varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of geological confidence in the prospect based on data density, quality, and geological understanding.']
  geological_province varchar [note: 'Name of the regional geological province or terrane in which the prospect is located.']
  heritage_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of cultural heritage and indigenous land use clearance for exploration activities in the prospect area.']
  last_drill_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent drilling activity conducted in the prospect.']
  last_resource_estimate_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent mineral resource estimate prepared for this prospect.']
  local_grid_easting decimal(12,2) [note: 'Easting coordinate of the prospect centroid in the local mine grid or project coordinate system, measured in meters.']
  local_grid_northing decimal(12,2) [note: 'Northing coordinate of the prospect centroid in the local mine grid or project coordinate system, measured in meters.']
  mineralization_style varchar [note: 'Type or style of mineralization present in the prospect (e.g., porphyry, epithermal, sediment-hosted, banded iron formation, pegmatite).']
  next_planned_activity varchar [note: 'Description of the next planned exploration activity or program for the prospect (e.g., drilling campaign, geophysical survey, feasibility study).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or comments about the prospect that do not fit structured fields.']
  prospect_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the prospect for identification in exploration systems and reporting.']
  prospect_name varchar [note: 'Business name or designation of the exploration prospect or target area.']
  prospect_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the exploration prospect in its lifecycle.']
  reserve_classification varchar [note: 'Highest level of ore reserve classification achieved for this prospect under JORC, NI 43-101, or SAMREC standards. None if no reserve has been declared.']
  resource_classification varchar [note: 'Highest level of mineral resource classification achieved for this prospect under JORC, NI 43-101, or SAMREC standards. None if no resource has been estimated.']
  secondary_commodity varchar [note: 'Secondary or by-product mineral commodity of interest in the prospect area, if applicable.']
  structural_domain varchar [note: 'Structural geological domain or zone characterizing the prospect area (e.g., fault block, fold belt, shear zone).']
  target_depth_max_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum depth below surface at which mineralization is targeted or expected, measured in meters.']
  target_depth_min_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum depth below surface at which mineralization is targeted or expected, measured in meters.']
  total_meters_drilled decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative total length of all drilling completed in the prospect, measured in meters.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this prospect record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record for each exploration prospect or target area identified during mineral exploration programs. Captures prospect name, centroid coordinates (latitude/longitude and local grid), commodity targets, discovery date, exploration stage (grassroots, advanced, feasibility), geological province, structural domain, tenement reference, and current status (active, dormant, relinquished, converted to mining). Serves as the top-level spatial and business anchor for all exploration activities, drill programs, resource estimates, and regulatory reporting within a defined area of interest.'
}

Table exploration.licence {
  licence_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for licence']
  affected_stakeholder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.stakeholder. Business justification: Exploration licences overlap traditional territories and community lands. Regulatory compliance requires tracking affected stakeholders for native title negotiations, heritage clearances, and consulta']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Exploration licences are cost centres for statutory expenditure tracking. Finance links licences to cost centres for minimum expenditure compliance reporting and regulatory audit trails.']
  land_compensation_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.land_compensation_agreement. Business justification: Land compensation agreements are negotiated for exploration licence areas to secure land access. Payment calculations, compliance tracking, and agreement administration require linking licences to the']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Exploration licences ARE tenements in mining regulatory frameworks. Direct link enables statutory reporting, expenditure compliance tracking, and regulatory obligation management. Licence_number maps ']
  annual_rent_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual rental or holding fee payable to the regulatory authority to maintain the exploration licence. Typically calculated per hectare or as a flat fee.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date on which the original application for the exploration licence was submitted to the regulatory authority.']
  area_square_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total surface area covered by the exploration licence measured in square kilometres. Alternative measurement for international reporting and comparison.']
  commodity_rights varchar [note: 'List of minerals and commodities for which exploration rights have been granted under this licence. May include iron ore, copper, gold, lithium, nickel, coal, or all minerals depending on jurisdiction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this exploration licence record was first created in the data platform.']
  environmental_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether separate environmental impact assessment or approval is required before commencing exploration activities. True if required, False otherwise.']
  expenditure_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all expenditure commitment amounts. Ensures consistent financial reporting across jurisdictions.']
  heritage_site_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licence area contains or overlaps with registered cultural heritage sites, archaeological sites, or sacred sites. True if heritage sites are present, False otherwise.']
  holder_registration_number varchar [note: 'Company registration number, business number, or other official identifier of the licence holder as registered with the corporate regulator.']
  joint_venture_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exploration licence is held under a joint venture arrangement with multiple parties. True if joint venture, False if sole holder.']
  last_renewal_date date [note: 'Date on which the exploration licence was most recently renewed. Null if the licence has never been renewed.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this exploration licence record was most recently modified in the data platform.']
  maximum_renewals_allowed varchar [note: 'Maximum number of renewals permitted under the applicable mining legislation. Once reached, the licence cannot be renewed further and must either be converted to a mining lease or surrendered.']
  minimum_expenditure_commitment_year_1 decimal(15,2) [note: 'Mandatory minimum exploration expenditure required in the first year of the licence term to maintain tenure. Failure to meet this commitment may result in penalties or licence forfeiture.']
  minimum_expenditure_commitment_year_2 decimal(15,2) [note: 'Mandatory minimum exploration expenditure required in the second year of the licence term to maintain tenure.']
  minimum_expenditure_commitment_year_3 decimal(15,2) [note: 'Mandatory minimum exploration expenditure required in the third year of the licence term to maintain tenure.']
  minimum_expenditure_commitment_year_4 decimal(15,2) [note: 'Mandatory minimum exploration expenditure required in the fourth year of the licence term to maintain tenure.']
  minimum_expenditure_commitment_year_5 decimal(15,2) [note: 'Mandatory minimum exploration expenditure required in the fifth year of the licence term to maintain tenure.']
  next_reporting_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next regulatory report or expenditure statement is due. Critical for compliance tracking and avoiding penalties.']
  operator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the entity responsible for managing and conducting exploration activities under the licence. May differ from the holder in joint venture arrangements.']
  regulatory_conditions varchar [note: 'Special conditions, restrictions, or obligations imposed by the regulatory authority as part of the licence grant. May include environmental protection measures, heritage site restrictions, or community consultation requirements.']
  renewal_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the exploration licence has been renewed since original grant. Used to track tenure history and assess remaining renewal opportunities.']
  renewal_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exploration licence is eligible for renewal based on compliance with expenditure commitments and regulatory conditions. True if eligible, False if not.']
  rent_fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the annual rent fee.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which exploration activity reports and expenditure statements must be submitted to the regulatory authority.']
  security_bond_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial security or bond amount required to be lodged with the regulatory authority to cover potential rehabilitation costs and ensure compliance with licence conditions.']
  security_bond_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the security bond amount.']

  Note: 'Master register of all exploration licences, permits, and tenements held for mineral exploration activities. Tracks licence number, issuing jurisdiction and regulatory authority, grant date, expiry date, renewal history, area in hectares/km², commodity rights granted, minimum expenditure commitments by year, security/bond requirements, reporting obligations, and regulatory conditions. Links to the tenement domain for broader land access context. Scoped exclusively to exploration-phase tenure — mining leases and production rights are owned by the tenement domain.'
}

Table exploration.drill_hole {
  drill_hole_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for the drill hole collar record. Primary key for the drill hole entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the drilling rig used to execute this hole. Used for rig utilization tracking, maintenance correlation, and quality control. Typically the contractors rig fleet number or name.']
  drill_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drilling campaign or program under which this hole was executed. Used for budget tracking, contractor assignment, and program-level reporting.']
  drilling_contractor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drilling contractor organization responsible for executing this hole. Used for contractor performance evaluation, cost tracking, and quality assurance.']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Individual drill holes are incident sites (rig failure, environmental spill, injury). Incident investigation requires drill hole context for root cause analysis and corrective action implementation. S']
  logged_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Geological logging requires qualified geologist assignment for QA/QC and competency tracking. Essential for resource estimate validation and training needs analysis. Replaces text field.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Individual drill holes may be tied to specific PO line items for detailed cost tracking, invoicing reconciliation, and cost-per-meter analysis. Enables accurate job costing and dispute resolution with']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the exploration prospect or tenement where this drill hole is located. Links the hole to the broader exploration project and land package.']
  target_commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Individual drill holes target specific commodities - determines sampling protocol, assay suite selection, and QAQC standards (e.g., gold requires fire assay, base metals use multi-element ICP). Normal']
  abandonment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for early termination or abandonment of the drill hole. Common reasons include equipment failure, ground conditions (cavity, water ingress), deviation beyond acceptable limits, or program budget constraints. Populated only when hole_status is abandoned.']
  actual_azimuth decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual measured horizontal direction of the drill hole at the collar, measured in degrees clockwise from grid north (0-360). Captured from downhole survey or collar survey after drilling commences. May differ from planned azimuth due to setup adjustments or ground conditions.']
  actual_depth decimal(8,2) [note: 'The actual total depth achieved by the drill hole measured along the hole trajectory from collar to end-of-hole, measured in meters. May differ from planned depth due to early termination, ground conditions, or program adjustments.']
  actual_dip decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual measured vertical angle of the drill hole at the collar, measured in degrees from horizontal. Captured from downhole survey or collar survey after drilling commences. May differ from planned dip due to setup adjustments or ground conditions.']
  collar_easting decimal(12,3) [note: 'The easting (X) coordinate of the drill hole collar in the local mine grid coordinate system, measured in meters. This is the authoritative spatial reference for the hole location used in resource modeling and mine planning.']
  collar_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The latitude of the drill hole collar in WGS84 geographic coordinate system, measured in decimal degrees. Used for global positioning, regulatory reporting, and integration with external GIS systems.']
  collar_longitude decimal(11,7) [note: 'The longitude of the drill hole collar in WGS84 geographic coordinate system, measured in decimal degrees. Used for global positioning, regulatory reporting, and integration with external GIS systems.']
  collar_northing decimal(12,3) [note: 'The northing (Y) coordinate of the drill hole collar in the local mine grid coordinate system, measured in meters. Used in conjunction with easting and RL to define the three-dimensional collar position.']
  collar_rl decimal(10,3) [note: 'The elevation (Z) coordinate of the drill hole collar in the local mine grid coordinate system, measured in meters above the site datum. RL (Reduced Level) is the mining industry standard term for elevation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'General free-text comments or notes about the drill hole, including operational issues, geological observations, or data quality notes. Used for contextual information that does not fit structured fields.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The date drilling operations were completed for this hole, including final survey and collar rehabilitation. Used for program reporting, contractor invoicing, and cycle time analysis.']
  core_diameter varchar [note: 'The nominal diameter of the drill core recovered, applicable only to diamond core drilling. Standard sizes include PQ (85mm), HQ (63.5mm), NQ (47.6mm), BQ (36.4mm). Used for sample volume calculations and metallurgical testwork planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PQ|HQ|NQ|BQ|NQ2|HQ3|NMLC|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this drill hole record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle tracking.']
  drill_type varchar [note: 'The drilling method used for this hole. RC (Reverse Circulation) provides chip samples; diamond core provides continuous core samples; RAB (Rotary Air Blast) and aircore are used for shallow reconnaissance; percussion and rotary are used for specific geological conditions; directional drilling is used for underground or complex geometries. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RC|diamond_core|RAB|aircore|percussion|rotary|directional — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hole_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the drill hole collar, typically assigned by the exploration team following site naming conventions. This is the externally-known collar identifier used in geological reports, assay certificates, and JORC/NI 43-101/SAMREC resource statements.']
  hole_purpose varchar [note: 'The primary objective of the drill hole. Exploration holes target new mineralization; infill holes increase resource confidence; geotechnical holes assess rock mechanics for mine design; sterilisation holes confirm absence of mineralization in infrastructure footprints; hydrogeological holes assess groundwater; metallurgical holes provide samples for processing testwork.']
  hole_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the drill hole. Planned holes are scheduled but not started; in-progress holes are actively being drilled; completed holes have reached target depth and been logged; abandoned holes were terminated early due to technical issues; suspended holes are temporarily halted; surveyed holes have had final downhole survey completed.']
  logged_date date [note: 'The date the geological logging of the drill hole was completed. Used for workflow tracking and data currency assessment.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this drill hole record was last modified in the system. Used for data currency assessment, change tracking, and audit trail.']
  planned_azimuth decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planned horizontal direction of the drill hole at the collar, measured in degrees clockwise from grid north (0-360). Azimuth defines the compass bearing of the hole trajectory.']
  planned_depth decimal(8,2) [note: 'The planned total depth of the drill hole measured along the hole trajectory from collar to end-of-hole, measured in meters. Defines the target depth for the drilling contractor.']
  planned_dip decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planned vertical angle of the drill hole at the collar, measured in degrees from horizontal. Positive values indicate downward inclination; negative values indicate upward inclination. A vertical hole has a dip of -90 degrees.']
  pre_collar_depth decimal(7,2) [note: 'The depth of pre-collar drilling (typically RC or aircore) completed before switching to diamond core drilling, measured in meters. Used in two-stage drilling programs to reduce diamond drilling costs through overburden.']
  qaqc_status varchar [note: 'The overall quality assurance and quality control status for this drill hole, based on review of sampling procedures, assay QAQC results, survey accuracy, and logging completeness. Passed holes are suitable for resource estimation; failed holes require remediation or exclusion.']
  recovery_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The average percentage of core recovered relative to the drilled interval, applicable to diamond core drilling. High recovery (>95%) indicates competent ground and good drilling practice; low recovery may indicate fractured ground, voids, or drilling issues. Critical for resource confidence classification.']
  sample_interval decimal(5,2) [note: 'The standard length interval used for sampling this drill hole, measured in meters. Typically 1m for RC drilling and variable (0.5-1.5m) for diamond core based on geological contacts. Used for sample planning and assay result interpretation.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date drilling operations commenced for this hole. Used for program scheduling, contractor performance tracking, and cost allocation.']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'The method used to survey the drill hole trajectory. Gyroscopic surveys are most accurate and unaffected by magnetic interference; magnetic surveys are cost-effective but affected by magnetic ore bodies; electronic multi-shot provides continuous trajectory; differential GPS and total station are used for collar positioning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gyroscopic|magnetic|electronic_multi_shot|single_shot|differential_gps|total_station|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_geology varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the geological target or formation being intersected by this drill hole. Includes target lithology, structure, or mineralization style. Used for geological interpretation and targeting review.']

  Note: 'Master record for every drill hole (collar) executed across all exploration and resource definition programs. Captures hole ID (unique collar identifier), prospect reference, drill program reference, drill type (RC, diamond core, RAB, aircore, percussion), collar coordinates (easting, northing, RL in local mine grid and WGS84), planned and actual azimuth, planned and actual dip, planned and actual total depth, start date, completion date, drilling contractor, rig identifier, hole purpose (exploration, infill, geotechnical, sterilisation, hydrogeological), and hole status (planned, in-progress, completed, abandoned). The authoritative SSOT for drill hole identity and spatial metadata used by geology, laboratory, and resource estimation workflows.'
}

Table exploration.drill_program {
  drill_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the drilling program. Primary key for the drill program entity.']
  affected_stakeholder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.stakeholder. Business justification: Drill programs trigger community engagement obligations, generate grievances (noise, dust, water impacts), and create local employment opportunities. Mining operations track which stakeholders are aff']
  capex_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_budget. Business justification: Drill programs are capital projects requiring budget approval and tracking. Finance links drill program expenditure to approved capex budgets for capital expenditure control and variance reporting.']
  competent_person_id varchar [note: 'FK to exploration.competent_person']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: Drilling programs must reference site emergency response plans (medical evacuation, spill response, rig fire). Operational requirement for contractor mobilization and safety induction. Plan ID include']
  exploration_licence_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the exploration licence under which this drilling program is authorized and conducted.']
  hse_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.audit. Business justification: Drilling programs are subject to HSE audits (contractor safety compliance, environmental permit adherence). Audit findings link to program for corrective action tracking and contractor performance eva']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Drilling programs must track HSE incidents for safety performance reporting, regulatory compliance (DMIRS/DNRME), and program risk assessment. Critical operational link for incident investigation and ']
  opex_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.opex_budget. Business justification: Drill programs have operational expenditure components (consumables, labor, contractor costs). Finance links drill programs to opex budgets for operating cost control and AISC calculation.']
  primary_drill_rig_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Drill programs assign a primary rig during planning for resource scheduling, budget estimation, and contractor mobilization. This supports program planning, rig availability forecasting, and contracto']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Drilling programs operate under framework agreements or master service agreements. Links program to procurement contract for pricing verification, terms compliance, scope management, and contract perf']
  program_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Drill programs require named managers for budget approval, HSE accountability, and contractor coordination. Essential for org chart and performance management. Replaces denormalized name field.']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral prospect or resource area targeted by this drilling program.']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Drilling programs require formal risk assessments (HAZID/HAZOP) before approval. Regulatory requirement in most Australian jurisdictions and standard practice for contractor mobilization. Risk assessm']
  target_commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Drill programs are designed to test commodity-specific targets - required for budget allocation, statutory expenditure reporting, and drilling contractor selection (e.g., RC vs diamond core). Normaliz']
  actual_expenditure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred for the drilling program to date. Used for budget variance analysis and cost control.']
  actual_hole_count varchar [note: 'Total number of drill holes actually completed as part of this program. Used for program performance tracking and variance analysis.']
  actual_metreage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total actual drilling metreage (in metres) completed for the program. Used for cost reconciliation and performance tracking against plan.']
  approved_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget for the drilling program in the reporting currency. Includes drilling, assay, geological support, and mobilization costs.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the approved budget and actual expenditure amounts (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the drill program record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  drilling_contractor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the drilling contractor or service provider engaged to execute the drilling program.']
  drilling_method varchar [note: 'Primary drilling technique employed in the program: diamond core (DD), reverse circulation (RC), rotary air blast (RAB), percussion, or sonic drilling. Determines sample quality and cost profile.']
  environmental_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the environmental approval, permit, or Environmental Impact Statement (EIS) under which the drilling program is authorized.']
  expenditure_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the drilling program expenditure as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX), Operating Expenditure (OPEX), or mixed. Critical for financial reporting and tax treatment under IFRS 6.']
  hse_incident_count varchar [note: 'Total number of recordable Health, Safety, and Environment incidents that occurred during the execution of the drilling program. Used for safety performance tracking and LTIFR/TRIFR calculation.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this drill program record is currently active and valid for operational and reporting purposes. Supports soft-delete and historical record management.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the drill program record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data governance.']
  planned_hole_count varchar [note: 'Total number of drill holes planned for this program as per the approved drilling plan.']
  planned_metreage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total planned drilling metreage (in metres) for the program as per the approved drilling plan. Critical for budget estimation and contractor scoping.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code assigned to the drilling program for external reference and reporting purposes.']
  program_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when drilling operations concluded for this program. Used for program duration tracking and contractor demobilization planning.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the drilling program identifying the campaign, location, or strategic objective.']
  program_objective varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the strategic and technical objectives of the drilling program, including target resource category upgrade, geological model validation, or feasibility study support.']
  program_start_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when drilling operations commenced for this program. Critical for Life of Mine (LOM) planning and resource update scheduling.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the drilling program: planned (design phase), approved (budget authorized), active (drilling underway), suspended (temporarily halted), completed (all holes drilled and signed off), or cancelled (terminated before completion).']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the drilling program based on its strategic purpose: scout (initial exploration), infill (increasing resource confidence), step-out (extending known mineralization), resource definition (JORC/NI 43-101 compliance), geotechnical (engineering studies), or condemnation (proving absence of mineralization).']
  qaqc_protocol_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal QAQC protocol (including certified reference materials, blanks, and duplicates) was applied during the drilling program to ensure sample integrity and assay reliability.']
  rehabilitation_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether site rehabilitation (drill pad restoration, collar capping, and environmental remediation) has been completed following the conclusion of the drilling program.']
  rehabilitation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when site rehabilitation activities were completed and the drill site was returned to its pre-disturbance condition or approved final land use.']
  sign_off_date date [note: 'Date when the Competent Person formally approved the drilling program results for inclusion in resource and reserve reporting.']
  sign_off_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the drilling program results by the Competent Person: pending (awaiting review), in_review (under technical review), approved (signed off for resource update), or rejected (requires additional work).']
  target_resource_category varchar [note: 'JORC/NI 43-101/SAMREC resource classification category that the drilling program aims to achieve or upgrade: inferred (lowest confidence), indicated (moderate confidence), or measured (highest confidence).']

  Note: 'Defines a planned and executed drilling campaign associated with a prospect or resource area. Captures program name, target commodity, program type (scout, infill, step-out, resource definition), planned versus actual hole count, planned versus actual metreage, program start and end dates, approved budget (CAPEX/OPEX), actual expenditure, program objectives, and sign-off status. Links to the exploration licence and prospect. Enables LOM drilling program tracking and JORC-compliant resource update planning.'
}

Table exploration.drill_hole_survey {
  drill_hole_survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the drill hole survey measurement record.']
  drill_hole_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent drill hole for which this survey measurement was taken.']
  surveyor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Downhole surveys require certified surveyors for accuracy validation and competency compliance. Links survey quality to workforce qualifications. Removes denormalized name field.']
  calculated_easting decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated easting coordinate at this survey depth based on collar position, azimuth, and dip measurements.']
  calculated_elevation decimal(10,2) [note: 'Calculated elevation (reduced level) at this survey depth based on collar elevation and dip measurements.']
  calculated_northing decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated northing coordinate at this survey depth based on collar position, azimuth, and dip measurements.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent calibration of the survey instrument prior to this measurement.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes regarding this survey measurement, including any anomalies, instrument issues, or special conditions.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Spatial reference system or coordinate system used for azimuth and spatial calculations (e.g., WGS84, local mine grid, UTM zone).']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or database from which this survey measurement was imported (e.g., Hexagon MinePlan, Geovia GEMS, contractor database).']
  deviation_from_planned decimal(6,2) [note: 'Calculated horizontal deviation distance from the planned drill hole trajectory at this survey depth, measured in meters.']
  instrument_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the specific survey instrument used for this measurement, enabling traceability and calibration verification.']
  magnetic_declination decimal(5,2) [note: 'Magnetic declination correction applied to convert magnetic azimuth to true azimuth at the survey location and date. Positive values indicate eastward declination.']
  magnetic_field_strength decimal(8,2) [note: 'Magnetic field strength measured at the survey point in nanoTesla. High magnetic interference may affect magnetic survey instrument accuracy.']
  measured_azimuth decimal(6,2) [note: 'Horizontal angle of the drill hole at this survey point, measured in degrees clockwise from true north (0-360).']
  measured_dip decimal(5,2) [note: 'Vertical angle of the drill hole at this survey point, measured in degrees from horizontal. Negative values indicate downward inclination.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survey measurement record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survey measurement record was last modified in the system.']
  survey_company varchar [note: 'Name of the company or contractor that performed the downhole survey, if external.']
  survey_date date [note: 'Date on which the downhole survey measurement was taken.']
  survey_depth decimal(10,2) [note: 'Depth down the hole at which this survey measurement was taken, measured in meters from the collar.']
  survey_instrument_type varchar [note: 'Type of downhole survey instrument used to capture this measurement. Gyroscope instruments are preferred for accuracy in magnetic environments.']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the survey was conducted: end of hole survey, during drilling at intervals, post-drilling, or continuous measurement.']
  survey_quality_code varchar [note: 'Quality assessment code indicating the reliability and verification status of this survey measurement.']
  survey_run_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying the survey run or trip down the hole. Multiple runs may be performed for verification.']
  survey_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of this survey measurement record in the data validation and approval process.']
  temperature_at_survey decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature recorded at the survey depth during measurement, in degrees Celsius. Temperature can affect instrument accuracy.']
  validated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the geologist or data manager who validated and approved this survey measurement.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date on which this survey measurement was validated and approved for use in resource estimation.']

  Note: 'Downhole survey measurements capturing the deviation of a drill hole from its planned trajectory at regular depth intervals. Records hole ID, survey depth, measured azimuth, measured dip, survey instrument type (gyroscope, magnetic, multi-shot), survey date, and surveyor. Essential for accurate 3D spatial positioning of assay intervals and geological logging data used in resource estimation and Geovia GEMS block model construction.'
}

Table exploration.geological_log {
  geological_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the geological log record. Primary key for the geological log entity. Inferred role: EVENT_LOG (append-only geological observations per drill hole interval).']
  drill_hole_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the drill hole from which this geological log was recorded. Links to the drill hole collar and survey data. Represents the EVENT_SOURCE_REFERENCE for this log entry.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the geologist who performed the logging. Used for quality control, audit trail, and JORC competent person requirements.']
  alteration_intensity varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the intensity or degree of alteration observed: none, weak, moderate, strong, or pervasive. Used in conjunction with alteration_type for resource modeling.']
  alteration_type varchar [note: 'Type of hydrothermal or weathering alteration observed in the interval (e.g., silicification, chloritisation, sericitisation, hematisation). Critical for understanding mineralization processes and JORC Table 1 Section 1 reporting.']
  colour varchar [note: 'Primary colour of the rock observed in the interval (e.g., grey, red, green, black, white). May indicate oxidation state, alteration, or lithology.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments and observations recorded by the geologist during logging. May include additional details not captured in structured fields.']
  core_condition varchar [note: 'Overall condition of the drill core: excellent, good, fair, poor, or very_poor. Reflects core integrity and quality for geological interpretation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this geological log record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage and JORC compliance.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source of the geological log data: field_logging (original logging at drill site), re_logging (subsequent re-interpretation), or historical_import (legacy data migration).']
  depth_from decimal(10,2) [note: 'Starting depth of the logged interval in meters below surface or collar. Used to define the from-to interval for geological observations.']
  depth_to decimal(10,2) [note: 'Ending depth of the logged interval in meters below surface or collar. Used to define the from-to interval for geological observations.']
  grain_size varchar [note: 'Classification of the grain size of the rock: very_fine, fine, medium, coarse, or very_coarse. Relevant for sedimentary and some igneous rocks.']
  hardness varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of rock hardness: very_soft, soft, medium, hard, or very_hard. Relevant for drilling performance and geotechnical assessment.']
  interval_length decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length of the logged interval in meters, calculated as depth_to minus depth_from. Represents the sample or observation interval.']
  lithology_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the primary rock type observed in this interval (e.g., BIF for Banded Iron Formation, GRN for Granite, SHL for Shale). Follows site-specific or regional lithology coding standards.']
  lithology_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the rock type, including texture, grain size, composition, and any distinguishing features observed by the geologist.']
  logging_date date [note: 'Date when the geological logging was performed. Represents the EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this observation. Critical for data lineage and JORC Table 1 Section 1 reporting.']
  logging_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the geological logging: visual (traditional manual logging), digital (tablet-based logging), or photographic (image-based logging with subsequent interpretation).']
  mineralisation_intensity varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the intensity or abundance of visible mineralization: none, trace, minor, moderate, or strong. Used for preliminary grade estimation and targeting.']
  mineralisation_style varchar [note: 'Description of the style and form of mineralization observed (e.g., disseminated, vein-hosted, massive sulfide, replacement, stockwork). Critical for understanding ore body geometry and JORC reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this geological log record was last modified. Part of audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.']
  oxidation_state varchar [note: 'Oxidation state of the mineralization: oxide (fully oxidized), transitional (partially oxidized), or fresh_sulfide (unoxidized). Critical for metallurgical processing and resource classification.']
  qaqc_status varchar [note: 'Quality control status of the geological log record: pending (awaiting review), approved (validated and accepted), rejected (failed QC checks), or under_review (currently being reviewed). Critical for JORC Table 1 Section 1 data quality reporting.']
  recovery_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of core recovered from the drilled interval, calculated as recovered core length divided by drilled interval length. Critical quality indicator for drill hole data and JORC Table 1 Section 1 reporting.']
  rock_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the rock type into major geological categories: igneous, sedimentary, metamorphic, or unconsolidated material.']
  rqd_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rock Quality Designation expressed as a percentage, calculated as the sum of core pieces greater than 10cm divided by the interval length. Standard geotechnical measure of rock mass quality ranging from 0 to 100 percent.']
  sample_collected_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a physical sample was collected from this interval for assay analysis. True if sample was collected, False otherwise.']
  structure_orientation varchar [note: 'Orientation of structural features recorded as dip direction and dip angle (e.g., 045/60 meaning dipping 60 degrees towards 045 azimuth). Critical for structural modeling and geotechnical assessment.']
  structure_type varchar [note: 'Type of structural feature observed in the interval (e.g., fault, shear zone, joint, fracture, foliation, bedding). Important for understanding ore controls and geotechnical properties.']
  texture varchar [note: 'Description of the rock texture (e.g., massive, foliated, banded, brecciated, porphyritic). Provides information on rock formation and deformation history.']
  vein_type varchar [note: 'Description of vein mineralogy and characteristics (e.g., quartz vein, calcite vein, sulfide vein). Critical for understanding mineralization style and ore genesis.']
  veining_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage of the interval occupied by veins. Important for understanding mineralization distribution and structural controls.']
  weathering_degree varchar [note: 'Degree of weathering observed in the rock: fresh, slightly_weathered, moderately_weathered, highly_weathered, or completely_weathered. Important for understanding near-surface ore characteristics and geotechnical properties.']

  Note: 'Detailed geological logging records for each drill hole interval during exploration and resource definition drilling. Captures lithology, rock type, alteration type and intensity, mineralisation style, structural features, RQD (Rock Quality Designation), recovery percentage, colour, grain size, weathering, oxidation state, and geologist identifier. Logged at defined depth intervals (from-to) per drill hole. Scoped to exploration-phase drilling — production-phase grade control logging is owned by the geology domain. Forms the primary geological dataset for resource estimation and JORC Table 1 Section 1 disclosures.'
}

Table exploration.exploration_sample {
  exploration_sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the exploration sample record. Primary key for the sample entity.']
  chemical_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.chemical_register. Business justification: Exploration samples may contain or require hazardous materials (acid digestion, cyanide leach testing). Chemical handling and disposal requires register linkage for SDS access and regulatory complianc']
  collected_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: JORC/NI 43-101 compliance requires chain-of-custody tracking to specific qualified employees. Critical for audit trails and competency verification. Replaces text field with FK.']
  collection_equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Exploration samples (soil, rock chip, channel) are collected using specific equipment (augers, excavators, portable drills). Tracking collection equipment supports QA/QC protocols, method validation, ']
  drill_hole_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drill hole from which this sample was collected. Null for non-drill samples such as soil, rock chip, or stream sediment samples.']
  finance_exploration_expenditure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.exploration_expenditure. Business justification: Laboratory sample analysis costs are exploration expenditures. Finance tracks assay costs per sample batch for IFRS6 capitalization decisions and area-of-interest expenditure accumulation.']
  parent_sample_exploration_sample_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original sample from which this sample was derived (e.g., for duplicates, replicates, or sub-samples). Null for primary field samples.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Assay laboratories are vendors providing analytical services. Links samples to vendor master for lab performance tracking, cost analysis, quality correlation, turnaround time monitoring, and contract ']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Exploration samples are collected from specific prospects. Currently sample stores prospect_code as a denormalized string identifier. Adding prospect_id FK to the prospect master table normalizes this']
  target_saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Exploration samples in feasibility-stage projects are tested against target product specifications (e.g., lump vs fines iron ore, concentrate grade) - critical for metallurgical testwork, product qual']
  alteration_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the type and intensity of hydrothermal or weathering alteration observed in the sample. Important for understanding mineralization controls.']
  analytical_method varchar [note: 'Requested analytical technique or method suite for this sample (e.g., fire assay, ICP-MS, XRF). Determines which elements or compounds will be analyzed and detection limits.']
  chain_of_custody_status varchar [note: 'Current physical location or custody status of the sample. Critical for sample tracking, security, and audit compliance.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Date when the sample was physically collected from the field or drill site. Critical for chain of custody and temporal analysis of exploration programs.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'The technique or equipment used to collect the sample from the field. Critical for understanding sample quality, representativeness, and potential biases.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Spatial reference system used for the sample location coordinates (e.g., WGS84 UTM Zone 50S, GDA94 MGA Zone 51). Critical for accurate spatial integration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sample record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  dispatch_batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch or shipment in which this sample was sent to the laboratory. Used for tracking, logistics, and grouping samples for analysis.']
  dispatch_date date [note: 'Date when the sample was dispatched from the field or core facility to the analytical laboratory. Part of chain of custody documentation.']
  easting_m decimal(12,2) [note: 'Easting coordinate of the sample collection point in meters, typically in a projected coordinate system (e.g., UTM). Used for spatial analysis and resource modeling.']
  elevation_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation of the sample collection point in meters above mean sea level. Used for 3D spatial modeling and geological interpretation.']
  from_depth_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Starting depth in meters of the sample interval measured from drill hole collar. Null for surface samples. Used with to_depth_m to define the sample interval.']
  geologist_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for geologist observations, notes, or interpretations recorded during sample collection or logging. Captures qualitative information not represented in coded fields.']
  is_composite boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this sample is a composite created by combining multiple individual samples. Composites are used for resource estimation and grade control.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sample record was last updated in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  length_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length of the sample interval in meters. For drill samples, typically calculated as to_depth_m minus from_depth_m. Critical for understanding sample support and compositing.']
  lithology_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the rock type or geological unit observed at the sample interval. Used for geological modeling and domain definition.']
  mineralization_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the style, type, or intensity of mineralization observed in the sample. Used for targeting and geological interpretation.']
  northing_m decimal(12,2) [note: 'Northing coordinate of the sample collection point in meters, typically in a projected coordinate system (e.g., UTM). Used for spatial analysis and resource modeling.']
  preparation_instructions varchar [note: 'Specific instructions for laboratory sample preparation including crushing, grinding, splitting, and sub-sampling protocols. May reference standard preparation codes or custom procedures.']
  qaqc_reference_material varchar [note: 'Identifier of the certified reference material (CRM) or standard used for this QAQC sample. Null for routine samples. Used to validate laboratory accuracy.']
  qaqc_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sample for quality assurance and quality control purposes. Routine samples are normal field samples; duplicates, blanks, standards, and replicates are control samples inserted to monitor analytical accuracy and precision.']
  recovery_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of core or sample material recovered relative to the drilled or sampled interval. Low recovery may indicate sample quality issues or geological conditions such as voids or fracturing.']
  sample_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique sample identifier assigned during collection. Used for laboratory tracking, chain of custody, and cross-referencing with assay results. Typically follows site-specific numbering conventions.']
  sample_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sample in the exploration workflow. Tracks progression from field collection through laboratory analysis to final data integration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: collected|dispatched|at_laboratory|prepared|assayed|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical sample based on collection method and material form. Determines handling, preparation, and analytical protocols. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drill_core_half|drill_core_quarter|rc_chips|channel|soil|rock_chip|stream_sediment — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the sample or sample remnants are currently stored (e.g., core shed, warehouse, laboratory). Used for sample retrieval and inventory management.']
  tenement_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mining tenement, exploration license, or land parcel where the sample was collected. Critical for regulatory compliance and resource reporting.']
  to_depth_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Ending depth in meters of the sample interval measured from drill hole collar. Null for surface samples. Used with from_depth_m to define the sample interval.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of the sample in kilograms as collected in the field. Used for quality control, recovery calculations, and preparation planning.']

  Note: 'Master record for every physical sample collected during exploration activities including drill core (half-core, quarter-core), RC chips, channel samples, soil samples, rock chips, and stream sediment samples. Captures sample ID, sample type, collection method, drill hole reference, from-to depth interval, sample weight, sample length, dispatch batch number, dispatch date to laboratory, chain of custody status, QA/QC type (routine, duplicate, blank, standard, replicate), and preparation instructions. The SSOT for sample identity and collection metadata in the exploration domain; analytical results (assays) are owned by the laboratory domain and linked back via sample ID foreign key.'
}

Table exploration.geochemical_survey {
  geochemical_survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the geochemical survey campaign. Primary key.']
  environmental_monitoring_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_monitoring. Business justification: Geochemical surveys may trigger environmental monitoring (soil contamination detection, water quality baseline). Survey results inform monitoring program design and regulatory reporting. Environmental']
  finance_exploration_expenditure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.exploration_expenditure. Business justification: Geochemical surveys are exploration activities with direct costs. Finance tracks survey expenditure for IFRS6 compliance, area-of-interest accounting, and technical feasibility assessment.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Geochemical survey labs are vendors. Links to vendor master for lab performance evaluation, cost tracking, and quality assurance. Essential for multi-survey lab performance comparison and contract man']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the exploration prospect or target area where this geochemical survey is being conducted.']
  survey_equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Geochemical surveys use specific equipment (soil augers, portable XRF analyzers, sampling rigs). Linking survey to equipment supports calibration tracking, data quality validation, equipment performan']
  target_commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Geochemical surveys target specific commodities using pathfinder elements (e.g., As/Sb for gold, Mo for porphyry copper) - drives exploration strategy and budget allocation. Normalizes existing target']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining tenement, exploration license, or land holding under which this geochemical survey is authorized.']
  analytical_method varchar [note: 'Laboratory analytical technique used for sample analysis (e.g., ICP-MS, ICP-OES, Fire Assay, XRF). Defines detection capabilities and data quality parameters.']
  anomalies_identified_count varchar [note: 'Number of discrete geochemical anomalies identified from this survey that exceed the anomaly threshold and warrant further investigation.']
  anomaly_threshold_ppm decimal(12,6) [note: 'Geochemical concentration threshold in parts per million (ppm) above which results are classified as anomalous and warrant follow-up investigation.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Reporting standard or code to which the survey data and interpretation must comply (e.g., JORC Code 2012, NI 43-101, SAMREC Code). Critical for public disclosure and resource estimation.']
  contractor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external contractor or service provider engaged to conduct the field sampling program. Null if conducted by internal exploration team.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Spatial reference system used for survey location data (e.g., WGS84, GDA2020, UTM Zone 50S). Critical for accurate spatial integration with other exploration datasets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this geochemical survey record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  data_quality_rating varchar [note: 'Overall assessment of the geochemical data quality based on QAQC results, sample recovery, and analytical performance. Influences confidence in interpretation.']
  detection_limit_ppm decimal(12,6) [note: 'Lower detection limit of the analytical method in parts per million (ppm) for the target commodity. Critical for interpreting anomaly significance.']
  follow_up_recommended_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the survey results warrant follow-up exploration activities such as detailed sampling, geophysics, or drilling based on anomaly significance.']
  geological_setting varchar [note: 'Description of the regional geological environment and rock types within the survey area. Provides context for geochemical anomaly interpretation.']
  geologist_responsible varchar [note: 'Name of the qualified geologist or exploration manager responsible for survey design, execution oversight, and data interpretation.']
  grid_spacing_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Spacing between sample collection points in meters, defining the resolution and density of the geochemical survey grid.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this geochemical survey record was last updated in the system. Tracks data currency and change history.']
  qaqc_protocol varchar [note: 'Quality assurance and quality control procedures applied during sampling and analysis, including standards, blanks, and duplicates insertion rates.']
  report_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference or identifier for the technical report containing detailed survey methodology, results, and interpretation.']
  sample_depth_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard depth in centimeters at which samples were collected below surface. Critical for data interpretation and comparison across surveys.']
  sample_medium varchar [note: 'Physical material type collected for geochemical analysis. Defines the sampling substrate and influences interpretation methodology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: soil|sediment|rock|vegetation|termite_mound|lag|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  survey_area_km2 decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total geographic area covered by the geochemical survey in square kilometers. Used for planning, cost estimation, and resource allocation.']
  survey_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the geochemical survey for tracking and reporting purposes across systems.']
  survey_end_date date [note: 'Date when field sampling activities for this geochemical survey campaign were completed. Null if survey is still in progress.']
  survey_name varchar [note: 'Business name or designation assigned to the geochemical survey campaign for identification and reference purposes.']
  survey_objective varchar [note: 'Primary exploration objective or purpose of the geochemical survey (e.g., regional reconnaissance, target generation, drill target refinement, resource extension).']
  survey_start_date date [note: 'Date when field sampling activities for this geochemical survey campaign commenced.']
  survey_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the geochemical survey campaign indicating progress and operational state.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the geochemical survey methodology employed. Defines the sampling medium and approach used for geochemical anomaly detection.']
  total_samples_collected varchar [note: 'Total number of geochemical samples collected during this survey campaign. Used for quality control and cost tracking.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'General weather and environmental conditions during the survey period that may have affected sampling quality or access (e.g., wet season, dry season, extreme heat).']

  Note: 'Records surface geochemical survey campaigns including soil sampling grids, stream sediment surveys, and rock chip programs. Captures survey name, survey type, target commodity, grid spacing, coordinate system, survey date range, number of samples collected, analytical method, detection limits, and anomaly threshold criteria. Provides the spatial and methodological context for interpreting geochemical anomalies and prioritising drill targets.'
}

Table exploration.survey {
  survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the exploration survey program. Primary key.']
  competent_person_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.competent_person. Business justification: Geophysical surveys reference the competent person responsible for survey design and interpretation. Currently survey stores competent_person_name and competent_person_registration as denormalized str']
  equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Geophysical surveys (magnetic, EM, gravity, IP) use specialized equipment that requires calibration tracking and maintenance records. Linking survey to asset supports data quality validation, equipmen']
  finance_exploration_expenditure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.exploration_expenditure. Business justification: Geophysical surveys are major exploration expenditures. Finance tracks survey costs for capitalization decisions, impairment testing, and area-of-interest expenditure reporting under IFRS6.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Geophysical survey contractors are vendors. Links to vendor master for contractor performance tracking, cost management, contract compliance, and procurement planning. Essential for multi-survey contr']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the exploration prospect or tenement area where this survey was conducted.']
  target_commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Geophysical surveys target commodity-specific signatures (magnetics for iron ore/magnetite, EM for sulfide copper/nickel, gravity for massive sulfides) - determines survey method selection and interpr']
  acquisition_end_date date [note: 'Date when field data acquisition or sample collection was completed for this survey program. Null if survey is ongoing.']
  acquisition_start_date date [note: 'Date when field data acquisition or sample collection commenced for this survey program.']
  anomaly_count varchar [note: 'Number of discrete anomalies identified from the survey that meet the anomaly threshold criteria and are recommended for follow-up investigation or drilling.']
  anomaly_threshold varchar [note: 'Statistical or empirical criteria used to define anomalous values that warrant follow-up investigation (e.g., \'mean + 2 standard deviations\', \'>100 ppb Au\'). Used for target generation.']
  area_km2 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total area covered by the survey program in square kilometers. Used for cost estimation and coverage analysis.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Spatial reference system used for survey location data (e.g., \'GDA2020 MGA Zone 50\', \'WGS84 UTM Zone 51S\'). Critical for GIS integration and spatial analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survey record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the survey cost (e.g., \'USD\', \'AUD\', \'CAD\').']
  data_format varchar [note: 'File format and structure of the raw survey data (e.g., \'XYZ ASCII\', \'Geosoft GDB\', \'CSV\', \'LAS\', \'SEGY\'). Critical for data integration and processing workflows.']
  data_storage_location varchar [note: 'File path, database location, or cloud storage URI where the raw and processed survey data files are stored. Used for data retrieval and audit trail.']
  detection_limit varchar [note: 'Minimum detectable concentration or signal threshold for the survey method. For geochemical: analytical detection limits (e.g., \'0.01 ppm Au\'). For geophysical: instrument sensitivity specifications.']
  drill_targets_generated varchar [note: 'Number of drill targets prioritized from this survey for inclusion in future drilling programs. Subset of total anomalies that pass economic and technical filters.']
  grid_spacing_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Distance in meters between sample points in a regular grid for geochemical surveys (e.g., soil sampling grid). Null for line-based geophysical surveys.']
  interpretation_status varchar [note: 'Current state of geological interpretation and anomaly identification: pending (not started), in_progress (geologist reviewing), completed (interpretation finished), peer_reviewed (technical review done), approved (sign-off for drill targeting).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this survey record is currently active (true) or has been archived/superseded (false). Used for filtering current operational surveys.']
  line_spacing_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Distance in meters between survey lines for geophysical surveys (e.g., airborne flight lines, ground traverse lines). Null for geochemical grid surveys.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survey record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, observations, limitations, or special conditions related to the survey (e.g., weather delays, equipment issues, access constraints, data quality concerns).']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current state of data processing workflow: raw (unprocessed field data), in_progress (undergoing corrections and transformations), completed (processing finished), validated (QC approved), archived (final data stored).']
  report_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference or identifier for the final survey interpretation report (e.g., internal report number, contractor report ID). Links to document management system.']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Total number of physical samples collected for geochemical surveys. Null for geophysical surveys that do not involve physical sampling.']
  sample_interval_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Distance in meters between individual sample collection points along a line or within a grid. Applicable to both geophysical station spacing and geochemical sample spacing.']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'Specific survey technique employed. For geophysical: airborne magnetics, gravity, electromagnetic (EM), induced polarization (IP), seismic, radiometric. For geochemical: soil sampling, stream sediment, rock chip, auger, lag sampling.']
  survey_name varchar [note: 'Business name or identifier for the survey program, typically including location and year (e.g., \'Mt Whaleback Airborne Magnetics 2023\').']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the survey method: geophysical (remote sensing, non-invasive) or geochemical (physical sampling and analysis).']
  total_line_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total length of survey lines flown or traversed in kilometers for geophysical surveys. Null for grid-based geochemical surveys.']

  Note: 'Master record for all exploration survey programs conducted over prospect areas, encompassing both geophysical methods (airborne magnetics, gravity, EM, IP, seismic, radiometric) and geochemical methods (soil sampling grids, stream sediment surveys, rock chip programs). Captures survey name, survey type (geophysical or geochemical), survey method, target commodity, contractor, acquisition/collection date range, line spacing or grid spacing, sample interval, coordinate system, number of samples collected (geochemical), detection limits, anomaly threshold criteria, data format, processing status, and interpretation status. Surveys are primary exploration targeting tools used to identify anomalies and prioritise drill targets. Unified register eliminates dual maintenance of geophysical and geochemical survey campaigns.'
}

Table exploration.resource_estimate {
  resource_estimate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the mineral resource estimate record. Primary key.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Resource estimates quantify tonnage and grade of specific commodities - mandatory for JORC/NI 43-101 reporting, investor disclosure, and mine planning. Normalizes existing commodity text field to ensu']
  competent_person_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.competent_person. Business justification: Resource estimates must be signed off by a JORC/NI 43-101 competent person. Currently resource_estimate stores competent_person_name, competent_person_credentials, and competent_person_organization as']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral deposit or prospect for which this resource estimate was prepared.']
  project_valuation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project_valuation. Business justification: Resource estimates feed NPV/IRR calculations in project valuations. Finance references specific resource estimates for valuation models, investment decisions, and impairment testing under IFRS6.']
  average_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'The average grade of the primary commodity across the estimated resource tonnage.']
  average_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for average grade (e.g., %, g/t, ppm, oz/t).']
  block_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file name of the geological block model used as the basis for this resource estimate.']
  block_model_version varchar [note: 'Version number or identifier of the block model used for this estimate.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments, notes, or qualifications regarding the resource estimate, including material assumptions or limitations.']
  contained_metal decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total quantity of contained metal calculated as tonnage multiplied by average grade, expressed in appropriate units (e.g., tonnes, ounces).']
  contained_metal_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for contained metal (e.g., t, kt, Mt, oz, koz, Moz).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this resource estimate record was first created in the system.']
  cut_off_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'The minimum economic grade used to define the resource boundary. Material below this grade is considered waste.']
  cut_off_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the cut-off grade (e.g., %, g/t, ppm).']
  density_assumption decimal(6,3) [note: 'Assumed bulk density (tonnes per cubic metre) used in tonnage calculations for the resource estimate.']
  drill_hole_count varchar [note: 'Total number of drill holes used as input data for this resource estimate.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this resource estimate is effective and represents the state of geological knowledge and data.']
  estimate_confidence_level varchar [note: 'Overall confidence level in the resource estimate based on data quality, density, and geological understanding.']
  estimate_name varchar [note: 'Business name or title of the resource estimate, typically referencing the deposit, prospect, or project name.']
  estimate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the resource estimate within the approval and governance workflow.']
  estimate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the estimate within the project lifecycle (e.g., initial, updated, revised, final).']
  estimation_method varchar [note: 'The geostatistical or geometric method used to estimate grades and tonnages (e.g., kriging, inverse distance weighting, nearest neighbour). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Ordinary Kriging|Indicator Kriging|Multiple Indicator Kriging|Inverse Distance Weighting|Nearest Neighbour|Polygonal|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  metallurgical_recovery_assumption decimal(5,2) [note: 'Assumed metallurgical recovery rate (percentage) used in economic assessment for reasonable prospects of eventual economic extraction.']
  mining_method_assumption varchar [note: 'The assumed mining method used in determining reasonable prospects for eventual economic extraction (e.g., open pit, underground, sub-level caving).']
  public_disclosure_date date [note: 'Date on which this resource estimate was publicly disclosed to the market or regulatory authorities.']
  report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the technical report or public filing document containing the full details of this resource estimate.']
  reporting_standard varchar [note: 'The international mineral resource reporting standard under which this estimate was prepared and reported.']
  resource_classification varchar [note: 'The confidence classification of the mineral resource: Measured (highest confidence), Indicated (moderate confidence), or Inferred (lowest confidence).']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Total number of assay samples used in the estimation process.']
  sign_off_date date [note: 'The date on which the Competent Person formally signed off and approved this resource estimate.']
  tonnage decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated tonnage of mineralized material in the resource category, typically expressed in million tonnes (Mt) or thousand tonnes (kt).']
  tonnage_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for tonnage: tonnes (t), thousand tonnes (kt), million tonnes (Mt), or billion tonnes (Gt).']
  total_metres_drilled decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total metres of drilling completed and used as the basis for this resource estimate.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this resource estimate record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'JORC/NI 43-101/SAMREC-compliant mineral resource estimate for a defined deposit or prospect.
