Captures estimate name, effective date, reporting standard (JORC 2012, NI 43-101, SAMREC), commodity, classification (Measured, Indicated, Inferred), estimation method (kriging, IDW, nearest neighbour), cut-off grade, tonnage, average grade, contained metal, block model reference, competent person name and credentials, and sign-off date. The authoritative SSOT for resource inventory reporting.'
}

Table exploration.ore_reserve {
  ore_reserve_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ore reserve statement. Primary key for the ore reserve entity.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Ore reserves are commodity-specific economic inventories - required for mine planning, production scheduling, and ASX/SEC disclosure. Normalizes existing commodity text field to enforce consistent com']
  competent_person_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.competent_person. Business justification: Ore reserves must be signed off by a JORC/NI 43-101 competent person. Currently ore_reserve stores competent_person_name, competent_person_credentials, and competent_person_organization as denormalize']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Ore reserves represent the economically mineable portion of an orebody. Every ore reserve declaration in mining operations must reference the specific orebody it pertains to for mine planning, product']
  project_valuation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project_valuation. Business justification: Ore reserves are the basis for mine project valuations. Every project valuation references a specific reserve estimate for NPV calculation, life-of-mine planning, and investment committee approvals.']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral deposit for which this ore reserve statement is prepared.']
  resource_estimate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying Measured or Indicated Mineral Resource estimate from which this ore reserve is derived.']
  target_saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Ore reserves in operating/near-production mines are converted to specific saleable products in life-of-mine plans - required for NPV modeling, offtake contract planning, and production scheduling. Lin']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the ore reserve statement was formally approved by the Competent Person and authorized for public disclosure.']
  average_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Weighted average grade of the valuable mineral or metal in the ore reserve, calculated after application of dilution and mining recovery factors.']
  average_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the average grade, typically percentage (%) for bulk commodities or grams per tonne (g/t) for precious metals.']
  board_sign_off_date date [note: 'Date on which the ore reserve statement was formally endorsed and signed off by the company board of directors for regulatory reporting and public disclosure.']
  commodity_price_assumption decimal(18,2) [note: 'The assumed commodity price used in the economic evaluation to determine the viability of the ore reserve.']
  commodity_price_currency varchar [note: 'The currency in which the commodity price assumption is expressed, using ISO 4217 three-letter currency code.']
  contained_metal decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total quantity of contained metal or mineral in the ore reserve, calculated as reserve tonnage multiplied by average grade.']
  contained_metal_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the contained metal, typically tonnes, kilograms, or ounces depending on the commodity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ore reserve record was first created in the system.']
  cut_off_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'The minimum economic grade threshold applied to define ore from waste. Material below this grade is classified as waste and excluded from the ore reserve tonnage.']
  cut_off_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the cut-off grade, typically percentage (%) for bulk commodities or grams per tonne (g/t) for precious metals.']
  dilution_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage or factor representing the expected dilution of ore grade due to unplanned inclusion of waste material during mining operations.']
  disclosure_date date [note: 'Date on which the ore reserve statement was publicly disclosed to the market and regulatory authorities.']
  economic_assumptions varchar [note: 'Summary of key economic assumptions used in the ore reserve calculation, including commodity prices, exchange rates, operating costs, and capital costs.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date as of which the ore reserve estimate is current and valid, representing the cut-off for all data and assumptions used in the reserve calculation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ore reserve record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  lom_years decimal(5,1) [note: 'Estimated life of mine in years based on the ore reserve tonnage and planned annual production rate.']
  metallurgical_recovery_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage or factor representing the expected proportion of contained metal that will be recovered during mineral processing and beneficiation.']
  mining_method varchar [note: 'The planned mining method to be applied for extraction of the ore reserve, which influences dilution, recovery, and economic viability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Open Pit|Underground|SLC|Block Cave|Longwall|Room and Pillar|Cut and Fill|Sublevel Stoping — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mining_recovery_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage or factor representing the expected proportion of in-situ ore that will be successfully extracted during mining operations.']
  modifying_factors_applied varchar [note: 'Description of the modifying factors applied to convert the Mineral Resource to an Ore Reserve, including mining, metallurgical, economic, marketing, legal, environmental, social, and governmental considerations.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or qualifications related to the ore reserve estimate, including material risks, uncertainties, or assumptions.']
  reporting_standard varchar [note: 'The international mineral resource and reserve reporting standard under which this ore reserve statement is prepared and disclosed.']
  reserve_classification varchar [note: 'Classification category of the ore reserve: Proved Reserve (highest confidence, derived from Measured Resource) or Probable Reserve (derived from Indicated Resource).']
  reserve_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code or identifier for the ore reserve used in reporting and planning systems.']
  reserve_name varchar [note: 'Business name or designation of the ore reserve, typically referencing the deposit or mining area.']
  reserve_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ore reserve statement, indicating whether it is in draft, approved for use, publicly disclosed, superseded by a newer estimate, or archived.']
  reserve_tonnage decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tonnage of ore in the reserve after application of modifying factors including dilution, mining recovery, and cut-off grade.']
  reserve_tonnage_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the ore reserve tonnage.']
  strip_ratio decimal(10,2) [note: 'Ratio of waste material (overburden) to ore that must be removed to access the ore reserve, applicable to open pit mining operations.']

  Note: 'JORC/NI 43-101/SAMREC-compliant ore reserve statement for a defined deposit, representing the economically mineable portion of a Measured or Indicated Mineral Resource. Captures reserve name, effective date, reporting standard, classification (Proved, Probable), mining method, dilution factor, mining recovery, cut-off grade, tonnage, average grade, contained metal, modifying factors applied, competent person credentials, and board sign-off date. Distinct from resource_estimate as it incorporates modifying factors and economic viability assessment.'
}

Table exploration.expenditure {
  expenditure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for expenditure']
  approval_authority_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Exploration expenditure requires delegated authority tracking for financial controls and audit compliance. Links spending to authorized employees. Replaces text field with FK.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP cost centre responsible for this expenditure, enabling financial reporting and variance analysis.']
  drill_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the exploration programme under which this expenditure was incurred or budgeted.']
  exploration_licence_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the exploration licence (tenement) against which this expenditure is recorded for statutory compliance tracking.']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: HSE incidents generate costs (medical treatment, environmental remediation, investigation) charged to exploration cost centers. Financial tracking requirement for incident cost recovery and budget var']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Exploration expenditure must trace to originating purchase orders for financial reconciliation, budget tracking, three-way match (PO-GR-Invoice), audit trail, and variance analysis. Essential for fina']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Exploration expenditure records track vendor payments for drilling, assaying, geophysics. Links to vendor master enables spend analysis by vendor, vendor performance correlation with exploration outco']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific prospect or target area within the exploration programme where expenditure was incurred.']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Statutory expenditure reporting to mining regulators requires direct tenement attribution. Not all exploration expenditure is prospect-specific (e.g., tenement holding costs, regional studies). Enable']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP WBS element for project-based expenditure tracking and hierarchical cost roll-up.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the expenditure or budget allocation in the specified currency.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget or expenditure was formally approved by the designated authority.']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the budget, enabling tracking of multiple budget revisions throughout the financial year (e.g., V1, V2, V3).']
  commitment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum statutory expenditure commitment amount required for the licence or programme in the specified financial period, used for compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this expenditure record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the expenditure amount (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this expenditure record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Pronto Xi, manual entry), supporting data lineage and reconciliation.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP financial document number (e.g., invoice number, journal entry number) associated with this expenditure, enabling traceability to source documents.']
  expenditure_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the expenditure type: drilling, geophysics, geochemistry, geological mapping, assaying, camp and logistics costs, feasibility studies, or other exploration-related activities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drilling|geophysics|geochemistry|mapping|assaying|camp_costs|studies|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expenditure_date date [note: 'The date on which the actual expenditure was incurred or the budget was allocated, serving as the principal business event timestamp for this record.']
  expenditure_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about the nature of the expenditure or budget allocation, including specific activities or deliverables.']
  financial_period varchar [note: 'The financial period (month) within the financial year, formatted as P01 through P12, enabling monthly budget vs actual tracking.']
  financial_year varchar [note: 'The financial year to which this expenditure or budget applies, formatted as FY followed by the four-digit year (e.g., FY2024).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this expenditure record was last modified, enabling change tracking and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments related to the expenditure, including explanations for variances, special circumstances, or audit trail information.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the expenditure transaction was posted to the general ledger in the SAP system, distinct from the expenditure date for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this record represents an original budget allocation, a revised budget, or actual expenditure incurred.']
  sap_cost_object_reference varchar [note: 'The SAP internal order, WBS element, or cost centre code used as the cost object for this expenditure in the ERP system.']
  statutory_expenditure_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this expenditure contributes toward satisfying minimum statutory expenditure commitments required under the exploration licence conditions.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated variance between budgeted and actual expenditure, enabling direct budget-vs-actual analysis. Positive values indicate under-spend, negative values indicate over-spend.']

  Note: 'Unified financial tracking for exploration programmes capturing approved budgets (original and revised versions by financial year and program) and actual expenditure incurred against exploration licences and programs. Records expenditure/budget category (drilling, geophysics, geochemistry, mapping, assaying, camp costs, studies), amount, currency, financial period, cost centre, record type (budget_original, budget_revised, actual), programme reference, prospect reference, SAP cost object reference, approval authority, budget version, variance calculation, and statutory expenditure compliance flag. Enables direct budget-vs-actual variance analysis at programme, prospect, and licence level. Satisfies minimum statutory expenditure commitment reporting under licence conditions. Distinct from the finance domain general ledger — scoped exclusively to exploration-phase programme financial management and planning.'
}

Table exploration.competent_person {
  competent_person_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the competent person record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Competent persons are employees requiring payroll integration, training currency verification, medical fitness tracking, and competency management. Critical for JORC/NI 43-101 compliance and HR system']
  commodity_expertise varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of mineral commodities for which the CP/QP has demonstrated expertise and experience (e.g., iron ore, copper, gold, lithium, nickel, coal).']
  competent_person_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the Competent Person record in the master register. Active CPs are eligible to sign off on resource/reserve statements.']
  consent_date date [note: 'The date on which the Competent Person provided written consent for their name to be included in the public report and for the form and context in which their statement appears. Required under ASX Listing Rules 5.22 and NI 43-101.']
  consent_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the CP/QP consent expires and must be renewed for continued use of their statement in public disclosures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this Competent Person record was first created in the system.']
  current_employer varchar [note: 'The name of the organization currently employing the Competent Person. Used to assess independence and potential conflicts of interest.']
  declaration_effective_from date [note: 'The start date from which the CP/QP declaration and sign-off is effective for resource/reserve reporting purposes.']
  declaration_effective_to date [note: 'The end date of the CP/QP declaration effective period. Nullable for ongoing declarations.']
  discipline_expertise varchar [note: 'The technical discipline(s) in which the CP/QP is qualified (e.g., geology, mining engineering, metallurgy, geophysics, resource estimation, mine planning).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contacting the Competent Person for verification and consent purposes.']
  employer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CP/QP\'s employer type, used to determine independence status for regulatory reporting.']
  five_year_experience_confirmed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag confirming that the CP/QP has at least five years of relevant experience in the style of mineralization and type of deposit under consideration, as required by JORC/NI 43-101/SAMREC.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the Competent Person or Qualified Person as registered with their professional body.']
  independence_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the CP/QP is independent of the issuer/company. Independent means the person is not an employee, officer, or director of the company and does not have material financial interest. Required disclosure under ASX, TSX, and SEC rules.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'The expiry date of the CP/QP\'s professional indemnity insurance policy, if applicable.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this Competent Person record was last updated.']
  last_verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the CP/QP credentials, membership status, and experience were last verified by the company\'s compliance team.']
  membership_grade varchar [note: 'The grade or class of membership held (e.g., Fellow, Member, Associate) which determines eligibility to act as a Competent Person.']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'The unique membership or registration number issued by the professional body to the Competent Person.']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current standing of the CP/QP membership with their professional body. Must be \'current\' for the person to sign off on resource/reserve statements.']
  next_verification_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the next verification of CP/QP credentials and membership status must be completed to maintain compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or qualifications related to the CP/QP\'s registration and authority.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the Competent Person.']
  professional_body varchar [note: 'The recognized professional organization through which the CP/QP is accredited. AusIMM (Australasian Institute of Mining and Metallurgy), AIG (Australian Institute of Geoscientists), CIM (Canadian Institute of Mining, Metallurgy and Petroleum), SACNASP (South African Council for Natural Scientific Professions), SME (Society for Mining, Metallurgy & Exploration), IMMM (Institute of Materials, Minerals and Mining).']
  professional_indemnity_insurance boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the CP/QP holds current professional indemnity insurance, which may be required by some professional bodies and reporting jurisdictions.']
  qualification_degree varchar [note: 'The highest relevant academic degree held by the CP/QP (e.g., Bachelor of Science in Geology, Master of Engineering in Mining).']
  qualification_institution varchar [note: 'The name of the educational institution that awarded the CP/QP\'s highest relevant degree.']
  registration_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country in which the CP/QP is registered with their professional body. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AUS|CAN|ZAF|USA|GBR|CHL|PER|BRA — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reporting_code_authority varchar [note: 'The mineral resource reporting code under which the CP/QP is authorized to sign off. JORC (Australia), NI 43-101 (Canada), SAMREC (South Africa), PERC (Europe), CRIRSCO (international umbrella).']
  signature_authority_level varchar [note: 'The level of reporting the CP/QP is authorized to sign off on: mineral resources only, ore reserves only, both, or exploration results only.']
  years_experience varchar [note: 'Total number of years of relevant experience in the mineral industry. Must be at least 5 years to qualify as a Competent Person under JORC/NI 43-101/SAMREC.']

  Note: 'Master register of Competent Persons (CPs) and Qualified Persons (QPs) authorised to sign off on JORC, NI 43-101, or SAMREC mineral resource and ore reserve statements. Captures CP/QP name, professional body membership (AusIMM, AIG, CIM, SACNASP, SME), membership number, membership status (current, lapsed), relevant commodity expertise, minimum 5-year experience confirmation, current employer, independence status (independent, non-independent), consent date, and declaration effective period. Required for regulatory compliance with ASX Listing Rules Chapter 5, SEC S-K 1300, TSX NI 43-101, and JSE SAMREC disclosure obligations.'
}

Currently resource_disclosure stores reserve_estimate_reference as a string code. Adding ore_']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iron_ore|copper|coal|lithium|nickel|gold|silver|zinc|lead|uranium|bauxite|rare_earth — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  disclosure_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing additional context, assumptions, qualifications, or explanatory notes accompanying the disclosure (e.g., changes in methodology, material assumptions, forward-looking statements).']
  disclosure_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this disclosure by the company or regulatory body (e.g., ASX announcement number, SEC filing reference).']
  disclosure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the disclosure: draft (in preparation), pending_approval (awaiting board or competent person sign-off), approved (authorized for release), published (publicly released), superseded (replaced by newer disclosure), withdrawn (retracted).']
  disclosure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the disclosure event: initial (first-time disclosure), update (routine update), annual (annual reporting cycle), material_change (triggered by significant change), restatement (correction of prior disclosure), withdrawal (retraction of prior disclosure).']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date as of which the resource and reserve estimates in this disclosure are current and valid (typically the date of the underlying geological model or mine plan).']
  exchange_or_regulator varchar [note: 'The stock exchange or regulatory body to which this disclosure was submitted: ASX (Australian Securities Exchange), TSX (Toronto Stock Exchange), NYSE (New York Stock Exchange), LSE (London Stock Exchange), JSE (Johannesburg Stock Exchange), SEC (U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission), ASIC (Australian Securities and Investments Commission). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ASX|TSX|NYSE|LSE|JSE|SEC|ASIC — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this disclosure record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  material_change_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this disclosure represents a material change (10% or greater change in resource or reserve tonnage or grade) compared to the previous disclosure.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'The name of the mining project or operation to which this resource and reserve disclosure applies.']
  reporting_entity_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the company or entity making the disclosure (typically the listed parent company or operating subsidiary).']
  reporting_standard varchar [note: 'The mineral resource and ore reserve reporting standard or code under which this disclosure was prepared: JORC (Joint Ore Reserves Committee - Australia), NI 43-101 (National Instrument 43-101 - Canada), SEC S-K 1300 (U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission), SAMREC (South African Mineral Resource Committee), PERC (Pan-European Reserves and Resources Reporting Committee).']
  reserve_cutoff_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'The minimum economic grade (percentage or grams per tonne) used to define the boundary of the ore reserve estimate, incorporating mining, processing, and economic factors.']
  resource_cutoff_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'The minimum economic grade (percentage or grams per tonne) used to define the boundary of the mineral resource estimate. Material below this grade is excluded from the resource.']
  technical_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or title of the supporting technical report (e.g., NI 43-101 Technical Report, JORC Table 1, SEC Technical Report Summary) that provides detailed documentation of the resource and reserve estimates.']
  total_indicated_resource_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tonnage (in metric tonnes) of Indicated Mineral Resource disclosed in this statement. Indicated Resource is a medium confidence resource category.']
  total_inferred_resource_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tonnage (in metric tonnes) of Inferred Mineral Resource disclosed in this statement. Inferred Resource is the lowest confidence resource category.']
  total_measured_resource_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tonnage (in metric tonnes) of Measured Mineral Resource disclosed in this statement. Measured Resource is the highest confidence resource category.']
  total_probable_reserve_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tonnage (in metric tonnes) of Probable Ore Reserve disclosed in this statement. Probable Reserve is derived from Measured and/or Indicated Resource.']
  total_proved_reserve_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tonnage (in metric tonnes) of Proved Ore Reserve disclosed in this statement. Proved Reserve is the highest confidence reserve category, derived from Measured Resource.']

  Note: 'Tracks formal public disclosures of mineral resource and ore reserve statements made to regulatory bodies and stock exchanges (ASX, TSX, NYSE, SEC). Captures disclosure date, reporting standard, exchange or regulator, disclosure type (initial, update, annual), resource estimate reference, ore reserve reference, competent person sign-off, board approval date, announcement reference number, and material change flag. Supports JORC, NI 43-101, SEC S-K 1300, and IFRS 6 compliance obligations.'
}

Table exploration.exploration_budget {
  exploration_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the exploration budget record. Primary key for the exploration budget entity.']
  capex_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_budget. Business justification: Exploration budgets for capital activities (e.g., drill rig purchases, infrastructure) link to capex_budget for consolidated capital planning, board approvals, and capital allocation reporting.']
  drill_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the exploration programme this budget is allocated to. Links budget to specific exploration initiatives.']
  opex_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.opex_budget. Business justification: Exploration budgets for operational activities (drilling, assays, field work) link to opex_budget for operating cost control, variance analysis, and AISC reporting.']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral prospect or tenement area this budget applies to. Enables budget tracking at prospect level.']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Tenement-level budget planning is required for minimum expenditure commitment forecasting and regulatory compliance planning. Enables tracking of statutory obligations versus planned spend, critical f']
  updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Budget change audit trails require employee attribution for financial governance and SOX compliance. Essential for tracking who modified budget allocations. Replaces user text field.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Type of exploration activity this budget is allocated for. Enables budget tracking by exploration discipline and work category.']
  actual_expenditure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual amount spent against this budget to date. Used for variance analysis and budget performance monitoring.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the authority who approved this budget (e.g., CFO, Exploration Manager, Board of Directors). Establishes accountability and governance.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this budget was formally approved by the designated authority. Marks the start of budget validity period.']
  approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The formally approved budget amount for this exploration programme or activity. Represents the authorized spending limit.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'Classification of budget as capital expenditure or operating expenditure. Critical for financial reporting and tax treatment under IFRS 6.']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the exploration budget. Used for external reporting and cross-system reference.']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the exploration budget allocation. Provides human-readable identification of the budget purpose.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exploration budget. Tracks budget through approval workflow and operational lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|active|suspended|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  capitalisation_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether expenditure under this budget is eligible for capitalisation under IFRS 6. True if expenditure can be capitalized as exploration and evaluation asset, false if must be expensed.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of budget that has been committed through purchase orders or contracts but not yet spent. Represents encumbered funds.']
  commodity_focus varchar [note: 'Primary mineral commodity targeted by this exploration budget. Enables commodity-specific budget analysis and resource allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iron_ore|copper|coal|lithium|nickel|gold|zinc|lead|rare_earths|multi_commodity — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cost_centre_code varchar [note: 'SAP cost centre code to which this exploration budget is allocated. Links budget to organizational accounting structure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget record (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).']
  end_date date [note: 'Effective end date of the budget period. Defines when spending against this budget must cease or be reallocated.']
  exploration_stage varchar [note: 'Stage of exploration maturity this budget supports. Greenfield represents early-stage exploration, brownfield represents near-mine exploration, resource definition focuses on JORC resource estimation, pre-feasibility and feasibility support economic studies.']
  financial_year varchar [note: 'The financial year this budget applies to, formatted as FY followed by four-digit year (e.g., FY2024). Aligns with corporate financial planning cycles.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of funding for this exploration budget. Identifies whether budget is funded internally, through joint venture partners, government grants, debt, or equity.']
  joint_venture_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage share of exploration budget attributable to the company in joint venture arrangements. Used to calculate net budget exposure and funding obligations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget record was last modified. Audit trail for record changes and version control.']
  minimum_expenditure_commitment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Statutory minimum expenditure required to maintain exploration license or tenement rights. Critical for regulatory compliance and license retention.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, assumptions, or constraints related to this budget allocation. Captures qualitative information for budget management.']
  start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the budget period. Defines when spending against this budget can commence.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between approved budget and actual expenditure. Positive values indicate under-spend, negative values indicate over-spend.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version type of the budget record. Original represents initial approved budget, revised reflects formal amendments, forecast represents updated projections, supplementary indicates additional allocations.']
  wbs_element_code varchar [note: 'SAP WBS element code linking this budget to a specific project or programme phase. Enables project-based budget tracking and reporting.']

  Note: 'Annual and program-level exploration budget records capturing approved budget amounts by exploration programme, prospect, activity type (drilling, geophysics, geochemistry, studies), financial year, budget version (original, revised), approval authority, and variance to actual expenditure. Distinct from the finance domain general ledger budget — this entity is scoped to exploration programme planning and statutory minimum expenditure commitment management.'
}

Table exploration.mineralisation_intercept {
  mineralisation_intercept_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the calculated mineralisation intercept record. Primary key for this data product.']
  calculated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: JORC compliance requires named competent persons for intercept calculations. Links results to qualified employees for audit and competency verification. Replaces text field.']
  drill_hole_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drill hole from which this mineralisation intercept was derived. Links to the drill hole master data.']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'FK to exploration.prospect']
  announcement_date date [note: 'Date when this intercept result was publicly announced to the stock exchange. Critical for continuous disclosure compliance and investor relations.']
  announcement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the ASX/TSX/stock exchange market announcement in which this intercept was publicly disclosed. Links to investor communications.']
  calculation_date date [note: 'Date when the mineralisation intercept was calculated. Critical for audit trail and version control of exploration results.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate the composite grade from individual assay intervals (e.g., length-weighted average, grade-thickness product, nearest neighbour, inverse distance weighting).']
  capping_grade decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum grade threshold applied to limit the influence of extreme high-grade outliers on the composite grade calculation. Nullable if no capping was applied.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, geological observations, calculation notes, or caveats related to this intercept. Supports JORC Table 1 commentary requirements.']
  commodity varchar [note: 'Primary mineral commodity of interest for this intercept (e.g., iron ore, copper, gold, lithium, nickel, coal). Determines the grade calculation methodology and reporting standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iron_ore|copper|gold|lithium|nickel|coal|zinc|lead|silver|uranium — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  composite_grade decimal(12,4) [note: 'Weighted average grade of the target commodity across the intercept interval, calculated from individual assay intervals. Expressed in commodity-specific units (e.g., % for iron ore, g/t for gold, ppm for lithium).']
  cutoff_grade decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum economic grade threshold applied to define the mineralisation intercept. Assay intervals below this grade are excluded from the composite calculation. Critical parameter for JORC/NI 43-101 disclosure.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or database from which the underlying assay data was extracted (e.g., LabWare LIMS, Geovia GEMS, acQuire). Supports data lineage and traceability.']
  downhole_width decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length of the mineralisation intercept measured along the drill hole axis, calculated as to_depth minus from_depth. Represents the drilled thickness.']
  from_depth decimal(10,2) [note: 'Starting depth of the mineralisation intercept measured in metres from the drill hole collar. Represents the top of the mineralised zone.']
  geological_domain varchar [note: 'Geological unit or domain code assigned to this intercept based on lithology, alteration, or mineralisation style. Used for resource domain modeling.']
  grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the composite grade value. Common units include percent (%), grams per tonne (g/t), parts per million (ppm), ounces per tonne (oz/t), or kilograms per tonne (kg/t).']
  intercept_number varchar [note: 'Sequential or business-assigned identifier for the intercept within the drill hole, used for reporting and cross-referencing in market announcements.']
  intercept_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the intercept calculation. Tracks progression from initial calculation through validation, approval, and public disclosure.']
  internal_dilution decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum continuous length of sub-cutoff grade material allowed within the intercept without breaking the composite. Represents waste material included in the mineralised zone.']
  minimum_intercept_width decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum downhole width threshold applied to define a reportable intercept. Intercepts narrower than this threshold are excluded from reporting.']
  number_of_samples varchar [note: 'Count of individual assay samples included in the composite grade calculation for this intercept. Indicates the data density supporting the result.']
  oxidation_state varchar [note: 'Weathering or oxidation classification of the mineralisation (e.g., oxide, transitional, fresh, supergene, hypogene). Impacts metallurgical recovery and processing method.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Name of the broader exploration or mining project to which this intercept belongs. Enables project-level rollup and resource aggregation.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this intercept record was first created in the data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this intercept record was last modified. Tracks changes to calculation parameters, validation status, or metadata updates.']
  reporting_standard varchar [note: 'International mineral resource reporting code applied to this intercept calculation (e.g., JORC 2012, NI 43-101, SAMREC, SEC S-K 1300, PERC). Determines disclosure requirements.']
  to_depth decimal(10,2) [note: 'Ending depth of the mineralisation intercept measured in metres from the drill hole collar. Represents the bottom of the mineralised zone.']
  true_width decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated true thickness of the mineralisation perpendicular to the orebody orientation, adjusted for drill hole angle and orebody dip. Critical metric for resource estimation.']
  validated_by varchar [note: 'Name of the senior geologist or competent person who validated the intercept calculation. Required for quality assurance and regulatory compliance.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date when the intercept calculation was validated and approved for reporting. Establishes the effective date for market disclosure.']

  Note: 'Calculated significant mineralisation intercepts derived from drill hole assay data, representing the key exploration result communicated to investors and regulators. Captures hole ID, from-to depth, true width, composite grade, cut-off grade applied, capping grade applied, commodity, calculation method, and the analyst who calculated the intercept. These intercepts are the primary data product for ASX/TSX market announcements and JORC Table 1 disclosures.'
}

Table exploration.prospect_laboratory_assignment {
  prospect_laboratory_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this prospect-laboratory assignment relationship. Primary key.']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the laboratory providing analytical services']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the exploration prospect receiving laboratory services']
  analytical_suite_specification varchar [note: 'Detailed specification of the analytical methods, analyte packages, and quality control protocols contracted for this prospect. May reference specific method codes, detection limits, and reporting requirements tailored to the prospect\'s commodity targets and exploration stage.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this prospect-laboratory assignment: active (in use), suspended (temporarily paused), expired (contract ended), or terminated (relationship ended early).']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory services contract or agreement expires or is scheduled for renewal for this prospect-laboratory pairing.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory services contract or agreement became effective for this prospect-laboratory pairing.']
  cost_per_sample_negotiated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Negotiated cost per sample for this prospect-laboratory pairing, which may differ from the laboratory\'s standard pricing due to volume commitments, contract terms, or analytical suite customization. Used for prospect-level budget forecasting and cost control.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this prospect-laboratory assignment record was created in the system.']
  laboratory_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of this laboratory for this prospect: primary (main analytical provider), umpire (dispute resolution), check (QA/QC verification), metallurgical (test work), or specialist (specific analyte or method).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this assignment record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this assignment record.']
  preferred_laboratory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this laboratory is designated as the preferred or primary provider for this specific prospect. Governs default laboratory selection for routine sample submissions from this prospect.']
  sample_volume_commitment varchar [note: 'Minimum number of samples committed to this laboratory for this prospect under the contract terms, used to secure volume-based pricing discounts.']
  turnaround_priority varchar [note: 'Default turnaround time priority level for samples from this prospect: standard (routine exploration), priority (active drilling program), rush (resource definition), or critical (feasibility study). Governs SLA expectations and cost per sample.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this assignment record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contract relationship between exploration prospects and analytical laboratories. It captures laboratory selection, contract terms, analytical specifications, and service level agreements specific to each prospect-laboratory pairing. Each record links one prospect to one laboratory with attributes governing vendor management, quality assurance protocols, and turnaround priorities for that specific exploration program.. Existence Justification: In mining exploration operations, prospects routinely engage multiple laboratories simultaneously for different analytical roles: a primary laboratory for routine assays, an umpire laboratory for dispute resolution and QA/QC verification, and specialist laboratories for metallurgical test work or specific analytes. Conversely, each laboratory services multiple exploration prospects across different projects, tenements, and commodity programs. This is an operational vendor management relationship where exploration teams actively create, negotiate, and manage laboratory assignments with prospect-specific contract terms, analytical specifications, and service level agreements.'
}

Table exploration.prospect_processing_campaign {
  prospect_processing_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this prospect-plant processing campaign record. Primary key.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the processing plant facility performing the processing']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the exploration prospect whose ore is being processed']
  average_recovery_achieved decimal(6,3) [note: 'Actual average metallurgical recovery rate achieved for ore from this prospect when processed through this plant, expressed as percentage (0-100). Tracks real performance vs nameplate.']
  blend_percentage_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage (0-100) of this prospects ore in the plant feed blend when multiple prospects are being processed simultaneously. Used for blending optimization.']
  campaign_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes about this specific prospect-plant processing campaign, including performance observations, blending considerations, or metallurgical issues.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this prospect-plant processing campaign. Active = currently processing; completed = campaign finished; suspended = temporarily halted; planned = future campaign.']
  cumulative_tonnes_processed decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cumulative tonnage of ore from this prospect that has been processed through this plant, measured in metric tonnes. Updated as processing occurs.']
  ore_routing_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority classification for routing ore from this prospect to this plant. Primary = preferred plant; secondary = backup plant; opportunistic = capacity-dependent; trial = test campaign.']
  processing_end_date date [note: 'Date when ore from this prospect ceased being processed through this plant for this campaign. Null if currently active.']
  processing_start_date date [note: 'Date when ore from this prospect first entered this processing plant for this campaign or processing period.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the processing campaign relationship between exploration prospects and processing plants. It captures the operational history of ore routing, blending campaigns, and processing performance for each prospect-plant combination. Each record links one prospect to one processing plant with attributes that track processing dates, tonnages, recoveries, and routing priorities—data that exists only in the context of this specific prospect being processed through this specific plant.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, a single exploration prospect\'s ore can be processed through multiple processing plants over its lifecycle due to plant upgrades, capacity constraints, ore blending strategies, or multi-plant site configurations. Conversely, a single processing plant routinely processes ore from multiple prospects simultaneously or sequentially through blending campaigns and feed scheduling. This is a fundamental operational relationship in mine-to-mill optimization where processing performance, tonnages, and routing priorities are tracked per prospect-plant combination.'
}

Table exploration.prospect_processing_route {
  prospect_processing_route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this prospect-circuit routing relationship record. Primary key.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the processing circuit that handles ore from this prospect']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the exploration prospect that is the source of ore being processed']
  average_feed_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average head grade of ore from this prospect when fed to this circuit, expressed as percentage of primary commodity. Tracks prospect-specific feed characteristics.']
  average_recovery decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average metallurgical recovery rate achieved for ore from this prospect when processed through this circuit, expressed as a percentage. Tracks circuit-specific performance by ore source.']
  circuit_assignment_date date [note: 'Date when this prospect ore was first routed to this specific processing circuit. Tracks when the routing relationship was established.']
  circuit_suitability_rating varchar [note: 'Metallurgical assessment of how suitable this circuit is for processing ore from this prospect based on ore characteristics and circuit design. Values: Optimal, Suitable, Marginal, Unsuitable.']
  last_processed_date date [note: 'Date when ore from this prospect was most recently processed through this circuit. Tracks recency of the routing relationship.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing metallurgical observations, processing challenges, or operational considerations specific to processing this prospect ore through this circuit.']
  primary_commodity_target varchar [note: 'Primary commodity being targeted for recovery when processing this prospect ore through this circuit (e.g., copper, gold, iron ore). May differ from prospect primary commodity if circuit is optimized for specific recovery.']
  processing_route varchar [note: 'Description of the processing route or flowsheet path that ore from this prospect follows through this circuit (e.g., \'Oxide Leach Route\', \'Sulphide Flotation Route\', \'Primary Comminution\'). Captures the specific metallurgical pathway.']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this prospect-circuit routing relationship. Values: Active (currently processing), Inactive (temporarily suspended), Planned (future routing), Decommissioned (no longer used).']
  tonnes_processed decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative total tonnes of ore from this prospect that have been processed through this specific circuit. Enables prospect-circuit level throughput tracking.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational routing relationship between exploration prospects and processing circuits. It captures the metallurgical suitability, processing performance, and circuit assignment decisions for ore from specific prospects. Each record links one prospect to one processing circuit with attributes that track circuit-specific performance metrics, processing volumes, and metallurgical characteristics that exist only in the context of this prospect-circuit pairing.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, ore from a single exploration prospect can be routed to multiple processing circuits based on metallurgical characteristics (e.g., oxide ore to leach circuits, sulphide ore to flotation circuits, different grade zones to different processing routes). Conversely, each processing circuit handles ore from multiple prospects as mine plans evolve and different ore sources are blended or sequenced through the plant. This is an operational routing relationship actively managed by metallurgical and processing teams.'
}

Table exploration.deployment {
  deployment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each equipment deployment assignment record. Primary key.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the mining equipment asset deployed to the prospect']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the exploration prospect where the equipment asset is deployed']
  cost_allocation_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center or WBS element code to which equipment operating costs and utilization hours are allocated for this deployment. Used for prospect-level cost tracking and financial reporting.']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the equipment deployment assignment (planned, active, suspended, completed, cancelled). Used for resource planning and deployment tracking.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment asset deployment to the prospect ended and the asset was released or redeployed. Null indicates active deployment. Used for deployment period tracking and cost allocation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes about the equipment deployment including specific activities, performance observations, or deployment constraints.']
  role varchar [note: 'Operational role or function of the equipment asset during this deployment to the prospect (e.g., drilling, trenching, sampling, geophysical survey, site preparation). Used for activity-based cost allocation and resource planning.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment asset was deployed to the prospect and became available for exploration activities. Used for deployment period tracking and cost allocation.']
  utilization_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total operational hours the equipment asset was actively utilized on exploration activities for this prospect during the deployment period. Used for cost allocation, productivity analysis, and equipment performance tracking.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the deployment assignment between exploration prospects and mining equipment assets. It captures the operational deployment of specific equipment to specific prospect areas for exploration activities including drilling, trenching, sampling, and geophysical surveys. Each record links one asset to one prospect for a defined deployment period with role, utilization tracking, and assignment status for cost allocation and resource planning purposes.. Existence Justification: In mining exploration operations, prospects require multiple types of equipment assets over their lifecycle (drill rigs for resource definition, excavators for trenching programs, sampling equipment, geophysical survey equipment), and equipment assets are deployed sequentially or concurrently across multiple prospects within regional exploration programs. The business actively manages these deployments as operational assignments with specific start/end dates, equipment roles, utilization tracking for cost allocation to prospects, and assignment status for resource planning.'
}

Table exploration.prospect_vendor_engagement {
  prospect_vendor_engagement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this prospect-vendor engagement record. Primary key.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor providing exploration services']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the exploration prospect receiving vendor services']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the contract governing this prospect-vendor engagement.']
  contract_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total contracted value for vendor services on this prospect, in the vendor\'s currency. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this engagement record was created.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor completed or ceased providing services to this prospect. Null if engagement is ongoing. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  engagement_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the vendor engagement for this prospect.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of vendor performance specific to this prospect engagement, used for future sourcing decisions. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of exploration service provided by the vendor to this prospect. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor began providing services to this prospect. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this engagement record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual engagement between an exploration prospect and a vendor providing exploration services (drilling, assay, geophysics). It captures the service relationship, contract terms, performance metrics, and financial details that exist only in the context of a specific prospect-vendor combination. Each record links one prospect to one vendor with attributes tracking the nature, duration, value, and quality of the service engagement.. Existence Justification: In mining exploration operations, each prospect requires services from multiple specialized vendors (drilling contractors, assay laboratories, geophysics contractors, geological consultants), and each vendor provides services across multiple prospects for the mining company. The business actively manages these engagements as distinct operational relationships, tracking service type, contract value, performance ratings, and engagement dates for each prospect-vendor combination to inform future sourcing decisions and vendor selection.'
}

Table geology.orebody {
  orebody_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the orebody or mineralised zone within the mining tenement portfolio. Primary key.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Orebodies are defined by primary commodity (iron ore, copper, lithium). Links to standardized commodity master for pricing indices, regulatory classification, JORC reporting, and market analysis. Repl']
  competent_person_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified competent person responsible for the geological characterisation and resource estimation of this orebody, as required by JORC/NI 43-101.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Orebodies are fundamental cost tracking units in mining operations. Each orebody accumulates exploration, development, and production costs through its lifecycle. Essential for AISC reporting, reserve']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Orebodies are profit-generating assets requiring P&L tracking for management reporting and IFRS segment disclosure. Mining companies report financial performance by orebody/mine for investor relations']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or operational location where this orebody is being or will be extracted.']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining tenement or lease within which this orebody is located. Links to land and tenements domain.']
  alteration_type varchar [note: 'Dominant hydrothermal or weathering alteration assemblage associated with mineralisation. Used as a vectoring tool in exploration and grade control.']
  average_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Mean grade of the primary commodity across the entire orebody resource. Key metric for economic evaluation and processing plant design.']
  average_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the average grade. Aligns with commodity type and industry reporting standards.']
  centroid_elevation_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation above sea level of the orebody centroid in meters. Used for mine planning, haulage design, and hydrological modeling.']
  centroid_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the orebody centroid in decimal degrees. Enables spatial analysis and GIS integration.']
  centroid_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the orebody centroid in decimal degrees. Enables spatial analysis and GIS integration.']
  contained_metal_kt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated quantity of contained metal or commodity in the orebody in thousand tonnes. Calculated as tonnage multiplied by average grade.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Spatial reference system or coordinate system used for orebody spatial data (e.g., WGS84, local mine grid). Ensures spatial data interoperability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this orebody record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cutoff_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum economic grade threshold used to define ore versus waste for this orebody. Critical parameter for resource-to-reserve conversion and mine planning.']
  cutoff_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the cut-off grade (e.g., percent for iron ore, grams per tonne for gold). Ensures correct interpretation of grade thresholds.']
  depletion_date date [note: 'Actual or forecast date when economically recoverable reserves in this orebody will be exhausted. Critical for mine closure planning.']
  deposit_style varchar [note: 'Geological classification of the deposit type. Defines the genetic model and controls exploration, mining method selection, and metallurgical processing approach. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: porphyry|vms|bif|sedimentary|epithermal|skarn|iocg|laterite|pegmatite|orogenic|carlin — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  discovery_date date [note: 'Date when the orebody was first discovered or identified through exploration activities. Key milestone for resource maturity tracking.']
  environmental_sensitivity varchar [note: 'Assessment of environmental sensitivity and regulatory constraints affecting this orebody. Influences permitting, mine design, and closure planning.']
  first_production_date date [note: 'Date when commercial ore extraction from this orebody commenced. Marks transition from development to production phase.']
  geological_confidence varchar [note: 'Highest level of geological confidence classification achieved for this orebody per JORC/NI 43-101 standards. Reflects drill density and estimation quality.']
  geological_model_date date [note: 'Date when the current geological model version was finalized and approved. Supports audit trail for resource and reserve reporting.']
  geological_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the current geological block model from which this orebody definition is derived. Tracks model updates and resource estimate revisions.']
  geotechnical_domain varchar [note: 'Geotechnical classification or domain assignment for the orebody based on rock mass characteristics. Informs slope design, ground support, and mining method.']
  host_lithology varchar [note: 'Primary rock type or lithological unit hosting the mineralisation. Influences geotechnical properties, mining method, and processing characteristics.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this orebody record is currently active in operational systems. Inactive records represent historical or superseded orebody definitions.']
  metallurgical_domain varchar [note: 'Metallurgical domain classification based on processing characteristics and recovery performance. Drives processing plant design and recovery assumptions.']
  mineralisation_type varchar [note: 'Style of mineralisation within the orebody, describing how the valuable minerals are distributed within the host rock. Influences mining selectivity and dilution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: disseminated|vein|massive_sulphide|replacement|stockwork|breccia|stratiform|alluvial — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mining_method varchar [note: 'Planned or current mining extraction method for this orebody. Drives mine design, equipment selection, and operating cost assumptions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open_pit|underground|slc|block_cave|longwall|room_and_pillar|open_stope|cut_and_fill|mixed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  orebody_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the orebody used across geological modeling, mine planning, and resource reporting systems. Externally known reference code.']
  orebody_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the orebody or mineralised zone, typically reflecting geographic location, discovery history, or geological characteristics.']
  orebody_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the orebody from exploration through to depletion. Drives investment decisions and operational planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exploration|resource_defined|reserve_declared|in_production|depleted|suspended|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  oxidation_state varchar [note: 'Degree of weathering and oxidation of the orebody. Critical for metallurgical processing route selection (oxide vs sulphide processing).']
  reserve_status varchar [note: 'Highest reserve classification achieved for this orebody. Indicates whether economic extraction has been demonstrated through feasibility studies.']
  spatial_extent_area_sqm decimal(18,2) [note: 'Horizontal surface area extent of the orebody footprint in square meters. Derived from GIS spatial analysis of geological model boundaries.']
  structural_control varchar [note: 'Description of the structural geological features (faults, folds, shear zones) that control the geometry and extent of mineralisation. Informs geological modeling and mine design.']
  tonnage_estimate_mt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total tonnage of mineralised material in the orebody in million tonnes. Represents the scale of the resource and drives LOM planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this orebody record was last modified. Tracks data currency and supports change management processes.']
  vertical_extent_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum vertical thickness or depth extent of the orebody from top to bottom in meters. Influences mining method selection and pit depth or underground level design.']

  Note: 'Master definition of each discrete orebody or mineralised zone within the mining tenement portfolio. Captures geological characterisation including commodity type, deposit style (porphyry, VMS, BIF, sedimentary), structural controls, mineralisation type, spatial extent, and discovery date. Serves as the foundational anchor entity linking all geological modelling, resource estimation, grade control, and mine planning activities. Each orebody record also owns its child geological domains (statistically homogeneous estimation zones). Sourced from Geovia GEMS and Hexagon MinePlan geological models.'
}

Table geology.geological_unit {
  geological_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the geological unit. Primary key for the geological unit reference catalogue.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Geological units with ore-bearing potential reference specific commodities for resource estimation and geological domain definition. Essential for block model estimation and resource categorization. R']
  alteration_type varchar [note: 'Type of hydrothermal or metamorphic alteration affecting the unit (e.g., potassic, phyllic, argillic, propylitic, silicification, chloritization). Nullable for unaltered units. Key indicator for ore body proximity and grade control.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the geological unit definition was formally approved for use in the enterprise geological model and resource estimation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the competent person or senior geologist who approved the geological unit definition. Ensures accountability and compliance with JORC/NI 43-101 competent person requirements.']
  colour_code_hex varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal colour code used for visualisation of the geological unit in cross-sections, block models, and GIS maps. Ensures consistent colour representation across all geological visualisations.']
  colour_code_rgb varchar [note: 'RGB colour code (comma-separated R,G,B values 0-255) for the geological unit. Alternative colour representation for systems that do not support hexadecimal codes.']
  density_g_cm3 decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average bulk density of the geological unit in grams per cubic centimetre. Used for tonnage calculations in resource estimation and block modelling.']
  depositional_environment varchar [note: 'Geological environment in which the unit was originally deposited or formed (e.g., marine, fluvial, volcanic, intrusive, metamorphic). Provides genetic context for the unit.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this geological unit definition became effective in the enterprise geological model. Supports temporal versioning of geological interpretations.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this geological unit definition was effective. Nullable for currently active units. Supports temporal versioning and historical analysis of geological model changes.']
  geological_age varchar [note: 'Geological age or epoch of the unit (e.g., Proterozoic, Archean, Permian, Jurassic). Provides temporal context for the formation of the unit and aids in regional correlation.']
  geological_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the geological unit in the enterprise geological model. Active units are in current use; deprecated units have been superseded by revised geological interpretations.']
  geotechnical_domain varchar [note: 'Geotechnical domain code or name to which this geological unit is assigned. Links geological units to rock mass strength, stability, and slope design parameters.']
  hardness_classification varchar [note: 'Relative hardness classification of the geological unit. Influences drilling rates, blasting design, and comminution energy requirements in mineral processing.']
  lithology_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the rock type, texture, grain size, colour, and primary mineralogy of the geological unit. Used by geologists for consistent logging and interpretation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional geological observations, interpretation notes, or special considerations related to the unit. Captures contextual information not covered by structured fields.']
  ore_bearing_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the geological unit is known to host economic mineralisation. True if the unit is part of the ore body definition; false if it is waste rock or gangue.']
  parent_formation varchar [note: 'Name of the parent geological formation or group to which this unit belongs. Establishes hierarchical stratigraphic relationships (e.g., a member within a formation, a sub-unit within a larger intrusive complex).']
  primary_mineralogy varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of the dominant minerals present in the geological unit (e.g., quartz, feldspar, biotite, magnetite, hematite, chalcopyrite). Critical for understanding ore-forming processes and metallurgical behaviour.']
  processing_domain varchar [note: 'Mineral processing domain or metallurgical type to which this geological unit is assigned (e.g., oxide, transitional, sulphide, high-grade, low-grade). Determines processing route and recovery method.']
  recovery_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical metallurgical recovery rate (percentage of valuable mineral extracted) for ore from this geological unit. Used in resource-to-reserve conversion and economic modelling. Nullable for non-ore units.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which the geological unit definition was extracted (e.g., Geovia GEMS, Hexagon MinePlan, Deswik). Provides data lineage and traceability.']
  structural_setting varchar [note: 'Description of the structural geological context of the unit (e.g., footwall, hanging wall, fault zone, fold limb, shear zone). Critical for understanding ore body geometry and mining method selection.']
  typical_grade_range varchar [note: 'Typical range of metal grades observed in this geological unit (e.g., 0.5-1.2% Cu, 55-62% Fe). Provides context for grade control and cut-off grade decisions. Nullable for non-ore units.']
  unit_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the geological unit across all mine sites. Used consistently in drillhole logging, block model coding, and grade control. Sourced from Geovia GEMS formation tables.']
  unit_name varchar [note: 'Full formal name of the geological unit (e.g., lithological formation, stratigraphic member, intrusive phase, alteration assemblage, weathering horizon). Human-readable identifier used in geological reports and cross-section interpretation.']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the geological unit by its primary geological nature: lithological (rock type), stratigraphic (formation/member), structural (fault/fold zone), alteration (hydrothermal/metamorphic assemblage), weathering (oxidation/supergene horizon), or intrusive (igneous phase).']
  weathering_state varchar [note: 'Degree of weathering or oxidation of the geological unit. Influences geotechnical properties, ore recovery rates, and processing methods (oxide vs. sulphide ore).']

  Note: 'Reference catalogue of all named geological units (lithological formations, stratigraphic members, intrusive phases, alteration assemblages, weathering horizons) recognised across the mine\'s geological model. Defines the formal lithostratigraphic, structural, and alteration classification scheme used consistently in drillhole logging, cross-section interpretation, block model coding, and grade control. Each unit has a unique code, full name, parent formation, geological age, typical mineralogy, and colour code for visualisation. Underpins consistent geological coding enterprise-wide. Sourced from Geovia GEMS formation tables.'
}

Table geology.production_drillhole {
  production_drillhole_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production drillhole record. Primary key for the production drillhole master register.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining project or tenement under which this drillhole was executed.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Grade control drilling is a direct mining operational cost allocated to cost centres for unit cost calculation and AISC reporting. Mining operations track drilling costs per cost centre for budget var']
  drill_hole_id varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the drillhole, typically a site-specific alphanumeric code used in geological logs, assay reports, and JORC disclosures. This is the externally-known unique code for the hole.']
  drill_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the exploration or grade control drill program under which this hole was planned and executed.']
  drill_rig_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Production drillholes must reference the specific drill rig asset for equipment utilization tracking, maintenance correlation with hole quality metrics, and operational planning. Replaces unlinked rig']
  geologist_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Grade control and production drilling require tracking which geologist logged the hole for accountability, QAQC audits, competency verification, and JORC compliance. Real-world mining operations link ']
  laboratory_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory_sample. Business justification: Production drillholes generate samples sent to laboratory for grade control assay. Links drilling location and depth to laboratory analysis for spatial grade distribution and mining reconciliation. Es']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Production drillholes target specific orebodies for grade control and resource definition. Currently production_drillhole has site_id but no orebody_id. Adding orebody_id FK enables direct linkage of ']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site where the drillhole is located.']
  azimuth decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned azimuth (bearing) of the drillhole at collar, measured in degrees from 0 to 360, where 0/360 is north, 90 is east, 180 is south, and 270 is west. Used to define hole trajectory.']
  collar_easting decimal(12,3) [note: 'Easting coordinate of the drillhole collar (surface location) in the site coordinate reference system, typically in meters. Used for spatial positioning in GIS and geological modeling systems.']
  collar_northing decimal(12,3) [note: 'Northing coordinate of the drillhole collar (surface location) in the site coordinate reference system, typically in meters. Used for spatial positioning in GIS and geological modeling systems.']
  collar_rl decimal(10,3) [note: 'Reduced Level (elevation) of the drillhole collar relative to site datum, typically in meters above mean sea level. Critical for accurate 3D geological modeling and resource estimation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording operational notes, drilling issues, geological observations, or other relevant information about the drillhole. Used for quality control and historical reference.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when drilling operations were completed or abandoned. Used for program scheduling, performance tracking, and cost allocation.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Coordinate reference system (CRS) or projection used for collar coordinates. Examples: MGA94 Zone 50, WGS84 UTM Zone 51S, local mine grid. Critical for spatial data integration.']
  core_diameter_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Diameter of the drill core or bit size in millimeters. Common sizes include HQ (63.5mm), NQ (47.6mm), PQ (85mm) for diamond drilling, or bit diameter for RC/RAB drilling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this drillhole record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  datum varchar [note: 'Vertical datum used for collar RL (elevation). Examples: AHD (Australian Height Datum), MSL (Mean Sea Level), site-specific datum. Critical for accurate 3D modeling.']
  dip decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned dip (inclination) of the drillhole at collar, measured in degrees from -90 (vertically down) to +90 (vertically up). Negative values indicate downward drilling. Used to define hole trajectory.']
  driller_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead driller or drilling supervisor responsible for executing the drillhole. Used for quality control and performance tracking.']
  drilling_contractor varchar [note: 'Name of the drilling contractor company that executed the drillhole. Used for quality control, performance tracking, and contractor management.']
  hole_purpose varchar [note: 'Primary purpose of the drillhole: Exploration (greenfield discovery), Resource Definition (resource estimation), Grade Control (short-term mine planning), Geotechnical (rock mechanics), Hydrogeological (water management), or Condemnation (infrastructure siting).']
