  estimation_method varchar [note: 'Statistical or geostatistical method used to estimate the grade of this block from drill hole or blast hole assay data. Common methods include Ordinary Kriging, Inverse Distance Weighting, and Nearest Neighbour.']
  insitu_tonnes decimal(14,3) [note: 'Estimated in-situ dry metric tonnes of material within the grade control block, calculated from block volume and bulk density. Represents the raw tonnage before mining dilution or recovery adjustments.']
  is_resampled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this grade control block has been subject to a re-assay or resampling event due to initial classification uncertainty or grade variability. True = block was resampled; False = original assay used. Supports audit trail for classification decisions.']
  mined_out_date date [note: 'Date on which the grade control block was fully extracted and the block status transitioned to \'mined_out\'. Used for production reconciliation, LOM tracking, and mine closure planning.']
  mineral_domain_code varchar [note: 'Geological domain or mineralisation zone code to which this block belongs, as defined in the resource model. Used for grade estimation and variogram modelling within homogeneous geological units.']
  primary_grade decimal(10,6) [note: 'Assay-derived grade of the primary commodity (e.g., Fe%, Cu%, Li ppm) for the grade control block. The principal quantitative fact driving ore/waste classification and dispatch decisions. Units depend on commodity (% for Fe/Cu, ppm for Li/Au).']
  primary_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the primary commodity grade value (e.g., \'pct\' for percentage, \'ppm\' for parts per million, \'g_per_t\' for grams per tonne). Ensures correct interpretation of the primary_grade field across different commodities.']
  recovery_rate_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Estimated percentage of the primary commodity that will be recovered during mineral processing of this block\'s material. Used for contained metal calculations and production forecasting.']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Number of drill hole or blast hole assay samples used in the grade estimation for this block. Higher sample counts generally indicate greater confidence in the grade estimate.']
  secondary_grade decimal(10,6) [note: 'Assay-derived grade of a secondary or by-product commodity (e.g., SiO2%, Al2O3%, S%) for the grade control block. Used for penalty/bonus calculations, blending decisions, and metallurgical planning.']
  secondary_grade_element varchar [note: 'Chemical element or compound symbol for the secondary grade value (e.g., \'SiO2\', \'Al2O3\', \'S\', \'P\'). Identifies which element the secondary_grade field represents for this block.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this grade control block record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data lineage, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Grade control block record representing a short-range ore classification unit used to direct ore and waste to appropriate destinations. Captures block ID, location, ore type, cut-off grade classification (ore/waste/low-grade), assay results, confidence level, and dispatch instruction. Sourced from LabWare LIMS and Geovia GEMS. The operational SSOT for ore/waste discrimination at the face.'
}

Table mine.shift_report {
  shift_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the shift report record. Primary key for the shift_report data product in the mine domain Silver layer.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Shift-level costs are allocated to cost centres for daily cost tracking, reconciliation against budget, and operational cost reporting. Standard shift-to-finance integration for mining operations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shift supervisor or leading hand responsible for the crew during this shift. Used for accountability, handover management, and workforce reporting. Maps to the workforce master record.']
  grievance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.grievance. Business justification: Shift reports document community complaints received during operations (noise, dust, traffic, safety concerns). Supervisors log grievances in shift handovers for follow-up. Real business process: oper']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Shift reports document incidents that occurred during the shift for handover and management reporting. Standard mining operational practice to reference incidents in shift documentation.']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.lom_plan. Business justification: Shift reports track progress against LOM plan. N:1 relationship (many shifts in one LOM plan). No bidirectional conflict. Valid planning link.']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'FK to mine.mine_site']
  opex_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.opex_budget. Business justification: Shift actuals are reconciled against opex budgets for daily cost control, forecast accuracy, and operational budget tracking. Real-time budget variance monitoring for mining operations.']
  pit_or_level_id varchar [note: 'FK to mine.pit_or_level']
  primary_commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Shift reports track which commodity was primarily mined during the shift. Required for daily production reporting, reconciliation against plan, and operational performance tracking. New attribute need']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine production schedule record that defines the targets for this shift. Links actuals to the scheduled plan for variance analysis and LOM planning reconciliation.']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Shift reports document production from specific prospects. Daily reporting attributes tonnes and grade to source prospect for production accounting, resource depletion tracking, and operational perfor']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.shift_schedule. Business justification: Shift reports capture actual production outcomes for a scheduled workforce shift. Linking to shift_schedule enables variance analysis between planned workforce deployment and actual production deliver']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Daily shift reports track production by tenement for expenditure commitment substantiation, quarterly regulatory reporting, and operational performance analysis by license area. Required for annual co']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.warehouse_location. Business justification: Shift reports track consumables usage from site warehouses (explosives, fuel, ground support). Business need: shift-level consumables reconciliation, warehouse demand forecasting, inventory replenishm']
  blasts_fired_count varchar [note: 'Number of blast events executed during the shift. Used for drill-and-blast productivity tracking, explosive consumption reconciliation, and regulatory blast notification compliance.']
  breakdown_delay_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total hours of production lost during the shift due to unplanned equipment breakdowns and mechanical failures. Used for Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) and Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) analysis and maintenance planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shift report record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  crew_headcount varchar [note: 'Total number of personnel (operators, technicians, supervisors) who worked during the shift. Used for workforce productivity analysis, labour cost allocation, and safety compliance (persons on site).']
  dilution_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of waste material mixed with ore during mining operations for the shift, expressed as a decimal. Dilution control is a critical operational metric impacting ore quality and processing recovery rates.']
  equipment_operating_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total aggregate operating hours across all production equipment (trucks, excavators, drills) during the shift. Sourced from Caterpillar MineStar FMS engine hours. Used for OEE calculation and maintenance scheduling.']
  equipment_scheduled_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total aggregate scheduled operating hours for all production equipment during the shift as per the mine production schedule. Used as the denominator for equipment utilisation rate calculation.']
  equipment_utilisation_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Actual equipment utilisation rate for the shift expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.8500 = 85%). Calculated as equipment operating hours divided by equipment scheduled hours. Key input to Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) reporting.']
  handover_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text handover communication from the outgoing shift supervisor to the incoming shift supervisor. Captures outstanding tasks, equipment status, safety hazards, and operational priorities for continuity of operations.']
  is_daily_rollup_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift report has been included in the daily production rollup for management reporting. True when the shift data has been aggregated into the daily summary; False when pending rollup.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shift report record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  lost_time_injury_count varchar [note: 'Number of Lost Time Injuries (LTIs) recorded during the shift — injuries resulting in at least one full shift of lost work time. Key input to Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate (LTIFR) regulatory reporting.']
  metres_developed decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total metres of underground development advance (drives, declines, crosscuts) completed during the shift. Applicable to underground mining operations. Used for development schedule tracking.']
  metres_drilled decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total metres of drilling completed during the shift across all drill rigs, including blast hole drilling and development drilling. Used for drill-and-blast performance tracking and production scheduling.']
  metres_drilled_target decimal(12,2) [note: 'Scheduled target metres of drilling for the shift as per the mine production schedule. Used for drill performance variance analysis.']
  mining_method varchar [note: 'The primary mining extraction method employed during this shift. Distinguishes open-cut from underground methods and specific underground techniques such as Sub-Level Caving (SLC), longwall, or cut-and-fill. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open_cut|underground_sublevel_caving|underground_longwall|underground_room_and_pillar|underground_cut_and_fill|underground_block_caving|surface_strip — promote to reference product]']
  ore_grade_fe_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Average head grade of iron (Fe) content in ore mined during the shift, expressed as a decimal percentage. Sourced from assay results and grade control data. Used for production reconciliation and mineral resource reporting.']
  ore_mined_target_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Scheduled target ore tonnes to be mined during the shift as per the mine production schedule. Used for variance analysis and production reconciliation against actuals.']
  ore_mined_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual tonnes of ore material mined and dispatched to the Run of Mine (ROM) pad or stockpile during the shift. Core production KPI. Sourced from Caterpillar MineStar FMS truck payload data and OSIsoft PI belt weigher integration.']
  planned_maintenance_delay_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total hours of production lost during the shift due to scheduled preventive maintenance activities. Distinguishes planned downtime from unplanned breakdowns for OEE and availability reporting.']
  safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Total number of safety incidents (including near misses, first aid, medical treatment, and lost time injuries) recorded during the shift. Used for LTIFR and TRIFR calculation and regulatory HSE reporting.']
  shift_date date [note: 'The calendar date to which this shift report is attributed for daily rollup and management reporting purposes. Aligns with the mine site operational day boundary (which may differ from midnight). Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  shift_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the shift concluded at the mine site. Used in conjunction with shift_start_timestamp to calculate shift duration and for production period boundary alignment.']
  shift_report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the shift report, typically formatted as SR-YYYY-MM-DD-SHF-NNN (e.g., SR-2024-07-15-DAY-001). Used for cross-system reference, regulatory production returns, and management reporting.']
  shift_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the shift commenced at the mine site. Principal business event timestamp for the shift. Used for production reconciliation, KPI dashboards, and regulatory production returns. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  shift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shift report record. Draft indicates data entry in progress; submitted indicates supervisor sign-off; approved indicates management review complete; closed indicates period locked; voided indicates record cancelled.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift period within the 24-hour operational cycle. Typical values: day (e.g., 06:00–18:00), night (e.g., 18:00–06:00), afternoon, or extended for non-standard shift patterns.']
  significant_events_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative capturing significant events during the shift including major breakdowns, safety incidents, geotechnical events, blast misfires, environmental exceedances, or other notable occurrences. Critical for shift handover and management reporting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this shift report data was sourced. Values: MINESTAR (Caterpillar MineStar FMS), OSI_PI (OSIsoft PI System), CREW_REPORT (crew reporting system), MANUAL (manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  strip_ratio_actual decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual strip ratio for the shift, calculated as waste tonnes moved divided by ore tonnes mined (overburden to ore ratio). A key economic indicator for open-cut mining operations used in OPEX and LOM planning.']
  waste_moved_target_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Scheduled target waste tonnes to be moved during the shift as per the mine production schedule. Used for strip ratio variance analysis and LOM planning reconciliation.']
  waste_moved_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual tonnes of waste rock (overburden and interburden) moved to waste dumps during the shift. Used to calculate the strip ratio and track overburden removal progress against the Life of Mine (LOM) plan.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather condition at the mine site during the shift. Used to contextualise production performance, explain delays, and support environmental and safety compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|cloudy|rain|heavy_rain|storm|fog|dust_storm|snow|extreme_heat|hail — promote to reference product]']
  weather_delay_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total hours of production lost during the shift due to adverse weather conditions (e.g., lightning stand-down, heavy rain, dust storm). Used for delay analysis and production variance reporting.']

  Note: 'Shift-level production performance record and the consolidated SSOT for all mine production actuals. Captures actual ore tonnes mined, waste tonnes moved, metres advanced (drill and development), blasts fired, equipment operating hours and utilisation rates versus scheduled targets, crew supervisor, significant events (breakdowns, safety incidents, weather delays), and handover notes. Includes daily rollup capability for management reporting. Sourced from Caterpillar MineStar FMS, OSIsoft PI, and crew reporting systems. Feeds production reconciliation, KPI dashboards, regulatory production returns, and management reporting.'
}

Table mine.waste_dump {
  waste_dump_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the waste rock dump facility record. Primary key for the waste_dump master data product.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Waste dumps are managed under cost centres for waste handling cost tracking, rehabilitation provision calculation, and environmental cost management. Core environmental accounting link.']
  environmental_monitoring_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_monitoring. Business justification: Waste dumps monitored for groundwater quality, geotechnical stability, and acid rock drainage. Regulatory requirement to link dump facility to monitoring program for compliance reporting.']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_permit. Business justification: Waste dumps require specific environmental permits for construction and operation covering geotechnical stability, water management, and rehabilitation. Regulatory requirement in all mining jurisdicti']
  hse_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.audit. Business justification: Waste dumps subject to periodic environmental and geotechnical audits per regulatory requirements. Links dump facility to audit findings for compliance tracking and corrective action management.']
  land_compensation_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.land_compensation_agreement. Business justification: Waste dump footprints often occupy compensated land parcels. Agreement terms govern placement, access, rehabilitation obligations, and ongoing payments. Real business process: land use compliance, com']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.lom_plan. Business justification: Waste dumps are planned as part of LOM plan. N:1 relationship (many dumps in one LOM plan). No bidirectional conflict. Valid planning link.']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'FK to mine.mine_site']
  pit_design_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the open-cut pit or underground mining area that primarily generates the waste material deposited in this dump. Supports strip ratio and waste tracking by source area.']
  rehabilitation_provision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.rehabilitation_provision. Business justification: Waste dumps trigger rehabilitation provisions for closure cost estimation, balance sheet liability management, and environmental accounting. Core environmental accounting link for mining closure oblig']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Waste dumps must be located within authorized tenement boundaries or have specific surface rights agreements. Critical for environmental compliance, Programme of Work approvals, and land use authoriza']
  acid_rock_drainage_risk varchar [note: 'Classification of the waste dump\'s potential to generate Acid Rock Drainage (ARD) based on net acid generation (NAG) testing and acid-base accounting (ABA) of the waste material. Drives encapsulation design, water management, and long-term monitoring requirements.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which waste emplacement operations formally ceased and the dump was closed to further filling. Null if the dump is still active. Used for LOM planning and rehabilitation scheduling.']
  commissioned_date date [note: 'Date on which the waste dump facility was formally commissioned and first received waste material. Marks the start of the operational lifecycle for compliance and LOM tracking purposes.']
  current_fill_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Cumulative mass of waste material currently emplaced in the dump expressed in dry metric tonnes. Derived from FMS truck payload data and survey reconciliation. Used for mass-balance and OPEX reporting.']
  current_fill_volume_bcm decimal(18,3) [note: 'Cumulative volume of waste material currently emplaced in the dump expressed in Bank Cubic Metres (BCM). Updated from survey data and FMS haulage records. Used to calculate remaining capacity and track fill progression against LOM schedule.']
  design_capacity_bcm decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total approved design capacity of the waste dump expressed in Bank Cubic Metres (BCM). Represents the maximum volume of in-situ material the facility is permitted and engineered to receive. Sourced from mine planning systems (Hexagon MinePlan, Deswik).']
  design_capacity_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total approved design capacity of the waste dump expressed in dry metric tonnes. Complements BCM capacity for mass-balance reconciliation and LOM planning. Sourced from mine planning systems.']
  dump_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the waste dump facility, used in mine planning systems (Hexagon MinePlan, Deswik) and regulatory submissions. Unique across the mine site.']
  dump_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the waste dump facility (e.g., \'North Waste Dump 1\', \'Southern Overburden Emplacement\'). Used in operational communications, mine plans, and regulatory reports.']
  dump_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the waste dump facility. Active indicates ongoing waste emplacement; closed indicates emplacement has ceased; rehabilitating indicates progressive or final rehabilitation is underway per the mine closure plan.']
  dump_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the waste dump by material type deposited. Overburden refers to material above the ore body; waste rock is non-economic mineralised material; low-grade stockpile holds sub-cut-off-grade ore for potential future processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: overburden|waste_rock|low_grade_stockpile|mixed|tailings_co_disposal — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  elevation_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Elevation of the waste dump crest above mean sea level in metres. Critical for geotechnical stability assessment, drainage design, and regulatory height limit compliance.']
  geotechnical_classification varchar [note: 'Geotechnical risk classification of the waste dump based on foundation conditions, material properties, and consequence of failure. Class 1 (lowest risk) to Class 4 (highest risk). Drives inspection frequency, monitoring requirements, and regulatory reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: class_1|class_2|class_3|class_4 — promote to reference product]']
  groundwater_monitoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ongoing groundwater quality monitoring is required for this waste dump as a condition of the environmental permit or based on ARD risk classification. True triggers monitoring schedule in IsoMetrix.']
  isometrix_facility_reference varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this waste dump facility as recorded in IsoMetrix HSE and Environmental Monitoring module. Used for data lineage and cross-system reconciliation.']
  last_survey_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent topographic survey conducted on the waste dump to update volume and surface area measurements. Survey data is used to reconcile FMS haulage records with actual fill volumes.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the waste dump centroid or reference point in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for GIS mapping, environmental monitoring zone delineation, and regulatory spatial reporting.']
  leachate_management_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the leachate collection and management system for the waste dump. Indicates whether leachate is being actively collected, passively monitored, or not required based on liner type and regulatory conditions.']
  liner_type varchar [note: 'Type of engineered liner system installed at the base of the waste dump to control leachate migration and protect groundwater. None indicates an unlined facility; HDPE geomembrane is a high-density polyethylene synthetic liner. Sourced from mine design records and IsoMetrix.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the waste dump centroid or reference point in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for GIS mapping, environmental monitoring zone delineation, and regulatory spatial reporting.']
  max_height_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted or current height of the waste dump from base to crest in metres. A key geotechnical design parameter governing slope stability and regulatory permit conditions.']
  mine_plan_dump_reference varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this waste dump as recorded in the mine planning system (Hexagon MinePlan or Deswik). Used for data lineage, cross-system reconciliation, and LOM schedule integration.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled geotechnical or environmental inspection of the waste dump as required by the geotechnical classification, permit conditions, or IsoMetrix inspection schedule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, geotechnical observations, or special conditions applicable to this waste dump that are not captured in structured fields. Used by mine planning and environmental teams.']
  overall_slope_angle_deg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Designed overall slope angle of the waste dump in degrees from horizontal. A critical geotechnical stability parameter reviewed against factor-of-safety thresholds in the geotechnical design report.']
  permit_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the environmental permit or licence for this waste dump expires and must be renewed. Triggers compliance workflow in IsoMetrix for permit renewal actions.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory permit or licence number issued by the relevant environmental authority authorising the construction and operation of this waste dump. Required for compliance tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  progressive_rehab_area_ha decimal(12,4) [note: 'Cumulative area of the waste dump that has been progressively rehabilitated to date, expressed in hectares. Used to track rehabilitation progress against permit commitments and LOM closure plan milestones.']
  progressive_rehab_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether progressive rehabilitation (rehabilitation of completed dump benches during the operational phase) is required as a permit condition or company commitment. True drives progressive rehab scheduling in the LOM plan.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this waste dump master record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this waste dump master record was most recently updated in the data platform. Used for change detection, incremental processing, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  rehabilitation_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial assurance (bond) amount in the site\'s functional currency held by the relevant regulatory authority to cover the estimated cost of rehabilitating this waste dump to an approved post-mining land use standard. Commercially sensitive.']
  rehabilitation_bond_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the rehabilitation bond amount (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Required for multi-jurisdiction financial reporting under IFRS.']
  rehabilitation_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or identifier for the approved mine rehabilitation and closure plan applicable to this waste dump. Links to the formal closure plan document registered in the document management system. Required for regulatory compliance and bond calculation.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the qualified geotechnical engineer responsible for the design, monitoring, and sign-off of this waste dump facility. Required for regulatory accountability and competent person declarations.']
  surface_area_ha decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total surface footprint of the waste dump facility in hectares, including all active and inactive benches. Used for rehabilitation cost estimation, environmental bond calculation, and land tenure management.']
  waste_rock_classification varchar [note: 'Geochemical classification of the predominant waste rock material deposited in the dump based on laboratory acid-base accounting. Non-Acid Forming (NAF), Potentially Acid Forming (PAF), or Acid Forming (AF). Determines encapsulation and placement strategy.']

  Note: 'Master record of waste rock dump facilities including dump name, location coordinates, design capacity (tonnes/BCM), current fill volume, geotechnical classification, liner type, leachate management status, and rehabilitation plan reference. Sourced from mine planning systems and IsoMetrix. The SSOT for waste dump inventory and geotechnical compliance.'
}

Table mine.survey_measurement {
  survey_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each mine survey measurement record. Primary key for the survey_measurement data product.']
  bench_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.bench. Business justification: survey_measurement has bench_level (STRING) but no FK to bench table. Adding bench_id FK normalizes bench reference and removes redundant bench_level string column. The bench table contains bench_code']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine area, pit, bench, or underground zone where the survey was conducted. Links to the mine area master data product for spatial context.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific geographic location or spatial feature (stockpile, dump, TSF cell, stope) being surveyed, as defined in the mine\'s GIS (Geographic Information System) location master.']
  mining_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mining_block. Business justification: Surveys can measure individual mining blocks. N:1 relationship (many surveys of one block). No bidirectional conflict. Valid operational link.']
  pit_design_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.pit_design. Business justification: Surveys measure actual pit progress against design. N:1 relationship (many surveys comparing to one design). No bidirectional conflict. Valid operational link.']
  pit_or_level_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.pit_or_level. Business justification: Survey measurements are conducted on pits or underground levels. Adding pit_or_level_id FK provides location context for survey records. No redundant columns to remove.']
  previous_survey_survey_measurement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding survey measurement record for the same location, enabling period-over-period volumetric change calculation and production reconciliation.']
  primary_survey_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed mine surveyor or survey crew responsible for conducting the field survey. Links to the workforce/personnel master data product.']
  rom_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.rom_stockpile. Business justification: Surveys measure ROM stockpile volumes. N:1 relationship (many surveys of one stockpile over time). No bidirectional conflict. Valid operational link.']
  stope_design_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.stope_design. Business justification: For underground surveys, linking to stope_design provides access to design parameters for comparison with surveyed actuals. This is essential for underground survey reconciliation. No redundant column']
  survey_equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Mine surveys (stockpile volumes, pit progression, waste dump monitoring) are performed using specific survey equipment (total stations, drones, GPS rovers). Tracking which asset performed each survey ']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Survey measurements of mined volumes must be attributed to tenements for production reconciliation, royalty calculations, and regulatory reporting. Essential for quarterly production returns and reser']
  waste_dump_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.waste_dump. Business justification: Surveys measure waste dump volumes. N:1 relationship (many surveys of one dump over time). No bidirectional conflict. Valid operational link.']
  wireframe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.wireframe. Business justification: Survey measurements validate as-built geometry against design wireframes. Required for reconciliation, identifying deviations from design, and updating wireframes based on actual mining progress.']
  accuracy_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the positional accuracy standard achieved by the survey method. Survey-grade (±0.05m) is required for production reconciliation; engineering-grade (±0.1–0.5m) for planning; reconnaissance for preliminary assessments.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the survey measurement record was formally approved by the authorised mine surveyor. Required for regulatory compliance and production reporting sign-off.']
  bulk_density decimal(10,4) [note: 'Bulk density factor in tonnes per cubic metre (t/m³) applied to convert surveyed volume to estimated tonnes. Value may vary by material type (ore, waste, ROM, concentrate) and moisture content.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'The geodetic coordinate reference system (CRS) used for the survey, expressed as an EPSG code or named datum (e.g., GDA2020 / MGA Zone 54, WGS84 UTM). Ensures spatial data interoperability with GIS and Hexagon MinePlan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this survey measurement record was first created in the system, including timezone offset. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  design_volume_m3 decimal(18,4) [note: 'The planned or design volume in cubic metres (m³) for the surveyed feature as per the mine design model in Hexagon MinePlan or Deswik. Used to calculate variance to design for production scheduling compliance.']
  dtm_file_ref varchar [note: 'File path or object storage URI referencing the Digital Terrain Model (DTM) or Digital Surface Model (DSM) derived from the survey, used as the basis for volume calculations in Hexagon MinePlan.']
  elevation_max_m decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum elevation in metres above Reduced Level (mRL) datum recorded within the survey extent. Used for crest height validation, dump height compliance, and TSF (Tailings Storage Facility) freeboard monitoring.']
  elevation_min_m decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum elevation in metres above Reduced Level (mRL) datum recorded within the survey extent. Used for bench floor validation, geotechnical monitoring, and pit design compliance.']
  geotechnical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this survey measurement was triggered or flagged for geotechnical monitoring purposes (e.g., slope stability monitoring, TSF embankment deformation, underground convergence). When true, the record is shared with the geotechnical team for risk assessment.']
  horizontal_accuracy_m decimal(8,4) [note: 'Estimated horizontal positional accuracy of the survey in metres (m), representing the root mean square error (RMSE) of check point comparisons. Governs fitness-for-purpose for production reconciliation and geotechnical monitoring.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary material type present in the surveyed feature. Distinguishes ore, waste rock, ROM (Run of Mine) material, overburden, tailings, or concentrate stockpiles for production accounting and reconciliation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the surveyor to record observations, anomalies, data quality caveats, or contextual information about the survey event (e.g., partial coverage due to access restrictions, equipment issues, or safety exclusion zones).']
  point_cloud_file_ref varchar [note: 'File path or object storage URI referencing the raw point cloud dataset (LAS/LAZ format) or photogrammetric model generated by the survey. Enables traceability back to source survey data in the data lake.']
  production_period varchar [note: 'The production reporting period (e.g., 2024-Q3, 2024-W42) to which this survey measurement is attributed for production reconciliation and LOM (Life of Mine) schedule tracking.']
  strip_ratio decimal(10,4) [note: 'The ratio of waste/overburden volume to ore volume derived from the survey measurement for the period, expressed as bcm waste per tonne of ore. A key open-cut mining efficiency metric used in LOM planning and OPEX (Operating Expenditure) tracking.']
  survey_area_m2 decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total horizontal surface area in square metres (m²) covered by the survey extent polygon. Used to assess survey coverage completeness and calculate average depth or height of material.']
  survey_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the physical survey was conducted in the field. This is the principal real-world event date used for production reconciliation and scheduling alignment.']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'The geospatial data capture technique used to conduct the survey. Common methods include drone photogrammetry, LiDAR (Light Detection and Ranging), total station, GPS RTK (Real-Time Kinematic), mobile mapping system (MMS), and terrestrial laser scanning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drone_photogrammetry|lidar|total_station|gps_rtk|mms|laser_scan|handheld_gps|aerial_photogrammetry — promote to reference product]']
  survey_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to the survey event, used for cross-referencing with Hexagon MinePlan survey records, field reports, and production reconciliation documents.']
  survey_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle status of the survey measurement record, from initial field capture through review and approval to archival.']
  survey_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the survey capture commenced, including timezone offset. Used for SCADA integration and time-series alignment with OSIsoft PI System historian data.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the survey target area or structure being measured. Covers open-cut pit floors, waste dumps, ROM (Run of Mine) stockpiles, underground workings, Tailings Storage Facilities (TSF), and haul roads. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pit_floor|dump|stockpile|underground|tsf|haul_road|portal|decline|berm — promote to reference product]']
  surveyed_tonnes decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated mass in dry metric tonnes (dmt) of material represented by the surveyed volume, derived by applying the appropriate bulk density factor. Used for production reconciliation against FMS (Fleet Management System) truck counts.']
  surveyed_volume_m3 decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total volumetric measurement of the surveyed feature (pit void, stockpile, dump, or underground void) in cubic metres (m³), as calculated from the survey point cloud or digital terrain model (DTM) in Hexagon MinePlan.']
  tonnes_change decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net change in estimated dry metric tonnes between the current and previous survey for the same location. Used as the primary production reconciliation metric against FMS-reported movement tonnes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this survey measurement record was last modified, including timezone offset. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance.']
  variance_to_design_m3 decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference in cubic metres (m³) between the surveyed volume and the design volume (surveyed minus design). Negative values indicate under-excavation; positive values indicate over-excavation or excess material. Critical for LOM (Life of Mine) plan compliance.']
  variance_to_design_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage variance between the surveyed volume and the design volume, expressed as (surveyed - design) / design × 100. Enables normalised comparison across survey areas of different sizes for production scheduling KPI reporting.']
  vertical_accuracy_m decimal(8,4) [note: 'Estimated vertical positional accuracy of the survey in metres (m), representing the RMSE of elevation check point comparisons. Critical for volumetric calculation reliability and bench floor compliance.']
  volume_change_m3 decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net volumetric change in cubic metres (m³) between the current survey and the previous survey for the same location. Positive values indicate material addition (e.g., stockpile build); negative values indicate material removal (e.g., pit excavation, reclaim).']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather conditions at the time of the survey, which may affect data quality for drone photogrammetry and LiDAR methods. Used for data quality qualification and survey validity assessment.']

  Note: 'Mine survey measurement record capturing periodic topographic and volumetric surveys of pits, dumps, stockpiles, and underground workings. Includes survey date, method (drone, LiDAR, total station), surveyed volume/tonnes, comparison to previous survey, and variance to design. Sourced from survey systems and Hexagon MinePlan. Enables production reconciliation and geotechnical monitoring.'
}

Table mine.production_reconciliation {
  production_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each production reconciliation record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  block_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.block_model. Business justification: Reconciliation validates specific block model versions against actual mining results. Critical for model versioning, competent person reviews, and determining when model updates are required.']
  geological_domain_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.geological_domain. Business justification: Reconciliation is performed by geological domain to identify model biases. Required for domain-specific model updates, understanding estimation performance, and refining variogram parameters.']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.lom_plan. Business justification: Reconciliation compares actuals to LOM plan predictions. N:1 relationship (many reconciliations reference one LOM plan). No bidirectional conflict. Valid planning link.']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mine_site. Business justification: production_reconciliation has mine_site_code (STRING) but no FK to mine_site table. Adding mine_site_id FK normalizes site reference and removes redundant code column. The mine_site table contains sit']
  mining_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mining_block. Business justification: Reconciliation is performed at mining block level. N:1 relationship (many reconciliation records per block over time). No bidirectional conflict. Valid operational link.']
  ore_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.ore_reserve. Business justification: Reconciliation tracks reserve depletion. Reserve reconciliation is a regulatory requirement comparing mined tonnes against declared reserves for stock exchange reporting, reserve replacement tracking,']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Reconciliation compares model vs actual performance by orebody. Required for resource model updates, reserve confidence assessment, and identifying systematic geological estimation biases.']
  pit_design_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.pit_design. Business justification: Reconciliation can compare actuals to pit design. N:1 relationship (many reconciliations for one pit design). No bidirectional conflict. Valid planning link.']
  primary_production_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the qualified geologist responsible for preparing and validating the reconciliation record. The responsible geologist is a Competent Person under JORC or Qualified Person under NI 43-101 who certifies the geological model inputs.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Reconciliation compares actuals to a specific production schedule. N:1 relationship (many reconciliations can reference one schedule period). No bidirectional conflict. Valid operational link.']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Reconciliation compares actual production against exploration resource estimates at prospect level. Monthly/quarterly reconciliation is a regulatory requirement linking mined tonnes back to the origin']
  resource_estimate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.resource_estimate. Business justification: Reconciliation compares actual results against resource estimates. F1/F2/F3 factor calculation requires linking mined blocks to the source resource estimate for model updating, resource classification']
  resource_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.resource_statement. Business justification: Reconciliation validates resource statement accuracy over time. Required for material change assessments, determining when resource restatements are needed, and maintaining competent person confidence']
  stope_design_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.stope_design. Business justification: For underground mining reconciliation, linking to stope_design provides access to design parameters for comparison with actuals. This is essential for underground production reconciliation. No redunda']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Production reconciliation must track planned vs actual by tenement for reserve reporting updates, royalty calculation verification, and expenditure commitment substantiation. Required for annual reser']
  actual_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual head grade (percentage or grams per tonne) of the primary commodity as determined by assay results from LabWare LIMS for the reconciliation period. Used as the numerator in F2 (grade reconciliation factor) calculation.']
  actual_metal_content decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual contained metal or mineral content (tonnes of metal) for the reconciliation period, derived from actual_tonnes and actual_grade assay data from LabWare LIMS. Used as the numerator in F3 (metal content reconciliation factor) calculation.']
  actual_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual ore tonnes mined and delivered to the ROM pad or processing plant during the reconciliation period, as measured by Caterpillar MineStar FMS payload data and survey. Used as the numerator in F1 (tonnes reconciliation factor) calculation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reconciliation record received final sign-off approval from the authorised approver. Null if reconciliation_status is not yet approved. Provides audit trail for JORC compliance and regulatory reporting.']
  assay_sample_count varchar [note: 'Number of assay samples collected and analysed in LabWare LIMS to determine the actual_grade for this reconciliation period. Higher sample counts increase grade confidence. Used to assess statistical reliability of the F2 grade reconciliation factor.']
  bench_elevation_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation in metres above sea level (mRL) of the mining bench or stope level from which the reconciled ore was extracted. Used to spatially locate the reconciliation within the mine design and correlate with the geological block model in Geovia GEMS.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Standard code for the primary commodity being reconciled (e.g., FE for iron ore, CU for copper, LI for lithium, NI for nickel, COAL). Used to align reconciliation data with sales, trading, and JORC resource reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production reconciliation record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for data lineage and compliance with JORC documentation requirements.']
  dilution_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Estimated percentage of waste rock (gangue) mixed with ore during the mining process, contributing to grade reduction relative to the geological model. Calculated from blast movement monitoring and survey data. High dilution is a primary driver of negative F2 factors.']
  f1_factor decimal(8,4) [note: 'F1 reconciliation factor representing the ratio of actual mined tonnes to geological model tonnes (actual_tonnes / model_tonnes). A value of 1.0 indicates perfect tonnes reconciliation. Values significantly above or below 1.0 indicate geological model uncertainty or mining dilution issues. Critical JORC resource model confidence metric.']
  f2_factor decimal(8,4) [note: 'F2 reconciliation factor representing the ratio of actual mined grade to geological model grade (actual_grade / model_grade). A value of 1.0 indicates perfect grade reconciliation. Deviations indicate geological estimation error, sampling bias, or dilution effects. Critical for resource model confidence and JORC compliance.']
  f3_factor decimal(8,4) [note: 'F3 reconciliation factor representing the ratio of actual metal content to geological model metal content (actual_metal_content / model_metal_content). Combines the effect of both F1 and F2 (F3 = F1 × F2). The primary indicator of overall geological model performance and resource model confidence for JORC and NI 43-101 reporting.']
  grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for model_grade and actual_grade fields. Percentage (pct) is used for base metals such as copper and iron ore; grams per tonne (g_per_t) for gold and silver; parts per million (ppm) for trace elements.']
  grade_variance_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage variance between actual mined grade and geological model grade, expressed as ((actual_grade - model_grade) / model_grade) × 100. Positive values indicate higher-than-predicted grade; negative values indicate grade underperformance. Drives cut-off grade and ore/waste classification reviews.']
  model_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Predicted head grade (percentage or grams per tonne) of the primary commodity for the reconciliation period as estimated in the geological block model. Used as the denominator in F2 (grade reconciliation factor) calculation.']
  model_metal_content decimal(18,3) [note: 'Predicted contained metal or mineral content (tonnes of metal) for the reconciliation period, calculated as model_tonnes multiplied by model_grade. Represents the geological model\'s expected metal inventory. Used in F3 (metal content reconciliation factor) calculation.']
  model_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Predicted ore tonnes for the reconciliation period as derived from the geological block model in Geovia GEMS or Hexagon MinePlan. Represents the mine plan scheduled tonnes before actual mining. Used as the denominator in F1 (tonnes reconciliation factor) calculation.']
  model_update_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation results have triggered a requirement to update the geological block model in Geovia GEMS. Set to True when F1, F2, or F3 factors fall outside acceptable tolerance thresholds, indicating the resource model requires re-estimation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, caveats, or contextual information related to the reconciliation record that do not fit into structured fields. May include references to specific blast events, equipment downtime, or exceptional geological conditions observed during the period.']
  ore_recovery_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage of the geological model ore tonnes that were successfully extracted and delivered to the ROM pad or processing plant, accounting for ore losses to waste dumps or misclassification. Distinct from metallurgical recovery rate in the processing plant.']
  ore_source_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific ore source (e.g., pit bench, stope, ore block, or mining block) from which the reconciled tonnes were extracted. Sourced from Geovia GEMS block model and Hexagon MinePlan scheduling.']
  ore_source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ore source by mining method or material origin. Distinguishes between open-cut bench, underground stope, ROM pad stockpile, or blended feed to the processing plant. Critical for strip ratio and dilution analysis.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the reconciliation period. Determines the reporting cadence and is used to aggregate or disaggregate reconciliation factors (F1/F2/F3) for LOM planning and JORC resource model confidence assessments.']
  reconciliation_period_end date [note: 'End date of the production period covered by this reconciliation. Together with reconciliation_period_start defines the temporal scope of the comparison between geological model and actual production.']
  reconciliation_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the production period covered by this reconciliation. Typically aligned to shift, daily, weekly, monthly, or quarterly reporting cycles.']
  reconciliation_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the reconciliation run, typically formatted as REC-<SITE>-<YEAR>-<PERIOD>. Used for cross-system traceability across Geovia GEMS, LabWare LIMS, and Caterpillar MineStar.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the reconciliation record. Tracks progression from initial draft through geologist and mine manager review to final approval or rejection. Superseded indicates a newer reconciliation has replaced this record.']
  resource_classification varchar [note: 'JORC resource or reserve classification of the ore source being reconciled. Measured and Proved Reserve classifications carry the highest geological confidence; Inferred carries the lowest. Reconciliation factors are tracked by classification to assess model confidence at each JORC category level. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: measured|indicated|inferred|proved_reserve|probable_reserve — promote to reference product if additional classifications such as SAMREC or NI 43-101 equivalents are required]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which the reconciliation data was primarily sourced. Geovia GEMS provides geological model data; Caterpillar MineStar provides actual tonnes via FMS; LabWare LIMS provides assay grade data. Manual indicates data was entered directly without system integration.']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'Method used to measure actual mined tonnes for the reconciliation period. Drone survey and laser scan provide volumetric measurements; GPS RTK and total station provide traditional survey data; truck payload uses Caterpillar MineStar FMS payload data. Affects accuracy confidence of actual_tonnes.']
  tonnes_variance_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage variance between actual mined tonnes and geological model tonnes, expressed as ((actual_tonnes - model_tonnes) / model_tonnes) × 100. Positive values indicate over-mining relative to model; negative values indicate under-mining. Used for production scheduling variance reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production reconciliation record was most recently modified. Tracks revisions to reconciliation factors, variance explanations, or approval status. Essential for data lineage in the Databricks silver layer.']
  variance_category varchar [note: 'Primary categorical classification of the reconciliation variance cause. Used for trend analysis and systematic identification of recurring model or operational issues. Supports resource model update prioritisation and mine planning improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: geological_model|dilution|ore_loss|sampling_bias|survey_error|blast_movement|equipment_error|classification_error — promote to reference product]']
  variance_explanation varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the primary causes of variance between geological model predictions and actual production results. Documents factors such as geological model uncertainty, blast movement, dilution, ore loss, sampling bias, or survey error. Required for JORC compliance documentation and resource model update decisions.']

  Note: 'Periodic production reconciliation record comparing geological model predictions to actual mined tonnes and grades. Captures reconciliation period, ore source, model tonnes vs actual tonnes, model grade vs actual grade, reconciliation factor (F1/F2/F3), variance explanation, and sign-off. Sourced from LabWare LIMS, Caterpillar MineStar, and Geovia GEMS. Critical for JORC compliance and resource model confidence.'
}

Table mine.plan_scenario {
  plan_scenario_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the mine plan scenario record. Primary key.']
  base_plan_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on plan_scenario (base_plan_scenario_id)']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Mine plan scenarios are created to evaluate capital project alternatives (different pit expansion sequences, processing capacity options, mining method changes). Each scenario evaluates a specific cap']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent Life of Mine plan that this scenario supports or derives from.']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site for which this scenario is planned.']
  parent_scenario_plan_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent scenario from which this scenario was derived or forked, enabling scenario lineage tracking.']
  primary_plan_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who created this scenario record.']
  project_valuation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project_valuation. Business justification: Mine plan scenarios are valued for sensitivity analysis, trade-off studies, and investment option evaluation. Scenario valuation link for mining project economics and decision-making.']
  resource_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.resource_statement. Business justification: Mine plan scenarios test sensitivity against resource statement assumptions. Required for feasibility studies, understanding resource confidence impacts on economics, and scenario planning.']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Mine plan scenarios evaluate extraction strategies constrained by tenement boundaries, expiry dates, and expenditure commitments. Essential for strategic planning, NPV analysis, and tenement renewal d']
  aisc_usd_per_t decimal(10,2) [note: 'All-In Sustaining Cost in US dollars per tonne calculated for this scenario.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this scenario was formally approved for use in decision-making or investment analysis.']
  average_ore_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average ore grade assumed across the scenario planning horizon.']
  commodity_price_assumption_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Assumed commodity price in US dollars per unit used for economic modeling in this scenario.']
  commodity_price_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the commodity price assumption (e.g., per tonne, per pound, per ounce).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scenario record was first created in the system.']
  cutoff_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum economic grade threshold used to classify material as ore in this scenario.']
  cutoff_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the cut-off grade (e.g., percent, grams per tonne, parts per million).']
  discount_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount rate percentage applied for Net Present Value (NPV) calculations in this scenario.']
  exchange_rate_assumption decimal(10,4) [note: 'Assumed foreign exchange rate used for currency conversion in economic modeling for this scenario.']
  exchange_rate_currency_pair varchar [note: 'Currency pair for the exchange rate assumption (e.g., USD/AUD, USD/CAD).']
  external_scenario_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier or file path in the source planning system (Deswik or Hexagon MinePlan) for traceability.']
  fleet_size_constraint varchar [note: 'Maximum number of equipment units (trucks, loaders, drills) assumed available in this scenario as a constraint parameter.']
  irr_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Internal Rate of Return percentage calculated for this scenario.']
  market_demand_constraint_tpa decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum annual market demand in tonnes per annum assumed as a constraint in this scenario.']
  mining_method varchar [note: 'Primary mining method assumed in this scenario (open pit, underground, sub-level caving, block caving, longwall, room and pillar, or mixed methods). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open_pit|underground|sub_level_caving|block_caving|longwall|room_and_pillar|mixed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  npv_musd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Present Value in million US dollars calculated for this scenario using the assumed discount rate.']
  planned_recovery_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned mineral recovery rate percentage from processing assumed in this scenario.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning horizon covered by this scenario.']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning horizon covered by this scenario.']
  planning_horizon_years varchar [note: 'Duration of the planning horizon in years for this scenario.']
  primary_commodity varchar [note: 'Primary commodity targeted in this mine plan scenario (e.g., iron ore, copper, coal, lithium, nickel).']
  processing_capacity_constraint_tpa decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum annual processing plant capacity in tonnes per annum assumed as a constraint in this scenario.']
  reporting_standard varchar [note: 'Mineral resource reporting standard applied to this scenario (JORC, NI 43-101, SAMREC, SEC S-K 1300, PERC).']
  resource_classification varchar [note: 'Resource classification category used in this scenario (e.g., Measured, Indicated, Inferred, Proved, Probable).']
  scenario_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the mine plan scenario, used for external reference and reporting.']
  scenario_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the scenario purpose, assumptions, and key differentiators from other scenarios.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the mine plan scenario for business user identification and reporting purposes.']
  scenario_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the mine plan scenario in the approval workflow.']
  scenario_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scenario type indicating the planning approach or purpose (base case, optimistic, pessimistic, sensitivity analysis, feasibility study, expansion plan).']
  scenario_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this scenario, incremented with each revision to support version control and audit trail.']
  sensitivity_parameter varchar [note: 'Key parameter being varied in sensitivity analysis scenarios (e.g., commodity price, exchange rate, discount rate, recovery rate).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this scenario was created or imported (Deswik, Hexagon MinePlan, Geovia GEMS, Manual entry, Other).']
  strip_ratio decimal(10,2) [note: 'Overall strip ratio (waste to ore ratio) assumed in this scenario.']
  superseded_date date [note: 'Date when this scenario was superseded by a newer version or alternative scenario.']
  total_capex_musd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capital expenditure in million US dollars assumed for this scenario.']
  total_opex_musd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total operating expenditure in million US dollars assumed for this scenario.']
  total_ore_reserve_mt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total ore reserve in million tonnes assumed in this scenario.']
  total_waste_mt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total waste material in million tonnes assumed in this scenario.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scenario record was last updated.']

  Note: 'Master record of mine plan scenarios and versions capturing scenario name, planning horizon, commodity price assumptions, discount rate, exchange rate assumptions, constraint parameters (fleet size, processing capacity, market demand), status (draft, approved, superseded), approval workflow, and creation date. Enables comparison of alternative LOM strategies, sensitivity analysis, and investment decision audit trail. Sourced from Deswik and Hexagon MinePlan.'
}

Table mine.equipment_schedule_assignment {
  equipment_schedule_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each equipment schedule assignment record in the lakehouse']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific mining asset (truck, excavator, drill, etc.) assigned to execute this production schedule']
  assigned_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine planner or production supervisor who created this equipment assignment. Used for accountability and assignment audit trail.']
  fleet_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment fleet or fleet configuration allocated to execute this production schedule. Links to the fleet management master record in Caterpillar MineStar FMS for execution tracking and OEE monitoring. [Moved from production_schedule: This attribute currently exists in production_schedule but represents the fleet configuration allocated to execute the schedule. In reality, equipment assignments are made at the individual asset level (not fleet level), and each asset assignment may have different parameters. If fleet_id is used for aggregate planning, it should remain in production_schedule as a planning input, but the actual asset-level assignments with their specific hours, utilisation, and fuel budgets belong in this association. However, upon review, equipment_fleet_id appears to be a fleet-level planning attribute (aggregate fleet configuration) rather than individual asset assignment data, so it should likely REMAIN in production_schedule as a planning input. Removing this from the move list.]']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the production schedule record that defines the mining block, time period, and production targets for this equipment assignment']
  assignment_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this equipment schedule assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and assignment history tracking.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'The calendar date when this asset assignment to the production schedule ends. May differ from the schedule period_end_date if equipment is reallocated before period completion.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'The calendar date when this asset assignment to the production schedule becomes effective. May differ from the schedule period_start_date if equipment is allocated mid-period.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the equipment assignment. Values: \'planned\' (assignment created but not yet active), \'active\' (asset currently deployed to this schedule), \'completed\' (schedule period finished), \'cancelled\' (assignment removed before completion), \'suspended\' (temporarily paused due to breakdown or reallocation). Used to track assignment lifecycle and enable dynamic fleet reallocation.']
  assignment_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this equipment schedule assignment record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit trail for fleet reallocation decisions.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking of this equipment assignment relative to other concurrent assignments for the same asset. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used when an asset is assigned to multiple overlapping schedules to determine allocation precedence.']
  scheduled_fuel_budget_litres decimal(12,2) [note: 'The planned fuel consumption budget in litres allocated for this asset during the schedule period. Used for fuel logistics planning and cost control.']
  scheduled_maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Planned maintenance window or downtime period scheduled for this asset during the production schedule period (e.g., \'Week 2 Day Shift\', \'Mid-period 24hr service\'). Used to coordinate production planning with maintenance planning.']
  scheduled_operating_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The planned number of operating hours for this asset during the schedule period. Used to calculate equipment availability and utilisation targets for the mining block or bench.']
  scheduled_utilisation_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The target utilisation percentage for this asset during the schedule period, calculated as productive hours divided by available hours. Used for fleet performance planning and KPI tracking.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment of mining fleet and fixed plant assets to specific production schedules. It captures the planned deployment of equipment (haul trucks, excavators, drills, shovels, dozers, graders) to mining blocks, benches, and stopes for defined schedule periods. Each record links one production schedule to one asset with operational planning attributes including scheduled operating hours, utilisation targets, fuel budgets, and maintenance windows. This is the core of mine production planning and fleet deployment optimization, enabling mine planners to allocate equipment resources across multiple concurrent schedules and track asset utilisation across different mining areas and time periods.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, production schedules require multiple pieces of equipment (haul trucks, excavators, drills, dozers) to be deployed simultaneously to mining blocks and benches, and each asset works across multiple schedule periods and locations throughout the year. Mine planners actively create and manage these equipment assignments as part of the production planning workflow, tracking scheduled hours, utilisation targets, fuel budgets, and maintenance windows for each asset-schedule combination. This is a core operational business process called Equipment Scheduling or Fleet Assignment Planning.'
}

Table mine.schedule_crew_assignment {
  schedule_crew_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each production schedule crew assignment record']
  assigned_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine planner or workforce coordinator who created this crew assignment. Used for accountability and assignment history tracking.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the crew assigned to execute this production schedule']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the production schedule that requires crew execution']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes specific to this crew assignment, such as special equipment requirements, coordination dependencies, or constraints.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this crew assignment to the production schedule. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data. Tracks progression from planning through execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this crew assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail.']
  crew_role varchar [note: 'The specific operational role this crew performs within this production schedule (e.g., drilling, blasting, loading, hauling). Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data. A crew may have different roles in different schedules.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this crew assignment record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  planned_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total planned hours this crew is allocated to work on this production schedule. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data. Used for capacity planning and cost estimation.']
  planned_shift_count varchar [note: 'The number of shifts this crew is planned to work on this production schedule during the assignment period. Derived from scheduled dates and crew roster pattern.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking of this assignment when a crew is assigned to multiple concurrent schedules. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for resource conflict resolution.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'The date when this crew is scheduled to complete work on this production schedule. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'The date when this crew is scheduled to begin work on this production schedule. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment of mine crews to production schedules. It captures the planned allocation of specific crews (drilling, blasting, loading, hauling, maintenance) to execute production schedules across different mining blocks, benches, and time periods. Each record links one production schedule to one crew with temporal and role-specific attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment relationship. Essential for workforce capacity planning, production schedule feasibility validation, and shift-based execution tracking.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, production schedules require multiple specialized crews to execute different phases of the extraction process - drilling crews prepare blast holes, blasting crews execute controlled explosions, loading crews operate excavators, and hauling crews transport material. Each crew is simultaneously assigned to multiple production schedules across different mining blocks, benches, and time periods based on equipment allocation, skill requirements, and operational sequencing. The assignment itself carries critical operational data including temporal coordination (scheduled start/end dates), role specification (what function the crew performs in this schedule), planned capacity (hours allocated), and status tracking (planned through completed).'
}

Table mine.stockpile_circuit_feed {
  stockpile_circuit_feed_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the stockpile-circuit feed relationship record. Primary key.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the processing circuit receiving feed from this stockpile']
  rom_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the ROM stockpile that feeds this circuit']
  blend_ratio_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Target percentage contribution of this stockpile to the total feed blend for this circuit. Used in multi-stockpile blending strategies to achieve target feed grade and quality.']
  conveyor_route_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the conveyor system route used to transport material from this stockpile to this circuit. Supports logistics and maintenance planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this stockpile-circuit feed relationship record was created in the system.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this stockpile-circuit feed relationship became or becomes active. Supports temporal feed scheduling and historical analysis.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this stockpile-circuit feed relationship remains or remained active. Null indicates currently active. Supports temporal feed scheduling and historical analysis.']
  feed_priority varchar [note: 'Integer ranking indicating the priority order in which this stockpile should feed this specific circuit. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used when multiple stockpiles can feed the same circuit.']
  feed_rate_tph decimal(10,3) [note: 'Target or actual feed rate from this stockpile to this circuit expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour. Drives production scheduling and circuit loading.']
  feed_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this stockpile-circuit feed relationship. Active = currently feeding, Planned = scheduled future feed, Suspended = temporarily halted, Completed = historical feed relationship ended.']
  reclaim_method varchar [note: 'Method used to reclaim material from this stockpile for feeding this circuit (e.g., Front-End Loader, Reclaimer, Apron Feeder). Drives operational planning and equipment allocation.']
  reclaim_priority varchar [note: 'Integer ranking indicating the priority order in which this stockpile should be reclaimed to the processing plant feed. Lower number = higher priority. Used by plant feed scheduling to manage blend quality and FIFO/grade-based reclaim strategies. [Moved from rom_stockpile: Reclaim priority is not an absolute property of the ROM stockpile itself, but rather depends on which circuit is being fed. A stockpile may have high priority for one circuit (e.g., oxide ore to leach circuit) but low priority for another (e.g., same stockpile to flotation circuit). This attribute belongs in the association as feed_priority.]']
  reclaim_rate_t_hr decimal(10,3) [note: 'Design or operational reclaim rate of material from this stockpile to the processing plant in tonnes per hour. Used for plant feed scheduling and production planning. [Moved from rom_stockpile: Reclaim rate varies by destination circuit based on circuit capacity, feed requirements, and blending strategy. A stockpile may feed different circuits at different rates. This attribute belongs in the association as feed_rate_tph.]']
  target_feed_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Target head grade of the primary valuable element for this specific stockpile-circuit feed pairing. May differ from circuit-level target when blending multiple stockpiles.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this stockpile-circuit feed relationship record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational feed scheduling relationship between ROM stockpiles and processing circuits. It captures the feed rate, blend ratio, and priority for each stockpile-circuit pairing, enabling mine-to-mill optimization and feed quality management. Each record links one ROM stockpile to one processing circuit with attributes that exist only in the context of this feed scheduling relationship.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, ROM stockpiles serve as intermediate storage between mining and processing, and the feed scheduling relationship is genuinely many-to-many. A single ROM stockpile can feed multiple processing circuits (e.g., high-grade material to primary circuit, low-grade to secondary circuit, oxide ore to leach circuit while sulphide goes to flotation). Conversely, a processing circuit draws from multiple stockpiles to achieve target feed grade through blending strategies. Plant metallurgists and production schedulers actively manage these feed relationships, adjusting blend ratios, feed rates, and priorities based on stockpile inventory, ore quality, and circuit performance.'
}

Table mine.stockpile_blend {
