  stockpile_blend_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this ROM stockpile to saleable product blend record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user (typically a mine planning engineer or metallurgist) who created this blend recipe record.']
  rom_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the ROM stockpile contributing material to this blend']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the saleable product being produced from this blend']
  actual_reclaim_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual tonnes reclaimed from this ROM stockpile for this saleable product blend to date. Used for reconciliation against target and inventory tracking.']
  blend_ratio_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage contribution of this ROM stockpile to the saleable product blend recipe. Represents the proportion of material from this stockpile in the final product blend. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  blend_recipe_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the blend recipe specification. Blend recipes are versioned as they are refined based on actual quality outcomes and changing stockpile characteristics.']
  blend_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this blend recipe. Planned: designed but not yet in use, Active: currently being executed, Suspended: temporarily paused, Completed: no longer in use (superseded), Cancelled: abandoned before execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this blend recipe record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this blend recipe is effective and operational. Blend recipes change over time as stockpile inventory, grades, and product specifications evolve. Part of effective_date_range identified in detection phase.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this blend recipe is effective. Null indicates current active blend. Part of effective_date_range identified in detection phase.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this blend recipe record.']
  quality_contribution_fe_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Contribution of this ROM stockpile to the iron (Fe) grade of the final saleable product, expressed as percentage. Used for blend quality modeling and product specification achievement. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  quality_contribution_sio2_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Contribution of this ROM stockpile to the silica (SiO2) content of the final saleable product, expressed as percentage. Critical for managing impurity levels in product specifications. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  reclaim_sequence varchar [note: 'Integer indicating the order in which this ROM stockpile should be reclaimed when producing this saleable product. Governs operational scheduling of reclaim equipment and material flow to processing plant. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  target_reclaim_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned tonnes to be reclaimed from this ROM stockpile for this saleable product blend over the effective period. Used for production planning and stockpile inventory management.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the blending operation between ROM stockpiles and saleable products. It captures the operational reality that saleable products are created by blending material from multiple ROM stockpiles, and each ROM stockpile contributes to multiple saleable products. Each record links one ROM stockpile to one saleable product with blend ratios, quality contributions, reclaim sequences, and effective date ranges that exist only in the context of this blending relationship.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, saleable products are created by blending material from multiple ROM stockpiles to achieve target grade specifications, and each ROM stockpile contributes material to multiple different saleable products over time. Blending is an active operational process managed by mine planning and metallurgy teams who create blend recipes specifying which stockpiles to reclaim, in what sequence, at what ratios, to produce each product grade. This is a core operational M:N relationship with substantial relationship-specific data.'
}

Table mine.mine_site {
  mine_site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for mine_site']
  parent_mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on mine_site (parent_mine_site_id)']
  annual_production_capacity_tonnes decimal(15,2) [note: 'Nameplate annual production capacity of the mine site in metric tonnes of ore or product.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the mine site ceased operations or entered closure phase. Nullable for active sites.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the mine site commenced commercial production operations.']
  commodity_primary varchar [note: 'The primary mineral or ore extracted at this mine site.']
  commodity_secondary varchar [note: 'Secondary or by-product minerals extracted at this mine site, if applicable. Comma-separated list.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the mine site administration.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the mine site office.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the mine site is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mine site record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for financial amounts associated with this mine site.']
  elevation_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average elevation of the mine site surface above sea level in meters.']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'Official environmental approval or permit number issued by the regulatory authority.']
  is_joint_venture boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the mine site is operated as a joint venture with other mining companies.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the mine site centroid in decimal degrees.']
  lease_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current mining lease or permit expires. Nullable for indefinite leases subject to renewal.']
  lease_number varchar [note: 'Official mining lease or permit number issued by the regulatory authority.']
  lease_start_date date [note: 'Date when the mining lease or permit became effective.']
  life_of_mine_years decimal(5,1) [note: 'Estimated remaining operational life of the mine in years based on current reserves and production rates.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the mine site centroid in decimal degrees.']
  mining_method varchar [note: 'Primary extraction technique employed at the mine site.']
  nearest_town varchar [note: 'Name of the nearest populated town or city to the mine site.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special conditions, or contextual information about the mine site.']
  operating_company varchar [note: 'Legal name of the company or joint venture entity that operates the mine site.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the mine site in its lifecycle.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage ownership stake held by the parent company in this mine site. 100.00 for wholly-owned operations.']
  postal_address varchar [note: 'Full postal address of the mine site office or administrative location.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the mine site location.']
  rehabilitation_bond_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial bond or guarantee amount held by regulatory authorities for site rehabilitation and closure obligations, in local currency.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Current safety performance rating of the mine site based on incident frequency and severity metrics.']
  site_area_hectares decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total land area covered by the mine site lease in hectares.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the mine site used in operational systems and reporting.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the mine site as registered with regulatory authorities and used in business communications.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the mine site based on extraction method and operational phase.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the mine site is located.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the mine site location (e.g., Australia/Perth, America/Santiago).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mine site record was last modified in the system.']
  workforce_count varchar [note: 'Total number of employees and contractors working at the mine site.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for mine_site. Referenced by mine_site_id.'
}

Table mine.site {
  site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for site']
  parent_site_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on site (parent_site_id)']
  annual_production_capacity_tonnes decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual production capacity of the site measured in tonnes of ore or product.']
  average_ore_grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average grade or concentration of the primary commodity in the ore body, expressed as a percentage.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the site ceased operations or is planned to cease operations.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the site commenced commercial production operations.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the site office.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the site office.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the site is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this site record was first created in the system.']
  distance_to_port_km decimal(8,2) [note: 'Distance from the site to the primary export port measured in kilometers.']
  elevation_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average elevation of the site above sea level measured in meters.']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued environmental approval or permit identifier authorizing mining operations.']
  has_port_facility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site includes or operates a dedicated port or ship loading facility.']
  has_processing_plant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site has an on-site ore processing or beneficiation plant.']
  has_rail_connection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site is connected to a rail network for product transportation.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site record is currently active and in use for operational reporting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this site record was last modified in the system.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the site centroid in decimal degrees.']
  lease_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current mining lease expires and requires renewal or extension.']
  life_of_mine_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated remaining operational life of the mine in years based on current reserves and production rates.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the site centroid in decimal degrees.']
  mining_lease_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued mining lease or tenement identifier authorizing extraction activities.']
  nearest_town varchar [note: 'Name of the nearest populated town or city to the site.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the site in its lifecycle.']
  ore_reserve_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total proven and probable ore reserves at the site measured in tonnes, as per JORC or NI 43-101 standards.']
  postal_address varchar [note: 'Full postal address of the site office for correspondence and deliveries.']
  primary_commodity varchar [note: 'The principal mineral or ore extracted at this site.']
  rehabilitation_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial security or bond amount held by regulatory authorities for site rehabilitation and closure obligations.']
  secondary_commodities varchar [note: 'Additional minerals or ores extracted as by-products at this site, comma-separated list.']
  site_area_hectares decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total land area covered by the mining site including pit, infrastructure, and buffer zones, measured in hectares.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the site, used in operational systems and reporting.']
  site_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the current site manager or general manager responsible for operations.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the mining site as registered with regulatory authorities.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the site based on mining method and operational purpose.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the site is located.']
  strip_ratio decimal(8,2) [note: 'Ratio of waste material to ore that must be removed, expressed as waste tonnes per ore tonne.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the site location used for operational scheduling and reporting.']
  workforce_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of personnel that can be accommodated at the site including employees and contractors.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for site. Referenced by site_id.'
}

Table mine.haul_route {
  haul_route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for haul_route']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site where this haul route operates.']
  origin_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the starting location of the haul route, typically a pit, bench, or stockpile.']
  return_haul_route_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on haul_route (return_haul_route_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the haul route record was first created in the system.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the ending location of the haul route, typically a crusher, ROM pad, or waste dump.']
  dust_suppression_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether active dust suppression measures are required on this route for environmental and health compliance.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date when the haul route became or will become operational and available for use.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date when the haul route is planned to be decommissioned or is no longer operational. Null for indefinite routes.']
  estimated_cycle_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned round-trip cycle time for a loaded and empty haul, used in production scheduling and fleet sizing.']
  gps_waypoints varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of GPS coordinate waypoints defining the haul route path for Fleet Management System (FMS) integration.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent maintenance activity was completed on the haul route.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the haul route record was most recently modified.']
  lighting_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the haul route has permanent lighting infrastructure for night shift operations.']
  maintenance_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Standard interval in days between scheduled maintenance activities for the haul route.']
  maximum_gradient_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Steepest gradient section along the haul route, critical for equipment selection and safety assessment.']
  maximum_vehicle_capacity_tonnes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum payload capacity in tonnes that vehicles are permitted to carry on this route, constrained by road design and gradient.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the next planned maintenance activity is scheduled for the haul route.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or constraints related to the haul route.']
  number_of_lanes varchar [note: 'Count of traffic lanes on the haul route. Single-lane routes require passing bays.']
  permitted_vehicle_class varchar [note: 'Classification of vehicles authorized to use this haul route based on size, weight, and operational capability.']
  road_surface_type varchar [note: 'Type of road surface material used on the haul route, affecting rolling resistance and maintenance requirements.']
  road_width_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Width of the haul road in meters, determining the size and number of vehicles that can safely operate.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the haul route. Used for operational communication and dispatch systems.']
  route_condition_rating varchar [note: 'Current assessed condition of the haul route based on surface quality, drainage, and structural integrity.']
  route_distance_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total physical distance of the haul route measured in kilometers, used for cycle time calculation and fuel consumption estimation.']
  route_gradient_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average gradient or slope of the haul route expressed as a percentage. Positive values indicate uphill, negative values indicate downhill.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the haul route, typically including origin and destination landmarks.']
  route_priority varchar [note: 'Operational priority level of the haul route for maintenance scheduling and resource allocation.']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the haul route indicating availability for use in production scheduling.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the haul route based on origin and destination function within the mining operation.']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed safety risk level of the haul route based on gradient, width, surface condition, and historical incident data.']
  speed_limit_kmh varchar [note: 'Maximum permitted vehicle speed on the haul route for safety and operational control.']
  traffic_control_type varchar [note: 'Method of traffic management and control used on the haul route to ensure safe vehicle movement.']
  weather_dependent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the route is significantly affected by weather conditions and may require closure during adverse weather.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for haul_route. Referenced by haul_route_id.'
}

Table mine.drill_pattern {
  drill_pattern_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for drill_pattern']
  adjacent_drill_pattern_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on drill_pattern (adjacent_drill_pattern_id)']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the authorized person who approved this drill pattern for operational use.']
  designed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining engineer or blast designer who created this drill pattern.']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site where this drill pattern is applicable or was designed for.']
  pit_or_level_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific open pit or mining area where this pattern is used.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this drill pattern was formally approved for operational use.']
  bench_height_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Vertical height of the mining bench in meters for which this drill pattern is designed.']
  blast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the blast operation this pattern is designed for, determining explosive loading and timing requirements.']
  burden_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Distance in meters from the blast hole to the free face, critical parameter for blast energy distribution and fragmentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this drill pattern record was first created in the system.']
  design_date date [note: 'Date when this drill pattern was originally designed or created.']
  drill_rig_type varchar [note: 'Recommended or required drilling equipment type for executing this pattern, ensuring operational compatibility.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this drill pattern becomes valid and available for operational use.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this drill pattern remains valid, nullable for patterns with indefinite validity.']
  explosive_type varchar [note: 'Primary explosive product specified for this drill pattern, determining blast performance characteristics.']
  fragmentation_target_p80_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target fragment size in millimeters where 80% of blasted material passes through, critical for downstream processing efficiency.']
  hole_depth_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned depth of blast holes in meters, typically including sub-drill below the bench floor.']
  hole_diameter_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Diameter of blast holes in millimeters, determining explosive capacity and fragmentation characteristics.']
  holes_per_row varchar [note: 'Typical number of blast holes per row in this pattern configuration.']
  initiation_system varchar [note: 'Detonation initiation technology used for blast timing control and sequencing.']
  is_standard_pattern boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a standard, reusable pattern or a custom design for specific conditions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this drill pattern record was last updated or modified.']
  number_of_rows varchar [note: 'Typical number of hole rows in a blast using this pattern, affecting blast volume and timing complexity.']
  ore_waste_classification varchar [note: 'Material classification indicating whether this pattern is used for ore extraction, waste removal, or transitional material.']
  pattern_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code identifying the drill pattern design, used for operational reference and communication.']
  pattern_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the drill pattern for identification and reporting purposes.']
  pattern_notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, constraints, or special instructions for applying this drill pattern in the field.']
  pattern_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the drill pattern indicating its availability for operational use.']
  pattern_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the drill pattern geometry used for blast design, determining hole spacing and burden configuration.']
  powder_factor_kg_per_m3 decimal(8,3) [note: 'Target explosive consumption in kilograms per cubic meter of rock, indicating blast intensity and fragmentation energy.']
  rock_strength_mpa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Unconfined compressive strength of the target rock in megapascals, determining drilling difficulty and explosive requirements.']
  rock_type varchar [note: 'Primary geological rock type or material classification this pattern is designed for, influencing drilling and blasting parameters.']
  safety_exclusion_zone_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum safe distance in meters required around the blast area when using this pattern, for personnel and equipment safety.']
  spacing_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Distance in meters between adjacent blast holes in a row, determining blast coverage and fragmentation uniformity.']
  stemming_length_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Length of inert material (stemming) placed at the top of the blast hole in meters to confine explosive gases.']
  sub_drill_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional drilling depth below the bench floor in meters to ensure complete breakage at the toe of the blast.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this drill pattern design, incremented when pattern parameters are modified.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for drill_pattern. Referenced by drill_pattern_id.'
}

Table mine.mine_area {
  mine_area_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for mine_area']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent mine site to which this area belongs.']
  parent_mine_area_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on mine_area (parent_mine_area_id)']
  rom_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary ROM pad where ore from this area is delivered for initial processing.']
  area_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code identifying the mine area, used in operational systems, planning documents, and reporting.']
  area_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the mine area, used for identification in operational communications and planning.']
  area_size_hectares decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total surface area of the mine area measured in hectares.']
  area_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the mine area based on its operational purpose and extraction method.']
  blast_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this area is designated for drill-and-blast operations.']
  closure_planned_date date [note: 'Planned date for closure of mining operations in this area based on life-of-mine planning.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the mine area was officially commissioned and began operational production.']
  commodity_primary varchar [note: 'Primary mineral or commodity extracted from this mine area.']
  commodity_secondary varchar [note: 'Secondary or by-product commodities extracted from this mine area, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mine area record was first created in the system.']
  depth_max_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum depth of the mine area below surface, measured in meters. Applicable to pits and underground workings.']
  elevation_max_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum elevation of the mine area measured in meters above sea level.']
  elevation_min_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum elevation of the mine area measured in meters above sea level.']
  environmental_sensitivity varchar [note: 'Classification of environmental sensitivity based on proximity to protected areas, water sources, or ecological significance.']
  equipment_fleet_assigned varchar [note: 'Designation of the primary equipment fleet assigned to operate in this mine area.']
  extraction_method varchar [note: 'Primary mining method employed in this area for ore extraction.']
  geotechnical_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of geotechnical stability and risk level for the mine area.']
  haulage_route_primary varchar [note: 'Primary haulage route designation for material transport from this area to processing or stockpile locations.']
  last_survey_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent surveying activity conducted in this mine area.']
  life_of_mine_years decimal(6,1) [note: 'Estimated remaining operational life of the mine area based on current reserves and production rates, measured in years.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, constraints, or special considerations for this mine area.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the mine area in its lifecycle.']
  ore_grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average ore grade of the primary commodity in this area, expressed as percentage.']
  ore_reserve_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated proven and probable ore reserves in this area, measured in tonnes.']
  permit_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the mining permit for this area.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory permit or license number authorizing mining operations in this area.']
  production_capacity_tonnes_per_year decimal(18,2) [note: 'Designed annual production capacity of the mine area, measured in tonnes per year.']
  rehabilitation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of mine closure and rehabilitation activities for the area.']
  responsible_superintendent varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the mining superintendent responsible for operations in this area.']
  scada_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this area is integrated with SCADA systems for real-time operational monitoring.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Operating shift pattern for this mine area, such as 24/7, day-shift-only, or FIFO roster pattern.']
  strip_ratio decimal(8,2) [note: 'Ratio of waste material to ore that must be removed to extract the ore, expressed as waste:ore.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mine area record was last updated in the system.']
  water_management_zone varchar [note: 'Designated water management zone or catchment area for environmental and operational water control.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for mine_area. Referenced by mine_area_id.'
}

Table mine.blast_pattern {
  blast_pattern_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for blast_pattern']
  adjacent_blast_pattern_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on blast_pattern (adjacent_blast_pattern_id)']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the authorized person (typically a senior mining engineer or blasting superintendent) who approved this blast pattern for operational use.']
  designed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining engineer or blast designer who created this blast pattern.']
  explosive_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary explosive product used in this blast pattern (e.g., ANFO, emulsion, heavy ANFO).']
  geological_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geological rock type or lithology for which this blast pattern is optimized (e.g., iron ore, overburden, coal, hard rock).']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site where this blast pattern is designed for use.']
  pit_or_level_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific open-cut pit or mining area where this blast pattern is applied.']
  application_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing operational guidance, constraints, or special instructions for applying this blast pattern in the field.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the blast pattern was formally approved for operational use.']
  bench_height_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Vertical height of the bench face in meters for which this blast pattern is designed. Critical parameter for calculating explosive load and fragmentation outcomes.']
  burden_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Perpendicular distance in meters from the blast hole to the free face. Primary design parameter controlling rock movement and fragmentation.']
  charge_length_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total length of the explosive column in meters within the blast hole.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this blast pattern record was first created in the system.']
  delay_timing_ms varchar [note: 'Time delay in milliseconds between detonation of adjacent blast holes, used to control vibration, fragmentation, and muck pile throw.']
  design_date date [note: 'Date when the blast pattern was originally designed.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this blast pattern becomes valid for operational use.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this blast pattern remains valid. Nullable for patterns with indefinite validity.']
  explosive_mass_per_hole_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total mass of explosive loaded into each blast hole in kilograms.']
  fragmentation_target_p80_mm decimal(6,1) [note: 'Target fragmentation size in millimeters where 80% of the blasted material passes through (P80 metric). Drives crusher feed requirements and downstream processing efficiency.']
  hole_depth_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total depth of the blast hole in meters, including sub-drill below the bench floor to ensure complete breakage.']
  hole_diameter_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Diameter of the blast holes in millimeters. Determines explosive charge capacity and influences fragmentation and powder factor.']
  initiation_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of detonation initiation system used in the blast pattern. Electronic systems offer precise timing; non-electric and shock tube systems are cost-effective alternatives.']
  is_standard_pattern boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a standard, repeatable blast pattern (true) or a custom, one-off design (false).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this blast pattern record was last updated in the system.']
  number_of_holes varchar [note: 'Total count of blast holes in the pattern design.']
  number_of_rows varchar [note: 'Number of rows of blast holes in the pattern, measured perpendicular to the free face.']
  pattern_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code identifying the blast pattern design, used in operational communications and drill-and-blast planning systems.']
  pattern_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the blast pattern, typically describing the geometry or application context.']
  pattern_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the blast pattern geometry. Square and rectangular patterns are common in open-cut operations; staggered patterns optimize energy distribution; echelon patterns suit angled benches; radial patterns are used in underground or specialized applications.']
  powder_factor_kg_per_m3 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Mass of explosive in kilograms required to break one cubic meter of rock. Key performance indicator for blast efficiency and cost control.']
  rock_strength_mpa decimal(7,2) [note: 'Unconfined compressive strength of the target rock in megapascals. Influences explosive selection and powder factor requirements.']
  safety_exclusion_zone_m decimal(6,1) [note: 'Minimum safe distance in meters from the blast area within which all personnel and equipment must be evacuated prior to detonation.']
  spacing_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Distance in meters between adjacent blast holes in the same row. Typically 1.0 to 1.5 times the burden distance.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the blast pattern. Active patterns are approved for operational use; draft patterns are under design; retired patterns are no longer used but retained for historical reference.']
  stemming_length_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Length of inert material (typically crushed rock or drill cuttings) placed at the top of the blast hole in meters to confine explosive gases and prevent premature venting.']
  sub_drill_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional drilling depth below the bench floor in meters to ensure complete rock breakage at the toe of the blast.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the blast pattern design, incremented when modifications are made to an existing pattern.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for blast_pattern. Referenced by blast_pattern_id.'
}

Table mine.pit_or_level {
  pit_or_level_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pit_or_level']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent mine site where this pit or level is located.']
  parent_pit_or_level_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pit_or_level (parent_pit_or_level_id)']
  rehabilitation_provision_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved environmental rehabilitation plan for this pit or level upon closure.']
  access_ramp_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary access ramp or decline providing haulage access to this pit or level.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when mining operations were completed at this pit or level. Used for historical analysis and future planning accuracy.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when mining operations commenced at this pit or level. Used for schedule variance analysis and performance tracking.']
  average_ore_grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average grade of ore at this pit or level, expressed as percentage of target mineral content. Business-sensitive data used for mine sequencing and blending strategies.']
  bench_height_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Vertical height of the mining bench at this level, measured in meters. Standard bench heights are critical for blast design and equipment selection.']
  blast_pattern_code varchar [note: 'Standard blast design pattern code used at this pit or level. References drill spacing, burden, and explosive configuration for this geological domain.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pit or level record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  cutoff_grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Minimum ore grade threshold for economic extraction at this pit or level, expressed as percentage. Material below this grade is classified as waste.']
  depth_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Vertical depth of the pit or level from surface or reference datum, measured in meters. Used for geotechnical analysis and ventilation planning.']
  design_capacity_tonnes_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned daily production capacity for this pit or level, measured in tonnes per day. Used for production scheduling and resource allocation.']
  dilution_factor_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Expected percentage of waste material mixed with ore during extraction at this pit or level. Used for grade reconciliation and mill feed planning.']
  elevation_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation of the pit floor or level datum above sea level, measured in meters. Critical for mine planning, haulage optimization, and surveying.']
  geotechnical_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of ground stability and geotechnical hazards at this pit or level: low (stable), moderate (manageable risks), high (significant controls required), or extreme (critical hazard management).']
  haulage_distance_km decimal(8,3) [note: 'Average haulage distance from this pit or level to the primary destination (ROM pad, crusher, or waste dump), measured in kilometers. Used for cycle time and cost modeling.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this pit or level record is currently active and valid for operational use. True indicates active, False indicates inactive or archived.']
  mining_method varchar [note: 'The extraction method employed at this pit or level: open-cut/open-pit for surface mining, underground methods including block cave, sublevel stoping, longwall, or room and pillar.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the pit or level for identification and communication purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special conditions, or additional context about this pit or level.']
  ore_reserve_tonnes decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated economically recoverable ore reserves at this pit or level, measured in tonnes. Business-sensitive reserve data used for Life of Mine (LOM) planning.']
  pit_or_level_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the pit or level, used in operational systems and reporting. Externally-known unique code.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for completion of mining operations at this pit or level. Used for Life of Mine (LOM) planning and closure planning.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for commencement of mining operations at this pit or level. Used for production scheduling and resource planning.']
  primary_commodity varchar [note: 'The primary mineral commodity being extracted from this pit or level: iron ore, copper, coal, lithium, nickel, gold, silver, zinc, lead, or bauxite.']
  recovery_factor_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Expected percentage of in-situ ore that will be successfully extracted and recovered from this pit or level. Accounts for mining losses and operational constraints.']
  secondary_commodity varchar [note: 'Secondary or by-product mineral commodity present at this pit or level, if applicable. Used for polymetallic deposit planning and revenue optimization.']
  slope_angle_degrees decimal(5,2) [note: 'Design slope angle for pit walls or level access at this location, measured in degrees from horizontal. Critical for geotechnical stability and safety.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pit or level: planned (future), design (engineering phase), development (construction), active (in production), depleted (ore exhausted), suspended (temporarily halted), closed (operations ceased), or rehabilitated (environmental restoration complete).']
  strip_ratio decimal(8,3) [note: 'Ratio of waste to ore for this pit or level, calculated as waste volume divided by ore volume. Key economic indicator for open-cut mining operations.']
  surveyed_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent survey measurement for this pit or level. Used for as-built reconciliation and spatial accuracy tracking.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the mining excavation structure: pit for open-cut operations, level for underground operations, bench for stepped excavation, stage for phased development, cutback for pit expansion, or pushback for sequential mining phases.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pit or level record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  ventilation_requirement_m3_per_sec decimal(10,2) [note: 'Required airflow rate for underground levels, measured in cubic meters per second. Critical for occupational health and safety compliance.']
  waste_volume_bcm decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated volume of waste material to be removed from this pit or level, measured in bank cubic meters. Critical for strip ratio calculation and waste dump planning.']
  water_inflow_rate_liters_per_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average groundwater inflow rate at this pit or level, measured in liters per minute. Used for dewatering system design and water management planning.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pit_or_level. Referenced by pit_or_level_id.'
}

Table mine.bench {
  bench_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for bench']
  access_ramp_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary access ramp providing haulage access to this bench.']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site where this bench is located.']
  parent_bench_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on bench (parent_bench_id)']
  pit_or_level_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pit or open-cut mine where this bench is located.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when mining operations were completed on this bench.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when mining operations commenced on this bench.']
  bench_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the bench used in mine planning and operational systems.']
  bench_height_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Vertical height of the bench face in meters, typically standardized within a pit design.']
  bench_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the bench.']
  bench_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the bench in its lifecycle: planned for future extraction, actively being mined, depleted of material, temporarily suspended, or permanently closed.']
  bench_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bench based on material type: ore-bearing, waste rock, mixed material, overburden, or interburden.']
  bench_width_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Horizontal width of the bench working area in meters, measured from crest to toe.']
  berm_width_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Width of the safety berm or catch bench in meters, designed to catch falling material and provide access.']
  blast_pattern_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the standard drill-and-blast pattern design used for this bench.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bench record was first created in the system.']
  design_capacity_bcm decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned total volume of material to be extracted from the bench in bank cubic meters (in-situ volume).']
  drill_hole_burden_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard distance from the drill hole to the free face (burden) in the blast pattern, measured in meters.']
  drill_hole_diameter_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard diameter of blast holes drilled in the bench, measured in millimeters.']
  drill_hole_spacing_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard horizontal spacing between drill holes in the blast pattern, measured in meters.']
  elevation_rl_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'The reduced level elevation of the bench floor in meters above mean sea level, used for vertical positioning in mine design.']
  face_angle_degrees decimal(5,2) [note: 'Angle of the bench face in degrees from horizontal, critical for geotechnical stability and safety.']
  geotechnical_domain varchar [note: 'Geotechnical classification or domain code for the bench, indicating rock mass characteristics and stability parameters.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the bench is currently active in production operations.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Primary geological material type present in the bench (e.g., iron ore, copper ore, coal, limestone, sandstone).']
  mining_method varchar [note: 'Primary extraction method used for the bench: drill-and-blast, rip-and-load (dozer ripping), direct loading, or selective mining.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, comments, or special instructions related to the bench.']
  ore_grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average ore grade percentage for the bench, representing the concentration of valuable mineral content.']
  ore_tonnes decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total tonnage of ore material within the bench.']
  phase_code varchar [note: 'Mine planning phase code indicating which development phase or pushback this bench belongs to.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when mining operations are planned to be completed on this bench, per the Life of Mine (LOM) plan.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when mining operations are planned to commence on this bench, per the Life of Mine (LOM) plan.']
  remaining_volume_bcm decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current remaining volume of material available for extraction from the bench in bank cubic meters.']
  rock_mass_rating varchar [note: 'Rock Mass Rating score for the bench, a geotechnical classification system indicating rock quality and stability.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Mining sequence order number for the bench within the pit development plan, indicating the planned extraction order.']
  strip_ratio decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of waste tonnes to ore tonnes for the bench, a key economic indicator for mining operations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bench record was last modified in the system.']
  waste_tonnes decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total tonnage of waste material within the bench.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for bench. Referenced by bench_id.'
}

Table mine.location {
  location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for location']
  origin_location_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on location (origin_location_id)']
  access_restriction varchar [note: 'Access control classification for the location. Used for safety management and operational security.']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is currently active in operational systems and available for use in planning and scheduling.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when operations ceased at this location. Nullable for active locations.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when operations commenced at this location.']
  area_sqm decimal(15,2) [note: 'Surface area of the location in square meters. Used for capacity planning, rehabilitation costing, and environmental impact assessment.']
  bench_height_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Vertical height of mining benches at this location in meters. Applicable to open-cut operations.']
  blast_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is within an active blast zone requiring special safety protocols.']
  capacity_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum material capacity of the location in tonnes. Used for stockpile management, ROM pad planning, and dump scheduling.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Spatial reference system used for location coordinates (e.g., WGS84, UTM Zone 50S, MGA94 Zone 51).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this location record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the location including operational context, boundaries, and special characteristics.']
  drainage_zone varchar [note: 'Water drainage zone or catchment area identifier for the location. Used for water management and environmental compliance.']
  elevation_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation of the location above mean sea level in meters. Critical for pit design, haul road planning, and drainage management.']
  environmental_zone varchar [note: 'Environmental management zone classification for the location (e.g., rehabilitation zone, buffer zone, heritage area).']
  geotechnical_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Geotechnical stability risk assessment rating for the location. Critical for safety and operational planning.']
  grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average ore grade at this location expressed as a percentage. Critical for mine planning, blending, and economic evaluation.']
  haul_distance_km decimal(8,3) [note: 'Average haul distance from this location to the primary destination (e.g., crusher, ROM pad, waste dump) in kilometers.']
  last_survey_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent survey or measurement of this location. Critical for maintaining accurate spatial data and volume calculations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this location record was last modified.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the location centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84).']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the location used across operational systems and reporting.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the location (e.g., pit name, dump name, stockpile name, ROM pad identifier).']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the location in its lifecycle.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the location within the mining operation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pit|dump|stockpile|rom_pad|crusher|processing_plant|workshop|office|haul_road|bench|ramp|portal|stope|drive|shaft|decline|ore_pass|waste_dump|tailings_storage_facility — promote to reference product]']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the location centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84).']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent mine site to which this location belongs.']
  mining_method varchar [note: 'Primary mining method employed at this location.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Primary ore or mineral type extracted or stored at this location (e.g., iron ore, copper ore, coal, lithium, nickel).']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business unit or department responsible for managing this location (e.g., Mining Operations, Processing, Maintenance).']
  parent_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent location in a hierarchical location structure (e.g., a bench within a pit, a drive within a stope). Nullable for top-level locations.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Planned date for completion or closure of operations at this location. Used in Life of Mine (LOM) planning.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for commencement of operations at this location. Used in Life of Mine (LOM) planning.']
  rehabilitation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location requires environmental rehabilitation upon closure or depletion.']
  strip_ratio decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of waste material to ore material at this location. Key metric for open-cut mining economics and scheduling.']
  volume_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Volume capacity of the location in cubic meters. Applicable to stockpiles, ROM pads, waste dumps, and tailings storage facilities.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for location. Referenced by origin_location_id.'
}

Table mine.explosive_type {
  explosive_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for explosive_type']
  parent_explosive_type_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on explosive_type (parent_explosive_type_id)']
  application_type varchar [note: 'Primary mining application context for this explosive type (surface, underground, both). Determines operational suitability.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or authority that has approved this explosive type for use (e.g., state mining department, federal agency).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which regulatory approval was granted for this explosive type. Used for compliance tracking and audit purposes.']
  approval_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current regulatory approval expires and requires renewal. Null if approval does not expire.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Primary classification category of the explosive based on form and composition (bulk, packaged, emulsion, ANFO, watergel, slurry).']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the explosive type in operational systems and blast designs. Typically 2-10 characters.']
  compatibility_group varchar [note: 'Single letter (A-S) indicating which explosives can be stored and transported together based on hazard compatibility.']
  cost_per_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard cost per kilogram of this explosive type in the base currency. Used for blast cost estimation and budgeting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this explosive type record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost_per_kg field (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).']
  density_kg_per_m3 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Typical density of the explosive in kilograms per cubic meter. Critical for blast design calculations and loading specifications.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the explosive type including composition, characteristics, and typical use cases in mining operations.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this explosive type record became effective and available for operational use.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this explosive type record ceased to be effective. Null if currently effective.']
  energy_content_mj_per_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Energy released per kilogram of explosive, measured in megajoules. Used for blast energy calculations and fragmentation modeling.']
  fume_class varchar [note: 'Classification of toxic fume production (Class 1 = lowest toxicity, Class 3 = highest). Important for underground operations and ventilation planning.']
  hazard_class varchar [note: 'Dangerous goods hazard class and division for the explosive (e.g., 1.1, 1.5) per international transport regulations.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this explosive type is currently active and available for use in blast designs. True = active, False = inactive/discontinued.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this explosive type record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary manufacturer or supplier of this explosive type. Used for procurement and quality assurance.']
  minimum_priming_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum primer or booster size and type required to reliably initiate this explosive type. Critical for blast design and safety.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full commercial or technical name of the explosive type (e.g., ANFO, Emulsion, Heavy ANFO, Bulk Emulsion).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this explosive type. Free-text field for operational guidance.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s product family or series name to which this explosive type belongs. Groups related explosive products.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory approval status for use in mining operations (approved, pending, expired, suspended, withdrawn).']
  relative_bulk_strength_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Explosive strength per unit volume relative to ANFO. Accounts for density differences in blast design calculations.']
  relative_weight_strength_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Explosive strength relative to ANFO (100%). Used to calculate equivalent explosive quantities and blast design parameters.']
  sensitivity_rating varchar [note: 'Sensitivity of the explosive to initiation by impact, friction, or heat (very_low, low, moderate, high, very_high). Affects handling and storage requirements.']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Maximum storage duration in months before the explosive degrades or requires re-certification. Used for inventory management and rotation.']
  temperature_range_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum ambient temperature in Celsius at which the explosive maintains reliable performance and safety characteristics.']
  temperature_range_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum ambient temperature in Celsius at which the explosive maintains reliable performance and safety characteristics.']
  typical_hole_diameter_max_mm decimal(7,2) [note: 'Maximum blast hole diameter in millimeters for which this explosive type is typically suitable. Guides blast design parameters.']
  typical_hole_diameter_min_mm decimal(7,2) [note: 'Minimum blast hole diameter in millimeters for which this explosive type is typically suitable. Guides blast design parameters.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN identification number for the explosive type used in transportation and regulatory compliance (e.g., UN0082, UN0241).']
  velocity_of_detonation_m_per_s decimal(8,2) [note: 'Speed at which the detonation wave travels through the explosive, measured in meters per second. Key performance indicator for explosive effectiveness.']
  water_resistance varchar [note: 'Ability of the explosive to maintain performance in wet conditions (none, poor, fair, good, excellent). Critical for wet hole applications.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for explosive_type. Referenced by explosive_type_id.'
}

Table mine.access_ramp {
  access_ramp_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for access_ramp']
  connects_to_access_ramp_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on access_ramp (connects_to_access_ramp_id)']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the access ramp was officially commissioned and put into operational service.']
  construction_completion_date date [note: 'Date when construction of the access ramp was completed and the ramp was ready for commissioning.']
  construction_method varchar [note: 'Engineering method used to construct the access ramp.']
  construction_start_date date [note: 'Date when construction of the access ramp commenced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this access ramp record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the access ramp was or is planned to be decommissioned and removed from active service.']
  design_capacity_tonnes_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'The planned maximum haulage throughput capacity of the ramp in tonnes per hour based on engineering design specifications.']
  drainage_system_installed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a drainage system is installed to manage water ingress and prevent flooding or erosion.']
  emergency_egress_route boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ramp is designated as an emergency evacuation route for personnel safety.']
  entry_elevation_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation above sea level in meters at the ramp entry point.']
  exit_elevation_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation above sea level in meters at the ramp exit point.']
  geotechnical_rating varchar [note: 'Current geotechnical stability assessment rating of the ramp based on rock mass quality and ground conditions.']
  gradient_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The slope or incline of the ramp expressed as a percentage, critical for haulage equipment performance and safety calculations.']
  ground_support_type varchar [note: 'Type of ground support system installed to maintain ramp stability and prevent rock falls.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety and structural inspection of the ramp.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this access ramp record was most recently modified in the system.']
  length_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total length of the access ramp in meters from entry to exit point.']
  lighting_installed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether permanent lighting infrastructure is installed along the ramp for operational safety.']
  maximum_vehicle_weight_tonnes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum gross vehicle weight in tonnes permitted to use this ramp based on structural and geotechnical design limits.']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site where this access ramp is located.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory inspection of the ramp based on regulatory and operational requirements.']
  ore_type_access varchar [note: 'Type of ore or mineral that this ramp provides access to, relevant for production planning and scheduling.']
  pit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific open pit or underground mine area that this ramp provides access to.']
  primary_use varchar [note: 'The primary operational purpose or function that this ramp serves in the mine operations.']
  ramp_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier code for the access ramp used in operational systems and mine planning documentation.']
  ramp_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the access ramp for operational reference and communication.']
  ramp_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the access ramp indicating its availability for use.']
  ramp_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ramp based on its primary operational purpose within the mine infrastructure.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, observations, or special conditions related to the access ramp.']
  speed_limit_kmh varchar [note: 'Maximum permitted vehicle speed on the ramp in kilometers per hour for safety compliance.']
  surface_type varchar [note: 'Type of surface material used on the ramp floor for traction and durability.']
  traffic_direction varchar [note: 'Permitted traffic flow direction on the ramp for operational safety and efficiency.']
  ventilation_type varchar [note: 'Type of ventilation system installed in the ramp for air quality and safety management, particularly relevant for underground ramps.']
  vertical_depth_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'The vertical distance in meters that the ramp descends or ascends, representing the elevation change from surface or reference level.']
  width_meters decimal(6,2) [note: 'The operational width of the ramp in meters, determining the size of equipment that can safely traverse it.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for access_ramp. Referenced by access_ramp_id.'
}

Table processing.plant {
  plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the mineral processing plant facility. Primary key for the processing_plant master register. SSOT for all processing facility master data.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corresponding asset record in the enterprise asset management system (Infor EAM / SAP S/4HANA PM) for this processing plant facility. Enables linkage to maintenance work orders, CAPEX/OPEX tracking, and asset lifecycle management.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Processing plants are delivered through capital projects. Tracks project that constructed/expanded the plant for handover, warranty, defects liability, performance guarantees, and capital cost allocat']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Processing plants are designed and optimized for specific commodities (iron ore, copper, coal). Essential for production planning, capacity allocation, JORC resource reporting, and LOM planning. Domai']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Every processing plant operates under a cost centre for opex tracking, AISC reporting, and variance analysis. processing_plant.opex_cost_centre (plain attr) is denormalized cost_centre_code; replace w']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_permit. Business justification: Processing plants operate under environmental permits governing air emissions, water discharge, and tailings management. Regulatory compliance tracking requires direct linkage between plant operations']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Processing plants are physical assets in the functional location hierarchy. Maintenance planning, work order routing, and asset management require linking plants to their functional location for CMMS ']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Plants send routine process control samples (shift composites, circuit optimization samples) to designated laboratories for analysis. Tracks primary lab relationship for turnaround management and meta']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing Life of Mine (LOM) plan under which this plant\'s design capacity, recovery targets, and throughput schedules are defined. Aligns plant master data with the mine planning SSOT.']
  primary_stakeholder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.stakeholder. Business justification: Processing plants engage with primary affected communities and indigenous groups. Required for stakeholder engagement registers, social licence reporting, community consultation tracking, and regulato']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or operational location where this processing plant is physically situated. Links the plant to its geographic and operational context.']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Processing plants operate under granted mining licences on specific tenements. Required for regulatory compliance reporting, expenditure commitment tracking, and operational boundary verification. Eve']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Plant capital expenditure is tracked via WBS elements for project accounting and asset capitalization. processing_plant.capex_wbs_element (plain attr) is denormalized WBS code; replace with FK for cap']
  aisc_benchmark_usd_per_t decimal(10,2) [note: 'The budgeted or benchmark All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) for processing at this plant expressed in USD per dry metric tonne of ore processed. Used as the financial performance baseline for C1 cash cost reporting and LOM plan economics.']
  annual_capacity_mt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Nameplate annual throughput capacity expressed in million dry metric tonnes per annum (Mtpa). Derived from design_capacity_tph and planned operating hours but stored as a master data attribute for LOM plan alignment and regulatory resource reporting.']
  care_maintenance_date date [note: 'The date on which the plant was placed into care-and-maintenance status. Null if the plant has never been placed on care-and-maintenance. Used for OPEX tracking, insurance, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  comminution_technology varchar [note: 'Primary size-reduction (comminution) technology installed in the plant. SAG (Semi-Autogenous Grinding), HPGR (High Pressure Grinding Rolls), ball mill, rod mill, crusher, AG (Autogenous Grinding), or combined circuit. Drives energy consumption benchmarking and maintenance planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAG|HPGR|ball_mill|rod_mill|crusher|AG|combined — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commissioned_date date [note: 'The date on which the processing plant was formally commissioned and accepted into commercial production. Used for asset depreciation calculations (IFRS 16), LOM plan baseline, and regulatory reporting of production commencement.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which the processing plant is located (e.g., \'AUS\', \'CHL\', \'ZAF\', \'CAN\'). Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (JORC, NI 43-101, SAMREC, MSHA) and environmental reporting obligations.']
  decommissioned_date date [note: 'The date on which the plant was permanently decommissioned and removed from the active asset register. Null if the plant is still operational or on care-and-maintenance. Triggers mine rehabilitation and closure obligations.']
  design_capacity_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Nameplate design throughput capacity of the plant expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (tph) of Run of Mine (ROM) feed. Represents the engineering-rated maximum throughput under optimal conditions. Used as the denominator for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculations.']
  elevation_masl decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation of the processing plant site in metres above sea level (mASL). Relevant for water balance modelling, slurry pipeline hydraulics design, and environmental impact assessments.']
  feed_grade_design_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Design ROM feed head grade (percentage of valuable mineral in the feed ore) for which the plant was engineered. Used as the baseline for metallurgical balance calculations, cut-off grade assessments, and LOM plan modelling.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the processing plant centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for GIS (Geographic Information System) mapping, spatial analysis, and environmental impact zone delineation.']
  lims_plant_code varchar [note: 'The plant identifier used in the LabWare LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System) for sample registration, assay result linkage, and metallurgical accounting. Enables traceability from plant feed to product assay.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the processing plant centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for GIS mapping, spatial analysis, and environmental impact zone delineation.']
  nameplate_recovery_rate_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Design-rated metallurgical recovery rate expressed as a percentage (0–100). Represents the percentage of the target valuable mineral in the feed that is expected to report to the concentrate or product stream under nameplate conditions. Key input to LOM plan financial modelling and JORC/NI 43-101 resource estimation.']
  number_of_processing_lines varchar [note: 'Total count of parallel processing lines (circuits) within the plant. Each line represents an independent processing train capable of treating ore from feed to product. Drives capacity planning, maintenance scheduling, and redundancy analysis.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the processing plant. \'active\' = fully operational; \'care-and-maintenance\' = temporarily idle but preserved; \'decommissioned\' = permanently closed; \'commissioning\' = in start-up phase; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted due to regulatory or operational reasons.']
  ore_type_compatibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ore/commodity types this plant is designed and certified to process (e.g., \'iron ore, copper, gold, lithium, nickel, coal, diamonds\'). Drives feed stream routing and LOM plan alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iron ore|copper|gold|lithium|nickel|coal|diamonds|zinc|lead|manganese — promote to reference product]']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the processing plant, used across ERP, SCADA, and FMS systems (e.g., \'PP-PILBARA-01\', \'PROC-CU-02\'). Unique across the enterprise and referenced in operational reporting.']
  plant_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the processing plant including key design features, processing circuit configuration, historical context, and any notable operational characteristics. Supports regulatory submissions, investor reports, and technical documentation.']
  plant_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the mineral processing plant facility (e.g., \'Pilbara Iron Ore Processing Plant 1\', \'Olympic Dam Copper Concentrator\'). Used in reports, dashboards, and regulatory submissions.']
  plant_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the processing plant by metallurgical process family. \'concentrator\' = physical separation to produce mineral concentrate; \'hydrometallurgical\' = chemical leaching/SX-EW; \'pyrometallurgical\' = smelting/roasting; \'beneficiation\' = upgrading without full extraction; \'refinery\' = final product refining; \'pelletising\' = iron ore pellet production.']
  power_consumption_design_kw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Nameplate design electrical power consumption in kilowatts (kW) at full throughput. Used for energy cost modelling (OPEX), carbon footprint reporting (GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement), and grid capacity planning.']
  primary_processing_method varchar [note: 'The principal mineral processing and beneficiation method employed by this plant. Comminution includes SAG/HPGR milling; flotation covers froth flotation separation; leaching includes heap and tank leaching; SX-EW (Solvent Extraction Electrowinning) for copper cathode production; DMS (Dense Media Separation) for coal/diamonds; gravity and magnetic separation for iron ore. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: comminution|flotation|leaching|SX-EW|DMS|gravity|magnetic — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  product_type varchar [note: 'The primary saleable product form produced by this plant (e.g., \'concentrate\' for copper/gold flotation plants, \'cathode\' for SX-EW copper plants, \'pellet\' for iron ore pelletising, \'fines\'/\'lump\' for iron ore beneficiation, \'doré\' for gold/silver). Drives sales contract and logistics configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concentrate|cathode|pellet|fines|lump|slurry|doré — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this processing plant master record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and regulatory record-keeping obligations.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this processing plant master record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  sap_functional_location varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance (PM) functional location code assigned to this processing plant. Enables linkage to maintenance work orders, preventive maintenance plans, and CAPEX/OPEX cost centre reporting in SAP.']
  scada_system_code varchar [note: 'The identifier of the SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) system instance or node that monitors and controls this processing plant. Used to link real-time process historian data (OSIsoft PI System) to the plant master record.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or territory in which the processing plant is located (e.g., \'Western Australia\', \'British Columbia\', \'Antofagasta\'). Used for jurisdictional regulatory compliance, environmental permitting, and geographic reporting.']
  tailings_generation_design_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Nameplate design rate of tailings (gangue and process residue) generated by the plant in dry metric tonnes per hour. Drives TSF (Tailings Storage Facility) capacity planning, environmental licence conditions, and ICMM tailings governance compliance.']
  target_product_grade_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Design target grade of the primary valuable mineral in the final product expressed as a percentage (e.g., 28.5% Cu in copper concentrate, 62% Fe in iron ore lump). Used as the benchmark for metallurgical performance assessment and sales contract compliance.']
  water_consumption_design_m3ph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nameplate design water consumption rate in cubic metres per hour (m³/h) for the plant under full throughput conditions. Critical for water balance management, environmental licence compliance, and EIS (Environmental Impact Statement) reporting.']

  Note: 'Master register of all mineral processing plant facilities. Captures plant identity, site location, design capacity (tph), commissioned date, primary processing method (comminution, flotation, leaching, SX-EW, DMS, gravity, magnetic), ore type compatibility (iron ore, copper, gold, lithium, nickel, coal, diamonds), operational status (active, care-and-maintenance, decommissioned), number of processing lines, and nameplate recovery rate. SSOT for all processing facility master data. Parent entity for circuits, feed streams, and all operational records.'
}

Table processing.circuit {
  circuit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a processing circuit record within the mineral processing plant. Primary key for the processing_circuit data product.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Circuits are engineered for specific commodities with tailored flowsheets and reagent schemes. Critical for metallurgical accounting, recovery optimization, and circuit performance reporting. Replaces']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Circuits are maintainable functional locations within plant hierarchy. Maintenance strategies, PM schedules, and work orders are assigned to circuit-level functional locations for granular maintenance']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan under which this circuit\'s design parameters and recovery targets were established. Enables traceability of circuit design basis to the governing mine plan.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant in which this circuit operates. Links circuit-level data to plant-level operational context.']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Processing circuits (flotation, leaching, SXEW, DMS) have distinct hazard profiles requiring dedicated risk assessments. Flotation uses chemical reagents, leaching uses cyanide/acid, SXEW involves hig']
  upstream_circuit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on circuit (upstream_circuit_id)']
  circuit_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the processing circuit within the plant flowsheet (e.g., PC-SAG-01, FL-RGH-02). Used in P&ID documentation, SCADA tags, and operational reporting.']
  circuit_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the processing circuit including its role in the plant flowsheet, key equipment, and any notable design features or operational constraints. Supports engineering documentation and onboarding.']
  circuit_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the processing circuit (e.g., Primary SAG Mill Circuit, Rougher Flotation Circuit, Leach Circuit 1). Used in operational dashboards and reporting.']
  circuit_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the processing circuit. Drives scheduling, maintenance planning, and production reporting inclusion/exclusion.']
  circuit_type varchar [note: 'Category of mineral processing technology employed in this circuit. Drives applicable KPIs, reagent profiles, and recovery benchmarks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: comminution|flotation|leaching|gravity_separation|dense_media_separation|sx_ew|thickening|filtration|classification — promote to reference product]']
