  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the processing circuit was formally commissioned and accepted into operational service. Used for asset age calculations, warranty tracking, and depreciation under IFRS.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the processing circuit was or is planned to be permanently decommissioned. Nullable for active circuits. Used for mine closure planning, asset write-off scheduling, and rehabilitation cost provisioning.']
  design_availability_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Design basis mechanical availability of the circuit expressed as a percentage of scheduled operating hours. Used as the denominator in OEE calculations and production planning. Derived from MTBF and MTTR estimates at design stage.']
  design_feed_size_p80_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Design basis P80 particle size of the feed material entering this circuit in millimetres. P80 represents the 80th percentile passing size. Critical for comminution circuit design and mill sizing.']
  design_ph_target decimal(4,2) [note: 'Design basis target pH value for the circuit process stream. Applicable to flotation (pH control for selectivity), leaching (acid/alkaline conditions), and SX-EW circuits. Drives lime/acid reagent consumption.']
  design_product_size_p80_mm decimal(8,3) [note: 'Design basis P80 particle size of the product leaving this circuit in millimetres. Defines the target grind size for comminution circuits and classification efficiency for downstream separation circuits.']
  design_residence_time_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'Design basis residence time of material within the circuit expressed in minutes. Determines tank/vessel sizing and is a key parameter for leach kinetics, flotation recovery, and thickener design.']
  design_slurry_density_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Design basis slurry density expressed as percentage solids by weight for circuits handling slurry streams. Critical for pump sizing, pipe design, and process control setpoints.']
  design_throughput_mtpa decimal(12,3) [note: 'Annualised nameplate design throughput capacity expressed in million dry metric tonnes per annum (Mtpa). Used for LOM plan alignment, capital project justification, and annual production reporting.']
  design_throughput_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Nameplate design throughput capacity of the circuit expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (tph). Represents the engineering design basis used for LOM plan benchmarking and OEE calculations.']
  design_utilisation_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Design basis utilisation rate of the circuit expressed as a percentage of available hours actually used for production. Combined with availability to derive OEE. Accounts for planned feed interruptions and scheduled downtime.']
  design_water_consumption_m3pt decimal(8,4) [note: 'Design basis fresh water consumption rate for this circuit expressed in cubic metres per dry metric tonne of feed processed. Used for water balance modelling, environmental licence compliance, and OPEX planning.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the current circuit configuration, design parameters, and targets are effective. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) management and historical performance benchmarking against the correct design basis.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which the current circuit configuration and design parameters are effective. Null for the current active version. Enables historical tracking of circuit design changes and target revisions over the LOM.']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the environmental operating permit or licence governing this circuit\'s operations (e.g., water use licence, air emission permit). Required for regulatory compliance reporting under ISO 14001 and EIS obligations.']
  feed_material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary feed material entering this circuit (e.g., ROM Ore for primary comminution, slurry for flotation). Determines applicable feed quality parameters and circuit design basis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rom_ore|crushed_ore|ground_ore|concentrate|slurry|leach_solution|tailings|recycled_stream — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  flowsheet_sequence_order varchar [note: 'Integer position of this circuit within the overall plant processing flowsheet. Defines the order of material flow from feed preparation through to final product, enabling flowsheet visualisation and mass balance calculations.']
  installed_power_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total installed electrical power capacity of the circuit in kilowatts (kW). Used for energy consumption benchmarking, OPEX modelling, and specific energy intensity calculations (kWh/t).']
  is_primary_circuit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this circuit is the primary (first-pass) processing circuit in the plant flowsheet as opposed to a secondary, scavenger, or retreatment circuit. Used for production reporting hierarchy and mass balance prioritisation.']
  number_of_units varchar [note: 'Count of parallel processing units (e.g., mills, flotation cells, leach tanks) operating within this circuit. Used for availability calculations, maintenance scheduling, and throughput modelling.']
  pi_af_element_path varchar [note: 'Full Asset Framework (AF) element path in the OSIsoft PI System for this processing circuit (e.g., \\Server\Plant\MillCircuit\SAG01). Enables direct linkage between the data product and real-time process historian data.']
  reagent_scheme_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the standard reagent addition scheme applied in this circuit (e.g., collector, frother, pH modifier for flotation; acid type for leaching). Links to reagent consumption planning and OPEX budgeting.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this processing circuit record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this processing circuit record was last modified. Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the process engineer accountable for the design, optimisation, and performance of this circuit. Used for accountability tracking and escalation routing.']
  scada_tag_prefix varchar [note: 'Root tag prefix used in the SCADA/DCS system (OSIsoft PI) to identify all process data points associated with this circuit (e.g., PLANT1.SAG01). Enables automated data ingestion from the PI historian into the lakehouse.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Specific processing sub-type within the circuit type category (e.g., SAG Mill under comminution, Rougher under flotation, CIL under leaching). Enables granular performance benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sag_mill|ball_mill|hpgr|rod_mill|rougher|scavenger|cleaner|recleaner|heap_leach|vat_leach|cil|cip|dms|jig|spiral|electrowinning|solvent_extraction — promote to reference product]']
  target_feed_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'LOM plan target head grade of the feed material entering this circuit expressed as a percentage of the primary commodity. Used for cut-off grade optimisation and circuit performance benchmarking.']
  target_product_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'LOM plan target grade of the circuit output product expressed as a percentage of the primary commodity (e.g., concentrate grade for flotation, cathode purity for SX-EW). Used for sales contract compliance and resource reporting.']
  target_recovery_rate_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'LOM plan target metallurgical recovery rate for the primary commodity expressed as a percentage (e.g., 87.500 for 87.5%). Represents the expected proportion of valuable mineral extracted relative to feed grade. Basis for reserve reporting under JORC/CIM.']

  Note: 'Defines individual processing circuits within a plant (e.g., primary comminution, secondary grinding, rougher flotation, cleaner flotation, leach circuit, SX-EW circuit). Captures circuit type, feed material, design throughput, target recovery rate, and sequence order within the processing flowsheet. Enables circuit-level performance tracking.'
}

Table processing.feed_stream {
  feed_stream_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each ore feed stream record entering the processing plant. Primary key for the feed_stream data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the primary conveyor or feeder equipment delivering material to this feed stream. Links to the equipment asset register for maintenance scheduling and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) tracking.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG/Ball Mill circuit, DMS circuit, flotation circuit) that this feed stream feeds into. Enables circuit-level mass balance and throughput analysis.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Feed streams carry specific commodities from mine to plant. Fundamental for grade control, blending decisions, and feed scheduling. Replaces denormalized commodity text field with proper FK for refere']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the LOM (Life of Mine) plan version that defines the target feed parameters for this stream. Enables variance analysis between planned and actual feed characteristics for production reconciliation.']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Feed streams originate from specific orebodies. Mine-to-mill optimization and metallurgical reconciliation require tracking which orebody supplies each feed stream to correlate geological characterist']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the mineral processing plant or facility that this feed stream supplies. Supports multi-plant operations and mass balance reporting at plant level.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Feed streams must reference production schedules to track which mine production periods feed which processing campaigns. Essential for ore type scheduling, blend optimization, and integrated mine-mill']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Feed streams originate from specific prospects/orebodies. Critical for mine-to-mill optimization, ore tracking, metallurgical reconciliation, and LOM planning. Real business need: tracking ore source ']
  rom_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the specific physical source location (ROM pad, stockpile, crusher station) from which this feed stream originates. Links to the plant location or stockpile master.']
  source_tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Feed streams originate from ore extracted from specific tenements. Essential for royalty calculations, grade reconciliation against tenement reserves, and compliance with tenement-specific production ']
  abrasion_index decimal(6,3) [note: 'Bond Abrasion Index (Ai) of the ore in the feed stream, a dimensionless measure of ore abrasiveness. Drives liner and media wear rate predictions for SAG mills, ball mills, and HPGR (High Pressure Grinding Rolls), informing maintenance scheduling and OPEX forecasting.']
  bond_work_index_kwh_t decimal(7,3) [note: 'Bond Ball Mill Work Index (BWi) of the ore expressed in kilowatt-hours per tonne (kWh/t). Measures ore hardness and grindability; used to predict mill power requirements, throughput, and energy consumption in comminution circuit design and optimisation.']
  bulk_density_t_m3 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Bulk density of the ore material in the feed stream expressed in tonnes per cubic metre (t/m³). Used to convert volumetric flow measurements to mass flow for mass balance calculations and stockpile volume reconciliation.']
  commissioned_date date [note: 'The date on which this feed stream was formally commissioned and accepted into operational service. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, warranty management, and CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) project closure reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this feed stream record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for data lineage and record provenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  cut_off_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The minimum economic grade threshold (cut-off grade) applied to material entering this feed stream. Material below cut-off grade is classified as waste and diverted to the waste dump. Defined per LOM plan and commodity economics.']
  design_capacity_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum rated throughput capacity of the feed stream infrastructure (conveyor, feeder, chute) in dry metric tonnes per hour. Used for bottleneck analysis and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculations.']
  dilution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of waste material mixed with ore in the feed stream due to mining dilution. Dilution reduces the effective head grade and is tracked for reconciliation between geological resource model grades and actual plant feed grades.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this feed stream definition and its associated parameters (nominal rate, target grade, processing route) became operationally effective. Supports temporal versioning of feed stream configurations aligned to LOM plan revisions.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this feed stream definition ceases to be operationally effective. Null indicates the stream is currently active with no planned end date. Supports LOM plan stage transitions and plant reconfiguration tracking.']
  feed_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature of the ore or slurry in the feed stream measured in degrees Celsius. Relevant for leaching circuits (SX-EW), autoclave operations, and cold-climate operations where freezing affects conveyor and crusher performance.']
  gangue_content_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage of waste rock (gangue) minerals in the feed stream material. High gangue content increases dilution, reduces effective head grade, and impacts reagent consumption in flotation and leaching circuits.']
  head_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The assay-determined grade of the primary valuable mineral in the feed stream expressed as a percentage (e.g., % Fe for iron ore, % Cu for copper). Head grade is the key input for recovery rate calculations and production reconciliation against the LOM plan.']
  moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content of the ore in the feed stream expressed as a percentage of total wet mass. Moisture affects conveyor performance, crusher throughput, mill power draw, and dry tonne calculations for mass balance and invoicing.']
  nominal_feed_rate_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'The design or nameplate feed rate for this stream expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (tph). Represents the target throughput capacity used in LOM (Life of Mine) plan scheduling and plant design. Distinct from actual measured feed rate.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Geological ore type classification of the material in this feed stream (e.g., oxide, sulphide, transitional, fresh, weathered). Ore type drives processing route selection, reagent dosing, and expected recovery rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oxide|sulphide|transitional|fresh|weathered|mixed|high_grade|low_grade — promote to reference product]']
  p50_particle_size_mm decimal(8,3) [note: 'The 50th percentile (median) passing particle size of the feed stream material in millimetres. Complements P80 to characterise the full particle size distribution (PSD) shape, supporting comminution circuit modelling and screen efficiency analysis.']
  p80_particle_size_mm decimal(8,3) [note: 'The 80th percentile passing particle size of the feed stream material expressed in millimetres (mm). P80 is the industry-standard metric for characterising particle size distribution (PSD) in comminution circuits. Critical input for SAG mill and HPGR (High Pressure Grinding Rolls) performance modelling.']
  pi_tag_feed_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'The OSIsoft PI System historian tag name for the real-time feed rate measurement instrument (belt scale or flow meter) associated with this feed stream. Enables direct linkage between the data product and the SCADA/PI process historian for real-time and historical data retrieval.']
  pi_tag_particle_size varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI System historian tag name for the online particle size analyser (PSA) instrument monitoring P80 for this feed stream. Supports real-time comminution circuit control and automated variance alerting.']
  processing_route varchar [note: 'The designated mineral processing route for material in this feed stream (e.g., SAG/Ball Mill comminution, DMS (Dense Media Separation), flotation, leaching, SX-EW (Solvent Extraction Electrowinning)). Determines downstream circuit routing and reagent requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sag_ball_mill|hpgr|dms|flotation|leaching|sx_ew|gravity|magnetic_separation|cil|cip — promote to reference product]']
  slurry_density_t_m3 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Density of the ore-water slurry mixture in the feed stream expressed in tonnes per cubic metre. Applicable to wet processing circuits (SAG mill discharge, flotation feed). Monitored via OSIsoft PI System for real-time process control.']
  slurry_solids_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of solids by mass in the slurry feed stream (percent solids). A key process control parameter for wet grinding and flotation circuits; directly affects mill power draw, pump performance, and flotation cell residence time.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical origin point from which ore material enters this feed stream. ROM (Run of Mine) pad indicates direct truck tip; stockpile indicates reclaimed material; crusher indicates post-comminution feed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rom_pad|primary_crusher|secondary_crusher|tertiary_crusher|stockpile|reclaim_conveyor|direct_tip|sag_discharge — promote to reference product]']
  stream_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the feed stream within the processing plant configuration (e.g., FS-ROM-001, FS-CRU-002). Used in mass balance reconciliation and plant P&ID references.']
  stream_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name of the feed stream (e.g., \'Primary ROM Feed\', \'SAG Mill Discharge\', \'Crusher Product Recirculating Load\'). Used in operational dashboards and reporting.']
  stream_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the feed stream definition within the processing plant. Active streams are currently receiving material; suspended streams are temporarily offline; decommissioned streams are permanently retired from the circuit.']
  stream_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the feed stream by its position and role in the comminution circuit. ROM (Run of Mine) feed is direct from the ROM pad; crushed feed is post-primary crusher; recirculating load is material returned for regrinding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rom_feed|crushed_feed|recirculating_load|classifier_overflow|classifier_underflow|screen_oversize|sag_discharge|ball_mill_discharge — promote to reference product]']
  target_feed_rate_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'The planned or scheduled feed rate for this stream in dry metric tonnes per hour as defined in the current production schedule or LOM plan. Distinct from nominal (design) feed rate; used for shift-level production planning and variance reporting.']
  target_head_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The planned head grade of the primary valuable mineral for this feed stream as per the current production schedule or LOM plan. Used for production reconciliation and variance analysis against actual assayed head grade.']
  target_p80_mm decimal(8,3) [note: 'The planned P80 particle size target for this feed stream in millimetres as specified in the processing plant design or LOM plan. Used to assess crusher and mill performance against design specifications.']
  top_size_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum lump size of material in the feed stream expressed in millimetres. Defines the upper bound of the particle size distribution and is critical for crusher gap settings, conveyor design, and mill liner protection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this feed stream record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data loading, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail requirements in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Defines ore feed streams entering the processing plant including ROM feed, crushed feed, and recirculating loads. Captures stream identity, source (ROM pad, crusher, stockpile), nominal feed rate (tph), particle size distribution (P80), moisture content, and ore type classification. Supports mass balance and throughput tracking.'
}

Table processing.shift_production_run {
  shift_production_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each shift production run record in the processing plant. Primary key for this transactional entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete operational shift event with a clear lifecycle in the mineral processing domain.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Shift-level production costs (labour, consumables, utilities) are allocated to cost centres for unit cost calculation, AISC reporting, and budget variance analysis. Core mining cost accounting process']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operating crew assigned to this production shift. Used for crew performance tracking, shift handover management, and workforce productivity analytics. Links to the crew or workforce master.']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Incidents occur during production shifts; linking enables shift-level incident analysis, LTIFR/TRIFR calculation by production period, and correlation of incident frequency with operational conditions']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Shift samples are dispatched to specific laboratories for analysis. Essential for tracking sample turnaround times, metallurgical accounting, and reconciling lab results with production data for perfo']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version against which this shift production run is being reconciled. Enables tracking of actual production performance versus the approved LOM plan targets for throughput, grade, and recovery.']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Production runs frequently trigger corrective maintenance work orders when equipment issues or process deviations are detected during shift operations. Links shift performance problems to maintenance ']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Shift production runs process ore from specific orebodies. Operational reporting, grade reconciliation, and metallurgical accounting require linking production shifts to source orebody to compare actu']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mineral processing plant where this shift production run occurred. Links to the plant master reference. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category (resource reference to the processing facility). Used for plant-level production aggregation and benchmarking.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Processing shift runs must align with mine production schedules for integrated mine-to-mill optimization, bottleneck analysis, and shift-level production reconciliation. Essential for real-time produc']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Production runs process ore from specific prospects. Essential for reconciliation between resource model predictions and actual metallurgical performance. Real business need: grade control, resource m']
  rom_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.rom_stockpile. Business justification: Processing shift runs track feed source from ROM stockpiles for inventory management, feed scheduling, and stockpile depletion tracking. Essential for real-time feed management and stockpile inventory']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Shifts produce specific saleable products (concentrate grades, cathodes). Essential for production reconciliation, inventory management, and linking shift output to sales contracts. Natural business r']
  source_tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Production runs process ore from specific tenements. Required for royalty payment calculations, tenement-specific statutory production reporting, and reconciliation of tenement reserves against actual']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the shift supervisor responsible for sign-off on this production run record. Required for accountability, regulatory compliance, and shift handover documentation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Every processing shift generates tailings sent to specific TSF. Required for material flow tracking, mass balance reconciliation, TSF capacity planning, and water balance closure. Fundamental operatio']
  actual_throughput_rate_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual average processing rate achieved during the shift, expressed in dry tonnes per hour (tph). Calculated as tonnes processed divided by operating hours. Compared against design throughput rate for performance gap analysis.']
  calendar_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of hours in the shift period as defined by the site calendar (typically 12 hours for a standard shift). Used as the denominator for plant utilisation and OEE calculations. Expressed in decimal hours.']
  circuit_availability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of calendar hours during which the processing circuit was available for production, calculated as (calendar hours minus planned and unplanned downtime) divided by calendar hours, expressed as a percentage. Key KPI for processing plant performance against LOM plan targets.']
  concentrate_produced_tonnes decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dry tonnes of mineral concentrate or final processed product produced during the shift. Used for production accounting, inventory management, and sales dispatch planning. Sourced from product weighbridge or belt scale measurements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shift production run record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring of data ingestion from OSIsoft PI System and MES. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  design_throughput_rate_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nameplate or design throughput rate of the processing plant circuit, expressed in dry tonnes per hour (tph), as specified in the LOM plan and plant design documentation. Used as the benchmark for actual throughput performance assessment.']
  feed_blend_oxide_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of oxide ore type in the ROM feed blend during the shift. Part of the feed blend composition tracking used to predict and explain metallurgical recovery variations and circuit performance. Sourced from grade control block data and ROM stockpile management.']
  feed_blend_sulphide_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sulphide ore type in the ROM feed blend during the shift. Used alongside oxide percentage to characterise the feed blend composition for metallurgical performance analysis and reagent dosage optimisation.']
  feed_grade_element varchar [note: 'Chemical element symbol for which the feed head grade is reported (e.g., Fe for iron ore, Cu for copper, Li for lithium). Enables multi-commodity processing plants to correctly attribute grade measurements to the target mineral. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Fe|Cu|Au|Ag|Li|Ni|Co|Zn|Pb|Mo — promote to reference product]']
  feed_head_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Assayed head grade of the primary valuable element (e.g., Fe%, Cu%, Li%) in the ROM feed during the shift, expressed as a percentage. Sourced from LabWare LIMS composite sample assay results. Critical input for metallurgical recovery calculations and LOM plan reconciliation.']
  feed_moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content of the ROM feed expressed as a percentage of wet weight, measured by on-belt moisture analyser or laboratory sample. Used to convert wet tonnes to dry tonnes for metallurgical accounting and mass balance calculations.']
  metallurgical_recovery_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the valuable element in the ROM feed that is successfully recovered into the final concentrate or product during the shift. Calculated from feed grade, product grade, and mass balance. Core KPI for processing efficiency and LOM plan reconciliation.']
  oee_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall Equipment Effectiveness expressed as a percentage, combining availability, performance, and quality rate for the processing plant during the shift. Industry-standard KPI for processing plant productivity benchmarking against LOM plan targets. Sourced from OSIsoft PI System.']
  operating_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Actual number of hours the processing plant was in productive operation during the shift, excluding planned and unplanned downtime. Used in OEE calculation and throughput rate derivation. Expressed in decimal hours.']
  planned_downtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Hours of scheduled downtime during the shift for planned maintenance, inspections, or scheduled shutdowns. Used in circuit availability and OEE calculations. Sourced from Infor EAM work order schedules.']
  plant_utilisation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of available hours during which the plant was actively processing ore, calculated as operating hours divided by available hours. Reflects how effectively available plant time is being used for production. Reported in daily production meetings.']
  primary_ore_type varchar [note: 'Dominant ore type or lithological classification of the ROM feed processed during the shift (e.g., oxide, sulphide, mixed, transitional, fresh). Determines the applicable processing circuit configuration and expected metallurgical recovery. Sourced from Geovia GEMS geological model and grade control data.']
  processing_circuit_type varchar [note: 'Primary processing circuit configuration active during the shift. Identifies the comminution and separation technology applied (e.g., SAG/ball mill, HPGR, DMS, flotation, leach, SX-EW). Used for circuit-level performance benchmarking and maintenance planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAG_ball_mill|HPGR|DMS|flotation|leach|SX_EW|gravity|magnetic_separation — promote to reference product]']
  product_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Assayed grade of the primary valuable element in the final concentrate or product produced during the shift, expressed as a percentage. Sourced from LabWare LIMS product sample assay. Used for product quality certification, sales contract compliance, and metallurgical accounting.']
  rom_feed_tonnes_dry decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dry tonnes of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed to the processing plant during the shift, calculated by applying the measured moisture factor to wet tonnes. Dry tonnes are the standard basis for metallurgical accounting, resource reporting, and LOM plan reconciliation.']
  rom_feed_tonnes_wet decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total wet tonnes of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed to the processing plant during the shift, as measured at the feed conveyor belt scale. Wet weight includes moisture content. Used for mass balance and throughput reporting.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shift production run record. \'open\' indicates the shift is in progress; \'completed\' indicates shift has ended and data is captured; \'under_review\' indicates supervisor review pending; \'approved\' indicates shift supervisor sign-off complete; \'cancelled\' indicates the shift was voided. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the production shift commenced. Used as the primary date dimension for daily production reporting, reconciliation against LOM plan targets, and regulatory reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category (date-level precision).']
  shift_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the production shift concluded, sourced from the MES or OSIsoft PI System historian. Used for shift duration calculation, handover documentation, and OEE reporting. ISO 8601 format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  shift_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes recorded by the shift supervisor covering significant events, process upsets, equipment issues, safety observations, or other contextual information relevant to the shift production run. Supports shift handover communication and exception reporting.']
  shift_number varchar [note: 'Designates the shift rotation within the calendar day: \'day\' (typically 06:00–18:00), \'night\' (18:00–06:00), or \'afternoon\' (14:00–22:00) depending on site roster configuration. Used for shift-based production reporting and crew scheduling.']
  shift_run_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the shift production run, used in daily production reports, shift handover documentation, and reconciliation against LOM plan targets. Format: SPR-YYYY-MM-DD-PLANTCODE-SEQ. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  shift_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the production shift commenced, sourced from the MES or OSIsoft PI System historian. Used for accurate shift duration calculation, OEE computation, and integration with SCADA event logs. ISO 8601 format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  supervisor_signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shift supervisor formally approved and signed off the shift production run record. Marks the transition from \'under_review\' to \'approved\' status. Required for daily production reporting and audit compliance.']
  tonnes_processed decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dry tonnes of ore that completed the primary processing circuit during the shift. May differ from ROM feed tonnes due to in-circuit inventory changes, spillage, or sampling losses. Used as the primary throughput metric for production reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.']
  unplanned_downtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Hours of unscheduled downtime during the shift due to equipment breakdowns, process upsets, or emergency stoppages. Key input for MTBF and MTTR analysis and OEE degradation reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shift production run record was last modified, including supervisor sign-off, data corrections, or status transitions. Supports audit trail and change tracking in the Silver layer. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather condition at the mine site during the shift that may have affected processing plant operations (e.g., extreme heat impacting reagent performance, rain increasing feed moisture, dust storms affecting conveyor operations). Used for operational exception reporting and HSE compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|storm|fog|high_wind|dust_storm|snow|extreme_heat — promote to reference product]']
  weather_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether adverse weather conditions materially impacted processing plant operations or throughput during the shift. Used to qualify production shortfalls in reporting and distinguish weather-related downtime from equipment or process failures.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each processing plant production shift — the operational heartbeat of the processing domain. Captures shift date, shift number (day/night/afternoon), plant ID, crew ID, tonnes fed (ROM dry and wet), tonnes processed, circuit availability (%), plant utilisation (%), OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness), feed grade (head grade by element), operating hours, calendar hours, actual throughput rate (tph), design throughput rate (tph), feed blend composition (ore type percentages), primary ore type, weather conditions affecting operations, and shift supervisor sign-off. Sourced from OSIsoft PI System historian and MES (Manufacturing Execution System). Serves as the single source of truth for shift and daily production reporting against LOM plan targets. All circuit-level process events, reagent consumption, utility consumption, and operational exception records link back to this shift record via FK. Supports daily production meetings, weekly/monthly reporting, and annual reconciliation.'
}

Table processing.unit_operation_event {
  unit_operation_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each unit operation event record in the mineral processing domain. Primary key for the silver-layer lakehouse table.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary processing equipment asset (e.g., SAG mill, ball mill, flotation cell, leach tank) involved in this unit operation event.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill circuit, flotation circuit, leach circuit) where this unit operation event occurred. Links to the circuit master.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Unit operations (crushing, grinding, separation) process specific commodities. Required for metallurgical accounting by commodity type, recovery tracking, and operational performance analysis. Core to']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Individual unit operations (mills, flotation cells, leach tanks) are cost-tracked for activity-based costing, energy consumption analysis, and process optimization ROI. Standard process costing practi']
  feed_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: Unit operations process specific feed streams. One event processes one feed stream; one feed stream has many events. This links the operation event to the feed stream being processed.']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Unit operation events (comminution, separation) often generate maintenance work orders when equipment performance degrades or failures occur. Links operational events to maintenance response for equip']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Comminution and processing unit operations track throughput and energy consumption against scheduled mine production for bottleneck analysis, cost allocation, and integrated mine-mill performance opti']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Unit operations process ore from specific prospects. Required for metallurgical domain modeling, recovery prediction by ore type, and process optimization. Real business need: ore-specific comminution']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this unit operation event was recorded. Enables shift-level metallurgical accounting and performance reporting.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Comminution/grinding events produce tailings streams directed to specific TSF. Essential for metallurgical accounting, TSF deposition planning, and environmental compliance reporting. Tracks material ']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for process operators or metallurgical accountants to record observations, abnormal conditions, equipment issues, or data quality notes associated with this unit operation event. Sourced from MES shift log entries.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this unit operation event record was first captured and written to the silver-layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  current_density_a_per_m2 decimal(8,3) [note: 'Electrical current density applied in electrowinning (EW) cells during SX-EW unit operations, expressed in A/m². Type-specific parameter for electrowinning operations. Controls cathode deposition rate, product purity, and specific energy consumption.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this unit operation event data was sourced. Supports data lineage, quality assessment, and reconciliation between SCADA historian (OSIsoft PI System), MES, and LabWare LIMS.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world timestamp when this unit operation event concluded, as recorded by the SCADA historian. Null if the event is still active. Used to calculate operating duration and throughput rates.']
  event_reference_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable externally-known business identifier for this unit operation event, used in metallurgical reports, process control logs, and MES reconciliation. Format: UOE-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world timestamp when this unit operation event commenced, as recorded by the SCADA historian (OSIsoft PI System). Used as the principal business event time for metallurgical accounting period alignment.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the unit operation event record. \'active\' indicates the shift event is in progress; \'completed\' indicates normal shift closure; \'aborted\' indicates unplanned stoppage; \'suspended\' indicates planned hold; \'pending_review\' indicates metallurgical accountant review required.']
  feed_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Head grade of the valuable mineral in the feed material expressed as a percentage (e.g., % Cu, % Fe, % Li2O). Sourced from online analysers or composite assay samples. Critical for recovery calculation and LOM plan grade reconciliation.']
  feed_tonnage_dry_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Dry metric tonnes of ore or material fed to the unit operation during the event period. Core throughput metric for metallurgical accounting and LOM plan variance analysis. Sourced from belt scale or flow meter readings in OSIsoft PI System.']
  froth_depth_mm decimal(7,2) [note: 'Depth of the froth layer in flotation cells, expressed in millimetres. Type-specific parameter for flotation unit operations (rougher, scavenger, cleaner). Controlled variable for concentrate grade and recovery optimisation. Sourced from ultrasonic level sensors via OSIsoft PI System.']
  is_lom_plan_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this unit operation event falls within an active LOM (Life of Mine) plan period with defined targets, enabling filtering of events for plan vs actual variance reporting.']
  leach_solution_grade_g_per_l decimal(8,4) [note: 'Concentration of the target metal in the pregnant leach solution (PLS), expressed in grams per litre. Type-specific parameter for heap, tank, vat, CIL, and CIP leaching operations. Used in leach circuit mass balance and SX-EW feed quality assessment.']
  lom_plan_feed_tonnage_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'LOM (Life of Mine) plan target for feed tonnage to this unit operation for the corresponding shift/period. Used for variance analysis between actual and planned throughput. Sourced from Deswik or Hexagon MinePlan scheduling outputs.']
  lom_plan_recovery_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'LOM (Life of Mine) plan target metallurgical recovery percentage for this unit operation and period. Used for variance analysis between actual and planned recovery. Sourced from process design basis and LOM plan assumptions.']
  medium_density_kg_per_m3 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Density of the dense medium (ferrosilicon or magnetite suspension) used in Dense Media Separation (DMS) unit operations, expressed in kg/m³. Type-specific parameter for DMS operations. Controls separation cut-point and product quality.']
  metallurgical_account_period varchar [note: 'The calendar month period (YYYY-MM) to which this unit operation event is assigned for metallurgical accounting and financial reporting purposes. Used to aggregate shift-level events into monthly mass balance reconciliation reports.']
  operating_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total hours the unit operation was in active production during the event period. Excludes planned and unplanned downtime. Used in OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation and availability reporting.']
  operation_subtype varchar [note: 'Detailed sub-classification of the unit operation within its type (e.g., SAG mill, ball mill, HPGR, crusher for comminution; rougher, scavenger, cleaner for flotation; heap, tank, vat, CIL, CIP for leaching; solvent extraction, electrowinning for SX-EW; spirals, shaking tables, jigs for gravity). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sag_mill|ball_mill|hpgr|crusher|rougher|scavenger|cleaner|heap_leach|tank_leach|vat_leach|cil|cip|solvent_extraction|electrowinning|spirals|shaking_tables|jigs|dense_media_separation|magnetic_separation|thickener|filter — promote to reference product]']
  operation_type varchar [note: 'Type discriminator identifying the class of mineral processing unit operation for this event. Drives which type-specific attribute groups are populated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: comminution|flotation|leaching|sx_ew|dms|gravity_separation|magnetic_separation|thickening_dewatering — promote to reference product]']
  p80_feed_microns decimal(10,2) [note: '80th percentile passing size of the feed material in microns (µm). Primary comminution circuit feed size parameter used to characterise ore hardness, set mill operating conditions, and benchmark against design P80. Applicable to comminution operation types (SAG, ball mill, HPGR, crusher).']
  p80_product_microns decimal(10,2) [note: '80th percentile passing size of the product material in microns (µm) exiting the comminution unit operation. Compared against target grind size to assess liberation efficiency and downstream flotation or leach performance.']
  plant_area_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the physical plant area or section within the processing facility where this unit operation is located (e.g., COMM-01 for comminution area 1, FLOT-02 for flotation area 2). Aligns with P&ID area designations.']
  power_consumption_kwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total electrical energy consumed by the unit operation during the event period, expressed in kWh. Used in energy cost allocation, OPEX reporting, and carbon footprint calculations. Sourced from power meters via OSIsoft PI System.']
  product_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Grade of the valuable mineral in the product or concentrate expressed as a percentage. For flotation concentrates this is the concentrate grade; for leach circuits this is the solution grade. Used in metallurgical accounting and concentrate quality reporting.']
  product_tonnage_dry_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Dry metric tonnes of product (concentrate, leach solution, or processed material) produced by the unit operation during the event period. Used in metallurgical accounting mass balance calculations.']
  reagent_dosage_g_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Total reagent addition rate for the primary reagent in the unit operation, expressed in grams per dry metric tonne of feed. Covers collectors, frothers, depressants (flotation), or lixiviants (leaching). Used in reagent cost tracking and process optimisation.']
  recovery_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the valuable mineral in the feed that is recovered into the product stream during this unit operation event. Calculated as (product mass × product grade) / (feed mass × feed grade) × 100. Core KPI for process performance and LOM plan variance.']
  slurry_density_kg_per_m3 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Density of the slurry (water and ore mixture) in the unit operation, expressed in kg/m³. Critical process control parameter for flotation, leaching, and thickening circuits. Sourced from nuclear density gauges or Coriolis meters via OSIsoft PI System.']
  slurry_ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'pH value of the process slurry measured during the unit operation event. Critical for flotation reagent effectiveness, leach chemistry control, and environmental compliance. Sourced from inline pH probes via OSIsoft PI System.']
  specific_energy_kwh_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Energy consumed per dry metric tonne of feed processed by the unit operation, expressed in kWh/t. Key efficiency metric for comminution circuits (SAG, ball mill, HPGR) and electrowinning. Sourced from power meters via OSIsoft PI System. Used in OPEX analysis and LOM energy cost modelling.']
  tailings_tonnage_dry_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Dry metric tonnes of tailings (gangue and waste material) generated by the unit operation during the event period. Used in mass balance reconciliation, TSF (Tailings Storage Facility) capacity planning, and environmental reporting.']
  throughput_rate_tph decimal(10,3) [note: 'Instantaneous or average throughput rate of the unit operation during the event period, expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (t/h). Compared against design capacity and LOM plan targets for process control and production reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this unit operation event record was last modified, including metallurgical accountant adjustments, data quality corrections, or status transitions.']
  water_consumption_m3 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total volume of process water consumed by the unit operation during the event period, expressed in cubic metres. Used in water balance reporting, environmental compliance (EIS), and OPEX cost allocation. Sourced from flow meters via OSIsoft PI System.']

  Note: 'Unified transactional record of all mineral processing operations per shift per circuit, using a type discriminator to capture comminution (SAG, ball mill, HPGR, crusher), flotation (rougher, scavenger, cleaner), leaching (heap, tank, vat, CIL/CIP), SX-EW (solvent extraction, electrowinning), DMS (dense media separation), gravity separation (spirals, shaking tables, jigs), magnetic separation, and thickening/dewatering events. Common attributes: circuit ID, shift reference, feed tonnage, product tonnage, recovery (%), specific energy (kWh/t). Type-specific attribute groups capture process-specific parameters (e.g., P80 for comminution, froth depth for flotation, medium density for DMS, current density for SX-EW). Sourced from OSIsoft PI System SCADA historian and MES. Supports metallurgical accounting, process control, and LOM plan variance analysis.'
}

Table processing.flotation_event {
  flotation_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each flotation circuit performance record per shift. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flotation circuit (e.g., rougher, scavenger, cleaner bank) where this event was recorded. Links to the processing circuit master resource. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE / resource reference category.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Flotation circuits target specific minerals/commodities (copper, nickel, zinc). Core to recovery tracking, circuit optimization, and metallurgical reporting. Domain experts expect commodity-specific f']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the metallurgist responsible for reviewing and approving this flotation event record. Ensures accountability in metallurgical accounting sign-off and supports regulatory reporting requirements. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category.']
  feed_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: Flotation events process specific feed streams. One event processes one feed stream; one feed stream has many events. This links the flotation event to the feed stream being processed.']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Flotation involves chemical reagents (collectors, frothers, depressants) and mechanical hazards (impellers, agitators). Incidents during flotation operations (chemical spills, equipment failures, expo']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Flotation circuit samples (feed, concentrate, tailings) are sent to designated labs for grade and recovery verification. Critical for process control, circuit optimization, and validating online analy']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Flotation cell mechanical issues (impeller wear, air system failures) during events trigger maintenance work orders. Links flotation performance problems to maintenance interventions for recovery opti']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or concentrator facility where the flotation circuit operates. Enables plant-level aggregation and benchmarking across sites.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Flotation events track recovery performance against scheduled mine production for metallurgical accounting, ore type performance analysis, and integrated mine-mill optimization. Essential for recovery']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Flotation performance varies by ore source/prospect. Critical for metallurgical testwork validation, circuit optimization, and ore blending strategies. Real business need: ore-specific recovery tracki']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Flotation events occur during a production shift run. One flotation event belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many flotation events. This links the event to the processing domain\'s shift entit']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this flotation event was recorded. Enables shift-level metallurgical accounting and performance comparison across day, afternoon, and night shifts.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Flotation circuits generate tailings streams deposited to specific TSF. Critical for metallurgical balance closure, TSF capacity management, and geochemical characterization. Tracks tailings_mass_t de']
  air_flow_rate_m3_per_min decimal(8,3) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of air injected into the flotation cells, measured in cubic metres per minute (m³/min). Air flow is a primary control variable affecting bubble generation, froth stability, and mineral recovery. Monitored via OSIsoft PI SCADA integration.']
  cell_count_operating varchar [note: 'Number of flotation cells actively operating in the circuit during this event period. Cells may be taken offline for maintenance or cleaning. Operating cell count affects residence time and overall circuit capacity. Sourced from Infor EAM work order data.']
  cell_volume_m3 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total active volume of the flotation cells in operation during this event, measured in cubic metres (m³). Combined with feed rate, determines slurry residence time which is a key flotation kinetics parameter.']
  circuit_availability_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage of the event period during which the flotation circuit was available and operating (not in planned or unplanned downtime). Calculated as operating hours divided by scheduled hours × 100. Contributes to Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) reporting.']
  circuit_recovery_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the valuable mineral in the feed that is recovered into the concentrate product for this flotation circuit stage. Calculated as: (concentrate mass × concentrate grade) / (feed mass × feed grade) × 100. Core metallurgical KPI for LOM plan tracking and reagent optimisation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.']
  circuit_stage varchar [note: 'The specific stage of the flotation circuit captured in this event record. Rougher is the primary separation stage; scavenger recovers minerals from rougher tailings; cleaner upgrades rougher concentrate grade; recleaner further upgrades cleaner concentrate. Critical for stage-by-stage metallurgical accounting.']
  collector_dosage_g_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Dosage rate of the collector reagent (e.g., xanthate, dithiophosphate) added to the flotation circuit, expressed in grams per dry metric tonne of feed (g/t). Collector promotes hydrophobicity of target mineral surfaces to enable bubble attachment. Key reagent optimisation variable.']
  concentrate_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Grade of the valuable mineral in the flotation concentrate product, expressed as a percentage (%). Measures the quality of the separation achieved. Higher concentrate grade reduces downstream smelting costs and improves payable metal. Sourced from LabWare LIMS.']
  concentrate_grade_target_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The planned or contractually required concentrate grade for the valuable mineral, expressed as a percentage (%). Used to assess whether the flotation circuit is meeting product quality specifications for offtake agreements and smelter contracts.']
  concentrate_mass_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dry mass of concentrate produced by the flotation circuit during the event period, measured in dry metric tonnes (t). Used in metallurgical accounting mass balance and payable metal calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flotation event record was first captured in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  depressant_dosage_g_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Dosage rate of the depressant reagent (e.g., sodium cyanide, zinc sulphate, CMC) added to the flotation circuit, expressed in grams per dry metric tonne of feed (g/t). Depressants selectively prevent flotation of gangue minerals or penalty elements, improving concentrate grade and selectivity.']
  downtime_reason varchar [note: 'Descriptive reason for any unplanned or planned downtime experienced by the flotation circuit during this event period (e.g., pump failure, cell maintenance, reagent outage, power interruption). Null if no downtime occurred. Used for reliability analysis and maintenance planning in Infor EAM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pump_failure|cell_maintenance|reagent_outage|power_interruption|planned_shutdown|instrumentation_fault — promote to reference product]']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world timestamp marking the end of the flotation measurement period (typically shift end). Combined with start timestamp to define the measurement window for throughput and reagent consumption calculations.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this flotation event record, formatted as FLT-YYYY-MM-DD-NNNN. Used for cross-system reference, metallurgical accounting reconciliation, and audit trail. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world timestamp marking the start of the flotation measurement period (typically shift start). Used for time-series alignment with OSIsoft PI historian data and SCADA records. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the flotation event record. Draft indicates data entry in progress; confirmed indicates shift data locked; reviewed indicates metallurgist sign-off; approved indicates inclusion in metallurgical balance; voided indicates record invalidated. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  feed_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Head grade of the valuable mineral (e.g., copper, lithium, nickel) in the flotation feed, expressed as a percentage (%). Derived from assay results and used as the denominator in circuit recovery calculations. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay data.']
  feed_particle_size_p80_um decimal(8,2) [note: 'The 80th percentile passing particle size (P80) of the flotation feed, measured in micrometres (µm). Particle size is a critical determinant of flotation performance — coarse particles are difficult to float while ultra-fine particles suffer from poor bubble attachment. Sourced from LabWare LIMS particle size analysis.']
  feed_rate_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mass flow rate of ore slurry fed into the flotation circuit during the event period, measured in dry metric tonnes per hour (t/h). Key throughput metric for metallurgical accounting and LOM plan performance tracking.']
  froth_depth_mm decimal(7,2) [note: 'Depth of the froth layer maintained in the flotation cell during the event period, measured in millimetres (mm). Froth depth is a critical control parameter influencing concentrate grade and recovery trade-off. Deeper froth generally improves grade at the expense of recovery.']
  frother_dosage_g_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Dosage rate of the frother reagent (e.g., MIBC, pine oil, polyglycol) added to the flotation circuit, expressed in grams per dry metric tonne of feed (g/t). Frother stabilises the froth layer and controls bubble size distribution. Critical for froth stability and concentrate transport.']
  impeller_speed_rpm decimal(7,2) [note: 'Average rotational speed of the flotation cell impellers during the event period, measured in revolutions per minute (RPM). Impeller speed controls slurry agitation, air dispersion, and bubble-particle contact. Monitored via OSIsoft PI SCADA.']
  lime_dosage_kg_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Dosage rate of lime (calcium hydroxide or calcium oxide) added to the flotation circuit for pH control, expressed in kilograms per dry metric tonne of feed (kg/t). Lime is typically the highest-volume reagent in sulphide flotation circuits and a significant OPEX driver.']
  mass_pull_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'The ratio of concentrate mass to feed mass expressed as a percentage (%). Indicates the proportion of total feed mass reporting to the concentrate. High mass pull may indicate over-flotation and gangue entrainment; low mass pull may indicate under-recovery. Key flotation control parameter.']
  mineral_type varchar [note: 'The primary valuable mineral being recovered in this flotation circuit event (e.g., copper, nickel, lithium). Determines the applicable reagent suite, recovery benchmarks, and product specifications. Essential for multi-commodity processing plants. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: copper|nickel|lithium|zinc|lead|molybdenum|gold|silver — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ore type being processed in the flotation circuit during this event (e.g., sulphide, oxide, mixed, transitional). Ore type significantly influences reagent selection, dosage rates, and achievable recovery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sulphide|oxide|mixed|transitional|primary|secondary — promote to reference product]']
  pulp_density_pct_solids decimal(6,2) [note: 'Solids content of the flotation feed slurry expressed as a percentage by mass (% solids). Pulp density affects reagent effectiveness, bubble-particle collision rates, and overall flotation kinetics. Monitored continuously via OSIsoft PI.']
  pulp_ph decimal(4,2) [note: 'pH of the flotation pulp during the event period. pH is a critical chemical environment parameter that governs mineral surface chemistry, collector adsorption, and selectivity between valuable minerals and gangue. Typically controlled with lime addition.']
  recovery_target_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'The planned or budgeted circuit recovery percentage for this flotation stage as defined in the LOM plan or metallurgical design criteria. Enables variance analysis between actual and target recovery for performance management and LOM plan tracking.']
  sample_reference varchar [note: 'The LabWare LIMS sample identifier for the assay samples collected during this flotation event (feed, concentrate, and tailings). Enables traceability between the flotation performance record and the underlying laboratory assay results supporting grade data.']
  slurry_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature of the flotation feed slurry during the event period, measured in degrees Celsius (°C). Slurry temperature affects reagent solubility, reaction kinetics, and froth stability. Particularly relevant in cold-climate operations and heated leach-flotation circuits.']
  tailings_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Grade of the valuable mineral remaining in the flotation tailings stream, expressed as a percentage (%). Indicates mineral losses to the Tailings Storage Facility (TSF). Used to calculate circuit recovery and identify optimisation opportunities. Sourced from LabWare LIMS.']
  tailings_mass_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dry mass of tailings discharged from the flotation circuit during the event period, measured in dry metric tonnes (t). Used in mass balance reconciliation and TSF volumetric planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flotation event record was last modified. Tracks data corrections, metallurgist adjustments, and re-assay updates. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  water_addition_m3_per_h decimal(8,3) [note: 'Rate of process water added to the flotation circuit to maintain target pulp density, measured in cubic metres per hour (m³/h). Water consumption is a key environmental and OPEX metric, particularly in water-stressed mining regions subject to ISO 14001 reporting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of flotation circuit performance per shift including rougher, scavenger, and cleaner stages. Captures feed grade, concentrate grade, tailings grade, mass pull (%), air flow rate (m³/min), froth depth (mm), reagent dosage rates, and circuit recovery (%). Supports metallurgical accounting and reagent optimisation.'
}

Table processing.leach_event {
  leach_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each leaching operation event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the leach_event data product.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the leach circuit or processing unit (heap leach pad, CIL/CIP tank, vat leach vessel) where this event occurred. Links to the processing circuit master.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Leaching extracts specific commodities (copper, gold, nickel, uranium). Fundamental process-commodity link for hydrometallurgical operations. Replaces denormalized commodity text field. Essential for ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the process operator or metallurgist responsible for supervising and recording this leach event. Links to the workforce/employee master. Supports accountability, training compliance, and shift handover documentation.']
  feed_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: Leach events process specific feed streams. One event processes one feed stream; one feed stream has many events. This links the leach event to the feed stream being processed.']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Leaching uses hazardous chemicals (cyanide for gold, sulfuric acid for copper). Incidents during leach operations (solution spills, gas releases, exposure events) require direct traceability to leach ']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Leach solution samples (PLS, raffinate, residue) analyzed at specific labs for extraction efficiency monitoring and reagent optimization. Standard hydrometallurgical process control requirement for co']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Leach tank/pad equipment failures (agitators, pumps, solution distribution) during leach events generate maintenance work orders. Links hydrometallurgical process issues to maintenance response for ex']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or facility where the leach event occurred. Supports multi-site reporting and plant-level performance benchmarking.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Leach events must reference production schedules for heap leach ore tracking, metal recovery forecasting, and ore type extraction performance analysis. Critical for heap leach operations and long-term']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Leach performance is ore-source dependent. Essential for heap leach/tank leach optimization, resource model metallurgical assumptions validation. Real business need: extraction efficiency tracking by ']
  sample_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the representative leach solution or residue sample submitted to the laboratory for assay analysis during this event. Links to LabWare LIMS sample management records for quality control and mass balance verification.']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Leach events occur during a production shift run. One leach event belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many leach events. This links the event to the processing domain\'s shift entity.']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this leach event was recorded. Enables shift-based production reporting and reconciliation.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Leach residues/tailings deposited to specific TSF. Required for geochemical risk management (ARD assessment), TSF design compliance, and environmental monitoring. Tracks ore_feed_mass_t minus recovere']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Operational batch or lot number assigned to the ore charge or solution batch processed in this leach event. Used for traceability from ROM feed through to final concentrate or pregnant leach solution (PLS). Sourced from LabWare LIMS or SCADA batch management.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text operational comments or notes recorded by the process operator or metallurgist for this leach event. May include observations on solution colour, foam, equipment behaviour, or other qualitative process indicators not captured by structured fields.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this leach event record was first captured and persisted in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system from which this leach event record was sourced. Supports data lineage, quality assessment, and reconciliation between automated sensor data (OSIsoft PI, SCADA) and manually entered or LIMS-derived records.']
  dissolved_metal_concentration_ppm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Concentration of the target dissolved metal in the pregnant leach solution (PLS) or leachate, measured in parts per million (ppm). Primary indicator of leaching effectiveness and solution grade. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results or inline OSIsoft PI analyser tags.']
  dissolved_oxygen_mg_l decimal(8,3) [note: 'Concentration of dissolved oxygen in the leach solution, measured in milligrams per litre (mg/L). Dissolved oxygen is a critical parameter for cyanide leaching of gold and silver, directly influencing metal dissolution rates. Monitored via OSIsoft PI DO analysers.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the leaching operation concluded for this event or batch. Used in conjunction with event_start_timestamp to derive leach cycle duration.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the leach event, typically sourced from the OSIsoft PI System or SCADA batch/event log. Used for cross-system traceability and operational reporting (e.g., LE-2024-00123).']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the leaching operation commenced for this event or batch. Principal real-world event time used for duration calculations, shift allocation, and production scheduling alignment.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the leach event. Indicates whether the leaching operation is actively running, has completed normally, was aborted due to process upset, is on hold, or is pending quality review before finalisation.']
  extraction_efficiency_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the target metal extracted from the ore feed into solution during this leach event, calculated as (metal dissolved / metal in feed) × 100. Key performance indicator for process optimisation, LOM plan reconciliation, and recovery rate reporting. Sourced from LabWare LIMS mass balance.']
  feed_grade_pct decimal(10,4) [note: 'Head grade of the ore feed entering the leach circuit, expressed as a percentage (%) for base metals or g/t equivalent converted to percentage for precious metals. Sourced from assay results in LabWare LIMS. Critical input for extraction efficiency and recovery rate calculations.']
  leach_duration_hrs decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time of the leaching operation for this event, measured in hours. Represents the actual leach time from solution application/charge to drain/discharge. Used for cycle time optimisation, throughput modelling, and LOM plan variance analysis.']
  leach_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the leaching method applied in this event. Distinguishes between heap leach (copper, gold), tank leach (CIL/CIP for gold), vat leach, and agitation leach configurations. Drives process parameter benchmarking and recovery rate analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: heap_leach|tank_leach|vat_leach|cil|cip|agitation_leach — promote to reference product]']
  ore_feed_mass_t decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total dry mass of ore charged to the leach circuit for this event, measured in metric tonnes. Represents the principal quantitative input for recovery rate calculation and throughput reporting against Life of Mine (LOM) plan targets.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Mineralogical classification of the ore processed in this leach event. Ore type significantly influences leaching chemistry, reagent selection, and achievable recovery rates. Sourced from Geovia GEMS geological model and grade control assay data.']
  oxidation_reduction_potential_mv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Oxidation-reduction potential (ORP) of the leach solution measured in millivolts (mV). Critical electrochemical parameter for monitoring leaching conditions, particularly in cyanide leach (gold) and SX-EW (copper) circuits. Sourced from OSIsoft PI inline analysers.']
  pi_tag_reference varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI System tag name or event frame reference associated with this leach event. Enables direct traceability back to the process historian for raw time-series data retrieval, audit, and re-processing. Applicable when data_source is pi_system or scada.']
  process_upset_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a process upset or abnormal operating condition occurred during this leach event (True = upset occurred, False = normal operation). Process upsets may include pH excursions, reagent dosing failures, pump trips, or temperature deviations. Triggers investigation and corrective action workflows in IsoMetrix.']
  reagent_concentration_g_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Concentration of the primary leaching reagent (e.g., cyanide for gold CIL/CIP, sulphuric acid for copper heap leach, caustic soda for lithium leach) in the leach solution, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Key process control parameter for optimising extraction efficiency and reagent cost (OPEX).']
  reagent_consumption_kg decimal(14,3) [note: 'Total mass of primary leaching reagent consumed during this leach event, measured in kilograms. Used for OPEX tracking, reagent cost allocation, and environmental reporting of chemical usage. Sourced from Pronto Xi inventory consumption records and SCADA flow meters.']
  reagent_type varchar [note: 'Type of primary leaching reagent used in this event. Determines applicable safety, environmental, and regulatory requirements. Examples include sodium cyanide (gold CIL/CIP), sulphuric acid (copper heap leach), and caustic soda (lithium leach circuits). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cyanide|sulphuric_acid|caustic_soda|ammonia|chlorine|hydrochloric_acid — promote to reference product]']
  residue_grade_pct decimal(10,4) [note: 'Grade of the target metal remaining in the leach residue (tailings) after the leaching event, expressed as a percentage. Used to calculate actual metal recovery and identify process losses. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results on residue samples.']
  slurry_density_g_cm3 decimal(8,4) [note: 'Density of the ore-solution slurry (water and ore mixture) in the leach circuit, measured in grams per cubic centimetre (g/cm³). Applicable to tank leach and CIL/CIP circuits. Monitored via OSIsoft PI density instruments. Affects mass transfer and reagent efficiency.']
  solution_flow_rate_m3_hr decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of leach solution through the circuit during this event, measured in cubic metres per hour (m³/hr). Monitored via OSIsoft PI flow instrumentation. Influences reagent distribution, contact time, and overall leaching kinetics.']
  solution_ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured pH of the leach solution at the time of this event or at the representative sampling point within the event window. Critical process control parameter for reagent efficiency, metal dissolution kinetics, and environmental compliance (acid mine drainage prevention).']
  solution_temperature_c decimal(7,2) [note: 'Temperature of the leach solution in degrees Celsius (°C) measured during this event. Temperature directly influences leaching kinetics, reagent consumption rates, and metal dissolution efficiency. Monitored via OSIsoft PI System instrumentation.']
  solution_volume_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Total volume of leach solution applied or circulated during this event, measured in cubic metres (m³). Used for water balance reporting, reagent dosing calculations, and environmental water management compliance.']
  tank_pad_reference varchar [note: 'Operational identifier of the specific leach tank, leach pad cell, or vat vessel used in this event (e.g., TANK-03, PAD-CELL-7B). Sourced from the OSIsoft PI asset hierarchy or SCADA tag configuration. Enables equipment-level performance tracking.']
  target_extraction_efficiency_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Planned or budgeted extraction efficiency percentage for this leach event type and ore type, as defined in the Life of Mine (LOM) plan or metallurgical test work. Enables variance analysis between actual and planned recovery performance.']
