  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this leach event record was last modified or updated in the data platform. Supports change detection, incremental processing, and audit compliance.']
  upset_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the process upset or abnormal condition that occurred during this leach event, if applicable. Populated when process_upset_flag is True. Captures the nature of the deviation for root cause analysis and corrective action tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of leaching operations (heap leach, tank leach, vat leach) per shift or batch. Captures leach tank/pad ID, solution pH, temperature (°C), reagent concentration (g/L), leach time (hrs), dissolved metal concentration (ppm), and extraction efficiency (%). Applicable to gold CIL/CIP, copper heap leach, and lithium leach circuits.'
}

Table processing.sxew_event {
  sxew_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each SX-EW operational event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SX-EW processing circuit (tankhouse or extraction circuit) where this event occurred. Links to the processing circuit master resource.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: SX-EW produces specific commodities (copper cathode). Direct production link essential for cathode quality tracking, production reporting, and linking hydrometallurgical output to saleable products. S']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the process operator or shift supervisor responsible for the SX-EW circuit during this event. Supports accountability, training compliance, and incident traceability.']
  feed_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: SX-EW events process specific feed streams (PLS feed). One event processes one feed stream; one feed stream has many events. This links the SX-EW event to the feed stream being processed.']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: SXEW involves high-voltage electrical systems (electrowinning cells) and corrosive solutions (sulfuric acid electrolyte). Incidents during SXEW operations (electrical faults, acid spills, cathode hand']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Electrolyte and cathode samples from SX-EW circuits sent to designated labs for copper grade, impurity analysis, and cathode quality verification. Essential for product quality control and sales contr']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: SX-EW circuit equipment issues (rectifier failures, cell short circuits, organic pumps) during production events trigger maintenance work orders. Links cathode production problems to maintenance inter']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or hydrometallurgical facility where the SX-EW circuit operates. Enables multi-site reporting and plant-level performance aggregation.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: SX-EW events track cathode production against scheduled mine production for integrated copper production planning, metal accounting, and sales contract fulfillment. Essential for copper operations wit']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this SX-EW event was recorded. Each SX-EW event is captured per shift as the primary reporting cadence.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: SX-EW operations generate raffinate/spent electrolyte waste streams to TSF. Needed for water balance reconciliation, environmental discharge reporting, and TSF water quality management. Tracks aqueous']
  acid_concentration_gl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Sulphuric acid concentration in the electrowinning electrolyte, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Acid balance is critical for cathode quality, current efficiency, and prevention of anode corrosion in the EW tankhouse.']
  aqueous_feed_grade_gl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Copper concentration in the aqueous feed (pregnant leach solution, PLS) entering the solvent extraction circuit, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Determines the extraction circuit loading and directly influences cathode production output.']
  cathode_grade_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Purity of the copper cathode produced, expressed as a percentage of copper content. LME Grade A copper cathode requires ≥99.99% Cu. Used for product quality certification, sales contract compliance, and LME registration eligibility.']
  cathode_production_kg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total mass of copper cathode produced during the shift, measured in kilograms. The primary production output metric for the SX-EW circuit, used for daily production reporting, LOM plan reconciliation, and sales volume tracking.']
  cathode_quality_grade varchar [note: 'Quality classification of copper cathode produced during the shift. \'LME_Grade_A\' indicates product meets London Metal Exchange Grade A specification (≥99.99% Cu); \'off_spec\' indicates product below LME standard but saleable; \'rejected\' indicates product not suitable for sale.']
  cathodes_harvested varchar [note: 'Number of individual copper cathode sheets harvested (stripped) from the electrowinning cells during the shift. Used for tankhouse productivity tracking, cathode cycle time management, and inventory reconciliation.']
  cell_voltage_v decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average operating voltage across the electrowinning cells during the shift, measured in volts (V). Used to monitor energy efficiency, detect anode/cathode fouling, and calculate specific energy consumption per tonne of copper produced.']
  cells_operating varchar [note: 'Number of electrowinning cells in active operation during the shift. Used to calculate effective circuit capacity, monitor cell availability, and assess impact of cell outages on production.']
  cells_total varchar [note: 'Total number of electrowinning cells installed in the circuit (nameplate capacity). Used as the denominator for cell availability calculations and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) reporting.']
  circuit_downtime_min varchar [note: 'Total unplanned and planned downtime for the SX-EW circuit during the shift, measured in minutes. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) calculation, maintenance planning via Infor EAM, and production loss reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SX-EW event record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and silver layer ingestion tracking. Populated automatically at record insertion.']
  current_density_am2 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Electrical current density applied across the electrowinning cathode surface, measured in amperes per square metre (A/m²). A primary electrowinning process control parameter governing copper deposition rate, cathode quality, and energy consumption.']
  current_efficiency_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Ratio of actual copper deposited to the theoretical copper that should have been deposited based on Faraday\'s Law, expressed as a percentage. A key electrowinning performance indicator; values below target indicate short-circuiting, impurity effects, or electrolyte imbalance.']
  downtime_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason code for circuit downtime recorded during the shift. Used for root cause analysis, maintenance prioritisation, and production loss categorisation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned_maintenance|unplanned_breakdown|process_upset|reagent_shortage|power_outage|other — promote to reference product if additional codes required]']
  electrolyte_temperature_c decimal(7,2) [note: 'Operating temperature of the electrowinning electrolyte during the shift, measured in degrees Celsius (°C). Temperature affects copper ion mobility, current efficiency, and cathode surface quality. Monitored via OSIsoft PI System.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the SX-EW shift event, formatted as SXEW-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used for cross-referencing with OSIsoft PI System historian records, lab assay reports, and production reconciliation.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the SX-EW event record. \'draft\' indicates data entry in progress; \'confirmed\' indicates shift data locked; \'reviewed\' indicates metallurgist review complete; \'approved\' indicates management sign-off; \'voided\' indicates record invalidated.']
  extraction_efficiency_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of copper extracted from the aqueous feed (PLS) into the organic phase during the solvent extraction stage, calculated as (PLS grade – raffinate grade) / PLS grade × 100. A key metallurgical recovery indicator for the SX circuit.']
  impurity_fe_mgl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Concentration of iron (Fe) impurity in the electrowinning electrolyte, measured in milligrams per litre (mg/L). Elevated iron levels reduce current efficiency and degrade cathode surface quality. Monitored via LabWare LIMS shift samples.']
  impurity_mn_mgl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Concentration of manganese (Mn) impurity in the electrowinning electrolyte, measured in milligrams per litre (mg/L). Manganese causes anode passivation and cathode surface defects. Tracked via LabWare LIMS for electrolyte bleed management.']
  lom_plan_cathode_kg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Planned cathode copper production target for the shift as defined in the Life of Mine (LOM) plan or short-term production schedule. Used for variance analysis between actual and planned production, and for LOM plan tracking.']
  organic_cu_concentration_gl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Concentration of copper in the organic (solvent) phase of the solvent extraction circuit, measured in grams per litre (g/L). A key process control parameter indicating the loading efficiency of the organic extractant (e.g., LIX reagent). Sourced from OSIsoft PI System online analysers or LabWare LIMS shift samples.']
  organic_flow_rate_m3h decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of the organic solvent phase circulating through the solvent extraction mixer-settlers, measured in cubic metres per hour (m³/h). Used to control the aqueous-to-organic (A:O) ratio and optimise copper extraction efficiency.']
  pls_flow_rate_m3h decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of the pregnant leach solution (PLS) entering the solvent extraction circuit, measured in cubic metres per hour (m³/h). Controls the aqueous-to-organic (A:O) ratio and extraction circuit residence time.']
  production_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the SX-EW production event occurred. Used for daily production reporting, reconciliation against Life of Mine (LOM) plan targets, and regulatory disclosure.']
  raffinate_grade_gl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Copper concentration in the raffinate (barren aqueous solution) exiting the solvent extraction circuit after copper has been extracted, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Used to calculate extraction efficiency and monitor copper losses to raffinate.']
  reagent_consumption_lm3 decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volume of organic extractant reagent (e.g., LIX 984N or equivalent) consumed per cubic metre of pregnant leach solution processed, measured in litres per cubic metre (L/m³). Used for reagent cost tracking, OPEX reporting, and supply chain planning via Pronto Xi.']
  shift_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the operational shift ended for this SX-EW event. Used to calculate shift duration and align with production reporting periods.']
  shift_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the operational shift commenced for this SX-EW event. Principal business event timestamp used for time-series analysis, production scheduling alignment, and OSIsoft PI historian correlation.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift period: day, afternoon, or night. Used for shift-pattern analysis, fatigue risk management, and production performance benchmarking across shift rotations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system from which this SX-EW event record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation between systems, and data quality root cause analysis in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  specific_energy_kwh_t decimal(12,4) [note: 'Electrical energy consumed per tonne of copper cathode produced, measured in kilowatt-hours per tonne (kWh/t Cu). A key OPEX and sustainability metric for the electrowinning circuit, used for energy cost allocation and carbon footprint reporting.']
  spent_electrolyte_grade_gl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Copper concentration in the spent electrolyte returning from the electrowinning cells to the stripping circuit, measured in grams per litre (g/L). The difference between strip solution grade and spent electrolyte grade represents the copper deposited per pass.']
  strip_solution_grade_gl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Copper concentration in the electrolyte strip solution (advance electrolyte) fed to the electrowinning tankhouse, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Directly controls cathode deposition rate and current efficiency in the EW circuit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SX-EW event record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and data quality monitoring in the Databricks silver layer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of Solvent Extraction Electrowinning (SX-EW) operations per shift. Captures organic phase copper concentration (g/L), aqueous feed grade, strip solution grade, current density (A/m²), cell voltage (V), cathode production (kg/shift), and current efficiency (%). Specific to copper SX-EW processing circuits.'
}

Table processing.reagent_consumption {
  reagent_consumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each reagent consumption record in the mineral processing circuit. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  chemical_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.chemical_register. Business justification: Reagents consumed in processing must be registered chemicals with SDS, GHS classification, and handling procedures. Links consumption records to chemical hazard data for regulatory compliance (thresho']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing circuit (e.g., flotation, leaching, SX-EW, DMS) where the reagent was applied. Enables circuit-level OPEX analysis.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Reagent costs are material opex components (often 10-30% of processing costs) allocated to cost centres for AISC calculation and budget control. reagent_consumption.cost_centre_code (plain attr) is de']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the process operator who recorded or confirmed the reagent consumption event. Supports accountability, training analysis, and audit trail requirements.']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Reagent quality samples (collectors, frothers, flocculants) analyzed at specific labs for specification compliance and batch acceptance testing. Supply chain quality control requirement to ensure reag']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or facility where the reagent was consumed. Supports multi-site OPEX reporting and benchmarking.']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Reagent consumption is recorded per shift run. One consumption record belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many consumption records. This links the consumption to the processing domain\'s shift ']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which the reagent was consumed. Links to the shift master for date, crew, and time-of-day context.']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reagent master record identifying the specific chemical reagent (collector, frother, flocculant, lime, cyanide, acid, solvent, etc.) consumed.']
  batch_lot_number varchar [note: 'Supplier batch or lot number of the reagent consumed. Enables traceability for quality incidents, reagent performance investigations, and environmental compliance audits.']
  consumption_record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this consumption record, formatted as RC-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN. Used for cross-referencing with SAP material documents, LIMS records, and environmental compliance reports.']
  consumption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reagent consumption record. Draft records are pending operator confirmation; confirmed records are locked for OPEX reporting; adjusted records have been corrected post-shift; cancelled records are voided.']
  consumption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world date and time at which the reagent consumption event occurred or was recorded by the process control system (OSIsoft PI / SCADA). Distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reagent consumption record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost and total cost fields (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Required for multi-currency OPEX reporting and financial consolidation.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the reagent consumption data: SCADA/OSIsoft PI automated reading, manual operator entry, LabWare LIMS import, SAP ERP goods issue, or estimated value. Critical for data quality assessment and audit trail.']
  dosage_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate at which the reagent was dosed into the circuit, expressed in grams per tonne (g/t) of ore processed or kilograms per hour (kg/hr) depending on the reagent type and circuit. Core metric for reagent optimisation and benchmarking against LOM plan targets.']
  dosage_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the reagent dosage rate field. Common units include grams per tonne (g/t) for solid reagents dosed relative to ore throughput, and kilograms per hour (kg/hr) for liquid reagents dosed by flow rate.']
  environmental_report_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reagent consumption event requires mandatory environmental compliance reporting (e.g., cyanide usage under ICMC, acid consumption under EIS conditions). Drives automated compliance workflow in IsoMetrix.']
  feed_grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Head grade (percentage) of the valuable mineral in the ore feed to the circuit during the shift. Contextualises reagent dosage decisions — higher feed grades may require adjusted reagent rates for optimal recovery.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reagent is classified as hazardous under applicable environmental and safety regulations (e.g., cyanide, sulphuric acid). Triggers mandatory environmental compliance reporting and PPE requirements per ISO 14001 and ISO 45001.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or supervisor notes regarding the reagent consumption event, such as reasons for deviation from planned dosage, equipment issues affecting dosing, or reagent quality observations.']
  ore_throughput_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dry metric tonnes of ore processed through the circuit during the shift. Used as the denominator for calculating specific reagent consumption (g/t) and benchmarking against LOM plan targets.']
  plan_dosage_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Life of Mine (LOM) plan target dosage rate for this reagent in this circuit, in the same unit as dosage_rate. Enables variance analysis between actual and planned reagent consumption for process optimisation.']
  plan_quantity_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Life of Mine (LOM) plan target quantity of reagent for this shift and circuit in kilograms. Used for budget variance reporting and OPEX performance management against the approved mine plan.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP or Pronto Xi purchase order number under which the reagent batch was procured. Enables three-way matching (PO, goods receipt, invoice) and links consumption to procurement contracts for OPEX tracking.']
  quantity_consumed_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total mass of reagent consumed during the shift in kilograms. Primary quantity field for OPEX cost calculation, reagent inventory reconciliation, and environmental compliance reporting of chemical usage volumes.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity consumed field. Defaults to kilograms (kg) for solid reagents; litres (L) for liquid reagents. Enables multi-reagent reporting with consistent unit handling.']
  recovery_rate_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the valuable mineral recovered from the feed ore during the shift in this circuit. Key process performance indicator correlated with reagent dosage to assess reagent effectiveness and optimise consumption.']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the shift during which the reagent was consumed. Used for daily and monthly OPEX roll-up reporting and LOM plan variance analysis.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the operational shift (day, night, or afternoon) during which the reagent was consumed. Supports shift-level performance analysis and reagent usage pattern identification.']
  slurry_density_g_per_cm3 decimal(6,4) [note: 'Density of the slurry in grams per cubic centimetre at the time of reagent consumption. Influences reagent dispersion and effectiveness; monitored via OSIsoft PI System for process control and optimisation.']
  slurry_ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'pH level of the slurry (water and ore mixture) in the circuit at the time of reagent consumption. Critical process parameter for reagent effectiveness — lime and acid dosage directly controls pH for flotation and leaching circuits.']
  specific_consumption_g_per_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Reagent consumption normalised per dry metric tonne of ore processed (grams per tonne). Calculated as quantity_consumed_kg × 1000 / ore_throughput_t. Key OPEX optimisation metric and LOM plan variance indicator.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supplier who provided the reagent batch consumed. Used for supplier performance management, procurement analysis, and reagent quality investigations.']
  total_cost decimal(16,4) [note: 'Total Operating Expenditure (OPEX) cost of reagent consumed during the shift, calculated as quantity_consumed_kg multiplied by unit_cost. Primary financial metric for reagent OPEX tracking, budget variance, and All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) reporting.']
  unit_cost decimal(14,4) [note: 'Cost per kilogram (or applicable unit) of the reagent at the time of consumption, sourced from the SAP purchase order or standard cost. Used for shift-level OPEX calculation and reagent cost variance analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reagent consumption record was last modified (e.g., post-shift adjustment, supervisor confirmation). Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) or Production Order number associated with the reagent consumption event. Links reagent usage to specific maintenance activities or production runs for detailed cost allocation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of reagent consumption per shift per circuit. Captures reagent type (collector, frother, flocculant, lime, cyanide, acid, solvent), dosage rate (g/t or kg/hr), total quantity consumed (kg), unit cost, batch lot number, and circuit applied. Supports reagent cost tracking (OPEX), optimisation, and environmental compliance reporting.'
}

Table processing.slurry_measurement {
  slurry_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each slurry measurement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this time-series observation table. Entity role: EVENT_LOG — append-only stream of immutable slurry parameter observations captured from OSIsoft PI System via OPC-UA at key mineral processing points.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical instrument or sensor asset record in the asset management system (Infor EAM) that generated this measurement. Enables traceability from measurement data to instrument maintenance history, calibration records, and asset lifecycle management. Supports instrument drift detection and predictive maintenance analytics.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the process control operator responsible for monitoring and managing the processing circuit during the period of this measurement. Relevant when data_quality_flag = \'substituted\' and substitution_reason = \'manual_entry\', providing accountability for manually entered values. Links to the workforce management system.']
  measurement_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the process measurement point (instrument tag location) where the slurry parameters were captured. Corresponds to the PI tag source location in the OSIsoft PI System, identifying the physical sensor or instrument node in the mineral processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill discharge, flotation feed, leach feed).']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or facility where the slurry measurement was recorded. Enables multi-site reporting and benchmarking across processing facilities within the mining operation.']
  process_circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific processing circuit (e.g., comminution, flotation, leaching, SX-EW) within the plant where the measurement was taken. Supports circuit-level mass balance and metallurgical accounting.']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Slurry measurements are taken during a production shift run. One measurement belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many measurements. This links the measurement to the processing domain\'s shift ']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this slurry measurement was recorded. Enables shift-level aggregation and reporting of process performance, throughput, and data quality statistics. Links to the shift schedule maintained in SAP S/4HANA HR or the mine operations shift management system.']
  alarm_high_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The configured upper alarm threshold for this measurement point in the SCADA/DCS system. When the measured value exceeds this limit, an automated high alarm is generated for operator response. Expressed in the same engineering unit as the measurement. Used for automated alarm generation and process safety management.']
  alarm_low_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The configured lower alarm threshold for this measurement point in the SCADA/DCS system. When the measured value falls below this limit, an automated low alarm is generated for operator response. Expressed in the same engineering unit as the measurement. Used for automated alarm generation and process safety management.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent calibration performed on the instrument or sensor that generated this measurement, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used to assess measurement reliability, identify instruments due for recalibration, and support data quality investigations. Instruments with expired calibration may have their measurements flagged as \'suspect\' in the data quality flag.']
  conductivity_ms_cm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Electrical conductivity of the slurry at the measurement point, expressed in millisiemens per centimetre (mS/cm). Indicator of dissolved ion concentration, relevant for process water quality management, reagent dosing control in flotation, and environmental monitoring of process water circuits.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI System data quality status flag for this measurement record, indicating the reliability of the captured value. \'good\' = valid measurement within instrument range; \'suspect\' = value outside expected range or instrument drift detected; \'bad\' = sensor fault or communication failure; \'substituted\' = value replaced by manual entry or interpolation. Critical for downstream metallurgical mass balance calculations and automated alarm generation. Aligns with OPC-UA quality codes.']
  deviation_from_setpoint decimal(18,6) [note: 'The arithmetic difference between the actual measured value and the configured process setpoint value (measured_value minus setpoint_value), expressed in the same engineering unit. A non-derived operational field captured directly from the PI System control deviation tag. Positive values indicate measurement above setpoint; negative values indicate below setpoint. Used for process control performance monitoring and alarm root cause analysis.']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measurement associated with the primary measurement value for this PI tag (e.g., t/m³, %w/w, m³/hr, pH, °C, cP, mS/cm, µm). Sourced from the PI tag engineering unit configuration. Supports dynamic unit-aware analytics and cross-tag comparisons in the metallurgical historian.']
  flow_rate_m3_hr decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of the slurry stream at the measurement point, expressed in cubic metres per hour (m³/hr). Measured by electromagnetic or Coriolis flow meter. Used in metallurgical mass balance, throughput calculations, and pump performance monitoring. Fundamental input for recovery rate and concentrate production calculations.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (UTC) at which this measurement record was ingested into the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer from the OSIsoft PI System source. Distinct from the measurement_timestamp (when the physical measurement occurred). Used for data pipeline latency monitoring, SLA tracking, and identifying late-arriving data in streaming ingestion pipelines.']
  is_alarm_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether an active process alarm condition existed at this measurement point at the time of measurement (True = alarm active; False = no alarm). Sourced from the SCADA/DCS alarm management system via OSIsoft PI System event frames. Enables alarm frequency analysis, process stability assessment, and operator response time reporting.']
  is_in_control boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the measurement value was within the defined process control limits (alarm_low_limit to alarm_high_limit) at the time of measurement (True = in control; False = out of control). Supports Statistical Process Control (SPC) analysis, process stability KPIs, and automated metallurgical mass balance quality gates.']
  mass_flow_rate_t_hr decimal(12,4) [note: 'Mass flow rate of dry solids in the slurry stream, expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (t/hr). Calculated from volumetric flow rate and slurry density adjusted for percent solids. Primary throughput metric for metallurgical accounting, LOM (Life of Mine) plan tracking, and production reporting against targets.']
  measurement_point_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the physical location and purpose of the measurement point within the processing circuit (e.g., \'SAG Mill 01 Discharge Slurry\', \'Flotation Feed Box\', \'CIL Tank 3 Overflow\'). Supports operator interpretation and alarm investigation.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (UTC) at which the slurry parameter observation was recorded by the OSIsoft PI System historian. This is the principal event time for the measurement, distinct from record ingestion timestamps. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Critical for time-series trending, process control, and metallurgical mass balance calculations.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical parameter being measured at this process point. Identifies which slurry property this record represents, enabling filtering and aggregation by parameter type across the processing circuit. Aligns with PI tag attribute classification in the OSIsoft PI System. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: density|percent_solids|flow_rate|ph|temperature|viscosity|conductivity|particle_size|mass_flow — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  next_calibration_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next calibration of the instrument or sensor, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used to proactively manage instrument maintenance schedules via Infor EAM and to flag measurements from instruments approaching or past their calibration due date. Supports data quality governance and regulatory compliance for measurement traceability.']
  opc_ua_node_reference varchar [note: 'The OPC-UA (OPC Unified Architecture) node identifier for the data source that feeds this measurement into the OSIsoft PI System. Provides the source system address for data lineage, integration troubleshooting, and SCADA connectivity management. Format follows OPC-UA NodeId specification (e.g., ns=2;s=SITE.AREA.UNIT.TAG).']
  particle_size_p80_um decimal(10,3) [note: 'The 80th percentile passing particle size of the solid fraction in the slurry, expressed in micrometres (µm). Key comminution performance indicator for SAG mill and HPGR circuits. P80 is the industry-standard metric for grind size control, directly impacting downstream flotation and leaching recovery rates.']
  percent_solids decimal(6,3) [note: 'Weight percentage of solid material in the slurry, expressed as percent weight-by-weight (%w/w). Derived from slurry density and known ore specific gravity. A key process control parameter for comminution (SAG, HPGR), flotation, and leaching circuits. Typical operating range: 20–75 %w/w depending on circuit type.']
  ph_value decimal(5,3) [note: 'Measured pH of the slurry at the measurement point on the standard pH scale (0–14). Critical process control parameter for flotation (reagent effectiveness), leaching (acid/alkaline conditions), and cyanide destruction circuits. Monitored continuously to ensure optimal mineral recovery and environmental compliance. Typical operating range varies by circuit: flotation 8–11, acid leach 1.5–2.5.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI System tag identifier (instrument tag name) associated with this measurement point. Serves as the source system natural key enabling traceability back to the PI historian tag configuration and SCADA/OPC-UA data source. Format follows site-specific PI tag naming conventions (e.g., SITE.AREA.UNIT.PARAMETER).']
  process_area_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the functional process area within the mineral processing plant where the measurement was taken (e.g., COMM for comminution, FLOT for flotation, LEACH for leaching, SXEW for SX-EW, THCK for thickening). Supports area-level operational reporting and alarm management.']
  raw_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The unprocessed, unvalidated measurement value as received directly from the OSIsoft PI System historian before any quality filtering, engineering unit conversion, or substitution is applied. Preserved for audit, instrument calibration verification, and data quality investigation. Enables comparison between raw and processed values to detect instrument drift.']
  sample_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'The configured sampling or scan interval for this PI tag in seconds, indicating how frequently the OSIsoft PI System captures a new measurement value (e.g., 1, 5, 10, 30, 60 seconds). Used to assess data density, identify gaps in time-series data, and configure alarm thresholds appropriately.']
  sensor_status_code varchar [note: 'Instrument or sensor status code as reported by the SCADA/OPC-UA system at the time of measurement. Provides granular diagnostic information beyond the data quality flag, including specific fault codes (e.g., COMM_FAIL, RANGE_HI, RANGE_LO, CAL_DUE, OFFLINE, NORMAL). Used for instrument maintenance scheduling via Infor EAM and sensor fault detection analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NORMAL|COMM_FAIL|RANGE_HI|RANGE_LO|CAL_DUE|OFFLINE|FROZEN|DRIFT — promote to reference product]']
  setpoint_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The target or desired setpoint value for this measurement parameter as configured in the DCS/SCADA process control system. Expressed in the same engineering unit as the measurement. Enables deviation analysis between actual measured values and process control targets, supporting process optimisation and LOM plan performance tracking.']
  slurry_density_t_m3 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured density of the slurry (water and ore mixture) at the measurement point, expressed in tonnes per cubic metre (t/m³). Captured from nuclear density gauge or Coriolis flow meter via OPC-UA. Critical parameter for metallurgical mass balance calculations, pump sizing, and process control. Typical range for mineral processing slurries: 1.0–2.2 t/m³.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The natural key or record identifier from the OSIsoft PI System historian for this specific measurement event (e.g., PI event frame ID or compressed archive record reference). Enables exact traceability back to the source historian record for data lineage, audit, and reconciliation purposes in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  substitution_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a measurement value was substituted when data_quality_flag = \'substituted\'. Values: \'manual_entry\' = operator manually entered value; \'interpolated\' = value interpolated from adjacent timestamps; \'last_known_good\' = previous valid value carried forward; \'calculated\' = derived from related measurements; \'not_applicable\' = no substitution occurred. Required for metallurgical mass balance audit trail and data governance compliance.']
  temperature_c decimal(7,3) [note: 'Measured temperature of the slurry at the measurement point, expressed in degrees Celsius (°C). Relevant for leaching kinetics (heap leach, pressure oxidation), reagent reaction rates, and viscosity calculations. Monitored for process optimisation and equipment protection (e.g., pump seal integrity).']
  viscosity_cp decimal(10,4) [note: 'Dynamic viscosity of the slurry at the measurement point, expressed in centipoise (cP). Affects pump performance, pipeline transport efficiency, and flotation cell hydrodynamics. Elevated viscosity can indicate high clay content or incorrect percent solids, impacting comminution and separation efficiency.']

  Note: 'Time-series measurement record of slurry parameters at key process points captured from OSIsoft PI System via OPC-UA. Captures measurement point ID, timestamp, slurry density (t/m³), percent solids (%w/w), flow rate (m³/hr), pH, temperature (°C), viscosity (cP), data quality flag (good/suspect/bad/substituted), and sensor status code. Data quality flags enable detection of sensor faults, instrument drift, and substituted values for downstream metallurgical mass balance calculations. Enables real-time process control, historian trending, and automated alarm generation.'
}

Table processing.concentrate_batch {
  concentrate_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each concentrate production batch record in the processing plant. Primary key for the concentrate_batch data product.']
  benefit_sharing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.benefit_sharing_agreement. Business justification: Production output (concentrate batches) triggers benefit-sharing payments in production-based royalty agreements. Required for benefit calculation, payment trigger identification, agreement compliance']
  cargo_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.cargo_shipment. Business justification: Concentrate batches are physically loaded onto vessels for export shipment. Sales invoicing, quality certification, and revenue recognition all require traceability from production batch to specific c']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.circuit. Business justification: Concentrate batches are produced by specific circuits. One batch produced by one circuit; one circuit produces many batches. This links the batch to the specific circuit that produced it. The plant_id']
  concentrate_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.concentrate_stockpile. Business justification: Concentrate batches are added to stockpiles. One batch is stored in one stockpile; one stockpile receives many batches. This is a standard parent FK. The storage_location_code string attribute becomes']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Concentrate batches are costed for inventory valuation (IAS 2), COGS calculation upon sale, and product-level profitability analysis. Links production output to financial inventory and revenue recogni']
  feed_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: Concentrate batches are produced from specific feed streams. One batch from one feed stream; one feed stream produces many batches. This links the batch to the feed stream that was processed. The ore_']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Concentrate handling (dust generation, moisture control, loading/transport) can trigger environmental incidents (dust emissions) or safety incidents (slips, equipment failures). Batch-level traceabili']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Concentrate samples sent to designated labs (often umpire labs) for final grade determination, moisture content, and penalty element analysis. Critical for sales contract settlement, invoicing, and co']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan against which this batch production is tracked and reconciled for long-term planning purposes.']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Concentrate batches are traced to source orebody for product provenance, quality control, and customer specifications. Required for sales contract compliance, penalty element tracking, and demonstrati']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant where this concentrate batch was produced. Links to the plant master record.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Concentrate production must be reconciled against mine production schedules for metal accounting, LOM plan tracking, and mine-to-mill optimization. Critical for integrated production reporting and fin']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Concentrate batches trace back to ore source for quality control, contract compliance, and resource reconciliation. Real business need: product traceability, grade reconciliation, and ore reserve depl']
  quality_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Sales contract compliance and customer quality assurance require traceability to qualified metallurgist who approved batch for dispatch. quality_approved_timestamp exists but no approver FK; regulator']
  quality_maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Concentrate quality issues (off-spec grade, high impurities) may trace to equipment maintenance needs (screen wear, cyclone apex wear, flotation cell condition). Links product quality problems to main']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Concentrate batches are instances of saleable products and must reference the product master for specification compliance, pricing basis, and quality parameters. The current product_type (string) shou']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Concentrate batches are produced during a production shift run. One batch belongs to one shift run; one shift run produces many batches. This links the batch to the processing domain\'s shift entity.']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this concentrate batch was produced. Used for shift-level production reporting and reconciliation.']
  source_tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Concentrate batches must be traced to source tenements for royalty payment calculations based on production value, regulatory reporting by tenement, and sales contract compliance requiring origin cert']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_specification. Business justification: Concentrate batches must meet contractual specifications for grade, moisture, impurities. Critical for quality control, sales contract compliance, and penalty/bonus calculations. Complements existing ']
  waste_rock_dump_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.waste_rock_dump. Business justification: Some concentrate production processes generate waste rock co-products (e.g., gravity separation rejects) sent to waste rock dumps. Material tracking requirement for inventory reconciliation and waste ']
  assay_number varchar [note: 'The unique laboratory assay reference number assigned by LabWare LIMS for the chemical analysis (assay) performed on this concentrate batch. Links the batch to its quality certificate and detailed elemental analysis results.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the concentrate batch, used across sales, logistics, and laboratory systems. Format: commodity prefix, year, and sequential number (e.g., CU-2024-000123). Sourced from LabWare LIMS batch numbering.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the concentrate batch through the production-to-dispatch workflow: in_progress (being produced), sampled (sample taken for assay), assayed (lab results received), approved (quality approved for sale), dispatched (shipped to customer), rejected (failed quality control). Sourced from LabWare LIMS.']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'The primary commodity contained in this concentrate batch (e.g., copper, iron ore, lithium, nickel, coal). Drives downstream sales, logistics, and regulatory reporting classification.']
  concentrate_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The grade of the primary commodity in the final concentrate product for this batch, expressed as a percentage (% Cu, % Fe, % Li, % Ni). The key product quality metric used in sales contracts and customer specifications. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this concentrate batch record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and governance compliance.']
  dispatch_mode varchar [note: 'The primary transport mode used to dispatch this concentrate batch from the processing plant to the port or customer (e.g., rail, road, ship, pipeline, conveyor). Determines logistics cost allocation and supply chain planning.']
  dry_weight_dmt decimal(18,4) [note: 'Dry weight of the concentrate batch in dry metric tonnes (DMT), calculated after moisture correction. The primary commercial weight measure used for sales contracts, invoicing, and production reporting. Key SSOT field for concentrate production output.']
  energy_consumption_kwh_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Electrical energy consumed per tonne of ore processed for this concentrate batch, expressed in kilowatt-hours per tonne (kWh/t). Sourced from OSIsoft PI System. Key OPEX and sustainability metric tracked against LOM plan targets.']
  feed_tonnes decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total dry metric tonnes of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed into the processing circuit to produce this concentrate batch. Used as the denominator in recovery rate and yield calculations.']
  head_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The grade of the primary commodity in the feed ore (Run of Mine (ROM)) entering the processing circuit for this batch, expressed as a percentage (%). Represents the input quality before beneficiation. Used for recovery rate calculation and reconciliation.']
  impurity_silica_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Silica (SiO2) content in the concentrate batch expressed as a percentage (%). A key gangue impurity that affects product quality, smelter penalties, and customer acceptance. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results.']
  impurity_sulfur_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Sulfur (S) content in the concentrate batch expressed as a percentage (%). A critical impurity for smelter operations and environmental compliance. High sulfur levels may attract penalties or rejection. Sourced from LabWare LIMS.']
  is_quality_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this concentrate batch has passed quality control review and been formally approved for sale or dispatch. True = approved; False = pending or rejected. Set by the metallurgist or quality manager in LabWare LIMS.']
  moisture_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Moisture content of the concentrate batch expressed as a percentage (%) of wet weight. Determined by laboratory analysis per ISO 3087. Critical for DMT calculation and product quality compliance.']
  particle_size_p80_um decimal(10,4) [note: 'The P80 particle size of the concentrate batch in micrometres (µm), representing the sieve size through which 80% of the material passes. Critical comminution (size reduction) quality parameter affecting downstream processing efficiency and product specification compliance.']
  price_basis varchar [note: 'The Incoterms trade term defining the price basis and risk transfer point for the sale of this concentrate batch (e.g., Free on Board (FOB), Cost Insurance and Freight (CIF)). Determines revenue recognition point and logistics cost allocation under IFRS 15.']
  process_route varchar [note: 'The mineral processing route used to produce this concentrate batch (e.g., flotation, leaching, Dense Media Separation (DMS), Solvent Extraction Electrowinning (SX-EW), High Pressure Grinding Rolls (HPGR), Semi-Autogenous Grinding (SAG)). Determines applicable quality standards and reagent profiles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flotation|leaching|DMS|SX-EW|HPGR|SAG|gravity|magnetic_separation — promote to reference product]']
  production_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this concentrate batch was produced at the processing plant. Used for daily production reporting and reconciliation against the production schedule.']
  production_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the concentrate batch production was completed and the batch was sealed/finalised at the processing plant. Sourced from OSIsoft PI System historian.']
  provisional_price_usd_dmt decimal(18,4) [note: 'The provisional sale price per dry metric tonne (DMT) in US dollars (USD) agreed at the time of dispatch, subject to final price adjustment based on quotation period commodity prices. Used for initial revenue recognition under IFRS 15.']
  quality_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the concentrate batch was formally approved for sale following quality control review. Represents a key lifecycle milestone in the batch workflow. Sourced from LabWare LIMS.']
  quality_grade varchar [note: 'The quality classification assigned to this concentrate batch based on assay results and product specification compliance. Grade A = premium specification met; Grade B = standard specification met; Grade C = marginal specification; off_spec = outside specification but saleable; rejected = failed quality control and cannot be sold.']
  reagent_consumption_kg_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total reagent consumption per tonne of ore processed for this concentrate batch, expressed in kilograms per tonne (kg/t). Aggregates all flotation, leaching, or other process reagents. Used for operating expenditure (OPEX) tracking and process efficiency analysis.']
  recovery_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The percentage of the valuable mineral in the feed ore that is successfully recovered into the concentrate product for this batch. Calculated as (concentrate grade × DMT) / (head grade × feed tonnes) × 100. Key processing efficiency KPI tracked against LOM plan targets.']
  sales_contract_number varchar [note: 'The sales contract reference number under which this concentrate batch is sold to a customer. Links the production batch to the commercial sales agreement. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity of contract terms.']
  sample_date date [note: 'The date on which the representative sample was taken from this concentrate batch for laboratory assay analysis. Used for sample chain-of-custody tracking and quality control compliance.']
  slurry_density_gcm3 decimal(8,4) [note: 'The density of the concentrate slurry (water and ore mixture) in grams per cubic centimetre (g/cm³) at the point of batch finalisation. Monitored via OSIsoft PI System SCADA. Used for process control and dewatering circuit optimisation.']
  throughput_tph decimal(10,4) [note: 'The average ore processing throughput rate in tonnes per hour (tph) achieved during the production of this concentrate batch. Sourced from OSIsoft PI System SCADA historian. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) and capacity utilisation analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this concentrate batch record was last modified. Audit trail field used for change tracking, data lineage, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  water_consumption_m3_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Process water consumed per tonne of ore processed for this concentrate batch, expressed in cubic metres per tonne (m³/t). Critical environmental compliance metric reported under ISO 14001 and GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement.']
  wet_weight_t decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total wet weight of the concentrate batch in metric tonnes (t) as measured at the processing plant prior to moisture correction. Used as the basis for moisture content calculation.']
  yield_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The mass yield of concentrate produced as a percentage of the total ore feed for this batch (DMT concentrate / feed tonnes × 100). Indicates the ratio of product output to ore input and is used for process efficiency benchmarking.']

  Note: 'Master record of each concentrate production batch produced at the processing plant. Captures batch number, commodity type (copper concentrate, iron ore fines, lithium hydroxide), production date, dry weight (dmt), moisture content (%), head grade, concentrate grade (% Cu, % Fe, % Li), and recovery rate (%). SSOT for concentrate production output linked to sales and product domains.'
}

Table processing.metallurgical_balance {
  metallurgical_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each metallurgical balance record in the processing domain. Primary key for the metallurgical_balance data product.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill circuit, flotation circuit, leach circuit, SX-EW circuit) within the plant for which this balance applies.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Metallurgical balances are commodity-specific for mass/metal accounting. Essential for JORC compliance, resource reconciliation, and ore reserve reporting. Replaces denormalized commodity text field. ']
  competent_person_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified competent person responsible for signing off this metallurgical balance, as required under JORC, NI 43-101, or SAMREC reporting standards.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Metallurgical accounting periods reconcile metal production to cost centres for financial reporting, variance analysis, and AISC calculation. Core mining finance process linking technical metallurgica']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Metallurgical balance samples analyzed at specific labs for reconciliation and reporting. Tracks which laboratory provided assay data for metal accounting, reserve reconciliation, and competent person']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version against which this metallurgical balance is being reconciled. Enables tracking of actual performance versus long-term plan targets.']
  mining_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mining_block. Business justification: Metallurgical balances reconcile processing performance against mining block model predictions for resource model updates, F1/F2/F3 factor calculation, and geological domain recovery characterization.']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Metallurgical balances are calculated per orebody to track recovery performance by ore source. Essential for resource-to-reserve conversion, mine planning optimization, and validating geological model']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or circuit where this metallurgical balance was recorded. Links to the plant master in the processing domain.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Metallurgical balances reconcile processing performance against scheduled mine production for period-based metal accounting, recovery variance analysis, and integrated mine-mill performance reporting ']
  resource_estimate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.resource_estimate. Business justification: Metallurgical balances validate resource estimate recovery assumptions. Required for reserve conversion, competent person sign-off, and JORC/NI 43-101 compliance. Real business need: resource-to-reser']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Metallurgical balances reconcile production against tenement reserves and are required for statutory reporting by tenement to regulatory authorities. Essential for reserve depletion tracking and compl']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Metallurgical balance periods must reconcile tailings_tonnes_dry sent to specific TSF. Fundamental accounting control for mass balance closure, regulatory reporting, and TSF capacity verification. Lin']
  accounting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the metallurgical accounting period covered by this balance record. Together with accounting_period_start defines the reconciliation window.']
  accounting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the metallurgical accounting period covered by this balance record (daily, weekly, or monthly). Aligns with the financial reporting calendar.']
  adjustment_authoriser varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the competent person or authorised metallurgist who approved the adjustment or restatement of this balance record. Required for JORC-compliant audit trail and regulatory sign-off.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for any adjustment or restatement applied to this version of the metallurgical balance (e.g., assay rerun, sampling error correction, instrument calibration update, stockpile reweigh). Required for audit trail and competent person sign-off.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this metallurgical balance record was formally approved and signed off by the competent person. Marks the transition to approved status in the balance workflow.']
  balance_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the metallurgical balance record, used in reports, sign-off documentation, and regulatory submissions. Format: METBAL-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN.']
  balance_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the metallurgical balance record: draft (in preparation), submitted (awaiting review), under_review (being assessed by competent person), approved (signed off), or rejected (returned for correction).']
  concentrate_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Assayed grade of the concentrate product expressed in the same unit as feed_grade_unit. Determines the commercial value and smelter payability of the concentrate.']
  concentrate_tonnes_dry decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total dry metric tonnes of concentrate product produced during the accounting period. Represents the valuable mineral product output from the processing circuit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this metallurgical balance record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  dilution_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Estimated percentage of waste rock (gangue) dilution in the feed material, representing the proportion of non-ore material included during mining and haulage. Impacts feed grade and recovery.']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total electrical energy consumed by the processing circuit during the accounting period in kilowatt hours. Key OPEX component and sustainability metric reported under GRI and ICMM frameworks.']
  feed_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Assayed head grade of the feed material expressed as percentage (%) for base metals or grams per tonne (g/t) for precious metals. Determined by LabWare LIMS assay results on feed samples.']
  feed_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the feed head grade value: pct (percentage), g_per_t (grams per tonne), kg_per_t (kilograms per tonne), or ppm (parts per million). Varies by commodity.']
  feed_moisture_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Moisture content of the feed material as a percentage of wet weight. Used to convert wet tonnes to dry tonnes for metallurgical accounting purposes.']
  feed_tonnes_dry decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total dry metric tonnes of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed to the processing circuit during the accounting period. Dry weight basis is the standard for metallurgical accounting.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record is the current (latest approved) version of the metallurgical balance for the given period and circuit. True = current version; False = superseded version retained for audit trail.']
  mass_balance_closure_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Mass balance closure expressed as a percentage: (concentrate_tonnes_dry + tailings_tonnes_dry) / feed_tonnes_dry × 100. Values outside 98–102% typically trigger investigation and adjustment.']
  measured_recovery_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Actual metallurgical recovery percentage calculated from assayed feed, concentrate, and tailings grades and tonnes using the standard two-product formula. Represents the proportion of valuable mineral recovered into concentrate.']
  metal_units_feed decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total contained metal units in the feed stream for the period, calculated as feed_tonnes_dry × feed_grade. Expressed in tonnes of metal or troy ounces depending on commodity. Key input to JORC resource-to-reserve reconciliation.']
  metal_units_recovered decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total contained metal units recovered into concentrate for the period, calculated as concentrate_tonnes_dry × concentrate_grade. Used in LOM plan tracking and sales reconciliation.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Geological ore type classification of the feed material processed during the period (e.g., oxide, sulphide, transitional, fresh, weathered). Ore type significantly influences metallurgical recovery and reagent consumption.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the metallurgical accounting period: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annual. Determines the reporting frequency and aggregation level for LOM plan tracking.']
  plant_availability_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the accounting period during which the processing plant was available for production (scheduled hours minus planned and unplanned downtime). Component of Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE).']
  reagent_consumption_kg_per_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total reagent consumption per dry metric tonne of feed processed during the period, aggregated across all reagents (collectors, frothers, pH modifiers, leach reagents). Key OPEX driver for processing cost reporting.']
  recovery_variance_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Difference between measured_recovery_pct and theoretical_recovery_pct (measured minus theoretical). Positive value indicates outperformance; negative indicates underperformance against LOM plan.']
  tailings_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Assayed grade of the tailings stream expressed in the same unit as feed_grade_unit. Indicates the residual valuable mineral content lost to tailings; key input to recovery calculation and environmental reporting.']
  tailings_tonnes_dry decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total dry metric tonnes of tailings (gangue and process waste) discharged to the Tailings Storage Facility (TSF) during the accounting period. Used in mass balance closure and TSF capacity planning.']
  theoretical_recovery_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Theoretical or planned recovery percentage for the period based on LOM plan targets, ore type characterisation, and process design parameters. Used as the benchmark for variance analysis.']
  throughput_rate_tph decimal(10,3) [note: 'Average processing throughput rate in dry metric tonnes per hour during the accounting period. Key operational KPI for plant performance against design capacity and LOM plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this metallurgical balance record was last modified. Updated on every version increment, adjustment, or status change. Supports audit trail requirements.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this metallurgical balance record for the given period and circuit. Increments with each adjustment or restatement, supporting full audit trail and competent person sign-off requirements.']
  water_consumption_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Total fresh water consumed by the processing circuit during the accounting period in cubic metres. Reported for environmental compliance under ISO 14001 and GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement.']

  Note: 'Periodic (daily/weekly/monthly) metallurgical accounting reconciliation record for a processing circuit or plant. Captures accounting period, feed tonnes, feed grade, concentrate tonnes, concentrate grade, tailings tonnes, tailings grade, calculated recovery (%), theoretical recovery, reconciliation variance, adjustment reason, adjustment authoriser, and version number for audit trail. Supports JORC-compliant resource-to-reserve reconciliation, LOM plan tracking, and regulatory reporting. Versioned records with adjustment metadata provide the audit trail required for metallurgical balance sign-off by competent persons.'
}

Table processing.processing_recovery_target {
  processing_recovery_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each recovery target record in the LOM plan-derived processing circuit performance baseline.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill circuit, flotation circuit, SX-EW circuit, DMS circuit) for which this recovery target applies.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Recovery targets are set per commodity in LOM plans and feasibility studies. Core to mine planning, economic modeling, and performance tracking. Replaces denormalized commodity text field with proper ']
  feasibility_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.feasibility_study. Business justification: Recovery targets originate from feasibility study assumptions and carry through to operations. Tracks study basis for performance guarantee baseline, variance analysis against study assumptions, reser']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version from which this recovery target is derived. Enables traceability of targets back to the authorised planning document.']
  mining_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mining_block. Business justification: Recovery targets vary by mining block ore type, grade, and metallurgical domain for domain-based planning, ore type scheduling, and metallurgical performance forecasting. Critical for LOM planning wit']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Recovery targets are set per orebody based on geological characteristics, mineralogy, and metallurgical testwork. Life-of-mine planning requires orebody-specific recovery assumptions to accurately for']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or facility where the circuit operates. Supports multi-site recovery target management.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Recovery targets must align with production schedules for integrated mine-mill planning, metal production forecasting, and performance variance analysis. Critical for LOM planning and short-term produ']
  resource_estimate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.resource_estimate. Business justification: Recovery targets are derived from resource estimate metallurgical assumptions. Critical for LOM planning, economic modeling, and feasibility study validation. Real business need: linking processing ta']
  revenue_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.revenue_forecast. Business justification: Revenue forecasts assume metallurgical recovery rates from processing targets to project saleable product volumes. Financial planning, investor reporting, and budget variance analysis require linking ']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Competent Person or authorised technical authority who approved this recovery target for use in LOM planning and variance reporting. Required for JORC/NI 43-101 traceability.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this recovery target was formally approved by the authorised Competent Person or planning authority. Provides audit trail for regulatory and governance compliance.']
  basis_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the technical study or planning document type that forms the basis for this recovery target (e.g., LOM plan, definitive feasibility study, pre-feasibility study, scoping study, annual budget). Indicates the confidence level and authority of the target.']
  concentrate_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target concentrate grade value (e.g., pct for percentage, g_per_t for grams per tonne). Ensures correct interpretation of the concentrate quality specification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this recovery target record was first created in the data platform. Provides audit trail for data lineage and governance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this recovery target version becomes the active baseline for variance analysis and performance reporting. Supports Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD) Type 2 versioning of LOM plan targets.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this recovery target version ceases to be the active baseline, typically when superseded by a revised LOM plan. Null indicates the record is currently active. Supports SCD Type 2 versioning.']
  feasibility_study_ref varchar [note: 'Document reference number or title of the feasibility study, pre-feasibility study (PFS), or definitive feasibility study (DFS) from which this recovery target is derived. Provides audit traceability to the authorised technical study.']
  head_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target head grade value (e.g., pct for percentage, g_per_t for grams per tonne, ppm for parts per million). Ensures correct interpretation of the grade figure across different commodities.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents the current active version of the recovery target for the given circuit, commodity, ore type, and period. Supports efficient querying of the latest approved targets without filtering on version history.']
  lom_plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the LOM plan from which this target is derived (e.g., LOM-2024-v3.1). Enables tracking of target changes across successive LOM plan revisions and supports variance analysis against the correct plan baseline.']
  metal_units_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target contained metal units (e.g., t for metric tonnes, oz for troy ounces, lb for pounds). Ensures correct interpretation across different commodity reporting conventions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording planning assumptions, metallurgical test work caveats, ore type blending assumptions, or other contextual information relevant to the interpretation of this recovery target.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ore type or metallurgical domain for which this target applies (e.g., oxide, sulphide, transitional, mixed, fresh). Ore type significantly influences achievable recovery rates and processing parameters.']
  processing_route varchar [note: 'The primary mineral processing method or beneficiation route for which this target applies (e.g., flotation, Dense Media Separation (DMS), leaching, Solvent Extraction Electrowinning (SX-EW), High Pressure Grinding Rolls (HPGR), Semi-Autogenous Grinding (SAG)). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flotation|leaching|dms|sxew|gravity|magnetic_separation|hpgr|sag|cil|cip|other — promote to reference product]']
  target_concentrate_grade decimal(10,5) [note: 'Planned grade of the final concentrate or product output from the processing circuit during the target period, expressed in the unit defined by concentrate_grade_unit. Represents the quality specification for the saleable product.']
  target_concentrate_kt decimal(12,3) [note: 'Planned total dry metric tonnes of concentrate (in kilotonnes) to be produced during the target period. Supports sales planning, logistics scheduling, and LOM revenue forecasting.']
  target_energy_consumption_kwh decimal(14,3) [note: 'Planned total electrical energy consumption in kilowatt-hours for the processing circuit during the target period. Used for energy cost budgeting, carbon footprint reporting, and OPEX modelling.']
  target_feed_moisture_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Planned moisture content of the Run of Mine (ROM) feed entering the processing circuit, expressed as a percentage. Affects dry tonne calculations, throughput, and reagent dosing. Derived from ore characterisation in the feasibility study.']
  target_head_grade decimal(10,5) [note: 'Planned feed grade (head grade) of the ore entering the processing circuit during the target period, expressed in the unit defined by head_grade_unit. Derived from the mine plan and geological resource model.']
  target_mass_pull_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Planned mass pull percentage — the proportion of feed mass reporting to the concentrate stream. A key flotation circuit design parameter that balances recovery against concentrate grade. Derived from metallurgical test work in the feasibility study.']
  target_metal_units decimal(14,3) [note: 'Planned quantity of contained metal units to be recovered during the target period, expressed in the unit defined by metal_units_unit (e.g., tonnes of copper, ounces of gold). Core metric for LOM production target and revenue planning.']
  target_period_end date [note: 'End date of the planning period to which this recovery target applies. Defines the upper boundary of the target window for performance measurement and LOM plan variance reporting.']
  target_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the planning period to which this recovery target applies. Combined with target_period_end to define the exact window for variance analysis against actual metallurgical balance results.']
  target_period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the planning period for which this recovery target is defined: monthly, quarterly, annual, or full Life of Mine (LOM). Determines the temporal resolution of variance analysis.']
  target_plant_availability_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Planned plant availability percentage for the processing circuit during the target period, representing the proportion of scheduled time the plant is available for production. Used in conjunction with throughput targets to derive planned production volumes.']
  target_reagent_consumption decimal(10,4) [note: 'Planned total reagent consumption rate in kilograms per dry metric tonne of ore processed. Aggregated across all reagents for the circuit. Used for OPEX budgeting and reagent procurement planning.']
  target_recovery_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Planned metallurgical recovery rate expressed as a percentage of valuable mineral recovered from feed to final product (concentrate or cathode). Derived from LOM plan or feasibility study metallurgical test work. Core KPI for processing performance variance analysis.']
  target_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this recovery target record, used in planning documents, feasibility studies, and variance reporting (e.g., RT-CU-FL-202401).']
  target_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this recovery target record: draft (under preparation), approved (authorised for use in variance reporting), superseded (replaced by a newer version), or archived (no longer active).']
  target_throughput_kt decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total planned dry metric tonnes (in kilotonnes) to be processed through the circuit during the target period. Complements the rate-based tph target for period-level production planning and LOM schedule adherence.']
  target_throughput_tph decimal(10,3) [note: 'Planned processing throughput rate in dry metric tonnes per hour (tph) for the circuit during the target period. Used to assess whether actual mill throughput meets LOM plan expectations.']
  target_utilisation_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Planned plant utilisation percentage — the proportion of available time the plant is actually operating and processing ore. Distinct from availability; utilisation reflects operational efficiency within available time.']
  target_water_consumption_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Planned total water consumption in cubic metres for the processing circuit during the target period. Supports water balance management, environmental licence compliance, and sustainability reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this recovery target record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'LOM plan-derived recovery rate targets per processing circuit, commodity, and ore type. Captures target period (month/quarter/year), circuit ID, ore type, target recovery (%), target throughput (tph), target head grade, target concentrate grade, and basis (LOM plan version, feasibility study reference). Enables variance analysis between actual and planned recovery performance.'
}

Table processing.operational_exception {
  operational_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for operational_exception']
