  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary piece of equipment directly involved in or causing the operational exception. Links to the equipment asset register.']
  change_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.change_order. Business justification: Operational exceptions during commissioning/defects liability trigger change orders against project contracts. Supports defects liability claims, contractor performance disputes, warranty claims, and ']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific processing circuit affected by the exception (e.g., SAG mill circuit, flotation circuit, leach circuit, SX-EW circuit, DMS circuit). Links to the circuit master.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Operational exceptions (downtime, process upsets) occur at specific functional locations. Links exceptions to maintenance functional location hierarchy for root cause analysis, maintenance planning, a']
  grievance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.grievance. Business justification: Plant downtime and operational upsets trigger community complaints (extended noise, visible emissions, safety concerns). Required for incident investigation linking operational exceptions to community']
  heritage_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.heritage_incident. Business justification: Processing plant operational exceptions can cause or coincide with cultural heritage incidents (vibration damage to sites, water discharge affecting sacred areas). Required for heritage incident inves']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Processing plant downtime events that trigger HSE incidents must reference the formal incident record for compliance reporting and root cause analysis. The current hse_incident_reference (string) shou']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version against which production loss from this exception is measured. Enables variance reporting between actual downtime impact and LOM plan targets.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mineral processing plant where the operational exception occurred. Links to the plant master record.']
  primary_operational_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant operator or control room operator responsible for logging and managing this operational exception. Links to the workforce/employee master. Used for accountability tracking and operator performance analysis.']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Operational exceptions occur during a production shift run. One exception belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many exceptions. This links the exception to the processing domain\'s shift entity.']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shift report during which this operational exception was recorded. Links to the mine or plant shift report.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance work order raised in response to this operational exception. Enables linkage to the maintenance domain for corrective or emergency work orders.']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The measured actual value of the process parameter at the time of the deviation or alarm event. Compared against setpoint_value to determine deviation magnitude and against alarm limits for threshold breach analysis.']
  alarm_limit_high decimal(18,4) [note: 'The configured high alarm threshold for the process parameter at the time of the event. Used to contextualise whether the actual_value breached the high alarm boundary and to support alarm rationalisation analysis.']
  alarm_limit_low decimal(18,4) [note: 'The configured low alarm threshold for the process parameter at the time of the event. Used to contextualise whether the actual_value breached the low alarm boundary.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action taken by the operator or maintenance team to resolve the operational exception and restore normal plant operations. Feeds maintenance knowledge base and SOP review processes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operational exception record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and record provenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the operational exception was first detected: scada_alarm (automated SCADA/PLC alarm), operator_observation (manual observation by plant operator), dcs_alert (Distributed Control System alert), lab_result (assay or process sample result), inspection (physical inspection finding), predictive_alert (predictive maintenance or process analytics alert).']
  deviation_magnitude decimal(18,4) [note: 'Absolute difference between actual_value and setpoint_value at the time of the exception. Stored as a business field to support process control quality analysis and trend reporting without requiring arithmetic on raw values.']
  duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed duration of the operational exception in minutes, calculated from event_start_timestamp to event_end_timestamp. Stored as a business field to support OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) availability loss reporting without requiring real-time timestamp arithmetic.']
  event_category varchar [note: 'Operational category of the exception cause: mechanical (equipment failure), electrical (power or instrumentation fault), process (reagent, slurry, or chemistry issue), planned_maintenance (scheduled shutdown), feed_shortage (insufficient ROM feed), or parameter_exceedance (process variable breach). Drives OEE loss categorisation and maintenance domain routing.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operational exception event was resolved and normal operations resumed. Null if the event is still active. Used with event_start_timestamp to calculate event duration.']
  event_reference varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the operational exception event, used for cross-referencing with maintenance work orders, shift reports, and process control logs. Format: OE-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operational exception event commenced, as recorded by the SCADA/PLC system or operator. This is the principal real-world event time used for duration calculation and OEE loss attribution.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the operational exception record: open (event active, investigation pending), in_progress (corrective action underway), resolved (root cause identified and corrective action completed), closed (verified and signed off), cancelled (duplicate or incorrectly raised).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the operational exception by its fundamental nature: downtime (equipment or circuit stoppage), parameter_deviation (process variable outside set-point tolerance), alarm (SCADA/PLC alarm triggered), near_miss (safety or process near-miss), or process_upset (uncontrolled process condition). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: downtime|parameter_deviation|alarm|near_miss|process_upset — promote to reference product if additional types are required]']
  hse_incident_raised boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operational exception also triggered a formal HSE incident report in the IsoMetrix system (True) or not (False). Enables cross-domain linkage between process exceptions and HSE incident management.']
  immediate_action varchar [note: 'Description of the immediate containment action taken at the time of the exception to prevent further production loss, equipment damage, or safety risk, prior to full corrective action. Distinct from corrective_action which addresses root cause.']
  is_planned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime or exception event was planned (True) or unplanned (False). Planned events include scheduled maintenance shutdowns and planned feed stoppages. Used to segregate planned vs unplanned downtime in OEE calculations.']
  oee_loss_category varchar [note: 'OEE loss category to which this exception is attributed for OEE calculation purposes: availability (equipment or circuit not running), performance (running below rated throughput), or quality (product not meeting grade specification). Aligns with ISO 22400 OEE decomposition.']
  parameter_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the process parameter that deviated, providing context beyond the SCADA tag code. Examples: SAG Mill Feed Rate (tph), Flotation Cell Level (%), Leach Tank pH.']
  parameter_tag varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI System or SCADA tag identifier for the process parameter that deviated from its set-point. Applicable only for event_type = parameter_deviation or alarm. Examples: SAG_MILL_FEED_TPH, FLOTATION_CELL_LEVEL_PCT, LEACH_TANK_PH.']
  parameter_unit varchar [note: 'Engineering unit of measure for the process parameter set-point and actual values. Examples: tph (tonnes per hour), %, pH, m3/h, kPa, degC, g/L. Ensures correct interpretation of setpoint_value and actual_value.']
  production_loss_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Estimated ore or concentrate production lost in dry metric tonnes as a direct consequence of this operational exception. Used for OEE production loss attribution, LOM plan variance reporting, and OPEX impact quantification.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardised code classifying the root cause of the exception per the plant\'s failure taxonomy (e.g., ISO 14224 cause codes). Enables structured root cause analysis and trend reporting across events.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the identified root cause of the operational exception, as determined through investigation. Supports root cause analysis (RCA), recurring failure identification, and process improvement initiatives.']
  setpoint_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The target or set-point value for the process parameter at the time of the deviation event. Used in conjunction with actual_value to quantify the magnitude of the deviation. Applicable for event_type = parameter_deviation.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the operational exception indicating its impact on plant throughput, safety, and production targets. Critical: full circuit or plant stoppage; High: significant throughput reduction; Medium: partial degradation; Low: minor deviation with negligible production impact.']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the shift during which the operational exception commenced. Used for daily and shift-level production reporting and exception trend analysis.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Type of shift during which the operational exception occurred: day, night, or afternoon. Supports shift-level performance analysis and identification of shift-specific exception patterns.']
  throughput_loss_tph decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated reduction in processing throughput rate in tonnes per hour (tph) attributable to this exception event. Supports OEE rate loss analysis and process bottleneck identification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operational exception record was most recently modified, such as when root cause, corrective action, or resolution details were updated. Supports audit trail and data currency tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of operational exceptions within the processing plant including unplanned/planned downtime events and process parameter deviations from set-point targets. Captures event type (downtime, parameter_deviation, alarm), start/end timestamps, duration, affected circuit, category (mechanical, electrical, process, planned maintenance, feed shortage, parameter exceedance), severity, set-point vs actual values for deviations, root cause description, corrective action taken, responsible operator, and production loss (tonnes). Feeds OEE calculation, process control quality analysis, root cause investigation, and maintenance domain work order linkage.'
}

Table processing.process_water_balance {
  process_water_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each process water balance record in the processing plant. Primary key for the silver layer lakehouse table.']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Water system imbalances (excessive losses, seepage, evaporation) often indicate equipment failures (pump leaks, pipe failures, valve issues) requiring maintenance work orders. Links water management i']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant for which this water balance is recorded. Links to the plant master record.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Water balances must reconcile against production schedules for integrated water management, environmental compliance reporting, and specific water consumption benchmarking. Required for water licence ']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Water balance is recorded per shift run. One water balance record belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many water balance records. This links the water balance to the processing domain\'s shift ']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this water balance was recorded. Used for shift-level water consumption tracking and reporting.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Process water balance tracks recycled_water_intake_m3 and tsf_return_water_m3 from specific TSF. Essential for closed water circuit management, water licence compliance, and integrated site water bala']
  water_licence_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the applicable water licence or water use permit under which the processing plant operates. Used for compliance tracking against licence limits.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the environmental or process engineer who approved this water balance record for regulatory submission and reporting purposes.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this water balance record was formally approved by the authorised environmental or process engineer.']
  balance_closure_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage closure of the water mass balance, calculated as total measured outputs divided by total measured inputs multiplied by 100. Values outside 95–105% indicate measurement errors or unaccounted losses requiring investigation.']
  balance_period_end timestamp [note: 'The end date and time of the water balance accounting period. Together with balance_period_start defines the measurement window.']
  balance_period_start timestamp [note: 'The start date and time of the water balance accounting period. May represent the beginning of a shift, day, or other reporting interval.']
  balance_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this water balance record, used in environmental reporting and regulatory submissions. Format: WB-YYYY-MM-DD-XXXX.']
  balance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the water balance record: draft (in preparation), submitted (pending review), approved (validated and accepted), or rejected (returned for correction).']
  concentrate_moisture_loss_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Volume of water lost from the processing circuit as moisture contained within the concentrate product dispatched during the balance period, measured in cubic metres.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this water balance record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality indicator for the water balance data: good (all measurements validated), suspect (one or more readings require verification), estimated (one or more values are engineering estimates), or missing (one or more required measurements unavailable).']
  evaporation_loss_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Estimated volume of water lost through evaporation from open process vessels, ponds, and the TSF surface during the balance period, measured in cubic metres.']
  fresh_water_intake_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Volume of fresh water drawn from external sources (rivers, bores, municipal supply) into the processing plant during the balance period, measured in cubic metres. Key input for water licence compliance.']
  fresh_water_licence_limit_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Maximum volume of fresh water permitted to be drawn during the balance period as specified in the water use licence. Used for compliance tracking and early warning of licence exceedance.']
  is_licence_limit_exceeded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fresh water intake during the balance period exceeded the permitted water licence limit. True = licence limit breached; False = within licence. Triggers regulatory notification obligations.']
  licence_utilisation_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the water licence limit consumed during the balance period, calculated as fresh water intake divided by the licence limit. Used for proactive licence management and environmental compliance dashboards.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to measure water volumes in this balance record: flow_meter (calibrated inline meter), manual_gauge (manual reading), scada (SCADA/PI System automated reading), or estimated (engineering estimate). Affects data quality confidence.']
  net_water_consumption_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Net volume of water consumed by the processing plant during the balance period, calculated as total water input minus total water output, measured in cubic metres. Key metric for water stewardship reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording operational observations, anomalies, measurement issues, or contextual information relevant to this water balance period (e.g., plant shutdown, meter calibration event, rainfall event).']
  ore_processed_t decimal(14,3) [note: 'Dry tonnes of ore processed through the plant during the balance period. Used as the denominator for specific water consumption calculations and throughput-normalised water metrics.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the water balance reporting period: shift-level, daily, weekly, or monthly. Determines the aggregation level for environmental and operational reporting.']
  process_water_intake_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Volume of water entering the processing circuit from ore moisture, reagent solutions, and other process-bound sources during the balance period, measured in cubic metres.']
  recycled_water_intake_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Volume of recycled or reclaimed water returned from tailings storage facility (TSF), thickeners, or other internal circuits and reused in the processing plant during the balance period, measured in cubic metres.']
  recycled_water_ratio_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of total water input that was sourced from recycled or reclaimed water during the balance period. Measures water recycling efficiency and supports sustainability reporting targets.']
  seepage_loss_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Estimated volume of water lost through ground seepage from process ponds, pipelines, and the TSF during the balance period, measured in cubic metres. Monitored for environmental compliance.']
  slurry_density_pct_solids decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average percentage of solids by weight in the process slurry during the balance period. Directly influences water consumption in comminution and flotation circuits and is a key process control parameter.']
  specific_water_consumption_m3_per_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Volume of fresh water consumed per tonne of ore processed during the balance period, measured in cubic metres per tonne (m³/t). Key operational efficiency and environmental performance indicator benchmarked against Life of Mine (LOM) plan targets.']
  specific_water_consumption_target_m3_per_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Life of Mine (LOM) plan target for specific water consumption in cubic metres per tonne of ore processed. Used to calculate variance against actual performance for operational and sustainability reporting.']
  tailings_slurry_density_pct_solids decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average percentage of solids by weight in the tailings slurry discharged to the TSF during the balance period. Determines the volume of water reporting to the TSF and influences TSF water recovery rates.']
  tailings_water_loss_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Volume of water discharged to the tailings storage facility (TSF) as part of the tailings slurry during the balance period, measured in cubic metres. Critical for TSF water management and environmental compliance.']
  total_water_input_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Sum of all water inputs (fresh, recycled, and process water) entering the processing plant during the balance period, measured in cubic metres. Used as the denominator for specific water consumption calculations.']
  total_water_output_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Sum of all water outputs (concentrate moisture, tailings, evaporation, seepage) leaving the processing circuit during the balance period, measured in cubic metres.']
  tsf_return_water_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Volume of water recovered from the tailings storage facility (TSF) decant pond and returned to the processing plant as recycled water during the balance period, measured in cubic metres.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this water balance record was last modified. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and incremental processing in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  water_source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary fresh water source supplying the processing plant during the balance period. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bore|river|dam|municipal|stormwater|tsf_return — promote to reference product if additional sources are required]']
  water_stress_zone varchar [note: 'Classification of the water stress level of the catchment area in which the processing plant operates, aligned with the World Resources Institute (WRI) Aqueduct framework. Informs water stewardship risk reporting.']

  Note: 'Periodic water balance record for the processing plant capturing water inputs (fresh, recycled, process), outputs (evaporation, concentrate moisture, tailings), and net consumption per shift or day. Captures water source volumes (m³), recycled water ratio (%), specific water consumption (m³/t), and compliance against water licence limits. Supports environmental reporting and water stewardship.'
}

Table processing.concentrate_stockpile {
  concentrate_stockpile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each concentrate stockpile inventory record in the processing domain. Primary key for this entity.']
  cargo_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.cargo_shipment. Business justification: Cargo shipments draw concentrate from stockpiles for vessel loading. Inventory reconciliation, demurrage management, and loading rate optimization require tracking which stockpile feeds each shipment.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Stockpiles hold specific commodities (iron ore concentrate, copper concentrate). Fundamental for inventory management, blending decisions, and production reconciliation. Replaces denormalized commodit']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Stockpile inventory is valued and tracked against cost centres for balance sheet reporting (IAS 2 inventory), NRV testing, and working capital management. Links physical inventory to financial asset a']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan against which this stockpile inventory is tracked for production target reconciliation.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the processing plant or facility that produced or holds this concentrate stockpile. Links to the plant master record.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles track inventory against scheduled production for shipment planning, metal accounting, and working capital management. Critical for sales contract fulfillment and financial repor']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Stockpiles contain specific saleable products awaiting dispatch. Essential for sales order fulfillment, inventory valuation, and linking physical inventory to sales contracts. Standard in mining logis']
  sales_shipment_nomination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.shipment_nomination. Business justification: Shipment nominations allocate specific stockpile inventory to customer orders and vessel schedules. Logistics planning, inventory management, and customer delivery commitments depend on linking nomina']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.warehouse_location. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles are physical storage locations requiring warehouse management for inventory reconciliation, site access control, environmental compliance, and security. Links processing output ']
  additions_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Cumulative dry metric tonnes of concentrate added to this stockpile from production since the last inventory reconciliation period. Represents inflows from the processing circuit (e.g., flotation, leaching, SX-EW). Used in mass balance and inventory reconciliation.']
  blend_target_grade decimal(8,4) [note: 'Target product grade (%) for this stockpile as specified in the blend optimisation plan or sales contract specification. Used to guide production blending decisions to meet customer or smelter feed requirements.']
  concentrate_type varchar [note: 'Mineralogical classification of the concentrate product in this stockpile. Determines applicable processing routes, smelter penalty elements, and sales contract terms. E.g., sulphide concentrate from flotation vs oxide concentrate from leaching/SX-EW.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this concentrate stockpile record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for record provenance and data lineage.']
  dust_suppression_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dust suppression measures (e.g., water sprays, chemical binders, covers) are currently applied to this stockpile. Relevant for environmental compliance, HSE reporting, and community impact management.']
  grade_element varchar [note: 'Chemical element or compound symbol for which the product_grade is reported (e.g., \'Cu\' for copper, \'Fe\' for iron, \'Li2O\' for lithium oxide, \'Ni\' for nickel). Ensures unambiguous interpretation of the grade value across commodities.']
  inventory_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Current physical inventory of concentrate in the stockpile expressed as dry metric tonnes (DMT), adjusted for moisture content. This is the primary inventory quantity used for sales nominations, shipping calculations, and inventory reconciliation. Updated after each survey event.']
  inventory_wmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Current physical inventory of concentrate in the stockpile expressed as wet metric tonnes (WMT), inclusive of moisture. Used for physical handling, transport weight calculations, and logistics planning. Distinct from DMT which is the commercial quantity.']
  is_shipment_nominated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stockpile inventory has been nominated against an active shipment or vessel loading schedule. True = nominated for an upcoming shipment; False = available for future nomination. Supports logistics scheduling and sales operations.']
  is_under_environmental_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stockpile is subject to an environmental hold that restricts movement or shipment of material (e.g., pending EIS review, acid rock drainage concern, or regulatory compliance investigation). True = hold in place; False = no environmental restriction.']
  last_assay_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent laboratory assay used to determine the current product grade and moisture content of the stockpile. Used to assess grade data currency for sales nominations and blend optimisation.']
  last_survey_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical survey or measurement used to determine the current stockpile inventory volume and tonnage. Critical for assessing inventory data currency and triggering re-survey workflows when data is stale.']
  lom_inventory_target_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Target concentrate inventory level in dry metric tonnes as specified in the Life of Mine (LOM) plan for the current period. Used to assess inventory performance against plan and trigger production rate adjustments.']
  max_capacity_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Engineered maximum storage capacity of the stockpile expressed in dry metric tonnes (DMT). Used for capacity planning, logistics scheduling, and triggering production rate adjustments when inventory approaches capacity limits.']
  max_moisture_spec decimal(6,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable moisture content (%) for this stockpile as defined by the applicable sales contract, shipping specification, or transportable moisture limit (TML) under the IMSBC Code. Exceedance triggers quarantine or drying operations.']
  min_grade_spec decimal(8,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable product grade (%) for this stockpile as defined by the applicable sales contract or offtake agreement. Material below this threshold may be subject to penalty pricing or rejection.']
  moisture_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Current moisture content of the concentrate stockpile expressed as a percentage of wet weight. Used to convert between WMT and DMT, assess handleability, and ensure compliance with shipping moisture limits (e.g., transportable moisture limit for bulk cargo under IMSBC Code).']
  nominated_shipment_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Quantity of concentrate in dry metric tonnes nominated against an active shipment or vessel loading schedule from this stockpile. Enables available-to-nominate calculation (inventory_dmt minus nominated_shipment_dmt) for logistics planning.']
  opening_inventory_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Dry metric tonne inventory at the start of the current reconciliation period. Together with additions_dmt and removals_dmt, enables closing inventory calculation and mass balance closure verification.']
  penalty_element_flags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of penalty elements detected in the concentrate above contractual threshold levels (e.g., \'As,Pb,Bi\'). Penalty elements attract price deductions in smelter contracts and may trigger quarantine or blending requirements. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results.']
  product_grade decimal(8,4) [note: 'Current assay-derived grade of the valuable mineral in the concentrate stockpile, expressed as a percentage (%). Represents the weighted average grade of all material currently in the stockpile based on the most recent survey or assay. Critical for sales nomination and blend optimisation.']
  reconciliation_period_end date [note: 'End date of the current inventory reconciliation period for which additions_dmt and removals_dmt are accumulated. Nullable if the period is still open.']
  reconciliation_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the current inventory reconciliation period for which additions_dmt and removals_dmt are accumulated. Aligns with metallurgical balance accounting periods for mass balance closure.']
  removals_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Cumulative dry metric tonnes of concentrate removed from this stockpile for shipment, blending, or transfer since the last inventory reconciliation period. Represents outflows to logistics, port, or downstream processing. Used in mass balance and inventory reconciliation.']
  stockpile_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this stockpile location, used in logistics scheduling, sales nominations, and inventory reconciliation reports. Sourced from SAP MM material document or plant layout register.']
  stockpile_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the concentrate stockpile (e.g., \'Plant Pad A — Copper Concentrate\', \'Port Shed 3 — Iron Ore Fines\'). Used in operational dashboards and logistics communications.']
  stockpile_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes recorded by the metallurgist or inventory controller regarding this stockpile, such as quality observations, blending instructions, survey anomalies, or logistics constraints. Supports operational decision-making and audit trail.']
  stockpile_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the stockpile. \'active\' = receiving or dispatching material; \'inactive\' = temporarily not in use; \'full\' = at maximum capacity; \'quarantine\' = material held pending quality resolution; \'closed\' = permanently decommissioned.']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'Method used to measure the current stockpile volume and derive inventory tonnage. Determines accuracy and frequency of inventory updates. Values: drone_lidar (aerial LiDAR scan), drone_photogrammetry (aerial photogrammetric survey), manual_survey (ground-based manual measurement), belt_scale (continuous belt weigher), weightometer (in-line weightometer), laser_scan (fixed laser scanner).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this concentrate stockpile record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Inventory record of concentrate stockpiles at processing plant pad and port storage facilities — the processing-domain view of physical inventory. Captures stockpile ID, location (plant pad, port shed, rail siding), commodity type, current inventory (dmt), grade (%), moisture (%), date of last survey (drone/LIDAR/manual), maximum capacity (dmt), and stockpile movement history (additions from production, removals for shipment). Supports logistics scheduling, sales nomination, blend optimisation, and inventory reconciliation. Note: this product owns the physical/operational stockpile state; commercial inventory positions, title transfer, and financial valuation are managed in the product and sales domains.'
}

Table processing.process_sample {
  process_sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each process sample record in the processing plant metallurgical accounting system.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill circuit, flotation circuit, leach circuit) within the plant from which the sample was collected.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Process samples assay specific commodities for grade control and metallurgical accounting. Core to quality control, mass balance reconciliation, and production reporting. Domain experts expect commodi']
  grade_control_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.grade_control_block. Business justification: Process samples validate grade control block predictions for reconciliation and resource model updates. Critical for closing the loop between mine grade control predictions and actual processing perfo']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Sampling activities involve exposure risks (dust inhalation, chemical contact, confined space entry). Incidents during sampling (exposure events, equipment failures, sample spills) need direct linkage']
  laboratory_sample_id varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the analytical laboratory (LabWare LIMS) upon receipt of the sample, enabling cross-domain linkage between the processing domain and the laboratory domain for assay results retrieval.']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan against which this sample\'s grade and recovery data is reconciled, supporting JORC-compliant resource and reserve reporting.']
  metallurgical_balance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the metallurgical balance period to which this sample contributes, supporting JORC-compliant mass balance closure and reconciliation.']
  mining_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mining_block. Business justification: Process samples reconcile against mining block model grades for resource model validation and geological domain characterization. Essential for F1/F2/F3 factor calculation and continuous improvement o']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Process samples are linked to source orebody for metallurgical accounting and grade reconciliation. Fundamental to mine-to-mill value chain tracking, validating geological models against actual proces']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the processing plant facility where the sample was collected. Links to the plant master record.']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Process samples are linked to ore source prospects for reconciliation and grade control. Critical for mine-to-mill optimization and production reconciliation. Real business need: tracking actual vs pr']
  rom_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.rom_stockpile. Business justification: Process samples validate ROM stockpile grade estimates for feed blending, grade control, and stockpile inventory reconciliation. Essential for closing the loop between stockpile survey grades and actu']
  sales_quality_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quality_certificate. Business justification: Quality certificates aggregate process sample assays to certify concentrate grade for contractual purposes. Dispute resolution, umpire assay reconciliation, and quality assurance audits require tracea']
  sampler_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who physically collected the sample at the sampling point, supporting chain of custody and quality assurance traceability.']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Process samples are collected during a production shift run. One sample belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many samples. This links the sample to the processing domain\'s shift entity.']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which the sample was collected, enabling shift-level metallurgical accounting and reconciliation.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Tailings samples (stream_type=\'tailings\') collected from specific TSF for quality control and environmental monitoring. QA/QC requirement for TSF geochemical characterization, ARD assessment, and regu']
  assay_element varchar [note: 'Primary commodity element or analyte for which the sample is being assayed (e.g., Cu, Fe, Au, Li, Ni, Co). Drives the metallurgical accounting grade reporting.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the process sample was physically collected at the sampling point. This is the principal real-world event time used for time-series analysis and metallurgical accounting period assignment.']
  composite_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'End time of the compositing period for composite samples, defining the close of the time window over which increments were collected. Null for grab or incremental samples.']
  composite_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Start time of the compositing period for composite samples, defining the beginning of the time window over which increments were collected. Null for grab or incremental samples.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this process sample record was first created in the system, providing the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and compliance.']
  dispatch_status varchar [note: 'Current dispatch status of the prepared sample to the analytical laboratory, tracking the chain of custody from plant to laboratory.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the prepared sample was physically dispatched to the analytical laboratory. Null if not yet dispatched.']
  head_grade decimal(12,6) [note: 'Assayed grade of the primary element in the sample, expressed in the unit defined by head_grade_unit. For feed samples this is the head grade; for concentrate or tailings samples this is the stream grade. Populated after laboratory analysis.']
  head_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the head_grade value: pct = percentage (%), g_per_t = grams per tonne, ppm = parts per million, ppb = parts per billion, kg_per_t = kilograms per tonne.']
  increment_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual increments combined to form a composite sample. Used to assess sampling representativeness per Theory of Sampling (TOS). Null for grab samples.']
  is_representative boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sample has been assessed as representative of the process stream for metallurgical accounting purposes. False samples are excluded from mass balance calculations.']
  mass_dry_g decimal(12,3) [note: 'Dry mass of the sample in grams after moisture removal during preparation. Used for dry-basis grade calculations and metallurgical accounting.']
  mass_wet_g decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total wet mass of the collected sample in grams as weighed at the sampling point prior to preparation. Used for mass balance calculations and sample representativeness assessment.']
  moisture_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Moisture content of the sample expressed as a percentage of wet mass, determined during sample preparation. Critical for converting wet-basis to dry-basis metallurgical accounting figures.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Mineralogical classification of the ore represented by the sample, used for metallurgical performance benchmarking and LOM plan reconciliation. Oxide, sulphide, mixed, transitional, and fresh rock types exhibit different processing behaviour.']
  particle_size_p80_um decimal(10,2) [note: '80th percentile passing particle size of the sample in micrometres (P80), characterising the grind size at the sampling point. Key comminution performance indicator for SAG and ball mill circuit control.']
  preparation_method varchar [note: 'Laboratory preparation method applied to the sample prior to assay, defining the comminution and splitting protocol. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crush_split|riffle_split|rotary_split|pulverise|filter_dry|evaporate|none — promote to reference product if additional methods required]']
  preparation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which sample preparation was completed in the sample preparation facility, marking readiness for dispatch to the laboratory.']
  pulp_density_pct_solids decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage solids by mass of the slurry at the sampling point, measured at time of collection. Relevant for slurry and pulp samples; null for dry ore samples.']
  qaqc_flag varchar [note: 'Quality Assurance and Quality Control (QAQC) classification of the sample: routine = standard production sample; blank = contamination check; standard = certified reference material for accuracy check; duplicate = laboratory duplicate for precision check; field_duplicate = field duplicate for sampling precision.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason a sample was rejected or flagged as non-representative, such as contamination, insufficient mass, sampler malfunction, or chain of custody breach. Null if sample is valid.']
  sample_medium varchar [note: 'Physical state or medium of the sample at the point of collection, distinguishing between slurry (wet ore/water mixture), dry ore, concentrate, leach solution (PLS), or pulp.']
  sample_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations recorded by the sampler or metallurgist at the time of collection or preparation, such as unusual process conditions, equipment anomalies, or visual observations.']
  sample_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique sample identifier assigned at collection, used for tracking through preparation, dispatch, and laboratory assay. Corresponds to the sample barcode or label number in LabWare LIMS.']
  sample_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the process sample tracking its workflow from collection through laboratory analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: collected|prepared|dispatched|received|analysed|rejected — promote to reference product if additional statuses required]']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the sample was collected: Composite = time or mass-proportional blend of multiple increments; Grab = single point-in-time collection; Incremental = single cut from a cross-stream sampler; Automatic = collected by automated sampler; Manual = collected by operator.']
  sampler_type varchar [note: 'Type of sampling device or method used to collect the sample: manual (operator-collected), automatic (automated cross-stream sampler), cross_belt, vezin (rotary cutter), or pipe_cross_cut. Affects representativeness assessment per TOS.']
  sampling_point_code varchar [note: 'Standardised code identifying the defined sampling point within the processing plant where the sample was collected (e.g., SAG_FEED, FLOAT_CONC, FLOAT_TAIL, CIL_FEED, LEACH_PLS). Corresponds to OSIsoft PI tag location.']
  sampling_point_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the sampling point location within the processing circuit (e.g., \'SAG Mill Discharge Screen Oversize\', \'Rougher Flotation Concentrate Launder\').']
  stream_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the process stream from which the sample was collected, indicating its position in the beneficiation flowsheet. Feed = ROM or circuit input; Concentrate = upgraded product stream; Tailings = waste stream; Intermediate = mid-circuit stream; Product = final saleable product; Recycle = recirculating load.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this process sample record was last modified, supporting data lineage, audit trail, and change tracking requirements.']

  Note: 'Record of process samples collected at defined sampling points within the processing plant for metallurgical accounting, quality control, and grade reconciliation. Captures sample ID, sampling point (feed, concentrate, tailings, intermediate stream), collection timestamp, sample type (composite, grab, incremental), mass (g), preparation method, and dispatch status to laboratory. Links to laboratory domain for assay results via sample ID. Supports JORC-compliant metallurgical accounting and process control.'
}

Table processing.plant_utility_consumption {
  plant_utility_consumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for plant_utility_consumption']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee (typically the plant metallurgist or environmental officer) who approved this utility consumption record for OPEX posting and regulatory reporting.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill, flotation, leaching, SX-EW) for which utility consumption is recorded. Enables circuit-level OPEX benchmarking.']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version against which utility consumption targets and budgets are benchmarked. Supports variance analysis against LOM plan targets.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mineral processing plant where utility consumption was recorded. Links to the plant master record.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Utility consumption must be tracked against production schedules for cost allocation, energy intensity benchmarking, and carbon accounting. Essential for AISC calculation and Scope 1/2 emissions repor']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Utility consumption is recorded per shift run. One consumption record belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many consumption records. This links the utility consumption to the processing domain\'']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production shift during which this utility consumption was recorded. Links to the shift production run record for operational context.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the utility consumption record was approved by the authorised employee. Marks the record as locked for OPEX cost posting and environmental compliance reporting.']
  carbon_budget_target_co2e_t decimal(14,4) [note: 'Approved carbon budget allocation for this circuit and period in tonnes of CO₂ equivalent (tCO₂e), derived from the LOM plan carbon budget. Used to calculate emissions variance and compliance status.']
  compressed_air_consumption_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of compressed air consumed by the circuit during the period in standard cubic metres (Sm³). Used for flotation cell aeration, instrumentation, and pneumatic equipment. Contributes to energy OPEX.']
  consumption_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known business reference number for this utility consumption record, used for cross-referencing with SAP S/4HANA CO cost postings and OPEX reporting. Format: UC-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this utility consumption record was first created in the system, sourced from OSIsoft PI System historian ingestion or manual entry. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary cost fields in this record (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Ensures consistent currency context for OPEX reporting and financial consolidation.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin system or method by which utility consumption data was captured. PI System and SCADA sources are automated and high-confidence; manual_entry and estimated records require additional validation before use in regulatory reporting.']
  electricity_consumption_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total electrical energy consumed by the circuit during the period in kilowatt-hours (kWh), sourced from OSIsoft PI System metering data. Primary input for energy OPEX calculation and carbon footprint reporting.']
  electricity_cost_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost of electricity consumed during the period in the reporting currency, calculated as electricity_consumption_kwh multiplied by electricity_unit_cost plus applicable demand charges. Posted to SAP S/4HANA CO cost centre.']
  electricity_unit_cost decimal(14,4) [note: 'Contracted or metered unit cost of electricity per kilowatt-hour ($/kWh) applicable to this period. Confidential commercial rate used for OPEX cost allocation and energy cost benchmarking.']
  energy_target_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned electrical energy consumption target for this circuit and period in kilowatt-hours (kWh), derived from the LOM plan energy budget. Used to calculate energy consumption variance for OPEX performance reporting.']
  evaporation_loss_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Estimated volume of water lost to evaporation from open process vessels, ponds, and the TSF during the period in cubic metres (m³). Required for water balance closure and environmental reporting.']
  fresh_water_intake_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of fresh water drawn from licensed water sources (rivers, bores, municipal supply) into the processing plant during the period in cubic metres (m³). Reported against water licence limits for regulatory compliance.']
  meter_reading_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the physical or virtual meter reading(s) underpinning this consumption record, as recorded in OSIsoft PI System or the utility provider invoice. Supports audit trail and data lineage for regulatory submissions.']
  natural_gas_consumption_gj decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of natural gas consumed by the circuit during the period in gigajoules (GJ), used for drying, heating, or on-site power generation. Input for Scope 1 greenhouse gas emissions calculation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording operational context, anomalies, meter faults, estimation methodology, or adjustment reasons relevant to this utility consumption record. Supports audit review and regulatory submissions.']
  peak_demand_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum instantaneous electrical power demand recorded during the period in kilowatts (kW). Used for utility tariff management, demand charge optimisation, and grid capacity planning.']
  period_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world end date and time of the utility consumption measurement period. Used with period_start_timestamp to calculate duration and validate completeness of the consumption record.']
  period_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world start date and time of the utility consumption measurement period. For shift records this aligns with shift start; for monthly records this is the first instant of the accounting month.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the consumption accounting period. Shift-level records are sourced from OSIsoft PI System historian; monthly records align with SAP S/4HANA CO accounting periods for OPEX reporting.']
  power_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Average power factor (ratio of real power to apparent power, dimensionless 0–1) for the circuit during the period. Low power factor incurs utility penalty charges and indicates reactive power inefficiency.']
  process_water_consumption_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total process water consumed within the circuit during the period (fresh intake plus recycled, minus discharge) in cubic metres (m³). Net water balance input for environmental reporting and water licence compliance.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle status of the utility consumption record. Draft records are pending submission; approved records are locked for OPEX and environmental reporting.']
  recycled_water_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of process water recycled from the Tailings Storage Facility (TSF) and other internal sources back into the processing circuit during the period in cubic metres (m³). Supports water stewardship reporting and licence compliance.']
  scope1_emissions_co2e_t decimal(14,4) [note: 'Direct greenhouse gas emissions from on-site combustion of natural gas or other fuels during the period in tonnes of CO₂ equivalent (tCO₂e). Supports carbon budget compliance and NGER/GRI reporting.']
  scope2_emissions_co2e_t decimal(14,4) [note: 'Indirect greenhouse gas emissions from purchased electricity consumption during the period in tonnes of CO₂ equivalent (tCO₂e), calculated using applicable grid emission factors. Supports carbon budget compliance and GRI/NGER reporting.']
  specific_energy_kwh_per_t decimal(12,4) [note: 'Electrical energy consumed per dry metric tonne of ore processed (kWh/t), calculated as electricity_consumption_kwh divided by tonnes_processed. Key comminution efficiency KPI benchmarked against LOM plan and AISC targets.']
  specific_water_m3_per_t decimal(12,4) [note: 'Process water consumed per dry metric tonne of ore processed (m³/t). Key water efficiency metric benchmarked against LOM plan targets and water licence conditions.']
  tonnes_processed decimal(18,3) [note: 'Dry metric tonnes of ore processed through the circuit during the period. Used as the denominator for calculating specific consumption rates (e.g., kWh/t, m³/t) for benchmarking and LOM plan variance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this utility consumption record was last modified. Tracks corrections, adjustments, and re-submissions for data lineage and audit compliance.']
  water_cost_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost of fresh water intake during the period in the reporting currency, including licence fees and supply charges. Posted to SAP S/4HANA CO cost centre for OPEX tracking.']
  water_licence_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fresh water intake during the period remained within the regulatory water licence limit (True = compliant, False = licence limit exceeded). Triggers environmental incident reporting workflow in IsoMetrix when False.']
  water_licence_limit_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Regulatory water licence limit for fresh water intake applicable to this circuit and period in cubic metres (m³). Used to calculate compliance headroom and trigger licence breach alerts.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of utility resource consumption per processing circuit per shift or accounting period. Covers electrical energy (kWh, kW peak demand, power factor, specific energy kWh/t), water balance (fresh water intake m³, recycled water m³, process water consumption m³/t, evaporation losses), compressed air, and other plant utilities. Captures circuit ID, shift date, consumption volumes, unit costs, and compliance metrics against water licence limits and carbon budgets. Supports OPEX tracking, environmental reporting (water stewardship, carbon footprint), energy efficiency benchmarking, and regulatory compliance against LOM plan targets.'
}

Table processing.dms_event {
  dms_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each DMS circuit operational event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Dense Media Separation circuit within the plant. A plant may operate multiple DMS circuits (primary, secondary, fines) each tracked independently.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Dense medium separation processes specific commodities (coal, diamonds, iron ore). Process-commodity link essential for DMS performance tracking and yield optimization. Replaces denormalized commodity']
  feed_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: DMS events process specific feed streams. One event processes one feed stream; one feed stream has many events. This links the DMS event to the feed stream being processed.']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Dense media separation uses heavy media (ferrosilicon slurry) and mechanical equipment (cyclones, magnetic separators). Incidents during DMS operations (media spills, equipment failures, exposure to m']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Dense media separation product and reject samples sent to labs for sink-float analysis and grade verification. Essential for validating separation efficiency, optimizing medium density, and ore charac']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version against which this DMS event\'s performance metrics are benchmarked. Enables variance analysis between actual DMS circuit performance and LOM plan targets for recovery rate, throughput, and product grade.']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Dense media separation equipment failures (cyclone wear, medium circuit pumps, magnetic separators) during DMS events generate maintenance work orders. Links separation efficiency problems to maintena']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant where this DMS circuit event occurred. Links to the plant master record for site-level reporting and LOM plan alignment.']
  primary_dms_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the plant operator responsible for operating the DMS circuit during this event. Used for accountability, training compliance, and incident investigation.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: DMS processing events must align with mine production schedules for ore type scheduling, blend optimization, and dense medium separation performance tracking against planned feed characteristics. Crit']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: DMS events occur during a production shift run. One DMS event belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many DMS events. This links the event to the processing domain\'s shift entity.']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this DMS event was recorded. Links to shift roster and production run records for shift-level performance reporting.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Dense media separation rejects (float_tonnes_dry) sent to specific TSF. Operational necessity for material tracking, TSF capacity planning, and mass balance. Tracks DMS tailings stream destination.']
  circuit_availability_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage of the scheduled operating time during which the DMS circuit was available and operational during this event period. Calculated as (operating hours / scheduled hours) × 100. Key input to Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) reporting.']
  circuit_recovery_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Metallurgical recovery rate of the DMS circuit for this event, expressed as the percentage of valuable mineral in the feed that is recovered in the product stream. Core KPI for DMS circuit performance against LOM plan targets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DMS event record was first created in the system, providing audit trail for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  cyclone_pressure_kpa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Operating pressure of the DMS cyclone(s) measured in kilopascals (kPa) during this event. Cyclone pressure directly affects separation efficiency and is a key SCADA-monitored process control parameter.']
  downtime_duration_min varchar [note: 'Total duration of unplanned and planned downtime experienced by the DMS circuit during this event, measured in minutes. Used for availability calculations, maintenance planning, and OPEX impact assessment.']
  downtime_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the primary cause of DMS circuit downtime during this event (e.g., cyclone blockage, pump failure, medium drain, planned maintenance, feed interruption). Used for root cause analysis and maintenance planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cyclone_blockage|pump_failure|medium_drain|planned_maintenance|feed_interruption|screen_blinding|magnetic_drum_fault|electrical_fault — promote to reference product]']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the DMS circuit operational event concluded. Used with start timestamp to calculate shift duration and throughput metrics.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing externally-known reference number for the DMS shift event, used for cross-system traceability and operational reporting. Format: DMS-YYYY-MM-DD-NNNN.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the DMS circuit operational event commenced, representing the principal real-world business event time. Sourced from OSIsoft PI System SCADA integration.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the DMS event record, tracking progression from data capture through metallurgist review and sign-off to final closure.']
  feed_moisture_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Moisture content of the ore feed expressed as a percentage of wet weight. Critical for converting wet tonnes to dry metric tonnes (DMT) for metallurgical accounting and product quality reporting.']
  feed_particle_size_p80_mm decimal(8,2) [note: '80th percentile particle size of the DMS feed in millimetres, representing the size at which 80% of feed particles pass. Influences separation efficiency and medium stability in the DMS circuit.']
  feed_rate_target_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned feed rate target for the DMS circuit in tonnes per hour (tph) for this event period, sourced from the production schedule or LOM plan. Enables throughput variance analysis against actual feed_rate_tph.']
  feed_rate_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mass flow rate of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed into the DMS circuit during this event, measured in tonnes per hour (tph). Key throughput metric benchmarked against design capacity and LOM plan targets.']
  feed_tonnes_wet decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total wet mass of ore fed into the DMS circuit during the event period, measured in wet metric tonnes. Used for mass balance calculations and shift production reconciliation.']
  float_tonnes_dry decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total dry metric tonnes (DMT) of material reporting to the float stream during this DMS event. Used for mass balance closure, metallurgical accounting, and clean product inventory tracking.']
  float_yield_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage of the DMS feed that reports to the float (light) product stream, expressed as a mass percentage. For coal DMS, the float is the clean coal product; for iron ore, the float is typically the gangue reject.']
  magnetic_recovery_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage of dense medium (ferrosilicon or magnetite) recovered from the product and reject streams via magnetic drum separators during this event. High magnetic recovery reduces medium losses and OPEX. Benchmarked against circuit design targets.']
  medium_density_sg decimal(6,3) [note: 'Specific gravity (SG) of the dense medium (typically ferrosilicon or magnetite suspension) used in the DMS circuit during this event. The primary separation control parameter — particles denser than the medium sink, lighter particles float.']
  medium_loss_kg_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Mass of dense medium (ferrosilicon or magnetite) lost per tonne of feed processed during this DMS event, measured in kg/t. Medium losses are a significant operating cost driver and are tracked for reagent consumption reporting and OPEX management.']
  medium_to_ore_ratio decimal(6,2) [note: 'Ratio of dense medium volume to ore feed volume in the DMS circuit during this event. A key process control parameter affecting separation efficiency and medium stability. Typically expressed as m³ medium per tonne of ore.']
  medium_type varchar [note: 'Type of dense medium used in the DMS circuit. Ferrosilicon is used for higher-density separations (diamonds, iron ore), while magnetite is common for coal DMS circuits. Affects reagent cost and medium recovery efficiency.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Geological classification of the ore feed processed during this DMS event (e.g., oxide, sulphide, transitional, ROM blend). Influences medium density settings and expected sink/float yields. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oxide|sulphide|transitional|rom_blend|high_ash|low_ash|coking|thermal — promote to reference product]']
  product_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Grade of the valuable mineral in the DMS product stream, expressed as a percentage. For coal, this is ash content of clean coal; for iron ore, this is Fe%; for diamonds, this is carats per hundred tonnes (cpht). Benchmarked against LOM plan targets.']
  production_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this DMS event occurred, used for daily production reporting, metallurgical balance reconciliation, and LOM plan variance tracking.']
  recovery_target_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Planned metallurgical recovery rate target for the DMS circuit during this event period, sourced from the LOM plan or shift production plan. Enables real-time variance tracking against actual circuit_recovery_pct.']
  reject_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Grade of the valuable mineral in the DMS reject (discard) stream, expressed as a percentage. Used to assess mineral losses to tailings and validate separation efficiency. High reject grade indicates potential recovery improvement opportunity.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift period during which the DMS event occurred (day, afternoon, or night). Used for shift-comparative performance analysis and crew scheduling.']
  sink_tonnes_dry decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total dry metric tonnes (DMT) of material reporting to the sink stream during this DMS event. Used for mass balance closure, metallurgical accounting, and product inventory reconciliation.']
  sink_yield_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage of the DMS feed that reports to the sink (dense) product stream, expressed as a mass percentage. For coal DMS, the sink is typically the reject (high-ash discard); for iron ore and diamonds, the sink is the valuable product.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system from which this DMS event record was sourced. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and data quality assessment in the silver layer lakehouse. Typically OSIsoft PI System via SCADA integration or manual entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this DMS event record, supporting audit trail and data quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of Dense Media Separation (DMS) circuit operations per shift. Captures feed rate (tph), medium density (SG), sink yield (%), float yield (%), product grade, reject grade, medium losses (kg/t), and cyclone pressure (kPa). Applicable to coal, iron ore, and diamond processing operations using DMS technology.'
}

Table processing.process_deviation {
  process_deviation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each process parameter deviation record within the processing plant. Primary key for the process_deviation data product.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill circuit, flotation circuit, leach circuit, SX-EW circuit) where the parameter deviation was recorded.']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Process deviations outside control limits often require maintenance intervention to restore equipment performance (sensor calibration, actuator repair, control valve maintenance). Links process contro']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the processing plant where the deviation occurred. Links to the processing_plant master record.']
  primary_process_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the plant operator responsible for the circuit at the time of the deviation, used for accountability tracking and training needs analysis.']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Process deviations occur during a production shift run. One deviation belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many deviations. This links the deviation to the processing domain\'s shift entity.']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which the deviation was detected, enabling shift-level performance analysis and operator accountability.']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The measured actual value of the process parameter at the time of deviation detection, as recorded by the SCADA/PI System historian or by manual operator measurement. Expressed in the unit defined by parameter_unit.']
  alarm_tag varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI System or SCADA tag identifier associated with the alarmed process parameter, enabling direct traceability back to the source historian record and real-time monitoring point.']
  circuit_stage varchar [note: 'The specific stage within the processing circuit where the deviation occurred, such as comminution (SAG/HPGR), flotation, leaching, SX-EW, or tailings management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: comminution|classification|flotation|leaching|sxew|thickening|filtration|tailings|other — promote to reference product]']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the corrective action taken by the operator or metallurgist to return the process parameter to within acceptable set-point tolerance, as recorded in the process control log.']
  corrective_action_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the corrective action taken, enabling aggregated analysis of response patterns and effectiveness across deviation types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reagent_adjustment|flow_adjustment|equipment_intervention|setpoint_change|process_shutdown|no_action_required|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this deviation record was first created in the system, which may differ from the detected_timestamp if the record was entered retrospectively.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the process parameter deviation was first detected, either by SCADA/PI System automated alarm or by operator observation. This is the principal real-world event time for the deviation.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the process parameter deviation was first identified, distinguishing between automated SCADA/PI System alarms and manual operator or laboratory detection.']
  deviation_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the actual parameter value exceeded the set-point (above_setpoint) or fell below it (below_setpoint), supporting directional root cause analysis and process optimisation.']
  deviation_magnitude decimal(18,4) [note: 'The absolute difference between the actual value and the set-point value (actual_value minus setpoint_value), expressed in the same engineering unit as the parameter. Positive values indicate exceedance above set-point; negative values indicate below set-point.']
  deviation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business reference number for the deviation event, used in corrective action reports, process control logs, and root cause analysis documentation. Format: DEV-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  deviation_pct decimal(10,4) [note: 'The deviation magnitude expressed as a percentage of the set-point value, enabling normalised comparison of deviation severity across parameters with different engineering units and scales.']
  deviation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the deviation record, tracking progression from initial detection through corrective action to closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|acknowledged|in_progress|resolved|closed|escalated — promote to reference product]']
  duration_mins varchar [note: 'Total duration in minutes for which the process parameter remained outside the acceptable control limits, from detected_timestamp to resolved_timestamp. Used for severity classification and SLA compliance assessment.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deviation was escalated beyond the shift operator level to the metallurgist, plant superintendent, or management due to severity, duration, or quality impact.']
  linked_work_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA PM or Infor EAM work order number raised as a result of this deviation, where equipment failure or instrumentation fault was identified as the root cause. Enables traceability between process deviations and maintenance activities.']
  lower_control_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum acceptable value for the process parameter before a deviation is triggered, as defined in the process control strategy. Expressed in the unit defined by parameter_unit.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the process parameter that deviated from its set-point target, such as pH, slurry density, pulp temperature, reagent flow rate, air flow rate, or feed rate. Aligns with OSIsoft PI System tag naming conventions.']
  parameter_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the process parameter type that deviated, enabling aggregated analysis by parameter category across circuits and plants. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pH|density|temperature|flow_rate|pressure|level|speed|grade|reagent_dosage|other — promote to reference product]']
  parameter_unit varchar [note: 'Engineering unit of measure for the process parameter (e.g., pH units, g/cm³, °C, m³/h, kPa, rpm, g/t). Required for correct interpretation of set-point, actual, and deviation magnitude values.']
  pi_event_frame_reference varchar [note: 'The unique event frame identifier from the OSIsoft PI System historian that corresponds to this deviation record, enabling direct cross-reference to the source process data historian for detailed time-series analysis.']
  production_loss_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated tonnes of concentrate or processed ore lost due to the deviation, as assessed by the metallurgist. Used for OPEX impact quantification and LOM plan variance reporting.']
  quality_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the process parameter deviation had a measurable impact on product quality (concentrate grade, recovery rate, or product specification compliance). True if quality impact was confirmed.']
  recovery_impact_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Estimated percentage point impact on metallurgical recovery rate attributable to this deviation, as assessed by the metallurgist. Negative values indicate recovery loss. Null if no recovery impact was identified.']
  repeat_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation is a recurrence of a previously recorded deviation for the same parameter and circuit within a defined lookback period, flagging chronic process control issues for targeted investigation.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the process parameter was returned to within acceptable set-point tolerance and the deviation was formally resolved. Null if deviation is still open.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorisation of the identified root cause of the deviation, used for trend analysis, recurring deviation identification, and process improvement initiatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|feed_variability|reagent_issue|operator_error|instrumentation_fault|utility_disruption|process_upset|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text description of the identified root cause of the process deviation, as determined through investigation by the metallurgist or process engineer. Supports root cause analysis (RCA) and continuous improvement.']
  setpoint_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The target or nominal set-point value for the process parameter as defined in the process control strategy or standard operating procedure (SOP). Expressed in the unit defined by parameter_unit.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Categorical severity classification of the deviation based on magnitude, duration, and potential impact on product quality, recovery rate, or plant safety. Drives escalation and corrective action urgency.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this deviation record originated, supporting data lineage and Silver layer provenance tracking. Values include OSIsoft PI System, SCADA, manual entry, LabWare LIMS, or MES.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this deviation record, used for data lineage and change tracking in the Silver layer.']
