  upper_control_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum acceptable value for the process parameter before a deviation is triggered, as defined in the process control strategy. Expressed in the unit defined by parameter_unit.']

  Note: 'Record of process parameter deviations and exceedances from set-point targets within the processing plant. Captures deviation timestamp, circuit ID, parameter name (pH, density, temperature, flow), set-point value, actual value, deviation magnitude, duration (mins), corrective action taken, and responsible operator. Supports process control quality and root cause analysis.'
}

Table processing.beneficiation_test {
  beneficiation_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each beneficiation testwork record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.circuit. Business justification: Beneficiation tests are conducted to optimize specific circuits. One test targets one circuit; one circuit has many tests. This links the test to the specific circuit being optimized. The plant_id is ']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Beneficiation tests evaluate processing routes for specific commodities. Essential for flowsheet design, LOM planning updates, and feasibility studies. Replaces denormalized commodity text field. Requ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified metallurgist or competent person responsible for designing, supervising, and interpreting this beneficiation test. Required for JORC and NI 43-101 competent person sign-off.']
  exploration_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.sample. Business justification: Beneficiation tests are performed on exploration samples to determine metallurgical characteristics. Fundamental to resource classification, processing route selection, and metallurgical testwork prog']
  feasibility_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.feasibility_study. Business justification: Metallurgical test work establishes processing route, recovery rates, and operating costs for feasibility studies. Required for JORC/NI 43-101 competent person sign-off, study basis documentation, and']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Beneficiation testwork (flotation, gravity, magnetic separation) conducted at specific laboratories for ore characterization, flowsheet design, and recovery optimization. Tracks which lab performed te']
  lims_sample_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the ore sample as registered in LabWare LIMS. Links this testwork record to the physical sample submission and chain-of-custody documentation.']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan that this testwork informs or is associated with. Enables traceability between metallurgical testwork outcomes and LOM plan updates.']
  mining_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mining_block. Business justification: Beneficiation tests validate processing performance predictions for specific mining blocks, critical for mine planning, ore type classification, and metallurgical domain definition. Essential for LOM ']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Beneficiation tests are conducted on samples from specific orebodies to determine processing parameters and recovery rates. Critical for flowsheet design, recovery forecasting, and validating processi']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the processing plant or pilot plant facility where the beneficiation test was conducted. Links to the processing_plant master record.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Beneficiation tests inform processing route decisions for scheduled mine production periods, ore type blending strategies, and circuit configuration optimization. Critical for short-term production pl']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Beneficiation tests characterize ore from specific prospects. Essential for processing plant design, ore blending strategies, and metallurgical domain modeling. Real business need: ore characterizatio']
  shutdown_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.shutdown_plan. Business justification: Metallurgical test campaigns are often scheduled during planned maintenance shutdowns when processing equipment is available for extended test work. Links test programs to shutdown planning for ore ch']
  source_tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Beneficiation tests are conducted on ore samples from specific tenements to optimize processing parameters for that tenement\'s ore characteristics. Results inform LOM planning and processing plant des']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this beneficiation test record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  energy_consumption_kwh_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Electrical energy consumed per tonne of feed processed during the beneficiation test, expressed in kWh/t. Used for OPEX estimation, All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) modelling, and carbon footprint assessments.']
  feed_grade_pct decimal(10,4) [note: 'Head grade of the ore sample fed into the beneficiation test, expressed as a percentage of the target commodity (e.g., % Cu, % Fe). Establishes the baseline for recovery and upgrade ratio calculations.']
  feed_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the feed grade value (e.g., percentage for base metals, grams per tonne for precious metals, ppm for trace elements). Ensures correct interpretation of the feed_grade_pct field.']
  feed_particle_size_p80_um decimal(10,2) [note: '80th percentile particle size (P80) of the feed material in micrometres, representing the grind size at which 80% of particles pass. Critical comminution parameter that directly influences liberation and recovery in downstream separation processes.']
  grind_target_p80_um decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target 80th percentile grind size (P80) in micrometres specified for the test. Compared against the achieved feed_particle_size_p80_um to assess whether the test was conducted at the intended grind size.']
  is_lom_plan_update_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the results of this beneficiation test necessitate an update to the LOM plan recovery assumptions or processing circuit design. Triggers the LOM plan review workflow when set to True.']
  lom_plan_recovery_target_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The recovery rate target from the current LOM plan against which this test result is benchmarked. Enables direct comparison of achieved test recovery versus planned recovery assumptions for resource estimation and financial modelling.']
  mass_pull_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the total feed mass that reports to the concentrate or product stream. Indicates the selectivity of the separation process; a lower mass pull with high recovery indicates better separation efficiency.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Geological classification of the ore sample used in the test (e.g., oxide, sulphide, mixed, transitional, fresh). Determines the appropriate processing route and expected metallurgical response.']
  product_grade_pct decimal(10,4) [note: 'Grade of the concentrate or product stream achieved in the beneficiation test, expressed as a percentage of the target commodity. Key output metric for assessing test performance against design targets.']
  pulp_density_pct_solids decimal(7,3) [note: 'Percentage solids by weight of the slurry (pulp) during the beneficiation test. A key operating parameter for flotation, leaching, and gravity separation that affects reagent dosage, residence time, and recovery.']
  pulp_ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'pH of the slurry during the beneficiation test. Critical operating parameter for flotation and leaching circuits; affects reagent selectivity, mineral surface chemistry, and recovery performance.']
  reagent_suite varchar [note: 'Description of the reagent combination tested, including collector, frother, depressant, activator, and pH modifier types and dosages (e.g., \'PAX 80g/t, MIBC 20g/t, CuSO4 200g/t\'). Captures the full reagent regime for circuit design and operating condition recommendations.']
  recommended_operating_conditions varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the optimal operating conditions identified from this beneficiation test, including recommended grind size, reagent dosages, pH, density, and temperature. Directly informs circuit design parameters and standard operating procedures (SOPs).']
  recovery_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the target commodity recovered from the feed into the product stream during the beneficiation test. Primary performance indicator for metallurgical testwork; informs LOM plan recovery assumptions.']
  report_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or URL for the formal metallurgical testwork report associated with this test. Provides traceability to the full technical report for audit, regulatory disclosure, and competent person review.']
  sample_mass_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total mass of the ore sample (in kilograms) used as feed for the beneficiation test. Determines the representativeness of the test and the reliability of mass balance calculations.']
  sample_source_description varchar [note: 'Descriptive reference to the origin of the ore sample used in the test, including mine site, pit, bench, drill hole, or stockpile location. Provides traceability for the sample provenance as required by JORC and NI 43-101.']
  tailings_grade_pct decimal(10,4) [note: 'Grade of the target commodity remaining in the tailings stream after beneficiation, expressed as a percentage. Used to assess metal losses and validate mass balance closure.']
  temperature_c decimal(7,2) [note: 'Operating temperature in degrees Celsius during the beneficiation test. Particularly relevant for leaching (heap leach, pressure oxidation), SX-EW, and bio-leach tests where temperature significantly affects reaction kinetics and recovery.']
  test_date date [note: 'The date on which the beneficiation test was conducted. Principal real-world event date for this testwork record.']
  test_duration_min varchar [note: 'Total duration of the beneficiation test in minutes, from feed introduction to final product collection. Used to calculate throughput rates and assess residence time requirements for circuit design.']
  test_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned by the laboratory or pilot plant (e.g., LabWare LIMS test ID) to uniquely identify this testwork trial. Used for cross-referencing with laboratory reports and LIMS records.']
  test_objective varchar [note: 'Statement of the specific metallurgical objective for this test (e.g., \'Determine optimal collector dosage for copper flotation\', \'Assess leach kinetics for oxide ore at 60°C\'). Provides context for interpreting results and linking to LOM plan requirements.']
  test_result_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the key findings and outcomes of the beneficiation test, including notable observations, anomalies, and conclusions. Supports metallurgical reporting and competent person review.']
  test_scale varchar [note: 'Scale at which the beneficiation test was conducted, distinguishing between bench-scale laboratory tests, locked-cycle tests, batch tests, and pilot plant trials. Determines the confidence level and applicability of results to full-scale circuit design.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the beneficiation test. Tracks progression from planning through execution to completion or cancellation.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the beneficiation or metallurgical testwork method applied. Includes flotation, leach, gravity separation, Dense Media Separation (DMS), magnetic separation, Solvent Extraction Electrowinning (SX-EW), High Pressure Grinding Rolls (HPGR), and comminution tests. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flotation|leach|gravity|DMS|magnetic_separation|SX-EW|HPGR|comminution|bio_leach|roasting — promote to reference product]']
  total_reagent_consumption_kg_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total mass of all reagents consumed per tonne of feed processed during the beneficiation test, expressed in kg/t. Used for operating cost estimation and OPEX modelling in LOM plan updates.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this beneficiation test record was last modified in the data platform. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and incremental data load processes.']
  upgrade_ratio decimal(8,3) [note: 'Ratio of product grade to feed grade, indicating the degree of concentration achieved in the beneficiation test. A higher upgrade ratio indicates more effective separation of the valuable mineral from gangue.']
  water_consumption_m3_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Volume of water consumed per tonne of feed processed during the beneficiation test, expressed in m³/t. Informs water balance modelling and environmental impact assessments for circuit design.']

  Note: 'Record of metallurgical testwork and beneficiation trials conducted on ore samples to determine optimal processing parameters. Captures test ID, ore sample reference, test type (flotation, leach, gravity, DMS), test date, laboratory or pilot plant, feed grade, product grade, recovery achieved (%), reagent suite tested, and recommended operating conditions. Informs circuit design and LOM plan updates.'
}

Table processing.circuit_analytical_protocol {
  circuit_analytical_protocol_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this circuit-method protocol assignment record']
  analytical_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the approved analytical method used for this circuit']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the processing circuit for which this analytical protocol is defined']
  approval_justification varchar [note: 'Business or technical rationale for selecting this analytical method for this circuit (e.g., detection limit requirements, matrix compatibility, accreditation needs)']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the technical authority (metallurgist, process engineer, or laboratory manager) who approved this method for this circuit']
  cost_allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of this circuit\'s analytical budget allocated to this specific method, used for cost tracking and budget forecasting']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this circuit-method protocol became active and operational']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this circuit-method protocol was superseded or retired. Null for currently active protocols.']
  method_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this analytical method was formally approved for use on this processing circuit by the technical authority']
  method_priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of this method for this circuit (primary for routine use, secondary for confirmation, backup for contingency, research for trials)']
  protocol_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this circuit-method protocol assignment']
  qaqc_insertion_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required frequency of quality control sample insertion for this circuit-method combination, expressed as percentage (e.g., 5.00 means one QC sample per 20 production samples)']
  sample_frequency_per_shift varchar [note: 'Required number of samples to be collected and analyzed using this method per operating shift for this circuit']
  turnaround_requirement_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable turnaround time from sample submission to result reporting for this circuit-method combination, driven by process control requirements']

  Note: 'This association product represents the approved analytical method assignment between processing circuits and laboratory analytical methods. It captures the operational protocol governing which analytical methods are approved for use on each circuit\'s samples, including method priority, turnaround requirements, cost allocation, and QAQC insertion rates. Each record links one processing circuit to one analytical method with attributes that exist only in the context of this circuit-method relationship.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, processing circuits require multiple analytical methods to characterize feed, intermediate streams, and final products - a flotation circuit may use XRF for rapid grade control, ICP-MS for trace element monitoring, and fire assay for precious metal accountability. Conversely, each analytical method (e.g., XRF pressed pellet) is approved for use across multiple circuits with circuit-specific protocols governing turnaround time, sampling frequency, QAQC insertion rates, and cost allocation. The business actively manages these circuit-method protocols as operational entities.'
}

Table processing.circuit_asset_assignment {
  circuit_asset_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each circuit-asset assignment record']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the physical asset assigned to the circuit']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the processing circuit to which the asset is assigned']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the assignment indicating whether the asset is actively operating in the circuit, temporarily inactive, under maintenance, or being reconfigured.']
  circuit_position varchar [note: 'Specific position or location code of the asset within the circuit flowsheet (e.g., PRIMARY_MILL_1, ROUGHER_CELL_3, CYCLONE_BANK_A). Used for circuit configuration management and maintenance work order routing.']
  duty_classification varchar [note: 'Operational duty classification of the asset within the circuit indicating whether it operates as primary equipment, secondary/parallel unit, or standby/backup capacity. Drives maintenance prioritization and availability calculations.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset was physically installed and commissioned into the processing circuit. Used for asset lifecycle tracking and performance period calculation.']
  operating_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative operating hours logged by the asset while assigned to this specific circuit. Used for circuit-specific maintenance scheduling and performance analysis. Distinct from the asset\'s total SMR hours across all assignments.']
  performance_contribution_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage contribution of this asset to the overall circuit throughput or recovery performance. Used for metallurgical accounting, bottleneck analysis, and asset performance attribution in multi-unit circuits.']
  removal_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset was removed from the processing circuit due to maintenance, reconfiguration, or decommissioning. Null indicates the asset is currently assigned.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Assignment between processing_circuit and asset. It captures the operational assignment of physical equipment assets to processing circuits within mineral processing plants, including duty classification, installation lifecycle dates, and performance contribution tracking. Each record links one processing_circuit to one asset with attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment relationship.. Existence Justification: In mining processing operations, circuits contain multiple physical assets (mills, pumps, cyclones, flotation cells, conveyors) that work together to process ore through a specific metallurgical process. Individual assets can be reassigned between circuits during plant reconfigurations, capacity expansions, or when equipment is moved from one circuit to another for operational optimization. The business actively manages these assignments with specific installation/removal dates, duty classifications (primary/standby), circuit positions, and tracks performance contribution for metallurgical accounting.'
}

Table processing.circuit_tailings_routing {
  circuit_tailings_routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this circuit-to-TSF routing configuration record']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the processing circuit that generates the tailings stream']
  pipeline_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical tailings pipeline infrastructure used for this circuit-to-TSF routing. Links routing configuration to physical asset register.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the Tailings Storage Facility receiving the tailings stream']
  design_slurry_density_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target slurry density for this specific circuit-to-TSF routing expressed as percentage solids by weight. May differ from circuit design density based on TSF deposition requirements and water recovery strategies.']
  design_solids_rate_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Design basis dry solids flow rate from this circuit to this TSF expressed in tonnes per hour (tph). Used for TSF capacity planning and deposition rate modeling.']
  design_water_recovery_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Target percentage of process water to be recovered from this tailings stream at the receiving TSF. Varies by TSF design (thickened tailings, paste, conventional) and water balance strategy.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this circuit-to-TSF routing configuration became or will become active. Supports time-based routing changes as TSFs reach capacity or new facilities are commissioned.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this circuit-to-TSF routing configuration ceased or will cease to be active. Null for currently active routings. Enables historical tracking of routing changes over mine life.']
  geochemical_routing_rule varchar [note: 'Business rule describing the geochemical criteria governing this routing decision (e.g., \'ARD-generating tailings from copper rougher circuit routed to lined TSF-3\', \'Benign tailings from final cleaner routed to unlined TSF-1\'). Critical for environmental compliance and acid rock drainage management.']
  routing_priority varchar [note: 'Integer priority ranking for this routing configuration when multiple TSF destinations are available for a circuit (1=primary, 2=secondary, 3=tertiary). Drives automated routing logic and operational decision-making during TSF transitions.']
  routing_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this routing configuration. ACTIVE routings are currently in use, PLANNED are future configurations, INACTIVE are temporarily suspended, DECOMMISSIONED are permanently retired.']
  tailings_destination_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Tailings Storage Facility (TSF) or waste stream destination for tailings generated by this circuit. Critical for environmental compliance, TSF capacity planning, and regulatory reporting. [Moved from processing_circuit: This attribute in processing_circuit currently stores a single TSF destination code, implying a 1:N relationship. In the true M:N business reality where circuits can route to multiple TSFs based on operational conditions, this attribute should be removed and replaced by the circuit_tailings_routing association which captures the full many-to-many routing configuration with time-based and priority-based routing rules.]']
  tailings_stream_designation varchar [note: 'Classification of the tailings stream type for this routing (e.g., PRIMARY for main tailings flow, ARD for acid-generating tailings requiring lined storage, BENIGN for non-reactive tailings, EMERGENCY for overflow routing). Drives environmental compliance and TSF selection logic.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational routing configuration between processing circuits and Tailings Storage Facilities (TSFs). It captures the business rules and parameters governing where each circuit\'s tailings stream is directed, including geochemical routing decisions (e.g., acid-generating vs benign tailings), capacity-based routing, and time-based routing changes. Each record links one processing circuit to one TSF with routing-specific attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship. Critical for tailings management compliance, TSF capacity planning, and environmental risk management.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, processing circuits can route tailings to multiple TSFs based on geochemical properties (acid-generating vs benign), operational capacity constraints, and time-based transitions as facilities reach capacity or new TSFs are commissioned. Conversely, a single TSF receives tailings from multiple processing circuits (rougher, scavenger, cleaner circuits may all route to the same facility). The business actively manages these routing configurations with specific rules, priorities, and effective dates.'
}

Table processing.circuit_product_configuration {
  circuit_product_configuration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each circuit-product configuration record. Primary key for the association.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the processing circuit that is configured to produce the saleable product']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the saleable product that can be produced by the processing circuit']
  circuit_configuration_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific operating configuration or mode used when producing this product from this circuit (e.g., CLEANER_ONLY, SCAV_RECYCLE, HIGH_GRADE_CAMPAIGN, BULK_PRODUCTION). Captures the operational setup that differentiates how different products are produced.']
  concentrate_product_code varchar [note: 'Product code of the primary output concentrate or processed product from this circuit (e.g., CU-CON-01 for copper concentrate). Links to sales contracts, logistics, and product quality specifications. [Moved from processing_circuit: This attribute in processing_circuit currently captures a single primary output product code. In a flexible processing operation where circuits can produce multiple products, this attribute should be removed from the circuit entity and the relationship should be captured through the circuit_product_configuration association. Each circuit-product configuration record will link to the saleable_product via FK, eliminating the need for a single product code on the circuit.]']
  configuration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this circuit-product configuration. ACTIVE: currently in production use; PLANNED: approved for future implementation; SUSPENDED: temporarily inactive; DECOMMISSIONED: permanently retired; TESTING: under commissioning or trial.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this circuit-product configuration record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which this circuit-product configuration ceased or will cease to be active. Null indicates currently active configuration. Enables tracking of configuration lifecycle.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any field in this circuit-product configuration record.']
  production_priority varchar [note: 'Integer ranking indicating the priority of this product within the circuit\'s production portfolio. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for production scheduling and campaign planning when a circuit can produce multiple products.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date from which this circuit-product configuration became or becomes active and operational. Enables tracking of configuration history and future planned configurations.']
  target_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Target product grade for this specific circuit-product combination expressed as a percentage of valuable mineral content. This is configuration-specific as different products from the same circuit may have different grade targets (e.g., cleaner concentrate vs. rougher concentrate).']
  target_recovery_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Target metallurgical recovery rate for this specific circuit-product combination expressed as a percentage. This is configuration-specific and may differ from the circuit\'s general design recovery rate depending on the product being produced and operating mode.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational configuration between a processing circuit and a saleable product it can produce. It captures the target metallurgical performance parameters, production priority, and configuration settings specific to each circuit-product combination. Each record links one processing circuit to one saleable product with attributes that exist only in the context of this production relationship. This enables flexible processing operations where circuits can be configured to produce different products through different operating modes (cleaner/scavenger splits, grade campaigns) and where products can be sourced from multiple circuits (primary/secondary lines, parallel processing trains).. Existence Justification: In flexible mineral processing operations, a single processing circuit can be configured to produce multiple saleable products through different operating modes (cleaner vs. scavenger splits, grade campaigns, bulk vs. premium production). Conversely, a single saleable product can be produced by multiple circuits (primary and secondary processing lines, parallel circuit trains, backup circuits). The business actively manages these circuit-product configurations with specific metallurgical targets, production priorities, and operating parameters for each combination.'
}

Table processing.tailings_routing {
  tailings_routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this specific plant-to-TSF routing configuration. Primary key for the tailings routing association.']
  pipeline_id varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the physical tailings pipeline that connects this processing plant to this TSF. Multiple plants may share pipeline infrastructure or a single plant may have dedicated pipelines to different TSFs. This attribute belongs to the routing relationship because it represents the physical connection infrastructure specific to this plant-TSF pair.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the source processing plant that generates the tailings stream']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the destination Tailings Storage Facility that receives the tailings stream']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when this specific plant-to-TSF routing configuration was commissioned and became operational. This is distinct from the plant commissioning date or TSF commissioning date because routing connections are established independently as infrastructure is built or modified. Critical for tracking operational history and regulatory compliance.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date when this specific plant-to-TSF routing configuration was decommissioned and ceased operation. Null if the routing is still active. Routing may be decommissioned when TSFs reach capacity, pipelines are retired, or plant operations are reconfigured, even if both the plant and TSF remain operational with other routing configurations.']
  design_flow_rate_m3ph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Design volumetric flow rate of tailings slurry through this routing configuration, expressed in cubic meters per hour (m³/h). This is specific to the routing because it depends on pipeline diameter, pump capacity, elevation change, and distance between the specific plant and TSF. Critical for hydraulic design and operational planning.']
  design_slurry_density_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Design solids content of the tailings slurry for this specific plant-to-TSF routing, expressed as percentage by weight (0-100). This varies by routing because different TSFs may require different slurry densities based on dam design, deposition method, and storage capacity constraints. Critical for pipeline design, pumping requirements, and TSF water balance.']
  elevation_gain_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Net elevation gain (or loss if negative) from the processing plant discharge point to the TSF deposition point for this specific routing, measured in meters. This is a relationship attribute because it describes the topographic profile of this specific plant-TSF connection. Critical for pump sizing and energy consumption calculations.']
  is_primary_destination boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this TSF is the primary (normal operating) destination for tailings from this processing plant. True = primary destination; False = secondary/emergency destination. A plant typically has one primary TSF and may have one or more secondary TSFs for contingency operations, maintenance periods, or capacity management. This attribute belongs to the routing relationship because it describes the operational priority of this specific plant-TSF connection.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of this specific plant-to-TSF routing configuration. Active = currently in use; Standby = commissioned but not currently routing tailings (emergency backup); Decommissioned = permanently retired; Under-construction = pipeline and infrastructure being built. This status is independent of the plant and TSF operational statuses because routing configurations can be in standby while both endpoints are active.']
  pipeline_length_km decimal(8,3) [note: 'Total length of the tailings pipeline for this specific plant-to-TSF routing, measured in kilometers. This is a relationship attribute because it describes the physical connection infrastructure between this specific plant-TSF pair. Relevant for pumping energy calculations, maintenance planning, and risk assessment.']
  tailings_stream_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tailings stream type being routed from this plant to this TSF. Conventional tailings are slurry with 20-30% solids; thickened tailings are 45-55% solids; paste tailings are 65-75% solids; filtered tailings are dewatered to 80%+ solids; co-disposal includes waste rock. This attribute belongs to the routing relationship because the same plant may produce different stream types for different TSFs depending on processing method and TSF design requirements.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational routing configuration between processing plants and Tailings Storage Facilities. It captures the physical pipeline connections, slurry specifications, and operational parameters that govern how tailings from each processing plant are delivered to each TSF. Each record links one processing plant to one TSF with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific routing relationship, including commissioning dates, slurry density specifications, stream type classification, and primary destination designation.. Existence Justification: In large mining operations, processing plants routinely route tailings to multiple TSFs based on operational requirements, capacity constraints, and contingency planning. For example, Olympic Dam operates multiple processing circuits (oxide, sulphide) that can route to different TSF cells or compartments. Conversely, a single TSF may receive tailings from multiple processing plants on the same mine site. The business actively manages these routing configurations, tracking pipeline infrastructure, slurry specifications, commissioning dates, and primary vs. emergency destination designations for each plant-TSF connection.'
}

Table processing.plant_product_campaign {
  plant_product_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the plant_product_campaign association']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the processing plant facility executing this production campaign']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the saleable product variant being produced in this campaign']
  actual_production_tonnes decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual production volume achieved during this campaign expressed in dry metric tonnes. Updated as production progresses. Used for campaign performance analysis and variance reporting.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for this production campaign used in operational reporting and plant scheduling systems']
  campaign_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this production campaign concluded or is planned to conclude. Null for ongoing campaigns. Used for campaign duration analysis and scheduling.']
  campaign_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes capturing campaign-specific conditions, challenges, process adjustments, or quality issues encountered during production.']
  campaign_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this production campaign commenced. Represents when the plant began processing ore to produce this specific saleable product variant.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the production campaign. Planned: scheduled but not started; Active: currently producing; Suspended: temporarily halted; Completed: finished successfully; Cancelled: terminated before completion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production campaign record was first created in the system']
  feed_ore_source varchar [note: 'Identification of the primary ore source (pit, stockpile, or blend) feeding this production campaign. Critical for traceability and understanding quality variations.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any field in this production campaign record']
  quality_grade_achieved_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average grade of the valuable mineral component achieved in the saleable product during this campaign, expressed as percentage. Campaign-specific quality outcome that may vary from target grade specification based on feed ore characteristics and plant performance.']
  recovery_rate_achieved_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Actual metallurgical recovery rate achieved during this campaign expressed as percentage. Campaign-specific performance metric comparing valuable mineral in product to valuable mineral in feed.']
  target_production_rate_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned throughput rate for this campaign expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (tph). Campaign-specific target that may differ from plant nameplate capacity based on ore characteristics and product specifications.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the production campaign relationship between processing_plant and saleable_product. It captures the operational reality that a single processing plant runs multiple production campaigns over time, each targeting a specific saleable product variant, and that a single saleable product can be produced by multiple plants across the mining operation. Each record represents one production campaign linking one processing plant to one saleable product with campaign-specific attributes including scheduling, target rates, actual production volumes, and quality outcomes achieved.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, processing plants run production campaigns where they switch between producing different saleable product variants based on ore feed characteristics, market demand, and operational optimization. A single plant produces multiple products over time (e.g., different concentrate grades, lump vs fines splits), and a single saleable product is produced by multiple plants across the mining operation. Production campaigns are actively managed operational entities with specific start/end dates, target rates, actual production volumes, and quality outcomes.'
}

Table processing.plant_community_contribution {
  plant_community_contribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this plant-program contribution record. Primary key.']
  investment_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the community investment program receiving the contribution from the processing plant']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the processing plant facility making the contribution to the community investment program']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this specific plant\'s contribution to this program was formally approved by the plant\'s management or the corporate CSR committee.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive, plant manager, or committee that approved this plant\'s contribution to the community investment program.']
  benefit_sharing_agreement_clause varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific clause or section in the benefit-sharing agreement that governs this plant\'s contribution obligations to this program.']
  contribution_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary contribution amount from this specific processing plant to this specific community investment program. Represents the plant\'s share of program funding.']
  contribution_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the contribution amount (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD, ZAR). May differ from the program\'s budget currency if plants operate in different jurisdictions.']
  contribution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this plant\'s contribution to the program: committed (pledged but not yet active), active (currently funding), suspended (temporarily paused), completed (funding period ended), or cancelled (commitment withdrawn).']
  contribution_type varchar [note: 'The nature of the contribution from the processing plant to the program: direct_funding (cash), in_kind (goods/services), technical_support, infrastructure, equipment, or personnel secondment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this plant-program contribution record was first created in the system.']
  funding_period_end date [note: 'The date when this processing plant\'s contribution commitment to this community investment program ends. May differ from the program\'s overall end date if plants have different commitment periods.']
  funding_period_start date [note: 'The date when this processing plant\'s contribution commitment to this community investment program begins. May differ from the program\'s overall start date if plants join at different times.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this plant-program contribution record was last updated or modified.']
  program_participation_level varchar [note: 'The level of involvement and responsibility this processing plant has in the community investment program: primary_sponsor (lead funder), co_sponsor (major partner), supporting (moderate contribution), or minor_contributor.']
  reporting_obligation varchar [note: 'Description of the specific reporting requirements and obligations this processing plant has for this community investment program, including frequency, format, and content of progress reports tied to this plant\'s contribution.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the financial and operational contribution relationship between processing plants and community investment programs. It captures the multi-site funding model where individual processing plants contribute to shared community programs, and programs receive funding from multiple plant facilities. Each record links one processing plant to one community investment program with contribution amounts, participation levels, funding periods, and reporting obligations that exist only in the context of this specific plant-program relationship.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, community investment programs are frequently funded by multiple processing plants as part of multi-site CSR strategies, particularly when programs serve regions with multiple facilities. Conversely, individual processing plants contribute to multiple community programs across different focus areas (education, health, infrastructure) to fulfill their social licence obligations. The business actively manages these contribution relationships, tracking contribution amounts, participation levels, funding periods, and reporting obligations specific to each plant-program pair.'
}

Table processing.stockpile_tenement_contribution {
  stockpile_tenement_contribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each stockpile-tenement contribution record']
  concentrate_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the concentrate stockpile that received material from the source tenement']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the source tenement that contributed material to the stockpile']
  average_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average assay grade (%) of the concentrate contributed from this tenement during the source production period. Used for blend quality reconciliation and tenement performance tracking.']
  contribution_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the stockpile\'s total inventory contributed by this tenement, calculated as (contribution_tonnage_dmt / stockpile.inventory_dmt) * 100. Used for blend composition reporting.']
  contribution_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this contribution record. active: material still in stockpile; depleted: material shipped out; reconciled: final tonnage confirmed; disputed: under investigation for grade or tonnage variance.']
  contribution_tonnage_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Dry metric tonnes of concentrate contributed from this tenement to this stockpile during the source production period. Used for royalty allocation and origin certification.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this contribution record, reflecting changes to tonnage, grade, or status during reconciliation processes.']
  recorded_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contribution record was created in the system, typically when material from the tenement was added to the stockpile.']
  royalty_allocation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Calculated royalty amount (in local currency) attributable to this tenement\'s contribution to the stockpile, based on contribution tonnage and the tenement\'s royalty rate. Used for financial accrual and payment processing.']
  source_production_period_end date [note: 'End date of the production period during which this tenement\'s material was added to the stockpile. Supports traceability and time-based royalty calculations.']
  source_production_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the production period during which this tenement\'s material was added to the stockpile. Supports traceability and time-based royalty calculations.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contribution relationship between concentrate stockpiles and source tenements in mining operations. It captures the blended origin of concentrate inventory, tracking which tenements contributed material to which stockpiles, with tonnage, grade, and time period attribution. Each record links one concentrate stockpile to one source tenement with contribution metrics required for royalty allocation, origin certification, and mine-to-market traceability.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, concentrate stockpiles are blended inventories containing material from multiple source tenements, and each tenement\'s production contributes to multiple stockpile batches over time as mining progresses. The business actively manages these contribution relationships to allocate royalties to the correct tenement holders, certify product origin for customers, and reconcile blend composition against production plans. This is an operational tracking requirement, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table processing.circuit_delivery {
  circuit_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this circuit delivery record, serving as the primary key for the association between a capital project and a processing circuit']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the capital project responsible for delivering this processing circuit']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the processing circuit being delivered by this capital project']
  actual_delivery_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual capital expenditure incurred to deliver this specific processing circuit as part of the broader capital project. Used for cost tracking and variance analysis at circuit level.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which this specific processing circuit was commissioned as part of this capital project. Marks the formal acceptance of the circuit into operational testing phase.']
  defects_liability_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of the defects liability period during which the project team or contractor remains responsible for rectifying defects in the delivered circuit. Typically 12-24 months from provisional acceptance.']
  delivery_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing key observations, issues, lessons learned, or special conditions related to the delivery of this circuit by this project']
  design_capacity_achieved_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the delivered processing circuit achieved its nameplate design capacity during performance testing. Used to track project delivery success and trigger milestone payments.']
  final_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the processing circuit received final acceptance after successful completion of the defects liability period, formally closing out the project delivery obligation']
  handover_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the circuit handover process from the project team to operations. Tracks progression through construction, commissioning, testing, acceptance, and defects liability phases.']
  performance_test_date date [note: 'Date on which performance acceptance testing was conducted for this circuit delivery to verify that design throughput, recovery rates, and other KPIs meet contractual specifications']
  provisional_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the processing circuit was provisionally accepted by operations, marking the start of the defects liability period and transfer of operational responsibility']

  Note: 'This association product represents the delivery event between a capital project and a processing circuit. It captures the commissioning, handover, and performance acceptance of a processing circuit as delivered by a capital project. Each record links one capital project to one processing circuit with attributes that exist only in the context of this project delivery relationship, including commissioning dates, performance test outcomes, handover status, and defects liability tracking.. Existence Justification: In mining capital projects, large brownfield expansion projects routinely deliver multiple processing circuits simultaneously (e.g., one project delivers primary crushing, secondary grinding, and flotation circuits). Conversely, individual processing circuits undergo upgrades, modifications, and capacity expansions through multiple capital projects over their operational life. The business actively manages circuit delivery as a formal handover process with commissioning milestones, performance testing, acceptance criteria, and defects liability tracking.'
}

Table processing.plant_contractor_engagement {
  plant_contractor_engagement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this specific engagement relationship between a processing plant and a contractor. Primary key.']
  contractor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the contractor engaged at the processing plant']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the processing plant where the contractor is engaged']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the plant employee (supervisor or superintendent) responsible for overseeing this contractor\'s work at this plant. Used for accountability and escalation.']
  daily_rate_aud decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily billing rate in Australian dollars for this contractor at this specific plant. May vary by plant due to remoteness, FIFO arrangements, or specialized equipment requirements.']
  engagement_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual end date of this contractor engagement at this processing plant. Null for ongoing engagements. Used for workforce planning and access expiry.']
  engagement_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this specific contractor engagement at this processing plant commenced. Used for tenure calculation and access control validation.']
  engagement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this plant-contractor engagement. Active = contractor currently working; Suspended = temporarily paused; Completed = engagement finished as planned; Terminated = engagement ended early.']
  permit_to_work_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the permit-to-work authorization for this contractor engagement at this plant. Links to HSE permit management system for high-risk work activities.']
  roster_pattern varchar [note: 'Work roster pattern for this contractor at this plant (e.g., \'2-weeks-on-1-week-off\', \'8-days-on-6-days-off\', \'day-shift-only\'). Plant-specific roster arrangements.']
  site_access_zones varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of plant zones and areas this contractor is authorized to access for this engagement (e.g., \'crushing-circuit,flotation-area,control-room,maintenance-workshop\'). Plant-specific access control.']
  work_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work scope and responsibilities for this contractor at this specific processing plant (e.g., \'SAG mill maintenance\', \'flotation circuit electrical work\', \'shutdown campaign - conveyor systems\').']

  Note: 'This association product represents the engagement contract between a processing plant and a contractor. It captures the specific work assignment, duration, access permissions, and work scope for each contractor engaged at each processing plant. Each record links one processing plant to one contractor with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific engagement relationship.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, contractors are engaged across multiple processing plants for specialized work (maintenance campaigns, shutdowns, commissioning projects), and each processing plant simultaneously engages multiple contractors across different trades (mechanical, electrical, civil, instrumentation). The engagement relationship is actively managed with plant-specific work scopes, access zones, permits, and billing rates that vary by plant and contractor combination.'
}

Table processing.measurement_point {
  measurement_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for measurement_point']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment or asset where the measurement instrument is installed (e.g., SAG mill, flotation cell, thickener).']
  process_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the process area or circuit section where this measurement point is located (e.g., comminution, flotation, dewatering).']
  process_stream_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the process stream or material flow being measured (e.g., ore feed, concentrate, tailings, reagent addition).']
  upstream_measurement_point_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on measurement_point (upstream_measurement_point_id)']
  accuracy decimal(8,4) [note: 'Specified accuracy or tolerance of the measurement instrument, typically expressed as a percentage of full scale or absolute value.']
  alarm_high decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper warning alarm threshold indicating the measurement is approaching critical limits.']
  alarm_high_high decimal(18,4) [note: 'Critical upper alarm threshold that triggers immediate operator intervention when exceeded.']
  alarm_low decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower warning alarm threshold indicating the measurement is approaching critical limits.']
  alarm_low_low decimal(18,4) [note: 'Critical lower alarm threshold that triggers immediate operator intervention when breached.']
  calibration_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Required interval in days between instrument calibration activities to maintain measurement accuracy and compliance.']
  control_system_tag varchar [note: 'Tag identifier used in the distributed control system or SCADA system to reference this measurement point for real-time monitoring and control.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this measurement point record was first created in the master data system.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the current data quality status from the control system, reflecting instrument health and signal validity.']
  historian_tag varchar [note: 'Tag identifier used in the process historian system (e.g., OSIsoft PI, Aspen IP.21) for time-series data storage and retrieval.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the measurement instrument was physically installed and commissioned at this location.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the measurement instrument or sensor technology used (e.g., magnetic flow meter, pressure transmitter, thermocouple, pH probe, laser particle size analyzer).']
  is_critical_control_point boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement point is designated as a critical control point for process safety, environmental compliance, or production quality.']
  is_kpi_input boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement point provides input data for calculating key performance indicators such as recovery rate, throughput, or product grade.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the instrument was last calibrated by maintenance or instrumentation technicians.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this measurement point record was most recently updated or modified.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the physical location of the measurement point within the plant, including building, floor, and proximity references.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer or vendor of the measurement instrument.']
  measurement_point_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the measurement point indicating its location and purpose within the processing circuit.']
  measurement_range_max decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the instruments calibrated measurement range.']
  measurement_range_min decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the instruments calibrated measurement range.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Category of physical or chemical property being measured at this point (e.g., flow, pressure, temperature, pH, density, particle size).']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model or part number of the measurement instrument.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration activity based on the calibration frequency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special considerations, or operational notes related to this measurement point.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the measurement point indicating whether it is actively collecting data, under maintenance, or out of service.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional group responsible for maintaining and monitoring this measurement point (e.g., Process Control, Instrumentation, Metallurgy).']
  sampling_frequency_seconds varchar [note: 'Interval in seconds at which the measurement point captures and records data values.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to the specific instrument unit.']
  tag_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric tag identifier assigned to the measurement instrument following plant instrumentation standards (e.g., FT-SAG-001, PT-FLOT-205).']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Desired or optimal measurement value as defined by the process control strategy or Life of Mine (LOM) plan.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit in which the measurement value is expressed (e.g., t/h, kPa, °C, %, g/L, µm, pH units).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for measurement_point. Referenced by measurement_point_id.'
}

Table processing.pipeline {
  pipeline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pipeline']
  associated_pump_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary pump equipment that drives flow through this pipeline, if applicable.']
  upstream_pipeline_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pipeline (upstream_pipeline_id)']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Internal asset management tag or barcode identifier used for tracking, maintenance, and inventory management.']
  coating_type varchar [note: 'Type of protective coating or lining applied to the internal or external surface of the pipeline to prevent corrosion or wear.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the pipeline was formally commissioned and approved for production operations following testing and validation.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code to which the pipeline operating and maintenance costs are allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pipeline record was first created in the system.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Risk-based classification of the pipeline importance to operations, considering production impact, safety, and environmental consequences of failure.']
  design_flow_rate_m3_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Nominal volumetric flow rate the pipeline is designed to handle under normal operating conditions, measured in cubic meters per hour.']
  design_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected operational lifespan of the pipeline in years as specified in the original engineering design.']
  design_pressure_kpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum operating pressure the pipeline is designed to withstand, measured in kilopascals.']
  design_throughput_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Mass throughput capacity the pipeline is designed to transport, measured in tonnes per hour, relevant for slurry and concentrate pipelines.']
  destination_location varchar [note: 'End point or facility where the pipeline terminates, such as thickener feed, concentrate storage, or tailings dam.']
  diameter_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Internal diameter of the pipeline measured in millimeters, critical for flow rate and pressure calculations.']
  elevation_change_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Net vertical elevation change from source to destination measured in meters, positive for uphill flow and negative for downhill flow.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the pipeline was originally installed and commissioned for operational use.']
  insulation_type varchar [note: 'Type of thermal insulation applied to the pipeline, if any, to maintain temperature or prevent freezing.']
  is_gravity_fed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pipeline relies on gravity for flow (true) or requires pumping (false).']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal inspection of the pipeline for integrity, wear, and compliance with safety standards.']
  length_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total physical length of the pipeline measured in meters from source to destination.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary maintenance approach applied to the pipeline, determining inspection frequency and intervention triggers.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the pipeline or its primary components.']
  material_of_construction varchar [note: 'Primary material used in the construction of the pipeline, such as high-density polyethylene, steel, or rubber-lined steel.']
  material_transported varchar [note: 'Specific material, slurry, or substance that the pipeline is designed to transport, including ore type, concentrate grade, or reagent name.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory inspection of the pipeline based on regulatory requirements and maintenance schedules.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special conditions, or historical information about the pipeline.']
  operating_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Typical operating temperature of the material being transported through the pipeline, measured in degrees Celsius.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the pipeline in its lifecycle, indicating availability for production use.']
  pipeline_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier code for the pipeline used in operational systems and reporting.']
  pipeline_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the pipeline used for identification and communication across operations and maintenance teams.']
  pipeline_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pipeline based on the material or substance it transports within the processing plant.']
  plant_area varchar [note: 'Functional area or section of the processing plant where the pipeline is located, such as crushing, grinding, flotation, or tailings.']
  remaining_wall_thickness_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Current minimum wall thickness of the pipeline measured in millimeters, monitored to ensure structural integrity and prevent failure.']
  replacement_value_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated current cost to replace the pipeline with equivalent new infrastructure, used for insurance and capital planning.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number or batch identifier for traceability and warranty purposes.']
  solids_concentration_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Design solids concentration by weight for slurry pipelines, expressed as a percentage, critical for rheology and pump selection.']
  source_location varchar [note: 'Origin point or facility where the pipeline begins, such as crusher discharge, flotation cells, or storage tank.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pipeline record was last modified in the system.']
  wear_rate_mm_per_year decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average rate of internal wear or erosion of the pipeline wall measured in millimeters per year, used for remaining life calculations.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pipeline. Referenced by pipeline_id.'
}

Table processing.process_area {
  process_area_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for process_area']
  parent_process_area_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on process_area (parent_process_area_id)']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of automation and control system integration for this process area.']
  availability_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target mechanical availability percentage for equipment in this process area, calculated as available time divided by total time.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the processing circuit or flowsheet section this area belongs to (e.g., primary grinding circuit, rougher flotation circuit).']
  code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the process area (e.g., SAG-01, FLOT-02, LEACH-A).']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the process area was commissioned and began operational service.']
  control_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of distributed control system (DCS), programmable logic controller (PLC), or supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) system managing this process area.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code for tracking operating expenses and capital expenditures associated with this process area.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this process area record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the process area including its purpose, key equipment, and role in the overall processing flowsheet.']
  design_capacity_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Nameplate design throughput capacity of the process area measured in tonnes per hour.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this process area configuration or record version became effective.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this process area configuration or record version remains effective, null for current records.']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory environmental permit or license number authorizing operation of this process area.']
  feed_size_p80_microns decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target feed particle size distribution P80 (80th percentile) in microns for material entering this process area.']
  hazardous_materials_present boolean [note: 'Indicates whether hazardous chemicals, reagents, or materials are used or stored in this process area.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this process area record is currently active in the system.']
  last_major_upgrade_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent major capital upgrade or expansion project for this process area.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the process area (e.g., Primary SAG Mill, Copper Flotation Circuit, Leach Tank Farm).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, special considerations, or historical context relevant to this process area.']
  operating_hours_per_day decimal(4,2) [note: 'Planned operating hours per day for this process area, accounting for scheduled maintenance windows.']
  planned_closure_date date [note: 'Planned decommissioning or closure date for this process area based on mine life or asset retirement plans.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific processing plant or concentrator facility containing this process area.']
  primary_commodity varchar [note: 'Primary mineral commodity processed in this area (e.g., iron ore, copper, coal, lithium, nickel).']
  process_method varchar [note: 'Specific processing technology or method employed in this area (e.g., SAG mill, HPGR, flotation, leaching, SX-EW).']
  process_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the process area by its primary function in the mineral processing value chain.']
  product_size_p80_microns decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target product particle size distribution P80 (80th percentile) in microns for material exiting this process area.']
  responsible_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee responsible for managing operations in this process area.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Safety risk classification of the process area based on hazard assessment and potential consequence severity.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining site or processing plant where this process area is located.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the process area.']
  target_product_grade_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target concentrate or product grade for output from this process area, expressed as percentage of valuable mineral content.']
  target_recovery_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target mineral recovery rate for this process area expressed as a percentage, aligned with LOM plan targets.']
  target_throughput_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current operational target throughput for the process area in tonnes per hour, may differ from design capacity based on ore characteristics and operational strategy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this process area record was last modified in the system.']
  utilization_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target utilization percentage for this process area, calculated as operating time divided by available time.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for process_area. Referenced by process_area_id.'
}

Table processing.process_stream {
  process_stream_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for process_stream']
  upstream_process_stream_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on process_stream (upstream_process_stream_id)']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when this process stream was commissioned and became operational.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary commodity or mineral being processed in this stream.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this process stream record was first created in the system.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification of this stream based on its impact on overall plant throughput and recovery.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date when this process stream was decommissioned or taken out of service, if applicable.']
  density_measurement_method varchar [note: 'Technology or method used to measure the density or specific gravity of slurry in this stream.']
  design_flow_rate_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Designed or nameplate flow rate capacity of the stream measured in tonnes per hour as per engineering specifications.']
  design_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Target grade or concentration of the valuable mineral in the stream expressed as weight percentage.']
  design_particle_size_p80_microns decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target particle size distribution where 80% of particles pass through, measured in microns, as per process design specifications.']
  design_solids_concentration_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target solids concentration by weight percentage for slurry streams as specified in process design.']
  destination_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment unit to which this stream flows.']
  environmental_monitoring_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stream requires environmental compliance monitoring for discharge or emissions.']
  flow_measurement_method varchar [note: 'Technology or method used to measure the flow rate of this stream.']
  life_of_mine_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life of mine plan version against which this stream design and targets are aligned.']
  mass_balance_node_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stream is a key node in plant-wide mass balance reconciliation calculations.']
  material_classification varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the material type flowing through this stream.']
  measurement_point_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the physical measurement or sampling point location on this stream.']
  nominal_pipe_diameter_mm varchar [note: 'Nominal internal diameter of the pipe or conduit carrying this stream, measured in millimeters.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, special handling requirements, or contextual information about this process stream.']
  ph_control_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether pH monitoring and control is required for this stream as part of process chemistry management.']
  piping_instrumentation_diagram_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number for the piping and instrumentation diagram detailing this stream and its control instrumentation.']
  piping_specification varchar [note: 'Engineering specification reference for the piping or conveyance system used for this stream.']
  process_circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the processing circuit that this stream belongs to (e.g., crushing, grinding, flotation, leaching).']
  process_flow_diagram_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number for the process flow diagram showing this stream in the overall plant flowsheet.']
  processing_plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the processing plant or concentrator facility where this stream exists.']
  reagent_addition_point_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stream is a reagent addition point where chemicals are introduced into the process.']
  sampling_frequency_minutes varchar [note: 'Standard interval in minutes at which samples are collected from this stream for quality analysis.']
  source_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment unit from which this stream originates.']
  stream_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the process stream used across operational systems and reporting.']
  stream_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the process stream describing the material flow or processing stage.']
  stream_pressure_kpa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Typical or design operating pressure of the stream measured in kilopascals.']
  stream_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the process stream in the plant lifecycle.']
  stream_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Typical or design operating temperature of the stream measured in degrees Celsius.']
  stream_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the process stream based on its role in the processing circuit.']
  target_ph_range_max decimal(4,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable pH value for this stream to maintain optimal processing conditions.']
  target_ph_range_min decimal(4,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable pH value for this stream to maintain optimal processing conditions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this process stream record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for process_stream. Referenced by process_stream_id.'
}

  core_diameter varchar [note: 'Standard drill core diameter designation for diamond drill samples. HQ (63.5mm), NQ (47.6mm), PQ (85mm), BQ (36.4mm), AQ (27mm). Applicable only to drill core samples.']
