  resubmission_assay_method varchar [note: 'Analytical method code to be used for the re-analysis. May be the same as the original method or a different method for verification purposes.']
  resubmission_date date [note: 'Date when the sample was formally resubmitted for re-analysis. Represents the business event timestamp for the resubmission workflow initiation.']
  resubmission_element_suite varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of elements or compounds to be analyzed in the re-analysis. May be a subset or superset of the original element suite.']
  resubmission_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the resubmission event. Format: RESUB-YYYYMMDD-NNNN where YYYYMMDD is the resubmission date and NNNN is a sequential counter.']
  resubmission_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the sample was resubmitted. QC_CRM_FAIL: Certified Reference Material out of tolerance; QC_BLANK_CONTAM: Blank contamination detected; QC_DUP_EXCEED: Duplicate precision exceedance; OVERLIMIT_DILUTION: Result over instrument limit requiring dilution; CHECK_ASSAY: Verification assay for resource estimation; UMPIRE_ASSAY: Inter-laboratory bias assessment.']
  resubmission_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the specific circumstances that triggered the resubmission, including reference values, tolerance limits, and deviation magnitude.']
  resubmission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the resubmission workflow. REQUESTED: Initial request logged; APPROVED: Management approval obtained; SUBMITTED: Sample dispatched to laboratory; IN_PROGRESS: Analysis underway; COMPLETED: New result received; SIGNED_OFF: Competent person validation complete; CANCELLED: Resubmission cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: REQUESTED|APPROVED|SUBMITTED|IN_PROGRESS|COMPLETED|SIGNED_OFF|CANCELLED — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  resubmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the resubmission event was recorded in the LIMS system, including time zone information.']
  resubmission_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the resubmission workflow. INTERNAL_REPEAT: Same laboratory re-analysis; EXTERNAL_CHECK: Different laboratory verification; UMPIRE_ASSAY: Third-party inter-laboratory comparison; DILUTION_RERUN: Re-analysis with sample dilution.']
  result_validation_decision varchar [note: 'Competent person decision on which result to accept for resource estimation and reporting. ACCEPT_NEW: Use new resubmission result; ACCEPT_ORIGINAL: Original result deemed acceptable; ACCEPT_AVERAGE: Use average of original and new; REJECT_BOTH: Both results rejected, further investigation required; PENDING: Decision not yet made.']
  tolerance_limit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Acceptable tolerance limit (as percentage or absolute value) for the QA/QC control sample. Exceedance of this limit triggers resubmission.']
  turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected or actual turnaround time in days from resubmission date to new result received date. Used for laboratory performance monitoring and resource estimation scheduling.']
  validation_decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the competent person rationale for the result validation decision, including statistical analysis, geological context, and compliance considerations.']

  Note: 'Tracks resubmission events where samples are re-analysed due to QA/QC failures (CRM out-of-tolerance, blank contamination, duplicate exceedance), over-limit results requiring dilution, check assays for resource estimation verification, or umpire assay requests for inter-laboratory bias assessment. Records original result reference, resubmission reason code, resubmission date, assigned laboratory, new result reference, and final accepted result with sign-off. Provides the audit trail for result validation decisions required under JORC Table 1 and NI 43-101 competent person sign-off.'
}

Table laboratory.laboratory {
  laboratory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the laboratory. Primary key.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.counterparty. Business justification: External laboratories are commercial counterparties with master service agreements, credit terms, payment schedules, and performance KPIs. This link enables contract management, vendor performance eva']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'The recognized accreditation authority that has certified the laboratory: NATA (National Association of Testing Authorities - Australia), SANAS (South African National Accreditation System), CALA (Canadian Association for Laboratory Accreditation), A2LA (American Association for Laboratory Accreditation), UKAS (United Kingdom Accreditation Service), or ISO/IEC 17025 generic certification.']
  accreditation_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the current accreditation certificate expires and requires renewal or re-assessment.']
  accreditation_number varchar [note: 'The unique certificate or registration number issued by the accreditation body to this laboratory.']
  accreditation_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the analytes, methods, and matrices covered under the laboratory\'s accreditation (e.g., fire assay for gold in ore, XRF for multi-element analysis, leach tests for copper recovery).']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address or location descriptor for the laboratory facility.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as building name, floor, or suite number.']
  approved_analyte_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of analytes (elements or compounds) that this laboratory is approved and accredited to analyze (e.g., Au, Cu, Fe, Ni, S, moisture, particle size).']
  approved_method_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of analytical methods that this laboratory is approved to perform (e.g., fire assay, XRF, ICP-MS, AAS, flotation testwork, leach testwork).']
  city varchar [note: 'City or town where the laboratory is located.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for laboratory correspondence, sample submission, and result queries.']
  contact_person_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person or laboratory manager responsible for sample coordination and queries.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the laboratory contact person or reception.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the current service contract or agreement with this laboratory expires or is due for renewal.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the current service contract or agreement with this laboratory commenced.']
  cost_per_sample_standard decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average contracted cost per sample for standard analytical packages, used for budgeting and laboratory performance benchmarking.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the laboratory is located (e.g., AUS for Australia, CAN for Canada, ZAF for South Africa).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this laboratory record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for laboratory costs (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD, ZAR).']
  laboratory_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the laboratory in operational systems and sample tracking workflows.']
  laboratory_name varchar [note: 'Full legal or trading name of the laboratory facility.']
  laboratory_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the laboratory: active (approved for use), inactive (not currently used), suspended (temporarily not approved due to quality issues), or under_review (pending audit or accreditation renewal).']
  laboratory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the laboratory based on its primary function: preparation (sample crushing, splitting), analytical (assay and chemical analysis), metallurgical (process testwork), geochemical (exploration samples), environmental (water, air, soil compliance), or third_party (external commercial laboratory).']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent internal or external audit conducted at this laboratory to verify compliance with quality and accreditation standards.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this laboratory record was last updated or modified.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next audit or quality review of the laboratory.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special instructions, historical performance notes, or operational constraints related to this laboratory.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory is owned and operated internally by the mining company, contracted externally, or part of a joint venture arrangement.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the laboratory address.']
  preferred_laboratory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this laboratory is designated as a preferred provider for specific sample types or programs, based on quality, cost, and turnaround performance.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Internal quality performance rating based on QA/QC (Quality Assurance/Quality Control) results, proficiency testing, and audit outcomes.']
  sample_type_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the sample types and matrices the laboratory is equipped to handle (e.g., drill core, ROM ore, concentrate, tailings, water, soil, vegetation).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the laboratory is located.']
  turnaround_time_rush_days varchar [note: 'Expedited turnaround time in calendar days for priority or rush samples, typically at a premium cost.']
  turnaround_time_standard_days varchar [note: 'Contracted standard turnaround time in calendar days from sample receipt to result delivery for routine samples, as per the Service Level Agreement (SLA).']

  Note: 'Master register of all internal and external laboratories used by the mining operation, including on-site preparation laboratories, on-site analytical laboratories, external commercial assay laboratories, and specialist metallurgical test facilities. Stores laboratory name, location, type (preparation, analytical, metallurgical), accreditation body (NATA, SANAS, CALA, A2LA), accreditation number, accreditation expiry, approved analyte and method scope, contracted turnaround time SLA by sample type, cost per sample, preferred laboratory flag, and active/inactive status. Governs laboratory selection rules for different sample types, programs, and priority levels, and supports laboratory performance benchmarking.'
}

  sample_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sample program or campaign. Primary key.']

  competent_person_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Competent Person (CP) or Qualified Person (QP) responsible for validating and signing off on the sample program design and data quality for regulatory reporting purposes.']
  consultation_stakeholder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.stakeholder. Business justification: Sample programs on traditional lands require stakeholder consultation and FPIC. Indigenous groups and landowners are consulted before drilling/sampling campaigns commence. This link tracks which stake']

Required for budget vs actual reporting and cost variance analysis in mining ope']
  drill_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.drill_program. Business justification: Sample programs are scoped to drill programs for budget allocation and statutory expenditure tracking.   employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this sample program record.']

Direct regulatory compliance linkage.']

Regulatory authorities require analytical expenditure to be reported by licence for a']

  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary laboratory facility (internal or external) contracted to perform analytical work for this program.']
  mine_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific mine area, pit, or underground zone where sampling is being conducted.']
  primary_sample_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geologist or metallurgist responsible for overseeing this sample program, including protocol compliance and data quality.']

Links analytical campaigns to geological targets, critical for resource estimation workflows, geological domain assignment, and compe']

  stage_gate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.stage_gate. Business justification: Sample programs are tied to stage-gate milestones with defined deliverables (e.g., PFS requires infill drilling, FS requires metallurgical testwork). Stage-gate criteria include completion of specifie']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Sample programs must track the tenement under which they operate for regulatory expenditure reporting, compliance with annual expenditure commitments, and resource estimation within tenement boundarie']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Exploration sample programs are tracked under WBS elements for IFRS6 exploration expenditure capitalization and project cost tracking. Essential for determining technical feasibility and reclassificat']
  actual_sample_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of samples collected to date under this program, excluding QA/QC samples. Updated as sampling progresses.']
  analytical_suite_specification varchar [note: 'Specification of which analytes and analytical methods apply to samples collected under this program, such as \'Fire Assay Au + ICP-MS multi-element\' or \'XRF Fe, SiO2, Al2O3, P, S + LOI\'.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the sample program was formally approved by the responsible authority (Chief Geologist, Competent Person, or Mine Manager) to commence.']
  blank_insertion_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Specific insertion rate for blank samples to detect contamination, typically 2-5% of total samples.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total allocated budget for this sample program covering drilling, sampling, sample preparation, analytical costs, and logistics. Expressed in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget amount (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sample program record was first created in the LIMS system.']
  duplicate_insertion_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Specific insertion rate for field or laboratory duplicate samples to assess sampling and analytical precision, typically 5-10% of total samples.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when sample collection and analysis was completed for this program. Nullable for ongoing programs.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sample program record was last updated in the LIMS system.']
  planned_sample_count varchar [note: 'Target number of samples to be collected under this program, excluding QA/QC samples.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the sample program, used in LIMS and reporting systems.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the sample program objectives, scope, and methodology, including geological targets, sampling density, and expected outcomes.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the sampling program or campaign, such as \'Q1 2024 Resource Definition Drilling\' or \'Grade Control Blast Hole Sampling\'.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sample program: draft (planning), active (sampling underway), suspended (temporarily paused), completed (all samples collected and analyzed), or cancelled.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sampling program by its business purpose: resource definition drilling, grade control, infill drilling, geotechnical investigation, process plant feed characterisation, metallurgical variability, or environmental monitoring programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: resource_definition|grade_control|infill_drilling|geotechnical_investigation|metallurgical_variability|process_plant_feed|environmental_monitoring|exploration|quality_assurance — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  qaqc_insertion_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required percentage of QA/QC control samples (blanks, standards, duplicates) to be inserted into the sample stream for this program, typically 5-20% depending on program criticality.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sample program is subject to regulatory reporting requirements under JORC, NI 43-101, SEC S-K 1300, or SAMREC codes (True) or is for internal operational purposes only (False).']
  reporting_standard varchar [note: 'The mineral resource reporting standard that this sample program data will support: JORC (Australia), NI 43-101 (Canada), SEC S-K 1300 (USA), SAMREC (South Africa), or internal (non-public reporting).']
  sample_security_level varchar [note: 'Security classification for samples in this program: standard (normal handling), high_value (precious metals requiring secure transport), or confidential (exploration samples requiring restricted access).']
  sampling_protocol_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the documented sampling protocol or Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that governs sample collection, handling, and chain of custody for this program.']
  standard_insertion_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Specific insertion rate for certified reference material (CRM) standards to monitor analytical accuracy, typically 5-10% of total samples.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when sample collection commenced for this program.']
  turnaround_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for laboratory turnaround time: routine (standard turnaround), expedited (faster than standard), urgent (high priority), or critical (immediate processing required for operational decisions).']

  Note: 'Defines the sampling program or campaign under which samples are collected, such as resource definition drilling, grade control, infill drilling, geotechnical investigation, process plant feed characterisation, metallurgical variability, or environmental monitoring programs. Stores program name, type, associated project or mine area, target commodity, sampling protocol reference, analytical suite specification (which analytes and methods apply), QA/QC insertion rate requirements, start and end date, budget, and responsible geologist or metallurgist. Serves as the governing entity that links samples to their originating business purpose and controls which analytical methods, QA/QC protocols, and turnaround priorities apply to all samples collected under the program.'
}

Table laboratory.instrument {
  instrument_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the analytical instrument. Primary key for the instrument register.']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory facility where this instrument is installed and operated.']
  accreditation_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the accredited scope of testing for this instrument under ISO/IEC 17025 or equivalent laboratory accreditation. Identifies which tests are covered by accreditation.']
  accuracy_specification varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s accuracy specification or laboratory-validated accuracy for the instrument. Expressed as percentage, absolute value, or relative standard deviation.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the instrument was acquired or purchased by the organization.']
  applicable_methods varchar [note: 'List of analytical methods or standard operating procedures (SOPs) that this instrument is qualified to perform. May reference industry standards such as ASTM, ISO, or internal method codes.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Internal asset tag or property number assigned by the organization for fixed asset tracking and inventory management.']
  calibration_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Standard calibration interval in days as defined by the instrument manufacturer, regulatory requirements, or laboratory quality procedures.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration status of the instrument. In Calibration (calibration in progress), Calibration Passed (within calibration validity period), Calibration Failed (failed last calibration check), Calibration Overdue (past due date), Not Applicable (instrument does not require calibration).']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the instrument was commissioned and put into operational service in the laboratory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this instrument record was first created in the system.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the instrument was decommissioned and removed from operational service. Null if still in service.']
  instrument_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the instrument used in laboratory operations and reporting. Typically assigned by LIMS or laboratory management.']
  instrument_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the analytical instrument used for identification in laboratory workflows.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the analytical instrument by technology type. XRF (X-Ray Fluorescence), ICP-MS (Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometry), ICP-OES (Inductively Coupled Plasma Optical Emission Spectrometry), AAS (Atomic Absorption Spectroscopy), LECO (Carbon/Sulfur Analyser), Fire Assay Furnace, Particle Size Analyser, XRD (X-Ray Diffraction), SEM (Scanning Electron Microscope). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: XRF|ICP-MS|ICP-OES|AAS|LECO|Fire Assay Furnace|Particle Size Analyser|XRD|SEM|Other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful calibration performed on the instrument.']
  last_calibration_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent calibration check. Pass (within tolerance), Fail (outside tolerance), Conditional Pass (marginal performance, monitoring required).']
  last_calibration_standard varchar [note: 'Identifier or description of the certified reference material or calibration standard used in the most recent calibration.']
  last_calibration_technician varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the technician who performed the most recent calibration. Required for ISO 17025 traceability.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance performed on the instrument.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this instrument record was last updated in the system.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical location of the instrument within the laboratory facility. Includes building, room number, or laboratory section identifier.']
  maintenance_schedule varchar [note: 'Description of the preventive maintenance schedule for the instrument (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly). May reference maintenance procedure documents.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the analytical instrument.']
  measurement_range_lower decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower limit of the instrument\'s measurement range or detection limit. Units depend on instrument type and analyte.']
  measurement_range_upper decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper limit of the instrument\'s measurement range. Units depend on instrument type and analyte.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the instrument\'s measurement range (e.g., ppm, percent, g/t, mg/L). Varies by instrument type and application.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model number or designation for the instrument. Used for parts procurement and technical support.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next calibration of the instrument. Critical for ISO 17025 compliance and result validity.']
  next_maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance activity on the instrument.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special handling instructions, known issues, or operational considerations for the instrument.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the instrument. Operational (available for use), Under Maintenance (scheduled or unscheduled maintenance), Out of Service (not functional), Calibration Required (due for calibration), Decommissioned (retired from service), Quarantined (results suspect pending investigation).']
  precision_specification varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s precision specification or laboratory-validated precision (repeatability) for the instrument. Typically expressed as relative standard deviation (RSD) or coefficient of variation (CV).']
  responsible_technician varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the laboratory technician or analyst responsible for the operation and routine maintenance of this instrument.']
  sample_throughput_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of samples the instrument can process per day under normal operating conditions. Used for capacity planning and scheduling.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific instrument unit. Critical for warranty, service contracts, and traceability.']
  service_contract_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current service or maintenance contract. Null if no active contract.']
  service_contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the active service or maintenance contract with the manufacturer or third-party service provider.']
  usage_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cumulative operating hours logged for the instrument since commissioning. Used for lifecycle management and maintenance planning.']

  Note: 'Master register of all analytical instruments in the laboratory including XRF spectrometers, ICP-MS, ICP-OES, AAS, LECO analysers, fire assay furnaces, and particle size analysers. Stores instrument ID, type, manufacturer, model, serial number, operational status, and full calibration history (calibration date, type, standard used, pass/fail, drift correction, next due date, certifying technician). Supports instrument traceability for ISO 17025 compliance, result validation, and preventive maintenance scheduling.'
}

Table laboratory.result_validation {
  result_validation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the result validation record. Primary key.']
  laboratory_sample_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory sample whose assay results are being validated.']
  resource_estimate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.resource_estimate. Business justification: Result validation approval is required for specific resource estimates under JORC/NI 43-101. Competent persons must certify that analytical data quality meets reporting standards for each estimate, li']
  sample_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.sample_batch. Business justification: Result validation is performed at the batch level - a competent person validates all results in a batch before release. Currently result_validation has validation_batch_number (STRING) but no FK. Addi']
  validator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee identifier of the validator, linking to the HR system for competency verification and audit purposes.']
  approved_for_process_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the validated assay results are approved for use in metallurgical process control and optimization. True if approved, False if not approved.']
  approved_for_resource_estimation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the validated assay results are approved for use in mineral resource estimation under JORC, NI 43-101, or SAMREC standards. True if approved, False if not approved.']
  blank_contamination_check boolean [note: 'Indicates whether blank samples showed acceptable low contamination levels. True if passed (no contamination detected), False if failed (contamination detected).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments or observations recorded by the validator. Used to capture context, special circumstances, or additional information not covered by structured fields.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action to be taken, such as re-assay of samples, recalibration of instruments, or investigation of laboratory procedures. Populated when corrective_action_required is True.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action is required following this validation (e.g., re-assay, investigation of laboratory procedures). True if corrective action is needed, False otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this validation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  duplicate_precision_check boolean [note: 'Indicates whether duplicate samples met precision acceptance criteria (e.g., relative percent difference within acceptable limits). True if passed, False if failed.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this validation record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  peer_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the peer review was completed. Populated only when peer review was performed.']
  peer_reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the peer reviewer who independently reviewed the validation, if peer review was performed. Used for additional quality assurance on critical validations.']
  qaqc_pass_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of QA/QC samples (standards, duplicates, blanks) that passed acceptance criteria during this validation. Used to quantify the quality of the assay batch.']
  qualification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting any qualifications, conditions, or limitations applied to the validated results. Used when validation outcome is \'qualified\' to explain the nature of the qualification and any restrictions on data use.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the assay results were rejected, including specific QA/QC failures, procedural errors, or data quality issues. Populated only when validation outcome is \'rejected\'.']
  standard_deviation_check boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assay results passed statistical standard deviation checks against certified reference materials (CRMs). True if passed, False if failed.']
  validation_date date [note: 'The date on which the validation was formally completed and signed off by the competent person. This is the principal business event timestamp for the validation lifecycle.']
  validation_method varchar [note: 'The method or technique used to validate the assay results. Indicates the type of quality control check performed (e.g., statistical analysis of QA/QC samples, comparison with certified reference materials, visual inspection of data patterns, peer review). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: statistical_review|qaqc_check|visual_inspection|duplicate_comparison|standard_comparison|blank_check|peer_review|automated_rule — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  validation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this validation event, used for audit trail and traceability.']
  validation_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the validation process: accepted (results approved for use), rejected (results failed validation), or qualified (results accepted with conditions or limitations).']
  validation_software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the LIMS or validation software used to perform the validation. Important for audit trail and reproducibility of validation decisions.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the validation record. Indicates whether the validation is pending review, under review, accepted, rejected, accepted with qualifications, or superseded by a later validation.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the validation was completed, including time-of-day. Used for detailed audit trail and chronological sequencing of validation events.']
  validator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the competent person (geologist, metallurgist, or laboratory manager) who performed and signed off on the validation. Required for JORC, NI 43-101, and SAMREC competent person requirements.']
  validator_professional_registration varchar [note: 'Professional registration or membership number of the validator with a recognized professional body (e.g., AusIMM, CIM, SACNASP). Required to demonstrate competent person status under JORC, NI 43-101, and SAMREC.']
  validator_role varchar [note: 'Professional role or title of the validator. Indicates the authority and competency level of the person performing the validation.']

  Note: 'Records the formal validation and sign-off of assay results by a competent geologist or metallurgist prior to release for use in resource estimation or process control. Stores validation date, validator name and role, validation method (statistical review, QA/QC check, visual inspection), validation outcome (accepted, rejected, qualified), and any qualification notes. Supports JORC, NI 43-101, and SAMREC competent person requirements.'
}

Table laboratory.approval {
  approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this laboratory approval record. Primary key.']
  competent_person_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the Competent Person who grants or manages the laboratory approval']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the laboratory that is approved for use by the Competent Person']
  accreditation_scope_approved varchar [note: 'The specific subset of the laboratory\'s accreditation scope (analytes, methods, matrices) that this Competent Person has approved for their work. May be narrower than the laboratory\'s full accreditation scope based on CP commodity expertise.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the Competent Person formally approved this laboratory for use in their resource estimation and reporting work.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the CP\'s approval of this laboratory for resource estimation work. Values: approved, pending, suspended, revoked, expired.']
  approved_commodity_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of mineral commodities for which this CP has approved this laboratory, aligned with the CP\'s commodity expertise and the laboratory\'s analytical capabilities.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this laboratory approval record was created in the system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this CP-laboratory approval expires and requires renewal or re-evaluation.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent audit or quality review of this laboratory conducted or reviewed by this Competent Person for their approval purposes.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this laboratory approval record was last updated.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next audit or quality review of this laboratory by or on behalf of this Competent Person to maintain approval status.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the Competent Person to document specific conditions, limitations, or qualifications related to their approval of this laboratory.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'The Competent Person\'s quality performance rating for this laboratory based on QA/QC results, turnaround time adherence, and accuracy of results for their specific work. Values: excellent, good, acceptable, marginal, unacceptable.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the approval relationship between a Competent Person and a laboratory for JORC/NI 43-101/SAMREC compliance purposes. It captures the formal approval status, accreditation scope, audit history, and quality ratings that exist only in the context of a specific CP-laboratory pairing. Each record links one competent person to one laboratory with attributes tracking the approval lifecycle, quality assurance, and regulatory compliance requirements for mineral resource and ore reserve reporting.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, Competent Persons must approve multiple laboratories for different commodities, analytical methods, and project types based on their professional judgment and the laboratory\'s accreditation scope. Simultaneously, each laboratory is approved by multiple Competent Persons across different commodity specializations and reporting codes (JORC, NI 43-101, SAMREC). This approval relationship is actively managed as a regulatory compliance requirement, with each CP-laboratory pairing having its own approval status, quality rating, audit schedule, and approved scope that cannot reside on either entity alone.'
}

Table laboratory.service_agreement {
  service_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this laboratory service agreement. Primary key.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the capital project receiving laboratory services under this agreement']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who created this laboratory service agreement record, typically the project geologist or contracts administrator.']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the laboratory providing analytical services under this agreement']
  last_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who last modified this laboratory service agreement record.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the laboratory service agreement: draft (under negotiation), active (in effect and receiving samples), suspended (temporarily paused), completed (contract period ended normally), terminated (ended early).']
  analytical_suite_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the analytical methods, analytes, and sample types covered under this specific project-laboratory agreement, which may be a subset of the laboratory\'s full accreditation scope tailored to project requirements.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the laboratory service agreement expires or is scheduled to terminate, after which the laboratory is no longer authorized to receive project samples unless renewed.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'External contract or purchase order reference number used in procurement and financial systems to track this laboratory service agreement.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the laboratory service agreement becomes effective and the laboratory is authorized to receive samples from the project.']
  cost_per_sample decimal(15,2) [note: 'Negotiated cost per sample for this specific project-laboratory agreement, which may differ from the laboratory\'s standard pricing based on sample volume commitments, contract duration, or project-specific analytical requirements.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this laboratory service agreement record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost_per_sample amount (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD, ZAR).']
  laboratory_role varchar [note: 'The role this laboratory plays in the project\'s quality assurance framework: primary (main analytical provider), check (independent verification of primary results), umpire (dispute resolution when primary and check disagree), or specialist (specific testwork such as metallurgical or geotechnical).']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this laboratory service agreement record.']
  sample_volume_commitment varchar [note: 'Minimum number of samples the project commits to submit to this laboratory over the contract period, used to negotiate preferential pricing and secure laboratory capacity allocation.']
  turnaround_sla_days varchar [note: 'Contracted service level agreement for turnaround time in calendar days from sample receipt to result delivery, negotiated specifically for this project and may differ from the laboratory\'s standard turnaround time based on project priority and volume commitments.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual service agreement between a laboratory and a capital project. It captures the specific terms, analytical scope, turnaround commitments, and pricing negotiated for each project-laboratory engagement. Each record links one laboratory to one capital project with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific service contract, enabling projects to engage multiple laboratories (primary, check, umpire) for different analytical suites while laboratories serve multiple concurrent projects.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, capital projects routinely engage multiple laboratories simultaneously to fulfill different analytical roles: a primary laboratory for routine assays, a check laboratory for independent QA/QC verification (typically 5-10% of samples), and an umpire laboratory for dispute resolution when results diverge. Conversely, each laboratory serves multiple concurrent projects across different sites and commodities. This is not a theoretical possibility but standard operational practice driven by quality assurance requirements, risk management, and the need for specialized analytical capabilities (e.g., metallurgical testwork vs routine geochemistry).'
}

Table laboratory.program_equipment_assignment {
  program_equipment_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each equipment assignment to a sample program. Primary key.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the mining asset (drill rig, excavator, sampler) assigned to the program']
  assigned_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (typically mine planner, geology supervisor, or equipment coordinator) who authorized this equipment assignment to the sample program.']
  sample_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sample program or campaign that this equipment assignment supports']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date when the asset completed work on this sample program or was deallocated. Null if assignment is ongoing. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about this equipment assignment, including special requirements, operational constraints, performance issues, or reasons for early termination.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was allocated to and began working on this sample program. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this equipment assignment: planned (scheduled but not started), active (equipment currently working on program), suspended (temporarily paused due to weather, maintenance, or program hold), completed (assignment finished successfully), cancelled (assignment terminated before completion).']
  cost_allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the asset\'s operating costs allocated to this sample program during the assignment period. Used when equipment is shared across multiple concurrent programs. Sum of cost_allocation_percent across all concurrent program assignments for an asset should equal 100%. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment assignment record was created in the system.']
  equipment_role varchar [note: 'The functional role the asset performed within this sample program: primary_drilling (main drill rig for sample collection), support_drilling (infill or verification drilling), excavation (pit access or trench sampling), sample_collection (dedicated sampling equipment), sample_transport (moving samples to storage), site_preparation (access roads, pads), other. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  equipment_utilization_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total engine hours or operating hours the asset spent working on this specific sample program. Used for cost allocation and program budget tracking. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  planned_utilization_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated or planned engine hours for this asset to complete its role in the sample program. Used for resource planning and variance analysis against actual utilization hours.']
  program_phase varchar [note: 'The phase of the sample program during which this equipment assignment is active: planning (equipment reserved but not yet deployed), mobilization (equipment being moved to site), active_sampling (equipment actively collecting samples), demobilization (equipment being removed from program), completed (assignment finished). Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment assignment record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Assignment between sample_program and asset. It captures the allocation of mining equipment (drill rigs, excavators, samplers) to specific sampling programs for the duration of their participation. Each record links one sample_program to one asset with attributes that track utilization hours, cost allocation, assignment timing, equipment role, and program phase. This enables accurate tracking of equipment utilization across concurrent programs and supports cost allocation for shared equipment resources.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, sample programs (resource definition drilling, grade control, metallurgical campaigns) routinely utilize multiple assets concurrently—a resource definition program may deploy 3 drill rigs, 2 excavators, and sample collection equipment simultaneously across different mine areas. Conversely, each asset (especially mobile equipment like drill rigs) participates in multiple sample programs over time and often concurrently when programs overlap geographically or temporally. The business actively manages these assignments to track equipment utilization hours per program, allocate operating costs across concurrent programs, and plan resource availability.'
}

Table laboratory.study_analytical_specification {
  study_analytical_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this study analytical specification record. Primary key.']
  analytical_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the analytical method selected for use in this feasibility study']
  feasibility_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the feasibility study for which this analytical method was specified']
  approved_for_study_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this method has been formally approved for use in this specific feasibility study by the competent person or study lead consultant.']
  cost_per_sample decimal(10,2) [note: 'Study-specific cost per sample for this analytical method, which may differ from the standard laboratory cost due to volume discounts, external laboratory pricing, or study-specific contract terms.']
  detection_limit_adequacy varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the methods detection limits are adequate for the expected grade ranges and resource estimation requirements of this specific feasibility study. Values: adequate, marginal, inadequate.']
  expected_sample_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of samples that will be analyzed using this method during this feasibility study, used for cost estimation and laboratory capacity planning.']
  laboratory_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the specific laboratory (internal or external) that will perform this analytical method for this feasibility study, as different studies may use different laboratory providers.']
  method_selection_rationale varchar [note: 'Documented business justification for why this specific analytical method was selected for this feasibility study, including technical suitability, cost considerations, accreditation requirements, and matrix compatibility with expected sample types.']
  primary_analyte_target varchar [note: 'The primary element or compound of interest for this method in the context of this feasibility study (e.g., Au for gold projects, Cu for copper projects), which may be a subset of the methods full analyte list.']
  specification_date date [note: 'Date when this analytical method was formally specified and approved for use in this feasibility study.']
  specification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this method specification within the feasibility study lifecycle. Values: proposed (under review), approved (ready for use), active (currently in use), completed (study finished), superseded (replaced by alternative method).']
  turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected turnaround time in days for this analytical method within the context of this feasibility study, accounting for study-specific logistics, sample volumes, and laboratory capacity constraints.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the specification and selection of analytical methods for feasibility studies. It captures the formal selection of which laboratory analytical methods will be used to analyze samples during a feasibility study, including the rationale for selection, adequacy assessment, and study-specific cost and timing parameters. Each record links one analytical method to one feasibility study with attributes that exist only in the context of this study-method pairing.. Existence Justification: In mining feasibility studies, multiple analytical methods are specified for different elements, matrices, and purposes (e.g., fire assay for gold, ICP-MS for multi-element suites, XRF for major oxides). Each method is reused across multiple feasibility studies over time. The relationship represents a formal specification decision made during study planning, where the study team selects which methods are appropriate for their specific commodity, expected grade ranges, and resource estimation requirements.'
}

Table equipment.asset {
  asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical mining asset record in the master register. Primary key for the asset data product.']
  asset_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset_class. Business justification: The asset table currently stores asset_class as a STRING attribute, but a dedicated asset_class master table exists with PK asset_class_id. This is a classic denormalization pattern that should be nor']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Mining equipment assets are acquired and commissioned within capital projects (mine expansions, new processing plants). Business process: asset capitalization tracking, project asset registers, and ha']
  parent_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent asset in a multi-component asset hierarchy (e.g., an engine assembly belonging to a haul truck). Enables parent-child asset relationship modelling for complex equipment units. Null for top-level assets.']
  ppe_issuance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.ppe_issuance. Business justification: Certain equipment requires specialized PPE (hearing protection for drill rigs, fall protection for elevated platforms, respiratory protection for enclosed cabs). Asset records should link to the PPE i']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Mining equipment has OEM suppliers; tracking vendor enables warranty claims, parts procurement, and supplier performance analysis. Real business process: OEM parts sourcing and warranty management. Ne']
  project_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_contract. Business justification: Equipment procurement contracts (haul trucks, excavators, processing equipment) are managed as project contracts. Business process: equipment supply agreement tracking, warranty management, and contra']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Mining assets are often dedicated to specific product streams (iron ore haul trucks, coal handling equipment). Production planning, asset allocation, and performance reporting require tracking which p']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Mining equipment operates within specific tenement boundaries. Real operations track which licence area each asset is deployed to for regulatory compliance, expenditure allocation, and operational pla']
  training_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.training_record. Business justification: Specific equipment classes require operator certification (haul truck licenses, dozer tickets, drill rig competencies). Assets should reference the required training/competency record to enforce that ']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original capital expenditure (CAPEX) purchase price of the asset in the reporting currency. Used for depreciation calculations, asset register valuation, and capital replacement planning under IAS 16 and IFRS.']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the asset, typically combining equipment type and fleet number (e.g., \'Haul Truck HT-042\', \'SAG Mill 01\'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and work orders.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the asset was formally commissioned and placed into operational service at the mine site. Marks the start of the asset\'s operational lifecycle for maintenance scheduling, warranty tracking, and depreciation under IFRS.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA cost center code to which the asset\'s operating expenditure (OPEX) and maintenance costs are allocated. Enables financial reporting by asset and supports OPEX tracking against budget.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Maintenance criticality classification of the asset based on its impact on production, safety, and environmental compliance if it fails. Drives maintenance strategy selection, spare parts stocking levels, and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) prioritisation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the acquisition cost and financial values associated with the asset (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Supports multi-currency asset register reporting.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date the asset was permanently removed from operational service and decommissioned. Null for assets still in service. Used for asset lifecycle reporting, capital replacement planning, and mine rehabilitation cost estimation.']
  eam_asset_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for the asset record in Infor EAM. Used for system-of-record traceability and integration with maintenance work orders, preventive maintenance schedules, and asset lifecycle management.']
  engine_power_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'OEM-rated engine or motor power output of the asset in kilowatts (kW). Used for fuel consumption benchmarking, energy efficiency analysis, and equipment selection for mine planning. Null for non-powered assets.']
  fleet_number varchar [note: 'Operational fleet number assigned to the asset at the mine site. Used by operators, dispatchers, and Fleet Management System (FMS) to identify and track the asset in the field. Sourced from Caterpillar MineStar and Infor EAM.']
  fuel_tank_capacity_litres decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum fuel tank capacity of the asset in litres. Used for fuel logistics planning, refuelling scheduling, and fuel consumption rate benchmarking. Null for electric or non-fuel-powered assets.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Primary energy source or fuel type powering the asset. Used for fuel consumption tracking, emissions reporting under GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement, and fleet electrification planning.']
  is_mobile boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset is a mobile mining unit (True) such as a haul truck, excavator, or drill rig, or a fixed plant asset (False) such as a conveyor, crusher, or mill. Drives different tracking, reporting, and maintenance workflows.']
  last_major_overhaul_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent major overhaul or rebuild performed on the asset. Used to assess remaining component life, plan future overhauls, and calculate Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) in the maintenance management system.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Descriptive operational location of the asset within the mine site (e.g., \'Pit 3 North Wall\', \'Processing Plant - Grinding Circuit\', \'ROM Pad\'). Complements the site code with granular positional context for maintenance and dispatch.']
  manufacture_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the asset was manufactured by the OEM. Used to calculate asset age, depreciation schedules, and Life of Mine (LOM) planning for fleet replacement.']
  model varchar [note: 'OEM model designation of the asset (e.g., CAT 793F, Komatsu 930E, Sandvik DR461i). Used for spare parts management, maintenance procedures, and performance benchmarking against OEM specifications.']
  next_scheduled_service_date date [note: 'Date of the next planned preventive maintenance service for the asset as scheduled in Infor EAM. Used for maintenance planning, production scheduling, and availability forecasting.']
  odometer_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current cumulative odometer reading in kilometres for mobile assets. Used alongside SMR hours for maintenance scheduling, tyre life management, and haul road condition monitoring. Null for fixed plant assets.']
  operating_weight_tonnes decimal(10,2) [note: 'OEM-rated operating weight of the asset in metric tonnes. Used for road and haul route load limit compliance, ground bearing pressure calculations, and mine infrastructure design.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the asset indicating whether it is available for production, undergoing maintenance, on standby, or permanently decommissioned. Drives availability calculations and maintenance scheduling in Infor EAM and MineStar.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the asset is company-owned, leased under a finance or operating lease, short-term hired, or contractor-supplied. Drives financial treatment under IFRS 16 and determines maintenance responsibility.']
  payload_capacity_tonnes decimal(10,2) [note: 'OEM-rated maximum payload capacity of the asset in metric tonnes. Applicable to haul trucks, loaders, and material handling equipment. Used for production planning, haulage cycle optimisation, and OEE calculations. Null for fixed plant assets.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset record was first created in the master register. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and compliance with asset register governance requirements.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset record was most recently updated in the master register. Used for change tracking, data quality monitoring, and integration refresh management in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  scada_tag varchar [note: 'SCADA tag identifier used to link the asset to its real-time process data streams in the OSIsoft PI System historian. Applicable primarily to fixed plant assets such as conveyors, crushers, mills, and processing equipment.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) serial number uniquely identifying the physical asset unit. Used for warranty tracking, OEM service records, and regulatory compliance. Sourced from Infor EAM.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the mine site where the asset is currently assigned and operated. Enables site-level fleet reporting, OEE benchmarking, and cross-site asset utilisation analysis. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Plant Master.']
  smr_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current cumulative Service Meter Reading (SMR) in engine hours recorded on the asset. Used as the primary trigger for preventive maintenance scheduling, component life tracking, and MTBF calculations in Infor EAM.']
  tag varchar [note: 'Internal asset tag or barcode label affixed to the physical asset for inventory and maintenance identification. Distinct from fleet number and serial number. Sourced from Infor EAM.']
  telematics_unit_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the onboard telematics or GPS tracking unit installed on the asset. Used to link real-time positional and operational data from Caterpillar MineStar and OSIsoft PI System for fleet management and equipment health monitoring.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'OEM-specified or engineering-assessed useful operational life of the asset in years. Used for straight-line depreciation calculations, Life of Mine (LOM) fleet planning, and capital replacement scheduling under IAS 16.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the OEM warranty for the asset expires. Used to trigger warranty claim processes, plan post-warranty maintenance strategies, and manage OEM service agreements in Infor EAM and Pronto Xi.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element associated with the asset for capital expenditure (CAPEX) tracking and project cost allocation. Used for mine development and capital project reporting.']

  Note: 'Master register of all mining fleet and fixed plant assets including haul trucks, excavators, drills, shovels, dozers, graders, conveyors, crushers, mills, and processing equipment. Each record represents a unique physical asset identified by fleet number and serial number. Captures OEM manufacturer, model, year of manufacture, commissioning date, current operational status, assigned site, and parent-child relationships for multi-component assets. Sourced from Infor EAM. Serves as the SSOT for equipment identity, classification, and lifecycle status across all mine sites.'
}

Table equipment.asset_class {
  asset_class_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each asset class record in the mining equipment classification hierarchy. Primary key for the asset_class reference master.']
  asset_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected useful life of the asset class expressed in calendar years for financial depreciation and LOM planning purposes. Used alongside useful_life_operating_hours to determine the binding depreciation constraint. Supports CAPEX modelling and NPV/IRR analysis.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the nominal_capacity field (e.g., t for payload tonnes, t/h for throughput tonnes per hour, m3 for bucket/bowl volume, mm for drill hole diameter, kW for power rating). Ensures correct interpretation of capacity values across equipment types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: t|t/h|m3|m3/h|mm|kW|MW|m|km/h — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  capex_budget_category varchar [note: 'Capital expenditure (CAPEX) budget category under which assets of this class are typically funded. Sustaining CAPEX covers replacement of existing capacity; expansion CAPEX covers new capacity additions. Used for capital allocation, LOM planning, and financial reporting under IFRS.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the asset class within the equipment classification hierarchy (e.g., HT-320T for 320-tonne haul truck class, SAG-36FT for 36-foot SAG mill class). Used as the business key across ERP, EAM, and FMS systems.']
  class_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the asset class including its primary function, operational context within the mine, and key distinguishing characteristics. Supports procurement specifications and capital budgeting documentation.']
  class_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the asset class describing the equipment type and key specification (e.g., \'Ultra-Class Haul Truck 320T\', \'Semi-Autogenous Grinding Mill 36ft\', \'Rotary Blasthole Drill 270mm\'). Used in reports, dashboards, and maintenance planning documents.']
  class_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the asset class record. Active classes are in current use for fleet planning and procurement. Phasing_out classes are being replaced by newer models. Retired classes are no longer procured but may still have operating assets. Controls visibility in procurement and planning systems.']
  co2_emission_factor_kg_per_hr decimal(10,4) [note: 'Carbon dioxide (CO2) emission factor for assets of this class in kilograms per operating hour, derived from OEM fuel consumption specifications and applicable emission conversion factors. Used for Scope 1 greenhouse gas emissions reporting under GRI, ICMM, and regulatory environmental disclosure requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset class record was first created in the system, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Provides audit trail for data governance and lineage tracking in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  critical_equipment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset class is designated as critical equipment whose failure would cause significant production loss, safety risk, or environmental incident. Critical equipment classes receive priority maintenance resourcing, enhanced spare parts stockholding, and elevated monitoring frequency in OSIsoft PI System.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting depreciation method applied to assets of this class for financial reporting and capital budgeting purposes. Units_of_production is common for mining equipment tied to ore tonnes mined. Aligns with IAS 16 and IFRS reporting requirements.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this asset class definition became effective and valid for use in fleet planning, procurement, and maintenance scheduling. Supports temporal validity tracking for the asset class master record.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this asset class definition ceases to be effective (nullable for open-ended active classes). Used to manage class version transitions when OEM specifications change or when a class is retired from the fleet register.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Functional equipment type within the fleet category (e.g., haul_truck, excavator, blasthole_drill, dozer, grader, water_cart, light_vehicle, conveyor, crusher, sag_mill, ball_mill, hpgr, flotation_cell, thickener, stacker, reclaimer). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: haul_truck|excavator|blasthole_drill|dozer|grader|water_cart|light_vehicle|conveyor|crusher|sag_mill|ball_mill|hpgr|flotation_cell|thickener|stacker|reclaimer — promote to reference product]']
  fleet_category varchar [note: 'Top-level classification distinguishing between mobile fleet assets (haul trucks, excavators, drills, dozers, graders, water carts, light vehicles) and fixed plant assets (conveyors, crushers, mills, flotation cells, thickeners). Drives maintenance strategy, depreciation treatment, and fleet sizing models.']
  fuel_consumption_rate_l_per_hr decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard OEM-rated fuel consumption rate for assets of this class in litres per operating hour under typical mine operating conditions. Used for fuel budget forecasting, OPEX modelling, carbon emissions estimation, and fleet energy management reporting.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class involve or carry hazardous materials (e.g., explosives transport vehicles, acid handling equipment in SX-EW circuits, cyanide dosing systems in leaching). Triggers mandatory HSE compliance controls, PPE requirements, and IsoMetrix risk assessment workflows.']
  infor_eam_class_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding asset class identifier in the Infor EAM system used for asset lifecycle management, preventive maintenance scheduling, and work order management. Enables bi-directional data reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational EAM system.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset class record was most recently modified, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and audit compliance in the silver layer.']
  maintenance_strategy_type varchar [note: 'Primary maintenance strategy applied to assets of this class: time_based (fixed interval PM schedules), condition_based (triggered by measured condition thresholds), predictive (AI/ML-driven failure prediction from sensor data), or run_to_failure (for low-criticality components). Drives maintenance planning in Infor EAM and CMMS scheduling.']
  major_overhaul_interval_hours varchar [note: 'Operating hour interval at which a major component overhaul or rebuild is required for assets of this class (e.g., 20,000 hours for engine rebuild, 40,000 hours for full structural overhaul). Drives long-range maintenance CAPEX planning and LOM cost modelling.']
  minestar_equipment_class_code varchar [note: 'Equipment class identifier as defined in the Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management System (FMS). Used to align telematics data, productivity metrics, and equipment health signals from MineStar with the enterprise asset class master.']
  nominal_capacity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Principal quantitative capacity specification for the asset class expressed in the relevant unit of measure (e.g., payload tonnes for haul trucks, throughput tonnes per hour for mills and crushers, bucket capacity m³ for excavators, hole diameter mm for drills). The principal numeric fact defining the class\'s productive capability.']
  oem_category varchar [note: 'Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) product category or model family designation as defined by the manufacturer (e.g., Caterpillar 793, Komatsu 930E, Liebherr T 284, Sandvik DR416i). Used for OEM warranty tracking, parts compatibility, and service agreement alignment.']
  oem_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer for this asset class (e.g., Caterpillar, Komatsu, Liebherr, Sandvik, Metso, FLSmidth). Used for vendor relationship management, warranty claims, and procurement sourcing strategies.']
  operating_weight_tonnes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross operating weight of the asset class in metric tonnes as specified by the OEM. Used for haul road design, bridge load ratings, ground bearing pressure calculations, and site infrastructure planning.']
  pm_interval_hours varchar [note: 'Standard preventive maintenance (PM) service interval in operating hours for assets of this class (e.g., 250 hours for minor service, 1000 hours for major service). Defines the base PM scheduling cadence in the CMMS and Infor EAM maintenance plans.']
  power_rating_kw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Installed engine or motor power rating for the asset class in kilowatts (kW) as specified by the OEM. Used for energy consumption modelling, fuel burn estimation, electrical infrastructure planning, and OPEX forecasting.']
  replacement_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current estimated replacement cost for a single asset unit of this class in US dollars. Used for insurance valuation, capital budgeting, CAPEX planning, and NPV/IRR analysis in LOM financial models. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity in procurement negotiations.']
  sap_asset_class_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding asset class code (ANLKL) in SAP S/4HANA Fixed Asset Accounting (FI-AA) module. Used to reconcile the equipment master data with the financial asset register and ensure consistent depreciation treatment across ERP and EAM systems.']
  size_capacity_tier varchar [note: 'Standardised size and capacity tier classification for the asset class used in fleet sizing models and capital budgeting (e.g., ultra_class for 300T+ haul trucks, large for 150-299T, medium for 90-149T). Enables fleet mix optimisation and benchmarking across mine sites.']
  target_availability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target physical availability percentage for assets of this class expressed as a percentage (e.g., 85.00 for 85%). Physical availability is the proportion of scheduled time the equipment is mechanically available for operation. Used as the benchmark for OEE calculations and maintenance performance KPIs.']
  target_mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) in operating hours for assets of this class. MTBF is the average operating time between unplanned mechanical failures. Used to set maintenance performance benchmarks, evaluate reliability improvement initiatives, and support predictive maintenance model calibration.']
  target_mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) in hours for assets of this class. MTTR measures the average time required to restore equipment to operational status following an unplanned failure. Used to benchmark maintenance crew efficiency and spare parts availability strategies.']
  target_oee_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) percentage for assets of this class, representing the combined product of availability, utilisation, and performance rate (e.g., 65.00 for 65%). OEE is the primary productivity benchmark for mining equipment classes used in production reporting and capital justification.']
  target_utilisation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target utilisation percentage for assets of this class expressed as a percentage of available hours actually used for productive work (e.g., 75.00 for 75%). Distinct from availability — utilisation measures productive use of available time. Used in fleet sizing models and production planning.']
  telematics_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class are equipped with telematics and real-time monitoring capability integrated with Caterpillar MineStar or OSIsoft PI System. Determines data availability for real-time OEE tracking, predictive maintenance, and fuel consumption monitoring.']
  useful_life_operating_hours varchar [note: 'Expected useful life of the asset class expressed in total operating hours as defined by OEM specifications and mine engineering standards (e.g., 80,000 hours for a large haul truck, 120,000 hours for a SAG mill). Drives depreciation schedules, LOM capital replacement planning, and fleet renewal strategies.']

  Note: 'Reference classification hierarchy for mining equipment types organised into mobile fleet (haul trucks, excavators, drills, dozers, graders, water carts, light vehicles) and fixed plant (conveyors, crushers, SAG mills, ball mills, HPGR, flotation cells, thickeners, stackers, reclaimers). Each class defines OEM category, asset class code, size/capacity tier, expected useful life in operating hours, depreciation method, maintenance strategy type (time-based, condition-based, predictive), and target availability percentage. Serves as the reference master for fleet sizing models, maintenance planning, and capital budgeting.'
}

Table equipment.fleet_assignment {
  fleet_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each fleet assignment record in the MineStar Fleet Management System. Primary key for the fleet_assignment data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mobile fleet asset (haul truck, drill, dozer, grader, loader, etc.) being assigned. Links to the equipment master register sourced from Infor EAM.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: During construction and commissioning phases, production equipment is assigned to support project activities (haul road construction, pre-stripping, commissioning support). Business process: project e']
  mine_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine area, pit, bench, or processing circuit to which the fleet asset is operationally assigned. Links to the mine location/area master.']
  primary_fleet_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the responsible supervisor or shift boss accountable for this fleet assignment. Links to the workforce/employee master.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine production schedule that this fleet assignment supports. Links to the production schedule in mine.production_schedule.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Each fleet assignment moves or processes a specific saleable product. Dispatch systems track product per assignment for production reconciliation, grade control, and stockpile management. Material_typ']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this fleet assignment is active. Links to the shift schedule master to identify day/night shift, shift date, and crew rotation.']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Fleet assignments occur within specific tenement areas. Mining operations must track which licence area work is performed in for expenditure reporting, compliance with tenement conditions, and allocat']
  assignment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual elapsed duration of the fleet assignment in minutes, calculated from assignment start to end timestamps. Stored as a business field for shift reporting and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis without requiring real-time computation.']
  assignment_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the fleet asset completed or was released from its operational assignment. Null if the assignment is still active. Used to calculate assignment duration and shift utilisation.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the fleet assignment, generated by MineStar Fleet Management System. Used for cross-system referencing, dispatch communications, and operational reporting.']
  assignment_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the fleet asset commenced its operational assignment at the designated mine area or task. This is the principal real-world event time for the assignment lifecycle.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fleet assignment. \'planned\' indicates a future assignment not yet commenced; \'active\' indicates the equipment is currently deployed; \'completed\' indicates the assignment has concluded; \'cancelled\' indicates the assignment was voided before commencement; \'suspended\' indicates a temporary halt due to breakdown, weather, or safety hold.']
  bench_level varchar [note: 'The specific bench elevation or level within the pit to which the fleet asset is assigned, expressed as a mine elevation reference (e.g., RL 450, Bench 12). Used for spatial tracking and production reconciliation.']
  delay_code varchar [note: 'The standardised delay classification code recorded when the fleet asset experienced a non-productive delay during the assignment (e.g., mechanical breakdown, waiting for blast, road block, operator break). Sourced from MineStar delay recording. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product with delay_code and delay_description]']
  delay_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The total duration in minutes of recorded delays experienced by the fleet asset during this assignment. Used in conjunction with delay_code for availability and utilisation analysis and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) reporting.']
