  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'The mine location code identifying where material is being delivered (ROM pad, waste dump, crusher, stockpile, processing plant). Used for material tracking and haulage cycle analysis.']
  dispatch_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking assigned by the MineStar dispatch system to determine the order in which fleet assets are assigned to tasks. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used for optimising fleet utilisation and meeting production targets.']
  distance_travelled_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'The total distance in kilometres travelled by the mobile fleet asset during this assignment, sourced from MineStar GPS telematics. Used for haulage cycle analysis, tyre management, and maintenance scheduling.']
  engine_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'The number of engine operating hours accumulated by the fleet asset during this assignment, sourced from MineStar telematics. Used for maintenance scheduling, MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) calculation, and asset lifecycle management.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'The category of mobile fleet equipment being assigned. Denormalised from the equipment master for operational reporting and dispatch efficiency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: haul_truck|drill|dozer|excavator|loader|grader|water_cart|conveyor|crusher — promote to reference product]']
  fuel_consumed_litres decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual volume of diesel fuel consumed in litres by the fleet asset during this assignment period, sourced from MineStar telematics or fuel management system. Used for OPEX (Operating Expenditure) tracking and fuel efficiency analysis.']
  idle_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The total number of minutes the fleet asset was in an idle state (engine running but not performing productive work) during this assignment. Used for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis and identifying productivity improvement opportunities.']
  is_planned_assignment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fleet assignment was pre-planned in the mine production schedule (True) or was an ad-hoc/reactive dispatch by the shift supervisor (False). Used to measure schedule adherence and dispatch planning effectiveness.']
  minestar_assignment_ref varchar [note: 'The native assignment reference or dispatch ID from the Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management System. Used for traceability and reconciliation back to the source system of record.']
  payload_tonnes decimal(12,3) [note: 'The actual payload weight in metric tonnes carried or processed during this fleet assignment, as recorded by on-board payload monitoring systems. Applicable primarily to haul truck assignments. Used for production reconciliation and truck factor analysis.']
  pit_name varchar [note: 'The name or code of the open-cut pit or underground mine area to which the fleet asset is assigned. Provides operational context for dispatch and production reporting.']
  planned_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The planned duration of the fleet assignment in minutes as per the mine production schedule or dispatch plan. Used to measure schedule adherence and identify variance between planned and actual assignment durations.']
  planned_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The originally scheduled end date and time for the fleet assignment as per the mine production schedule or dispatch plan. Used to measure schedule adherence.']
  planned_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The originally scheduled start date and time for the fleet assignment as per the mine production schedule or dispatch plan. Used to measure schedule adherence and dispatch efficiency.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fleet assignment record was first captured in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fleet assignment record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  shift_date date [note: 'The calendar date of the shift during which this fleet assignment occurs. Used as the primary date dimension for daily production reporting and shift-based performance analysis.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'The shift classification during which the fleet assignment is active. Typically day or night shift in a 12-hour rotation. Used for shift-based production reporting and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis.']
  source_location_code varchar [note: 'The mine location code identifying where material is being loaded or work is being performed (dig face, blast block, stockpile). Used for material tracking and production reconciliation in MineStar.']
  supervisor_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes recorded by the shift supervisor regarding this fleet assignment, including any special instructions, safety observations, or operational exceptions. Sourced from MineStar dispatch comments.']
  target_payload_tonnes decimal(12,3) [note: 'The target or design payload weight in metric tonnes for the fleet asset during this assignment, based on equipment specifications and mine planning targets. Used to measure payload efficiency and identify overloading or underloading.']
  task_type varchar [note: 'The primary operational task type assigned to the mobile fleet asset during this assignment. Classifies the nature of work being performed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: haulage|drilling|dozing|blasting_support|loading|grading|water_cart|ancillary — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Tracks the operational assignment of mobile fleet assets to specific mine areas, pits, benches, or processing circuits. Records assignment start and end dates, assigned shift, operating location, task type (haulage, drilling, dozing), and the responsible supervisor. Sourced from MineStar Fleet Management System.'
}

Table equipment.oee_record {
  oee_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each OEE composite metric record. Primary key for the oee_record data product in the equipment domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining fleet or fixed plant asset (e.g., haul truck, drill, shovel, crusher, conveyor) for which this OEE record is computed. Sourced from Infor EAM asset register and Caterpillar MineStar equipment master.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP cost centre responsible for this asset during the OEE period. Used for OPEX allocation and budget vs actual reporting against fleet performance.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: OEE targets and performance vary by product line in multi-product mining operations (iron ore lump vs fines circuits, different coal products). Production reporting and continuous improvement initiati']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this OEE record was captured. Links to the shift schedule master to derive shift type, crew, and duration context.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or operational location where the asset is deployed. Enables site-level OEE benchmarking and multi-site fleet performance comparison.']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: OEE metrics are tracked by tenement for performance reporting and expenditure commitment validation. Real mining operations report productivity by licence area to demonstrate compliance with minimum e']
  actual_cycle_time_sec decimal(12,4) [note: 'The measured average time per production unit (e.g., haul cycle, crusher pass) achieved during the period. Compared against ideal_cycle_time_sec to derive Performance Rate. Sourced from MineStar dispatch and OSIsoft PI process historian.']
  actual_operating_time_hrs decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual hours the asset was in productive operation during the period, after deducting unplanned downtime and breakdowns. Core input to OEE Availability Rate calculation. Sourced from MineStar equipment health and OSIsoft PI historian.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee ID of the production supervisor or engineer who approved this OEE record in the reconciliation workflow. Supports audit trail requirements for OPEX reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  availability_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The ratio of actual operating time to planned operating time, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9250 = 92.50%). First component of the OEE composite metric. Calculated as actual_operating_time_hrs / planned_operating_time_hrs. Stored as a raw rate for precision; percentage display handled in reporting layer.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for production supervisors or engineers to record contextual notes about this OEE record, such as reasons for low availability, abnormal operating conditions, or data quality caveats.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this OEE record was first created in the data platform, either by automated ingestion from MineStar/OSIsoft PI or by manual entry. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the primary operational system from which this OEE record was sourced or reconciled. Supports data lineage tracing and audit of production reconciliation processes.']
  distance_travelled_km decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total distance travelled by mobile fleet assets (e.g., haul trucks, dozers) during the period, in kilometres. Used for tyre wear analysis, fuel efficiency benchmarking, and haul road condition assessment. Not applicable to fixed plant assets.']
  engine_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total engine running hours accumulated by the asset during the period. Used for maintenance scheduling, MTBF calculation, and asset lifecycle management in Infor EAM. Sourced from MineStar equipment health telematics.']
  fuel_consumption_l decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total diesel fuel consumed by the asset during the period, measured in litres. Used for fuel efficiency analysis, OPEX cost allocation, and carbon emissions reporting. Sourced from MineStar fuel management or SAP MM fuel issue records.']
  fuel_efficiency_t_per_l decimal(10,6) [note: 'Ratio of actual throughput in tonnes to fuel consumed in litres during the period. Key OPEX efficiency metric for haul trucks and mobile fleet. Enables benchmarking across fleet and identification of underperforming units.']
  ideal_cycle_time_sec decimal(12,4) [note: 'The theoretical minimum time required to produce one unit (e.g., one haul cycle, one crusher pass) under optimal conditions. Used as the benchmark denominator in OEE Performance Rate calculation. Sourced from engineering design specifications or MineStar baseline models.']
  is_reconciled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this OEE record has been through the production reconciliation process and validated against shift reports and source system data. Unreconciled records are provisional and should not be used for OPEX reporting.']
  load_count varchar [note: 'Total number of discrete load cycles (e.g., truck loads, shovel passes) completed by the asset during the period. Used for cycle time analysis and Performance Rate validation. Sourced from MineStar dispatch system.']
  oee_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The composite OEE metric expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.7500 = 75.00%), calculated as the multiplicative product of availability_rate × performance_rate × quality_rate. This is the primary KPI for fleet performance benchmarking, bottleneck identification, and OPEX reporting. Stored as a pre-reconciled rate from the production reconciliation system.']
  oee_target_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The budgeted or contractually agreed OEE target rate for this asset type and period, expressed as a decimal. Used for variance analysis between actual OEE and planned OEE in OPEX reporting and fleet performance benchmarking.']
  payload_avg_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average payload per load cycle in dry metric tonnes during the period. Used to assess truck loading efficiency against rated payload capacity. Sourced from MineStar payload monitoring system (VIMS/PLM).']
  performance_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The ratio of actual production speed to ideal production speed during operating time, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8750 = 87.50%). Second component of the OEE composite metric. Calculated as (ideal_cycle_time_sec × total_units_produced) / actual_operating_time_hrs. Stored as raw rate.']
  period_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time marking the end of the production period for which OEE is calculated. Combined with period_start_timestamp to derive total period duration.']
  period_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time marking the beginning of the production period (shift or sub-shift interval) for which OEE is calculated. Principal business event timestamp for this record. Sourced from MineStar shift dispatch or OSIsoft PI historian.']
  planned_downtime_hrs decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total hours the asset was taken out of service for scheduled preventive maintenance, inspections, or planned shutdowns during the period. Excluded from planned operating time in OEE Availability calculation.']
  planned_operating_time_hrs decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total scheduled hours the asset was planned to be in productive operation during the period, excluding planned maintenance windows and scheduled downtime. Core input to OEE Availability Rate calculation. Sourced from MineStar shift schedule.']
  production_date date [note: 'Calendar date associated with this OEE record, representing the production day. Used for daily and monthly OEE trend reporting and OPEX period alignment.']
  quality_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The ratio of good units produced to total units produced, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9900 = 99.00%). Third component of the OEE composite metric. Calculated as (total_units_produced - reject_unit_count) / total_units_produced. For mobile fleet assets where quality is not applicable, this defaults to 1.0000.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this OEE record was formally reconciled and approved in the production reconciliation system. Null if is_reconciled is false.']
  record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this OEE record, formatted as OEE-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN. Used for cross-system referencing between MineStar, OSIsoft PI, and SAP PM reporting.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this OEE record within the production reconciliation workflow. Draft records are provisional; approved records are used for OPEX reporting and fleet benchmarking.']
  reject_unit_count varchar [note: 'Number of production units that did not meet quality specification during the period (e.g., crusher throughput below grade spec, off-spec concentrate batches). Core input to OEE Quality Rate calculation. Applicable primarily to fixed plant assets; set to zero for mobile fleet where quality is not directly measured.']
  speed_avg_kmh decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average travel speed of the mobile asset during the period, in km/h. Used for haul road performance analysis, speed compliance monitoring, and Performance Rate benchmarking. Sourced from MineStar telematics.']
  throughput_actual_t decimal(14,4) [note: 'Actual mass of material (ore or waste) processed or moved by the asset during the period, measured in dry metric tonnes. Primary quantitative production outcome for the period. Sourced from MineStar payload monitoring or OSIsoft PI belt scale historian.']
  throughput_target_t decimal(14,4) [note: 'Planned or budgeted throughput in dry metric tonnes for this asset during the period. Used for production variance analysis and OEE Performance Rate benchmarking against mine plan.']
  total_units_produced varchar [note: 'Total count of production units (e.g., haul cycles completed, tonnes crushed, loads dispatched) achieved during the period. Used in Performance Rate and Quality Rate calculations. Sourced from MineStar dispatch or OSIsoft PI throughput counters.']
  unplanned_downtime_hrs decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total hours the asset was unavailable due to unplanned breakdowns, mechanical failures, or unscheduled stoppages during the period. Key driver of Availability Rate degradation. Sourced from Infor EAM work order records.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this OEE record was most recently modified, including reconciliation updates, corrections, or status changes. Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring in the Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Periodic OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) composite metric records capturing the multiplicative product of availability rate, performance rate, and quality rate for each asset per shift or production period. Distinct from utilisation_record which tracks raw time-based utilisation only. Includes planned operating time, actual operating time, ideal cycle time, total pieces produced, and reject count where applicable (e.g., crusher throughput vs spec). OEE percentage = Availability × Performance × Quality. Sourced from MineStar, OSIsoft PI, and production reconciliation systems. Used for fleet performance benchmarking, bottleneck identification, and OPEX reporting against budget.'
}

Table equipment.downtime_event {
  downtime_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each equipment downtime event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific piece of mining fleet or fixed plant equipment (e.g., haul truck, drill, shovel, conveyor) that experienced the downtime event. Sourced from Infor EAM asset register and Caterpillar MineStar.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Equipment downtime during commissioning and ramp-up phases impacts project schedules and handover readiness. Business process: commissioning performance tracking, defect management, and project milest']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.corrective_action. Business justification: Equipment downtime events (especially safety-related failures) trigger corrective actions to prevent recurrence and improve reliability. This captures the HSE continuous improvement cycle. While work_']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Downtime events have direct cost implications (lost production, maintenance costs) that must be allocated to cost centres for variance analysis and cost performance reporting in mining operations.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shift crew or maintenance team responsible for managing or responding to this downtime event. Used for crew-level performance analysis and accountability tracking.']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated HSE incident record in IsoMetrix when is_safety_related is True. Links the downtime event to the formal safety investigation and regulatory reporting workflow. Null when no safety incident is associated.']
  primary_downtime_reported_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (operator, supervisor, or maintenance technician) who initially reported or logged the downtime event. Supports accountability tracking and communication workflows.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Unplanned downtime repairs often trigger emergency parts procurement; linking downtime events to purchase orders enables cost tracking, procurement response time analysis, and maintenance cost allocat']
  related_downtime_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on downtime_event (related_downtime_event_id)']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which the downtime event commenced. Used to analyse downtime distribution across day, afternoon, and night shifts and to support crew performance reporting.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or operational location where the downtime event occurred (e.g., open pit, underground, processing plant).']
  tertiary_downtime_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor or maintenance superintendent who approved the closure of this downtime event and the return of the equipment to service. Supports governance, audit trail, and regulatory compliance requirements.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Infor EAM maintenance work order raised in response to or associated with this downtime event. Null for non-maintenance downtime categories such as weather or standby.']
  actual_repair_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual number of labour hours expended to complete the repair or maintenance activity for this downtime event. Recorded upon event closure. Used to calculate Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) and to benchmark against estimated_repair_hours for continuous improvement.']
  component_code varchar [note: 'Standardised code identifying the specific equipment component or sub-assembly that failed or required attention (e.g., engine assembly, hydraulic pump, front suspension, conveyor idler, crusher liner). Aligned with the equipment Bill of Materials (BOM) in SAP S/4HANA MM and Infor EAM component hierarchy.']
  component_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the specific equipment component or sub-assembly that failed or was serviced during this downtime event. Complements component_code with a plain-language label for reporting and communication purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this downtime event record was first created in the source system (Infor EAM or Caterpillar MineStar) and ingested into the silver layer. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance monitoring.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the maintenance_cost field (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Supports multi-currency operations across international mine sites.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the downtime event was first detected. Operator_report indicates the equipment operator raised the fault. SCADA_alarm indicates detection via OSIsoft PI System or PLC alarm. FMS_alert indicates detection via Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management System. Condition_monitoring indicates detection via predictive maintenance sensors. Scheduled_inspection indicates detection during a planned inspection round. Third_party_report indicates detection reported by a contractor or external party.']
  downtime_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the downtime event type. Unplanned breakdown indicates an unexpected mechanical or electrical failure. Planned maintenance covers scheduled preventive or corrective maintenance windows. Standby covers equipment waiting for work assignment. Weather delay covers stoppages due to adverse environmental conditions. Operational delay covers delays caused by operational constraints such as blasting exclusion zones. Process delay covers upstream or downstream process bottlenecks.']
  downtime_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardised reason code identifying the specific cause of the downtime event (e.g., engine failure, tyre change, hydraulic leak, conveyor belt tear, electrical fault). Sourced from Infor EAM failure code library and Caterpillar MineStar reason code catalogue. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engine_failure|tyre_change|hydraulic_leak|conveyor_belt_tear|electrical_fault|structural_damage|operator_error|fuel_system|cooling_system|brake_system — promote to reference product]']
  downtime_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the downtime event record. Open indicates the event has been logged but resolution has not commenced. In_progress indicates maintenance or corrective action is underway. Resolved indicates the equipment has been returned to service. Cancelled indicates the event was logged in error. Pending_approval indicates the event record is awaiting supervisory sign-off.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed duration of the downtime event expressed in whole minutes, calculated as the difference between end_timestamp and start_timestamp. Stored as a business field to support direct reporting and MTTR calculations without requiring timestamp arithmetic at query time. Null if end_timestamp is not yet recorded.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the equipment was returned to operational service and the downtime event concluded. Null if the event is still open or in progress. Used in conjunction with start_timestamp to calculate actual downtime duration.']
  environmental_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this downtime event resulted in or had the potential to cause an environmental impact (e.g., hydraulic fluid spill, dust emission exceedance, tailings pump failure). True triggers environmental notification and reporting obligations under ISO 14001 and site Environmental Impact Statement (EIS) conditions.']
  estimated_repair_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated number of labour hours required to complete the repair or maintenance activity associated with this downtime event, as assessed at the time of event creation. Used for maintenance scheduling, resource planning, and comparison against actual repair duration to improve future estimates.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the downtime event as recorded by the operator or maintenance technician at the time of reporting. Captures contextual details not covered by structured fields, including observed symptoms, environmental conditions, and initial diagnosis. Sourced from Infor EAM notification text or Caterpillar MineStar operator notes.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the downtime event, formatted as DT-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used for cross-referencing with Infor EAM work orders, MineStar dispatch logs, and SAP PM notifications. Supports traceability across operational systems.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Describes the specific manner in which the equipment failed or the mechanism of the downtime (e.g., fatigue fracture, corrosion, overheating, wear, contamination, misalignment). Aligned with ISO 14224 failure mode taxonomy and used in Reliability Centred Maintenance (RCM) analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fatigue_fracture|corrosion|overheating|wear|contamination|misalignment|vibration|leakage|blockage|electrical_short — promote to reference product]']
  functional_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA PM or Infor EAM functional location code identifying the specific physical location or system hierarchy node where the equipment was positioned at the time of the downtime event (e.g., pit bench, crusher circuit, conveyor section). Supports spatial analysis of downtime patterns.']
  is_safety_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this downtime event was caused by or resulted in a safety incident, near-miss, or equipment condition that posed a risk to personnel. True triggers mandatory notification to the Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) team and may require IsoMetrix incident reporting. Supports Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate (LTIFR) and Total Recordable Injury Frequency Rate (TRIFR) compliance reporting.']
  maintenance_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total direct cost incurred to resolve this downtime event, including labour, parts, and consumables as captured in Infor EAM and SAP S/4HANA CO. Expressed in the site operating currency. Used for Operating Expenditure (OPEX) tracking, cost-per-hour analysis, and maintenance budget variance reporting.']
  maintenance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance activity associated with this downtime event. Corrective maintenance addresses failures after they occur. Preventive maintenance is time-based scheduled work. Predictive maintenance is triggered by condition monitoring data. Condition-based maintenance is triggered by measured parameter thresholds. Emergency maintenance addresses safety-critical failures requiring immediate response. Null for non-maintenance downtime categories.']
  oee_impact_category varchar [note: 'Classification of how this downtime event impacts the Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) calculation. Availability losses arise from unplanned breakdowns and planned maintenance. Performance losses arise from reduced speed or throughput. Quality losses arise from off-spec production. Used to decompose OEE into its three constituent factors for targeted improvement initiatives.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification assigned to the downtime event to drive maintenance resource allocation and response urgency. Critical indicates immediate safety or production-stopping impact. High indicates significant production impact requiring same-shift response. Medium indicates moderate impact with next-shift response acceptable. Low indicates minor impact with scheduled response acceptable.']
  production_impact_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual number of production hours lost as a direct consequence of this downtime event. Accounts for the impact on the production schedule and is used to quantify the effect on Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) and Life of Mine (LOM) plan adherence. Sourced from Caterpillar MineStar productivity tracking.']
  production_tonnes_lost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Estimated tonnage of ore or material (Run of Mine — ROM) that was not moved or processed as a direct result of this downtime event. Used to quantify the financial and operational impact of equipment unavailability on the mine production schedule.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the corrective actions taken to resolve the downtime event and return the equipment to service. Recorded by the maintenance technician upon event closure. Supports knowledge management, recurring fault analysis, and Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) improvement.']
  return_to_service_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the equipment was formally cleared and returned to operational service following resolution of the downtime event. May differ from end_timestamp if post-repair inspection or commissioning steps are required before the equipment is released to operations.']
  root_cause_analysis_status varchar [note: 'Status of the formal Root Cause Analysis (RCA) investigation for this downtime event. Not_required for minor or routine events. Pending indicates RCA has been triggered but not yet commenced. In_progress indicates investigation is underway. Completed indicates findings have been documented and corrective actions assigned. Mandatory for critical and high-priority events per site maintenance governance standards.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift during which the downtime event commenced (day, afternoon, or night). Stored directly on the event to support shift-level reporting without requiring a join to the shift master when shift_id is unavailable.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the equipment became unavailable or the downtime event commenced. This is the principal real-world event time used for Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) and Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculations. Sourced from Caterpillar MineStar or OSIsoft PI System historian.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this downtime event record was most recently modified in the source system or silver layer. Used for change data capture, incremental load processing, and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Records each individual equipment downtime event including unplanned breakdowns, planned maintenance windows, standby, and weather delays. Captures start and end timestamps, downtime category, downtime reason code, responsible crew, and impact on production. Used to calculate MTBF and MTTR and to drive maintenance prioritisation.'
}

Table equipment.telemetry_event {
  telemetry_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for telemetry_event']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master equipment or fleet asset record that generated this telemetry event. Links to the equipment master register in the Equipment domain.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the workforce record of the operator assigned to and operating the equipment at the time of this telemetry event. Used for operator behaviour analysis.']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this telemetry event was recorded. Enables shift-level aggregation of equipment performance and utilisation metrics.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or operational location where the equipment was operating when this telemetry event was captured.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geofence zone in which the equipment was located at the time of the telemetry event. Used for geofence compliance monitoring, blast exclusion zone enforcement, and haulage route adherence.']
  ambient_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Ambient air temperature in degrees Celsius recorded by the equipment\'s external temperature sensor at the time of the telemetry event. Used for equipment performance normalisation and heat stress risk assessment under ISO 45001.']
  battery_voltage_v decimal(6,2) [note: 'Onboard electrical system battery voltage in volts at the time of the telemetry event. Low voltage readings indicate potential electrical system faults and are used to trigger preventive maintenance actions in Infor EAM.']
  brake_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service brake was applied at the time of the telemetry event. Used for operator behaviour analysis, brake wear monitoring, and safety compliance reporting under ISO 45001.']
  coolant_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Engine coolant temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of the telemetry event. A critical health indicator; values exceeding thresholds trigger predictive maintenance alerts and potential equipment shutdown.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality assessment flag applied during silver layer processing to indicate the reliability of this telemetry record. Suspect or invalid records may result from sensor malfunction, communication dropout, or out-of-range values. Used to filter records in analytics and predictive maintenance models.']
  elevation_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Altitude of the equipment above mean sea level in metres at the time of the telemetry event. Used for bench-level positioning, grade resistance calculations, and mine design alignment.']
  engine_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative engine operating hours recorded on the equipment odometer at the time of this telemetry event. Primary driver for preventive maintenance scheduling and Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) calculations in Infor EAM.']
  engine_load_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Engine load as a percentage of maximum rated capacity at the time of the telemetry event. Indicates whether the equipment is operating within design parameters. Used for OEE and fuel consumption analysis.']
  engine_rpm varchar [note: 'Instantaneous engine speed in revolutions per minute at the time of the telemetry event. Used for engine load analysis, fuel efficiency monitoring, and predictive maintenance trigger detection.']
  equipment_status varchar [note: 'The operational state of the equipment at the moment of this telemetry event as reported by the Fleet Management System (FMS). Supports Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) and utilisation calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operating|idle|standby|down|tramming|loading|dumping — promote to reference product]']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (UTC) at which the sensor snapshot or event trigger was recorded by the onboard telematics system. Sub-minute frequency. This is the principal real-world event time, distinct from ingestion audit timestamps.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator classifying the nature of the telemetry event. Periodic snapshots are time-driven; threshold breach, engine, geofence, payload, and fault code events are trigger-driven. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: periodic_snapshot|threshold_breach|engine_event|geofence_event|payload_event|fault_code_event — promote to reference product]']
  fault_code varchar [note: 'Diagnostic fault or alarm code generated by the equipment\'s onboard diagnostic system (OBD/J1939) at the time of the event. Null for periodic snapshots with no active fault. Used to trigger work orders in Infor EAM and predictive maintenance workflows.']
  fault_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the fault code raised at the time of this telemetry event. Critical and high severity faults may trigger immediate equipment shutdown or dispatch of maintenance crew. Null when no fault is active.']
  fuel_consumption_rate_lph decimal(8,3) [note: 'Instantaneous fuel burn rate in litres per hour as reported by the onboard fuel flow sensor at the time of the telemetry event. Used for OPEX (Operating Expenditure) monitoring and All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) calculations.']
  fuel_level_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current fuel tank level expressed as a percentage of total tank capacity at the time of the telemetry event. Used for fuel management, dispatch planning, and refuelling schedule optimisation. Distinct from discrete fuel dispensing events recorded in fuel_consumption.']
  geofence_breach boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment was in breach of an active geofence boundary at the time of this telemetry event. True when the equipment has entered a restricted or exclusion zone without authorisation. Supports safety compliance and HSE reporting.']
  heading_degrees decimal(6,2) [note: 'Compass bearing of the equipment direction of travel in degrees (0–360) at the time of the telemetry event. Used for directional analysis and geofence zone entry/exit detection.']
  hydraulic_pressure_kpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hydraulic system pressure in kilopascals at the time of the telemetry event. Used for monitoring hydraulic system health on shovels, drills, and haul trucks. Anomalies indicate potential hydraulic failure.']
  ingested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this telemetry record was received and loaded into the lakehouse silver layer from the source system (MineStar, Wenco, Modular Mining, or OSIsoft PI). Used for data latency monitoring and pipeline audit.']
  latitude double [note: 'WGS84 decimal degrees latitude of the equipment position at the time of the telemetry event. Sourced from onboard GPS unit. Used for geofence compliance, haulage route analysis, and GIS integration.']
  longitude double [note: 'WGS84 decimal degrees longitude of the equipment position at the time of the telemetry event. Sourced from onboard GPS unit. Used for geofence compliance, haulage route analysis, and GIS integration.']
  odometer_km decimal(12,3) [note: 'Cumulative distance travelled by the equipment in kilometres as recorded on the onboard odometer at the time of the telemetry event. Used for tyre life management, component replacement scheduling, and haulage distance reporting.']
  payload_weight_t decimal(10,3) [note: 'Instantaneous payload weight in metric tonnes as measured by the onboard payload monitoring system at the time of the telemetry event. Used for haulage productivity analysis, truck factor compliance, and ROM (Run of Mine) tonnage tracking.']
  signal_quality varchar [note: 'Quality classification of the wireless or satellite communication signal at the time the telemetry event was transmitted. Used to assess data reliability and identify underground or remote areas with poor connectivity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating operational system or IoT integration platform that produced this telemetry event record. Supports data lineage and multi-source reconciliation across MineStar, Wenco, Modular Mining, and OSIsoft PI.']
  speed_kmh decimal(6,2) [note: 'Instantaneous travel speed of the equipment in kilometres per hour at the time of the telemetry event. Used for speed limit compliance monitoring, operator behaviour analysis, and haulage cycle time analysis.']
  transmission_gear varchar [note: 'Current transmission gear position of the equipment at the time of the telemetry event. Used for operator behaviour analysis, ramp grade compliance, and drivetrain health monitoring.']
  tyre_pressure_front_left_kpa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Tyre pressure of the front-left tyre in kilopascals at the time of the telemetry event. Tyre pressure monitoring is critical for haul truck safety and tyre life management on large mining vehicles.']
  tyre_pressure_front_right_kpa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Tyre pressure of the front-right tyre in kilopascals at the time of the telemetry event. Tyre pressure monitoring is critical for haul truck safety and tyre life management on large mining vehicles.']
  tyre_pressure_rear_left_kpa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Tyre pressure of the rear-left tyre in kilopascals at the time of the telemetry event. Rear tyres on haul trucks carry the majority of loaded payload weight and are subject to higher wear and failure risk.']
  tyre_pressure_rear_right_kpa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Tyre pressure of the rear-right tyre in kilopascals at the time of the telemetry event. Rear tyres on haul trucks carry the majority of loaded payload weight and are subject to higher wear and failure risk.']

  Note: 'High-frequency telemetry and sensor readings from onboard equipment systems including GPS position, engine hours, speed, payload weight, tyre pressure, hydraulic pressure, fuel level, coolant temperature, and engine RPM. Each record represents a single sensor snapshot or event trigger at sub-minute frequency. Sourced from MineStar, Wenco, Modular Mining, and OSIsoft PI System via SCADA/IoT integration. Supports real-time equipment health monitoring, geofence compliance, predictive maintenance trigger detection, and operator behaviour analysis. Distinct from fuel_consumption which records discrete dispensing events at much lower frequency.'
}

Table equipment.fuel_consumption {
  fuel_consumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each fuel dispensing event or shift-end fuel measurement record in the silver layer lakehouse.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP cost centre to which the fuel cost is allocated. Supports OPEX cost allocation to fleet and C1 cash cost per tonne reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment operator or driver who was assigned to the asset at the time of the fuelling event. Used for operator-level fuel efficiency analysis and accountability.']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Fuel spills during dispensing are reportable environmental incidents. Fuel transactions involved in spills, overfills, or contamination events must link to the incident record for regulatory reporting']
  primary_fuel_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining fleet or fixed plant asset (haul truck, drill, shovel, dozer, etc.) that consumed the fuel. Links to the equipment master register in the equipment domain.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Fuel transactions reference bulk fuel purchase orders (contracts with fuel suppliers); linking enables reconciliation of dispensed fuel against contracted supply, invoice verification, and fuel cost a']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which the fuelling event occurred. Enables shift-level fuel cost allocation and OPEX reporting.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Fuel consumed during capital development projects (mine construction, infrastructure) is capitalized and tracked against WBS elements for project cost control and capitalization accounting.']
  anomaly_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the reason the transaction was flagged as an anomaly (e.g., \'Quantity exceeds tank capacity\', \'Location outside mine boundary\', \'Duplicate transaction within 30 minutes\'). Populated only when is_anomaly_flagged is True.']
  authorised_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the supervisor or fuel controller who authorised the dispensing event. Required for fuel theft detection controls and audit compliance.']
  co2e_emissions_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Calculated Scope 1 greenhouse gas emissions in kilograms of CO2 equivalent for this fuel consumption event, derived by applying the applicable emissions factor for the fuel type to the quantity dispensed. Used for GRI and ICMM carbon reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fuel consumption record was first created in the silver layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring of data pipeline latency.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fuel cost amounts (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Required for multi-jurisdiction mining operations where fuel is procured in different currencies.']
  dispensing_location_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location where fuel was dispensed (fuel bay, ROM pad field refuelling point, tank farm). Links to the site location master.']
  dispensing_method varchar [note: 'Physical method by which fuel was delivered to the asset: fixed_bay (permanent fuel bay infrastructure), bowser (mobile tank truck field refuelling), drum (manual drum dispensing for remote equipment), or pipeline (direct pipeline connection at processing plant).']
  emissions_factor_kg_per_litre decimal(10,6) [note: 'The greenhouse gas emissions factor applied to this transaction, in kg CO2e per litre, specific to the fuel type. Captured at transaction time to preserve the factor version used for regulatory reporting and audit purposes.']
  engine_hours_at_fill decimal(12,1) [note: 'Cumulative engine hours recorded on the asset at the time of the fuelling event. Used to calculate fuel consumption per engine hour, track MTBF intervals, and schedule preventive maintenance in Infor EAM.']
  fuel_burn_rate_l_per_hr decimal(10,3) [note: 'Instantaneous or average fuel consumption rate in litres per engine hour at the time of the fuelling event or measurement period. Sourced from MineStar telematics. Used for OEE analysis and fleet fuel efficiency benchmarking.']
  fuel_docket_number varchar [note: 'Physical or electronic docket/receipt number issued at the point of dispensing. Used for three-way reconciliation between FMS records, physical dockets, and SAP purchase orders.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Type of fuel dispensed: diesel (standard ultra-low sulphur diesel), LNG (liquefied natural gas for LNG-powered haul trucks), biodiesel B20 blend (20% biodiesel), biodiesel B100 (pure biodiesel), or HVO (hydrotreated vegetable oil). Drives emissions factor selection for Scope 1 carbon accounting.']
  gps_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'WGS84 decimal latitude of the fuelling event location as recorded by MineStar telematics or the FMS. Used for GIS-based fuel consumption heat mapping and field refuelling route optimisation.']
  gps_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'WGS84 decimal longitude of the fuelling event location as recorded by MineStar telematics or the FMS. Used in conjunction with gps_latitude for spatial analysis of fuel consumption patterns.']
  is_anomaly_flagged boolean [note: 'Boolean flag set to True when the fuel transaction has been identified as a potential anomaly by automated detection rules (e.g., quantity dispensed exceeds tank capacity, fill rate outside normal range, location mismatch). Triggers investigation workflow for fuel theft detection.']
  mine_site_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the mine site where the fuelling event occurred (e.g., PILBARA-01, HUNTER-VALLEY-02). Supports multi-site OPEX reporting and C1 cash cost benchmarking across operations.']
  odometer_km_at_fill decimal(12,1) [note: 'Cumulative distance travelled in kilometres recorded on the asset at the time of the fuelling event. Applicable to mobile fleet (haul trucks, light vehicles). Null for fixed plant or tracked equipment without odometers.']
  quantity_dispensed_litres decimal(12,3) [note: 'Volume of fuel dispensed during this transaction, measured in litres to three decimal places. The primary quantitative fact for OPEX cost allocation, C1 cash cost per tonne calculation, and Scope 1 emissions accounting.']
  source_record_code varchar [note: 'The native primary key or transaction identifier from the originating source system (MineStar, FMS, PI System, or Infor EAM). Retained for data lineage, deduplication, and reconciliation back to the system of record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this fuel consumption record was ingested: MineStar Fleet Management, a standalone Fuel Management System (FMS), OSIsoft PI System SCADA, Infor EAM, or manual entry. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  tank_capacity_litres decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum rated fuel tank capacity of the asset in litres. Used to calculate fill percentage and validate that dispensed quantity does not exceed physical tank capacity.']
  tank_level_after_litres decimal(10,3) [note: 'Fuel level in the asset\'s onboard tank immediately after the dispensing event, in litres. Used with tank_level_before_litres to validate dispensed quantity and detect discrepancies indicative of fuel theft.']
  tank_level_before_litres decimal(10,3) [note: 'Fuel level in the asset\'s onboard tank immediately before the dispensing event, in litres. Used with tank_level_after_litres to validate dispensed quantity and detect fuel theft or leakage anomalies.']
  total_fuel_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost of fuel dispensed in this transaction (quantity_dispensed_litres × unit_cost_per_litre), in the site operating currency. Used for OPEX cost allocation to fleet and C1 cash cost per tonne reporting. Note: this is a transaction-level cost capture, not a derived aggregate.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned by the Fuel Management System (FMS) or MineStar to identify this dispensing event or measurement. Used for reconciliation with source systems and audit trails.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the fuel transaction record. Confirmed transactions are included in OPEX and emissions reporting. Voided or disputed records are excluded from cost allocation until resolved.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the fuel dispensing event commenced or the shift-end measurement was recorded. This is the principal real-world event time used for time-series analysis and Scope 1 emissions accounting.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the fuel record: a discrete refuelling event at a fuel bay (refuel), a periodic shift-end fuel measurement (shift_measurement), a manual tank dip reading (tank_dip), a field refuelling by bowser (field_refuel), or a bulk fuel transfer between storage tanks (bulk_transfer).']
  unit_cost_per_litre decimal(10,4) [note: 'Contracted or spot price paid per litre of fuel at the time of the transaction, in the site operating currency. Confidential commercial pricing data. Used for OPEX cost allocation and C1 cash cost per tonne calculation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this fuel consumption record in the silver layer. Tracks corrections, status changes, and anomaly flag updates for audit compliance.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Infor EAM work order number to which this fuel consumption is charged, if the fuelling is associated with a specific maintenance or operational work order. Enables work-order-level OPEX tracking.']

  Note: 'Records discrete fuel dispensing events and periodic fuel usage measurements per asset. Each record represents a single refuelling transaction or shift-end fuel measurement. Captures fuel type (diesel, LNG, biodiesel blend), quantity dispensed in litres, fuel burn rate (L/hr), odometer or engine hours at fill, dispensing location (fuel bay, field refuelling), tank truck ID, and operator. Sourced from MineStar, Fuel Management Systems (FMS), and tank farm SCADA. Used for OPEX cost allocation to fleet, C1 cash cost per tonne reporting, carbon emissions accounting (Scope 1), and fuel theft detection.'
}

Table equipment.utilisation_record {
  utilisation_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each shift-level or daily utilisation record. Primary key for the utilisation_record data product in the equipment domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining fleet or fixed plant asset (e.g., haul truck, drill, shovel, conveyor) for which this utilisation record applies. Sourced from Infor EAM asset register.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Equipment utilization hours drive cost allocation to cost centres for mining cost accounting, unit cost calculation, and AISC reporting. Essential for cost per tonne analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary equipment operator assigned to the asset during this shift or utilisation period. Sourced from MineStar operator assignment records.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Equipment utilisation is tracked per product campaign in multi-product mining operations. Asset performance reporting and production planning require product-segmented utilisation analysis to optimize']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this utilisation record was captured. Links to the shift master for crew, date, and shift-type context.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or operational location where the asset was deployed during this utilisation period.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the shift supervisor or production controller who approved this utilisation record after reconciliation. Provides accountability for official production reporting.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the utilisation record was formally approved by the shift supervisor or production controller. Marks the record as authoritative for inclusion in production and fleet performance reports.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for shift supervisor or production controller notes explaining anomalies, reconciliation adjustments, or contextual information about the utilisation record (e.g., blast delay reason, equipment swap details).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this utilisation record was first created in the source system (MineStar FMS or manual entry). Audit trail field for data lineage and reconciliation tracking.']
  cycle_count varchar [note: 'Number of complete operational cycles (e.g., load-haul-dump cycles for trucks, drill-hole completions for drills) performed by the asset during the period. Used for productivity rate analysis and equipment wear modelling.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System or method from which this utilisation record was sourced (e.g., MineStar FMS automated telemetry, manual shift report entry, SCADA, OSIsoft PI System). Supports data lineage and quality assessment.']
  delay_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hours lost to operational delays not attributable to maintenance (e.g., blast delays, traffic management, shift change, fuelling, operator fatigue breaks). Captured separately to support delay category analysis.']
  distance_travelled_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total distance travelled by the asset during the utilisation period in kilometres, sourced from MineStar GPS telematics. Used for tyre life management, road condition analysis, and haulage productivity benchmarking.']
  downtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total hours the asset was unavailable for production due to planned or unplanned maintenance, breakdowns, or inspections. Used in MTBF and MTTR calculations and physical availability reporting.']
  engine_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total engine-on hours recorded by the asset\'s onboard telematics during the period. Used for maintenance scheduling, component life tracking, and MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) calculations in Infor EAM.']
  fuel_burn_rate_lph decimal(8,3) [note: 'Average fuel consumption rate in litres per operating hour during the period. Benchmarked against OEM specifications to identify engine inefficiency, overloading, or operator behaviour issues.']
  fuel_consumed_litres decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total volume of diesel or other fuel consumed by the asset during the utilisation period, measured in litres. Used for OPEX (Operating Expenditure) tracking, fuel burn rate benchmarking, and emissions reporting.']
  idle_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hours the asset was powered on and available but not performing productive work (e.g., waiting for trucks, queuing at crusher). Key metric for identifying operational inefficiencies and fleet sizing opportunities.']
  is_reconciled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this utilisation record has been reconciled against the corresponding shift report. Unreconciled records are flagged for review before inclusion in official production reporting.']
  oee_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) score for the asset during the period, expressed as a percentage. Combines availability, performance, and quality dimensions. Sourced from MineStar or calculated during silver layer processing.']
  operating_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual hours the asset was actively engaged in productive work (e.g., hauling, drilling, loading) during the period. Core numerator for utilisation percentage calculation.']
  operating_zone varchar [note: 'Mine area or operational zone where the asset was primarily deployed during the period (e.g., pit bench level, ROM pad, processing plant, waste dump). Supports spatial productivity analysis and fleet allocation optimisation.']
  payload_tonnes decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total material payload moved by the asset during the utilisation period in dry metric tonnes. Applicable to haul trucks and loaders. Key productivity metric for ROM (Run of Mine) material movement tracking.']
  period_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time marking the end of the utilisation measurement period (shift end or day end). Used with period_start_timestamp to validate that hours components sum correctly.']
  period_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time marking the beginning of the utilisation measurement period (shift start or day start). Conforms to ISO 8601 with timezone offset for multi-timezone mine operations.']
  physical_availability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of scheduled hours during which the asset was mechanically available (not in maintenance downtime). Calculated as ((scheduled_hours - downtime_hours) / scheduled_hours) * 100. Distinct from utilisation — measures mechanical readiness.']
  planned_maintenance_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hours the asset was unavailable due to scheduled preventive maintenance activities. Subset of downtime_hours. Used to distinguish planned versus unplanned downtime for maintenance strategy analysis.']
  primary_delay_category varchar [note: 'The dominant category of delay or downtime that most impacted utilisation during the period (e.g., blast exclusion, fuelling, shift change, mechanical breakdown, weather, traffic). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blast_exclusion|fuelling|shift_change|mechanical_breakdown|weather|traffic|operator_fatigue|waiting_for_instructions|road_conditions|other — promote to reference product]']
  reconciliation_variance_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Difference in total hours between the MineStar FMS automated record and the manually submitted shift report. Non-zero values trigger investigation. Supports data quality management and audit compliance.']
  record_date date [note: 'Calendar date for which this utilisation record applies. Used for daily aggregation, trend analysis, and LOM (Life of Mine) planning.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Workflow status of the utilisation record indicating its data quality and approval state. Records progress from draft through reconciliation against shift reports to approved status.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the utilisation record indicating whether it represents a single shift, a daily roll-up, or a weekly summary. Determines the time horizon of the hours captured.']
  scheduled_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total hours the asset was scheduled to be available for production during the shift or day, as defined in the mine production schedule. Denominator for physical availability calculations.']
  shift_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the paper or digital shift report against which this utilisation record was reconciled. Provides audit traceability between MineStar FMS data and manual shift supervisor records.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift during which utilisation was recorded (e.g., day, night, afternoon, rotating). Supports shift-pattern analysis and crew productivity benchmarking.']
  standby_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hours the asset was available and ready for deployment but not assigned to a task (e.g., awaiting operator, weather hold, blast exclusion zone). Distinguishes planned non-productive time from idle time.']
  unplanned_maintenance_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hours the asset was unavailable due to unexpected breakdowns or corrective maintenance. Subset of downtime_hours. High values indicate reliability issues and drive MTBF and MTTR analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this utilisation record was last modified, including reconciliation adjustments or corrections. Supports change tracking and silver layer incremental processing.']
  use_of_availability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of operating hours to available hours (scheduled minus downtime), expressed as a percentage. Measures how effectively the operations team uses the mechanically available asset. Complements physical availability to form the OEE framework.']
  utilisation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of operating hours to scheduled hours expressed as a percentage. Primary KPI for fleet productivity and LOM (Life of Mine) planning. Calculated as (operating_hours / scheduled_hours) * 100.']

  Note: 'Shift-level or daily utilisation records per asset capturing scheduled hours, operating hours, idle hours, standby hours, and utilisation percentage. Distinguishes physical availability from utilisation to support fleet sizing decisions and LOM planning. Sourced from MineStar FMS and reconciled against shift reports.'
}

Table equipment.payload_cycle {
  payload_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual truck payload cycle record (load-haul-dump event) as captured by Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Haulage cycles generate costs allocated to cost centres based on material type (ore, waste) and destination for mining cost accounting and cost per tonne reporting.']
  dump_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination location where the truck dumped its load during this cycle (e.g., ROM pad, crusher, waste dump, stockpile). Links to the mine operations location master.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment operator (employee) who performed this payload cycle, linking to the workforce domain for operator performance analysis and compliance reporting.']
  load_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the spatial location (bench, face, or dig block) where the truck was loaded during this cycle, linking to the mine operations location or mining block master.']
  primary_payload_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the haul truck or loader asset that performed this payload cycle, linking to the equipment master register in the equipment domain.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Each haul cycle transports a specific saleable product. Production reconciliation, grade control, and stockpile accounting require product-level cycle tracking. Material_type and commodity are denorma']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this payload cycle was executed, enabling shift-level productivity aggregation and ROM tonnage reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payload cycle record was first created in the data platform, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cycle_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the payload cycle was completed, typically recorded as the moment the truck completed the dump and cleared the dump point in MineStar.']
  cycle_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned by MineStar Payload Management to uniquely identify this load-haul-dump cycle for cross-system reconciliation and reporting.']
  cycle_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the payload cycle commenced, typically recorded as the moment the truck arrived at the load point or the loading sequence began in MineStar.']
  cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payload cycle record. Completed indicates a full load-haul-dump sequence was executed. Partial indicates the cycle was interrupted. Aborted indicates the cycle was terminated before dump. Cancelled indicates the cycle was voided.']
  cycle_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time for the complete load-haul-dump cycle in minutes, from arrival at the load point to completion of the dump. Sourced from MineStar cycle time tracking. Key input for haulage productivity analysis and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the data quality status of this payload cycle record as assessed during ingestion into the Silver layer. Valid indicates the record passed all quality checks. Suspect indicates anomalies detected (e.g., payload outside expected range). Invalid indicates the record failed quality checks. Corrected indicates the record was manually reviewed and adjusted.']
  dump_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or code of the dump destination (e.g., ROM Pad 1, Primary Crusher, Waste Dump North, Stockpile A). Used for material routing analysis and ROM tonnage reconciliation.']
  dump_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Time in minutes taken to complete the dump operation at the dump point during this cycle, from arrival at the dump to departure. Used to identify dump point congestion and assess dump efficiency.']
  fuel_consumed_litres decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volume of diesel fuel consumed in litres during this payload cycle as recorded by the truck\'s on-board fuel monitoring system integrated with MineStar. Used for fuel cost allocation, OPEX tracking, and carbon emissions reporting.']
  haul_distance_empty_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Distance in metres travelled by the truck empty from the dump point back to the load point during this cycle, as recorded by MineStar GPS/telematics. Used for total cycle distance calculation and fuel consumption analysis.']
  haul_distance_loaded_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Distance in metres travelled by the truck from the load point to the dump point while carrying a full load during this cycle, as recorded by MineStar GPS/telematics. Used for haulage cost modelling and route optimisation.']
  haul_time_loaded_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Time in minutes the truck spent travelling from the load point to the dump point while carrying a full load during this cycle. Used for haulage route analysis and cycle time optimisation.']
  load_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or code of the load point where the truck was loaded (e.g., pit bench designation, dig block identifier, ROM pad zone). Used for spatial haulage analysis and ore source tracking.']
  load_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Time in minutes taken to load the truck at the dig face or load point during this cycle, from first bucket to last bucket. Used to assess loader productivity and number of passes per load.']
  minestar_cycle_code varchar [note: 'The native cycle identifier assigned by Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management system at the source. Retained for traceability and reconciliation back to the operational system of record.']
  number_of_passes varchar [note: 'The number of bucket or dipper passes (swings) made by the loading unit to fill the truck to the target payload during this cycle. Used to assess loader efficiency and optimise pass-matching strategy.']
  operator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the equipment operator who performed this payload cycle as recorded in MineStar at the time of the cycle. Retained for operational reporting and shift reconciliation where the operator ID may not be immediately resolvable.']
  ore_grade_indicator varchar [note: 'The estimated or assigned grade classification of the material hauled in this cycle (e.g., high-grade, medium-grade, low-grade, cut-off-grade) as determined by the grade control model at the time of loading. Used for ore quality tracking and mill feed grade management.']
  overload_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the truck was loaded beyond its rated payload capacity (overloaded) during this cycle as determined by MineStar payload monitoring. True indicates an overload event. Used for truck health monitoring, tyre management, and compliance with OEM load limits.']
  payload_measurement_method varchar [note: 'The method used to measure the payload weight for this cycle. Onboard scale uses the truck\'s integrated weighing system. Strut pressure uses suspension strut pressure sensors. Load cell uses a fixed weighbridge. Estimated indicates a calculated value. Manual indicates a manually entered value. Affects data quality confidence for ROM reconciliation.']
  payload_variance_tonnes decimal(10,3) [note: 'The difference between the actual payload weight and the target payload weight in metric tonnes (actual minus target). Positive values indicate overloading; negative values indicate underloading. Used for truck health monitoring and haulage efficiency analysis. Note: this is a raw recorded field from MineStar, not a derived KPI.']
  payload_weight_tonnes decimal(10,3) [note: 'The measured weight of material loaded onto the truck during this cycle, expressed in metric tonnes as recorded by the on-board payload measurement system (OBMS) in MineStar. This is the principal quantitative fact for the cycle and the primary input to ROM tonnage reconciliation.']
  production_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this payload cycle was executed, used for daily production reporting, ROM tonnage reconciliation against the Life of Mine (LOM) plan, and shift-based aggregation.']
  queue_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Time in minutes the truck spent waiting in queue at the load point before loading commenced during this cycle. Used to identify loader bottlenecks, optimise truck-shovel matching, and reduce non-productive time.']
  return_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Time in minutes the truck spent travelling empty from the dump point back to the load point during this cycle. Used for haulage route analysis and cycle time component breakdown.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the production shift during which this cycle occurred (day, night, or afternoon). Used for shift-comparative productivity analysis and fatigue risk management.']
  target_payload_tonnes decimal(10,3) [note: 'The planned or nominal target payload weight in metric tonnes for this truck model and material type as configured in MineStar. Used to calculate payload variance and assess overloading or underloading against truck rated capacity.']
  truck_fleet_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable fleet identification number assigned to the haul truck or loader (e.g., HT-042, LD-017) as registered in the Fleet Management System (FMS). Used for operational reporting and shift reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payload cycle record was last modified in the data platform, used for change tracking and incremental data processing.']

  Note: 'Individual truck payload cycle records capturing each load-haul-dump cycle for haul trucks and loaders. Includes load location, dump location, payload weight (tonnes), cycle time, queue time, travel time, and operator. Sourced from MineStar Payload Management. Used for haulage productivity analysis and ROM tonnage reconciliation.'
}

Table equipment.drill_activity {
  drill_activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual drill rig activity event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the drill_activity data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drill rig asset record in the equipment master register. Links this activity to the specific drill rig (e.g., rotary blast hole drill, DTH drill) that performed the activity.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Drilling operations target specific commodities (gold, iron ore, coal). Geological reporting, resource estimation, and mine planning require commodity linkage for drill activities. Essential for explo']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Drilling activities (production or development) are costed to specific cost centres for mine cost accounting, metres drilled cost analysis, and AISC calculation.']
  drill_hole_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the individual drill hole associated with this activity, as assigned in the drill pattern or geology system. Used to link drill activity to assay results, blast design, and geological models.']
  drill_pattern_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drill pattern design record that governs the spatial layout, burden, spacing, and sub-drill parameters for this activity. Sourced from Hexagon MinePlan or Deswik drill-and-blast design.']
  mining_block_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining block or bench where this drill activity occurred. Supports grade control integration and production reconciliation against the mine plan.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee record of the drill rig operator who performed this activity. Used for workforce productivity tracking, competency compliance, and shift attribution.']
  preceding_drill_activity_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on drill_activity (preceding_drill_activity_id)']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Drilling operations require activity-specific risk assessments covering ground conditions, blast proximity, overhead services, and geotechnical hazards. Each drill activity must reference its governin']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shift record during which this drill activity was performed. Enables shift-based production reporting and operator attribution.']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Drilling activities occur within specific tenement boundaries and directly contribute to statutory expenditure commitments. Real mining operations must report drilling meterage and costs by tenement f']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Development drilling for mine construction or expansion is capitalized and tracked against capital project WBS elements for project cost control and capitalization accounting.']
  activity_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the drill rig completed the activity event. Used to calculate activity duration and productive drilling time per shift.']
  activity_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the drill activity event, sourced from MineStar or the drill monitoring system. Used for cross-system traceability and drill-and-blast planning references.']
  activity_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the drill rig commenced the activity event (e.g., start of collar setup, start of drilling). Principal real-world event timestamp sourced from MineStar telemetry.']
  activity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the drill activity event. Tracks progression from planned through execution to completion or cancellation. Used for real-time production monitoring and drill-and-blast scheduling.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the drill activity by its operational purpose. Distinguishes blast hole drilling from grade control, geotechnical, exploration, and dewatering activities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blast_hole|pre_split|grade_control|geotechnical|exploration|dewatering — promote to reference product if additional types are required]']
  actual_depth_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'The actual total depth drilled in metres for this activity, as recorded by the drill monitoring system or MineStar. Compared against planned depth to assess drill execution accuracy and identify refusal or over-drill events.']
  air_pressure_kpa decimal(8,2) [note: 'The average air pressure applied during drilling in kilopascals, sourced from drill monitoring sensors or OSIsoft PI historian. Used for DTH and rotary drill performance analysis and equipment health monitoring.']
  bench_level varchar [note: 'The mine bench level designation where this drill activity was performed (e.g., \'BL_1050\', \'RL_980\'). Used for spatial organisation of drill activities within the open pit or underground mine design.']
  bit_serial_number varchar [note: 'The serial number of the specific drill bit used during this activity. Enables bit life tracking, wear analysis, and consumable cost allocation per hole or per shift.']
  bit_type varchar [note: 'The type of drill bit used during this activity. Bit type selection is driven by rock hardness, hole diameter, and drilling method. Impacts penetration rate, bit life, and consumable cost tracking.']
  collar_easting_m decimal(12,3) [note: 'Easting coordinate (X) of the drill hole collar position in metres, referenced to the mine grid coordinate system. Critical for spatial integration with GIS, geological models, and blast design.']
  collar_elevation_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Elevation (Z) of the drill hole collar position in metres above sea level or mine datum. Used for bench level verification, sub-drill calculation, and geological model integration.']
