  collar_northing_m decimal(12,3) [note: 'Northing coordinate (Y) of the drill hole collar position in metres, referenced to the mine grid coordinate system. Used for spatial positioning in geological models and blast pattern verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this drill activity record was first captured in the system of record. Audit trail field for data lineage and silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  deviation_from_plan_m decimal(8,3) [note: 'The measured deviation of the actual drill hole collar position from the planned collar position in metres. Indicates drill execution accuracy and is used for blast design quality assurance and fragmentation outcome prediction.']
  drill_method varchar [note: 'The drilling method employed for this activity. Rotary and DTH (Down-The-Hole) are common for blast hole drilling; RC (Reverse Circulation) and diamond core are used for grade control and exploration. Drives equipment selection and cost classification.']
  fuel_consumed_litres decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total fuel consumed by the drill rig during this activity in litres. Sourced from MineStar fuel management or onboard sensors. Used for OPEX tracking, carbon emissions reporting, and equipment efficiency benchmarking.']
  hole_azimuth_deg decimal(6,2) [note: 'The azimuth bearing of the drill hole in degrees from true north. Used in conjunction with inclination to define the 3D trajectory of the hole for blast design and geological logging.']
  hole_diameter_mm decimal(6,1) [note: 'The diameter of the drill hole in millimetres. Determines explosive charge capacity, blast fragmentation outcomes, and is a key parameter in blast design and drill bit selection.']
  hole_inclination_deg decimal(5,2) [note: 'The inclination angle of the drill hole from vertical in degrees. Angled holes are used in pre-split and controlled blasting designs. Sourced from drill monitoring system or survey.']
  is_sample_collected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a drill chip or core sample was collected during this activity for assay or geological logging. When true, a corresponding sample record should exist in the LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System).']
  metres_drilled decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total metres drilled during this activity event. A primary production quantity metric for drill rig productivity reporting, shift targets, and LOM (Life of Mine) progress tracking.']
  penetration_rate_m_per_hr decimal(8,2) [note: 'The rate at which the drill bit advances through rock, measured in metres per hour. A key productivity metric for drill rig performance benchmarking, bit selection optimisation, and rock hardness inference.']
  pit_name varchar [note: 'The name or code of the open pit, underground stope area, or mine section where this drill activity was performed. Supports spatial aggregation of drill productivity by mine area.']
  planned_depth_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'The designed total depth of the drill hole in metres as specified in the drill pattern or blast design. Used for variance analysis against actual drilled depth and drill-and-blast planning.']
  pulldown_force_kn decimal(8,2) [note: 'The average pulldown (crowd) force applied to the drill bit in kilonewtons during this activity. Influences penetration rate, bit wear, and rock hardness index calculation.']
  refusal_depth_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'The depth in metres at which the drill rig encountered refusal (inability to advance further due to extremely hard rock or obstruction). Null if no refusal occurred. Used for geotechnical assessment and blast design adjustment.']
  rock_hardness_index decimal(6,2) [note: 'A numeric index representing the hardness of the rock encountered during drilling, derived from drill monitoring parameters (e.g., specific energy, penetration rate, rotary torque). Used for geology grade control integration, blast design optimisation, and comminution planning.']
  rock_type varchar [note: 'Geological classification of the dominant rock type encountered during drilling (e.g., fresh rock, transitional, oxide, sulphide, waste, ore). Sourced from geological logging or drill monitoring inference. Supports grade control and blast design. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fresh|transitional|oxide|sulphide|waste|ore|overburden|gangue — promote to reference product]']
  rotary_speed_rpm decimal(7,2) [note: 'The average rotary speed of the drill string in revolutions per minute during this activity. A key operational parameter for drill performance optimisation and rock hardness inference.']
  sample_reference varchar [note: 'The sample identifier assigned to the drill chip or core sample collected during this activity, as registered in LabWare LIMS. Enables traceability between drill activity and assay results for grade control and resource estimation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this drill activity record was sourced. Supports data lineage, quality assessment, and silver layer provenance tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this drill activity record was last modified in the source system or silver layer. Supports incremental load detection and audit compliance.']
  water_encountered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether groundwater or surface water was encountered during drilling. Relevant for blast hole charging decisions, dewatering planning, and geotechnical risk assessment.']

  Note: 'Records individual drill rig activity events including drill hole collar position, hole depth, penetration rate (m/hr), bit type, drill pattern reference, rock hardness index, and drill status. Sourced from MineStar and drill monitoring systems. Supports drill-and-blast planning and geology grade control integration.'
}

Table equipment.inspection {
  inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each equipment inspection record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — a discrete, lifecycle-managed business event (pre-start or periodic inspection) with regulatory standing.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment asset (haul truck, drill, shovel, conveyor, processing plant unit, etc.) that was inspected. Links to the equipment master register sourced from Infor EAM and Caterpillar MineStar. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE / MASTER_RESOURCE category.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Pre-commissioning and commissioning inspections are project deliverables and handover requirements. Business process: handover certification, acceptance testing, and punch list closure require linking']
  checklist_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the versioned checklist template applied during this inspection, specific to the equipment class (e.g., haul truck pre-start checklist v3.2). Ensures traceability of which checklist version was in force at the time of inspection.']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.corrective_action. Business justification: Failed equipment inspections generate corrective actions to remediate defects and prevent recurrence. This is a core compliance workflow in mining safety management systems. Inspectors must track that']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Inspection costs (labor, materials) are allocated to cost centres for maintenance budget tracking and compliance cost reporting in mining operations.']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to an associated HSE incident record in IsoMetrix if this inspection was triggered by or linked to a safety incident (e.g., post-incident inspection). Null for routine pre-start and periodic inspections.']
  inspection_supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor who reviewed and approved the inspection record. Required for statutory and post-incident inspections. Null for self-certified pre-start inspections where supervisor sign-off is not mandated.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the workforce record of the person who conducted the inspection (operator, maintainer, or supervisor). Used for competency verification and regulatory accountability. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category.']
  previous_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on inspection (previous_inspection_id)']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Equipment inspections identify hazards (worn components, safety system failures, structural defects) that feed into risk assessments. Inspection findings directly inform residual risk ratings and cont']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or operational location where the inspection was conducted. Supports site-level compliance reporting and regulatory submissions per jurisdiction.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective maintenance work order generated in Infor EAM as a result of defect findings identified during this inspection. Null if no corrective action was required or work order has not yet been raised.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supervising officer approved the inspection record. Null if not yet approved or approval not required. Supports regulatory audit trails for statutory inspections.']
  checklist_items_completed varchar [note: 'Number of checklist items that were assessed and recorded during this inspection. A value less than checklist_items_total may indicate an incomplete inspection, which is a compliance risk for pre-start and statutory inspections.']
  checklist_items_total varchar [note: 'Total number of checklist items included in the inspection for this equipment class and inspection type. Used to calculate completion rate and to validate that all mandatory items were assessed.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more defect findings from this inspection require a corrective action to be raised. True triggers work order generation in Infor EAM and/or a corrective action task in IsoMetrix. Critical for HSE compliance workflow integration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection record was first created in the source system. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  defect_count_critical varchar [note: 'Number of checklist items rated as critical severity during this inspection. Critical defects require immediate equipment shutdown and corrective action before operation can resume. A value greater than zero should result in a fail overall result.']
  defect_count_major varchar [note: 'Number of checklist items rated as major severity during this inspection. Major defects require corrective action within a defined timeframe but may allow conditional operation under specified controls.']
  defect_count_minor varchar [note: 'Number of checklist items rated as minor severity during this inspection. Minor defects are recorded for monitoring and scheduled maintenance but do not prevent equipment operation.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspector completed and submitted the inspection. Used to calculate inspection duration and to confirm the inspection was completed before equipment operation commenced (pre-start compliance).']
  equipment_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the equipment type being inspected. Determines which checklist template applies and which regulatory requirements govern the inspection. Sourced from the equipment master register in Infor EAM and Caterpillar MineStar. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: haul_truck|drill|shovel|excavator|dozer|grader|conveyor|crusher|processing_plant|light_vehicle — promote to reference product]']
  equipment_grounded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment was taken out of service (grounded) as a result of this inspection. True means the equipment must not be operated until corrective action is completed and a return-to-service inspection is passed. Directly impacts fleet availability and OEE calculations.']
  equipment_hours_at_inspection decimal(10,1) [note: 'Engine or operating hours recorded on the equipment at the time of inspection. Used to determine whether periodic maintenance-based inspections are due and to track equipment utilisation context at the time of the inspection. Sourced from Caterpillar MineStar telematics.']
  equipment_tag varchar [note: 'Human-readable fleet number or asset tag of the equipment being inspected (e.g., HT-042, DR-007). Denormalized from the equipment master for operational convenience and to support inspection records even when the equipment master FK is temporarily unavailable.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the inspection was captured and submitted. Mobile app refers to field-based digital capture. Paper form indicates a manual record subsequently digitised. Desktop portal is a web-based entry. Integrated OBC (on-board computer) refers to direct capture from Caterpillar MineStar on-board systems.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the inspection record as assigned by the source system (IsoMetrix or Infor EAM). Used for cross-system reference, regulatory submissions, and audit trails. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the inspection record. Draft indicates the inspection is in progress or not yet submitted. Submitted means the inspector has completed and lodged the record. Approved indicates supervisor sign-off. Rejected means the record was returned for correction. Cancelled applies to inspections that were voided. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection by its regulatory or operational trigger. Pre-start inspections are legally mandated before equipment operation in most mining jurisdictions. Periodic inspections occur at scheduled intervals. Post-incident inspections follow a safety event. Statutory inspections are required by specific mining regulations. Return-to-service inspections occur after maintenance or repair.']
  inspector_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative comments provided by the inspector to describe defect findings, operational context, or observations not captured by the structured checklist items. Supports incident investigation and root cause analysis.']
  inspector_competency_code varchar [note: 'Competency or certification code held by the inspector that authorises them to conduct this class of inspection (e.g., operator licence number, statutory inspector certificate). Required for regulatory compliance verification. Sourced from SAP HR competency records.']
  inspector_role varchar [note: 'Role of the person conducting the inspection. Operators conduct pre-start checks. Maintainers conduct post-maintenance checks. Supervisors conduct periodic and statutory inspections. Statutory inspectors are government-appointed or certified inspectors. Third-party inspectors are external contractors.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific location within the mine site where the inspection was conducted (e.g., ROM pad, pit level 3, crusher feed area). Supplements the site_id with operational granularity for incident investigation and compliance reporting.']
  odometer_km decimal(10,1) [note: 'Odometer reading in kilometres recorded at the time of inspection, applicable to mobile equipment such as haul trucks and light vehicles. Null for fixed plant and equipment without odometers. Supports distance-based maintenance scheduling.']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'Aggregate pass/fail outcome of the inspection. Pass indicates all checklist items met the required standard. Fail indicates one or more critical or major defects were found that prevent safe operation. Conditional pass indicates minor defects noted but equipment may operate under specified conditions pending corrective action.']
  photo_evidence_count varchar [note: 'Number of photographic evidence items attached to this inspection record to document defect findings. Supports regulatory audit requirements and insurance claims. Photos are stored in the document management system with references captured at the checklist item level.']
  regulatory_standard varchar [note: 'The specific mining safety regulation or standard under which this inspection is mandated or conducted. Enables jurisdiction-specific compliance reporting and regulatory submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MSHA_30CFR56|MSHA_30CFR57|WHS_ACT|DMIRS_WA|MINES_ACT_QLD|ISO_45001 — promote to reference product]']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'The shift during which the inspection was conducted. Pre-start inspections are typically conducted at the commencement of each shift. Used for shift-level compliance reporting and to correlate inspection outcomes with shift patterns.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this inspection record was ingested into the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and troubleshooting of ETL pipelines.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native primary key or record identifier of this inspection in the originating source system (IsoMetrix, Infor EAM, or mobile app). Enables back-referencing to the source system for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspector commenced the inspection. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category. Critical for regulatory compliance — pre-start inspections must be completed before equipment operation commences.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection record was last modified in the source system. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Tracks amendments, supervisor approvals, and corrective action updates.']

  Note: 'Pre-start and periodic equipment inspection records completed by operators, maintainers, and supervisors as required by mining safety regulations (e.g., WHS Act, MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57). Captures inspection type (pre-start, periodic, post-incident, statutory), checklist items per equipment class, defect findings with photo evidence references, severity rating (critical/major/minor), pass/fail status, corrective action required flag, and inspector identity with competency verification. Critical for regulatory compliance — pre-start inspections are legally mandated before equipment operation in most mining jurisdictions. Linked to HSE incident workflows and maintenance work order generation. Sourced from IsoMetrix, Infor EAM, and mobile inspection apps.'
}

Table equipment.asset_lifecycle_event {
  asset_lifecycle_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each asset lifecycle event record in the silver layer. Primary key for the asset_lifecycle_event data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining fleet or fixed plant asset (e.g., haul truck, drill, shovel, conveyor, processing equipment) that this lifecycle event applies to. Links to the equipment master register.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Major lifecycle events (commissioning, major relocations, decommissioning) occur within capital projects. Business process: asset lifecycle management, project asset tracking, and capitalization decis']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Major lifecycle events (commissioning, decommissioning, major overhauls) can trigger incidents (commissioning accidents, decommissioning spills) or be incident outcomes (asset written off after major ']
  previous_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site from which the asset was relocated, applicable when event_type is \'relocation\'. Enables tracking of inter-site asset movements for fleet optimisation and fixed asset register updates.']
  primary_asset_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal department, crew, or external contractor responsible for executing the lifecycle event. Links to the workforce or vendor master. Critical for accountability tracking and contractor cost allocation.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or operational location where the asset resides at the time of the lifecycle event. Supports multi-site fleet management and capital planning by site.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Major lifecycle events (commissioning, major overhauls, decommissioning) may be capitalized and tracked against WBS elements for capital project accounting and asset valuation.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'SAP Plant Maintenance or Infor EAM work order number associated with this lifecycle event. Provides traceability to the originating maintenance work order for audit and cost reconciliation purposes.']
  asset_condition_rating varchar [note: 'Assessed physical condition of the asset immediately following the lifecycle event, as determined by the maintenance engineer or inspector. Used for insurance valuation, capital planning, and remaining useful life estimation. Ratings: excellent = like-new post-rebuild; good = minor wear; fair = moderate wear requiring monitoring; poor = significant degradation; critical = imminent failure risk.']
  asset_hours_at_event decimal(12,1) [note: 'Total cumulative operating hours recorded on the asset at the time of the lifecycle event (e.g., engine hours at rebuild). Used to calculate Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF), validate rebuild intervals, and benchmark against OEM recommended service intervals.']
  book_value_at_event decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net book value of the asset on the fixed asset register at the time of the lifecycle event, in the reporting currency. Used for CAPEX capitalisation decisions, impairment testing, and disposal gain/loss calculations.']
  component_replaced varchar [note: 'Description of the specific major component replaced or rebuilt during this lifecycle event (e.g., \'Engine Assembly - CAT 3516C\', \'Transmission - Allison 6000 Series\', \'Hydraulic Pump Assembly\', \'Bucket Assembly\'). Applicable for component_replacement and major_rebuild event types.']
  component_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number of the replaced or installed major component (e.g., engine serial number, transmission serial number). Enables component-level traceability, warranty tracking, and OEM service history management.']
  cost_centre_code varchar [note: 'SAP cost centre code to which the lifecycle event cost is allocated. Enables departmental cost tracking, budget variance analysis, and OPEX/CAPEX reporting by operational area.']
  cost_classification varchar [note: 'Financial classification of the cost incurred for this lifecycle event. CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) = costs that extend asset life or add capability, capitalised to the fixed asset register; OPEX (Operating Expenditure) = costs expensed in the period. Critical for fixed asset register reconciliation, tax treatment, and financial reporting under IFRS.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the event_cost amount (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Required for multi-currency operations across international mining jurisdictions.']
  counterparty_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external party involved in an ownership change event (e.g., lessor name for lease return, buyer name for asset sale, OEM vendor for major rebuild contract). Applicable when event_type is \'ownership_change\' or \'major_rebuild\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lifecycle event record was first created in the data platform (silver layer ingestion time). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and reconciliation with source systems.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Depreciation method applied to the asset following this lifecycle event. May be updated after a major rebuild or change in remaining useful life estimate. Straight_line = equal annual charge; units_of_production = charge based on actual production output; declining_balance = accelerated depreciation.']
  eam_event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the lifecycle event as recorded in Infor EAM (e.g., work order number, event reference number). Used for cross-system reconciliation with SAP Fixed Asset Register and insurance systems.']
  event_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for this lifecycle event in the reporting currency. Includes labour, materials, contractor fees, and overheads. Used for CAPEX/OPEX tracking, insurance valuation, and All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) calculations.']
  event_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the lifecycle event occurred or was formally executed (e.g., commissioning date, rebuild completion date, decommissioning date). This is the principal real-world business event date used for fixed asset register updates and insurance valuation.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the lifecycle event work was completed and the asset was returned to service or formally closed. Used to calculate event duration and Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) impact.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional technical or operational notes recorded by the maintenance engineer or planner regarding this lifecycle event (e.g., observed failure mode, special conditions, deviations from standard procedure, OEM recommendations).']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the lifecycle event work commenced (e.g., when the rebuild began, when the asset was powered down for decommissioning). Used for duration calculation and OEE impact analysis.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the lifecycle event. Planned = scheduled but not yet started; in_progress = work underway; completed = event fully executed and closed; cancelled = event voided before execution; on_hold = temporarily suspended pending approval or parts.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the significant lifecycle milestone for the asset. Commissioning = first entry into service; major_rebuild = engine, transmission, or hydraulic system overhaul; component_replacement = scheduled or unscheduled part swap; mid_life_refurbishment = comprehensive mid-cycle restoration; relocation = transfer between mine sites or operational areas; standby_mothballing = temporary suspension of operations; ownership_change = lease return or sale; decommissioning_disposal = permanent retirement and disposal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commissioning|major_rebuild|component_replacement|mid_life_refurbishment|relocation|standby_mothballing|ownership_change|decommissioning_disposal — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hse_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) clearance or permit was required to execute this lifecycle event (e.g., confined space entry permit for major rebuild, environmental permit for decommissioning). Supports ISO 45001 and ISO 14001 compliance tracking.']
  hse_permit_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the HSE permit or work clearance certificate issued for this lifecycle event (e.g., Permit to Work number, Isolation Certificate number). Populated when hse_clearance_required is true. Traceable to IsoMetrix HSE system.']
  insurance_valuation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Updated insurance replacement value of the asset following this lifecycle event, in the reporting currency. Revised after major rebuilds, refurbishments, or condition assessments. Used for insurance policy management and asset register reconciliation.']
  odometer_km_at_event decimal(12,1) [note: 'Total cumulative distance in kilometres recorded on the asset at the time of the lifecycle event. Applicable for mobile mining equipment (haul trucks, light vehicles). Complements operating hours for maintenance interval tracking.']
  ownership_transfer_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the nature of the ownership change when event_type is \'ownership_change\'. Lease_return = asset returned to lessor at end of lease term; sale = asset sold to third party; purchase = asset acquired; internal_transfer = transfer between legal entities within the mining group.']
  planned_event_date date [note: 'The originally scheduled date for the lifecycle event as per the maintenance or capital plan. Compared against event_date to measure schedule adherence and support Life of Mine (LOM) capital planning.']
  remaining_useful_life_months varchar [note: 'Updated estimate of the asset\'s remaining useful life in months following this lifecycle event, as assessed by the maintenance engineer. Revised after major rebuilds, refurbishments, or condition assessments. Critical for capital planning, depreciation schedules, and Life of Mine (LOM) fleet replacement planning.']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal team, maintenance crew, or external contractor responsible for executing the lifecycle event (e.g., \'OEM Rebuild Team - Caterpillar\', \'Site Maintenance Crew A\'). Retained for reporting without requiring a join.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this lifecycle event record was sourced. Infor_eam = Infor Enterprise Asset Management; sap_pm = SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance; minestar = Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; manual = manually entered record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lifecycle event record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental load detection, change data capture, and audit trail management.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the OEM or contractor warranty for the rebuilt or replaced component expires following this lifecycle event. Used to manage warranty claims and schedule post-warranty maintenance.']

  Note: 'Tracks significant lifecycle milestones for each asset including commissioning, major rebuild (engine, transmission, hydraulic system), component replacement, mid-life refurbishment, relocation between sites, temporary standby/mothballing, change of ownership (lease return, sale), and final decommissioning/disposal. Captures event type, event date, responsible party, cost incurred (CAPEX vs OPEX classification), resulting asset condition rating, and updated remaining useful life estimate. Critical for fixed asset register reconciliation, insurance valuation, and capital planning. Sourced from Infor EAM and finance system (SAP/Oracle).'
}

Table equipment.component_register {
  component_register_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each serialised component record in the master register. Primary key for the component_register data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent mining asset (haul truck, drill, shovel, conveyor, mill, etc.) on which this component is currently installed. Sourced from Infor EAM equipment master.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Critical components installed during project commissioning (engines, gearboxes, conveyors) are tracked for warranty and as-built documentation. Business process: warranty management, spare parts plann']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Component rebuild and replacement costs are allocated to cost centres for maintenance cost tracking, AISC calculation, and cost per operating hour analysis. Reusing existing cost_centre_code.']
  parent_component_register_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on component_register (parent_component_register_id)']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Components (engines, transmissions, hydraulics) have OEM suppliers; tracking vendor per component enables targeted parts sourcing, supplier performance analysis, and warranty claims. Real business pro']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the most recent Infor EAM work order under which this component was installed, rebuilt, or last serviced.']
  component_class varchar [note: 'High-level engineering classification grouping components by their functional and failure mode characteristics. Used to apply appropriate condition monitoring strategies and maintenance standards.']
  component_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the component (e.g., \'Engine Assembly\', \'Final Drive LH\', \'SAG Mill Liner Set\', \'Hydraulic Pump Main\'). Human-readable label used in maintenance planning and reporting.']
  component_number varchar [note: 'Unique business-facing identifier for the component record within the Infor EAM system. Used for cross-referencing with maintenance work orders and procurement records.']
  component_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the component. Drives inventory availability, maintenance scheduling, and rebuild/replace decision workflows within Infor EAM.']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the major replaceable component category. Drives applicable life limits, rebuild standards, and condition monitoring protocols. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engine|transmission|final_drive|hydraulic_pump|get|mill_liner|conveyor_drive|differential|torque_converter|alternator|pump|motor|gearbox|brake_assembly|suspension — promote to reference product]']
  condition_assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent condition assessment was performed for this component. Used to determine assessment currency and trigger re-inspection schedules.']
  condition_assessment_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to determine the current condition rating of the component. Supports traceability of condition data and selection of appropriate monitoring strategies.']
  condition_rating varchar [note: 'Current assessed condition rating of the component based on inspection, oil analysis, vibration monitoring, or visual assessment. Drives condition-based maintenance (CBM) decisions and rebuild/replace recommendations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to rebuild_cost and replacement_cost fields (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Supports multi-currency operations across international mining sites.']
  eam_component_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this component record within Infor EAM. Enables data lineage traceability and reconciliation between the Silver layer data product and the operational system of record.']
  hours_at_installation decimal(10,2) [note: 'Accumulated hours recorded on this component at the time of its most recent installation onto the parent asset. Used to calculate hours accrued during the current installation period.']
  hours_since_new decimal(10,2) [note: 'Accumulated operating hours on this component since it was manufactured new. Includes all installation periods across its full lifecycle. Key metric for life-to-date tracking and OEM warranty assessment.']
  hours_since_rebuild decimal(10,2) [note: 'Accumulated operating hours on this component since its most recent rebuild or remanufacture. Primary metric for condition-based maintenance triggers and rebuild interval management.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date on which this component was most recently installed onto the parent asset. Used to calculate time-in-service and trigger scheduled replacement intervals.']
  is_critical_component boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component is classified as safety-critical or production-critical under the site\'s critical equipment register. Critical components require enhanced inspection frequencies and mandatory hold points per ISO 45001.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal inspection or condition monitoring event performed on this component. Used to track inspection compliance and schedule next inspection.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date on which this component was originally manufactured. Used for age-based life assessments, warranty eligibility determination, and calendar-based replacement planning.']
  max_rebuild_limit varchar [note: 'OEM or engineering-approved maximum number of rebuilds permitted for this component type before mandatory condemnation and replacement. Enforces safe life limits.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'OEM model or specification number for this component. Distinguishes variants within the same component type (e.g., different engine ratings or transmission ratios) for compatibility verification.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory inspection or condition monitoring event for this component. Drives preventive maintenance scheduling in Infor EAM and ensures compliance with site inspection regimes.']
  oem_part_number varchar [note: 'Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) catalogue part number for this component. Used for procurement, warranty claims, and interchangeability verification against approved parts lists.']
  oil_sample_reference varchar [note: 'Laboratory reference number for the most recent oil or fluid analysis sample taken from this component. Links to LabWare LIMS for detailed oil analysis results used in condition-based maintenance decisions.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Standardised code identifying the specific mounting position on the parent asset where this component is installed (e.g., \'LH_FRONT\', \'DRIVE_1\', \'MILL_FEED_END\'). Enables position-specific wear pattern analysis.']
  rebuild_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for the most recent rebuild of this component in the site operating currency. Used for rebuild/replace economic analysis and OPEX reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA PM/CO.']
  rebuild_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this component has been rebuilt or remanufactured over its lifetime. Used in rebuild/replace decision analysis and to assess cumulative fatigue against OEM rebuild limits.']
  rebuild_replace_decision varchar [note: 'Current engineering or maintenance decision on the disposition of this component at end of its current service life. Informs CAPEX/OPEX planning and component exchange pool management.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this component register record in the Silver layer. Supports incremental data loading, audit trails, and change detection for downstream consumers.']
  remaining_life_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated remaining operating hours before this component reaches its target life limit or requires rebuild/replacement. Calculated as target_life_hours minus hours_since_rebuild. Drives proactive maintenance scheduling.']
  removal_date date [note: 'Date on which this component was most recently removed from the parent asset. Null if the component is currently installed. Used to calculate actual service life per installation.']
  replacement_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Current estimated cost to replace this component with a new OEM unit. Used in rebuild/replace decision analysis and CAPEX budget planning. Sourced from Pronto Xi procurement catalogue.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number uniquely identifying this physical component unit. Enables full traceability across its entire lifecycle including multiple installations and rebuilds.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical warehouse bin, yard, or storage location where this component is held when not installed (e.g., \'WAREHOUSE-A-B12\', \'REBUILD-SHOP-FLOOR\'). Sourced from Pronto Xi inventory management.']
  target_life_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'OEM or site-defined target operating hours for this component before scheduled replacement or rebuild. Basis for component life planning and budget forecasting within the Life of Mine (LOM) plan.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the OEM or remanufacturer warranty for this component expires. Ensures warranty claims are lodged within valid periods and informs make-vs-buy decisions for repairs.']

  Note: 'Master register of major replaceable components tracked at individual serial number level including engines, transmissions, final drives, hydraulic pumps, GET (ground engaging tools), mill liners, and conveyor drives. Each record represents a serialised component with OEM part number, serial number, currently installed asset, installation date, accumulated hours since last rebuild, condition rating, and rebuild history count. Supports component life tracking, condition-based maintenance triggers, and rebuild/replace decision-making. Sourced from Infor EAM.'
}

Table equipment.tyre_record {
  tyre_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each tyre lifecycle record in the tyre management system. Primary key for the tyre_record data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mobile equipment unit (haul truck, articulated dump truck, grader, or other mobile plant) on which this tyre is or was installed. Links to the equipment master register.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Tyre costs are significant in mining operations and tracked by cost centre for cost per tonne analysis, AISC calculation, and maintenance budget management.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Tyres are high-cost consumables procured from specific vendors; linking enables vendor performance tracking (cost-per-hour by supplier), warranty claims, and procurement optimization. Real business pr']
  retreaded_from_tyre_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on tyre_record (retreaded_from_tyre_record_id)']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Infor EAM work order associated with the tyre installation or removal event, enabling traceability to the maintenance activity.']
  accumulated_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total engine/operating hours accumulated on this tyre across its entire service life (including prior fitments if retreaded or rotated). Sourced from Caterpillar MineStar or Infor EAM. Key metric for tyre life forecasting and TKPH compliance.']
  accumulated_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total kilometres travelled by the tyre across its entire service life. Sourced from fleet management telematics. Used alongside accumulated_hours for tyre life benchmarking and rotation scheduling.']
  application_code varchar [note: 'TRA/ISO application category code indicating the equipment type the tyre is designed for: E=Earthmover/Off-the-Road, L=Loader, G=Grader, C=Compactor, IND=Industrial. Drives TKPH rating selection and procurement.']
  axle_position varchar [note: 'Axle group on which the tyre is mounted (front, rear, tag/pusher, or all for single-axle equipment). Complements position_code for load distribution and wear analysis.']
  compound_grade varchar [note: 'OEM-designated rubber compound or product line grade (e.g., E4, L5, VSDP, XLDD2). Indicates the tyre construction and application suitability for specific haul road conditions.']
  cost_per_hour decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated cost per operating hour in the operating currency, derived from purchase_cost divided by accumulated_hours at removal or current point in service. Key performance indicator for tyre vendor comparison and OPEX benchmarking.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used for end-of-life tyre disposal. Null if tyre is still in service. Supports environmental compliance reporting under ISO 14001 and regulatory waste management obligations. Retreaded tyres re-enter service and generate a new tyre_record.']
  eam_asset_number varchar [note: 'Infor EAM asset number assigned to this tyre as a tracked asset. Provides the cross-reference key between the tyre management system (Kal Tire TOMS / Klinge) and the Infor EAM asset register for maintenance cost allocation.']
  haul_road_condition varchar [note: 'Assessed condition rating of the primary haul road on which this tyre operated at time of removal. Contextualises tyre wear and damage patterns; poor haul road conditions are a leading cause of premature tyre failure in open-cut mining.']
  inflation_pressure_kpa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Most recently recorded tyre inflation pressure in kilopascals (kPa) at the last inspection. Correct inflation is critical for tyre life, fuel efficiency, and safety. Compared against OEM-recommended cold inflation pressure.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the tyre was fitted to the vehicle at the current position. Start of the active service period for accumulated hours and kilometres tracking.']
  is_retreaded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tyre currently in service is a retreaded carcass (True) or a new tyre (False). Retreaded tyres typically have different cost profiles and performance expectations.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical tyre inspection including tread depth measurement, visual damage check, and pressure verification. Drives inspection scheduling compliance.']
  load_rating_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum static load capacity in kilograms as specified by the OEM for this tyre at the recommended inflation pressure. Used to validate tyre fitment against vehicle gross vehicle mass (GVM) requirements.']
  mine_site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the mine site where this tyre is or was in service. Enables multi-site tyre performance benchmarking and fleet-wide OPEX analysis across the mining portfolio.']
  oem_brand varchar [note: 'Tyre manufacturer brand name (e.g., Bridgestone, Michelin, Goodyear, Titan). Used for vendor performance comparison and procurement decisions.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Standardised position code indicating where the tyre is mounted on the vehicle (e.g., LF=Left Front, RF=Right Front, LR=Left Rear, RR=Right Rear for rigid trucks; steer/drive/tag for articulated trucks). Used for rotation optimisation and wear pattern analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LF|RF|LRI|LRO|RRI|RRO|steer|drive_left|drive_right|tag — promote to reference product]']
  purchase_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Original purchase cost of the tyre in the operating currency. Used as the basis for cost per hour (CPH) and cost per kilometre calculations. Represents a significant component of mobile fleet Operating Expenditure (OPEX) at 10-15% of total.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP or Pronto Xi purchase order number under which this tyre was procured. Enables traceability from tyre record back to procurement contract and vendor invoice for cost allocation and warranty claims.']
  recommended_pressure_kpa decimal(8,2) [note: 'OEM-recommended cold inflation pressure in kilopascals (kPa) for this tyre size and load rating. Reference value for pressure compliance checks and deviation alerting.']
  recorded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tyre record was first created in the system, representing the record audit creation time. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  removal_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the tyre was removed from the vehicle. Null if the tyre is currently in service. Combined with installation_date to calculate service duration.']
  removal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for tyre removal from service. Drives failure mode analysis, vendor performance benchmarking, and haul road condition assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: worn|damaged|rotated|scrapped|puncture|sidewall_failure|heat_separation|scheduled_change|bead_damage|cut — promote to reference product]']
  retread_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this tyre carcass has been retreaded. Tracks carcass reuse for cost optimisation and quality assurance. OEM carcass inspection standards typically limit retreads to 1-2 cycles for large OTR tyres.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number stamped on the tyre sidewall. Used as the primary physical identifier for tyre tracking across its full lifecycle from receipt to disposal.']
  size_specification varchar [note: 'Standard tyre size designation in OEM format (e.g., 40.00R57, 27.00R49, 37.00R57). Encodes width, aspect ratio, and rim diameter per TRA/ETRTO standards. Critical for procurement and fitment compatibility.']
  speed_rating_kmh varchar [note: 'Maximum permissible operating speed in kilometres per hour as specified by the OEM. Relevant for haul road speed limit compliance and tyre selection for high-speed haulage applications.']
  tkph_actual decimal(8,2) [note: 'Calculated actual TKPH value based on the haul truck payload, haul road distance, and cycle time recorded by Caterpillar MineStar. Compared against tkph_rating to assess thermal overload risk.']
  tkph_rating decimal(8,2) [note: 'OEM-specified Tonne-Kilometres Per Hour (TKPH) rating for the tyre, representing the maximum thermal load the tyre can sustain. Compliance with TKPH rating is critical to prevent heat-related tyre failures in open-cut mining haul cycles.']
  tread_depth_current_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Most recent tread depth measurement in millimetres recorded at the last inspection. Used to assess remaining tyre life and trigger rotation or replacement decisions.']
  tread_depth_new_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Original tread depth in millimetres when the tyre was new, as specified by the OEM. Baseline reference for calculating tread wear percentage and remaining tread life.']
  tread_depth_removal_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Tread depth in millimetres measured at the time of tyre removal. Null if tyre is still in service. Used to determine whether the tyre reached its designed wear limit or was removed prematurely.']
  tyre_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the tyre. Tracks whether the tyre is actively mounted, held in the tyre bay, undergoing repair/retread, scrapped, or disposed. Supports inventory management and lifecycle cost analysis.']
  tyre_type varchar [note: 'Tyre construction category indicating internal structure. Radial is standard for large haul trucks; bias ply used on some older or smaller equipment; solid and foam-filled used in specific underground or loader applications.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this tyre record, capturing inspection results, status changes, or measurement updates. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the OEM or supplier warranty for this tyre expires. Enables timely warranty claims for premature failures and supports procurement negotiations with tyre vendors.']

  Note: 'Dedicated tyre management records for haul truck and mobile equipment tyres including tyre serial number, OEM brand and compound, size specification (e.g., 40.00R57), position on vehicle (LF, RF, LR, RR for rigid trucks; steer/drive for articulated), installation date, removal date, removal reason (worn, damaged, rotated, scrapped), accumulated hours and kilometres, tread depth measurements at each inspection, TKPH rating compliance, cost per hour, and disposal method. Tyre costs represent 10-15% of mobile fleet OPEX in open-cut mining. Supports tyre life forecasting, rotation optimisation, and vendor performance comparison. Sourced from tyre management systems (Klinge, Kal Tire TOMS) and Infor EAM.'
}

Table equipment.dispatch_instruction {
  dispatch_instruction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dispatch instruction record. Primary key for the dispatch instruction entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining fleet or fixed plant asset (haul truck, loader, shovel, ancillary equipment) to which this dispatch instruction is assigned.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Equipment dispatch during construction phases (material movement for haul road construction, pre-stripping, infrastructure development) supports project execution. Business process: project logistics ']
  haul_route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the assigned haul route that the equipment must follow from source to target location. Null for non-haulage instructions (park, standby, fuel).']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site where this dispatch instruction was executed. Supports multi-site fleet management and reporting.']
  primary_dispatcher_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the human dispatcher who issued this instruction. Null if the instruction was generated by an automated dispatch optimization algorithm.']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this dispatch instruction was issued and executed.']
  superseded_dispatch_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dispatch_instruction (superseded_dispatch_instruction_id)']
  target_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination location for this dispatch instruction (e.g., crusher, ROM pad, waste dump, parking area, fuel station).']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Dispatch instructions route equipment within specific tenement areas. Real mining dispatch systems track which licence area work is assigned to for compliance with tenement conditions, cost allocation']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin location for this dispatch instruction (e.g., shovel face, ROM pad, waste dump, stockpile, fuel station).']
  acknowledgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the equipment operator acknowledged receipt of the dispatch instruction via the in-cab terminal or mobile device. Null if not yet acknowledged.']
  actual_cycle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual total cycle time in minutes including loading, hauling, dumping, and return travel. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) calculation and productivity benchmarking.']
  actual_travel_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual time in minutes taken by the equipment to travel from source to target location. Used for dispatch optimization model calibration and route performance analysis.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispatch instruction was completed (e.g., load delivered, dump completed, equipment parked). Null if not yet completed.']
  compliance_outcome varchar [note: 'Final compliance outcome indicating whether the operator followed the dispatch instruction as issued. Critical for truck-shovel match factor optimization and operator performance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dispatch instruction record was first created in the Fleet Management System (FMS) database. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  dispatch_algorithm_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the automated dispatch optimization engine or algorithm that generated this instruction (e.g., MineStar Dispatch, Wenco, Modular). Null if issued by a human dispatcher.']
  dispatch_zone varchar [note: 'Operational zone or area within the mine site where this dispatch instruction applies (e.g., Pit 3, ROM Area, Waste Dump North). Used for zone-based dispatch optimization.']
  estimated_cycle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated total cycle time in minutes including loading, hauling, dumping, and return travel. Used for truck-shovel match factor optimization and queue time minimization.']
  estimated_travel_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated time in minutes for the equipment to travel from source to target location along the assigned haul route, calculated by the dispatch optimization engine based on route distance, grade, and equipment performance characteristics.']
  expected_payload_tonnes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Expected payload weight in tonnes that the equipment will carry for this dispatch instruction. Used for truck-shovel match factor calculation and production forecasting.']
  fuel_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fuel level of the equipment as a percentage of tank capacity at the time the dispatch instruction was issued. Used to trigger fuel dispatch instructions.']
  haul_route_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or code of the assigned haul route (e.g., Route P3-C1, Haul Road 12A).']
  instruction_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the dispatch instruction, typically generated by the Fleet Management System (FMS) dispatcher or automated dispatch optimization engine.']
  instruction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispatch instruction. Tracks progression from issuance through operator acknowledgement to final compliance outcome. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: issued|acknowledged|in_progress|completed|overridden|timed_out|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  instruction_type varchar [note: 'Type of routing decision or operational command issued to the fleet asset. Defines the primary action the operator must execute. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: load|dump|park|fuel|standby|queue|relocate|maintenance|emergency — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_emergency_dispatch boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this dispatch instruction was issued as an emergency response (e.g., equipment breakdown, safety incident, production bottleneck). Emergency dispatches override normal dispatch optimization rules.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispatch instruction was issued by the dispatcher or automated dispatch optimization engine. Primary business event timestamp for real-time fleet productivity tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dispatch instruction record was last updated in the Fleet Management System (FMS) database. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Type of material being hauled or handled in this dispatch instruction. Critical for production reconciliation and material movement tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ore|waste|overburden|coal|concentrate|tailings|fuel|water — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the operator or dispatcher for why the dispatch instruction was overridden or not followed. Null if instruction was complied with.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the reason for dispatch instruction override. Used for root cause analysis of dispatch compliance issues. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: safety|equipment_failure|road_condition|weather|operational_priority|dispatcher_override|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking of this dispatch instruction (1=highest priority, 10=lowest priority). Used to sequence instructions when multiple tasks are pending for the same equipment.']
  queue_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time in minutes that the equipment spent waiting in queue at the source or target location. Critical metric for queue time minimization and dispatch optimization.']
  road_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the haul road at the time the dispatch instruction was issued. Affects travel time, safety, and route selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: good|fair|poor|wet|icy|under_maintenance|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the source location (e.g., Pit 3 Shovel 12, ROM Pad A, Waste Dump North).']
  start_execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operator began executing the dispatch instruction (e.g., started moving toward source location). Null if not yet started.']
  target_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the target location (e.g., Primary Crusher, ROM Pad B, Waste Dump East, Parking Bay 5).']
  timeout_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispatch instruction expired due to operator non-compliance or system timeout. Null if instruction did not time out.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather condition at the time the dispatch instruction was issued. Impacts travel time estimates and route selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|fog|dust|snow|wind|extreme_heat — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Real-time operator dispatch instructions issued by the FMS dispatcher or automated dispatch optimisation engine (MineStar Dispatch, Wenco, Modular) to haul trucks, loaders, and ancillary fleet. Each record represents a single routing decision capturing instruction type (load, dump, park, fuel, standby, queue, relocate), source location (shovel face, ROM pad, waste dump), target location, assigned haul route, estimated travel time, issuing dispatcher or algorithm ID, issue timestamp, operator acknowledgement timestamp, and compliance outcome (complied, overridden, timed-out). Drives real-time fleet productivity, truck-shovel match factor optimisation, and queue time minimisation.'
}

Table equipment.equipment_shutdown_scope {
  equipment_shutdown_scope_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for equipment_shutdown_scope']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific mining asset included in this shutdown scope']
  predecessor_scope_item_equipment_shutdown_scope_id varchar [note: 'Reference to another shutdown_scope record that must be completed before work on this asset can commence, establishing sequencing dependencies within the shutdown critical path']
  responsible_supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance supervisor or foreman accountable for coordinating and completing work on this asset during the shutdown']
  shutdown_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the planned maintenance shutdown event']
  shutdown_scope_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this asset-shutdown scope record']
  actual_asset_downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual duration in hours that this asset was unavailable during shutdown execution, measured from isolation to return-to-service']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred for work on this asset during shutdown execution, aggregated from work orders and purchase orders']
  actual_labour_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual total labour hours consumed for work on this asset during shutdown execution, captured from timesheets and work orders']
  asset_criticality_for_shutdown varchar [note: 'Criticality classification indicating whether this asset is on the shutdown critical path, near-critical (within float tolerance), or non-critical for overall shutdown duration']
  asset_readiness_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this asset within the shutdown workflow, tracking progression through readiness gates from pre-shutdown preparation to return-to-service']
  asset_shutdown_priority varchar [note: 'Priority classification for this asset within the shutdown scope, indicating urgency and resource allocation precedence']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage completion of work on this asset, used for shutdown progress tracking and earned value analysis']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Current completion status of work on this asset within the shutdown, tracking whether work is complete, in progress, or deferred to a future shutdown']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost for work on this asset including labour, materials, and contractor services, contributing to total shutdown cost']
  estimated_labour_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated total labour hours required to complete work on this asset during the shutdown, used for resource planning and cost estimation']
  isolation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this asset was formally isolated and locked out for shutdown work, marking the start of asset-specific downtime']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing issues, delays, scope changes, or other relevant information specific to this asset\'s participation in the shutdown']
  planned_asset_downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated duration in hours that this specific asset will be unavailable during the shutdown, used for production loss calculations and resource planning']
  post_shutdown_commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when post-shutdown commissioning and performance testing was completed, authorizing return-to-service']
  post_shutdown_commissioning_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset requires formal commissioning and performance testing before being returned to operational service after shutdown work completion']
  pre_shutdown_inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the pre-shutdown inspection was completed for this asset, establishing baseline condition and confirming scope']
  pre_shutdown_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset requires a formal pre-shutdown inspection to assess condition and finalize work scope before shutdown commencement']
  return_to_service_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this asset was returned to operational service after shutdown work completion and commissioning, marking the end of asset-specific downtime']
  scope_item_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the scope item within the shutdown, used for work package tracking and communication (e.g., SD2024-001, SD2024-002)']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Sequencing order for this asset within the shutdown critical path, indicating when work on this asset should commence relative to other assets (e.g., crusher must be down before feeder work starts)']
  trade_required varchar [note: 'Primary trade or craft skill required for work on this asset (e.g., mechanical fitter, electrician, boilermaker, millwright), used for resource leveling']
  work_package_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the maintenance work to be performed on this asset during the shutdown, including specific tasks, inspections, and repairs']

  Note: 'This association product represents the inclusion of a specific asset in a planned maintenance shutdown event. It captures the shutdown-specific planning and execution data for each asset participating in the shutdown, including criticality ranking, sequencing constraints, planned versus actual downtime, readiness gates, and inspection requirements. Each record links one asset to one shutdown plan with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific shutdown participation.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, planned maintenance shutdowns involve multiple assets (crushers, conveyors, mills, haul trucks) that must be taken offline for maintenance, and each asset participates in multiple shutdowns throughout its operational lifecycle. Shutdown coordinators actively manage asset-shutdown scope items as operational records, tracking each asset\'s criticality ranking, sequencing constraints, planned versus actual downtime, readiness gates, and inspection requirements specific to each shutdown event. This is a true operational many-to-many relationship where the association itself (the scope item) carries substantial business data and is actively managed throughout the shutdown planning and execution process.'
}

Table equipment.asset_crew_assignment {
  asset_crew_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each asset-crew assignment record. Primary key.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the mining asset being assigned to the crew']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the crew assigned to operate or maintain the asset']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date when this crew\'s assignment to this asset ended or is planned to end. Null for currently active assignments. Used for assignment history and planning future crew rotations.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'Date when this crew was assigned operational or maintenance responsibility for this asset. Supports historical tracking of asset-crew assignments across roster changes and operational reconfigurations.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this asset-crew assignment. Active = currently operational, Planned = scheduled future assignment, Suspended = temporarily inactive, Completed = historical assignment ended. Used for operational planning and reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  maintenance_responsibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crew has maintenance responsibility for this asset (true) or purely operational responsibility (false). Maintenance crews are accountable for preventive maintenance tasks, pre-start inspections, and minor repairs during their shift.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes specific to this asset-crew assignment, such as special operating instructions, crew-specific asset configurations, or temporary restrictions.']
  primary_operator_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crew is the primary operator responsible for this asset (true) or a secondary/backup crew (false). Used to determine accountability for production targets and asset care. An asset may have one primary crew per shift pattern.']
  shift_pattern_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the shift pattern during which this crew operates this asset (e.g., DAY, NIGHT, SWING). An asset may be assigned to different crews for different shift patterns. Critical for 24/7 operations planning.']
  utilization_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target equipment utilization percentage for this asset when operated by this crew, expressed as a percentage (0.00-100.00). Used for crew performance measurement and production planning. Varies by crew type and shift pattern.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment of mining assets to work crews across shifts and time periods. It captures which crews are authorized to operate which assets, the period of assignment, primary responsibility designation, utilization targets, and maintenance accountability. Each record links one asset to one crew for a specific time period with attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment relationship. Essential for shift planning, production accountability, equipment utilization tracking, and maintenance responsibility allocation.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, assets are operated by multiple crews across different shifts (day crew, night crew, maintenance crew), and crews operate multiple assets simultaneously (a production crew may operate 3 haul trucks, 1 excavator, 1 dozer). The assignment relationship is actively managed by operations planners and shift supervisors, with specific assignment periods, primary responsibility designation, utilization targets, and maintenance accountability tracked for each asset-crew pairing.'
}

Table equipment.asset_stakeholder_concern {
  asset_stakeholder_concern_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each asset-stakeholder concern record. Primary key for the association.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific mining asset that is the subject of stakeholder concern or engagement']
  stakeholder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the community stakeholder who has raised concerns or requires engagement regarding the asset']
  concern_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of concern or impact the stakeholder has regarding this specific asset. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  concern_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific concern this stakeholder has about this asset, including context and stakeholder perspective.']
  concern_raised_date date [note: 'Date when the stakeholder first raised this concern about the asset. Establishes the timeline for the concern relationship.']
  concern_resolved_date date [note: 'Date when the concern was formally resolved to the stakeholder\'s satisfaction, if applicable.']
  engagement_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of engagement with this stakeholder specifically about this asset, based on concern severity and stakeholder expectations. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Current escalation status of the concern indicating urgency and management attention required. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  grievance_lodged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stakeholder has lodged a formal grievance through the grievance mechanism regarding this asset.']
  grievance_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal grievance lodged regarding this asset-stakeholder concern, if applicable.']
  impact_assessment_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal impact assessment has been completed for this asset-stakeholder concern combination.']
  last_engagement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent engagement activity specifically regarding this asset-stakeholder concern. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  mitigation_measures varchar [note: 'Description of specific mitigation actions implemented or planned to address this stakeholder concern about this asset.']
  mitigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of mitigation measures implemented to address the stakeholder concern about this asset. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  next_engagement_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next engagement activity with this stakeholder regarding this specific asset concern.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes and context regarding the asset-stakeholder concern relationship, engagement history, and stakeholder feedback.']
  responsible_officer varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the community relations officer responsible for managing this specific asset-stakeholder concern.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the community engagement relationship between mining assets and community stakeholders. It captures stakeholder concerns about specific assets (haul trucks affecting village roads, drill rigs near sacred sites, conveyors creating dust), engagement activities, mitigation measures, and escalation status. Each record links one asset to one stakeholder with attributes that exist only in the context of this concern relationship. Serves as the operational register for managing community impacts of individual equipment and infrastructure.. Existence Justification: Mining operations actively manage community stakeholder concerns about specific assets as an operational business process. A single asset (e.g., haul truck) can generate concerns from multiple stakeholders (village affected by road traffic, indigenous group concerned about cultural sites, environmental NGO monitoring dust), and a single stakeholder can have concerns about multiple assets (community concerned about both haul trucks and drill rigs operating near their land). Community relations teams create, track, and manage these concern records with engagement schedules, mitigation plans, and escalation status specific to each asset-stakeholder combination.'
}

Table equipment.asset_contract_coverage {
  asset_contract_coverage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the asset_contract_coverage association']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the mining equipment asset covered under this contract']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the procurement contract providing coverage for this asset']
  added_to_contract_date date [note: 'Date when this asset was formally added to the contract coverage. Used for audit trail and amendment tracking.']
  annual_coverage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annualized monetary value of coverage for this specific asset under this contract. Used for cost allocation, budget tracking, and contract value analysis.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Date when contract coverage expires for this specific asset. Used to trigger renewal processes and prevent coverage gaps.']
  coverage_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special conditions, exclusions, or requirements specific to this asset-contract coverage (e.g., \'Excludes tire replacement\', \'Requires monthly inspection report\').']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'Date when contract coverage becomes effective for this specific asset. May differ from the master contract start date if assets are added mid-term.']
  coverage_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the coverage relationship: active (in force), suspended (temporarily paused), expired (coverage period ended), pending activation (scheduled future start), or cancelled (terminated early).']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Type of service coverage provided under this contract for this asset: full maintenance (parts and labor), parts supply only, labor only, preventive maintenance only, breakdown support only, telematics monitoring, or warranty extension.']
  included_service_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of service labor hours included in the coverage for this asset during the coverage period. Nullable if coverage is parts-only or unlimited hours.']
  removed_from_contract_date date [note: 'Date when this asset was removed from contract coverage (if applicable). Nullable for currently covered assets.']
  response_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contractually committed maximum response time in hours for service calls on this asset. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and vendor performance measurement.']
  service_level_agreement varchar [note: 'SLA tier or service level commitment applicable to this asset under this contract (e.g., gold, silver, bronze). Defines priority, response times, and service scope.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the service contract coverage relationship between mining equipment assets and procurement contracts. It captures which assets are covered under which service or maintenance contracts, including coverage periods, service level agreements, response time commitments, and coverage type. Each record links one asset to one contract with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific coverage arrangement. This enables tracking of multi-vendor service agreements across the fleet, contract compliance monitoring, SLA performance measurement, and ensures assets receive appropriate maintenance coverage.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, individual assets (haul trucks, excavators, drills) are covered by multiple service contracts simultaneously - a haul truck may have a maintenance contract, a parts supply agreement, and a telematics monitoring contract all active at once. Conversely, each service contract (especially blanket maintenance or parts supply agreements) covers multiple assets across the fleet. The business actively manages these coverage relationships, tracking which assets are covered under which contracts, with specific SLA commitments, response times, and coverage periods that vary by asset-contract combination.'
}

Table equipment.inspection_checklist {
