  incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the incident record if an incident occurred during the decant operation.']
  laboratory_sample_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the laboratory sample taken during the decant operation for detailed water quality analysis.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.processing_plant. Business justification: Decanted water from TSF returns to specific processing plant for reuse. Closed water circuit tracking requirement for water balance, recycled_water_ratio_pct calculation, and water licence compliance.']
  primary_decant_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or operator who performed or supervised the decant operation.']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_master. Business justification: Decant operations may use water treatment chemicals (flocculants, pH adjusters) to meet discharge quality standards. Linking to material master enables chemical consumption tracking, inventory plannin']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the tailings storage facility where the decant operation occurred.']
  water_balance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.water_balance. Business justification: Decant operations are water outflow events that contribute to TSF water balance calculations. Each decant operation occurs within a specific water balance period and its volume is accounted for in tha']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the decant operation was formally approved by the authorising person.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the decant operation met all applicable water licence conditions and regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this decant operation record was first created in the data system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system from which this decant operation record was captured (e.g., SCADA, manual log, OSIsoft PI System).']
  decant_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the decant operation was completed.']
  decant_event_date date [note: 'The date on which the decant operation was performed. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  decant_event_reference varchar [note: 'Business reference number or code assigned to this decant operation event for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  decant_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the decant operation commenced, including time of day.']
  decant_structure_type varchar [note: 'Type of decant structure or mechanism used to extract supernatant water from the TSF (e.g., penstock, decant tower, pump, spillway, siphon).']
  destination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination where the decanted water was directed (process water return to plant, evaporation pond, treatment plant, discharge to environment, storage dam).']
  flow_rate_m3_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average flow rate of water during the decant operation, measured in cubic meters per hour.']
  freeboard_after_decant_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Vertical distance between the water level and the crest of the TSF embankment after the decant operation, measured in meters. Critical safety parameter.']
  incident_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether any incident, anomaly, or safety event occurred during the decant operation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this decant operation record was last updated or modified in the data system.']
  licence_condition_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific water licence condition or permit requirement that governs this decant operation.']
  non_compliance_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of any non-compliance issues identified during or after the decant operation.']
  operation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and observations recorded by the operator or supervisor regarding the decant operation, including any anomalies or special conditions.']
  operation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the decant operation (planned, in progress, completed, suspended, aborted, failed).']
  rainfall_during_operation_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total rainfall recorded during the decant operation period, measured in millimeters. Relevant for water balance calculations.']
  volume_decanted_m3 decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total volume of supernatant water decanted from the TSF during this operation, measured in cubic meters.']
  water_level_after_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation of the supernatant water level in the TSF pond after the decant operation was completed, measured in meters above datum.']
  water_level_before_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation of the supernatant water level in the TSF pond before the decant operation commenced, measured in meters above datum.']
  water_quality_ph decimal(4,2) [note: 'pH level of the decanted water measured at the point of decant. Critical water quality parameter.']
  water_quality_tds_mg_per_l decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total dissolved solids concentration in the decanted water, measured in milligrams per liter.']
  water_quality_tss_mg_per_l decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total suspended solids concentration in the decanted water, measured in milligrams per liter.']
  water_quality_turbidity_ntu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Turbidity of the decanted water measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU).']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions during the decant operation (e.g., clear, rain, storm). Weather can impact decant operations and water quality.']

  Note: 'Records decant and supernatant water management operations at TSFs, including decant event date, decant structure used (penstock, decant tower, pump), volume decanted, water quality at point of decant, destination (process water return, evaporation pond, treatment plant), and compliance with water licence conditions. Manages the return of process water from TSF to the processing plant.'
}

Table tailings.decant_structure {
  decant_structure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for decant_structure']
  superseded_decant_structure_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on decant_structure (superseded_decant_structure_id)']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tailings storage facility where this decant structure is located.']
  asset_condition_rating varchar [note: 'Current physical condition assessment rating of the decant structure.']
  blockage_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the risk level for blockage or clogging of the decant structure.']
  construction_contractor varchar [note: 'Name of the contractor responsible for constructing and installing the decant structure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the decant structure record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the decant structure was decommissioned and removed from active service.']
  design_capacity_m3_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum designed flow rate capacity of the decant structure measured in cubic meters per hour.']
  design_engineer varchar [note: 'Name of the engineering firm or individual responsible for the design of the decant structure.']
  design_standard varchar [note: 'Engineering standard or code used in the design of the decant structure.']
  emergency_response_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the emergency response plan applicable to this decant structure.']
  inlet_elevation_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation of the decant structure inlet measured in meters above sea level.']
  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Required interval in days between consecutive inspections of the decant structure.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the decant structure was installed and commissioned for operation.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inspection conducted on the decant structure.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the decant structure location in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the decant structure location in decimal degrees.']
  maintenance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting maintenance activities, observations, and recommendations for the decant structure.']
  monitoring_system_installed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an automated monitoring system is installed on the decant structure.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory inspection of the decant structure.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the decant structure within its lifecycle.']
  outlet_elevation_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation of the decant structure outlet measured in meters above sea level.']
  pipe_diameter_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Internal diameter of the decant pipe measured in millimeters.']
  pipe_material varchar [note: 'Material composition of the decant pipe used in the structure.']
  regulatory_permit_number varchar [note: 'Official permit or authorization number issued by regulatory authorities for the decant structure operation.']
  rehabilitation_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the rehabilitation plan associated with the decant structure closure.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the decant structure based on consequence of failure assessment.']
  structure_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the decant structure for identification and tracking purposes.']
  structure_name varchar [note: 'Business name or designation of the decant structure for operational reference.']
  structure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the decant structure based on its design and operational mechanism.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the decant structure record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for decant_structure. Referenced by decant_structure_id.'
}

Table tailings.destination_facility {
  destination_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for destination_facility']
  destination_tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Destination facilities are where decanted water is sent. In mining operations, one common destination is transfer to another TSF for water reuse. When the destination facility IS a TSF, this FK links ']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved emergency response plan document specific to this destination facility.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified engineer accountable for the technical oversight and safety of this destination facility.']
  parent_destination_facility_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on destination_facility (parent_destination_facility_id)']
  rehabilitation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved rehabilitation and closure plan for this destination facility.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent mining site or operation where this destination facility is located.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address or location descriptor for the destination facility.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as building, zone, or area designation for the destination facility.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the destination facility is located.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the destination facility was officially commissioned and approved for operational use.']
  consequence_classification varchar [note: 'Risk-based classification of potential consequences from facility failure, used for determining monitoring and governance requirements.']
  construction_method varchar [note: 'Engineering construction methodology used for the tailings storage facility, critical for risk assessment and regulatory compliance.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for operational communications regarding the destination facility.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the destination facility operations team.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code identifying the country where the destination facility is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this destination facility record was first created in the system.']
  crest_elevation_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation of the dam crest above mean sea level, measured in meters, used for monitoring and raise planning.']
  current_capacity_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current utilized volumetric capacity of the destination facility, measured in cubic meters, reflecting actual tailings or waste stored.']
  dam_height_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum vertical height of the tailings dam structure measured from the foundation to the crest, in meters.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the destination facility including key characteristics, purpose, and operational context.']
  design_capacity_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity of the destination facility as per engineering design specifications, measured in cubic meters.']
  estimated_closure_liability_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial liability for closure and rehabilitation of the destination facility, denominated in United States Dollars.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the destination facility for operational reference and reporting.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the destination facility as recognized in operational and regulatory documentation.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination facility based on its primary operational purpose within the tailings management system.']
  geotechnical_monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of geotechnical monitoring activities including piezometer readings, settlement surveys, and stability assessments.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal safety and compliance inspection conducted on the destination facility.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the destination facility centroid in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the destination facility centroid in decimal degrees.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory inspection of the destination facility per regulatory and internal requirements.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the destination facility indicating its operational readiness and regulatory standing.']
  permit_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current regulatory permit, requiring renewal or facility closure.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory permit or license number authorizing the operation of this destination facility.']
  planned_closure_date date [note: 'Anticipated date for facility closure and commencement of rehabilitation activities as per mine closure plan.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the destination facility location.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the government or regulatory body with jurisdiction over this destination facility.']
  remaining_capacity_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Available volumetric capacity remaining in the destination facility before reaching design limits, measured in cubic meters.']
  seepage_monitoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether continuous seepage monitoring is mandated for this facility based on risk classification and regulatory requirements.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the destination facility is located.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this destination facility record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for destination_facility. Referenced by destination_facility_id.'
}

Table tailings.deposition_plan {
  deposition_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for deposition_plan']
  raise_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dam raise schedule associated with this deposition plan, coordinating embankment construction with tailings placement.']
  superseded_by_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the deposition plan that supersedes this plan, establishing plan lineage and succession. Nullable if plan is current.']
  superseded_deposition_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on deposition_plan (superseded_deposition_plan_id)']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tailings storage facility where this deposition plan will be executed.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or internal authority that approved the deposition plan.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the deposition plan received formal approval from the designated authority.']
  approval_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference or permit number assigned by the approval authority for tracking and compliance purposes.']
  closure_rehabilitation_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the closure and rehabilitation plan document that outlines post-deposition reclamation activities and long-term stewardship.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deposition plan record was first created in the system.']
  deposition_method varchar [note: 'Specific technique used for tailings placement: spigot (point discharge), cyclone (classification discharge), subaerial (above water), subaqueous (below water), beach discharge (distributed along beach), or tower discharge (central tower distribution).']
  deposition_rate_m3_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned average daily rate of tailings deposition in cubic meters per day.']
  design_engineer_license_number varchar [note: 'Professional engineering license or registration number of the design engineer, required for regulatory compliance.']
  design_engineer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the professional engineer or engineering firm responsible for designing the deposition plan.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the deposition plan is scheduled to end or be superseded. Nullable for open-ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the deposition plan becomes effective and operational execution begins.']
  emergency_response_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the emergency preparedness and response plan document for incidents related to this deposition plan.']
  environmental_impact_statement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the Environmental Impact Statement document associated with this deposition plan.']
  estimated_closure_liability_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial liability for closure and rehabilitation activities associated with this deposition plan, in the operating currency.']
  geotechnical_stability_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the planned deposition configurations geotechnical stability: very high (excellent stability), high (good stability), moderate (acceptable with monitoring), low (requires mitigation), or very low (significant risk).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deposition plan record was most recently updated or modified.']
  monitoring_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the geotechnical and environmental monitoring plan document that defines surveillance requirements during plan execution.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the deposition plan, used in operational documentation and regulatory submissions.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the deposition plan objectives, methodology, and key operational considerations.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the deposition plan, describing the planned tailings placement strategy.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the deposition plan: draft (under development), approved (authorized for execution), active (currently being executed), suspended (temporarily halted), completed (execution finished), superseded (replaced by newer plan), or cancelled (terminated).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deposition method: upstream (raise built on deposited tailings), downstream (raise built downstream), centerline (hybrid method), dry stack (filtered tailings), filtered (dewatered), thickened (high-density slurry), or paste (very high-density slurry).']
  planned_capacity_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total volume of tailings material planned for deposition under this plan, measured in cubic meters.']
  planned_capacity_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total mass of tailings material planned for deposition under this plan, measured in metric tonnes.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision of the deposition plan was issued or approved.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version or revision number of the deposition plan, incremented with each formal update or amendment.']
  risk_classification varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification of the deposition plan based on consequence of failure analysis: extreme (catastrophic potential), very high (major potential), high (serious potential), significant (moderate potential), or low (minor potential).']
  seepage_control_strategy varchar [note: 'Description of the planned approach for managing and controlling seepage from the tailings facility, including drainage systems, liners, and collection methods.']
  target_beach_slope_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned slope angle of the tailings beach expressed as a percentage, critical for stability and drainage management.']
  target_freeboard_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned vertical distance between the water surface and the crest of the dam, measured in meters, required for flood containment and safety.']
  water_management_strategy varchar [note: 'Description of the planned approach for managing water balance, including reclaim water, stormwater, and process water within the deposition plan.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for deposition_plan. Referenced by plan_id.'
}

Table tailings.capacity_model {
  capacity_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for capacity_model']
  superseded_capacity_model_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on capacity_model (superseded_capacity_model_id)']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tailings storage facility for which this capacity model applies.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this capacity model received formal approval for operational use.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the responsible engineer or authority who approved this capacity model for operational use.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory or industry standard framework that this capacity model complies with, such as ICMM Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management or local Environmental Impact Statement requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity model record was first created in the system.']
  current_capacity_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining volumetric capacity available for tailings deposition at the time of model calculation, measured in cubic meters.']
  current_capacity_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining mass capacity available for tailings deposition at the time of model calculation, measured in metric tonnes.']
  current_elevation_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current elevation of the tailings surface above sea level at the time of model calculation, measured in meters.']
  deposition_rate_m3_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average daily volumetric rate at which tailings are deposited into the storage facility, measured in cubic meters per day.']
  deposition_rate_tonnes_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average daily mass rate at which tailings are deposited into the storage facility, measured in metric tonnes per day.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this capacity model becomes operationally effective and applicable for planning and reporting.']
  estimated_life_years decimal(6,2) [note: 'Projected number of years until the tailings storage facility reaches full capacity based on current deposition rates and available capacity.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this capacity model is no longer valid or has been superseded by a newer version. Nullable for models with indefinite validity.']
  freeboard_requirement_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum required vertical distance between the tailings surface and the crest of the dam for safety and operational purposes, measured in meters.']
  maximum_elevation_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum elevation above sea level that the tailings storage facility is designed to reach, measured in meters.']
  model_assumptions varchar [note: 'Key assumptions and constraints underlying the capacity model calculations and projections.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code assigned to the capacity model for external reference and reporting purposes.']
  model_confidence_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the confidence level in the capacity model predictions based on data quality, methodology robustness, and validation results.']
  model_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the capacity model including methodology, assumptions, and key parameters.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the capacity model used for tailings storage facility planning and analysis.']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the capacity model methodology used for tailings storage facility capacity estimation and forecasting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity model record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date when the next scheduled review and validation of the capacity model is due.']
  raise_method varchar [note: 'Engineering method used for raising the tailings dam embankment to increase storage capacity.']
  raise_stage_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying the current or planned raise stage of the tailings dam embankment.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Required frequency in months for periodic review and validation of the capacity model to ensure continued accuracy.']
  settlement_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Factor accounting for consolidation and settlement of tailings over time, applied to capacity calculations.']
  solids_concentration_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average percentage of solids by weight in the tailings slurry deposited into the storage facility.']
  solids_density_tonnes_per_m3 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average density of tailings solids used in capacity calculations, measured in metric tonnes per cubic meter.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capacity model indicating its operational state and approval level.']
  total_design_capacity_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity of the tailings storage facility as designed, measured in cubic meters.']
  total_design_capacity_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum mass capacity of the tailings storage facility as designed, measured in metric tonnes.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total design capacity currently utilized by deposited tailings, calculated as (used capacity / total design capacity) × 100.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the capacity model to track revisions and updates over time.']
  void_ratio decimal(6,3) [note: 'Ratio of void volume to solids volume in deposited tailings, used for capacity and settlement calculations.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for capacity_model. Referenced by capacity_model_id.'
}

Table tailings.monitoring_location {
  monitoring_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for monitoring_location']
  parent_monitoring_location_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on monitoring_location (parent_monitoring_location_id)']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tailings storage facility where this monitoring location is installed.']
  access_difficulty varchar [note: 'Classification of the difficulty level for field personnel to physically access the monitoring location for manual readings or maintenance.']
  alert_threshold_lower decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower boundary threshold value that triggers an alert condition when readings fall below this level.']
  alert_threshold_upper decimal(12,4) [note: 'Upper boundary threshold value that triggers an alert condition when readings exceed this level.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent calibration or verification of the monitoring instrument to ensure measurement accuracy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring location record was first created in the system.']
  critical_threshold_lower decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower boundary critical threshold value that triggers emergency response protocols when readings fall below this level.']
  critical_threshold_upper decimal(12,4) [note: 'Upper boundary critical threshold value that triggers emergency response protocols when readings exceed this level.']
  data_logger_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the automated data logger or telemetry system connected to this monitoring location for continuous data capture.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring location was decommissioned or removed from active service. Null if still active.']
  elevation_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation of the monitoring location above mean sea level in meters.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring location or instrument was installed and commissioned for operation.']
  installation_depth_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Depth below ground surface at which the monitoring instrument is installed, measured in meters. Applicable to subsurface instruments such as piezometers and inclinometers.']
  instrument_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer or supplier of the monitoring instrument installed at this location.']
  instrument_model varchar [note: 'Model number or designation of the monitoring instrument installed at this location.']
  instrument_serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to the monitoring instrument for traceability and warranty purposes.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the monitoring location in decimal degrees.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the monitoring location for field identification and reporting purposes.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the monitoring location (e.g., Piezometer PZ-12A, Seepage Weir SW-03).']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the monitoring location in decimal degrees.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for readings collected at this monitoring location (e.g., kPa for pore pressure, mm for settlement).']
  monitoring_purpose varchar [note: 'Primary purpose or objective of the monitoring location within the tailings management framework.']
  monitoring_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the monitoring instrument or location type used for geotechnical or environmental surveillance.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration or verification of the monitoring instrument.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations, maintenance history, or contextual information about the monitoring location.']
  reading_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which readings are collected from this monitoring location as per the monitoring plan.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether readings from this monitoring location must be included in regulatory compliance reports submitted to governing authorities.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name of the geotechnical engineer or responsible person assigned to oversee this monitoring location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the monitoring location in its lifecycle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring location record was last modified in the system.']
  zone_classification varchar [note: 'Geotechnical zone or area within the tailings storage facility where the monitoring location is positioned.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for monitoring_location. Referenced by monitoring_location_id.'
}

Table hse.incident {
  incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for incident']
  affected_stakeholder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.stakeholder. Business justification: Mining incidents (dust, noise, water contamination, blasting) frequently affect nearby community stakeholders. Incident management requires tracking affected parties for notification, consultation, an']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: HSE incidents frequently involve specific equipment assets (vehicle collisions, equipment rollovers, machinery failures causing injuries). Incident investigations require linking to the involved asset']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Mining capital projects track HSE incidents during construction/commissioning for safety performance reporting, regulatory compliance (ESIA commitments), stage-gate reviews, and handover certification']
  chemical_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.chemical_register. Business justification: Incidents involving hazardous chemicals (spills, exposures, releases) should reference the specific chemical from the register. This creates traceability from incident to chemical properties, SDS info']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Commodity-level incident classification is standard in mining (lithium fires, coal dust explosions, iron ore dust exposure). Regulatory bodies require commodity classification in incident reports. HSE']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: HSE incidents incur costs (investigation, remediation, lost time) that must be allocated to cost centres for management reporting. This FK enables tracking of HSE cost performance by organizational un']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: When an HSE incident occurs, it may trigger activation of a specific emergency response plan. Linking incident to the ERP that was activated creates traceability between incident response and document']
  incident_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person leading the incident investigation team.']
  incident_injured_person_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person injured in the incident. Applicable only for injury incidents.']
  management_of_change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.management_of_change. Business justification: Incidents may occur during or shortly after a Management of Change implementation, indicating that the change introduced new risks or that risk controls were inadequate. Linking incidents to MOC helps']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Mining incidents occur within specific orebodies; incident investigation requires geological context (ore type, mining method, geotechnical domain). Real-world incident reports cite orebody location f']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Incidents frequently involve specific products (chemical spills, contamination, handling injuries, TML exceedance). Incident investigation and regulatory reporting require product identification. Mini']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_master. Business justification: Incidents involving materials (equipment parts, consumables, non-chemical items causing injury/environmental release) require material master linkage for investigation, root cause analysis, and regula']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mine site or facility where the incident occurred.']
  activity_being_performed varchar [note: 'Description of the work activity or task being performed at the time of the incident (e.g., drilling, blasting, hauling, maintenance, processing).']
  affected_media varchar [note: 'For environmental incidents, the environmental media or receptor affected: air, water, soil, groundwater, vegetation, wildlife, heritage site, or none (for non-environmental incidents). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: air|water|soil|groundwater|vegetation|wildlife|heritage_site|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  containment_actions varchar [note: 'Description of immediate containment or emergency response actions taken to control the incident and prevent escalation.']
  contributing_factors varchar [note: 'Description of contributing factors that enabled or exacerbated the incident (e.g., equipment condition, training gaps, procedural deficiencies, environmental conditions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incident record was first created in the HSE management system.']
  days_lost varchar [note: 'Number of scheduled work days lost due to the injury. Applicable for LTI incidents.']
  environmental_volume_released decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of material released in an environmental spill or discharge incident, measured in cubic meters or liters. Applicable only for environmental incidents.']
  immediate_cause varchar [note: 'Description of the immediate or direct cause of the incident as identified during initial assessment.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique incident reference number assigned by IsoMetrix or site HSE system for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the incident: reported, under investigation, investigation complete, closed, or regulatory submitted.']
  incident_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident occurred. Principal business event timestamp.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the incident: injury (LTI, MTI, FAI, RWI), near-miss, environmental spill, unauthorized discharge, permit exceedance, heritage disturbance, or biodiversity impact. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: injury|near_miss|environmental_spill|unauthorized_discharge|permit_exceedance|heritage_disturbance|biodiversity_impact — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  injury_body_part varchar [note: 'Body part affected by the injury (e.g., hand, leg, head, back). Applicable only for injury incidents.']
  injury_nature varchar [note: 'Nature or type of injury sustained (e.g., fracture, laceration, contusion, burn, strain). Applicable only for injury incidents.']
  investigation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the formal incident investigation was completed and final report issued.']
  investigation_methodology varchar [note: 'Root cause analysis methodology used for the investigation: ICAM (Incident Cause Analysis Method), Bow-Tie, 5-Why, Root Cause Tree, TapRoot, or not applicable if no formal investigation conducted.']
  investigation_report_status varchar [note: 'Status of the formal investigation report: not started, in progress, draft, final, or approved.']
  investigation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a formal investigation is required for this incident based on severity and company policy.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the formal incident investigation commenced.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incident record was last updated or modified.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific location within the site where the incident occurred (e.g., pit bench, processing plant, ROM pad, workshop).']
  ltifr_contribution_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this incident contributes to the Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate (LTIFR) calculation. True for LTI and fatal incidents.']
  permit_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the environmental permit or authorization that was exceeded or breached. Applicable only for permit exceedance incidents.']
  regulatory_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this incident requires notification to regulatory authorities (MSHA, state environmental agencies, etc.).']
  regulatory_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory notification: not required, pending, submitted, or acknowledged by the authority.']
  regulatory_submission_deadline date [note: 'Deadline date by which the incident must be reported to regulatory authorities (e.g., MSHA 24-hour or 10-day reporting requirements).']
  remediation_description varchar [note: 'Description of remediation or corrective actions required to address the incident impact and prevent recurrence.']
  remediation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether environmental remediation or corrective actions are required as a result of this incident.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was first reported into the HSE management system.']
  root_cause_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the root cause(s) identified through the investigation, including immediate, underlying, and systemic causes.']
  severity_classification varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the incident based on actual or potential consequence. For injuries: fatal, lost time injury (LTI), medical treatment injury (MTI), restricted work injury (RWI), first aid injury (FAI). For near-misses: high potential or low potential. For environmental: minor, moderate, or major. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fatal|lost_time_injury|medical_treatment_injury|restricted_work_injury|first_aid_injury|high_potential_near_miss|low_potential_near_miss|minor_environmental|moderate_environmental|major_environmental — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the incident occurred: day, night, or swing shift.']
  trifr_contribution_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this incident contributes to the Total Recordable Injury Frequency Rate (TRIFR) calculation. True for LTI, MTI, RWI, and fatal incidents.']

  Note: 'Master record of all health, safety, and environmental incidents and their investigations at mine sites. Covers injuries (LTI, MTI, FAI, RWI), near-misses, environmental spills, unauthorized discharges, permit exceedances, heritage disturbances, and biodiversity impacts. Captures incident type, severity classification, LTIFR/TRIFR contribution flags, affected media (for environmental events), location, shift, activity being performed, immediate causes, containment actions, remediation requirements, and regulatory notification status. For investigated incidents, records root cause analysis methodology (ICAM, Bow-Tie, 5-Why), contributing factors, immediate and underlying causes, investigation team members, and final investigation report status. Tracks regulatory submission deadlines for MSHA and state authority notifications. Sourced from IsoMetrix HSE event management. SSOT for ALL HSE incident records and their formal investigations.'
}

  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the incident investigation that identified this corrective action requirement. Null if not investigation-derived.']

CAPAs should l']

Current responsible_owner is denormalized text; FK enables workforce integr']

Business justification: Corrective actions for material quality issues, handling procedure updates, or storage requirement changes require material master linkage for material-specific improvement tracking, quality managemen']

  action_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the corrective or preventive action, typically formatted as CAPA-NNNNNN for external reference and tracking.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the corrective action: open (assigned but not started), in_progress (work underway), pending_verification (action completed awaiting review), verified (effectiveness confirmed), closed (completed and verified), overdue (past due date), cancelled (no longer required). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|in_progress|pending_verification|verified|closed|overdue|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the action: corrective (addresses existing nonconformity), preventive (prevents potential nonconformity), improvement (enhances performance beyond compliance), or observation (noted for awareness without formal action).']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual financial cost incurred to implement the corrective action. Null until action is completed and costs are reconciled.']
  closed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual with authority who formally closed the corrective action after confirming effectiveness.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was formally closed after verification and effectiveness review confirmed successful resolution.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, lessons learned, implementation challenges, or other relevant information not captured in structured fields.']
  communication_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this corrective action requires formal communication to workforce, contractors, or external stakeholders (True) or is internal only (False).']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the corrective action implementation was completed. Null if action is still open or in progress.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the corrective or preventive action to be taken, including specific steps, controls to be implemented, and expected outcomes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this corrective action record was first created in the system, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for estimated and actual costs (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD, ZAR).']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the corrective action must be completed, established based on risk level and operational constraints.']
  effectiveness_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the corrective action successfully addressed the root cause and prevented recurrence: effective (fully resolved), partially_effective (some improvement), ineffective (issue persists), not_yet_assessed (awaiting review period).']
  effectiveness_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for follow-up review to assess the long-term effectiveness of the corrective action, typically 30-90 days after implementation.']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost to implement the corrective action, including materials, labor, equipment, and downtime costs. Used for CAPEX/OPEX planning and cost-benefit analysis.']
  hierarchy_of_controls varchar [note: 'Classification of the corrective action according to the hierarchy of controls framework: elimination (remove hazard), substitution (replace with less hazardous), engineering (isolate people from hazard), administrative (change work procedures), PPE (Personal Protective Equipment - last resort).']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the workplace inspection that identified this corrective action requirement. Null if not inspection-derived.']
  interim_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of any immediate interim or temporary controls implemented while the permanent corrective action is being developed and executed.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this corrective action record was last updated, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the corrective action based on risk severity and business impact: critical (immediate action required), high (urgent), medium (standard timeline), low (routine).']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this corrective action addresses a recurring issue (True) or a first-time occurrence (False). Used to identify systemic problems requiring deeper intervention.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area responsible for executing the corrective action (e.g., Mine Operations, Processing, Maintenance, HSE).']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Documented root cause identified through investigation techniques (5 Whys, Fishbone, Fault Tree Analysis) that led to the need for this corrective action.']
  site_location varchar [note: 'Mine site, processing plant, or facility location where the corrective action is to be implemented.']
  sop_updated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this corrective action resulted in updates to Standard Operating Procedures (True) or not (False).']
  source_trigger_type varchar [note: 'Type of event or activity that triggered the creation of this corrective action: incident (actual HSE event), investigation (formal inquiry), audit (compliance review), inspection (routine check), risk_assessment (hazard identification), observation (safety observation), complaint (stakeholder concern), near_miss (potential incident). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: incident|investigation|audit|inspection|risk_assessment|observation|complaint|near_miss — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether implementation of this corrective action requires workforce training or competency development (True) or not (False).']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was verified as implemented and effective. Null if verification has not yet occurred.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the corrective action has been effectively implemented: inspection (physical check), audit (systematic review), observation (behavioral verification), document_review (records check), testing (performance test), measurement (quantitative assessment).']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who performed the verification of corrective action effectiveness.']

  Note: 'Tracks corrective and preventive actions (CAPAs) arising from incident investigations, risk assessments, audits, inspections, and safety observations. Records action description, action type (corrective/preventive/improvement), responsible owner, priority, due date, completion date, verification method, effectiveness review date, and closure status. Supports hierarchy of controls tracking and ISO 45001/ISO 14001 continual improvement requirements. Links to source trigger (investigation, audit, inspection, or risk assessment).'
}

Business justification: Capital projects require HSE risk assessments for ESIA approvals, stage-gate decisions, design reviews, and PSSR (pre-startup safety review). Regulatory authorities require project-specific risk asses']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Commodity-level hazard assessments capture inherent risks (lithium reactivity, coal spontaneous combustion, nickel toxicity). Mining HSE frameworks require commodity-specific risk profiles for regulat']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Risk assessments drive control measure implementation with associated costs. Cost centre link enables budgeting for risk mitigation activities and tracking actual spend against site/area budgets for H']
  delivery_destination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_destination. Business justification: Risk assessments for loading operations at customer ports, rail terminals, and delivery facilities are mandatory in mining logistics. Assessing berth conditions, equipment compatibility, environmental']
  hazard_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hazard record in the enterprise-wide hazard register that this risk assessment evaluates.']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Hazard assessments are orebody-specific due to varying geotechnical conditions, mining methods, ground control requirements, and commodity-specific hazards (coal dust, radioactive minerals). JORC/regu']
  primary_risk_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who conducted the risk assessment.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who reviewed the risk assessment for technical accuracy and completeness.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Product-specific risk assessments are mandatory for hazardous materials, moisture-sensitive products (TML risks), and products with special handling requirements. Mining operations conduct JSAs and ri']
  superseded_by_assessment_risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer risk assessment that replaces this one, if applicable.']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_master. Business justification: Material-specific risk assessments (HAZOP, JSA for handling/storage of explosives, fuels, reagents, spare parts) require linkage to material master for proper hazard identification, control measure sp']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or facility where this risk assessment applies.']
  activity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work activity, task, or operation being assessed for risk.']
  affected_personnel_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of workers potentially exposed to the assessed hazard.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the risk assessment was formally approved by authorized personnel.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person who approved the risk assessment.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the risk assessment was conducted or last updated.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the risk assessment, typically following organizational numbering convention (e.g., RA-2024-001).']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk assessment record.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Methodology used for the risk assessment. JSA (Job Safety Analysis), TRA (Task Risk Assessment), HIRA (Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment), HAZOP (Hazard and Operability Study), FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis), Bow Tie (Bow Tie Analysis).']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person who conducted the risk assessment.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or context relevant to the risk assessment, including consultation outcomes and worker participation details.']
  control_adequacy_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the implemented control measures are sufficient to manage the risk to an acceptable level.']
  control_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Highest level of control hierarchy applied per ISO 45001. PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) is the lowest level of control.']
  control_measures varchar [note: 'Detailed description of all control measures implemented to mitigate the risk, following the hierarchy of controls (elimination, substitution, engineering, administrative, PPE).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk assessment record was first created in the system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the risk assessment expires and must be renewed or superseded.']
  hazard_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the hazard type being assessed: Physical (noise, vibration, radiation), Chemical (dust, fumes, gases), Biological (bacteria, viruses), Ergonomic (manual handling, repetitive strain), Psychosocial (stress, fatigue), Environmental (spills, emissions).']
  hazard_source varchar [note: 'The origin or cause of the hazard (e.g., mobile equipment, explosives, confined space, high voltage, falling objects).']
  inherent_consequence_rating varchar [note: 'Severity of potential harm or loss before any control measures are applied, using a 5-level scale.']
  inherent_likelihood_rating varchar [note: 'Probability of the hazard occurring before any control measures are applied, using a 5-level scale.']
  inherent_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification before controls based on the inherent risk score and organizational risk matrix.']
  inherent_risk_score varchar [note: 'Calculated risk matrix score before controls (likelihood × consequence), typically ranging from 1 to 25.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk assessment record was last updated in the system.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review of this risk assessment.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of applicable mining safety regulations, MSHA (Mine Safety and Health Administration) standards, or jurisdictional requirements that govern this risk assessment.']
  residual_consequence_rating varchar [note: 'Severity of potential harm or loss after control measures are applied, using a 5-level scale.']
  residual_likelihood_rating varchar [note: 'Probability of the hazard occurring after control measures are applied, using a 5-level scale.']
  residual_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification after controls based on the residual risk score and organizational risk matrix.']
  residual_risk_score varchar [note: 'Calculated risk matrix score after controls (likelihood × consequence), typically ranging from 1 to 25.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Required interval in months for periodic review and revalidation of the risk assessment.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person who reviewed the risk assessment.']
  risk_acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Whether the residual risk level is accepted by management or requires further treatment.']
  work_area varchar [note: 'Specific work area, pit, underground level, processing plant section, or operational zone where the assessed hazard is present.']

  Note: 'Master record of hazard identification, the enterprise-wide hazard register, and formal risk assessments conducted across mine operations. Maintains the complete hazard inventory covering physical, chemical, biological, ergonomic, and psychosocial hazards with hazard category, source, affected work areas, and control hierarchy (elimination through PPE). Supports Job Safety Analysis (JSA), Task Risk Assessment (TRA), and Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA) methodologies. Captures likelihood rating, consequence rating, risk matrix score (inherent and residual), control measures, control adequacy rating, review frequency, and approval status. SSOT for both hazard inventory and risk assessment records. Aligned with ISO 45001 Clause 6.1.'
}

  fatigue_indicator_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fatigue was identified as a contributing factor or declared by the worker (True = fatigue present, False = no fatigue).']

  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether further investigation or corrective action is required beyond immediate response (True = follow-up needed, False = resolved on site).']
  hazard_category varchar [note: 'Category of hazard or risk observed (e.g., fall from height, mobile equipment interaction, electrical, confined space, fatigue, ergonomic).']

  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the observation record was last updated.']
  leading_indicator_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the observation for leading indicator reporting and trend analysis (e.g., behavioral safety, housekeeping, equipment condition, procedural compliance).']
  likelihood_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the likelihood or probability of the observed hazard resulting in an incident.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific location within the site where the observation was made (e.g., pit floor, processing plant, ROM pad, workshop).']
  observation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of what was observed, including context, behavior, condition, and circumstances.']
  observation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the safety observation in the format OBS-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.']
  observation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the safety observation.']
  observation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the safety observation was made in the field.']
  observation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the observation: safe act, unsafe act, safe condition, unsafe condition, fatigue declaration, or positive recognition.']
  observed_person_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person observed (nullable for anonymous observations or condition-based observations).']
  observer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person who made the safety observation.']
  observer_role varchar [note: 'Role or position of the person who made the observation.']
  photo_attachment_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to photographic evidence captured at the time of observation.']
  ppe_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether proper PPE was being worn at the time of observation (True = compliant, False = non-compliant).']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Calculated risk score based on severity and likelihood ratings, used for prioritization.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the potential severity or consequence of the observed hazard or unsafe condition.']
  stop_work_authority_exercised_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stop work authority was exercised as a result of this observation (True = work stopped, False = work continued).']
  work_activity varchar [note: 'Specific work activity or task being performed at the time of observation (e.g., drilling blast holes, loading ore, equipment maintenance, sample collection).']
  work_area varchar [note: 'Functional work area or zone where the observation occurred (e.g., drilling, blasting, haulage, processing, maintenance).']

  Note: 'Records field-level safety observations, positive interactions, stop-work authority events, fatigue declarations, and behavioural safety interactions made by supervisors, safety officers, and workers during site operations. Captures observation type (safe/unsafe act, safe/unsafe condition, fatigue, positive recognition), observer, location, work activity, crew/shift, immediate action taken, and follow-up required. Feeds leading indicator reporting for TRIFR trend analysis and fatigue risk management programs.'
}

Table hse.environmental_monitoring {
  environmental_monitoring_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the environmental monitoring record.']

  delivery_destination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_destination. Business justification: Mining companies monitor environmental parameters (dust emissions, noise levels, water quality, air quality) at customer delivery points—especially ports and rail terminals—to comply with environmenta']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_permit. Business justification: Environmental monitoring activities are conducted to verify compliance with specific permit conditions and limits. Each monitoring record should reference the permit it is verifying against, creating ']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to an environmental incident record if this monitoring result is associated with an incident investigation. Null if not incident-related.']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory that performed the analysis. Null for field measurements.']
  notification_stakeholder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.stakeholder. Business justification: Monitoring points near communities require stakeholder notification when permit limits are exceeded (air quality, water quality, noise). Regulatory requirement in most mining jurisdictions and critica']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Monitoring points track environmental impacts from specific orebody extraction; acid mine drainage chemistry varies by ore mineralogy, dust composition by commodity, groundwater drawdown by mining met']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Product-specific environmental monitoring is standard (dust emissions from specific product stockpiles, runoff contamination from product storage areas).   sample_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical sample record in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS). Null for direct field measurements.']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site where environmental monitoring was conducted.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.warehouse_location. Business justification: Environmental monitoring points at or near storage facilities (dust, emissions, groundwater contamination from stored fuels/chemicals/reagents) require warehouse location linkage for compliance monito']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring equipment was last calibrated prior to this measurement.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations, anomalies, or contextual information about the monitoring event.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the monitoring result triggered a requirement for corrective action due to exceedance or other concern.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the monitoring data indicating how the measurement was captured.']
  exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measured value exceeded the permit limit. True if exceeded, False otherwise.']
  exceedance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by which the measurement exceeded the permit limit. Null if no exceedance occurred.']
  gis_latitude decimal(10,8) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the monitoring point in decimal degrees for spatial analysis and mapping.']
  gis_longitude decimal(11,8) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the monitoring point in decimal degrees for spatial analysis and mapping.']
  laboratory_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference or job number assigned by the laboratory for traceability. Null for field measurements.']
  measurement_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric value of the environmental measurement recorded.']
  monitoring_equipment_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the monitoring instrument or equipment used (e.g., air quality analyzer, water quality meter, noise meter).']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which this monitoring activity is performed as per permit or management plan requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continuous|hourly|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|annual|event_based — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  monitoring_point_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the specific monitoring location or station (e.g., surface water sampling point, air quality station, groundwater bore).']
  monitoring_point_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the monitoring location for business user reference.']
  monitoring_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the monitoring record in the workflow from sampling through validation.']
  monitoring_type varchar [note: 'Category of environmental monitoring activity being performed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: air_quality|water_quality_surface|water_quality_groundwater|noise|vibration|biodiversity|soil — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  parameter_measured varchar [note: 'Specific environmental parameter or pollutant being measured (e.g., PM10, PM2.5, pH, dissolved oxygen, total suspended solids, heavy metals, noise level dB).']
  permit_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Regulatory or permit-defined threshold limit for the measured parameter. Null if no limit applies.']
  record_created_datetime timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this environmental monitoring record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_datetime timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this environmental monitoring record was last modified.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this monitoring result must be included in regulatory compliance reporting to authorities.']
  sampled_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or team who collected the sample or performed the measurement.']
  sampling_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the environmental sample was collected or measurement was taken.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'Methodology or protocol used for sample collection or measurement (e.g., grab sample, composite sample, continuous monitoring, manual measurement).']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient or sample temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of measurement.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the measurement value is expressed (e.g., mg/L, µg/m³, dB, ppm, NTU).']
  validated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the environmental specialist who validated the monitoring result.']
  validation_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the monitoring result was validated.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Quality assurance validation status indicating whether the result has been reviewed and approved by environmental personnel.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions at the time of sampling or measurement (e.g., clear, rainy, windy) that may influence results.']
  wind_direction varchar [note: 'Cardinal direction from which the wind was blowing at the time of measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: N|NE|E|SE|S|SW|W|NW — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  wind_speed_mps decimal(5,2) [note: 'Wind speed in meters per second at the time of measurement. Relevant for air quality and dust monitoring.']

  Note: 'Records of environmental monitoring activities including air quality (dust, particulates), water quality (surface and groundwater), noise, vibration, and biodiversity monitoring at mine sites and surrounding areas. Captures monitoring point, parameter measured, measurement value, units, exceedance flags against permit limits, sampling method, and laboratory reference. Supports ISO 14001 and EIS compliance reporting.'
}

Table hse.environmental_permit {
  environmental_permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the environmental permit record. Primary key.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Environmental permits require ongoing compliance costs (permit fees, monitoring, reporting, financial assurance). Cost centre allocation enables tracking these regulatory costs against site budgets an']
  legal_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.legal_register. Business justification: Environmental permits are issued under specific legal and regulatory requirements documented in the legal register. Each permit should reference the legal obligation(s) it fulfills, creating traceabil']
  programme_of_work_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.programme_of_work. Business justification: Environmental permits authorize programme of work activities. Compliance verification, operational authorization, and regulatory tracking require this link. Mining operations obtain permits to execute']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mining site, operation, or facility to which this environmental permit applies. Links the permit to the physical location and operational context.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall assessment of whether the mining operation is currently meeting all conditions and obligations of this permit. Aggregated from individual permit condition compliance assessments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this permit record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the permit becomes legally binding and the mining operation is authorized to conduct the permitted activity.']
  enforcement_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of any regulatory enforcement action, penalty notice, improvement order, or formal warning issued by the regulatory authority in relation to this permit.']
  enforcement_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any regulatory enforcement action, penalty, or formal notice has been issued in relation to this permit. True if enforcement action is active or pending.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the permit expires and the authorization ceases unless renewed. Nullable for permits with indefinite duration subject to ongoing compliance.']
  financial_assurance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the financial assurance, bond, or guarantee required under the permit conditions. Expressed in the operating currency of the mining operation.']
  financial_assurance_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial assurance amount.']
  financial_assurance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the permit requires the mining operation to provide financial assurance, bond, or guarantee to cover potential environmental liabilities or rehabilitation costs.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the environmental permit was officially issued or granted by the regulatory authority.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the government agency, regulatory body, or environmental authority that issued the permit. Examples include state environmental protection agencies, water boards, or mining departments.']
  last_compliance_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent internal or external compliance assessment or audit conducted for this permit.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this permit record. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for environmental monitoring and measurement activities specified in the permit conditions. Determines the cadence of compliance data collection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continuous|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|annually|event_based — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  next_reporting_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next regulatory compliance report must be submitted to the issuing authority. Used for proactive compliance tracking and deadline management.']
  non_compliance_count varchar [note: 'Number of permit conditions currently assessed as non-compliant. Provides a quantitative indicator of compliance risk.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, historical notes, or operational considerations related to this permit.']
  permit_conditions_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of the key conditions, restrictions, and obligations imposed by the permit. Detailed condition-level tracking is maintained in a related permit conditions entity.']
  permit_document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or URL to the official permit document, licence certificate, or approval letter issued by the regulatory authority.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Official permit number or licence identifier issued by the regulatory authority. This is the externally-known unique identifier used in all regulatory correspondence and submissions.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the environmental permit. Indicates whether the permit is in force, requires renewal action, or has been terminated.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the environmental permit based on the regulated activity or environmental medium. Determines the applicable regulatory framework and monitoring requirements.']
  regulated_activity varchar [note: 'Description of the specific activity, discharge, emission, or environmental aspect that is authorized under this permit. Examples include water extraction, tailings discharge, air emissions from processing plant, or waste rock disposal.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the permit renewal application must be submitted to the regulatory authority to avoid lapse. Typically set in advance of the expiry date per regulatory requirements.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the permit renewal process. Tracks whether renewal action is required and the stage of the renewal application.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for submitting compliance reports to the regulatory authority as specified in the permit conditions.']
  responsible_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual, role, or department responsible for managing compliance with this permit. Accountable for monitoring, reporting, and renewal activities.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Name of the mining site, operation, or facility covered by this permit. Provides human-readable context for the permit location.']

  Note: 'Master register of all environmental permits, licences, and approvals held by the mining operation, including water licences, air emission permits, waste discharge approvals, and environmental protection orders. Records permit number, issuing authority, expiry date, renewal status, and associated monitoring requirements. Includes granular permit conditions with individual compliance obligations, monitoring frequencies, reporting deadlines, responsible owners, condition compliance status (compliant/non-compliant/under review), and evidence of compliance. SSOT for regulatory permit obligations and condition-level compliance tracking. Supports regulatory submissions and ISO 14001 compliance.'
}
