Table hse.ppe_issuance {
  ppe_issuance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the PPE issuance transaction record.']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: PPE issuance tracks unit_cost and total_cost with cost_centre_code but needs GL account for proper financial posting. Enables correct expense classification (PPE inventory vs. expense) and supports fi']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific PPE item from the master catalogue (hard hat, respirator, safety boots, etc.).']
  primary_ppe_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the worker or contractor to whom the PPE was issued.']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_master. Business justification: PPE items (hard hats, safety boots, respirators, gloves) are materials in procurement system. Linking issuance to material master enables PPE inventory management, cost allocation to cost centres, reo']
  acknowledgement_signature varchar [note: 'Digital or scanned signature of the worker acknowledging receipt of the PPE and understanding of its proper use and care requirements.']
  acknowledgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the worker signed the acknowledgement of PPE receipt and training.']
  actual_return_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the PPE was returned by the worker, used to track compliance and lifecycle completion.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturing batch or lot number of the PPE item, used for quality control and recall management.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is compliant with role-based PPE requirements following this issuance, used for ISO 45001 and MSHA audit readiness.']
  condition_at_issue varchar [note: 'The physical condition of the PPE at the time of issuance to the worker.']
  cost_centre_code varchar [note: 'The cost centre to which the PPE issuance cost is allocated, typically the workers department or project.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this issuance record was first created in the lakehouse, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost values (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).']
  expected_return_date date [note: 'The date by which the PPE is expected to be returned for inspection, replacement, or end of assignment, applicable to reusable or high-value items.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the issued PPE item reaches the end of its certified service life or manufacturer warranty, applicable to items such as respirator cartridges, harnesses, and helmets.']
  fit_test_date date [note: 'The date on which the worker completed a fit test for the issued PPE, applicable to respirators and breathing apparatus.']
  fit_test_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a fit test is required for the issued PPE (e.g., respirators), per regulatory and manufacturer requirements.']
  issuance_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique transaction number for the PPE issuance event, typically generated by IsoMetrix or stores system.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the PPE was issued to the worker.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the PPE was issued to the worker, captured from the stores or HSE system.']
  issuing_location varchar [note: 'The mine site, warehouse, or stores location from which the PPE was issued.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this issuance record was last updated in the lakehouse, used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the issuing officer regarding special circumstances, worker requests, or equipment condition observations at the time of issuance.']
  quantity_issued varchar [note: 'The number of units of the PPE item issued in this transaction.']
  replacement_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for issuing replacement PPE, used for cost tracking, compliance reporting, and trend analysis.']
  return_condition varchar [note: 'The physical condition of the PPE at the time of return, used to determine whether it can be reissued, requires repair, or must be disposed of.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'The unique serial or asset number of the specific PPE unit issued, used for tracking high-value or calibrated equipment such as gas detectors or respirators.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this issuance record in the source operational system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this issuance record originated (e.g., IsoMetrix, Pronto Xi, SAP MM).']
  total_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total cost of the PPE issuance transaction, calculated as quantity issued multiplied by unit cost.']
  training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker has completed mandatory training on the proper use, maintenance, and limitations of the issued PPE.']
  training_date date [note: 'The date on which the worker completed PPE-specific training, used for compliance verification and audit trails.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The cost per unit of the PPE item at the time of issuance, used for financial tracking and OPEX reporting.']

  Note: 'Manages the complete Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) lifecycle including the master catalogue of approved PPE types (hard hats, respirators, hearing protection, safety boots, high-visibility garments, gas detection equipment) with standards/certifications (AS/NZS, ANSI), applicable hazard classes, inspection frequencies, service life, and approved suppliers. Tracks transactional issuance of PPE to individual workers and contractors capturing PPE type, quantity issued, issue date, condition at issue, return date, and reason for replacement. Monitors PPE compliance per worker against role-based requirements. SSOT for PPE catalogue, issuance records, and compliance tracking supporting ISO 45001 and MSHA inspections.'
}

Table hse.health_surveillance {
  health_surveillance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the health surveillance record. Primary key for the health surveillance data product.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Health surveillance programs (medical examinations, audiometry, spirometry, biological monitoring) incur significant costs that must be allocated to site cost centres for budget planning, variance tra']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine worker who is the subject of this health surveillance record. Links to workforce master data.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the HSE incident record if this health surveillance assessment was triggered by a specific workplace incident or exposure event. Links to incident management data.']
  action_level_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Action level threshold value that triggers additional monitoring, medical surveillance, or control measures. Typically set at 50% of the OEL/PEL or as defined by regulatory standards.']
  baseline_test_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a baseline health surveillance test conducted at the start of employment or upon first exposure to a specific hazard. Baseline tests establish reference values for future comparison.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments, observations, or notes related to the health surveillance test, examination findings, or follow-up actions. May include clinical observations or contextual information.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this health surveillance record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for record creation tracking.']
  examining_physician_name varchar [note: 'Name of the occupational physician or medical professional who conducted the health surveillance examination or reviewed the exposure monitoring results.']
  exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the measured value exceeded the applicable OEL, PEL, or action level threshold. True indicates an exceedance requiring follow-up action.']
  exposure_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Duration of exposure to the occupational hazard during the monitoring period, measured in hours. Used for calculating time-weighted average exposures and cumulative dose assessments.']
  fitness_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the worker\'s fitness for work based on the medical examination or health assessment. Determines whether the worker can perform their assigned duties without restrictions, with restrictions, or is unfit for work.']
  follow_up_action varchar [note: 'Follow-up action required based on the health surveillance results. May include repeat testing, referral to specialist, workplace exposure assessment, engineering controls implementation, or administrative controls.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the follow-up action must be completed. Used for tracking compliance with medical surveillance schedules and ensuring timely follow-up on abnormal findings.']
  hazard_type varchar [note: 'Type of occupational hazard being monitored or assessed. Includes noise exposure, airborne dust (respirable silica, coal dust, diesel particulate matter), chemical exposure, biological agents, ergonomic risks, and heat stress.']
  hearing_protection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether hearing protection (Personal Protective Equipment - PPE) is required for this worker based on noise exposure monitoring or audiometric test results. True indicates mandatory hearing protection.']
  incident_related_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this health surveillance record is related to a specific workplace incident, injury, or exposure event requiring immediate medical assessment.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this health surveillance record was last modified or updated. Audit trail field for tracking record changes and medical review updates.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the surveillance measurement value. Common units: dB (decibels for audiometry), dB TWA (time-weighted average noise exposure), mg/m³ (airborne dust concentration), µg/dL (blood lead level), L (liters for spirometry volume), L/min (spirometry flow rate), percent (lung function percentage), mmHg (blood pressure). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dB|dB_TWA|mg_m3|ug_dL|L|L_min|percent|mmHg — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  measurement_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantitative result of the health surveillance test or exposure monitoring. For audiometry: hearing threshold in dB; for spirometry: lung function values; for noise dosimetry: dB TWA (Time-Weighted Average); for dust sampling: concentration in mg/m³; for blood lead: µg/dL.']
  medical_facility_name varchar [note: 'Name of the medical facility, clinic, or occupational health service provider where the health surveillance test or examination was conducted.']
  next_test_due_date date [note: 'Date when the next health surveillance test or assessment is due for this worker. Calculated based on test frequency requirements and used for scheduling periodic medical examinations.']
  oel_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Occupational Exposure Limit (OEL) or Permissible Exposure Limit (PEL) threshold value applicable to this hazard type and measurement. Regulatory limit against which the measurement is compared to determine compliance.']
  periodic_examination_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a periodic (routine) medical examination conducted at regular intervals as part of the ongoing health surveillance program.']
  physician_recommendation varchar [note: 'Recommendation provided by the occupational physician or medical professional based on the health surveillance results. May include follow-up testing, work restrictions, referral to specialist, or clearance for duty.']
  ppe_type_required varchar [note: 'Type of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) required based on the health surveillance findings. May include hearing protection (earplugs, earmuffs), respiratory protection (dust masks, respirators), or other protective equipment.']
  pre_employment_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this health surveillance record is part of a pre-employment medical examination conducted before the worker commences employment at the mine site.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the health surveillance record. Tracks the record through draft, completion, medical review, approval, and archival stages.']
  regulatory_submission_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this health surveillance record must be submitted to regulatory authorities such as MSHA or state mining regulators as part of mandatory health reporting requirements.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when this health surveillance record was submitted to regulatory authorities for compliance reporting purposes.']
  surveillance_type varchar [note: 'Type of health surveillance or medical assessment conducted. Discriminates between audiometric testing, spirometry, blood lead testing, medical examinations, fitness-for-work assessments, and exposure monitoring.']
  test_date date [note: 'Date when the health surveillance test, medical examination, or exposure monitoring was conducted. Principal business event timestamp for the surveillance record.']
  test_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Required frequency for this type of health surveillance test or assessment, measured in months. Determines the interval between periodic medical examinations or exposure monitoring based on regulatory requirements and risk assessment.']
  test_location varchar [note: 'Location where the health surveillance test or assessment was conducted. May be mine site medical facility, external clinic, or field location for exposure monitoring.']
  work_restrictions varchar [note: 'Specific work restrictions or limitations imposed based on the health surveillance findings. May include restrictions on noise exposure, dust exposure, heavy lifting, confined space entry, or specific tasks.']

  Note: 'Records of occupational health surveillance, fitness-for-work assessments, and exposure monitoring for mine workers. Covers audiometric testing, spirometry, personal noise dosimetry (dB TWA), airborne dust sampling (respirable silica, coal dust, DPM in mg/m³), blood lead levels, heat stress assessments, fatigue alertness testing (PVT), and pre-employment/periodic medical examinations. Captures worker reference, test/monitoring type, hazard type discriminator (noise, dust, chemical, biological, ergonomic), measurement value, units, exceedance flags against OEL/PEL/action levels, exposure duration, fitness classification, occupational physician recommendation, restrictions, hearing protection requirements, and follow-up actions. Supports MSHA health and noise standards, Coal Mine Health and Safety Act, and ISO 45001 worker health monitoring.'
}

Table hse.emergency_response_plan {
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the emergency response plan record. Primary key.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Capital projects require construction-phase emergency response plans distinct from operational plans, covering construction scenarios, contractor coordination, and temporary facilities. Regulatory app']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mine site to which this emergency response plan applies.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the emergency response plan was formally approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the emergency response plan by the designated authority.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name and title of the person who approved this emergency response plan, typically the site manager or HSE manager.']
  assembly_point_primary varchar [note: 'Primary designated assembly point location where personnel should gather during an emergency evacuation.']
  assembly_point_secondary varchar [note: 'Secondary or alternate assembly point location to be used if the primary assembly point is compromised or inaccessible.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this emergency response plan record was first created in the system.']
  document_location varchar [note: 'Physical or digital location where the full emergency response plan document is stored and accessible.']
  drill_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of emergency drill frequency against regulatory and corporate requirements.']
  drill_frequency_required_months varchar [note: 'Required frequency for emergency drills or exercises in months, as mandated by regulation or corporate policy.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this emergency response plan becomes effective and operational.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this emergency response plan expires or is superseded. Nullable for plans with no defined expiry.']
  emergency_contact_primary varchar [note: 'Primary emergency contact person and phone number for this plan, typically the site emergency coordinator or incident controller.']
  emergency_contact_secondary varchar [note: 'Secondary or backup emergency contact person and phone number for this plan.']
  equipment_resources varchar [note: 'List of emergency equipment and resources required for this plan, including location, quantity, and maintenance status (e.g., self-rescuers, fire extinguishers, spill kits, medical supplies).']
  external_agency_contacts varchar [note: 'Contact details for external emergency response agencies including fire brigade, ambulance, police, environmental protection, and mine rescue services.']
  last_drill_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent emergency drill or exercise for this plan was conducted.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this emergency response plan record was last modified or updated.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the emergency response plan was last reviewed for accuracy, completeness, and relevance.']
  next_drill_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory emergency drill or exercise for this plan.']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review of the emergency response plan, typically annual or as required by regulation.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the emergency response plan, typically following a site-specific naming convention.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the emergency response plan, identifying the scenario or scope covered.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the emergency response plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the emergency response plan by scope and applicability.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory requirement or standard that mandates this emergency response plan (e.g., MSHA 30 CFR Part 49, ISO 45001 Clause 8.2).']
  response_procedures varchar [note: 'Detailed step-by-step response procedures to be followed when the emergency scenario occurs, including immediate actions, escalation protocols, and containment measures.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Required frequency for plan review in months, as mandated by regulation or corporate policy.']
  revision_notes varchar [note: 'Summary of changes made in the current version of the emergency response plan, documenting updates, corrections, or enhancements.']
  scenario_category varchar [note: 'Primary emergency scenario category that this plan addresses, aligned with mining-specific hazards.']
  scenario_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the emergency scenario, including triggering conditions, potential impacts, and scope of the emergency.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the emergency response plan document, following a major.minor versioning convention.']

  Note: 'Master register of emergency response plans (ERPs) and drill/exercise records for mine sites. Covers scenarios such as underground entrapment, explosives incidents, tailings dam failure, chemical spills, fire, and medical emergencies. Records plan type, site applicability, scenario description, response procedures, assembly points, emergency contacts, equipment resources, last review date, and approval status. Includes drill and exercise records capturing drill type, scenario tested, participants, date, duration, performance observations, debrief findings, and improvement actions. Tracks drill frequency compliance against ERP requirements and regulatory mandates. SSOT for emergency preparedness and drill compliance. Supports ISO 45001 Clause 8.2 and MSHA emergency response requirements.'
}

  training_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for training_record']

Handover certificates require confirm']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Commodity-specific safety training is standard in mining (lithium handling procedures, coal dust management, asbestos awareness for certain ore types). Regulatory compliance requires commodity-level c']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Training records track cost_amount and cost_currency but need cost centre allocation for budget tracking. Essential for managing training budgets, variance analysis, and ensuring training costs are pr']

Business justification: Mining companies provide safety training to customer personnel (stevedores, vessel crew, logistics staff) on loading procedures, emergency response, and hazard awareness. Tracking the counterparty rec']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: Training records for emergency response drills, exercises, and competency assessments should link to the specific emergency response plan being trained on. This creates traceability between plan requi']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Training costs must be posted to appropriate GL accounts for expense classification (training expense vs. capex for major competency programs). Enables proper financial reporting, opex/capex split, an']
  primary_training_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine worker or contractor who received the training.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: External training providers are vendors. Linking training records to vendor master enables vendor-delivered training cost allocation, vendor performance evaluation for training services, contract mana']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Product-specific handling training is mandatory for hazmat-classified products, moisture-sensitive materials (TML training), and products with special packaging requirements. Mining operations track c']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or facility where the training was delivered or is applicable.']
  training_course_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the HSE training program or course catalog entry.']
  assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training requires a formal assessment or examination for completion.']
  assessment_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the training assessment: pass (met competency standard), fail (did not meet standard), not_assessed (no assessment required), or pending (assessment scheduled but not completed).']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved in the training assessment, typically expressed as a percentage.']
  attendance_status varchar [note: 'Participant attendance outcome: attended (full participation), absent (did not attend), partial (attended part of session), or excused (authorized absence).']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the training certificate expires and refresher training is required.']
  certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the training certificate was issued.']
  certificate_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a training certificate or competency card was issued to the participant upon successful completion.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the training certificate or competency card issued.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or observations recorded by the trainer or training coordinator regarding the session or participant performance.']
  competency_level varchar [note: 'Level of competency achieved or required for this training: basic (foundational), intermediate (operational), advanced (supervisory), or expert (specialist/trainer).']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the participant successfully completed the training.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the training was completed, used for audit trail and compliance verification.']
  cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for delivering this training session, including trainer fees, materials, and facility costs.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the training cost amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this training record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Mode of training delivery: classroom (in-person instruction), online (e-learning), on_the_job (practical field training), simulation (virtual or physical simulator), or blended (combination of methods).']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total duration of the training session in hours.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this training record was last updated.']
  pass_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to pass the training assessment, expressed as a percentage.']
  refresher_due_date date [note: 'Date by which refresher training must be completed to maintain competency and compliance.']
  regulatory_requirement varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory or statutory requirement that mandates this training (e.g., MSHA Part 46, ISO 45001 clause, state mining act section).']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Date on which the training session was scheduled to occur.']
  trainer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the instructor or facilitator who delivered the training.']
  training_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the training course in the HSE training catalog.']
  training_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the training was conducted (e.g., training room, online platform, field site).']
  training_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training record: scheduled (planned but not started), in_progress (underway), completed (successfully finished), cancelled (did not proceed), or expired (certificate validity lapsed).']
  training_title varchar [note: 'Full name of the HSE training course or module delivered.']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Category of HSE training delivered: induction (site orientation), statutory (legally mandated such as first aid, warden, explosives handling), refresher (periodic recertification), competency (skill validation), emergency_response (evacuation, rescue), or ppe (Personal Protective Equipment usage).']
  validity_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months for which the training certification remains valid before refresher training is required.']

  Note: 'Records of HSE-specific training and competency activities delivered to mine workers and contractors, including inductions, statutory training (first aid, warden, explosives handling), and refresher courses. Captures training type, delivery method, trainer, participant reference, completion date, assessment result, certificate expiry, and regulatory requirement linkage. Distinct from general workforce training — focused on HSE compliance obligations.'
}

Table hse.chemical_register {
  chemical_register_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the chemical register entry. Primary key for the chemical register product.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Chemical register tracks inventory quantities and storage locations. Chemical inventory holding costs, handling costs, and disposal costs must be allocated to the site cost centre responsible for the ']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Regulatory requirement to maintain chemical supplier information for SDS management, emergency contact during chemical incidents, supply chain transparency, and vendor HSE performance tracking. Remove']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Some saleable products are hazardous materials requiring chemical registration (lithium concentrate, uranium oxide, certain coal products). Mining operations must maintain SDS and GHS classification f']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mine site where the chemical is used, stored, or generated.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique numerical identifier assigned by the Chemical Abstracts Service to every chemical substance described in the open scientific literature.']
  chemical_name varchar [note: 'Common or trade name of the hazardous chemical or substance as used in operations (e.g., Sodium Cyanide, Sulphuric Acid, Xanthate, ANFO).']
  chemical_register_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the chemical in the register (active = currently in use, inactive = no longer used but may have residual inventory, restricted = use limited by permit or policy, phased_out = scheduled for elimination, pending_approval = awaiting regulatory or management approval).']
  chemical_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the chemical based on its primary use or function in mining operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reagent|explosive_precursor|fuel|solvent|acid|base|oxidizer|process_chemical — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the chemical register record was first created in the system.']
  current_inventory_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Current quantity of the chemical on hand at the site, as of the last inventory count or system update.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Approved method for disposal of the chemical or its waste residues (e.g., Licensed Hazardous Waste Contractor, Neutralization and Discharge, Return to Supplier, Incineration).']
  emergency_contact_number varchar [note: '24-hour emergency contact phone number for the chemical supplier or emergency response service (e.g., CHEMTREC, supplier hotline).']
  environmental_impact_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing known or potential environmental impacts of the chemical, including aquatic toxicity, persistence, bioaccumulation, or soil contamination risks.']
  first_registered_date date [note: 'Date when the chemical was first registered in the site chemical register.']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'Hazard classification code(s) according to the GHS standard (e.g., Acute Toxicity Category 2, Corrosive to Metals Category 1, Environmental Hazard Acute Category 1).']
  ghs_pictogram_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of GHS pictogram codes representing hazard symbols (e.g., GHS05 for Corrosion, GHS06 for Skull and Crossbones, GHS09 for Environment).']
  handling_procedure_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or title of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing safe handling, use, and storage of this chemical.']
  hazard_statement_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of H-codes describing the nature of hazards (e.g., H290 May be corrosive to metals, H314 Causes severe skin burns and eye damage, H400 Very toxic to aquatic life).']
  health_hazard_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of primary health hazards associated with exposure to this chemical (e.g., Corrosive to skin and eyes, Respiratory irritant, Carcinogen Category 1A, Acute toxicity if ingested).']
  incompatible_materials varchar [note: 'List of materials or chemical classes that must not be stored or mixed with this chemical due to risk of dangerous reactions (e.g., Strong oxidizers, Acids, Alkalis, Water-reactive substances).']
  inventory_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maximum inventory quantity (e.g., kilograms, litres, tonnes, cubic metres, units).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the chemical register record was last updated or modified.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the chemical register entry was last reviewed for accuracy and compliance.']
  maximum_inventory_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity of the chemical permitted to be stored on site at any time, as defined by permit conditions or internal risk assessments.']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review of this chemical register entry.']
  permit_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the environmental or operational permit authorizing the use or storage of this chemical at the site.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Mandatory personal protective equipment required when handling this chemical (e.g., Chemical-resistant gloves, Face shield, Respirator with organic vapor cartridge, Acid-resistant apron).']
  precautionary_statement_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of P-codes describing recommended measures to minimize or prevent adverse effects (e.g., P280 Wear protective gloves, P305+P351+P338 IF IN EYES rinse cautiously with water).']
  regulatory_threshold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemical quantity exceeds regulatory reporting thresholds (e.g., EPCRA Section 312 Tier II, state environmental reporting).']
  regulatory_threshold_type varchar [note: 'Description of the specific regulatory threshold or reporting requirement triggered (e.g., EPCRA Tier II Reporting, State Hazardous Substance Inventory).']
  sds_reference_number varchar [note: 'Document control number or identifier for the current Safety Data Sheet (formerly MSDS) on file for this chemical.']
  sds_revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revision of the Safety Data Sheet for this chemical.']
  storage_area_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storage facility based on its design and environmental controls.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility code where the chemical is stored at the mine site (e.g., Reagent Storage Shed A, Explosives Magazine 3, Fuel Farm).']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit number that identifies hazardous substances in the framework of international transport (e.g., UN1789 for Hydrochloric Acid).']
  usage_purpose varchar [note: 'Description of the primary operational purpose or process for which the chemical is used (e.g., Gold leaching, Flotation reagent, Blasting agent, Equipment lubrication, Dust suppression).']
  waste_classification_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory waste classification code for disposal purposes (e.g., EPA Hazardous Waste Code, Basel Convention Code).']

  Note: 'Master register of all hazardous chemicals and substances used, stored, or generated at mine sites, including reagents (cyanide, sulphuric acid, xanthates), explosives precursors, fuels, and process chemicals. Records chemical name, CAS number, SDS reference, hazard classification (GHS), storage location, maximum inventory quantity, regulatory threshold flags, and disposal requirements. SSOT for chemical inventory supporting ISO 14001 and MSHA chemical standards.'
}

  environmental_permit_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether environmental permit conditions were verified during the audit or inspection.']

  inspection_frequency varchar [note: 'Planned frequency of the inspection or audit (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual, ad-hoc).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit or inspection record was last updated in the system.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead auditor or inspector responsible for conducting the assessment.']
  major_nonconformance_count varchar [note: 'Number of major non-conformances (critical failures to meet requirements) identified during the audit.']
  minor_nonconformance_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor non-conformances (less critical failures to meet requirements) identified during the audit.']
  observation_count varchar [note: 'Number of observations (opportunities for improvement that are not non-conformances) identified during the audit.']
  ppe_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether personal protective equipment compliance was verified during the inspection.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority conducting or requiring the audit or inspection (e.g., MSHA, state mining department).']
  report_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the formal audit or inspection report document.']
  report_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal audit or inspection report was issued to stakeholders.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for the audit or inspection to commence.']
  standard_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the standard or regulatory framework against which the audit or inspection was conducted (e.g., ISO 45001:2018, ISO 14001:2015, MSHA 30 CFR Part 56).']
  team_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of names or identifiers of additional auditors or inspectors participating in the assessment.']
  total_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of findings (conformances, non-conformances, observations) identified during the audit or inspection.']

  Note: 'Records of all formal HSE management system audits and field-level compliance assessments including internal audits, third-party certification audits (ISO 45001, ISO 14001), regulatory compliance audits, ICMM performance audits, planned workplace inspections, pre-start equipment checks, statutory inspections, and unplanned field inspections. Captures assessment type discriminator (audit/inspection), scope, assessor/inspector details, checklist items, findings (conformances, non-conformances, observations), corrective action requirements, and certification outcome where applicable. SSOT for all HSE compliance verification activities. Supports MSHA inspection readiness, ISO 45001 internal audit programs, and field-level compliance verification.'
}

Table hse.legal_register {
  legal_register_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for legal_register']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Legal register tracks compliance obligations that incur costs (legal advice, compliance activities, permit fees, reporting). Cost centre allocation enables tracking legal compliance costs against resp']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mine site or facility to which this legal requirement applies.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or update to the legislation that affects this compliance obligation.']
  amendment_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the most recent amendment or change to the legislation and its impact on compliance obligations.']
  applicability_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or context regarding the applicability of this legal requirement to the specific site, operation, or activity.']
  compliance_evidence_location varchar [note: 'Reference to the location or system where evidence of compliance with this legal requirement is stored (e.g., document management system path, IsoMetrix record ID, file server location).']
  compliance_obligation_summary varchar [note: 'Concise summary of the specific compliance obligation or requirement imposed by the legislation on the mine site (e.g., submit annual environmental report, maintain dust monitoring records, conduct quarterly safety audits).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the site against this legal requirement (compliant, non-compliant, under review, not applicable, pending).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this legal register entry was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the legislation or legal requirement came into force and became applicable to the site.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this legal requirement is currently active and applicable (true) or has been superseded, repealed, or is no longer applicable (false).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or administrative jurisdiction where the legislation applies (e.g., Queensland, Western Australia, Federal, New South Wales).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this legal requirement was last reviewed for applicability and compliance status.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this legal register entry was last modified or updated.']
  legislation_name varchar [note: 'Full name or title of the applicable legislation, act, regulation, code of practice, or standard (e.g., Mine Safety and Health Act 1996, Environmental Protection Act 1994).']
  legislation_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number, citation, or identifier for the legislation (e.g., Act No. 82 of 1996, Regulation 2019/123).']
  legislation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal instrument (act, regulation, code of practice, standard, licence condition, permit condition).']
  legislation_url varchar [note: 'Web link or URL to the official source or full text of the legislation for reference.']
  licence_number varchar [note: 'Mining licence, environmental permit, or authorization number to which this legal requirement is attached (if applicable).']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Description of penalties, fines, or sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance with this legal requirement.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the government agency or regulatory authority responsible for administering and enforcing the legislation (e.g., Mine Safety and Health Administration (MSHA), Department of Environment and Science, Resources Safety and Health Queensland).']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which compliance reporting or submission is required under this legal obligation (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annually, as required, not applicable). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|annually|as_required|not_applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  responsible_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person, role, or department responsible for ensuring compliance with this legal requirement (e.g., HSE Manager, Environmental Superintendent, Site Manager).']
  review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review of this legal requirement to ensure continued applicability and compliance.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to non-compliance with this legal requirement based on potential impact to health, safety, environment, or business operations (critical, high, medium, low).']
  superseded_by_legislation varchar [note: 'Name or reference of the legislation that has superseded or replaced this legal requirement (if applicable).']

  Note: 'Master register of applicable HSE legal and regulatory requirements for each mine site and jurisdiction, including acts, regulations, codes of practice, standards, and licence conditions. Records legislation name, jurisdiction, regulatory body, applicable site, compliance obligation summary, review date, compliance status, and responsible owner. SSOT for legal compliance obligations supporting ISO 45001 Clause 6.1.3 and ISO 14001 Clause 6.1.3.'
}

Table hse.management_of_change {
  management_of_change_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Management of Change request record. Primary key.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the authorized manager or executive who granted final approval for the change.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: MOC process is triggered by project design changes, scope modifications, or commissioning changes affecting HSE risk profile. PSSR requires MOC closure verification. Standard mining project change man']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: MOC tracks estimated_cost and actual_cost in currency_code but needs cost centre allocation for budget approval workflows, variance tracking, and financial accountability. Essential for managing chang']
  primary_management_initiator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who originated the MOC request.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HSE or technical specialist currently reviewing the MOC request.']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the formal risk assessment conducted to evaluate HSE hazards introduced or modified by this change.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: MOC processes are triggered by changes to product specifications, blending recipes, or handling procedures. Mining operations require risk assessment and approval workflow for any product-related chan']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_specification. Business justification: Product specification changes are a primary MOC trigger in mining. Direct link to the specific spec revision enables traceability of HSE risk assessments, training updates, and regulatory notification']
  superseded_management_of_change_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on management_of_change (superseded_management_of_change_id)']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mine site or facility where the change will be implemented.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: When MOC involves capital projects (equipment modifications, facility upgrades, process changes requiring capex), costs must be tracked against WBS elements for proper capex accounting, project cost c']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final actual cost incurred for implementing the change.']
  affected_area varchar [note: 'Specific operational area, work zone, or department impacted by the change (e.g., underground stope, processing plant, tailings storage facility, maintenance workshop).']
  affected_process varchar [note: 'Name or code of the operational process being modified (e.g., blasting sequence, ventilation system, ore crushing, chemical leaching, ground support installation).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the MOC request received final authorization to proceed with implementation.']
  approval_workflow_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the multi-level approval process (e.g., supervisor review, HSE review, engineering review, site manager approval, general manager approval).']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized manager or executive who granted final approval for the change.']
  change_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of what is being changed: equipment (machinery, tools), process (operational workflow), chemical (hazardous substances), organizational (structure, roles), facility (infrastructure, buildings), or procedure (Standard Operating Procedure / SOP).']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the proposed modification to mine operations, equipment, processes, chemicals, or organizational structures, including scope, rationale, and expected outcomes.']
  change_title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title summarizing the proposed change for quick identification and reporting.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change duration and intent: temporary (time-limited), permanent (indefinite), emergency (immediate safety response), or trial (pilot/testing phase).']
  closure_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the MOC was formally verified as complete, all actions closed, and the change fully integrated into normal operations.']
  closure_verified_by varchar [note: 'Name of the HSE or operations manager who verified and authorized closure of the MOC.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or context relevant to the MOC request, review, or implementation.']
  control_measures varchar [note: 'Summary of risk controls and mitigation actions to be implemented as part of the change, following hierarchy of controls (elimination, substitution, engineering, administrative, Personal Protective Equipment / PPE).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the MOC record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost amounts (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD, ZAR).']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected total cost of implementing the change, including materials, labor, equipment, and downtime.']
  hazard_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of hazard types introduced or affected by the change (e.g., ground instability, chemical exposure, confined space, electrical, mobile equipment interaction).']
  implementation_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when all implementation activities were completed and the change became operational.']
  implementation_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed plan outlining steps, resources, timeline, and responsibilities for executing the approved change.']
  implementation_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when implementation activities commenced.']
  inherent_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk severity rating before implementation of control measures, derived from the linked risk assessment.']
  initiator_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area of the initiating employee (e.g., Mine Operations, Processing, Maintenance, Engineering).']
  initiator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who originated the MOC request.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the MOC record was last updated.']
  moc_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the MOC request, used for tracking and communication across departments and regulatory submissions.']
  moc_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MOC request in the approval and implementation workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|implementation|completed|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pssr_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the Pre-Startup Safety Review was completed and the change was verified safe to commission.']
  pssr_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the Pre-Startup Safety Review: passed (safe to commission), failed (deficiencies must be corrected), or conditional (minor items to be closed post-startup).']
  pssr_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Pre-Startup Safety Review is mandated before the change can be commissioned, typically required for high-risk modifications to critical systems (ventilation, ground support, blasting, chemical processes).']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory authority was formally notified of the change.']
  regulatory_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change must be reported to regulatory authorities (e.g., Mine Safety and Health Administration / MSHA, state mining inspectorate) due to its nature or risk level.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided if the MOC request was rejected, including specific concerns or deficiencies that must be addressed.']
  required_implementation_date date [note: 'Target date by which the change must be implemented to meet operational or regulatory requirements.']
  residual_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk severity rating after implementation of proposed control measures, derived from the linked risk assessment.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the technical or HSE review was completed.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the HSE or technical specialist currently reviewing the MOC request.']
  sop_updated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether relevant Standard Operating Procedures have been revised to reflect the change.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the MOC request was formally submitted for review and approval.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when all required training for affected personnel was completed.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether workforce training or competency verification is required before the change can be implemented.']

  Note: 'Master record of formal Management of Change (MOC) requests for modifications to mine operations, equipment, processes, chemicals, or organizational structures that may introduce new HSE risks. Captures change description, change type (temporary/permanent), risk assessment linkage, affected areas, approval workflow status, implementation plan, pre-startup safety review, and closure verification. Required by ICMM Performance Expectations, ISO 45001 Clause 8.1.3, and critical for mining operations where changes to ventilation, ground support, blasting patterns, or chemical processes can introduce catastrophic risk.'
}

Table hse.investigation {
  investigation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for investigation']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or HSE authority who approved and signed off on the investigation report.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee assigned as the primary investigator responsible for leading the investigation.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the HSE incident that triggered this investigation.']
  related_investigation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on investigation (related_investigation_id)']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mining site or facility where the investigated event occurred.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when the investigation was completed and findings were finalized.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date when the investigation report was formally approved by management.']
  closed_date date [note: 'The date when the investigation was formally closed after all corrective actions were verified.']
  contributing_factors varchar [note: 'Description of additional factors that contributed to the incident or event but were not the primary cause.']
  corrective_actions_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective actions are required as a result of this investigation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this investigation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated cost impact (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level of the investigation data for access control and information security purposes.']
  downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total hours of operational downtime caused by the incident being investigated.']
  environmental_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident had an environmental impact requiring investigation under ISO 14001 or environmental regulations.']
  estimated_cost_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total financial impact of the incident including damage, downtime, remediation, and investigation costs.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Executive summary of the key findings from the investigation.']
  immediate_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the immediate or direct cause(s) that led to the incident or event.']
  initiated_date date [note: 'The date when the investigation was formally initiated.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the investigation was formally initiated.']
  investigation_method varchar [note: 'The structured investigation methodology or framework applied to analyze the event.']
  investigation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the investigation, formatted as INV-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the investigation in its workflow lifecycle.']
  investigation_team_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of employee names or identifiers who are part of the investigation team.']
  investigation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigation based on the triggering event or purpose.']
  is_recordable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident is recordable under MSHA or OSHA regulations for LTIFR/TRIFR tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this investigation record was last updated or modified.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Key lessons learned from the investigation that should be shared across the organization for continuous improvement.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the specific location within the site where the event occurred (e.g., pit level, processing plant area, haul road section).']
  recommendations varchar [note: 'Detailed recommendations for corrective and preventive actions arising from the investigation findings.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'The date when regulatory authorities were notified of the investigation findings, if applicable.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigation findings require notification to regulatory authorities such as MSHA or environmental agencies.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the regulatory authority for this investigation or incident report.']
  report_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the full investigation report document stored in the document management system.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the underlying root cause(s) identified through systematic analysis.']
  severity_classification varchar [note: 'Severity rating assigned to the investigation based on actual or potential consequences of the triggering event.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'The planned or required date by which the investigation should be completed.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for investigation. Referenced by investigation_id.'
}

Table hse.hazard {
  hazard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for hazard']
  parent_hazard_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on hazard (parent_hazard_id)']
  affected_job_roles varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of the job roles, occupations, or worker categories that are exposed to or may encounter this hazard during their work activities (e.g., \'drill operators\', \'blasters\', \'maintenance technicians\', \'haul truck drivers\').']
  affected_work_areas varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of the specific work areas, zones, or operational locations within the mine site where this hazard is present or may be encountered (e.g., \'underground stopes\', \'processing plant\', \'haul roads\', \'maintenance workshop\').']
  control_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The primary level of control applied to manage this hazard according to the hierarchy of controls: elimination (remove hazard), substitution (replace with less hazardous), engineering controls (isolate people from hazard), administrative controls (change work practices), or personal protective equipment (PPE).']
  control_measures varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific control measures, safeguards, or mitigation strategies implemented to reduce or eliminate the risk associated with this hazard.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this hazard record was first created in the HSE management system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this hazard definition and its associated controls became effective and applicable in the organization\'s health and safety management system.']
  emergency_response_procedure varchar [note: 'Summary or reference to the emergency response procedures, first aid measures, or incident response protocols to be followed if an incident involving this hazard occurs.']
  exposure_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum permissible exposure level or threshold limit value for this hazard, including units of measure (e.g., \'0.05 mg/m³ for respirable crystalline silica\', \'85 dBA for noise\'). Applicable primarily to chemical, physical, and environmental hazards.']
  gri_disclosure_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the applicable GRI disclosure standard or indicator related to this hazard, used for sustainability and ESG reporting (e.g., \'GRI 403: Occupational Health and Safety\').']
  hazard_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the hazard into major categories: physical (noise, vibration, radiation), chemical (dust, fumes, gases), biological (bacteria, viruses), ergonomic (repetitive strain, manual handling), psychosocial (stress, fatigue), or environmental (spills, emissions).']
  hazard_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the hazard type for reporting and classification purposes. Follows organizational hazard coding taxonomy.']
  hazard_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the hazard, including its nature, potential sources, and circumstances under which it may be encountered in mining operations.']
  hazard_name varchar [note: 'The common business name or title of the hazard used for identification and communication across the organization.']
  hazard_type varchar [note: 'Specific sub-classification or detailed type of hazard within the broader category (e.g., \'Silica Dust\', \'Ground Instability\', \'Heavy Equipment Operation\', \'Confined Space\').']
  is_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incidents involving this hazard must be reported to external regulatory authorities such as MSHA, state mining departments, or environmental agencies. True if reportable, False otherwise.']
  last_incident_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent recorded incident, injury, or near-miss event associated with this hazard. Used for trend analysis and control effectiveness evaluation.']
  likelihood_rating varchar [note: 'The probability or frequency with which the hazard is expected to cause harm, ranging from almost certain (expected to occur frequently) to rare (may occur only in exceptional circumstances).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this hazard record was last updated or modified. Supports change tracking and audit trail.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'The required or recommended frequency for monitoring, inspecting, or reassessing this hazard to ensure controls remain effective and exposure remains within acceptable limits.']
  permit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a work permit, hot work permit, confined space entry permit, or other authorization is required before work involving this hazard can commence. True if permit is mandatory, False otherwise.']
  ppe_required varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of personal protective equipment required when working in the presence of this hazard (e.g., \'hard hat\', \'safety glasses\', \'respirator\', \'hearing protection\', \'high-visibility vest\', \'steel-toed boots\').']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of applicable mining safety regulations, standards, or legal requirements that govern the management of this hazard (e.g., MSHA 30 CFR Part 56, state mining acts, ISO standards).']
  review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next formal review or reassessment of this hazard and its control measures to ensure continued relevance and effectiveness.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk level derived from the combination of severity and likelihood, used to prioritize control measures and management attention. Extreme and high risks require immediate action.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'The potential severity of harm or consequence if the hazard materializes, rated from catastrophic (fatality or permanent total disability) to minor (first aid injury).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hazard record. Active indicates the hazard is currently recognized and managed; under review indicates reassessment is in progress; retired indicates the hazard is no longer present; superseded indicates the hazard definition has been replaced by a newer version.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specific safety training or competency certification is required for workers who may be exposed to this hazard. True if training is mandatory, False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for hazard. Referenced by hazard_id.'
}

Table customer.counterparty {
  counterparty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the commodity buyer, offtake counterparty, or trading partner. Primary key for the counterparty master record.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Mining companies track primary cost centre association for each customer/supplier to enable customer-specific cost allocation, contract profitability analysis, and AISC reporting by counterparty. Esse']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales or account manager responsible for managing the commercial relationship with this counterparty. Links to employee or user master data.']
  parent_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent or ultimate holding company if this counterparty is part of a corporate group. Enables consolidated credit management and group-level reporting.']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Revenue attribution and segment profitability reporting require linking customers to profit centres. Mining companies must track which customers drive profit centre performance for strategic account m']
  stakeholder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.stakeholder. Business justification: Mining companies track which counterparties are also community stakeholders for social licence risk management, conflict-of-interest screening, and local content reporting. Essential for benefit-shari']
  abn varchar [note: '11-digit Australian Business Number issued by the Australian Taxation Office for entities registered in Australia.']
  acn varchar [note: '9-digit Australian Company Number issued by ASIC to companies registered under the Corporations Act 2001.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification of the counterparty based on their primary industry and role in the commodity supply chain. Determines applicable commercial terms and risk profiles.']
  communication_language varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO language code representing the counterparty\'s preferred language for contracts, invoices, and commercial correspondence.']
  counterparty_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the counterparty relationship. Active counterparties are eligible for new transactions; suspended or blacklisted counterparties are restricted.']
  country_of_incorporation varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the jurisdiction where the counterparty is legally incorporated or registered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the counterparty master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure limit approved for the counterparty, expressed in United States Dollars. Governs the total value of outstanding receivables and open orders.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Current credit rating assigned to the counterparty by the specified rating agency. Influences credit limits, payment terms, and security requirements.']
  credit_rating_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the credit rating agency that issued the counterparty\'s credit rating. Used for credit risk assessment and exposure management.']
  duns_number varchar [note: '9-digit unique identifier assigned by Dun & Bradstreet for global business entity identification and credit assessment.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the counterparty relationship became active and eligible for commodity transactions. Marks the start of the commercial relationship lifecycle.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date when the counterparty relationship was terminated or suspended. Null for active relationships. Used for historical analysis and compliance reporting.']
  incoterm_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred Incoterm for commodity sales contracts. FOB (Free on Board) and CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight) are most common in mining. Defines responsibility for freight, insurance, and risk transfer.']
  invoice_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for delivering invoices to the counterparty. EDI and portal integrations enable automated accounts payable processing.']
  is_sanctioned_entity boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the counterparty appears on any international sanctions lists (OFAC, UN, EU). True indicates the entity is sanctioned and transactions are prohibited.']
  kyc_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent KYC due diligence process was successfully completed. Used to track KYC refresh requirements.']
  kyc_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current KYC certification expires and requires renewal. Typically 12-24 months from completion date depending on risk classification.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Know Your Customer due diligence process. Completed KYC is mandatory before executing commodity sales contracts.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the counterparty master record was most recently updated. Tracks data currency and supports change data capture processes.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the counterparty as it appears on incorporation documents and contracts. Used for legal agreements and regulatory reporting.']
  lei_code varchar [note: '20-character alphanumeric Legal Entity Identifier code used for financial transaction reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms expressed as number of days from invoice date. Common values include 30, 60, or 90 days. Negotiated based on credit rating and relationship strength.']
  preferred_commodity_grades varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of preferred mineral grades or product specifications that the counterparty typically purchases. Examples: 62% Fe iron ore fines, 99.99% copper cathode, premium coking coal.']
  preferred_discharge_ports varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of preferred destination ports where the counterparty receives commodity deliveries. Aligns with their processing facilities and distribution networks.']
  preferred_loading_ports varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of preferred mine or port loading locations for commodity shipments. Reflects counterparty\'s logistics network and freight optimization preferences.']
  preferred_vessel_size_dwt varchar [note: 'Preferred shipping vessel size category for bulk commodity deliveries, based on port infrastructure and logistics capabilities. Influences freight terms and delivery scheduling.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for commercial communications with the counterparty. Used for contract correspondence, invoices, and operational notifications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the counterparty, including country code. Used for urgent commercial and operational communications.']
  registered_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the official registered address of the counterparty as recorded with the incorporation authority.']
  registered_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the official registered address, typically containing building, suite, or floor information.']
  registered_city varchar [note: 'City or locality component of the counterparty\'s registered address.']
  registered_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the country component of the counterparty\'s registered address.']
  registered_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code component of the counterparty\'s registered address.']
  registered_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region component of the counterparty\'s registered address.']
  reporting_format_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred format for delivery of commercial reports, certificates of analysis, and shipping documentation. Supports integration with counterparty\'s procurement systems.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number or VAT registration number issued by the counterparty\'s tax authority. Required for tax reporting and withholding compliance.']
  trading_name varchar [note: 'Commercial trading name or brand name used by the counterparty in day-to-day business operations. May differ from legal name.']

  Note: 'Master record for all commodity buyers, offtake counterparties, and trading partners including steel mills, smelters, energy companies, and commodity traders. Single source of truth for counterparty identity, legal entity details (ABN/ACN, DUNS number), country of incorporation, registered address, credit rating, KYC status, commercial classification, and operational preferences (preferred commodity grades, vessel sizes, loading ports, communication language, reporting format, invoice delivery method, relationship manager assignment). Includes counterparty-specific commercial and logistics preferences that drive tailored account management. Primary anchor entity for the customer domain in mining commodity sales.'
}

Table customer.counterparty_contact {
  counterparty_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the counterparty contact person. Primary key for the counterparty contact entity.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent counterparty organisation (customer, offtake partner, trading entity) to which this contact belongs.']
  relationship_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Each customer contact has an assigned relationship manager from the mining company\'s commercial team. Drives account planning, territory management, communication routing, and escalation protocols. St']
  stakeholder_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.stakeholder_contact. Business justification: Individual contacts at counterparty organizations may also be registered as community stakeholder contacts (e.g., traditional owner representatives who are also suppliers). Required for relationship m']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact person by their primary functional role within the counterparty organisation.']
  counterparty_contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact person within the counterparty relationship. Active contacts are available for business communications; inactive contacts have left the organisation or role.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contact record was first created in the system.']
  credit_approval_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person has authority to approve credit terms, payment schedules, or financial arrangements.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organisational department or division to which the contact person belongs (e.g., Procurement, Trading, Operations, Finance).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this contact person became active in their role with the counterparty organisation.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this contact person ceased to be active in their role, if applicable. Null for currently active contacts.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary business email address for the contact person. Used for commercial correspondence, contract negotiations, and operational communications.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Facsimile number for the contact person, if applicable for formal document transmission.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the contact person.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete name of the contact person as it should appear in formal correspondence and documentation.']
  is_decision_maker boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person has authority to make binding commercial decisions on behalf of the counterparty organisation.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is the primary or main point of contact for the counterparty organisation.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official position or role title of the contact person within their organisation.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent business communication or interaction with this contact person.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who most recently modified this contact record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contact record was most recently updated.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the contact person.']
  logistics_coordinator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person coordinates shipping, delivery schedules, and logistics arrangements.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the contact person, if applicable.']
  mobile_number varchar [note: 'Mobile or cellular telephone number for the contact person, used for urgent communications and field coordination.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording additional information about the contact person, including communication preferences, relationship history, or special considerations.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary business telephone number for the contact person, including country and area codes.']
  preferred_communication_method varchar [note: 'The contact person\'s preferred channel for business communications and correspondence.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code representing the contact person\'s preferred language for business communications (e.g., EN, ZH, ES, PT, JA).']
  salutation varchar [note: 'Formal title or honorific used when addressing the contact person.']
  secondary_email_address varchar [note: 'Alternative email address for the contact person, used for backup communication or specific functional purposes.']
  signing_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person has legal authority to sign contracts, purchase orders, or other binding commercial documents.']
