  technical_liaison boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person serves as the technical liaison for product specifications, quality requirements, and technical compliance matters.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the contact person\'s primary business location, used for scheduling meetings and coordinating communications across global operations.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this contact record.']

  Note: 'Individual contact persons associated with a counterparty organisation, including commercial representatives, technical liaisons, logistics coordinators, and finance contacts. Tracks name, role, department, communication preferences, and active status for each contact at a buyer or trading partner entity.'
}

Table customer.counterparty_hierarchy {
  counterparty_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the counterparty hierarchy relationship record. Primary key for this entity.']
  child_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subsidiary, child entity, or lower-level counterparty in the hierarchy relationship. References the counterparty entity that is owned or controlled by the parent.']
  parent_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent, holding company, or higher-level counterparty in the hierarchy relationship. References the counterparty entity that owns or controls the child.']
  primary_ultimate_parent_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ultimate beneficial owner or top-level parent entity in the corporate group structure. Used for consolidated credit exposure and group-level reporting across all subsidiaries.']
  consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child counterparty should be included in consolidated financial reporting and group-level credit exposure calculations for the parent entity.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the parent exercises control over the child counterparty. Direct control is through direct ownership; indirect is through intermediary entities; joint is shared control with other parties.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this counterparty hierarchy relationship record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_aggregation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether credit exposure from this child counterparty should be aggregated with the parent for group credit limit monitoring and risk assessment purposes.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date when the hierarchical relationship became effective. Supports temporal analysis of corporate structure changes and historical credit exposure reporting.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date when the hierarchical relationship ended or is scheduled to end. Null for currently active relationships. Used for historical analysis and audit trails.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the organizational hierarchy tree, with 1 representing the ultimate parent and increasing numbers representing deeper subsidiary levels. Enables depth-based queries and reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this counterparty hierarchy relationship record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.']
  legal_entity_identifier varchar [note: 'ISO 17442 Legal Entity Identifier for the child counterparty in this relationship. 20-character alphanumeric code used for global entity identification in financial transactions.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of equity ownership or control that the parent counterparty holds in the child counterparty. Used to determine consolidation requirements and credit exposure allocation.']
  relationship_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about the hierarchical relationship, including special arrangements, control mechanisms, or commercial considerations relevant to credit and trading decisions.']
  relationship_source varchar [note: 'Source of information used to establish the hierarchical relationship. Indicates the reliability and verification level of the relationship data.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hierarchical relationship. Active relationships are used for credit aggregation and group reporting; inactive relationships are historical.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hierarchical relationship between the parent and child counterparties. Defines the nature of corporate control or affiliation.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the hierarchical relationship was last verified or confirmed. Used for data quality monitoring and periodic review requirements.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification status of the hierarchical relationship data. Verified relationships have been confirmed through reliable sources; unverified relationships require validation.']

  Note: 'Defines the corporate ownership and commercial relationship hierarchy among counterparties, capturing parent-subsidiary relationships, group structures, and ultimate beneficial ownership. Enables consolidated credit exposure, group-level reporting, and offtake volume aggregation across related entities such as a steel group with multiple mill subsidiaries.'
}

Table customer.delivery_destination {
  delivery_destination_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery destination record. Primary key.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Delivery destinations (ports, customer facilities) incur specific logistics costs (stevedoring, demurrage, handling) that must be allocated to cost centres for accurate delivered cost calculation, AIS']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the customer entity that owns or operates this delivery destination.']
  cultural_heritage_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.cultural_heritage_site. Business justification: Delivery destinations (ports, rail sidings, stockpile areas) may be near or overlap with cultural heritage sites, requiring access protocol compliance and heritage management plan adherence. Essential']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Mining operations track which vendors operate/own discharge ports and loading facilities for inbound materials (explosives, fuel, consumables). Required for vendor performance tracking by location, fr']
  site_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Delivery destinations (ports, customer facilities) require a designated site manager or logistics coordinator from the mining company for operational coordination, quality disputes, demurrage manageme']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address or location descriptor for the delivery destination facility.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as building, terminal, or berth number.']
  berth_name varchar [note: 'Specific berth or wharf name within the port terminal where vessels dock for loading or unloading. Null for non-maritime destinations.']
  berth_number varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier for the specific berth position. Null for non-maritime destinations.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the delivery destination is located.']
  commodity_types_accepted varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of commodity types that this destination is approved to receive (e.g., iron_ore, coal, copper_concentrate, lithium_spodumene, nickel_ore). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iron_ore|coal|copper_concentrate|lithium_spodumene|lithium_hydroxide|nickel_ore|bauxite|manganese — promote to reference product]']
  conveyor_system_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the destination has conveyor belt infrastructure for direct commodity transfer.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the delivery destination is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery destination record was first created in the system.']
  customs_clearance_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customs clearance services are available at this destination for international shipments.']
  delivery_destination_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the delivery destination indicating whether it is available for commodity shipments.']
  destination_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the delivery destination, typically a port code, terminal code, or facility identifier used in shipping documentation and logistics systems.']
  destination_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the delivery destination (e.g., Port of Qingdao Terminal 3, Rio Tinto Smelter Intake Point, Baosteel Stockyard).']
  destination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery destination based on facility type and operational purpose.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this delivery destination became active and approved for commodity shipments.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this delivery destination remains active. Null for indefinite destinations.']
  environmental_restrictions varchar [note: 'Description of any environmental restrictions, dust control requirements, or operational constraints applicable to this destination.']
  incoterms_applicable varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) applicable to this destination (e.g., FOB, CIF, CFR, DAP, DDP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|DAP|DDP|EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAT|DPU — promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery destination record was last updated.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery destination in decimal degrees for logistics planning and vessel routing.']
  loading_rate_tph decimal(8,2) [note: 'Typical or maximum loading rate in tonnes per hour for commodity discharge or loading operations at this destination.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery destination in decimal degrees for logistics planning and vessel routing.']
  maximum_vessel_beam_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel beam (width) in meters that the berth can accommodate.']
  maximum_vessel_draft_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable vessel draft in meters that the berth or port can accommodate, critical for vessel selection and cargo planning.']
  maximum_vessel_loa_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel length overall (LOA) in meters that the berth can accommodate.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or logistics constraints specific to this destination.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours or schedule for the destination (e.g., 24/7, business_hours_only, daylight_only).']
  port_code varchar [note: 'Five-letter UN/LOCODE port identifier for maritime destinations (e.g., CNQIN for Qingdao, AUHED for Port Hedland). Null for non-port destinations.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the delivery destination address.']
  rail_access_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the destination has direct rail access for commodity delivery.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or state/province where the destination is located (e.g., Western Australia, Shandong Province, Queensland).']
  road_access_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the destination has road access for truck-based commodity delivery.']
  storage_capacity_tonnes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total storage or stockpile capacity in tonnes available at the destination for commodity inventory.']

  Note: 'Master register of all approved delivery destinations for commodity shipments, including port terminals, smelter intake points, power station stockyards, and warehouse locations. Captures destination name, port code, country, Incoterms applicability (FOB, CIF, CFR), berth specifications, draft restrictions, and logistics constraints relevant to iron ore, coal, copper concentrate, and lithium product deliveries.'
}

Table customer.credit_limit {
  credit_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the credit limit record. Primary key for the credit limit entity.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity buyer, offtake counterparty, or trading partner to whom this credit limit is assigned. Links to the customer master data.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or title of the individual, committee, or governance body that approved this credit limit. Examples include Chief Financial Officer (CFO), Credit Committee, Board of Directors, or Regional Credit Manager. Provides audit trail for credit limit authorization.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the credit limit was formally approved by the designated approval authority. Establishes the authorization date for audit and compliance purposes.']
  available_credit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining credit capacity available for new transactions. Calculated as limit_amount minus utilisation_amount. Negative values indicate over-limit exposure requiring management intervention.']
  collateral_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Value of the collateral or credit enhancement securing this credit limit. Typically expressed in the same currency as the credit limit. For parent guarantees this may be the guaranteed amount, for standby LCs this is the LC face value.']
  collateral_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the collateral instrument securing this credit limit. For standby LCs this is the LC number, for guarantees this is the guarantee reference, for cash deposits this is the deposit account reference.']
  collateral_type varchar [note: 'Type of credit enhancement or collateral arrangement securing the credit limit. Parent guarantees are common for subsidiary buyers, standby letters of credit (LC) provide bank-backed security, cash deposits are held as security, bank guarantees are issued by financial institutions, insurance bonds are provided by credit insurers, and asset pledges involve physical or financial assets as security. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|parent_guarantee|standby_lc|cash_deposit|bank_guarantee|insurance_bond|asset_pledge — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this credit limit record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record creation.']
  credit_limit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the credit limit agreement. Externally visible reference number used in credit management communications and approvals.']
  credit_limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit limit. Draft limits are being prepared, pending approval limits await credit committee authorization, active limits are in force, suspended limits are temporarily blocked due to payment issues or risk concerns, expired limits have passed their validity date, cancelled limits have been terminated, and under review limits are being reassessed due to changed circumstances. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|expired|cancelled|under_review — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  credit_limit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credit limit based on its purpose and duration. Master limits are ongoing, temporary limits are short-term extensions, project-specific limits are tied to individual mining projects or shipments, seasonal limits accommodate cyclical demand, spot trade limits cover one-off transactions, and offtake agreement limits are tied to long-term supply contracts.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'External or internal credit rating assigned to the counterparty that forms the basis for the credit limit determination. Ratings follow standard agency notation (AAA highest, D default, NR not rated). May be sourced from agencies like Moody\'s, S&P, Fitch, or internal credit risk models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D|NR — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  credit_rating_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the credit rating agency or internal risk assessment team that provided the credit rating. Examples include Moody\'s, Standard & Poor\'s, Fitch Ratings, or Internal Credit Risk Committee.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the credit limit is denominated. Common currencies for mining commodity trading include USD, EUR, CNY, JPY, and AUD.']
  discount_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which payment must be received to qualify for the early payment discount. Typically shorter than the full payment terms (e.g., 10 days for discount vs 30 days net).']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered to the customer for early payment within a specified discount period. For example, 2% discount if paid within 10 days (2/10 net 30 terms). Incentivizes prompt payment and reduces Days Sales Outstanding (DSO).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the credit limit becomes active and enforceable. Shipments and invoices on or after this date are subject to this credit limit.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the credit limit ceases to be valid. Nullable for open-ended master limits that remain in force until explicitly revised or cancelled.']
  insurance_coverage_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the credit limit covered by credit insurance. Typically ranges from 80% to 95%, with the mining company retaining a deductible or co-insurance portion to align incentives for credit risk management.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the credit insurance policy covering this counterparty exposure, if applicable. Credit insurance protects against non-payment risk and is common for high-value or high-risk commodity trading relationships.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent periodic review of this credit limit. Updated each time the credit limit undergoes scheduled or ad-hoc reassessment by the credit risk team.']
  late_payment_penalty_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual percentage rate charged on overdue balances as a penalty for late payment. Applied to the outstanding amount for each day or month beyond the payment due date. Helps enforce payment discipline and compensates for financing costs.']
  limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum approved credit exposure amount that the counterparty is authorized to utilize. Represents the total outstanding receivables (invoiced but unpaid) that the customer may accumulate before further shipments are blocked.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this credit limit record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute of the credit limit is changed, supporting change tracking and audit requirements.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this credit limit. Calculated based on the last review date plus the review frequency. Triggers workflow notifications to credit risk analysts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or commentary related to this credit limit. May include details on negotiation history, special approval conditions, temporary adjustments, or risk mitigation measures.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment instrument or mechanism agreed for settlement of invoices under this credit limit. Telegraphic transfer (TT) is direct bank wire, letter of credit (LC) provides bank-backed payment assurance, documentary collection involves bank-mediated document exchange, cash against documents (CAD) requires payment before document release, open account allows payment after delivery, and advance payment requires prepayment before shipment.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days from Bill of Lading (B/L) date or invoice date within which payment is due. Common terms in commodity trading include 30, 60, 90, or 120 days. Aligned with the offtake agreement or sales contract payment terms.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between scheduled reviews of this credit limit. High-risk counterparties may be reviewed quarterly (3 months), standard counterparties semi-annually (6 months), and low-risk counterparties annually (12 months). Ensures credit limits remain aligned with counterparty creditworthiness.']
  risk_classification varchar [note: 'Internal risk category assigned to the counterparty based on credit assessment, payment history, financial health, and market conditions. Low risk counterparties have strong financials and payment track record, medium risk have acceptable credit profiles, high risk require enhanced monitoring, watch list indicates deteriorating conditions, and default indicates payment failure or insolvency.']
  utilisation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current outstanding receivables balance against this credit limit. Calculated in real-time as the sum of all unpaid invoices and open deliveries. When utilisation approaches or exceeds the limit amount, shipment blocks are triggered.']

  Note: 'Manages approved credit exposure limits and payment terms assigned to each counterparty, including total credit limit amount, currency, effective/expiry dates, credit rating basis, collateral arrangements (parent company guarantees, standby LCs), approval authority, payment days from B/L date, payment method (TT, LC, documentary collection), discount terms, and late payment penalties. Tracks real-time utilisation against limit and supports credit risk management for commodity trading and offtake agreements. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA credit management module.'
}

Table customer.credit_review {
  credit_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the credit review record. Primary key.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the counterparty (steel mill, smelter, energy company, commodity trader) being reviewed.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the credit analyst who performed the review, for audit trail and accountability.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the credit review was formally approved by the credit committee or authorized approver.']
  approval_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or conditions recorded by the credit committee or approver at the time of approval, including any modifications to the recommendation or special conditions.']
  approval_outcome varchar [note: 'The final decision by the credit committee: approved as recommended (no changes), approved with modifications (limit or terms adjusted), rejected (recommendation not accepted), or deferred (requires additional information).']
  approved_credit_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final approved credit limit for this counterparty in United States Dollars (USD) following credit committee approval. This becomes the new active limit upon approval.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the credit committee member or senior risk officer who approved the review outcome.']
  collateral_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether collateral or security is required for transactions with this counterparty (True) or not (False), based on the credit review outcome.']
  collateral_type varchar [note: 'The type of collateral or security required if collateral_required_flag is True: letter of credit, bank guarantee, parent company guarantee, cash deposit, none, or other.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credit review record was first created in the system, for audit trail purposes.']
  current_credit_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The existing approved credit limit for this counterparty in United States Dollars (USD), representing the maximum exposure the company is willing to accept prior to this review.']
  current_ratio decimal(10,2) [note: 'The counterparty\'s current assets divided by current liabilities, a liquidity metric indicating short-term financial health.']
  external_credit_rating varchar [note: 'The credit rating assigned by the external rating agency (e.g., Aaa, Baa2 for Moody\'s; AAA, BBB+ for S&P/Fitch). Free text to accommodate different agency scales.']
  external_rating_agency varchar [note: 'The primary external credit rating agency whose rating is referenced in this review (Moody\'s, Standard & Poor\'s, Fitch, DBRS, Japan Credit Rating, or Other).']
  external_rating_date date [note: 'The date on which the external credit rating was issued or last updated by the rating agency.']
  financial_statement_date date [note: 'The as-of date of the counterparty\'s financial statements used as the basis for the financial analysis in this credit review.']
  interest_coverage_ratio decimal(10,2) [note: 'The counterparty\'s EBIT (Earnings Before Interest and Tax) divided by interest expense, indicating ability to service debt obligations.']
  internal_credit_rating varchar [note: 'The internal credit rating assigned to the counterparty by the mining company\'s credit risk team, using a scale aligned with external agency methodologies (AAA highest quality, D default). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  internal_rating_outlook varchar [note: 'The outlook for the internal credit rating: positive (potential upgrade), stable (no change expected), negative (potential downgrade), developing (uncertain direction).']
  leverage_ratio decimal(10,2) [note: 'The counterparty\'s total debt to equity ratio or net debt to EBITDA ratio, a key financial metric analyzed during the credit review to assess financial risk.']
  limit_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for any recommended change to the credit limit, including references to financial performance, market conditions, or strategic relationship considerations.']
  loss_given_default_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The estimated loss severity in the event of default, expressed as a percentage of exposure at default (0.00 to 100.00), used for IFRS 9 ECL calculation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credit review record was last modified in the system, for audit trail purposes.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next periodic credit review of this counterparty, typically 12 months from current review for investment grade, 6 months for sub-investment grade.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The recommended payment terms for this counterparty based on the credit review (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, Letter of Credit at Sight, Cash in Advance).']
  probability_of_default_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The estimated probability of default over a 12-month horizon, expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00), used for IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss (ECL) calculation.']
  recommended_credit_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The credit limit recommended by the credit analyst based on this review, in United States Dollars (USD). May be higher, lower, or equal to the current limit.']
  review_date date [note: 'The date on which the credit review was conducted or completed. Principal business event timestamp for this review.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the credit review, typically formatted as CR-YYYYNNNNNN for external reference and audit trail.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the credit review: draft (initial preparation), in_progress (under analysis), pending_approval (submitted to credit committee), approved (finalized and active), rejected (not accepted), superseded (replaced by newer review).']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review trigger: periodic (scheduled), event-driven (material change in counterparty financials), ad-hoc (management request), annual (mandatory yearly), pre-contract (new customer onboarding), limit increase (customer requests higher exposure), or downgrade trigger (external rating agency downgrade). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: periodic|event_driven|ad_hoc|annual|pre_contract|limit_increase|downgrade_trigger — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the credit analyst or risk officer who conducted the review.']
  risk_commentary varchar [note: 'Detailed qualitative assessment of the counterparty\'s credit risk, including analysis of financial position, industry outlook, management quality, and any material risk factors identified during the review.']

  Note: 'Records periodic and event-driven credit reviews conducted for counterparties, capturing review date, reviewer, credit rating assigned, financial analysis inputs (leverage ratios, liquidity), external agency ratings (Moody\'s, S&P), recommended limit changes, and approval outcome. Provides an audit trail of credit decisions supporting IFRS and internal risk governance.'
}

Table customer.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer segment classification record. Primary key.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer entity being classified into this segment. Links to the customer master data.']
  local_employment_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.local_employment_commitment. Business justification: Customer segmentation strategies may drive local employment commitments (e.g., domestic market customers requiring local workforce targets, government contracts with indigenous employment quotas). Lin']
  annual_volume_maximum_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum annual offtake volume in metric tonnes that defines the upper bound of this segments volume band. Null indicates no upper limit.']
  annual_volume_minimum_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum annual offtake volume in metric tonnes that defines the lower bound of this segments volume band.']
  commodity_focus varchar [note: 'Primary mineral commodity or commodity group that this customer segment purchases. Examples include iron ore, copper concentrate, metallurgical coal, thermal coal, lithium, nickel.']
  contract_type_preference varchar [note: 'Typical contract structure preference for customers in this segment. Long-Term Offtake for multi-year commitments, Annual Framework for yearly agreements, Quarterly for short-term contracts, Spot for transactional purchases, Mixed for diversified approach.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the customers primary country of operation or delivery destination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this customer segment classification record was first created in the system.']
  credit_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Internal credit risk assessment rating for this customer segment. AAA represents lowest risk, D represents default or highest risk. Used for credit limit determination and commercial terms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  diversification_target_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this customer segment is targeted for portfolio diversification efforts to reduce concentration risk. True indicates diversification target.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this customer segment classification becomes effective and applicable for commercial treatment and reporting.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this customer segment classification remains effective. Null indicates the segment is currently active with no defined end date.']
  end_use_industry varchar [note: 'Primary industry sector in which the customer operates and consumes the mineral commodities. Critical for understanding demand drivers and market positioning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Steel|Energy|Battery Materials|Smelting|Manufacturing|Construction|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region where the customer operates or takes delivery. Used for regional portfolio analysis and logistics planning.']
  incoterm_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred Incoterms delivery basis for this customer segment. FOB (Free on Board) for port delivery, CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight) for delivered pricing, CFR (Cost and Freight), DAP (Delivered at Place), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid), EXW (Ex Works) for mine gate pickup.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this customer segment classification record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this customer segment classification record was last updated or modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this customer segment classification was last reviewed and validated by the commercial team.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review of this customer segment classification.']
  payment_terms_category varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms category applicable to this customer segment. Defines credit period and payment security requirements.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Commercial pricing approach applied to this customer segment. Premium for strategic long-term partners, Standard for preferred customers, Competitive for market-rate buyers, Spot Market for transactional pricing, and Negotiated for bespoke arrangements.']
  quality_specification_tier varchar [note: 'Product quality specification tier typically required by customers in this segment. Premium Grade for highest purity/lowest impurities, Standard Grade for typical commercial specifications, Commercial Grade for broader tolerance ranges, Spot Grade for variable quality spot market material.']
  relationship_manager varchar [note: 'Name of the commercial relationship manager or account executive responsible for managing customers in this segment.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between scheduled reviews of this customer segment classification. Typically 6 or 12 months for strategic segments, longer for stable spot segments.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the segment classification for system processing and reporting.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the customer segment, including defining characteristics, strategic rationale, and commercial treatment guidelines.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the customer segment classification.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this customer segment classification. Active indicates current use, Under Review for segments being reassessed, Suspended for temporarily inactive segments, Archived for historical segments no longer in use.']
  service_level_tier varchar [note: 'Service level commitment tier for this customer segment. Platinum receives highest priority for supply allocation, logistics coordination, and technical support. Standard receives baseline service.']
  strategic_priority_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this customer segment is designated as a strategic priority for business development and relationship investment. True indicates strategic priority status.']
  tier_classification varchar [note: 'Strategic tier classification indicating the commercial importance and relationship depth. Tier 1 Strategic represents highest-value long-term partnerships, Tier 2 Preferred for regular preferred customers, Tier 3 Spot for transactional buyers, and Tier 4 Inactive for dormant relationships.']
  volume_band varchar [note: 'Classification of customer based on annual offtake volume ranges. Very High represents multi-million tonne buyers, High for significant volume, Medium for regular buyers, Low for small-scale, and Minimal for occasional spot purchases.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this customer segment classification record.']

  Note: 'Classification of counterparties into commercial segments for strategic account management, pricing strategy, and relationship prioritisation. Segments include tier classifications (Tier 1 strategic, Tier 2 preferred, Tier 3 spot), end-use industry (steel, energy, battery materials, smelting), geographic region, and volume band. Enables differentiated commercial treatment and portfolio analysis.'
}

Table customer.bank_account {
  bank_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bank account record. Primary key.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or counterparty who owns this bank account. Links to steel mills, smelters, energy companies, commodity traders, and other offtake partners.']
  account_holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the account holder as registered with the bank. Must match customer legal name for payment reconciliation and anti-money laundering compliance.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The bank account number used for payment processing and financial settlement. May be domestic account number or IBAN depending on jurisdiction.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the bank account record. Only active accounts can be used for payment processing.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bank account type. Corporate and current accounts are most common for commodity trading settlements.']
  bank_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the bank branch physical address. Required for letter of credit documentation and compliance verification.']
  bank_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the bank branch physical address for additional location details.']
  bank_branch_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific bank branch where the account is held.']
  bank_city varchar [note: 'City where the bank branch is located.']
  bank_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the bank location. Critical for compliance with international trade finance regulations and sanctions screening.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the financial institution holding the account.']
  bank_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the bank branch address.']
  bank_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the bank branch location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the account. Common currencies include USD, EUR, CNY, JPY for commodity sales settlements.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this bank account becomes valid for payment processing. Supports time-bound account changes.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this bank account remains valid. Null indicates no expiration. Used for account closure or replacement scenarios.']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number for cross-border payments. Used primarily for European and international commodity transactions under CIF and FOB terms.']
  intermediary_bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of intermediary or correspondent bank used for routing international payments when direct settlement is not available.']
  intermediary_swift_bic_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT BIC code of the intermediary bank for multi-hop international wire transfers.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary bank account for the customer. Used as default for payment routing in ERP systems.']
  is_sanctions_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bank and account holder have been screened against international sanctions lists (OFAC, UN, EU). Mandatory for international commodity trading compliance.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this bank account record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this bank account record. Critical for audit trail and change tracking.']
  payment_method_supported varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of payment methods supported by this account. Critical for FOB and CIF transaction settlement routing.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Domestic routing number for bank transfers. Format varies by country (ABA routing number in USA, sort code in UK, BSB in Australia).']
  sanctions_screening_date date [note: 'Date of most recent sanctions screening. Must be refreshed periodically per compliance policy.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for special payment routing instructions, reference codes, or beneficiary notifications required by the customer or bank.']
  swift_bic_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT BIC code identifying the bank and branch for international wire transfers and letter of credit processing in commodity sales.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the bank account was successfully verified. Required for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the bank account authenticity and ownership.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of bank account verification process. Verified accounts have passed validation checks including test deposits or third-party verification services.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this bank account record.']

  Note: 'Master record of counterparty bank account details used for payment processing, letter of credit (LC) issuance, and financial settlement of commodity sales. Captures bank name, SWIFT/BIC code, IBAN or account number, currency, country, account holder name, and verification status. Supports secure payment routing for FOB and CIF commodity transactions.'
}

Table customer.payment_term {
  payment_term_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment term record. Primary key.']
  applicable_commodity_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of commodity types to which this payment term applies (e.g., iron_ore, copper_concentrate, coal, lithium, nickel). Null if applicable to all commodities.']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'Organizational authority level required to approve the use of this payment term in a new offtake agreement. Higher-risk terms (e.g., extended payment days, open account) require senior approval.']
  bank_guarantee_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a bank guarantee or performance bond is required from the counterparty when this payment term is used. True if guarantee is mandatory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment term record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  credit_insurance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether credit insurance or trade finance insurance must be in place for transactions using this payment term. True if insurance is mandatory.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which payment must be made (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY, AUD). Most mining commodity contracts are denominated in USD.']
  discount_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from the payment basis date within which early payment qualifies for a discount (e.g., 10 days in a 2/10 Net 30 term). Null if no early payment discount applies.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the invoice amount if payment is made within the discount days period (e.g., 2.00 for a 2% discount). Null if no discount applies.']
  documentary_requirements varchar [note: 'List of documents required to be presented for payment under this term (e.g., Bill of Lading, Commercial Invoice, Certificate of Origin, Quality Certificate, Weight Certificate). Particularly relevant for LC and documentary collection terms.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this payment term becomes valid and available for use in offtake agreements and invoicing. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this payment term remains valid. Null for open-ended terms. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the payment due date before late payment penalties are applied. Provides a buffer period for payment processing delays. Null if no grace period applies.']
  installment_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payment may be made in multiple installments rather than a single lump sum. True if installment payments are permitted under this term.']
  installment_count varchar [note: 'Number of installments into which payment may be divided if installment_allowed_flag is true. Null if installments are not allowed.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this payment term record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment term record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  late_payment_penalty_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual interest rate (percentage) applied to overdue amounts for late payment. Typically expressed as a percentage per annum (e.g., 8.00 for 8% p.a.). Null if no penalty applies.']
  letter_of_credit_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of Letter of Credit if payment_method is letter_of_credit. Irrevocable and confirmed LCs are preferred for high-value commodity transactions. Null if payment method is not LC. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: irrevocable|revocable|confirmed|unconfirmed|sight|deferred|transferable|standby — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  maximum_transaction_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum invoice or shipment value (in the currency specified by currency_code) for which this payment term may be applied without additional credit approval. Null if no maximum applies.']
  minimum_transaction_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum invoice or shipment value (in the currency specified by currency_code) for which this payment term may be applied. Used to enforce stricter terms for smaller transactions. Null if no minimum applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or exceptions applicable to this payment term. May include references to specific customer segments or regional requirements.']
  payment_basis varchar [note: 'The reference event from which payment days are calculated. Common bases in mining commodity sales include Bill of Lading (B/L) date for FOB (Free on Board) terms and delivery date for CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight) terms.']
  payment_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from the reference event (e.g., Bill of Lading date, invoice date, shipment date) until payment is due. Standard terms in mining include 30, 60, 90, or 120 days.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The instrument or mechanism by which payment is executed. Telegraphic Transfer (TT) and Letter of Credit (LC) are most common in international commodity trading. Documentary Collection (D/C) used for established relationships.']
  payment_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this payment term for cash flow planning and treasury operations. High priority terms (e.g., cash in advance, short-dated LCs) receive expedited processing.']
  payment_term_code varchar [note: 'Standardized short code identifying the payment term (e.g., NET30, NET60, LC90, COD). Used as the business key across offtake agreements and invoicing systems.']
  payment_term_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the payment term including conditions, calculation basis, and any special clauses applicable to commodity trading.']
  payment_term_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the payment term (e.g., Net 30 Days from Bill of Lading Date, 90 Days Letter of Credit).']
  payment_term_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment term. Active terms are available for use in new agreements. Deprecated terms are retained for historical reference but not available for new contracts.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Credit and counterparty risk rating associated with this payment term. Cash in advance and confirmed LCs are low risk; extended open account terms are high risk.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this payment term record in the system.']

  Note: 'Reference data defining the payment terms applicable to counterparties and offtake agreements, including payment days (e.g., 30 days from B/L date), payment method (telegraphic transfer, letter of credit, documentary collection), currency, discount terms, and late payment penalty rates. Provides standardised payment term codes referenced across offtake agreements and invoicing.'
}

Table customer.letter_of_credit {
  letter_of_credit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the letter of credit record. Primary key.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity buyer or offtake counterparty who arranged this letter of credit as payment security.']
  issuing_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.bank_account. Business justification: Letters of credit are issued by the buyer\'s bank against a specific bank account. The counterparty (buyer) has one or more bank accounts registered in the bank_account table. Linking the LC to the spe']
  loading_port_delivery_destination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_destination. Business justification: Letters of credit specify loading and discharge ports for commodity shipments. Currently stored as text fields, these should reference delivery_destination master data. Loading port is the origin port']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Mining operations issue LCs to vendors for high-value procurement: capital equipment purchases, bulk explosives, long-lead mining equipment, imported consumables. Required for procurement payment secu']
  relationship_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Letters of credit for commodity sales are managed by specific trade finance or commercial employees who coordinate with banks, customers, and shipping agents. Essential for trade finance management, L']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Letters of credit often secure payment for capital project deliveries or major equipment purchases. Mining companies track LC coverage against specific WBS elements for project cash flow management, c']
  advising_bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution that advises or confirms the letter of credit to the beneficiary (seller).']
  advising_bank_swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code of the advising bank for international payment routing.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of amendments that have been made to this letter of credit since its original issuance.']
  applicant_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the applicant (the buyer or importer) who requested the issuing bank to open this letter of credit.']
  available_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining monetary amount available for drawing under this letter of credit (LC amount minus utilised amount).']
  beneficiary_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the beneficiary (the mining company or seller) entitled to draw payment under this letter of credit.']
  commodity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the mineral commodity or product covered by this letter of credit (e.g., iron ore fines, copper concentrate, metallurgical coal).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this letter of credit record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the letter of credit amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  discharge_port varchar [note: 'Name of the destination port or location where the commodity shipment must be discharged as specified in the letter of credit.']
  document_requirements varchar [note: 'Comprehensive list of documents required for presentation to the bank to draw payment under this letter of credit (e.g., bill of lading, commercial invoice, certificate of origin, assay certificate, packing list, insurance certificate).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the letter of credit expires and can no longer be drawn upon by the beneficiary.']
  governing_rules varchar [note: 'International rules and practices governing this letter of credit (e.g., UCP 600, ISP98 for standby letters of credit, eUCP for electronic presentation).']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery terms and risk transfer point for the commodity shipment (e.g., FOB, CIF, CFR). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the letter of credit was issued by the issuing bank.']
  issuing_bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution that issued the letter of credit on behalf of the buyer.']
  issuing_bank_swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code of the issuing bank for international payment routing.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment made to this letter of credit.']
  latest_shipment_date date [note: 'The last permissible date by which the commodity shipment must be dispatched as stipulated in the letter of credit terms.']
  lc_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the letter of credit available for drawing by the beneficiary.']
  lc_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique alphanumeric identifier assigned by the issuing bank to this letter of credit instrument.']
  lc_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the letter of credit instrument based on its terms and conditions (irrevocable, revocable, confirmed, unconfirmed, transferable, standby).']
  letter_of_credit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the letter of credit (draft, active, partially utilised, fully utilised, expired, cancelled).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this letter of credit record was last modified in the system.']
  partial_shipment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether partial shipments of the commodity are permitted under the terms of this letter of credit.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms specified in the letter of credit defining when payment is due (sight payment, deferred payment, usance, acceptance).']
  presentation_period_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days after the shipment date within which documents must be presented to the bank for payment.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the commodity to be shipped under this letter of credit.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the commodity quantity (e.g., MT for metric tonnes, DMT for dry metric tonnes, WMT for wet metric tonnes).']
  shipment_period_from date [note: 'Start date of the permissible shipment period window specified in the letter of credit.']
  shipment_period_to date [note: 'End date of the permissible shipment period window specified in the letter of credit.']
  tenor_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after presentation of compliant documents before payment is due, applicable for deferred payment or usance letters of credit.']
  tolerance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Permissible variance percentage (plus or minus) allowed for quantity and/or amount under the letter of credit terms.']
  transhipment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transhipment (transfer of goods from one vessel to another during transit) is permitted under this letter of credit.']
  utilised_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary amount that has been drawn or utilised against this letter of credit to date.']

  Note: 'Manages letters of credit (LC) issued by buyers as payment security for commodity shipments, capturing LC number, issuing bank, advising bank, LC amount, currency, expiry date, shipment period, document requirements (bill of lading, certificate of origin, assay certificate), amendment history, and utilisation status. Critical for managing payment risk in international commodity trade.'
}

Table customer.commercial_interaction {
  commercial_interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the commercial interaction record. Primary key for the commercial interaction entity.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity buyer, offtake counterparty, or trading partner involved in this commercial interaction. Links to the customer master data.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Commercial interactions (customer meetings, negotiations, site visits) are conducted by mining company employees. Essential for CRM, sales performance tracking, relationship continuity, and account ma']
  primary_counterparty_contact_counterparty_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.counterparty_contact. Business justification: Commercial interactions are conducted with specific contact persons at the counterparty organization. The current design stores contact name and role as text fields, which is denormalized. Adding FK t']
  stakeholder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.stakeholder. Business justification: Commercial discussions with counterparties who are also stakeholders (e.g., indigenous-owned suppliers, local community businesses) must be tracked for benefit-sharing reporting, local content complia']
  additional_counterparty_participants varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional counterparty representatives who participated in the commercial interaction. Supports relationship breadth analysis.']
  additional_internal_participants varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional internal employees who participated in the commercial interaction. Supports multi-stakeholder engagement tracking.']
  commodity_discussed varchar [note: 'Primary mineral commodity or product that was the focus of the commercial interaction. Aligns with the mining company\'s core product portfolio. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iron_ore|copper|coal|lithium|nickel|mixed|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the commercial interaction record and associated discussion content. Governs access control and information sharing policies.']
  contract_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contract renewal, extension, or renegotiation was discussed during the interaction. True if contract terms were addressed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the commercial interaction record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  follow_up_actions varchar [note: 'List of agreed follow-up tasks, deliverables, or commitments arising from the commercial interaction. Supports action tracking and accountability.']
  follow_up_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal employee responsible for executing the follow-up actions. Ensures accountability for post-interaction deliverables.']
  interaction_date date [note: 'The date on which the commercial interaction occurred or is scheduled to occur. Primary business event date for the interaction.']
  interaction_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the commercial interaction measured in minutes. Supports time allocation analysis and meeting efficiency tracking.']
  interaction_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the commercial interaction concluded. Used for calculating interaction duration and resource allocation.']
  interaction_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the commercial interaction took place. May include mine site names, office locations, or virtual meeting platform identifiers.']
  interaction_mode varchar [note: 'The communication channel or medium through which the commercial interaction was conducted. Distinguishes between face-to-face, remote, and written interactions.']
  interaction_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, observations, relationship insights, or qualitative details from the commercial interaction.']
  interaction_outcome varchar [note: 'Summary of the results, decisions, agreements, or conclusions reached during the commercial interaction. Captures the business impact and next steps.']
  interaction_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the commercial interaction based on strategic importance, revenue impact, or relationship criticality.']
  interaction_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the commercial interaction, used for tracking and reporting purposes. Format: CI-YYYYMMDD.']
  interaction_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the commercial interaction commenced. Used for detailed scheduling and duration tracking.']
  interaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commercial interaction. Tracks the progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation.']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commercial interaction type. Defines the nature and purpose of the engagement with the counterparty.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified the commercial interaction record. Supports audit trail and change management.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the commercial interaction record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  market_intelligence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether market trends, competitor insights, demand forecasts, or industry intelligence were exchanged during the interaction. True if market intelligence was shared.']
  next_interaction_date date [note: 'Scheduled or proposed date for the next commercial interaction with the counterparty. Supports relationship cadence planning and pipeline management.']
  outcome_classification varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the interaction outcome from a commercial relationship perspective. Supports pipeline health and account management scoring.']
  price_negotiation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether pricing terms, benchmark adjustments, or commercial rates were discussed or negotiated during the interaction. True if pricing was a topic.']
  strategic_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty is classified as a strategic or key account requiring executive-level relationship management. True for strategic accounts.']
  topics_discussed varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the key topics, agenda items, and discussion points covered during the commercial interaction. Supports knowledge management and continuity.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created the commercial interaction record. Supports data governance and accountability.']

  Note: 'Transactional log of significant commercial interactions with counterparties including price negotiations, contract renewal discussions, market intelligence exchanges, site visits, mine tours, and relationship management meetings. Captures interaction date, type, participants (internal and counterparty contacts), topics discussed, outcomes, follow-up actions, and next meeting date. Supports key account management, commercial pipeline tracking, and counterparty performance assessment for strategic mining commodity buyers.'
}

Table customer.kyc_record {
  kyc_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the KYC compliance record.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or trading partner entity this KYC record applies to.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance officer responsible for reviewing and approving this KYC record.']
  adverse_media_screening_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent adverse media screening was performed.']
  adverse_media_screening_result varchar [note: 'Result of screening for negative news or adverse media coverage related to the counterparty, including allegations of financial crime, corruption, or sanctions violations.']
  aml_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall AML risk rating assigned to the counterparty based on risk assessment factors including jurisdiction, business nature, transaction patterns, and PEP status.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the KYC record was formally approved by the compliance officer.']
  beneficial_ownership_documented boolean [note: 'Indicates whether beneficial ownership documentation has been collected and verified, identifying individuals who ultimately own or control the counterparty entity.']
  beneficial_ownership_verification_date date [note: 'Date when beneficial ownership documentation was last verified.']
  business_relationship_purpose varchar [note: 'Documented purpose and intended nature of the business relationship with the counterparty, including expected transaction types and volumes.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes regarding the KYC verification process, risk factors, or special considerations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this KYC record was first created in the system.']
  document_repository_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or path to the secure document repository where KYC supporting documents are stored.']
  documentation_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required KYC documentation has been collected and is complete.']
  due_diligence_level varchar [note: 'Level of customer due diligence applied based on risk assessment: standard for normal risk, enhanced for high-risk counterparties, simplified for low-risk entities.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current KYC verification expires and requires renewal.']
  identity_verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the identity of the counterparty: document review, in-person verification, electronic verification, or third-party verification service.']
  jurisdiction_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with the counterparty\'s country of incorporation or primary operating jurisdiction, based on FATF assessments and international AML standards.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the KYC verification process for this counterparty.']
  last_transaction_review_date date [note: 'Date when the counterparty\'s transaction activity was last reviewed as part of ongoing monitoring.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic KYC review, determined by the due diligence level and risk rating.']
  ongoing_monitoring_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty is subject to ongoing transaction monitoring and periodic review as part of continuous KYC compliance.']
  pep_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the PEP status: domestic PEP, foreign PEP, international organization official, family member of PEP, close associate of PEP, or not applicable.']
  pep_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty or its beneficial owners are identified as Politically Exposed Persons, requiring enhanced due diligence.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this KYC record requires reporting to regulatory authorities due to suspicious activity or high-risk classification.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months for periodic KYC reviews based on risk rating: typically 12 months for standard, 6 months for enhanced due diligence.']
  risk_assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the AML risk assessment was last performed or updated.']
  sanctions_screening_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent sanctions screening was performed.']
  sanctions_screening_result varchar [note: 'Result of screening against international sanctions lists including OFAC, UN, EU, and other jurisdictional sanctions databases.']
  source_of_funds_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the source of funds for the counterparty has been verified as part of enhanced due diligence.']
  source_of_wealth_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the source of wealth for the counterparty has been verified, typically required for high-risk or PEP counterparties.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this KYC record was last updated or modified.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the KYC verification was completed and approved.']

  Note: 'Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance record for each counterparty, capturing KYC status, verification date, due diligence level (standard, enhanced), sanctions screening result, beneficial ownership documentation, politically exposed person (PEP) flag, anti-money laundering (AML) risk rating, and next review date. Supports regulatory compliance obligations under FATF, OFAC, and applicable national AML legislation for international commodity trading.'
}

Table customer.counterparty_preference {
  counterparty_preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the counterparty preference record.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the counterparty (customer, offtake partner, trading partner) to whom these preferences apply.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee assigned as the primary relationship manager for this counterparty. Supports account management and escalation workflows.']
  preferred_payment_term_payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.payment_term. Business justification: Counterparty preferences include preferred payment terms, currently stored as free text. The payment_term table is the master reference for payment terms with full details (days, discount, penalties, ']
  secondary_contact_counterparty_contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the secondary or backup relationship manager for this counterparty.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether counterparty prefers automatic contract renewal at expiry (True) or manual renegotiation (False).']
  carbon_footprint_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether counterparty requires carbon footprint disclosure for shipped products (True/False).']
  communication_language varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred language for business communication (ISO 639-1 two-letter code or full language name, e.g., EN, ZH, JA, KO, PT).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this preference record was first created in the system.']
  demurrage_tolerance_hours varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s acceptable demurrage tolerance in hours before penalties apply. Supports port operations planning.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which these preferences become effective and should be applied to new transactions.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which these preferences remain valid. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  invoice_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred method for receiving invoices (email, postal mail, Electronic Data Interchange, customer portal, API integration).']
  invoice_email_address varchar [note: 'Email address for invoice delivery when email is the preferred delivery method.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this preference record was last modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when these preferences were last reviewed and confirmed with the counterparty.']
  maximum_shipment_quantity_tonnes decimal(15,3) [note: 'Counterparty\'s maximum acceptable shipment quantity in metric tonnes, often constrained by port or storage capacity.']
  minimum_shipment_quantity_tonnes decimal(15,3) [note: 'Counterparty\'s minimum acceptable shipment quantity in metric tonnes. Supports order planning and vessel utilization.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review of these preferences with the counterparty.']
  preference_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional preference details, special instructions, or context not covered by structured fields.']
  preference_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the preference record (active, inactive, pending review, superseded by newer preferences).']
  preference_type varchar [note: 'Category of preference being captured (commercial terms, operational requirements, logistics, communication, reporting, invoicing).']
  preferred_assay_method varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred laboratory assay method or standard for quality determination (e.g., ISO 3082 for iron ore, ISO 1171 for coal ash content).']
  preferred_commodity_grade varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred commodity grade specification (e.g., Fe 62%, Cu 25%, thermal coal 6000 kcal/kg). Supports tailored product offerings and contract negotiations.']
  preferred_contract_duration_months varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred contract duration in months for long-term offtake agreements.']
  preferred_discharge_port varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred destination port for cargo discharge. Enables end-to-end supply chain planning.']
  preferred_dispute_resolution varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred method for resolving commercial disputes (arbitration, litigation, mediation, negotiation).']
  preferred_incoterm varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) for defining responsibilities in shipping. FOB (Free on Board), CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight), CFR (Cost and Freight), EXW (Ex Works), FCA (Free Carrier), CPT (Carriage Paid To), CIP (Carriage and Insurance Paid To), DAP (Delivered at Place), DPU (Delivered at Place Unloaded), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  preferred_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred legal jurisdiction for contract governance (e.g., English Law, Singapore Law, New York Law).']
  preferred_laycan_window_days varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred laycan (laydays and cancelling date) window in days for vessel arrival. Supports shipping schedule coordination.']
  preferred_loading_port varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred port of loading for shipments. Supports logistics planning and freight optimization.']
  preferred_price_index varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred commodity price index for index-linked contracts (e.g., Platts Iron Ore Index, LME Copper, ICE Newcastle Coal). Supports pricing formula alignment.']
  preferred_pricing_basis varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred pricing mechanism (spot market, long-term contract, index-linked, fixed price, provisional pricing with final adjustment).']
  preferred_sampling_protocol varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred sampling protocol for quality assurance (e.g., ISO 3081 for iron ore sampling, ISO 13909 for coal sampling).']
  preferred_shipment_frequency varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred frequency for receiving shipments. Supports production planning and logistics scheduling.']
  preferred_vessel_size varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred vessel size category for shipment logistics. Capesize (150,000+ DWT), Panamax (60,000-80,000 DWT), Handymax (40,000-60,000 DWT), Handysize (10,000-40,000 DWT), VLOC (Very Large Ore Carrier 200,000+ DWT), Supramax (50,000-60,000 DWT).']
  quality_certificate_requirement varchar [note: 'Indicates whether counterparty requires quality certificates (assay results, chemical analysis) with each shipment.']
  reporting_format_preference varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred format for receiving reports, shipment documents, and quality certificates (PDF, Excel, CSV, XML, EDI, API integration).']
  sustainability_reporting_requirement varchar [note: 'Indicates whether counterparty requires sustainability and ESG (Environmental, Social, Governance) reporting with shipments.']

  Note: 'Captures counterparty-specific commercial and operational preferences including preferred commodity grade, preferred vessel size (Capesize, Panamax, Handymax), preferred loading port, communication language, reporting format preferences, invoice delivery method, and relationship manager assignment. Supports tailored account management and logistics planning for key buyers.'
}

Table customer.counterparty_document {
