  benefit_sharing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.benefit_sharing_agreement. Business justification: Counterparty contracts (offtake agreements, joint ventures) with indigenous partners or local communities may be governed by or reference benefit-sharing agreements. Required for legal compliance veri']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Customer contracts, quality certificates, insurance policies, and compliance documents are often associated with specific mine sites/cost centres for regulatory reporting, audit trails, site-specific ']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the counterparty (customer, trading partner, offtake party) to whom this document belongs. Links to the master counterparty record.']
  letter_of_credit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.letter_of_credit. Business justification: Letters of credit generate supporting documents including LC amendments, SWIFT MT700 messages, beneficiary certificates, inspection certificates, and bank confirmations. These documents are stored in ']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales contract or offtake agreement to which this document is attached or supports. Nullable if document is not contract-specific.']
  superseded_counterparty_document_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on counterparty_document (superseded_counterparty_document_id)']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or contractual compliance requirement that this document satisfies (e.g., KYC, AML, credit insurance, parent guarantee clause).']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level indicating the sensitivity and access restrictions for the document.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this document record was first created in the system.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the document is eligible for secure disposal or archival per retention policy.']
  document_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the document content, purpose, and any special conditions or clauses relevant to credit review and contractual compliance.']
  document_format varchar [note: 'Format or media type of the stored document.']
  document_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, special conditions, or observations related to the document.']
  document_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number or code assigned to the document for tracking and retrieval purposes.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document indicating its validity and usability for credit and compliance purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_verification|verified|active|expired|revoked|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Full title or name of the document as it appears on the original document.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document type. Indicates the nature and purpose of the document held against the counterparty. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parent_company_guarantee|insurance_certificate|trade_reference|financial_statement|board_resolution|power_of_attorney|credit_report|kyc_documentation|compliance_certificate|tax_clearance|bank_reference|legal_opinion — promote to reference product]']
  document_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value or coverage amount associated with the document (e.g., guarantee amount, insurance coverage limit). Nullable for documents without monetary value.']
  document_value_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code (ISO 4217) for the document value amount.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the document becomes legally binding and enforceable for credit and compliance purposes.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the document ceases to be valid or enforceable. Nullable for documents without expiration.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the document was originally issued or executed by the issuing party.']
  issuing_party_country varchar [note: 'Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3) of the jurisdiction where the issuing party is domiciled or registered.']
  issuing_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or individual that issued or executed the document (e.g., parent company, insurance provider, bank, legal authority).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this document record in the system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this document record was last updated in the system.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the document was last reviewed by the credit or compliance team.']
  legal_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction or governing law under which the document is enforceable (e.g., country or state code).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the document by the credit or compliance team.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the document must be retained per regulatory, legal, or internal policy requirements.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which the document must be reviewed for continued validity and compliance.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical or digital location where the original or certified copy of the document is stored.']
  storage_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier, file path, or URI pointing to the stored document in the document management system or physical archive.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the document was last verified by the credit or compliance team.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the document has been verified for authenticity, completeness, and compliance with internal credit and legal requirements.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the person who performed the document verification.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created this document record in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record of documents held against a counterparty including parent company guarantees, insurance certificates, trade references, financial statements, board resolutions, and power of attorney documents. Tracks document type, issue date, expiry date, verification status, and storage reference. Supports credit review processes and contractual compliance verification for international commodity trading counterparties.'
}

Table customer.customer_project_contract {
  customer_project_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for customer_project_contract']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the counterparty (contractor, supplier, or service provider) party to this project contract']
  project_contract_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this project contract record. Primary key.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the WBS element (capital project component) under which this contract is managed']
  billing_milestone varchar [note: 'Description of the billing milestone or payment schedule terms for this contract (e.g., \'30% on mobilization, 60% on progress, 10% on completion\'). Used for cash flow planning and payment processing.']
  commitment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total contracted or committed amount for this counterparty on this WBS element, in the WBS element\'s currency. Represents the financial commitment for cost control and budget tracking.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The scheduled or actual completion date for this contract. Used for project schedule tracking and vendor performance management.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'The external contract number or purchase order reference for this counterparty-project engagement. Used for legal and procurement tracking.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this project contract: draft (under negotiation), active (work in progress), suspended (temporarily halted), completed (work finished), terminated (contract cancelled).']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this project contract record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when this contract or commitment became effective. Used for contract lifecycle management and milestone tracking.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this project contract record was last updated.']
  vendor_performance_rating varchar [note: 'Performance rating for this counterparty on this specific WBS element, used for vendor performance management and future contract decisions.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual relationship between a counterparty (contractor, supplier, or service provider) and a WBS element (capital project component). It captures vendor-specific commitments, billing milestones, and payment schedules for CAPEX project cost control. Each record links one counterparty to one WBS element with contract-specific commercial terms, commitment amounts, and milestone tracking that exist only in the context of this project-vendor relationship.. Existence Justification: In mining capital projects, a single WBS element (e.g., mill construction, mine development phase) typically engages multiple counterparties simultaneously (EPC contractor, equipment suppliers, engineering consultants, logistics providers), and large counterparties (e.g., major EPC firms, OEM suppliers) work across multiple WBS elements and projects concurrently. The business actively manages these relationships as contracts with specific commitment amounts, billing milestones, payment schedules, and performance tracking per counterparty-WBS combination.'
}

Table sales.offtake_agreement {
  offtake_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for offtake_agreement']

Required for project bankability assessments, debt covenant compliance, and demo']
  contract_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Offtake agreements require a designated commercial manager responsible for negotiation, execution, and ongoing management. Critical for contract accountability, approval workflows, renewal management,']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Long-term offtake agreements are assigned to cost centres representing specific mine sites for revenue allocation, cost recovery tracking, and life-of-mine financial planning. Essential for mine-level']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the buyer counterparty (steel mill, smelter, energy company) who is party to this offtake agreement.']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.lom_plan. Business justification: Offtake agreements are negotiated based on LOM production capacity and reserve life. Commercial teams align contracted volumes with mine production forecasts to ensure deliverability over multi-year c']
  ore_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.ore_reserve. Business justification: Offtake agreements are negotiated and underwritten based on declared ore reserves. Buyers require reserve certification before committing to long-term purchase contracts. This link is essential for co']
  price_index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_index. Business justification: offtake_agreement has benchmark_index (STRING) which is a code reference to the price_index master used in the pricing mechanism. Adding price_index_id FK allows proper normalization. The benchmark_in']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Offtake agreements are allocated to profit centres for segment reporting, commodity-level profitability analysis, and strategic planning. Required for IFRS segment disclosure and management reporting.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Offtake agreements contractually specify the exact saleable product (grade, form, specification) being supplied. Essential for contract compliance tracking, quality certificate preparation, pricing fo']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Long-term offtake agreements require reserve backing from specific tenements for project financing and bankability. Lenders and offtake partners require certification that contracted volumes are suppo']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the offtake agreement, used in commercial correspondence and invoicing.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the offtake agreement structure: term (long-term fixed volume), spot (one-off shipment), framework (umbrella with call-offs), or master (multi-commodity parent).']
  annual_volume_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Annual contracted volume in metric tonnes for term agreements. Null for spot agreements.']
  arbitration_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes an arbitration clause for dispute resolution (e.g., ICC, LCIA, SIAC arbitration).']
  base_price_usd_per_tonne decimal(18,2) [note: 'Base or reference price per metric tonne in USD for fixed-price agreements, or the floor/ceiling price for index-linked agreements.']
  contracted_volume_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total volume of commodity contracted under this agreement, measured in metric tonnes. For term agreements, this is the aggregate volume over the agreement tenure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this offtake agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the agreement pricing and invoicing (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  delivery_basis varchar [note: 'Incoterms delivery basis defining the point at which risk and cost transfer from seller to buyer: FOB (Free on Board), CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight), CFR (Cost and Freight), DAP (Delivered at Place), EXW (Ex Works).']
  destination_port varchar [note: 'Name of the destination port or delivery location specified in the agreement (e.g., Qingdao, Rotterdam, Yokohama).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the offtake agreement becomes legally binding and operative.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the offtake agreement terminates or expires. Null for evergreen or open-ended agreements.']
  force_majeure_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes a force majeure clause allowing suspension or termination due to unforeseeable events (natural disasters, strikes, regulatory changes).']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of the agreement (e.g., Western Australia, England and Wales, New York).']
  loading_port varchar [note: 'Name of the port or terminal from which the commodity will be shipped (e.g., Port Hedland, Dampier, Newcastle).']
  lom_alignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this offtake agreement is aligned with and tracked against the Life of Mine production forecast for revenue planning.']
  maximum_shipment_size_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum cargo size per shipment in metric tonnes, as stipulated in the agreement.']
  minimum_shipment_size_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum cargo size per shipment in metric tonnes, as stipulated in the agreement (e.g., Panamax 60,000 tonnes, Capesize 150,000 tonnes).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this offtake agreement record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional commercial notes, special terms, or amendments related to the offtake agreement.']
  offtake_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the offtake agreement: draft (under negotiation), active (in force), suspended (temporarily paused), expired (reached end date), terminated (cancelled before expiry).']
  payment_term_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date or bill of lading date within which payment is due (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days).']
  price_adjustment_formula varchar [note: 'Mathematical formula or business rule for calculating price adjustments based on quality, moisture, impurities, or market conditions.']
  pricing_mechanism varchar [note: 'Method by which the commodity price is determined: index-linked (tied to Platts, TSI, LME), fixed (agreed price), benchmark (reference price with adjustments), or formula (custom calculation).']
  quality_penalty_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes penalties or price adjustments for commodity quality deviations from specification.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiry by which a party must provide notice of intent to renew or terminate.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Terms governing renewal of the agreement upon expiry: automatic (auto-renews unless terminated), mutual_consent (both parties must agree), buyer_option (buyer can extend), seller_option (seller can extend), none (no renewal).']
  shipment_frequency varchar [note: 'Expected frequency of shipments under the agreement (e.g., monthly, quarterly, as-nominated).']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date on which the offtake agreement was executed and signed by all parties.']
  tenure_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Duration of the agreement in years, calculated from effective date to expiry date. Null for spot agreements.']

  Note: 'Master record of long-term and spot offtake agreements with commodity buyers (steel mills, smelters, energy companies). Captures agreement type (term/spot), commodity, contracted volumes, pricing mechanism (index-linked, fixed, benchmark), delivery basis (FOB/CIF), tenure, renewal terms, and counterparty reference. SSOT for all commercial sales contracts governing commodity sales.'
}

Table sales.commodity_order {

  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Orders may reference the source capital project (specific mine phase or expansion) for production traceability and project revenue attribution. Useful for tracking which capital investments are genera']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Individual commodity orders are allocated to cost centres for revenue recognition, mine-site level sales tracking, and production-sales reconciliation. Essential for operational financial management a']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the buyer counterparty to whom the commodity is being sold. Links to the customer counterparty master.']
  offtake_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent term offtake agreement under which this order was raised. Null for spot orders that are not tied to a long-term contract.']
  order_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Commodity orders require a sales coordinator or trader responsible for order execution, customer communication, and delivery coordination. Essential for operational accountability, customer relationsh']
  price_index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_index. Business justification: commodity_order has benchmark_index (STRING) which is a code reference to the price_index master used for pricing. Adding price_index_id FK allows proper normalization. The benchmark_index string colu']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Short-term commodity orders drive production scheduling. Mine planners schedule extraction and processing to meet specific order delivery windows and grade specifications. Essential for order fulfillm']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Orders specify the exact saleable product being purchased, not just commodity type. Required for order fulfillment, stockpile allocation, quality certificate matching, pricing accuracy, and ensuring s']
  spot_trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.spot_trade. Business justification: commodity_order has spot_market_reference (STRING) which for spot orders should link to the spot_trade record. Adding spot_trade_id FK establishes the relationship between spot orders and their trade ']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the order was cancelled. Null if the order has not been cancelled. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order was cancelled. Null if the order has not been cancelled. Used to track order cancellations and their timing.']
  confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order was confirmed and became a binding commitment. Marks the transition from draft to confirmed status.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity order record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for order lifecycle tracking.']
  delivery_destination varchar [note: 'Named port, terminal, or facility where the commodity is to be delivered. Examples include Port Hedland, Qingdao Port, Rotterdam.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'Incoterms delivery terms defining the point at which risk and cost transfer from seller to buyer. Common terms include Free on Board (FOB), Cost Insurance and Freight (CIF), Cost and Freight (CFR).']
  delivery_window_end date [note: 'End date of the delivery window during which the commodity must be shipped or delivered to the buyer. Defines the latest acceptable delivery date.']
  delivery_window_start date [note: 'Start date of the delivery window during which the commodity must be shipped or delivered to the buyer. Defines the earliest acceptable delivery date.']
  final_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final settled price per unit after all adjustments for assay, quality, and market conditions. Null until final pricing is determined.']
  lom_forecast_period varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) production forecast period against which this order is tracked. Used to reconcile actual sales commitments against long-term production plans.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this order record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity order record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to the order details.']
  order_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information related to this order. Used for operational coordination and audit trail.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the commodity order. Used in communications with buyers, logistics providers, and internal reporting.']
  order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Committed volume of commodity to be delivered under this order. Measured in the unit of measure specified in the order.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commodity order. Tracks progression from initial commitment through delivery and invoicing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|confirmed|nominated|in_transit|delivered|invoiced|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this order is raised under a term offtake agreement or executed as a spot trade. Term orders are part of long-term contracts; spot orders are immediate market transactions.']
  order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the order calculated as order quantity multiplied by the applicable price (provisional or final). Represents the gross revenue commitment for this order.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the commodity price is denominated. Most mining commodities are priced in USD.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the price (e.g., USD per Dry Metric Tonne, USD per pound). Must align with the quantity unit of measure.']
  pricing_basis varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the order price is determined. Fixed pricing is agreed upfront; provisional pricing is subject to final assay and market adjustment; index-linked pricing references a benchmark (e.g., Platts, TSI); formula pricing applies a contractual calculation.']
  provisional_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Initial price per unit agreed at the time of order confirmation, subject to final adjustment based on assay results and market conditions. Used in provisional pricing arrangements.']
  quality_specification varchar [note: 'Detailed quality parameters and tolerances agreed for this order (e.g., Fe content, moisture limits, impurity thresholds). Used for quality assurance and contract compliance.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the order quantity. Common units include Dry Metric Tonnes (DMT), Wet Metric Tonnes (WMT), Metric Tonnes (MT), pounds, and troy ounces depending on commodity type.']
  settlement_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms and conditions agreed for this order (e.g., 30 days after Bill of Lading, Letter of Credit, advance payment). Defines when and how the buyer will pay.']
  shipment_nomination_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment nomination record that allocates this order to a specific vessel or transport movement. Links the order to the physical logistics execution.']
  trade_date date [note: 'Date on which the commodity order was executed or agreed. For spot orders, this is the date the trade was struck. For term orders, this is the date the order was raised against the agreement.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created this order record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Individual sales order raised against a term offtake agreement or executed as a spot trade, representing a confirmed commitment to deliver a specified commodity volume to a buyer. Captures order quantity, commodity grade/specification, delivery window, pricing snapshot (provisional or fixed), order status, shipment nomination reference, order type discriminator (term/spot), trade date, counterparty, settlement terms, pricing basis, and delivery terms. For spot orders: includes agreed spot price, market reference, and immediate/near-term delivery window. Core transactional record driving the order-to-cash lifecycle from commitment through to invoicing. SSOT for all confirmed sales commitments regardless of term or spot origin.'
}

Table sales.shipment_nomination {
  shipment_nomination_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shipment_nomination']
  cargo_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.cargo_shipment. Business justification: Shipment nomination is the planned/nominated shipment; cargo_shipment is the actual executed shipment. Adding cargo_shipment_id to shipment_nomination links the nomination to its execution, enabling t']
  commodity_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent commodity sales order or offtake agreement under which this shipment is nominated.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Shipment nominations represent committed sales volumes allocated to cost centres for production planning and revenue forecasting. Essential for mine-level sales planning and financial forecasting.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or trading counterparty receiving this shipment.']
  primary_shipment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales or logistics user who created and submitted this shipment nomination.']
  rom_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.rom_stockpile. Business justification: Shipment nominations specify which ROM stockpile to draw from for vessel loading. Logistics coordinates loading schedules with available inventory by grade and quality. Critical for stockpile manageme']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Nominations specify the exact product grade and form being loaded onto vessels. Critical for vessel loading instructions, quality certificate preparation, customs documentation, customer acceptance cr']
  vessel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vessel. Business justification: Shipment nomination requires formal vessel reference. Currently has vessel_name and imo_number as strings, but vessel is a master reference table in the domain. Adding vessel_id FK allows proper refer']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date when the counterparty formally accepted this shipment nomination.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when this shipment nomination was cancelled.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why this shipment nomination was cancelled.']
  confirmation_date date [note: 'Date when the shipment nomination was confirmed and locked in for logistics execution.']
  contract_price_usd_per_tonne decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed contract price per tonne for this shipment nomination, expressed in United States Dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shipment nomination record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the contract price and revenue (typically USD for commodity trading).']
  discharge_port_code varchar [note: 'Five-character UN/LOCODE for the destination port or terminal where the commodity will be discharged.']
  discharge_port_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the destination port or terminal where the commodity will be discharged.']
  estimated_discharge_date date [note: 'Estimated date when discharge operations are expected to commence at the destination port.']
  estimated_loading_date date [note: 'Estimated date when loading operations are expected to commence at the loading port.']
  estimated_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total revenue for this shipment nomination, calculated as nominated quantity multiplied by contract price.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) rule governing the transfer of risk and responsibility between seller and buyer. FOB = Free on Board, CIF = Cost Insurance and Freight, CFR = Cost and Freight. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|DDP|EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  laycan_end_date date [note: 'End date of the laycan (lay days cancelling) window during which the vessel must arrive at the loading port. Defines the latest acceptable arrival date before cancellation rights apply.']
  laycan_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the laycan (lay days cancelling) window during which the vessel must arrive at the loading port. Defines the earliest acceptable arrival date.']
  loading_port_code varchar [note: 'Five-character UN/LOCODE for the port or terminal where the commodity will be loaded onto the vessel.']
  loading_port_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the port or terminal where the commodity will be loaded.']
  nominated_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of commodity nominated for this shipment, expressed in the unit of measure specified.']
  nomination_date date [note: 'Date when this shipment nomination was formally submitted to the counterparty or logistics provider.']
  nomination_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this shipment nomination, used in communications with counterparties and logistics providers.']
  nomination_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment nomination in the commercial and logistics workflow.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information related to this shipment nomination.']
  pricing_basis varchar [note: 'Benchmark pricing index or formula used for this shipment (e.g., Platts IODEX 62% Fe, TSI FOB Australia, ICE Newcastle Coal).']
  pricing_period varchar [note: 'Time period over which the benchmark price will be averaged or fixed for this shipment (e.g., M+1, M+2, quotational period).']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the nominated quantity. MT = Metric Tonnes, DMT = Dry Metric Tonnes, WMT = Wet Metric Tonnes.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when the counterparty rejected this shipment nomination.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the counterparty for rejecting this shipment nomination.']
  tolerance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allowable percentage variance (plus or minus) from the nominated quantity, as per contract terms.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for this shipment nomination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shipment nomination record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Formal nomination of a vessel or transport mode for a specific cargo delivery under a commodity order. Records vessel name, laycan window, port of loading, port of discharge, nominated quantity, nomination status, and counterparty acceptance. Drives the logistics execution of commodity sales.'
}

Table sales.benchmark_price {
  benchmark_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the benchmark price record. Primary key for the benchmark price data product.']
  price_index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_index. Business justification: benchmark_price is daily price data for commodity indices; price_index is the index master/reference. benchmark_price should reference price_index to normalize index metadata. The target price_index t']
  assessment_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the methodology used by the publishing agency to assess or calculate this benchmark price (e.g., survey-based, transaction-based, bid-offer spread, volume-weighted average). Important for understanding price reliability and contract applicability.']
  commodity varchar [note: 'The mineral commodity that this benchmark price index represents (e.g., Iron Ore, Copper, Coal, Lithium, Nickel). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Iron Ore|Copper|Coal|Lithium|Nickel|Zinc|Lead|Aluminum|Gold|Silver — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commodity_grade varchar [note: 'The specific grade or quality specification of the commodity (e.g., 62% Fe, 65% Fe, Grade A Copper Cathode, Thermal Coal 6000 kcal/kg NAR, Battery Grade Lithium Carbonate).']
  contract_specification varchar [note: 'Detailed specification of the underlying contract or assessment criteria, including quality parameters, delivery terms, and any adjustments or premiums/discounts applied by the publishing agency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this benchmark price record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the benchmark price (e.g., USD, EUR, AUD, CNY).']
  data_source_url varchar [note: 'The URL or reference to the publishing agency\'s official data source for this benchmark price. Used for audit trail and data lineage verification.']
  delivery_basis varchar [note: 'The Incoterms delivery basis for the benchmark price (e.g., CFR - Cost Insurance and Freight, FOB - Free on Board, CIF - Cost Insurance and Freight). Defines the point at which risk and cost transfer.']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'The geographic location or port associated with the delivery basis (e.g., China, Qingdao Port, Rotterdam, Newcastle). Used in conjunction with delivery_basis to define the pricing point.']
  index_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this benchmark price index became active and available for use in offtake contracts. Defines the start of the index\'s lifecycle.']
  index_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this benchmark price index was discontinued or became inactive. Null for currently active indices.']
  index_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benchmark price index. Active indices are currently published and used in contracts; Inactive or Discontinued indices are no longer published but historical data is retained.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, commentary, or context provided by the publishing agency regarding this price value or index (e.g., market conditions, supply disruptions, data quality caveats).']
  price_close decimal(18,4) [note: 'The closing or settlement price value for the price_date, applicable for exchange-traded indices (e.g., LME). Often used as the official settlement price in contracts.']
  price_date date [note: 'The date for which this benchmark price value is published. This is the business event date representing the pricing period or assessment date, not the publication timestamp.']
  price_high decimal(18,4) [note: 'The highest price value published for the price_date, if the publishing agency provides a price range or intraday high. Used for volatility analysis and risk assessment.']
  price_low decimal(18,4) [note: 'The lowest price value published for the price_date, if the publishing agency provides a price range or intraday low. Used for volatility analysis and risk assessment.']
  price_open decimal(18,4) [note: 'The opening price value for the price_date, applicable for exchange-traded indices (e.g., LME). Used for intraday price movement analysis.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'The type of price represented by this index (e.g., Spot - immediate delivery, Forward - future delivery, Futures - exchange-traded contract, Assessment - agency-assessed price, Settlement - official settlement price).']
  price_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The published benchmark price value for the given price_date, expressed in the unit_of_measure and currency_code. This is the core pricing data used in provisional pricing calculations and final settlement adjustments.']
  publication_frequency varchar [note: 'How frequently the publishing agency releases new price values for this index (e.g., Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Real-time).']
  publication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the publishing agency released this price value. Used to track data freshness and ensure timely price updates for provisional pricing calculations.']
  quotational_period_convention varchar [note: 'The time period convention used for price quotation and settlement in offtake contracts (e.g., Monthly Average of Daily Prices, Spot, Forward Month+1, Forward Month+2, Quarterly Average). Defines how prices are averaged or selected for provisional and final invoicing.']
  revision_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this price value has been revised or corrected by the publishing agency after initial publication. True if revised, False if original publication.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the publishing agency for why this price value was revised (e.g., data correction, methodology adjustment, late transaction inclusion).']
  revision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price value was revised by the publishing agency, if applicable. Null if no revision has occurred.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the benchmark price (e.g., USD/DMT - US Dollars per Dry Metric Tonne, USD/WMT - US Dollars per Wet Metric Tonne, USD/lb - US Dollars per Pound, USD/oz - US Dollars per Troy Ounce).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this benchmark price record was last modified in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  volume_traded decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total volume of commodity traded at this benchmark price on the price_date, if reported by the publishing agency (e.g., for exchange-traded indices). Expressed in the commodity\'s standard unit (tonnes, pounds, ounces).']

  Note: 'Reference master and daily price repository for commodity benchmark indices used in offtake agreement pricing formulas. Captures both the index definition catalog (index code, index name, publishing agency — Platts, TSI, LME, Fastmarkets — commodity, unit of measure, quotational period convention, active status) and the associated daily/periodic price values (price date, price value, currency). Serves as the single source of truth for all benchmark pricing reference data and price history used in index-linked sales contracts, provisional pricing settlement, and quotational period calculations. Subsumes the price index definition catalog — index metadata and price values are co-located in this product.'
}

Table sales.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for invoice']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Sales invoices require approval by authorized commercial or finance personnel for financial controls, segregation of duties, and audit compliance. Critical for revenue recognition controls, fraud prev']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Revenue invoices attributed to specific capital projects enable project-level P&L tracking, NPV realization monitoring, and post-investment reviews. Mining companies need to track actual revenue again']
  cargo_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.cargo_shipment. Business justification: invoice has cargo_reference and bill_of_lading_number (both STRING) which identify the shipment being invoiced. Adding cargo_shipment_id FK establishes proper relationship between invoice and shipment']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Sales invoices are allocated to cost centres for revenue recognition and mine-site P&L tracking. Complements existing GL and profit centre links for complete financial allocation structure.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity buyer or customer to whom this invoice is issued. Links to the counterparty master record.']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Sales invoices post revenue to general ledger accounts. This FK is essential for revenue recognition, financial reporting, and audit trail. Enables proper revenue classification by commodity type, cus']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.payment_term. Business justification: Invoice processing and payment reconciliation require structured payment term data including discount schedules, late payment penalties, and approval authorities. Credit control teams use payment_term']
  price_index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_index. Business justification: invoice has benchmark_index (STRING) which is a code reference to the price_index master used for pricing. Adding price_index_id FK allows proper normalization. The benchmark_index string column becom']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Sales invoices must be attributed to profit centres for segment reporting and profitability analysis. This FK enables revenue tracking by mine site, commodity type, and business unit, supporting manag']
  quality_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quality_certificate. Business justification: invoice has assay_certificate_reference (STRING) which identifies the quality certificate supporting the invoice. Adding quality_certificate_id FK establishes proper relationship. The assay_certificat']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Invoices bill for specific saleable products with defined quality specifications. Essential for revenue recognition by product line, margin analysis, quality dispute resolution, pricing accuracy verif']
  sales_offtake_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying sales contract or offtake agreement governing this invoice. Links to the master sales contract record.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Sales invoices may be allocated to WBS elements when revenue is tied to specific capital projects or development phases, such as pre-production sales or project-specific offtake agreements.']
  adjustment_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity adjustment applied during final settlement due to assay results, moisture content, or other quality factors that differ from provisional assumptions. May be positive or negative.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the provisional-to-final price adjustment process. Indicates whether adjustments are pending, in progress, completed, or not applicable for this invoice.']
  adjustment_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the price adjustment resulting from the difference between provisional and final prices, or from quantity adjustments. Represents the delta between provisional and final invoice value.']
  buyer_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or purchase order number provided by the buyer for invoice reconciliation and payment processing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which the commodity cargo was delivered to the buyer or destination port. Relevant for FOB (Free on Board) and CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight) terms.']
  final_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final benchmark-settled price per unit after the quotational period has concluded. This is the definitive price used for revenue recognition and represents the SSOT for final pricing.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'INCOTERMS code defining the delivery terms and transfer of risk between seller and buyer. Common terms in mining include FOB (Free on Board) and CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique invoice number issued to the commodity buyer. Business identifier for the invoice used in all commercial correspondence and payment reconciliation.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice. Tracks progression from draft through provisional pricing to final settlement and payment. Key states include draft, issued, provisional, final_settled, paid, overdue, disputed, and cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|provisional|final_settled|paid|overdue|disputed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoiced_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of commodity invoiced, typically measured in dry metric tonnes (DMT) or wet metric tonnes (WMT) depending on the commodity and contract terms.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the invoice was formally issued to the buyer. Marks the start of the payment terms period and is used for accounts receivable tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to the invoice data for audit and change management purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the invoice. May include dispute details, payment arrangements, or other commercial clarifications.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is due from the buyer according to the agreed payment terms. Used for accounts receivable aging and cash flow forecasting.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the invoice pricing. Commodity pricing is typically denominated in USD for international trade.']
  provisional_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Initial price per unit applied at the time of shipment or delivery, subject to final settlement based on benchmark pricing during the quotational period. Represents the preliminary pricing before final adjustment.']
  quality_specification varchar [note: 'Description of the quality specifications or grade of the commodity invoiced, including key assay parameters (e.g., Fe content, moisture, impurities) as per contract terms.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the invoiced quantity. Common units include DMT (Dry Metric Tonnes), WMT (Wet Metric Tonnes), or MT (Metric Tonnes).']
  quotational_period_end date [note: 'End date of the quotational period during which benchmark prices are averaged. Marks the conclusion of the pricing window for final settlement calculation.']
  quotational_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the quotational period during which benchmark prices (Platts, TSI) are averaged to determine the final settlement price. Critical for provisional-to-final price adjustment calculation.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the final price settlement was completed and the invoice transitioned from provisional to final pricing. Represents the definitive pricing event for revenue recognition.']
  shipment_date date [note: 'Date on which the commodity cargo was shipped or dispatched to the buyer. Used for revenue recognition timing and logistics tracking.']
  total_invoice_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the invoice after all adjustments, representing the final amount due from the buyer. This is the SSOT for revenue recognition.']

  Note: 'Commercial invoice issued to commodity buyers upon shipment or delivery of a cargo, serving as the SSOT for the full pricing lifecycle from provisional through final settlement including all price adjustments. Captures invoice number, buyer reference, commodity, invoiced quantity, provisional price, final benchmark-settled price, adjustment quantity, adjustment value, total invoice value, currency, payment terms, due date, invoice status, quotational period reference, settlement date, and adjustment status. Tracks the complete provisional-to-final price adjustment lifecycle — provisional adjustments are modelled as state transitions within the invoice record, not as separate entities. SSOT for revenue recognition and final settled price.'
}

Table sales.provisional_adjustment {
  provisional_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the provisional price adjustment record. Primary key for the provisional adjustment entity.']
  cargo_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity shipment for which this provisional adjustment applies. Links to the shipment that was initially invoiced at provisional pricing.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Provisional pricing adjustments impact revenue and are allocated to cost centres for accurate mine-level profitability tracking. Essential for final revenue recognition and variance analysis.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or trading counterparty to whom the commodity was sold under provisional pricing terms.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original provisional invoice that is being adjusted. Links to the billing document issued at provisional pricing.']
  price_index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_index. Business justification: provisional_adjustment has benchmark_source (STRING) which identifies the price index used for final settlement. Adding price_index_id FK allows proper normalization. The benchmark_source string colum']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Provisional adjustments affect segment-level revenue and must be allocated to profit centres for accurate financial reporting and commodity-level performance tracking. Required for IFRS compliance.']
  adjustment_invoice_date date [note: 'Date on which the adjustment invoice or credit/debit note was issued to the counterparty.']
  adjustment_invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice document number issued for the provisional adjustment. May be a credit note (for negative adjustments) or debit note (for positive adjustments).']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the provisional adjustment transaction. Externally visible reference number used in financial reconciliation and customer communication.']
  adjustment_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of commodity (in tonnes or other unit of measure) to which the price adjustment applies. May differ from original shipment quantity due to final assay adjustments.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the provisional adjustment. Tracks progression from calculation through final settlement and revenue recognition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending|calculated|approved|invoiced|settled|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the adjustment event. Distinguishes between initial provisional-to-final settlement, interim adjustments during quotational period, and supplementary corrections.']
  adjustment_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the provisional adjustment, calculated as price difference multiplied by adjustment quantity. Represents the revenue impact to be recognized.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the finance or sales manager who approved the provisional adjustment for invoicing and revenue recognition. Required for financial control and SOX compliance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the provisional adjustment was approved for processing. Audit trail for financial control and compliance.']
  assay_adjustment_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether final assay results triggered a quantity or quality adjustment that impacts the provisional adjustment calculation. True if final assay differed from provisional assay.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the mineral commodity subject to provisional pricing adjustment (e.g., iron ore, copper concentrate, lithium carbonate).']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the master sales contract or offtake agreement under which the provisional pricing terms are defined.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this provisional adjustment record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this adjustment record (e.g., USD, AUD, EUR).']
  final_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final settled price per unit determined by the quotational period benchmark. Represents the actual market price against which revenue is finalized.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this provisional adjustment record. Audit trail for accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this provisional adjustment record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, explanations, or special circumstances related to the provisional adjustment (e.g., dispute resolution details, manual override justification).']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment of the adjustment amount is due from the counterparty (for positive adjustments) or to the counterparty (for negative adjustments).']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Actual date on which payment for the adjustment was received from (or paid to) the counterparty. Null if payment is still outstanding.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of payment collection or refund for the adjustment amount. Tracks whether the financial settlement has been completed.']
  price_difference decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated difference between final price and provisional price per unit. Positive values indicate upward adjustment (additional revenue), negative values indicate downward adjustment (revenue reversal).']
  pricing_formula varchar [note: 'Text description of the contractual pricing formula used to calculate the final price from the benchmark (e.g., \'Average of Platts IODEX CFR China 62% Fe over QP minus USD 2.50 discount\').']
  provisional_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Original provisional price per unit applied at the time of shipment and initial invoicing. Represents the estimated market price used for preliminary revenue recognition.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the adjustment quantity. Common values: MT (Metric Tonne), DMT (Dry Metric Tonne), WMT (Wet Metric Tonne), KG (Kilogram), LB (Pound), OZ (Troy Ounce).']
  quotational_period_end date [note: 'End date of the quotational period over which the final benchmark price is averaged or determined per the sales contract terms.']
  quotational_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the quotational period over which the final benchmark price is averaged or determined per the sales contract terms.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the provisional adjustment is finalized and the adjustment value is recognized in revenue. Represents the accounting event date for revenue true-up.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this provisional adjustment record. Audit trail for accountability.']

  Note: 'Records the price adjustment between provisional and final invoice values for commodity shipments where pricing is settled against a quotational period benchmark. Captures original provisional price, final benchmark-settled price, adjustment quantity, adjustment value, settlement date, and adjustment status. Critical for revenue recognition accuracy.'
}

Table sales.revenue_forecast {
  revenue_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the revenue forecast record. Primary key.']
  block_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.block_model. Business justification: Revenue forecasts must reference the specific block model version used for tonnage and grade assumptions. Critical for forecast reconciliation, audit trail, and understanding forecast variance when mo']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Revenue forecasts in mining are directly tied to specific capital projects (new mines, expansions, brownfield developments) that will generate the forecast production volumes. Essential for project NP']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity being forecast (iron ore, copper, coal, lithium, nickel, etc.).']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Revenue forecasts are allocated to cost centres for mine-level AISC and C1 cost reporting, profitability analysis, and variance tracking. Essential for mine site financial planning and performance eva']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.lom_plan. Business justification: Revenue forecasts must align with LOM production volumes, grades, and mine life. Finance uses this link for NPV calculations, board reporting, and investor guidance. The existing mine_site_id(unlinked']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or operation from which the forecasted production and sales volume originates.']
  offtake_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.offtake_agreement. Business justification: revenue_forecast has sales_contract_reference (STRING) which should link to the offtake_agreement being forecasted. Adding offtake_agreement_id FK (note: using offtake_agreement_id as the column name,']
  ore_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.ore_reserve. Business justification: Ore reserves (proven economically mineable resources) are the primary basis for revenue forecasting in mining. Sales teams must align forecasts with declared reserves for regulatory compliance and inv']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Revenue forecasts in mining are prepared at orebody level for Life-of-Mine planning, linking forecast volumes and prices to specific geological resources. Essential for mine planning and investor repo']
  plan_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mine_plan_scenario. Business justification: Revenue forecasts are scenario-specific, modeling different price/volume/cost assumptions. Finance generates multiple revenue forecasts aligned with mine plan scenarios for sensitivity analysis, risk ']
  price_index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_index. Business justification: revenue_forecast has benchmark_price_source (STRING) which identifies the price index used for revenue forecasting. Adding price_index_id FK allows proper normalization. The benchmark_price_source str']
  primary_revenue_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (typically CFO, Commercial Director, or authorized approver) who approved this forecast version.']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Revenue forecasts must align with profit centre segment reporting for IFRS compliance, board reporting, and commodity/geographic segment performance tracking. Required for management reporting and str']
  resource_estimate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.resource_estimate. Business justification: Revenue forecasts in mining are built directly on resource estimates for LOM planning, investor reporting, and budget preparation. This is a fundamental business relationship - finance teams reference']
  resource_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.resource_statement. Business justification: Revenue forecasts must align with published JORC/NI 43-101 resource statements for regulatory compliance and investor disclosure. Links forecast assumptions to the formal resource declaration underpin']
  revenue_prepared_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (typically Sales Analyst, Commercial Planner, or Finance Analyst) who prepared this forecast.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this forecast version was formally approved.']
  assumed_benchmark_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The assumed benchmark price per unit used in the revenue forecast (e.g., Platts Iron Ore Index, LME Copper, TSI Coal Index).']
  assumptions_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing key assumptions, market conditions, risk factors, or qualitative commentary supporting this forecast (e.g., \'Assumes stable Chinese steel demand\', \'Includes impact of new mine ramp-up\').']
  board_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forecast version is designated for board-level reporting and external guidance (True = board-level, False = internal planning only).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this forecast record was first created in the system.']
  estimated_revenue_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'The total estimated revenue for the forecast period, calculated as forecast volume × assumed benchmark price × price adjustment factor.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter to which this forecast period belongs: Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this forecast period belongs (e.g., 2024, 2025).']
  forecast_confidence_level varchar [note: 'The confidence level assigned to this forecast based on market conditions, contract certainty, and production reliability: high (>80% confidence), medium (50-80% confidence), low (<50% confidence).']
  forecast_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the forecast period (e.g., end of fiscal quarter or year).']
  forecast_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the forecast period (e.g., beginning of fiscal quarter or year).']
  forecast_preparation_date date [note: 'The date on which this forecast was prepared or last updated.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the forecast: draft (in preparation), submitted (awaiting review), under_review (being evaluated), approved (approved for use), rejected (not approved), superseded (replaced by newer version).']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast: budget (initial annual plan), revised (mid-year update), latest_estimate (current best estimate), board_guidance (board-approved forecast), lom_aligned (aligned to Life of Mine production schedule).']
  forecast_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the forecast (e.g., \'Q1-2024-V1\', \'Annual-2024-V3\') to track revisions and iterations.']
  forecast_volume_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Forecasted sales volume in metric tonnes for the commodity in the specified period.']
  forecast_volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the forecast volume: tonnes (metric tonnes), dmt (dry metric tonnes), wmt (wet metric tonnes), pounds, ounces.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) applicable to the forecast: FOB (Free on Board), CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight), CFR (Cost and Freight), EXW (Ex Works), FCA (Free Carrier), DAP (Delivered at Place), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|EXW|FCA|DAP|DDP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this forecast record was last modified or updated.']
  lom_production_alignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forecast is aligned with the approved Life of Mine (LOM) production schedule (True = aligned, False = not aligned or deviation exists).']
  price_adjustment_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the benchmark price to reflect quality premiums, discounts, freight differentials, or contract-specific pricing terms (e.g., 0.95 for 5% discount, 1.10 for 10% premium).']
  pricing_mechanism varchar [note: 'The pricing mechanism used for this forecast: spot (spot market pricing), term_fixed (fixed price term contract), term_provisional (provisional pricing with final settlement), index_linked (linked to benchmark index), formula_based (custom pricing formula).']
  product_grade varchar [note: 'The grade or quality specification of the commodity being forecast (e.g., \'62% Fe Iron Ore Fines\', \'Coking Coal Premium Low Vol\', \'Copper Concentrate 28% Cu\').']
  revenue_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated revenue amount (e.g., USD for US Dollar, AUD for Australian Dollar).']
  risk_adjustment_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustment applied to the forecast to account for identified risks (e.g., market volatility, production uncertainty, geopolitical risk). Negative values indicate downward risk adjustment.']
  shipment_destination_region varchar [note: 'The primary geographic region or market for the forecasted shipments (e.g., China, Europe, Japan, India).']
  variance_to_prior_forecast_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'The variance in revenue amount compared to the immediately prior forecast version for the same period (positive indicates increase, negative indicates decrease).']
  variance_to_prior_forecast_percentage decimal(10,4) [note: 'The percentage variance in revenue compared to the immediately prior forecast version for the same period.']

  Note: 'Forward-looking revenue forecast for commodity sales aligned to Life of Mine (LOM) production schedules and annual sales volume plans. Captures forecast period, commodity, forecast volume, assumed benchmark price, estimated revenue, currency, forecast version, approval status, and variance to prior forecast. Supports CFO reporting, commercial planning, and board-level guidance against LOM production targets. One record per commodity per forecast period per version.'
}

Table sales.volume_plan {
  volume_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for volume_plan']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Sales volume plans must align with capital project delivery schedules (mine commissioning dates, plant expansion completions). Critical for production-sales alignment, LOM planning, and ensuring offta']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Sales volume plans are prepared by cost centre to align production capacity with sales commitments. Essential for mine-level P&L forecasting, production planning, and cost recovery analysis.']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.lom_plan. Business justification: Sales volume plans must align with LOM production capacity and reserve depletion schedules. Commercial teams plan annual sales volumes within mine production constraints to ensure contracted volumes a']
  ore_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.ore_reserve. Business justification: Sales volume planning must align with declared ore reserves and mine life plans. The volume_plan already has lom_production_forecast_alignment flag, and linking to the specific reserve estimate ensure']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Sales volume plans are developed per orebody to align with mine production schedules and geological resource availability. Essential for matching sales commitments to specific ore sources, particularl']
  price_index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_index. Business justification: volume_plan has benchmark_index (STRING) which is a code reference to the price_index master. Adding price_index_id FK allows proper normalization and resolves the silo issue. The benchmark_index stri']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Volume plans drive profit centre revenue targets and are required for segment reporting, management performance evaluation, and commodity-level strategic planning. Links sales planning to financial re']
  resource_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.resource_statement. Business justification: Volume plans must be constrained by published resource statements to ensure sales commitments do not exceed declared resources. Critical governance control for preventing over-commitment and ensuring ']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Sales volume plans must align with tenement production capacity, ore reserve estimates, and regulatory extraction limits. Quarterly sales planning requires tenement-level capacity constraints to ensur']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the volume plan was formally approved by commercial leadership.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive or manager who approved the volume plan.']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Type of mineral commodity covered by this volume plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this volume plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for planned pricing (e.g., USD, AUD, EUR).']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Type of customer or end-user segment targeted by this volume plan.']
  delivery_basis varchar [note: 'Incoterms delivery basis defining responsibility transfer point. FOB (Free on Board) and CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight) are most common in mining commodity sales.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary destination market.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this volume plan record was last updated.']
  logistics_mode varchar [note: 'Primary transportation mode planned for commodity delivery to market.']
  lom_production_forecast_alignment varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this volume plan is aligned with the Life of Mine production forecast. Critical for ensuring offtake agreements match production capacity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, or constraints related to the volume plan.']
  offtake_coverage_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of planned volume already covered by executed offtake agreements. Used to track commercial risk and contract coverage.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the volume plan, used for external reference and reporting.']
  plan_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this volume plan becomes effective and is used for commercial baseline.']
  plan_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this volume plan ceases to be effective. Typically end of quarter or year.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the sales volume plan for business user identification.']
  plan_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter for which this volume plan applies. ANNUAL indicates a full-year plan.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the volume plan. ACTIVE indicates the plan is in effect for the period.']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which this volume plan applies.']
  planned_price_per_tonne decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target or assumed price per metric tonne used for revenue planning. Confidential commercial information.']
  planned_volume_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Target sales volume in metric tonnes for the plan period. Core commercial baseline for offtake coverage.']
  port_of_loading varchar [note: 'Name of the port or terminal from which the commodity will be shipped for FOB/CIF deliveries.']
  pricing_mechanism varchar [note: 'Method by which the commodity price is determined. SPOT for immediate market price, TERM_FIXED for locked-in price, TERM_INDEX for benchmark-linked pricing (e.g., Platts, TSI).']
  product_grade varchar [note: 'Specification grade of the commodity product (e.g., Fe 62%, Cu 25%, Thermal Coal 6000 kcal/kg). Defines quality and pricing tier.']
  product_specification_code varchar [note: 'Reference code for the detailed product specification document defining quality parameters, impurity limits, and physical characteristics.']
  revenue_forecast_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected revenue for the plan period calculated from planned volume and price. Used for financial planning and budgeting.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Commercial risk assessment for achieving the planned volume and pricing targets.']
  shipment_nomination_window varchar [note: 'Time period or month range during which shipments are planned to occur (e.g., Jan-Mar 2024, Q2 2024).']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Geographic market or region where the commodity is planned to be sold (e.g., China, Europe, Domestic).']
  variance_tolerance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable percentage variance from planned volume before triggering plan revision or escalation.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the volume plan. Incremented when plan is revised or updated.']

  Note: 'Annual and quarterly sales volume plan by commodity, product grade, and market destination. Captures planned volumes, target markets, delivery basis (FOB/CIF), planned pricing mechanism, and alignment to LOM production forecast. Serves as the commercial baseline for offtake agreement coverage and revenue planning.'
}

Table sales.cargo_shipment {
  cargo_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cargo shipment record. Primary key. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  commodity_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.commodity_order. Business justification: cargo_shipment has sales_contract_number (STRING) which represents the link to the originating commodity_order. Adding commodity_order_id FK establishes proper parent-child relationship between order ']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Shipments are allocated to cost centres for revenue recognition timing and mine-level sales volume tracking. Essential for production-sales reconciliation and financial reporting.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or counterparty who is the consignee or buyer of this cargo shipment.']
  freight_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.freight_contract. Business justification: Each cargo shipment is executed under a governing freight contract. Adding freight_contract_id links the shipment to its contractual terms, enabling freight cost validation and contract utilization tr']
  letter_of_credit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.letter_of_credit. Business justification: Documentary credit compliance verification requires linking shipments to structured LC records with issuing bank details, expiry dates, and document requirements. Trade finance settlement and shipping']
  logistics_coordinator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Cargo shipments require a logistics coordinator responsible for vessel operations, port coordination, documentation, and demurrage management. Critical for operational accountability, dispute resoluti']
  rom_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.rom_stockpile. Business justification: Cargo shipments are loaded from specific ROM stockpiles. Logistics tracks which stockpile supplied each vessel for inventory reconciliation, grade traceability, and stockpile depletion tracking. Essen']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Shipments transport specific saleable products with defined quality parameters. Required for bill of lading accuracy, quality certificate linkage, customs HS code determination, IMSBC classification c']
  vessel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vessel. Business justification: Cargo shipment must reference the vessel used for transport. Currently has vessel_name and vessel_imo_number as strings, but vessel is a master reference table. Adding vessel_id FK enables proper refe']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Unique bill of lading number issued by the carrier. Legal document of title and receipt of goods. Externally-known business identifier for this shipment.']
  bl_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the bill of lading was issued by the carrier. Principal business event timestamp for legal title transfer.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the shipping line or carrier responsible for transporting the cargo.']
  consignee_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the consignee (importer or buyer) as stated on the bill of lading.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cargo shipment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction (e.g., USD, AUD, EUR).']
  demurrage_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Demurrage charges incurred when laytime used exceeds laytime allowed, expressed in United States Dollars.']
  despatch_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Despatch money earned when laytime used is less than laytime allowed, expressed in United States Dollars.']
  discharge_commenced_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when cargo discharge operations commenced at the port of discharge.']
  discharge_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when cargo discharge operations were completed at the port of discharge.']
