  documentary_credit_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment documentation complies with the terms and conditions of the letter of credit. True if compliant, False if discrepancies exist.']
  insurance_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total insurance cost for this shipment in United States Dollars. Applicable when insurance is borne by the seller under CIF terms.']
  invoice_value_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoiced value of the cargo shipment in United States Dollars, used for revenue recognition and customs declaration.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cargo shipment record was last modified in the system.']
  laytime_allowed_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total laytime (loading and discharge time) allowed under the charter party or sales contract, expressed in hours.']
  laytime_used_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual laytime used for loading and discharge operations, expressed in hours.']
  loading_commenced_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when cargo loading operations commenced at the port of loading.']
  loading_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when cargo loading operations were completed at the port of loading.']
  moisture_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of moisture content in the shipped cargo, used to calculate dry weight from wet weight.']
  net_dry_weight_dmt decimal(18,3) [note: 'Net weight of cargo after adjusting for moisture content, expressed in dry metric tonnes. Used for invoicing and revenue recognition.']
  notify_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party to be notified upon arrival of the cargo at the discharge port, as stated on the bill of lading.']
  port_of_discharge_code varchar [note: 'Five-letter UN/LOCODE for the port where cargo discharge occurred or is planned.']
  port_of_loading_code varchar [note: 'Five-letter UN/LOCODE for the port where cargo loading occurred.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or comments related to the cargo shipment.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cargo shipment in the logistics workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|nominated|loading|loaded|in_transit|discharged|completed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  shipped_quantity_wmt decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of cargo shipped in wet metric tonnes, including moisture content.']
  shipper_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the shipper (exporter) as stated on the bill of lading.']
  terminal_name varchar [note: 'Name of the terminal or berth at the port of loading where the cargo was loaded.']
  voyage_number varchar [note: 'Unique voyage number assigned by the carrier for this specific sailing or trip.']

  Note: 'Operational record of an actual commodity cargo shipment from port of loading to port of discharge, serving as the SSOT for physical logistics execution, legal title transfer (bill of lading), and documentary credit presentation. Captures vessel name, BL number, BL issue date, shipper, consignee, notify party, loading/discharge dates, shipped quantity, moisture-adjusted dry weight, commodity grade, freight terms (FOB/CIF), port/terminal/berth allocation, shipment status, carrier details, gross weight, net dry weight, moisture content, and documentary credit compliance fields. The bill of lading is modelled as an integral component of this record — BL attributes (legal title document, carrier acknowledgment) are first-class fields within cargo_shipment, not a separate entity.'
}

Table sales.bill_of_lading {
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bill of lading record. Primary key for the bill of lading entity.']
  cargo_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment record that this bill of lading documents.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Bills of lading trigger revenue recognition events allocated to cost centres for mine-level sales tracking. Essential for revenue recognition timing and production-sales reconciliation.']
  issued_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Bills of lading require issuance by authorized shipping personnel for legal validity, customs clearance, and title transfer. Essential for accountability, regulatory compliance, and dispute resolution']
  primary_bill_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipper party (typically the mining company or seller) consigning the cargo.']
  sales_offtake_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying sales contract or offtake agreement that governs this shipment.']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_specification. Business justification: Bills of lading reference the contractual product specification being shipped. Required for customs clearance, quality dispute resolution, contractual compliance verification, and ensuring shipping do']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipping carrier or freight company issuing the bill of lading.']
  vessel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel transporting the commodity cargo.']
  bl_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique bill of lading number issued by the carrier. Legal document identifier used for title transfer and documentary credit in commodity trade.']
  bl_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bill of lading document.']
  bl_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bill of lading document type indicating its legal status and usage.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the shipping carrier or freight company issuing the bill of lading.']
  commodity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the commodity cargo being shipped including mineral type, grade, and specifications as stated on the bill of lading.']
  consignee_address varchar [note: 'Full address of the consignee party including street, city, state, and country.']
  consignee_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the consignee party as stated on the bill of lading.']
  container_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of container identification numbers if cargo is shipped in containers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bill of lading record was first created in the system.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Indicates whether freight charges are prepaid by the shipper or collected from the consignee.']
  gross_weight_mt decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total weight of the commodity cargo including moisture and packaging, measured in metric tonnes.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'International standardized system of names and numbers to classify traded products for customs and tariff purposes.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Standard trade terms defining the responsibilities of buyers and sellers for the delivery of goods. Common terms in mining include Free on Board (FOB) and Cost Insurance and Freight (CIF). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|FAS|EXW|DDP|DAP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_negotiable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bill of lading is negotiable (transferable title document) or non-negotiable (straight consignment).']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the bill of lading was issued by the carrier. Critical for documentary credit and title transfer timing.']
  marks_and_numbers varchar [note: 'Identifying marks, numbers, or labels on the packages or containers for cargo identification and tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bill of lading record was last modified in the system.']
  moisture_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of moisture content in the commodity cargo as determined by laboratory assay. Used to calculate net dry weight for pricing.']
  net_dry_weight_mt decimal(18,3) [note: 'Weight of the commodity cargo on a dry basis excluding all moisture content, measured in metric tonnes. Critical for pricing and settlement in commodity trade.']
  net_weight_mt decimal(18,3) [note: 'Weight of the commodity cargo excluding packaging and moisture, measured in metric tonnes.']
  notify_party_address varchar [note: 'Full address of the notify party including contact details.']
  notify_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party to be notified upon arrival of the cargo at the destination port.']
  number_of_originals varchar [note: 'Total number of original bill of lading documents issued. Typically 3 originals are issued in international trade.']
  number_of_packages varchar [note: 'Total count of packages, containers, or units comprising the shipment.']
  on_board_date date [note: 'Date when the cargo was loaded on board the vessel. May differ from issue date for received-for-shipment bills of lading.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging or container used for the commodity cargo.']
  place_of_delivery varchar [note: 'Final destination where the cargo will be delivered to the consignee, which may differ from the port of discharge for multimodal transport.']
  place_of_receipt varchar [note: 'Location where the carrier received the cargo from the shipper, which may differ from the port of loading for multimodal transport.']
  port_of_discharge_code varchar [note: 'United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations (UN/LOCODE) for the port where cargo is unloaded from the vessel.']
  port_of_discharge_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the port where the commodity cargo is unloaded from the vessel.']
  port_of_loading_code varchar [note: 'United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations (UN/LOCODE) for the port where cargo is loaded onto the vessel.']
  port_of_loading_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the port where the commodity cargo is loaded onto the vessel.']
  shipper_address varchar [note: 'Full address of the shipper party including street, city, state, and country.']
  shipper_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the shipper party as stated on the bill of lading.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Additional handling instructions, clauses, or remarks specific to this shipment as noted on the bill of lading.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vessel carrying the commodity cargo as stated on the bill of lading.']
  voyage_number varchar [note: 'Voyage or trip number assigned by the carrier for this specific journey.']

  Note: 'Legal shipping document issued by the carrier confirming receipt of commodity cargo for shipment. Captures BL number, vessel, shipper, consignee, port of loading, port of discharge, commodity description, gross weight, net dry weight, moisture content, and date of issue. SSOT for title transfer and documentary credit in commodity trade.'
}

Table sales.quality_certificate {
  quality_certificate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for quality_certificate']
  authorized_signatory_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Quality certificates require sign-off by authorized personnel (chief geologist, quality manager) for regulatory compliance and legal validity. Essential for dispute resolution, audit trails, and ensur']
  cargo_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.cargo_shipment. Business justification: quality_certificate has cargo_reference and bill_of_lading_number (both STRING) which identify the shipment being certified. Adding cargo_shipment_id FK establishes proper relationship between certifi']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Quality certificates drive pricing adjustments that impact cost centre revenue and AISC calculations. Essential for accurate mine-level financial performance tracking and quality-based revenue adjustm']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or counterparty receiving this quality certificate.']
  laboratory_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory_sample. Business justification: Sales quality certificates are based on laboratory assay results for the shipped cargo. This FK links the commercial quality certificate to the underlying LIMS sample record, enabling traceability fro']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_specification. Business justification: Quality certificates verify cargo compliance against contractual product specifications. Critical for contractual compliance verification, penalty/bonus calculation, customer acceptance, dispute resol']
  alumina_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of alumina content in the ore sample. Penalty element for iron ore quality.']
  analysis_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory analysis was completed and results finalized.']
  benchmark_index_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the pricing benchmark index used for settlement (e.g., Platts IODEX 62% Fe, TSI 62% Fe CFR China).']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique certificate number issued by the laboratory or inspection body for this cargo shipment.']
  certificate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quality certificate in the commercial workflow.']
  certificate_type varchar [note: 'Type of quality certificate issued for the shipment (e.g., certificate of analysis, inspection certificate, weight certificate).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this quality certificate record was first created in the system.']
  discharge_port varchar [note: 'Destination port where the cargo will be or was discharged.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quality certificate results are under dispute by the counterparty.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for dispute if the certificate is contested by the counterparty.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the certificate is valid and binding for commercial settlement purposes.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the certificate is no longer valid for commercial purposes. Nullable for certificates without expiry.']
  iron_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of iron content in the ore sample. Key quality parameter for iron ore shipments.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the quality certificate was officially issued by the laboratory or inspection body.']
  issuing_laboratory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited laboratory or inspection body that issued the quality certificate.']
  laboratory_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Accreditation number of the issuing laboratory under ISO/IEC 17025 or equivalent standard.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this quality certificate record was last updated in the system.']
  loading_port varchar [note: 'Port where the cargo was loaded onto the vessel.']
  loss_on_ignition_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of weight loss when the sample is heated to high temperature. Indicates volatile content and combined water.']
  moisture_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of moisture content in the ore sample. Affects payable weight and pricing.']
  penalty_deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary penalty deduction applied to the invoice based on quality parameters exceeding contractual limits (e.g., high silica, high alumina).']
  penalty_deduction_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty deduction amount.']
  phosphorus_content_percent decimal(5,3) [note: 'Percentage of phosphorus content in the ore sample. Penalty element affecting steel quality.']
  price_adjustment_factor decimal(8,5) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the base price based on quality results. Values above 1.0 indicate premium, below 1.0 indicate discount.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, notes, or qualifications provided by the laboratory or inspection body regarding the analysis or certificate.']
  sales_contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying sales contract or offtake agreement governing this shipment.']
  sample_collection_date date [note: 'Date when the physical samples were collected from the cargo for laboratory analysis.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'Method used to collect samples for analysis (e.g., mechanical sampling, manual sampling, composite sampling).']
  shipment_quantity_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of commodity in the shipment covered by this certificate, measured in metric tonnes.']
  silica_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of silica content in the ore sample. Penalty element for iron ore quality.']
  size_fraction_minus_6_3mm_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of material passing through 6.3mm screen. Part of size distribution specification for fines content.']
  size_fraction_plus_31_5mm_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of material retained on 31.5mm screen. Part of size distribution specification for lump ore.']
  sulfur_content_percent decimal(5,3) [note: 'Percentage of sulfur content in the ore sample. Penalty element affecting steel quality.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vessel carrying the cargo shipment for which this certificate was issued.']

  Note: 'Certificate of analysis issued for a commodity cargo shipment confirming the chemical and physical quality specifications (grade, moisture, size distribution, penalty elements). Captures certificate number, cargo reference, assay results, moisture content, size fractions, penalty deductions, and issuing laboratory. Drives final invoice price adjustments.'
}

Table sales.spot_trade {
  spot_trade_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the spot commodity trade transaction. Primary key.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Spot trades are allocated to cost centres for immediate revenue recognition and variance-to-plan analysis at mine level. Required for operational financial tracking and opportunistic sales management.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the buyer or seller counterparty involved in this spot trade transaction.']
  price_index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_index. Business justification: spot_trade has pricing_index_reference (STRING) which should be normalized to a FK to price_index master. This allows proper referential integrity for commodity pricing indices used in spot trades. Th']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Spot trades must be allocated to profit centres for segment reporting and commodity-level performance tracking. Essential for financial reporting and management analysis of spot market activity.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Spot trades specify exact product grade being traded on the spot market. Essential for pricing accuracy, delivery specification clarity, quality commitment definition, and ensuring trade execution mat']
  trader_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Spot trades require attribution to the specific trader who executed the transaction for performance measurement, risk management, and trading desk accountability. Replaces denormalized trader_name wit']
  agreed_price_per_tonne decimal(18,4) [note: 'The negotiated unit price per metric tonne for this spot trade. Business-confidential pricing information.']
  broker_commission_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Commission rate (as a decimal percentage) paid to the broker for facilitating the trade. Business-confidential commercial information.']
  broker_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a third-party broker or intermediary was involved in facilitating this spot trade transaction.']
  broker_name varchar [note: 'Name of the brokerage firm or intermediary that facilitated the spot trade, if applicable.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal sales contract or confirmation document governing this spot trade.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this spot trade record was first created in the data system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the trade transaction (typically USD for commodity trades).']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Named port, terminal, or location where commodity delivery will occur as per the delivery terms (e.g., Port Hedland, Qingdao Port, Rotterdam).']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) defining delivery obligations, risk transfer, and cost allocation between buyer and seller (e.g., FOB - Free on Board, CIF - Cost Insurance and Freight, CFR - Cost and Freight).']
  delivery_window_end date [note: 'The latest date within the agreed delivery period for this spot trade. Defines the delivery deadline.']
  delivery_window_start date [note: 'The earliest date within the agreed delivery period for this spot trade. Spot trades typically have near-term delivery windows.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this spot trade record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this spot trade record was most recently updated or modified.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment conditions for the spot trade (e.g., 30 days from bill of lading, letter of credit, advance payment, cash against documents).']
  pricing_basis varchar [note: 'The benchmark or pricing mechanism used to determine the trade price (e.g., Platts IODEX, TSI, Metal Bulletin, Argus, fixed price, index-linked).']
  quality_adjustment_clause varchar [note: 'Terms defining price adjustments based on actual delivered quality versus specification (e.g., penalties for off-spec material, bonuses for premium quality).']
  quality_specification varchar [note: 'Detailed quality parameters and specifications agreed for the commodity (e.g., Fe content, moisture, impurities, size distribution). May reference standard specifications or custom requirements.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which financial settlement and payment for the spot trade is due or completed.']
  total_trade_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the spot trade, calculated as trade volume multiplied by agreed price. Business-confidential financial information.']
  trade_date date [note: 'The date on which the spot trade was executed and agreed between parties. Distinct from settlement or delivery dates.']
  trade_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or remarks related to this spot trade transaction.']
  trade_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the spot trade, used in contracts and communications.']
  trade_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the spot trade transaction. Tracks progression from draft through settlement or cancellation.']
  trade_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the spot trade was executed, including timezone information.']
  trade_volume_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of commodity being traded, measured in metric tonnes (dry metric tonnes for bulk commodities).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created this spot trade record in the system.']

  Note: 'Record of a spot commodity trade executed outside of a term offtake agreement. Captures trade date, commodity, volume, agreed price, pricing basis, delivery terms, counterparty, settlement date, and trade status. Distinct from term offtake orders — spot trades are one-off market transactions with immediate or near-term delivery.'
}

Table sales.offtake_schedule {
  offtake_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the offtake schedule record. Primary key.']
  cargo_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.cargo_shipment. Business justification: Offtake schedule represents the planned delivery schedule; cargo_shipment is the actual executed shipment. Adding cargo_shipment_id links the schedule line to its fulfillment shipment, enabling schedu']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Offtake schedules detail lifting plans by cost centre for production-sales alignment and revenue timing. Essential for mine-level operational and financial planning.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the buyer counterparty for this scheduled lifting.']
  offtake_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent term offtake agreement from which this schedule is derived.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Offtake schedules specify which product grade is being lifted in each period. Required for production planning alignment, stockpile allocation, blending strategy, vessel nomination accuracy, and ensur']
  shipment_nomination_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment nomination record associated with this scheduled lifting, if nominated.']
  benchmark_index varchar [note: 'Commodity pricing benchmark index used for this scheduled lifting. Platts, TSI (The Steel Index), Argus, LME (London Metal Exchange), Metal Bulletin.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this offtake schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the scheduled price and revenue amounts.']
  delivery_basis varchar [note: 'Incoterms delivery basis defining the transfer of risk and cost responsibility for this scheduled shipment. FOB (Free on Board), CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight), CFR (Cost and Freight), DAP (Delivered at Place), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid), EXW (Ex Works).']
  delivery_window_end_date date [note: 'End date of the delivery window during which the cargo lifting must occur.']
  delivery_window_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the delivery window during which the cargo lifting must occur.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the destination country for this scheduled delivery.']
  destination_port varchar [note: 'Port facility where the commodity is scheduled to be delivered.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the scheduled lifting is being fulfilled according to plan, tracking shortfall or excess against contracted volumes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|on_track|at_risk|delayed|fulfilled|shortfall|excess — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this offtake schedule record was last updated.']
  lifting_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the cargo lifting within the schedule period.']
  lom_alignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scheduled lifting is aligned with the Life of Mine production forecast.']
  nominated_vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vessel nominated by the buyer for this cargo lifting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, exceptions, or special instructions for this scheduled lifting.']
  port_allocation_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific port berth or allocation slot assigned for this lifting.']
  port_of_loading varchar [note: 'Port facility from which the commodity is scheduled to be loaded for shipment.']
  pricing_mechanism varchar [note: 'Pricing method applied to this scheduled lifting as per the offtake agreement terms.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the offtake schedule, typically combining agreement code and period identifier.']
  schedule_month varchar [note: 'Calendar month (1-12) to which this offtake schedule applies, if applicable.']
  schedule_period_type varchar [note: 'Frequency of scheduled deliveries under this offtake schedule.']
  schedule_quarter varchar [note: 'Calendar quarter (1-4) to which this offtake schedule applies, if applicable.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this offtake schedule lifting.']
  schedule_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year to which this offtake schedule applies.']
  scheduled_lifting_date date [note: 'Target date for the cargo lifting within the delivery window.']
  scheduled_price_per_tonne decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned or estimated price per tonne for this scheduled lifting at the time of schedule creation.']
  scheduled_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned revenue amount for this scheduled lifting, calculated as scheduled volume multiplied by scheduled price.']
  scheduled_volume_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned volume of commodity to be delivered in this lifting, measured in metric tonnes.']
  tolerance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allowable variance percentage for the scheduled volume as per the offtake agreement terms.']
  vessel_nomination_deadline date [note: 'Deadline by which the buyer must nominate the vessel for this cargo lifting.']
  volume_variance_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Difference between scheduled and actual volume (actual minus scheduled), measured in metric tonnes.']

  Note: 'Periodic delivery schedule derived from a term offtake agreement, specifying the planned cargo liftings by period (monthly/quarterly), volume per lifting, delivery window, and port allocation. Tracks scheduled vs actual liftings and identifies shortfall or excess against contracted volumes.'
}

Table sales.performance_actual {
  performance_actual_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for performance_actual']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Actual sales performance is recorded against cost centres for variance analysis, AISC calculation, and mine-level profitability tracking. Core requirement for operational performance reporting and cos']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or counterparty to whom the commodity was sold.']
  offtake_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the offtake agreement under which this sale was executed, if applicable.']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Actual sales performance is tracked and reconciled by orebody to compare realized revenue against orebody-specific forecasts and resource models. Essential for orebody-level P&L analysis, reserve depl']
  prepared_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Sales performance actuals require preparation by commercial analysts for management reporting, variance analysis, and board reporting. Critical for accountability, audit trails, and ensuring data qual']
  price_index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_index. Business justification: performance_actual has benchmark_index (STRING) which is a code reference to the price_index master used for performance measurement. Adding price_index_id FK allows proper normalization. The benchmar']
  revenue_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.revenue_forecast. Business justification: Performance actuals should reference the revenue forecast they are measured against. This enables variance analysis between forecast and actual. The FK allows tracking which forecast version the actua']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Performance tracking by specific product grade is essential for product line profitability analysis, pricing strategy optimization, portfolio management decisions, and understanding margin contributio']
  actual_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total revenue realized from commodity sales during the period, in the reporting currency.']
  actual_volume_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'The actual volume of commodity sold during the period, measured in metric tonnes.']
  aisc_per_tonne decimal(12,2) [note: 'The All-In Sustaining Cost per tonne for the commodity during the period, used for margin calculation.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the actuals record was approved for final posting.']
  benchmark_price_per_tonne decimal(12,2) [note: 'The benchmark or reference price per tonne for the commodity during the period (e.g., Platts, TSI index price).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this actuals record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, AUD, EUR).']
  delivery_basis varchar [note: 'The Incoterms delivery basis for the sale (e.g., FOB - Free on Board, CIF - Cost Insurance and Freight, CFR - Cost and Freight).']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination country for the commodity shipment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this actuals record was last modified or updated.']
  lom_forecast_alignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the actual performance aligns with the Life of Mine production forecast (True/False).']
  margin_per_tonne decimal(12,2) [note: 'The AISC-adjusted margin per tonne, calculated as realised price minus AISC per tonne.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the actual performance, including explanations for significant variances or adjustments.']
  performance_status varchar [note: 'The status of the actuals record (e.g., confirmed, provisional, adjusted, final) indicating the level of finalization.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the reporting period for which actuals are posted.']
  period_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month (1-12) for which this actual performance is recorded.']
  period_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter (1-4) for which this actual performance is recorded.']
  period_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year for which this actual performance is recorded (e.g., 2024).']
  planned_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The planned or budgeted revenue for the period, in the reporting currency, used for variance calculation.']
  planned_volume_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'The planned or budgeted volume of commodity for the period, measured in metric tonnes, used for variance calculation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the actual performance record was posted to the system.']
  price_variance_per_tonne decimal(12,2) [note: 'The variance between realised price and benchmark price per tonne (realised minus benchmark).']
  price_variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage variance between realised and benchmark price, calculated as (realised - benchmark) / benchmark * 100.']
  realised_price_per_tonne decimal(12,2) [note: 'The actual average price achieved per tonne of commodity sold during the period, calculated as actual revenue divided by actual volume.']
  revenue_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The variance between actual and planned revenue (actual minus planned), in the reporting currency.']
  revenue_variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage variance between actual and planned revenue, calculated as (actual - planned) / planned * 100.']
  target_market varchar [note: 'The primary geographic market or region to which the commodity was sold (e.g., China, Europe, Domestic).']
  total_margin_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total AISC-adjusted margin for the period, calculated as margin per tonne multiplied by actual volume.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'The version number of this actuals record, incremented with each modification to support audit trail and change tracking.']
  volume_variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage variance between actual and planned volume, calculated as (actual - planned) / planned * 100.']
  volume_variance_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'The variance between actual and planned volume (actual minus planned), measured in metric tonnes.']

  Note: 'Periodic (monthly/quarterly) actuals record of commodity sales performance against plan, capturing actual volumes sold, actual revenue, realised price, benchmark price achieved, variance to plan, and AISC-adjusted margin. Operational record — not an aggregation layer — representing the confirmed actuals posted per period per commodity.'
}

Table sales.freight_contract {
  freight_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the freight contract record. Primary key.']
  contract_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Freight contracts require a commercial manager responsible for negotiation, execution, and ongoing management. Critical for contract accountability, renewal management, freight cost optimization, and ']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Freight contracts impact net-back pricing and are allocated to cost centres for AISC calculation, delivered cost analysis, and mine-level profitability. Essential for cost management and pricing decis']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipping company, freight forwarder, or vessel operator engaged under this contract.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when internal approval was granted for this freight contract.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the sales or procurement executive who approved this freight contract.']
  arbitration_clause varchar [note: 'Terms defining dispute resolution mechanism, including arbitration venue and rules (e.g., London Maritime Arbitrators Association, Singapore Chamber of Maritime Arbitration).']
  benchmark_index varchar [note: 'Reference freight index used for pricing or settlement (e.g., Baltic Capesize Index, Baltic Panamax Index, Platts freight assessments).']
  bunker_adjustment_clause varchar [note: 'Terms defining how changes in bunker fuel prices affect the freight rate, including base fuel price and adjustment mechanism.']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Type of mineral commodity covered by this freight contract, aligned with the mining company\'s product portfolio.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the freight contract becomes binding and operative.']
  contract_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the freight contract terminates. Nullable for spot charters or open-ended arrangements.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the freight contract, used for reference in commercial documentation and communications.']
  contract_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the contract in months. Applicable primarily to time charters and contracts of affreightment.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the freight contract. Draft indicates under negotiation, active indicates in force, suspended indicates temporarily paused, completed indicates all obligations fulfilled, terminated indicates early cancellation, expired indicates natural end of term.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the freight contract structure. Voyage charter is for a single voyage, time charter is for a period, contract of affreightment (COA) is for multiple shipments over time, freight forward agreement (FFA) is a derivative instrument, and spot charter is an ad-hoc arrangement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this freight contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this contract.']
  demurrage_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Daily penalty rate charged when loading or discharge operations exceed the agreed laytime. Typically expressed in USD per day.']
  despatch_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Daily bonus rate paid when loading or discharge operations are completed ahead of the agreed laytime. Typically expressed in USD per day, often 50% of demurrage rate.']
  discharge_port varchar [note: 'Destination port or port range where the commodity cargo will be discharged from the vessel.']
  force_majeure_clause varchar [note: 'Contractual terms defining events beyond parties\' control that excuse performance (e.g., natural disasters, war, strikes, port closures).']
  freight_basis varchar [note: 'INCOTERM defining the freight responsibility. FOB (Free on Board) - buyer arranges freight, CFR (Cost and Freight) - seller pays freight, CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight) - seller pays freight and insurance, DAP (Delivered at Place), EXW (Ex Works).']
  freight_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Contracted freight rate per unit. The unit is defined in freight_rate_unit (e.g., USD per tonne, USD per day for time charter).']
  freight_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the freight rate. Per tonne for voyage charters, per day for time charters, per voyage for lump sum arrangements.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this contract (e.g., English Law, New York Law, Australian Law).']
  insurance_responsibility varchar [note: 'Party responsible for arranging and paying for cargo insurance during transit.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this freight contract record was last updated in the system.']
  laytime_allowed_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total time allowed for loading and discharge operations before demurrage applies, expressed in hours.']
  loading_port varchar [note: 'Port or port range where the commodity cargo will be loaded onto the vessel.']
  maximum_cargo_quantity_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cargo quantity in metric tonnes that can be shipped per voyage or contract period.']
  minimum_cargo_quantity_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum cargo quantity in metric tonnes that must be shipped per voyage or contract period.']
  net_back_impact_usd_per_tonne decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated impact of this freight contract on net-back realisation per tonne of commodity sold. Critical for sales margin analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special terms, or operational notes related to this freight contract.']
  payment_due_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after invoice date or completion of voyage when payment is due.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Description of payment terms and conditions, including timing, method, and any advance payment requirements.']
  port_range varchar [note: 'Geographic range of ports covered by this contract (e.g., Australian East Coast, Chinese North Ports, Rotterdam-Hamburg range).']
  price_adjustment_formula varchar [note: 'Formula or mechanism for adjusting freight rates based on fuel prices, market indices, or other variables (e.g., bunker adjustment factor).']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when the freight contract was formally executed by all parties.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number tracking amendments and revisions to the freight contract over its lifecycle.']
  vessel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of vessel size and capability. Capesize (150,000+ DWT), Panamax (60,000-80,000 DWT), Handymax (40,000-60,000 DWT), Handysize (10,000-40,000 DWT), VLOC (Very Large Ore Carrier 200,000+ DWT), Supramax (50,000-60,000 DWT).']

  Note: 'Master record of freight and shipping contracts engaged to transport commodity cargoes, including voyage charters, time charters, and freight forward agreements. Captures vessel type, freight rate, freight basis (FOB/CFR/CIF), contract period, port range, demurrage rate, and despatch terms. Owned by sales as freight cost directly impacts net-back realisation.'
}

Table sales.demurrage_claim {
  demurrage_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demurrage or despatch claim record. Primary key for the demurrage claim entity.']
  cargo_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.cargo_shipment. Business justification: demurrage_claim has bill_of_lading_number (STRING) which identifies the shipment for which demurrage is claimed. Adding cargo_shipment_id FK establishes proper relationship. The bill_of_lading_number ']
  claims_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Demurrage claims require a designated claims manager responsible for dispute resolution, negotiation, and recovery. Essential for accountability, performance tracking, legal compliance, and optimizing']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Demurrage claims affect net revenue and are charged to cost centres for AISC impact assessment and operational performance tracking. Required for accurate mine-level cost and revenue reporting.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the counterparty (buyer, shipping line, or freight forwarder) involved in the demurrage or despatch claim.']
  freight_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.freight_contract. Business justification: demurrage_claim has freight_contract_reference (STRING) which identifies the freight contract under which the claim arises. Adding freight_contract_id FK establishes proper relationship between claim ']
  sales_offtake_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent offtake agreement under which this shipment and claim occurred, if applicable.']
  settlement_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.invoice. Business justification: Demurrage claims result in settlement invoices (either demurrage charges to customer or despatch credits from carrier). Adding settlement_invoice_id (FK to invoice.sales_invoice_id) links the claim to']
  berth_name varchar [note: 'Specific berth or wharf at the port where the vessel was berthed during loading or discharge operations.']
  cargo_quantity_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of commodity loaded or discharged, measured in metric tonnes.']
  claim_agreed_date date [note: 'Date on which the counterparty agreed to the claim amount and terms.']
  claim_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the demurrage or despatch claim in USD, calculated from laytime variance and applicable rate.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the demurrage or despatch claim, used in correspondence with counterparties and freight forwarders.']
  claim_raised_date date [note: 'Date on which the demurrage or despatch claim was formally raised with the counterparty.']
  claim_settled_date date [note: 'Date on which the demurrage or despatch claim was financially settled (payment received or credit applied).']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demurrage or despatch claim in the settlement workflow.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Type of claim: demurrage (penalty for exceeding laytime) or despatch (credit for completing within laytime).']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Type of mineral commodity shipped (e.g., iron ore, copper concentrate, coal, lithium, nickel).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this demurrage claim record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the claim amount (typically USD for international commodity trade).']
  demurrage_rate_usd_per_day decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual demurrage rate in USD per day charged for exceeding the allowed laytime.']
  despatch_rate_usd_per_day decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual despatch rate in USD per day credited for completing operations within the allowed laytime.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim is currently under dispute with the counterparty (True) or not (False).']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for dispute if the claim is contested by the counterparty (e.g., laytime calculation disagreement, force majeure event, weather delays).']
  dispute_resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes on dispute resolution activities, arbitration proceedings, or settlement negotiations.']
  imo_number varchar [note: 'Unique seven-digit IMO ship identification number assigned to the vessel for permanent identification.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Incoterm defining the delivery terms and responsibility for freight costs (e.g., FOB, CIF, CFR).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this demurrage claim record was last updated.']
  laytime_allowed_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total laytime (in hours) contractually allowed for loading or discharge operations as per the charter party or freight contract.']
  laytime_used_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual laytime (in hours) consumed during loading or discharge operations, calculated from Notice of Readiness (NOR) to completion.']
  laytime_variance_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference between laytime used and laytime allowed (positive indicates demurrage, negative indicates despatch).']
  loading_commenced_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when actual loading or discharge operations commenced.']
  loading_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when loading or discharge operations were completed and the vessel was ready to depart.']
  net_back_impact_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial impact of the demurrage or despatch claim on the sales net-back realization (negative for demurrage, positive for despatch).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to the demurrage or despatch claim, including operational context or special circumstances.']
  notice_of_readiness_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vessel tendered Notice of Readiness (NOR) to commence loading or discharge operations.']
  port_code varchar [note: 'Five-character UN/LOCODE code identifying the port of loading or discharge.']
  port_of_loading varchar [note: 'Name of the port where the commodity was loaded onto the vessel.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Party responsible for the demurrage or despatch claim (seller, buyer, shipping line, or freight forwarder) as per contract terms.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vessel involved in the loading or discharge operation that triggered the demurrage or despatch claim.']

  Note: 'Record of demurrage and despatch claims arising from vessel loading or discharge operations exceeding or completing within the agreed laytime at port. Captures vessel name, port, berth, laytime allowed (hours), laytime used, demurrage/despatch rate (USD/day), claim amount, claim status (open/agreed/disputed/settled), counterparty, and dispute resolution notes. Directly impacts sales net-back realisation and is reconciled against freight contracts.'
}

Table sales.price_index {
  price_index_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for price_index']
  adjustment_factor_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether quality or specification adjustments are typically applied when using this index in pricing formulas (e.g., Fe content adjustments, moisture adjustments, impurity penalties).']
  alternative_index_code varchar [note: 'Reference to an alternative or successor index code that should be used if this index is deprecated or unavailable. Supports continuity planning for pricing formulas.']
  commodity_grade varchar [note: 'The specific grade or quality specification tracked by this index (e.g., 62% Fe, Premium Hard Coking Coal, Grade A Copper Cathode, Battery Grade Lithium Carbonate).']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'The mineral commodity that this index tracks (e.g., Iron Ore, Copper, Coal, Lithium, Nickel). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Iron Ore|Copper|Coal|Lithium|Nickel|Gold|Silver|Zinc|Lead|Aluminium|Other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this price index record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the index is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, AUD, CNY).']
  data_source_url varchar [note: 'Web URL or reference link to the publishing agency\'s official page for this index, used for data validation and audit purposes.']
  delivery_basis varchar [note: 'The Incoterm delivery basis for the index pricing (e.g., CFR - Cost Insurance and Freight, FOB - Free on Board). Defines where price risk and title transfer occur. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CFR|FOB|CIF|EXW|FCA|DAP|DDP|Other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'The geographic location or port associated with the delivery basis (e.g., CFR China, FOB Australia, CIF Rotterdam). Used to specify the pricing point.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'The date on which this index was discontinued or deprecated by the publishing agency. Null if the index is still active.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this index definition became effective and available for use in pricing formulas and offtake agreements.']
  index_category varchar [note: 'The type or category of price index (e.g., Spot market index, Forward curve, Futures settlement, Benchmark reference).']
  index_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the price index (e.g., PLATTS_62FE, TSI_COKING, LME_COPPER_CASH). Used in pricing formulas and offtake agreements.']
  index_family varchar [note: 'Grouping or family name for related indices from the same publisher (e.g., Platts IODEX Family, TSI Coking Coal Suite, LME Base Metals). Used for hierarchical organization and reporting.']
  index_methodology_description varchar [note: 'Brief description of how the index is calculated or derived by the publishing agency (e.g., volume-weighted average of spot transactions, survey-based assessment, exchange settlement price).']
  index_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the price index (e.g., Platts IODEX 62% Fe CFR China, TSI Premium Hard Coking Coal FOB Australia).']
  index_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price index. Active indices are currently used in pricing formulas; Inactive or Deprecated indices are retained for historical reference only.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this price index record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this price index record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or business notes related to this price index (e.g., usage guidelines, known limitations, historical context).']
  publication_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the index is published or updated by the publishing agency (e.g., Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Real-time).']
  publishing_agency varchar [note: 'The organization that publishes and maintains this price index (e.g., Platts, TSI - The Steel Index, LME - London Metal Exchange, Fastmarkets). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Platts|TSI|LME|Fastmarkets|Metal Bulletin|Argus|Bloomberg|CME|ICE|Other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quotational_period_convention varchar [note: 'The standard time window convention used for price averaging in contracts referencing this index (e.g., M+1 month average, M+2 month average, spot month, quarterly average). QP = Quotational Period defines the averaging window for provisional and final pricing.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this index is recognized or approved for use in regulatory reporting or compliance contexts (e.g., SEC S-K 1300 disclosure, IFRS fair value measurement).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the index price is quoted (e.g., USD per Dry Metric Tonne, USD per Wet Metric Tonne, USD per pound, USD per kilogram). DMT = Dry Metric Tonne, WMT = Wet Metric Tonne. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD/DMT|USD/WMT|USD/tonne|USD/lb|USD/kg|USD/oz|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of active offtake agreements or pricing formulas currently referencing this index. Used for impact analysis when indices are deprecated or changed.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this price index record in the system.']

  Note: 'Reference master of commodity price indices used in offtake agreement pricing formulas, including index code, index name, publishing agency (Platts, TSI, LME, Fastmarkets), commodity, unit of measure, quotational period convention, and active status. Distinct from benchmark_price (which holds the actual daily price values) — this is the index definition catalog.'
}

Table sales.agreement_stakeholder_consultation {
  agreement_stakeholder_consultation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the agreement_stakeholder_consultation association']
  sales_offtake_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the offtake agreement subject to stakeholder consultation']
  stakeholder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the community stakeholder being consulted']
  agreement_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which this stakeholder formally accepted or provided consent for this offtake agreement. Null if consent not yet obtained or not required.']
  benefit_entitlement_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of revenue or benefits from this specific offtake agreement that this stakeholder is entitled to receive under benefit-sharing arrangements. Null if stakeholder has no financial entitlement.']
  conditions_imposed varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any conditions or requirements this stakeholder imposed as part of their consent or consultation response for this agreement. May include environmental conditions, benefit requirements, or operational constraints.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of consent or approval from this stakeholder for this specific offtake agreement. Tracks FPIC status, regulatory approvals, or community consent processes.']
  consultation_date date [note: 'Date on which formal consultation with this stakeholder regarding this offtake agreement was conducted. Records when engagement occurred.']
  consultation_method varchar [note: 'The method or process used to consult this stakeholder on this agreement. Captures whether traditional protocols, FPIC processes, or regulatory hearings were used.']
  consultation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the consultation process with this stakeholder for this agreement. Tracks progress through the engagement lifecycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consultation record was first created in the system.']
  stakeholder_role varchar [note: 'The specific role this stakeholder plays in relation to this offtake agreement. Consulted party indicates formal consultation requirement, consent authority indicates FPIC or regulatory approval authority, beneficiary indicates entitled to benefits from the agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consultation record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the consultation and consent relationship between offtake agreements and community stakeholders. It captures the formal engagement process where stakeholders (indigenous groups, local communities, government bodies) are consulted on, provide consent for, or hold benefit entitlements in specific offtake agreements. Each record links one offtake agreement to one stakeholder with attributes that exist only in the context of this consultation relationship.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, a single offtake agreement can require consultation, consent, or benefit-sharing arrangements with multiple community stakeholders (indigenous groups with native title, local communities affected by transport routes, government regulatory bodies, traditional owners). Conversely, a single stakeholder may be consulted on or hold entitlements in multiple offtake agreements across different commodities, mine sites, or time periods. The consultation relationship itself carries critical operational data including consent status, benefit entitlements, consultation dates, and conditions imposed.'
}

Table sales.vessel {
  vessel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for vessel']
  sister_vessel_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vessel (sister_vessel_id)']
  beam_width_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum width of the vessel in meters, critical for determining port and canal passage feasibility.']
  build_year varchar [note: 'Year the vessel was constructed, used to assess vessel age, maintenance requirements, and insurance premiums.']
  call_sign varchar [note: 'Unique radio call sign assigned to the vessel for maritime communication and identification purposes.']
  cargo_hold_capacity_m3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total volumetric capacity of all cargo holds in cubic meters, used to determine maximum commodity volume that can be loaded.']
  charter_end_date date [note: 'Date when the current charter agreement for the vessel expires, critical for long-term shipment nomination planning.']
  charter_start_date date [note: 'Date when the current charter agreement for the vessel becomes effective, used for shipment planning and cost allocation.']
  charter_type varchar [note: 'Type of charter agreement under which the vessel operates, affecting cost structure and operational control for commodity shipments.']
  classification_society varchar [note: 'Name of the independent organization that certifies the vessel meets safety and construction standards (e.g., Lloyd\'s Register, DNV GL, ABS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vessel record was first created in the system, used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  daily_charter_rate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Daily cost in USD for chartering the vessel under time charter agreements, used for freight cost forecasting and budget planning.']
  deadweight_tonnage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight in metric tonnes that the vessel can safely carry including cargo, fuel, crew, provisions, and ballast. Critical for matching vessel capacity to shipment volume requirements.']
  draft_depth_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Vertical distance between the waterline and the bottom of the vessel hull in meters when fully loaded, used to assess port depth requirements and loading limits.']
  flag_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the nation under whose laws the vessel is registered, affecting regulatory compliance and taxation.']
  fuel_consumption_ballast_mt_per_day decimal(8,2) [note: 'Daily fuel consumption in metric tonnes when the vessel is traveling without cargo, used for repositioning cost calculations.']
  fuel_consumption_laden_mt_per_day decimal(8,2) [note: 'Daily fuel consumption in metric tonnes when the vessel is fully loaded, used for voyage cost estimation and carbon footprint calculation.']
  gross_tonnage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Measure of the overall internal volume of the vessel in register tonnes, used for regulatory compliance and port fee calculations.']
  ice_class_rating varchar [note: 'Classification indicating the vessels capability to operate in ice-covered waters, critical for winter shipments from northern ports.']
  imo_number varchar [note: 'Unique seven-digit ship identification number assigned by the International Maritime Organization for vessel tracking and regulatory compliance.']
  is_vetting_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vessel has passed the companys vetting process for safety, environmental compliance, and operational standards.']
  last_survey_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent classification society survey or inspection, used to assess vessel compliance and maintenance status.']
  length_overall_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum length of the vessel from bow to stern in meters, used to determine port berthing compatibility and canal transit eligibility.']
  mmsi_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit unique identifier used by maritime digital selective calling, automatic identification systems, and other radio applications for vessel tracking.']
  net_tonnage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Measure of the usable cargo volume of the vessel in register tonnes, used for calculating port charges and canal transit fees.']
  next_survey_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory classification society survey, critical for planning vessel availability and maintenance downtime.']
  number_of_holds varchar [note: 'Total count of separate cargo holds on the vessel, relevant for segregating different commodity grades or types during shipment.']
  operator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company or entity responsible for the day-to-day operation and management of the vessel.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity that owns the vessel, used for contract negotiations and liability determination.']
  port_of_registry varchar [note: 'Name of the port where the vessel is officially registered, determining the legal jurisdiction and regulatory framework.']
  shipyard_name varchar [note: 'Name of the shipyard where the vessel was constructed, relevant for quality assessment and maintenance history.']
  speed_ballast_knots decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average cruising speed of the vessel in knots when traveling without cargo (in ballast condition), used for repositioning time calculations.']
  speed_laden_knots decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average cruising speed of the vessel in knots when fully loaded with cargo, used for voyage duration estimation and shipment scheduling.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vessel record was last modified, used for tracking data currency and change management.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Official registered name of the vessel used for commodity shipment and transportation.']
  vessel_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the vessel indicating availability for commodity shipment nominations and offtake agreements.']
  vessel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of vessel based on cargo capacity and design, critical for matching vessel to commodity shipment requirements.']
  vetting_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current vetting approval expires and the vessel must be re-evaluated for continued use in commodity shipments.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for vessel. Referenced by vessel_id.'
}

Table product.commodity {
  commodity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the commodity. Primary key for the commodity master catalog.']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Mining companies maintain dedicated GL accounts for each commodity type (iron ore revenue, copper revenue, coal revenue) for financial reporting, commodity-level P&L, and revenue recognition. Essentia']
  benchmark_pricing_index varchar [note: 'The market pricing index or benchmark used for commercial pricing of this commodity (e.g., Platts Iron Ore Index, LME Copper, Newcastle Coal Index, Fastmarkets Lithium Hydroxide).']
  cim_definition_standard varchar [note: 'CIM definition standard reference for the commodity used in Canadian mineral resource and reserve reporting.']
  commodity_class varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the commodity type. Used for strategic segmentation and market analysis.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Standardized business identifier code for the commodity used across systems and external reporting. Aligns with SAP material master commodity grouping and JORC commodity classification.']
  commodity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the commodity including its characteristics, typical applications, and distinguishing features.']
  commodity_group varchar [note: 'Higher-level grouping or family to which this commodity belongs for reporting and analysis purposes (e.g., Iron Ore Products, Copper Products, Coal Products).']
  commodity_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the commodity (e.g., Iron Ore, Copper Concentrate, Thermal Coal, Lithium Hydroxide, Nickel Matte).']
  commodity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commodity in the product catalog. Active commodities are currently produced and sold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this commodity record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this commodity record became active and valid in the product catalog.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this commodity record is valid. Null indicates the record is currently active with no planned end date.']
  environmental_classification varchar [note: 'Environmental impact classification or category for the commodity based on extraction, processing, and disposal considerations.']
  export_license_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an export license or permit is required for international shipment of this commodity due to regulatory or strategic controls.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'International Harmonized System tariff classification code used for customs, trade documentation, and export/import compliance.']
  is_hazardous_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, and transportation protocols.']
  jorc_commodity_type varchar [note: 'JORC-compliant commodity type classification used for resource and reserve reporting to ensure alignment with Australian mineral reporting standards.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this commodity record was last updated or modified.']
  primary_end_use_market varchar [note: 'The principal industry or market segment that consumes this commodity (e.g., steel manufacturing, energy generation, battery production, construction materials).']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance classification code for the commodity as defined by governing bodies (e.g., JORC resource category, SEC S-K 1300 classification, CIM definition standards).']
  royalty_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether government or third-party royalties apply to the production and sale of this commodity.']
  samrec_commodity_code varchar [note: 'SAMREC-compliant commodity code for operations reporting under South African mineral resource standards.']
  standard_packaging_type varchar [note: 'The standard packaging or shipment format for this commodity (e.g., bulk vessel, containerized, bagged, drummed).']
  standard_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The standard unit of measure used for quantifying and trading this commodity across all transactions and reporting.']
  strategic_importance_level varchar [note: 'Internal strategic importance rating of the commodity to the business based on revenue contribution, market position, and long-term value.']
  trading_currency varchar [note: 'The standard currency in which this commodity is priced and traded internationally. Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code.']
  typical_grade_range varchar [note: 'The typical range of mineral grade or purity for this commodity as produced (e.g., 62-65% Fe for iron ore, 25-30% Cu for copper concentrate). Expressed as a text range for flexibility.']
  typical_recovery_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The typical percentage of valuable mineral extracted during processing for this commodity. Used for production planning and resource estimation.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of mineral commodity types produced by the mining operation (iron ore, copper, coal, lithium, nickel). Each record defines a top-level commodity identity with commodity code, commodity class (base metal, precious metal, energy mineral, industrial mineral, battery mineral), primary end-use market, standard unit of measure, and regulatory classification. Acts as the root of the product hierarchy with saleable_product records beneath it. Aligns with JORC commodity classification and SAP material master commodity grouping.'
}

Table product.saleable_product {
  saleable_product_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each distinct saleable mineral product variant. Primary key. This is the system of record identifier that sales contracts, shipment nominations, quality certificates, and pricing schedules reference.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.capital_project. Business justification: Mining projects are designed to produce specific saleable products. Feasibility studies, production planning, and capital allocation decisions require tracking which project delivers each product to m']
  certifying_laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Export products require quality certificates from specific accredited laboratories for market access and customer acceptance. Tracks the approved certifying laboratory relationship for regulatory comp']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Establishes the fundamental product hierarchy: each saleable product is a variant of a commodity type (e.g., iron ore fines, lump, pellets are all variants of the iron ore commodity). The existing com']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Mining operations track which cost centre (processing plant, mine site) produces each saleable product for AISC/C1 cost reporting, product profitability analysis, and cost allocation. Essential for ma']
  customer_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.counterparty. Business justification: Mining companies create customer-specific product grades (e.g., iron ore fines to exact Fe% for a steel mill, coal blends for specific power plants). Enables customer-specific product catalog manageme']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Revenue recognition and inventory valuation require linking products to specific GL accounts (finished goods inventory accounts). Essential for automated inventory postings, month-end close, and IAS 2']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Saleable products are sourced from specific orebodies, establishing product traceability and provenance. Critical for quality variance analysis, customer provenance requirements, mine-to-market chain ']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Required for IFRS 8 segment reporting, commodity-level P&L, and product line profitability tracking. Mining companies report financial performance by commodity/product segment; this link enables produ']
  source_tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Saleable products must be traceable to source tenement for royalty obligations, regulatory compliance, and responsible sourcing certification. Required for statutory production reporting and ESG prove']
  available_for_sale_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is currently available for inclusion in new sales contracts and shipment nominations. False when product is temporarily unavailable due to plant maintenance, feedstock constraints, or quality holds.']
  benchmark_index varchar [note: 'Name of the published commodity price index or assessment used as the reference for contract pricing. Examples: \'Platts IODEX 62% Fe CFR China\', \'LME Nickel 3-month\', \'IHS McCloskey Newcastle Coal\', \'Fastmarkets MB Lithium Hydroxide CIF Asia\'. Empty for products with purely negotiated pricing.']
  carbon_footprint_kg_co2e_per_tonne decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated greenhouse gas emissions intensity for producing one tonne of this saleable product, expressed as kilograms of CO2 equivalent. Includes Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions from mining, processing, and on-site logistics. Used for customer carbon accounting and regulatory reporting.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the mineral was mined and processed. Required for customs declarations, trade compliance, and country-of-origin certifications. Examples: AUS (Australia), CAN (Canada), CHL (Chile), ZAF (South Africa).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this saleable product record was first created in the system. Represents when the product was formally added to the commercial product catalog.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this product definition and specifications are valid and can be referenced in new sales contracts. Supports versioning when product specifications are updated.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this product definition is valid. Null for currently active products. Populated when product is superseded by a new specification version or discontinued. Existing contracts referencing this product remain valid through their term.']
  environmental_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental or sustainability certification(s) held for this product, if any. Examples: \'ResponsibleSteel\', \'Copper Mark\', \'Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance (IRMA)\', \'ISO 14001 Certified Site\'. Increasingly required by customers for ESG compliance and supply chain transparency.']
  export_license_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an export license or permit is required from the country of origin government to ship this product internationally. True for strategic minerals, rare earths, or products subject to export controls or quotas.']
  grade_specification varchar [note: 'Target grade or concentration of the valuable mineral component expressed as percentage or parts per million. Examples: \'62.0% Fe\', \'25% Cu\', \'70% Ni\', \'99.5% LiOH·H2O\'. This is the headline specification that drives product pricing and customer acceptance.']
  hazmat_classification varchar [note: 'International hazardous materials classification code if the product is regulated for transport. Examples: UN 3077 (Environmentally Hazardous Substance, Solid), UN 3264 (Corrosive Liquid, Acidic, Inorganic). Empty if product is non-hazardous. Governs shipping documentation, vessel requirements, and emergency response procedures.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'International Harmonized System tariff classification code used for customs declarations and trade statistics. 6-digit HS code is internationally standardized; additional digits may be country-specific. Examples: 260111 (Iron ore and concentrates, non-agglomerated), 260300 (Copper ores and concentrates), 283691 (Lithium carbonates).']
  impurity_limits varchar [note: 'Specification of maximum allowable concentrations for deleterious impurity elements that negatively impact customer processing or final product quality. Expressed as comma-separated element:limit pairs. Examples: \'P:0.08%, S:0.05%, As:0.01%\' for iron ore; \'Hg:1ppm, Cd:5ppm\' for copper concentrate. Penalties may apply if limits are exceeded.']
  incoterm_default varchar [note: 'Default Incoterm used for pricing and risk transfer in sales contracts for this product. FOB (Free on Board) and CIF (Cost, Insurance and Freight) are most common for bulk mineral commodities. Defines seller and buyer responsibilities for logistics, insurance, and customs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
