  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods receipt has been reversed or cancelled. When true, the inventory movement has been negated and the original transaction is no longer valid.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'The coded reason for reversing the goods receipt (e.g., damaged goods, quantity discrepancy, wrong material delivered). Used for root cause analysis and vendor performance evaluation.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized equipment or high-value assets. Enables individual asset tracking and lifecycle management.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'The inventory status category of the received goods. Unrestricted stock is available for use; quality inspection stock awaits testing; blocked stock cannot be used; restricted stock has limited usage.']
  tare_weight decimal(18,3) [note: 'The weight of packaging, containers, or transport equipment. Calculated as gross weight minus net weight.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the received quantity is measured (e.g., TON for tonnes, KG for kilograms, EA for each, M3 for cubic meters). Must align with material master and purchase order UOM.']
  unloading_point varchar [note: 'The specific physical location or dock where the delivery vehicle was unloaded (e.g., Dock 3, ROM Pad A, Reagent Bay 2). Used for logistics planning and congestion management.']
  valuation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of the goods receipt in the local currency. Used for inventory valuation and financial accounting. Calculated as quantity received multiplied by material price.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'The valuation category for split-valuation scenarios where the same material is valued differently based on procurement source or quality grade (e.g., domestic vs. imported ore).']
  vendor_batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number assigned by the vendor to the delivered materials. Used for traceability back to the suppliers production records and quality certificates.']
  weight_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which weights are measured (e.g., TON for tonnes, KG for kilograms, LB for pounds). Must be consistent across gross, net, and tare weights.']

  Note: 'Records the physical receipt of materials at a mine site warehouse or ROM pad against a purchase order or inbound delivery. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt (MIGO/MSEG). Captures GR document number, posting date, delivery note reference, plant, storage location, material, quantity received, unit of measure, batch number, movement type, and quality inspection flag. Triggers inventory update and three-way match for invoice verification.'
}

Table procurement.goods_issue {
  goods_issue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the goods issue transaction. Primary key for the goods issue record.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Material consumption posts to cost centres for production cost tracking, AISC calculation, and budget variance analysis. Fundamental to mining operations cost accounting.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Stock transport orders are a type of purchase order in SAP used for inter-plant transfers. Goods issue can be executed against STO. N:1 relationship (many goods issues against one STO). Converting STR']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_master. Business justification: Goods issue records movement of a specific material. N:1 relationship (many goods issues for one material). Converting STRING material_number to FK enables proper material master linkage. Material det']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.warehouse_location. Business justification: Goods issue originates from a warehouse storage location. N:1 relationship (many goods issues from one location). Converting STRING storage_location_code to FK enables proper inventory location tracki']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Capital project material issues post to WBS for project cost tracking and asset capitalization. Required for accurate capital project accounting.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number for batch-managed materials. Enables traceability of specific production runs or procurement lots.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who created the material document. Provides audit trail for transaction origination.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'Outbound delivery document number for stock transport orders. Links the goods issue to the logistics execution document.']
  document_date date [note: 'Date on the physical document or the date the transaction was initiated. May differ from posting date.']
  entry_date date [note: 'Date when the material document was created in the system. Represents when the record was first captured.']
  entry_time timestamp [note: 'Time when the material document was created in the system (HH:MM:SS format).']
  goods_recipient varchar [note: 'Name of the person or organizational unit receiving the material. Used for accountability and tracking.']
  header_text varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered at the material document header level. Provides additional context for the transaction.']
  material_document_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the material document. Represents the sequence of individual material movements within a single document.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP material document number (MBLNR) that uniquely identifies the goods movement transaction in the source system. This is the externally-known business identifier for the goods issue.']
  material_document_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the material document was posted. Part of the compound key in SAP along with material document number.']
  movement_indicator varchar [note: 'Single-character indicator that further classifies the movement (e.g., B for goods movement for purchase order, F for production order, L for delivery).']
  movement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goods issue transaction. Indicates whether the movement has been successfully posted, reversed, or is pending.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code that classifies the nature of the goods movement. Common values include 201 (goods issue to cost center), 261 (goods issue to work order/production order), 301 (plant-to-plant transfer), 311 (storage location to storage location transfer), 551 (scrapping from storage location), 202/262 (reversal of 201/261).']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the mine site or plant from which the material was issued. Represents the source location for the goods movement.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the goods issue was posted to inventory and financial accounting. This is the principal business event timestamp for the transaction.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material issued in the base unit of measure. Positive values represent outbound movements; negative values represent reversals.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the business reason for the goods issue (e.g., planned maintenance, breakdown repair, operational consumption, scrapping due to damage).']
  receiving_plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the destination mine site or plant for inter-plant transfers (movement type 301). Null for consumption issues.']
  receiving_storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the destination storage location for inter-storage-location transfers (movement type 311). Null for consumption issues.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference document number (e.g., delivery note, packing slip, transfer note) associated with the goods issue.']
  reservation_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the reservation document.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Material reservation number that pre-allocated the stock for this issue. Links the goods issue to the planning document.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this goods issue has been reversed (true) or is an active transaction (false).']
  reversal_movement_type varchar [note: 'Movement type used for the reversal transaction (e.g., 202 reverses 201, 262 reverses 261). Populated only if the transaction has been reversed.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'Single-character code indicating special stock categories (e.g., E for sales order stock, Q for project stock, K for consignment stock).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the issued quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, L for liters, M for meters, TON for tonnes).']
  unloading_point varchar [note: 'Physical location or area within the receiving site where the material is delivered (e.g., workshop bay, crusher station, ROM pad).']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Split valuation type for materials managed with multiple stock types (e.g., own stock vs consignment stock).']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Maintenance or production work order number to which the material is issued for movement type 261. Links material consumption to specific maintenance activities or production runs.']

  Note: 'Unified material movement ledger recording all stock movements out of, between, and within warehouse locations across the mining operation. Covers consumption issues to cost centres and work orders (SAP movement type 201/261), inter-plant and inter-site stock transfers (movement type 301/311), stock transport orders between mine sites, scrapping, and returns. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (MIGO/MSEG). Captures document number, posting date, movement type, issuing plant, storage location, receiving plant/storage location (for transfers), receiving cost centre or work order (for consumption), material, quantity, unit of measure, transfer order number, transport reference, and movement status. Serves as the SSOT for all material movements within and between mine sites — no other product in this domain records material movements.'
}

Table procurement.inventory_balance {
  inventory_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory balance record.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the plant where the material is stored.']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material being tracked in inventory.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location within the plant where the material is physically stored.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number for batch-managed materials, enabling traceability of material origin and processing history.']
  blocked_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material blocked from use due to quality issues, damage, or other restrictions.']
  count_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Cycle count assignment code indicating the frequency category for this material and location.']
  count_frequency_category varchar [note: 'Classification of count frequency based on material criticality and ABC analysis, determining how often physical counts are performed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|annual|abc_a|abc_b|abc_c — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  counted_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual quantity counted during the physical inventory count.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory balance record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the stock value. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|AUD|CAD|CNY|JPY|ZAR|BRL|CLP — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  in_transit_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material currently in transit between storage locations or plants.']
  last_count_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent physical inventory count was performed for this material and location.']
  last_goods_issue_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent goods issue transaction that decreased stock at this location.']
  last_goods_receipt_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent goods receipt transaction that increased stock at this location.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory balance record was last modified.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the material, typically the SAP material number or internal SKU.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short text description of the material for business user identification.']
  maximum_stock_level_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable stock level for the material at this location to prevent overstocking.']
  physical_inventory_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the most recent physical inventory count for this material and location.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of the physical inventory count posting to the general ledger and material ledger.']
  quality_inspection_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material currently under quality inspection and not yet released for use.']
  recount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recount was required due to significant variance or count discrepancy.']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum stock level that triggers a replenishment order to prevent stockouts.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Buffer stock maintained to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory balance snapshot was captured, enabling historical trend analysis.']
  stock_accuracy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated stock accuracy percentage for this material and location, used for inventory KPI reporting.']
  stock_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the stock based on availability and business rules.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the storage location within the plant.']
  system_quantity_at_count decimal(18,3) [note: 'System-recorded quantity at the time of the physical inventory count, used for variance calculation.']
  total_stock_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of all stock at this location, calculated using standard cost or moving average price.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the stock quantities, such as kilograms, metric tons, liters, or pieces. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|MT|TON|L|M3|EA|PC|M|FT|GAL|LB — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unrestricted_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material available for unrestricted use and consumption in operations.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Method used to value the inventory, such as standard cost, moving average price, FIFO, or LIFO.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Difference between system quantity and counted quantity, calculated as counted minus system quantity.']
  variance_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the variance between system and counted quantities.']

  Note: 'Current and historical stock balance for each material at each storage location and plant, incorporating physical count records, cycle count results, and reconciliation outcomes. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (MARD/MCHB/MI01/MI07). Captures material, plant, storage location, batch, unrestricted stock, quality inspection stock, blocked stock, in-transit stock, reorder point, safety stock, maximum stock level. Also records all physical inventory counts including count document number, count date, system quantity, counted quantity, variance quantity, variance value, recount flag, posting status, count cycle assignment, and count frequency category. Serves as the SSOT for real-time inventory visibility, physical count audit trail, stock accuracy KPI reporting, and cycle count compliance — no other product in this domain records stock balances or physical counts.'
}

Table procurement.stock_transfer {
  stock_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stock transfer record. Primary key.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the cost centre that will bear the cost of the stock transfer, if applicable. Used for internal cost allocation and management accounting.']
  primary_stock_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the specific storage location within the sending plant from which the material is being issued (e.g., central warehouse, maintenance stores, ROM pad).']
  receiving_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the plant or site to which the material is being delivered. Links to the plant/site master data.']
  receiving_storage_location_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the specific storage location within the receiving plant where the material will be received and stored (e.g., remote site stores, maintenance workshop, processing plant warehouse).']
  sending_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the plant or site from which the material is being dispatched. Links to the plant/site master data.']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the material master record being transferred. Links to the material product in the procurement or inventory domain.']
  actual_receipt_date date [note: 'The actual date when the material was physically received and goods receipt was posted at the receiving storage location. Null if not yet received.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number of the material being transferred, if batch management is active for the material. Critical for traceability of consumables, chemicals, explosives, and quality-controlled items.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'The name of the logistics carrier, freight company, or internal fleet responsible for transporting the material between locations (e.g., \'Internal Fleet\', \'ABC Logistics\', \'Mine Haul Trucks\').']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The SAP user ID or employee identifier of the person who created the stock transfer order in the system. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the stock transfer order record was first created in SAP S/4HANA MM. Audit trail timestamp for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the valuation amount (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD, ZAR). Reflects the company code currency or plant-specific valuation currency.']
  goods_issue_posted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the goods issue (dispatch) has been posted in SAP MM, reducing inventory at the sending location. True if posted, False if not yet posted.']
  goods_receipt_posted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the goods receipt has been posted in SAP MM, increasing inventory at the receiving location. True if posted, False if not yet received.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The SAP user ID or employee identifier of the person who last modified the stock transfer order. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the stock transfer order record was last updated in SAP S/4HANA MM. Audit trail timestamp for record modification.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short text description of the material being transferred, providing human-readable identification of the item (e.g., \'Hydraulic Pump Assembly\', \'Grinding Media Balls 50mm\', \'PPE Safety Helmet\').']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'The SAP MM movement type code (3-digit numeric) that classifies the inventory transaction (e.g., 301 for transfer posting plant to plant, 311 for transfer posting storage location to storage location).']
  priority varchar [note: 'The urgency level assigned to the stock transfer order, influencing logistics scheduling and dispatch sequencing. Critical transfers support emergency breakdowns or safety-critical operations.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Optional code indicating the business reason for the stock transfer (e.g., \'MAINT\' for maintenance requirement, \'PROJ\' for project demand, \'REBAL\' for inventory rebalancing, \'EMERG\' for emergency supply).']
  receiving_storage_location_code varchar [note: 'The SAP storage location code (4-character alphanumeric) within the receiving plant identifying the specific warehouse, bin, or stockpile area where material will be placed upon receipt.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the receiving plant or site requires the material to arrive. Used for planning and logistics scheduling to ensure operational continuity.']
  sending_storage_location_code varchar [note: 'The SAP storage location code (4-character alphanumeric) within the sending plant identifying the specific warehouse, bin, or stockpile area from which material is dispatched.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'The unique serial number of the material item being transferred, if serial number management is active. Used for high-value equipment components, critical spare parts, and serialized assets.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'SAP MM special stock indicator code if the material is held under special stock conditions (e.g., consignment stock, project stock, sales order stock). Blank for unrestricted stock.']
  transfer_date date [note: 'The planned or actual date when the stock transfer order was executed and goods were dispatched from the sending location. Business event date for the transfer transaction.']
  transfer_order_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique business identifier for the stock transfer order as recorded in SAP S/4HANA MM. Format: STO followed by 10 digits.']
  transfer_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material being transferred in the base unit of measure. Supports up to 3 decimal places for precision in measuring spare parts, consumables, or bulk materials.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock transfer order: draft (not yet released), released (approved for execution), in transit (goods dispatched but not received), partially received (some items received), completed (all items received), or cancelled.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stock transfer based on the nature of the movement: plant-to-plant, storage location to storage location within same plant, site-to-site across mine operations, warehouse to mine site, or central warehouse to remote location.']
  transport_reference_number varchar [note: 'The shipment or transport document number linking this stock transfer to the logistics carrier, freight forwarder, or internal fleet dispatch record. May reference bill of lading, consignment note, or internal haul ticket.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure for the transfer quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, MT for metric tonnes, L for litres, M for metres). ISO standard 2-3 character codes.']
  valuation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the stock being transferred, calculated based on the material valuation price and transfer quantity. Used for internal accounting and inventory valuation purposes.']
  vehicle_registration varchar [note: 'The registration or fleet number of the vehicle (truck, haul truck, light vehicle) used to transport the material. Enables tracking of transport asset utilization and route compliance.']

  Note: 'Records the movement of materials between plants, storage locations, or mine sites within the same organisation (e.g., transferring spare parts from central warehouse to a remote mine site). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM Stock Transfer Order (STO). Captures transfer order number, sending plant, receiving plant, sending storage location, receiving storage location, material, quantity, transfer date, transport reference, and status.'
}

Table procurement.warehouse_location {
  warehouse_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the warehouse location record. Primary key.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_centre']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or port facility where this warehouse location is physically situated.']
  access_restriction_level varchar [note: 'Security classification level governing personnel access to the warehouse location. Determines required clearances and access control procedures.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address or location descriptor for the warehouse facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as building number, zone, or area designation. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  capacity_pallet_positions varchar [note: 'Number of standard pallet positions available in the warehouse location. Used for space utilization planning.']
  capacity_volume_m3 decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum storage capacity of the location measured in cubic meters. Applicable for enclosed storage facilities.']
  capacity_weight_tonnes decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity of the storage location in metric tonnes. Critical for structural load limits and safety compliance.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the warehouse location is situated. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for warehouse location inquiries and communications. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the warehouse location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the warehouse location is situated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this warehouse location record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this warehouse location became operational and available for use.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this warehouse location ceased operations or was decommissioned. Null for currently active locations.']
  environmental_monitoring_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location has automated environmental monitoring systems for temperature, humidity, gas detection, or other parameters.']
  explosives_license_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the explosives storage license. Requires renewal before this date to maintain compliance.']
  explosives_license_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued license number authorizing storage of explosives at this magazine location. Applicable only for explosives storage facilities.']
  fire_suppression_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of fire suppression system installed at the warehouse location. Critical for emergency response planning and insurance compliance.']
  hazardous_goods_classification varchar [note: 'UN hazardous materials classification code(s) for materials authorized to be stored at this location. Multiple classes separated by semicolon. Empty if non-hazardous storage only.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety or compliance inspection conducted at this warehouse location.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this warehouse location record was last updated in the system.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the warehouse location in decimal degrees format. Used for logistics routing and emergency response.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the warehouse location used across procurement and inventory systems. Typically follows site-plant-area-bin hierarchy encoding.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the warehouse location or storage facility.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storage facility type based on its primary function and storage characteristics. Determines handling procedures and access controls. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bulk_storage|hazmat_bunker|cold_store|open_laydown|explosives_magazine|general_warehouse|parts_store|consumables_store|quarantine_area|scrap_yard — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the warehouse location in decimal degrees format. Used for logistics routing and emergency response.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory safety or compliance inspection of the warehouse location.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the warehouse location. Format: days and time ranges (e.g., Mon-Fri 0600-1800, Sat 0800-1200).']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the warehouse location indicating availability for storage and retrieval operations.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code representing the operational unit or processing facility associated with this warehouse location.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the warehouse location address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Mandatory personal protective equipment required for entry to this warehouse location. Pipe-separated list of PPE types (e.g., hard_hat|safety_boots|high_vis_vest|respirator).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the warehouse location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warehouse location has climate control systems for temperature-sensitive materials storage.']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum operating temperature maintained in the storage location in degrees Celsius. Applicable for temperature-controlled facilities.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum operating temperature maintained in the storage location in degrees Celsius. Applicable for temperature-controlled facilities.']
  warehouse_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person responsible for managing operations at this warehouse location. Business reference, not direct PII.']

  Note: 'Master register of all physical warehouse facilities, storage locations, laydown areas, and hazardous materials stores across mine sites and port facilities. Captures location code, name, plant, site, location type (bulk storage, hazmat bunker, cold store, open laydown, explosives magazine), capacity (volume/weight/pallet positions), hazardous goods classification, GPS coordinates, access restrictions, and operational status. Serves as the SSOT for all storage infrastructure managed by the supply function.'
}

Table procurement.inbound_delivery {
  inbound_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inbound delivery record. Primary key.']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.freight_order. Business justification: Inbound delivery is the receiving-side record of a freight order. One freight order results in one inbound delivery (1:1 or N:1). Freight order manages logistics execution; inbound delivery tracks sit']
  laboratory_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory_sample. Business justification: Bulk deliveries (chemicals, fuels, explosives) sampled at delivery for quality/safety verification. Links delivery to laboratory sample and certificate of analysis. Required for hazmat compliance, ven']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating purchase order that this delivery fulfills. Links to procurement transaction.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor who dispatched the materials. Links to the vendor master data.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Inbound deliveries of finished products from processing plants to port facilities require product specification matching for quality verification, inventory posting, and stockpile allocation. Critical']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logistics carrier responsible for transporting the materials. May be the same as vendor for direct shipments or a third-party logistics provider.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.warehouse_location. Business justification: Inbound deliveries are received at specific warehouse storage locations. The inbound_delivery table has destination_storage_location (STRING) but no FK to warehouse_location. Adding destination_storag']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'The actual date when the delivery arrived at the destination plant. Null if delivery has not yet arrived. Used for performance tracking and variance analysis.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'The ISO standard container identification number for containerized sea freight. Format: 4 letters (owner code) + 7 digits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inbound delivery record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  customs_clearance_date date [note: 'The date when customs clearance was completed for international shipments. Null for domestic deliveries or pending clearances.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'The status of customs clearance for international shipments. Not required for domestic deliveries.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'The official customs declaration reference number for international shipments. Used for compliance tracking and audit.']
  dangerous_goods_declaration_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number to the dangerous goods declaration document if the delivery contains hazardous materials. Required for compliance with transport safety regulations.']
  dangerous_goods_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery contains dangerous goods requiring special handling and documentation per international transport regulations.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known delivery note number issued by the vendor or carrier. This is the business identifier used for tracking and reference across systems.']
  delivery_notes varchar [note: 'General notes and comments related to this delivery. May include vendor remarks, carrier observations, or internal receiving notes.']
  delivery_priority varchar [note: 'The business priority assigned to this delivery. Critical deliveries may contain materials needed for urgent production or maintenance activities.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inbound delivery. Tracks progression from planning through final goods receipt. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|dispatched|in_transit|arrived|goods_received|cancelled|delayed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  destination_plant_code varchar [note: 'The mine site or processing plant code where the materials are to be received. Aligns with SAP plant master data.']
  dispatch_date date [note: 'The date when the vendor dispatched the materials from the origin location. Marks the start of the logistics journey.']
  expected_arrival_date date [note: 'The planned date when the delivery is expected to arrive at the destination plant. Used for planning and scheduling receipt operations.']
  freight_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total freight cost charged for transporting this delivery. Includes carrier charges and may include fuel surcharges and accessorial fees.']
  freight_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO currency code for the freight cost amount.']
  goods_receipt_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the goods receipt process has been completed in the system. True when all items have been received and posted to inventory.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'The International Commercial Terms code defining the responsibilities and risk transfer point between vendor and buyer. Common mining terms include FOB (Free on Board) and CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inbound delivery record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']
  origin_location varchar [note: 'The dispatch location from which the materials were shipped. May be vendor warehouse, port, or distribution center.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incoming materials require quality inspection before goods receipt posting. True for materials subject to quality control procedures.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'The security seal number applied to the container or truck. Used to verify that the shipment has not been tampered with during transit.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment based on delivery urgency and consolidation strategy.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling requirements for this delivery such as temperature control, fragile materials, or specific unloading procedures.']
  total_gross_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'The total gross weight of the delivery including packaging and pallets, measured in kilograms. Used for freight cost calculation and logistics planning.']
  total_net_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'The net weight of the materials excluding packaging, measured in kilograms. Represents the actual usable material weight.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total volumetric space occupied by the delivery, measured in cubic meters. Used for transport capacity planning and freight cost calculation.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'The primary mode of transportation used for this delivery. Multimodal indicates multiple transport legs using different modes.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'The four-digit UN number identifying the dangerous goods classification if applicable. Used for hazardous material identification and handling.']

  Note: 'Tracks the inbound logistics of materials from vendor dispatch to mine site receipt, including road, rail, and sea freight legs. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM Inbound Delivery (VL31N). Captures delivery note number, vendor, carrier, origin location, destination plant, expected arrival date, actual arrival date, transport mode, freight cost, customs clearance status, dangerous goods declaration reference, and delivery status.'
}

Table procurement.outbound_shipment {
  outbound_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the outbound shipment record. Primary key for the outbound shipment entity.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer purchasing and receiving the shipment, linking to the customer master data.']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.freight_order. Business justification: Outbound shipment is the shipping-side record of a freight order. One freight order results in one outbound shipment (1:1 or N:1). Freight order manages logistics execution; outbound shipment tracks c']
  origin_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.warehouse_location. Business justification: Outbound shipments originate from specific warehouse/port locations. The outbound_shipment table has origin_location_code (STRING) but no FK to warehouse_location. Adding origin_warehouse_location_id ']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Outbound shipments transport specific saleable products with defined grade specifications. Essential for cargo manifests, quality certificates, sales contract fulfillment, and revenue recognition. Dir']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Actual date the vessel arrived at the destination port.']
  actual_departure_date date [note: 'Actual date the vessel departed from the origin port or loading facility.']
  average_grade_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average grade or concentration of the valuable mineral in the shipped product, expressed as a percentage (e.g., iron content in iron ore concentrate).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shipment record was first created in the system.']
  demurrage_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total demurrage cost incurred due to delays beyond agreed laytime, calculated based on daily demurrage rate and number of demurrage days.']
  demurrage_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of days the vessel was delayed beyond the agreed laytime at the loading or discharge port, potentially incurring demurrage charges.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the destination country of the shipment.']
  destination_port_code varchar [note: 'Five-letter UN/LOCODE identifying the destination port where the shipment will be delivered.']
  destination_port_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the destination port where the customer will receive the shipment.']
  estimated_arrival_date date [note: 'Estimated date the vessel will arrive at the destination port, updated based on voyage progress and weather conditions.']
  freight_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code in which the freight rate is denominated.']
  freight_rate_per_tonne decimal(12,2) [note: 'Negotiated freight cost per metric tonne for transporting the shipment from origin to destination.']
  imo_number varchar [note: 'Seven-digit IMO ship identification number assigned to the vessel for permanent identification.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter INCOTERMS code defining the delivery terms and transfer of risk between seller and buyer (e.g., FOB for Free on Board, CIF for Cost Insurance and Freight). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shipment record was most recently modified.']
  laycan_end_date date [note: 'End date of the laycan window, representing the latest date the vessel may arrive for loading before the charter party may be cancelled.']
  laycan_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the laycan (lay days cancelling) window, representing the earliest date the vessel may arrive for loading.']
  loaded_quantity_tonnes decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of commodity product loaded onto the vessel, measured in metric tonnes as per draft survey or weighbridge.']
  loading_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when loading operations were completed and the vessel was ready to depart.']
  loading_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when loading operations commenced at the origin port or stockpile.']
  moisture_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content of the shipped commodity product, expressed as a percentage of total weight, used for quality and pricing adjustments.']
  nominated_quantity_tonnes decimal(15,3) [note: 'Planned or nominated quantity of commodity product to be shipped, measured in metric tonnes.']
  origin_location_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the origin port, mine site, or stockpile facility from which the shipment departs.']
  quality_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the quality certificate or certificate of analysis issued for the shipped commodity product, documenting grade and specifications.']
  quantity_variance_tonnes decimal(15,3) [note: 'Difference between nominated and loaded quantity in metric tonnes, calculated as loaded minus nominated.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or observations related to the shipment (e.g., weather delays, quality issues, customer requests).']
  sales_contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the sales contract or offtake agreement under which this shipment is being fulfilled.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the shipment, used for tracking and reference across systems and with customers.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the outbound shipment, tracking progress from planning through to delivery completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|loading|in_transit|arrived|discharged|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  shipping_agent_name varchar [note: 'Name of the shipping agent or freight forwarder managing the logistics and documentation for this shipment.']
  total_freight_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total freight cost for the shipment, calculated as loaded quantity multiplied by freight rate per tonne.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vessel or ship transporting the mineral concentrate or commodity product.']

  Note: 'Manages the outbound logistics of processed mineral concentrate or commodity product from mine site or port stockpile to customer delivery point. Captures shipment number, origin port or stockpile, destination port, vessel name, voyage number, bill of lading number, commodity product, nominated quantity, loaded quantity, laycan dates, actual departure date, ETA, Incoterms (FOB/CIF), freight rate, and shipment status. Coordinates with the sales domain for offtake fulfilment.'
}

Table procurement.port_stockpile {
  port_stockpile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the port stockpile. Primary key for this entity.']
  cargo_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment or sales order that this stockpile is currently allocated to for vessel loading. Links to shipment master data.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operator or supervisor currently responsible for managing this stockpile. Links to workforce master data.']
  laboratory_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory_sample. Business justification: Port stockpiles sampled for grade certification before shipment. Links stockpile inventory to representative sample and assay results. Critical for sales contract compliance, customer quality guarante']
  port_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port facility where this stockpile is located. Links to the port facility master data.']
  primary_port_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary stacker equipment assigned to this stockpile for stack-in operations. Links to equipment master data.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity product grade held in this stockpile (e.g., iron ore fines, lump ore, copper concentrate). Links to product master data.']
  stockpile_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.warehouse_location. Business justification: Port stockpiles are physical storage locations that should reference the warehouse_location master. The port_stockpile table has stockpile_code and location-related attributes but no FK to warehouse_l']
  al2o3_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Alumina content percentage of the stockpile material. Key impurity parameter affecting product grade and customer specifications.']
  blending_instructions varchar [note: 'Operational instructions for blending this stockpile with other stockpiles to achieve target quality specifications for vessel loading. May include blend ratios and target quality parameters.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stockpile record was first created in the system. Represents the initial establishment of the stockpile in port operations.']
  current_tonnage decimal(15,3) [note: 'Current inventory balance of material in the stockpile measured in metric tonnes. Represents the net result of all stack-in and reclaim movements.']
  dust_suppression_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dust suppression systems are currently active for this stockpile. Critical for environmental compliance and Health Safety and Environment (HSE) management.']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'Environmental permit or authorization number under which this stockpile operates. Required for compliance with environmental regulations and dust management.']
  fe_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Iron content percentage of the stockpile material. Critical quality parameter for iron ore products, typically measured through assay analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the stockpile record. Tracks when any attribute of the stockpile was last changed.']
  last_movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent material movement (stack-in or reclaim) affecting this stockpile. Used for activity monitoring and operational reporting.']
  last_survey_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical survey or measurement of the stockpile tonnage. Used for inventory reconciliation and accuracy verification.']
  last_survey_tonnage decimal(15,3) [note: 'Measured tonnage from the most recent physical survey in metric tonnes. Used to reconcile system inventory against physical stockpile volume.']
  maximum_tonnage_threshold decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum storage capacity of the stockpile in metric tonnes. Represents the physical or operational limit for stacking operations.']
  minimum_tonnage_threshold decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum allowable tonnage level for the stockpile in metric tonnes. Triggers alerts when inventory falls below this level to ensure continuous vessel loading capability.']
  moisture_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content percentage of the stockpile material. Important for shipping weight calculations and product quality specifications.']
  p_percent decimal(5,3) [note: 'Phosphorus content percentage of the stockpile material. Critical impurity parameter for steel-making grade iron ore, typically measured in parts per thousand.']
  quarantine_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the stockpile is quarantined or restricted from use. Populated when stockpile_status is quarantined due to quality issues, contamination, or regulatory hold.']
  sio2_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Silica content percentage of the stockpile material. Important gangue mineral parameter for product quality and customer acceptance.']
  size_fraction_mm varchar [note: 'Particle size distribution range of the stockpile material in millimeters (e.g., 6.3-31.5 for lump, 0-6.3 for fines). Critical for product classification and customer specifications.']
  stockpile_area_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Physical footprint area of the stockpile in square meters. Used for capacity planning and space utilization analysis.']
  stockpile_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the stockpile, used for operational reference and reporting. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by port operations.']
  stockpile_height_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Current height of the stockpile in meters. Used for volume calculations and operational safety monitoring.']
  stockpile_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or other relevant information about the stockpile that does not fit structured fields.']
  stockpile_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the stockpile indicating whether it is available for stacking or reclaim operations.']
  stockpile_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stockpile based on the physical form and processing state of the commodity (lump ore, fines, concentrate, blend, pellets, sinter feed).']
  survey_variance_tonnage decimal(15,3) [note: 'Difference between system inventory and last physical survey tonnage in metric tonnes. Positive values indicate system over-reporting, negative values indicate under-reporting.']

  Note: 'Tracks the inventory balance, quality parameters, and complete tonnage movement history of commodity product held at port stockpile facilities awaiting vessel loading. Captures stockpile ID, port facility, commodity product grade, stockpile type (lump, fines, concentrate), current tonnage, quality parameters (Fe%, moisture, size fraction, Al2O3, SiO2, P), blending instructions, min/max tonnage thresholds, last survey date, and stockpile status. Records all individual movements (stack-in, reclaim, blend, vessel loading) with movement ID, movement type, tonnage, quality at movement, timestamp, equipment used (stacker/reclaimer), operator, source/destination stockpile (for blends), and reference shipment or train number. Serves as the SSOT for port inventory management, stockpile reconciliation, all movement history, and vessel loading planning — no other product in this domain records stockpile movements.'
}

Table procurement.stockpile_movement {
  stockpile_movement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stockpile movement transaction. Primary key for the stockpile movement record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary equipment unit (stacker, reclaimer, loader, or conveyor system) used to execute this movement.']
  blend_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the blending recipe or specification used if this movement is part of a blending operation. Nullable for non-blend movements.']
  destination_stockpile_port_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination stockpile for blend or transfer movements. Nullable for reclaim or vessel loading movements.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment operator or supervisor responsible for executing this stockpile movement.']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.freight_order. Business justification: Links stockpile movements to freight orders when the movement is part of inbound or outbound logistics. Stockpile_movement has train_number, truck_number, vessel_name fields indicating freight movemen']
  laboratory_sample_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical sample taken during this movement for quality assurance and laboratory analysis.']
  outbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the vessel shipment record if this movement is part of a vessel loading operation. Nullable for non-loading movements.']
  primary_port_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the port stockpile involved in this movement transaction.']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational shift during which this movement was executed.']
  source_stockpile_port_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source stockpile for blend or transfer movements. Nullable for stack-in movements from external sources.']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material type being moved (iron ore, copper concentrate, coal, etc.).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or observations recorded by the operator or supervisor during the movement operation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this stockpile movement record was first created in the system.']
  delay_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of delays in minutes for this movement operation, used for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation.']
  delay_reason varchar [note: 'Description of any delays or interruptions encountered during the movement operation, used for root cause analysis and continuous improvement.']
  fines_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of fine-sized material (typically -6mm) in the stockpile movement, important for product specification compliance.']
  grade_al2o3_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Alumina content percentage of the material at the time of movement, important quality parameter for mineral processing.']
  grade_fe_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Iron content percentage of the material at the time of movement, critical quality parameter for iron ore products.']
  grade_p_percent decimal(5,3) [note: 'Phosphorus content percentage of the material at the time of movement, critical impurity parameter for steel-making ore.']
  grade_sio2_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Silica content percentage of the material at the time of movement, key gangue indicator for ore quality.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this stockpile movement record was last updated in the system.']
  lump_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of lump-sized material (typically +6mm) in the stockpile movement, important for product specification compliance.']
  moisture_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content percentage of the material at the time of movement, affects tonnage calculations and shipping specifications.']
  movement_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stockpile movement operation was completed.']
  movement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the stockpile movement transaction, used for tracking and reconciliation across systems.']
  movement_priority varchar [note: 'Operational priority level assigned to this movement, used for scheduling and resource allocation decisions.']
  movement_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stockpile movement operation commenced.']
  movement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stockpile movement transaction in the operational workflow.']
  movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stockpile movement operation was executed or completed in the physical operation.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stockpile movement operation: stack_in (material added to stockpile), reclaim (material removed from stockpile), blend (material transferred for blending), vessel_load (material loaded onto ship), rail_discharge (material received from rail), truck_discharge (material received from truck).']
  planned_tonnage decimal(15,3) [note: 'Target mass of material planned to be moved in this transaction, measured in metric tonnes. Used for variance analysis against actual tonnage.']
  tonnage decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total mass of material moved in this transaction, measured in metric tonnes. Critical for stockpile balance reconciliation and inventory management.']
  train_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the rail train if this movement involves rail discharge to stockpile. Nullable for non-rail movements.']
  truck_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the haulage truck if this movement involves truck discharge to stockpile. Nullable for non-truck movements.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vessel being loaded if this movement is a vessel loading operation. Nullable for non-vessel movements.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather conditions during the movement operation, relevant for operational efficiency analysis and safety compliance.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the work order or production order authorizing this stockpile movement operation.']

  Note: 'Records all tonnage movements into and out of port stockpiles including reclaim, stacking, blending, and vessel loading events. Captures movement ID, stockpile, movement type (stack-in, reclaim, blend, load), tonnage, quality parameters at movement, timestamp, equipment used (stacker/reclaimer), operator, and reference shipment or train number. Enables real-time stockpile balance reconciliation and quality tracking.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_vendor_performance {
  procurement_vendor_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for vendor_performance']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor being evaluated. Links to the vendor master record in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor performance evaluation was formally approved by the designated authority.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the manager or executive who approved the vendor performance evaluation and its recommendations.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date when the vendor performance evaluation was approved.']
  average_issue_resolution_time_days decimal(8,2) [note: 'The average number of days taken by the vendor to resolve reported issues, complaints, or non-conformances during the evaluation period.']
  commodity_category varchar [note: 'The commodity or material category for which the vendor is being evaluated (e.g., explosives, drilling consumables, steel, fuel, PPE). Aligns with SAP material group classification.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective actions required from the vendor, including specific performance gaps and expected improvements.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action is required from the vendor to address performance deficiencies identified during the evaluation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor performance evaluation record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total procurement value (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).']
  delivery_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The score measuring the vendor\'s delivery performance, including on-time delivery rate and order fulfillment metrics.']
  evaluation_date date [note: 'The date when the vendor performance evaluation was formally conducted and recorded.']
  evaluation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments from the evaluator providing additional context, observations, or recommendations regarding the vendor\'s performance.']
  evaluation_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the performance evaluation period. Defines the conclusion of the assessment window for vendor KPIs.']
  evaluation_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the performance evaluation period. Defines the beginning of the assessment window for vendor KPIs.']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the vendor performance evaluation record. Tracks the evaluation through draft, approval, and closure stages.']
  evaluator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the procurement or category manager who conducted the vendor performance evaluation.']
  evaluator_role varchar [note: 'Job role or title of the person who conducted the evaluation (e.g., Procurement Manager, Category Manager, Supply Chain Lead).']
  hse_incident_count varchar [note: 'Total number of health, safety, and environmental incidents attributed to the vendor during the evaluation period. Sourced from IsoMetrix HSE incident management system.']
  invoice_accuracy_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of vendor invoices that matched purchase orders and goods receipts without discrepancies (three-way match success rate). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI invoice verification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor performance evaluation record was last updated. Audit trail field for tracking changes.']
  ncr_count varchar [note: 'The total number of Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs) raised against the vendor during the evaluation period for quality issues.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next vendor performance evaluation. Supports periodic review cycles (e.g., quarterly, semi-annual, annual).']
  on_time_delivery_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of purchase orders delivered on or before the scheduled delivery date during the evaluation period. Key delivery performance KPI.']
  order_fill_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of purchase order line items completely fulfilled by the vendor without shortages or backorders during the evaluation period.']
  overall_performance_rating varchar [note: 'Composite performance rating summarizing the vendor\'s overall performance across all evaluation dimensions. Used for contract renewal and preferred supplier decisions.']
  overall_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The composite overall performance score for the vendor across all evaluation dimensions, typically expressed as a percentage or weighted average.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'The overall performance rating classification assigned to the vendor based on the evaluation results, used for vendor rationalization and preferred supplier decisions.']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has been designated as a preferred supplier based on this performance evaluation. Preferred vendors receive priority in sourcing decisions.']
  price_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The score measuring the vendor\'s price competitiveness and adherence to contracted pricing.']
  price_variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage variance between actual invoiced prices and contracted prices during the evaluation period. Positive values indicate overcharges, negative values indicate discounts.']
  quality_acceptance_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of goods receipts that passed quality inspection without rejection or non-conformance during the evaluation period. Calculated from LabWare LIMS and SAP quality inspection data.']
  quality_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The score measuring the vendor\'s quality performance, including rejection rates and non-conformance records.']
  rejection_count varchar [note: 'Total number of goods receipts rejected due to quality non-conformance during the evaluation period. Used to calculate quality acceptance rate.']
  rejection_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of goods receipts rejected due to quality defects or non-conformance during the evaluation period.']
  responsiveness_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the vendor\'s responsiveness to inquiries, requests, and communications during the evaluation period.']
  responsiveness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Qualitative score (0-100) assessing the vendor\'s responsiveness to inquiries, issue resolution, and communication timeliness. Evaluated by procurement and operational teams.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'The supply risk level assigned to the vendor based on performance evaluation results, used for supply risk management and mitigation planning.']
  service_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The score measuring the vendor\'s service quality, including responsiveness and issue resolution capabilities.']
  total_goods_receipt_count varchar [note: 'Total number of goods receipts processed for the vendor during the evaluation period. Used to calculate delivery and quality metrics.']
  total_po_count varchar [note: 'The total number of purchase orders issued to the vendor during the evaluation period that were included in this performance assessment.']
  total_po_line_count varchar [note: 'The total number of purchase order line items issued to the vendor during the evaluation period.']
  total_procurement_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of all procurement transactions with the vendor during the evaluation period. Represents the vendor\'s business volume.']
  total_purchase_order_count varchar [note: 'Total number of purchase orders issued to the vendor during the evaluation period. Provides context for performance metrics.']
  total_rejection_count varchar [note: 'The total number of goods receipts rejected due to quality defects or non-conformance during the evaluation period.']

  Note: 'Tracks and records periodic vendor performance evaluations against key supply metrics for vendors delivering to mining operations. Captures evaluation period, vendor, overall score, delivery score (on-time delivery rate, order fill rate), quality score (rejection rate, NCR count), price score (price variance to contract), service score (responsiveness, issue resolution time), number of POs evaluated, number of rejections, and evaluator. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Evaluation (ME61). Distinct from procurement domain\'s strategic supplier evaluation — this product focuses on operational delivery performance metrics measured against actual goods receipts and PO fulfilment. Supports vendor rationalisation, preferred supplier decisions, and supply risk management.'
}

Table procurement.requisition {
  requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase requisition record. Primary key.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Requisitions originate from cost centres for budget control, approval workflow, and commitment tracking. Essential for procurement budget management in mining.']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Requisitions specify GL account for budget checking, commitment tracking, and cost element classification. Required for financial control and reporting.']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Requisition may reference a framework contract for pricing and terms. Converting STRING contract_number to FK enables proper contract governance and pricing lookup. N:1 relationship (many requisitions']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the preferred or assigned vendor for this requisition. Populated when a source of supply is pre-determined or suggested by the requisitioner.']
  requisitioner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Requisitions are raised by employees. Tracking who requested materials is essential for approval workflows, cost centre validation, budget accountability, and audit trails. Currently stored as text na']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the material master record for stock materials. Null for service requisitions or non-stock materials.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Capital requisitions link to WBS for project budget control, approval workflow, and commitment reporting. Critical for capital project procurement management.']
  account_assignment_category varchar [note: 'Classification of how the requisition expenditure is assigned for financial accounting purposes. Determines whether costs are tracked against cost centres, capital projects, assets, or sales orders.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition received final approval and became eligible for conversion to purchase order. Null if still pending approval or rejected.']
  approval_workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current stage in the multi-level approval workflow. Requisitions above certain value thresholds require manager, department head, and executive approvals.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the current or final approver in the approval chain. Identifies the person responsible for authorizing the procurement expenditure.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'Twelve-digit fixed asset number when the requisition is for asset procurement or asset-related maintenance. Used for asset lifecycle tracking and depreciation.']
  conversion_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the requisition has been converted to a purchase order. Tracks the progression from demand signal to formal procurement commitment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requisition record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated value. Typically AUD for Australian mining operations, USD for international procurement.']
  deletion_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating the requisition line has been marked for deletion. Soft delete mechanism preserving audit trail while excluding from active procurement workflows.']
  estimated_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total value of the requisition line in the requisition currency. Used for budgeting and approval threshold determination.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'Twelve-digit internal order number for tracking costs against specific operational activities or maintenance campaigns. Used when account assignment category is internal order.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the requisition record. Tracks changes throughout the requisition lifecycle for audit and compliance purposes.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the requisition. Identifies individual material or service requests within a single requisition document.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Nine-character hierarchical code classifying the material or service category. Used for spend analysis, supplier segmentation, and procurement strategy.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'Three-character code identifying the MRP (Material Requirements Planning) controller responsible for material planning. Relevant for requisitions generated automatically by MRP run.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the mine site, processing plant, or facility where the material or service is required. Represents the receiving location.']
  priority_indicator varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the requisition. Urgent requisitions are expedited for critical operational needs such as equipment breakdowns or safety-critical items.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Three-character code identifying the procurement team responsible for sourcing this requisition. Examples include mining equipment, consumables, services, or contracts teams.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material or service units being requested. Precision supports fractional quantities for materials measured in decimal units.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the approver when a requisition is rejected. Captures business justification for denial such as budget constraints, alternative sourcing, or specification issues.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the requested material or service must be delivered to the requesting location. Drives procurement planning and supplier selection.']
  requisition_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase requisition was created in the system. Represents the formal initiation of the procurement demand signal.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the purchase requisition as displayed in SAP S/4HANA MM. Ten-digit numeric code used for tracking and reference across procurement workflows.']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the requisition in the approval and conversion workflow. Tracks progression from creation through approval to purchase order conversion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|rejected|partially_converted|fully_converted|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requisition_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the requisition based on procurement scenario: standard purchase, stock transfer between plants, subcontracting with vendor, consignment arrangement, or service procurement.']
  requisitioner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the requisitioner for communication regarding the requisition status, approvals, and delivery notifications.']
  short_text varchar [note: 'Brief description of the material or service being requisitioned. Used when material master does not exist or for additional context.']
  source_determination_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether automatic source determination should be performed to identify preferred vendors or contracts. True triggers sourcing logic in procurement workflow.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the specific storage location within the plant where material should be delivered. Examples include warehouse zones, ROM (Run of Mine) pads, or maintenance stores.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the requisitioned quantity is expressed. ISO standard unit codes such as EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter), TON (tonne).']

  Note: 'Internal purchase requisition raised by mine site departments or generated automatically by MRP run, requesting procurement of materials or services. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (EBAN). Captures requisition number, requesting cost centre, plant, material or service description, quantity, required delivery date, estimated value, priority (urgent/normal), approval workflow status, approver chain, and conversion status to purchase order. Serves as the SSOT for procurement demand signals. Initiates the formal procurement workflow and provides input to supply planning and material plan execution.'
}

Table procurement.framework_agreement {
  framework_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for framework_agreement']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Contracts often tied to specific cost centres for budget allocation, spend tracking, and contract performance monitoring. Common in site-specific mining contracts.']
  primary_framework_contract_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier party with whom this framework agreement is established.']
  sourcing_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.sourcing_event. Business justification: Links framework agreements to the sourcing events that generated them. Framework agreements may result from formal competitive sourcing processes (RFPs, tenders). This FK captures that relationship wh']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the framework agreement was formally approved by the authorized signatory or procurement governance board.']
  base_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard unit price agreed in the framework agreement for fixed-price contracts, or baseline price for variable pricing mechanisms. Expressed per unit of measure.']
  commodity_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the goods or services covered by this framework agreement, such as explosives, reagents, fuel, tyres, spare parts, or professional services. Aligns with procurement category taxonomy.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity or company code on behalf of which this framework agreement is established. Relevant for multi-entity mining groups.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the framework agreement, typically a 10-character alphanumeric code assigned by the procurement system.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the framework agreement. Draft indicates under negotiation, active means in force, suspended means temporarily halted, expired means validity period ended, terminated means cancelled before expiry, closed means completed and archived.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the framework agreement type. Quantity contracts specify total quantity, value contracts specify total spend, scheduling agreements define delivery schedules, blanket orders allow multiple releases, master agreements set terms for multiple sites, and call-off contracts enable on-demand ordering.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this framework agreement record was first created in the procurement system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the contract value and pricing are denominated (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD, ZAR).']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of calendar days from order placement to expected delivery at the designated location, as agreed in the framework agreement.']
