  end_date date [note: 'Effective end date after which the framework agreement expires and no further orders can be placed unless renewed. Nullable for open-ended agreements.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms code defining the allocation of costs, risks, and responsibilities between buyer and seller for delivery under this framework agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port specified in the Incoterms clause, such as the delivery destination, port of loading, or place of transfer of risk.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this framework agreement record was last updated in the procurement system.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal performance review conducted for this framework agreement.']
  maximum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity that can be ordered in a single release or call-off against this framework agreement. Nullable if no upper limit per order.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered in each release or call-off against this framework agreement. Enforces vendor minimum batch or economic order size.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal performance review of this framework agreement.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code defining the due date calculation and discount conditions for invoices under this framework agreement (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30).']
  performance_review_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which vendor performance under this framework agreement is formally reviewed and documented.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Primary mine site, processing plant, or operational facility for which this framework agreement is intended. Nullable for corporate-wide agreements.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'Quantity of units to which the base price applies. For example, if base_price is 100 USD per 10 units, price_unit is 10.']
  pricing_condition_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which pricing is determined under this framework agreement. Fixed price remains constant, variable price adjusts per order, cost-plus adds margin to vendor cost, index-linked ties to commodity index, market-based uses spot rates, tiered pricing varies by volume.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Buyer or procurement team responsible for day-to-day administration and call-offs against this framework agreement.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for negotiating and managing this framework agreement. Typically a centralized procurement function or regional procurement office.']
  renewal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this framework agreement is eligible for automatic renewal upon expiry. True if renewal clause exists, false otherwise.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to contract end date by which either party must provide notice if they do not wish to renew. Nullable if no renewal clause.']
  start_date date [note: 'Effective start date from which the framework agreement becomes binding and orders can be placed against it.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the framework agreement was terminated early, if applicable. Nullable if contract ran to natural expiry or is still active.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the framework agreement before its natural expiry date.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the framework agreement was terminated early, such as vendor performance issues, business requirement changes, or mutual agreement.']
  total_contract_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity of goods or services committed under this framework agreement for quantity contracts. Nullable for value contracts or service agreements without fixed quantities.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value committed under this framework agreement for value contracts, or estimated total spend for quantity contracts. Represents the financial ceiling or budget allocation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the contracted quantity, such as tonnes (MT), litres (L), kilograms (KG), units (EA), or hours (HR). Aligns with material master UOM.']

  Note: 'Long-term supply agreements and framework contracts with vendors for the ongoing provision of operational inputs such as explosives, reagents, fuel, and tyres. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement (EKKO type MK/WK). Captures contract number, vendor, contract type (quantity/value contract, scheduling agreement), commodity category, start date, end date, total contract value, currency, pricing conditions, minimum order quantity, and contract status. Distinct from the procurement domain\'s contract lifecycle management.'
}

Table procurement.freight_order {
  freight_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the freight order. Primary key for the freight order entity.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer receiving goods for outbound freight movements. Null for inbound and inter-site movements.']
  origin_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.warehouse_location. Business justification: Freight orders have origin locations that should reference warehouse_location master. Currently freight_order has origin_location_code and origin_location_name as denormalized strings. Creating this F']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor dispatching goods for inbound freight movements. Null for outbound and inter-site movements.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Freight orders transport specific saleable products requiring product-specific handling, packaging, and hazmat classification. Essential for logistics planning, freight cost allocation by product, and']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logistics carrier or freight forwarder responsible for transporting the cargo.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the freight arrived at the destination location.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the freight departed from the origin location.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of lading number issued by the carrier for sea freight, serving as receipt and contract of carriage. Null for non-sea transport.']
  cargo_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the cargo being transported, including packaging and handling requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the freight order record was first created in the system.']
  customs_clearance_date date [note: 'Date when customs clearance was completed for international freight. Null if not applicable or not yet cleared.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of customs clearance for international freight movements: not-required, pending, cleared, held, or rejected.']
  dangerous_goods_class varchar [note: 'UN dangerous goods classification code (e.g., 1.1, 3, 8) if cargo contains hazardous materials. Null if not dangerous goods.']
  dangerous_goods_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cargo contains dangerous goods requiring special handling and regulatory compliance.']
  dangerous_goods_un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying the dangerous goods substance (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline). Null if not dangerous goods.']
  delivery_note_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the delivery note or goods receipt document associated with this freight order.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the destination location of the freight movement (plant, port, customer site, or inter-site facility).']
  destination_location_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the destination location for the freight movement.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Direction of freight movement: inbound (vendor to site), outbound (site to customer), or inter-site (between mining sites).']
  expected_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned or scheduled date when the freight is expected to arrive at the destination location.']
  expected_departure_date date [note: 'Planned or scheduled date when the freight is expected to depart from the origin location.']
  freight_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total freight cost for this shipment in the specified currency.']
  freight_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the freight cost amount (e.g., USD, AUD, EUR).']
  freight_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique freight order number used for tracking and reference across logistics operations.']
  freight_rate_per_tonne decimal(18,2) [note: 'Freight rate charged per metric tonne of cargo. Applicable primarily to bulk commodity shipments.']
  freight_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the freight order: planned, booked, in-transit, arrived, delivered, or cancelled.']
  gross_weight_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the cargo including packaging and containers, measured in metric tonnes.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for freight costs and risk transfer (e.g., FOB, CIF). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the freight order record was last updated in the system.']
  laycan_end_date date [note: 'End date of the laycan period (latest date vessel can arrive for loading) for sea freight. Null for non-sea transport.']
  laycan_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the laycan period (earliest date vessel can arrive for loading) for sea freight. Null for non-sea transport.']
  loaded_quantity_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of commodity loaded onto the vessel, measured in metric tonnes. Applicable to sea freight.']
  net_weight_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Net weight of the cargo excluding packaging and containers, measured in metric tonnes.']
  nominated_quantity_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of commodity nominated or planned for loading onto the vessel, measured in metric tonnes. Applicable to sea freight.']
  tracking_reference varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking reference number for real-time shipment visibility and status updates.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for this freight movement: road, rail, sea, air, pipeline, or multimodal.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vessel for sea freight movements. Null for non-sea transport modes.']
  volume_cubic_metres decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total volume of the cargo measured in cubic metres.']
  voyage_number varchar [note: 'Voyage or sailing number for sea freight movements. Null for non-sea transport modes.']

  Note: 'Unified logistics execution entity managing all freight movements across the mining supply chain — inbound material deliveries from vendors (road/rail/sea), outbound commodity shipments to customers (road/rail/sea), and inter-site transfers. Captures freight order number, direction (inbound/outbound/inter-site), transport mode (road/rail/sea), carrier, vendor (for inbound), customer (for outbound), origin location, destination plant/port, cargo description, commodity product, gross weight/tonnage, volume, dangerous goods flag, freight cost, expected/actual departure and arrival dates, customs clearance status, delivery note reference, tracking reference, and freight status. For sea freight: vessel name, voyage number, bill of lading number, laycan dates, nominated/loaded quantity, loaded quantity, Incoterms (FOB/CIF), freight rate, and shipment status. For inbound: vendor dispatch tracking, carrier ETA, and dangerous goods declaration reference. Serves as the SSOT for end-to-end transport visibility across all logistics legs and directions — no other product in this domain records freight or shipment movements.'
}

Table procurement.physical_inventory {
  physical_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the physical inventory count document. Primary key for the physical inventory record.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Inventory variance posting requires cost centre for write-off/adjustment allocation and budget impact tracking. Essential for inventory accounting and cost control.']
  counter_employee_id varchar [note: 'The employee identifier of the person who performed the physical count.']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Inventory adjustments post to GL for financial reporting, variance analysis, and audit trail. Required for accurate financial statements and inventory valuation.']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the material being counted in the physical inventory.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.warehouse_location. Business justification: Physical inventory counts are conducted at specific warehouse locations. The physical_inventory table has storage_location_code (STRING) but no FK to warehouse_location. Adding storage_location_id FK ']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the physical inventory variance was approved for posting.']
  approval_required_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the variance requires management or supervisor approval before posting.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who approved the physical inventory variance for posting.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number of the material counted, if batch management is active for the material.']
  book_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The system-recorded inventory quantity (book inventory) at the time of the physical count.']
  count_date date [note: 'The date on which the physical inventory count was conducted at the storage location.']
  count_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the physical inventory count document in its lifecycle.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'The type of physical inventory count performed: scheduled periodic count, ad-hoc count, cycle count, annual stocktake, or spot check.']
  counted_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The actual physical quantity counted during the inventory count process.']
  counter_name varchar [note: 'The name of the person or team who performed the physical count.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who created the physical inventory count document in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the physical inventory count document was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the variance value (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The unique document number assigned to the physical inventory count in the source system (SAP MI01/MI07).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the physical inventory count was conducted.']
  freeze_book_inventory_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the book inventory was frozen at the time of count to prevent movements during counting.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who last modified the physical inventory count document.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the physical inventory count document was last modified.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'The descriptive name of the material being counted.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'The material document number generated when the physical inventory variance is posted to update stock levels.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'The material number (SKU) of the item being counted in the physical inventory.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The mine site or plant code where the physical inventory count was performed.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the inventory variance was posted to the financial and stock accounts.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory variance has been posted to the general ledger and stock accounts.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'The code indicating the reason for the inventory variance (e.g., theft, damage, counting error, system error).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description or notes explaining the reason for the inventory variance.']
  recount_date date [note: 'The date on which the recount was performed, if applicable.']
  recount_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a recount was required or performed due to significant variance or discrepancy.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicator for special stock types such as consignment stock, project stock, or vendor-owned inventory.']
  tolerance_exceeded_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the variance exceeded the configured tolerance threshold requiring management approval.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the material quantity is expressed (e.g., EA, KG, MT, L).']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The difference between the book quantity and the counted quantity (counted minus book).']
  variance_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the inventory variance, calculated as variance quantity multiplied by material valuation price.']

  Note: 'Records scheduled and ad-hoc physical stock counts conducted at mine site warehouses and storage locations to reconcile system inventory balances against actual on-hand quantities. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory (MI01/MI07). Captures count document number, plant, storage location, count date, material, system quantity, counted quantity, variance quantity, variance value, recount flag, and posting status.'
}

Table procurement.hazmat_register {
  hazmat_register_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the hazardous material register record.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Hazmat storage locations tie to cost centres for compliance cost tracking, liability allocation, and environmental provision calculation. Required for environmental accounting.']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Hazardous materials (acids, reagents, explosives) may be stored/managed by specific laboratories. Links hazmat register to responsible laboratory for compliance tracking, emergency response, audit tra']
  primary_hazmat_supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the material master record for this hazardous material.']
  tertiary_hazmat_material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to procurement.material_master']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.warehouse_location. Business justification: Hazardous materials are stored at specific licensed warehouse locations. N:1 relationship (many hazmat items at one location). Converting STRING storage_location_code to FK enables proper location com']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'The unique numerical identifier assigned by the Chemical Abstracts Service to every chemical substance.']
  chemical_name varchar [note: 'The technical or chemical name of the hazardous substance as per regulatory classification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this hazmat register record was created in the system.']
  current_inventory_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The current quantity of this hazardous material held in inventory across all storage locations.']
  dangerous_goods_licence_expiry_date date [note: 'The expiry date of the dangerous goods licence.']
  dangerous_goods_licence_number varchar [note: 'The regulatory licence number authorizing the storage and handling of dangerous goods at this site.']
  emergency_response_procedure_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the documented emergency response procedure or Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for incidents involving this hazardous material.']
  explosives_licence_expiry_date date [note: 'The expiry date of the explosives licence, after which storage and handling is not permitted.']
  explosives_licence_number varchar [note: 'The regulatory licence number authorizing the storage and handling of explosives at this site.']
  hazard_class varchar [note: 'The primary hazard classification of the material according to UN dangerous goods classification (e.g., Class 1 Explosives, Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 8 Corrosives).']
  hazard_division varchar [note: 'The subdivision within the hazard class that further categorizes the type of hazard (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 1.3 for explosives).']
  hazchem_code varchar [note: 'The emergency action code used by emergency services to identify appropriate response procedures for the hazardous material.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'The date when the hazardous material storage location was last inspected for compliance and safety.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this hazmat register record was last updated or modified.']
  material_status varchar [note: 'The operational status of this hazardous material in the supply chain inventory.']
  maximum_permitted_storage_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The maximum quantity of this hazardous material that is permitted to be stored at the site under regulatory licence conditions.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next regulatory or safety inspection of the hazardous material storage.']
  packing_group varchar [note: 'The packing group indicating the degree of danger: I (high danger), II (medium danger), III (low danger).']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of the required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) for handling this hazardous material (e.g., gloves, respirator, face shield, protective clothing).']
  registration_date date [note: 'The date when this hazardous material was first registered in the hazmat register.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'The current regulatory compliance status of this hazardous material registration.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the handling, storage, or regulatory requirements of this hazardous material.']
  responsible_person_contact varchar [note: 'The contact phone number or email of the responsible person for emergency situations.']
  responsible_person_name varchar [note: 'The name of the person responsible for managing and overseeing this hazardous material at the site.']
  restricted_use_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the use of this hazardous material is restricted to specific operations or requires special authorization.']
  restricted_use_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the material is restricted, including regulatory or operational reasons.']
  sds_reference_number varchar [note: 'The reference number or document identifier for the Safety Data Sheet (formerly MSDS) for this hazardous material.']
  sds_revision_date date [note: 'The date when the Safety Data Sheet was last revised or updated.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the mining site or facility where this hazardous material is stored.']
  storage_compatibility_group varchar [note: 'The compatibility group code that determines which hazardous materials can be stored together safely (e.g., explosives compatibility groups A-S).']
  storage_conditions varchar [note: 'Required environmental and physical conditions for safe storage of the hazardous material (e.g., temperature range, humidity, ventilation requirements).']
  storage_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the maximum permitted storage quantity (e.g., KG for kilograms, L for litres, T for tonnes).']
  un_number varchar [note: 'The four-digit UN identification number assigned to the hazardous material for transport purposes.']

  Note: 'Register of all hazardous materials held in supply chain inventory including explosives, ammonium nitrate, cyanide, acids, and fuel. Captures hazmat record ID, material, UN number, hazard class, packing group, storage compatibility group, maximum permitted storage quantity, regulatory licence reference (explosives licence, dangerous goods licence), emergency response procedure reference, and site-specific storage location. Supports HSE compliance and dangerous goods regulatory reporting.'
}

Table procurement.material_plan {
  material_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for material_plan']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Material planning references contracts for sourcing strategy and supply assurance. Converting STRING contract_number to FK enables proper planning-to-contract alignment. N:1 relationship (many plan li']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preferred or assigned vendor for this material plan. Populated when source_of_supply is vendor or contract.']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for which this plan is created. Links to the material being planned for procurement or production.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this material plan. Null if not yet approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this material plan record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  criticality_indicator varchar [note: 'Business classification of how critical this material is to operations. Critical materials require higher safety stock and expedited procurement to avoid production stoppages.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the planned procurement cost. Typically the local operating currency of the plant or company code.']
  last_modified_date date [note: 'The date when this material plan record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the plan.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days required from order placement to material receipt at the plant. Used to calculate order release dates from planned delivery dates.']
  lom_alignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this material plan is aligned with the long-term Life of Mine plan. True if the plan supports strategic LOM objectives, false for tactical short-term planning.']
  maximum_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The upper limit for inventory holding, used to prevent overstocking and optimize working capital. Relevant for storage capacity constraints and cost control.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this material plan record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'The person or team responsible for managing the material planning process for this material at this plant. Three-character code identifying the planner.']
  plan_approval_date date [note: 'The date when this material plan was formally approved by the MRP controller or procurement manager. Null if still in draft status.']
  plan_creation_date date [note: 'The date when this material plan record was initially created in the system. Represents the planning run date.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the material requirements plan, typically generated by the MRP (Material Requirements Planning) system. Format: MP-YYYYMMDD.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the material plan. Tracks progression from initial draft through approval, execution, and closure.']
  planned_delivery_date date [note: 'The target date by which the material must be available at the plant or storage location to meet operational requirements. Used to calculate procurement lead time.']
  planned_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material to be procured or produced to satisfy the planned requirement, adjusted for existing stock, safety stock, and lot-sizing rules. Represents net procurement need.']
  planned_procurement_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost for procuring the planned order quantity, based on current pricing, contracts, or moving average price. Used for budget planning and cost control.']
  planned_requirement_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The forecasted or calculated quantity of material required for the planning period, derived from production schedules, maintenance plans, or consumption forecasts. Represents gross demand.']
  planning_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for MRP controller or planner to document special considerations, assumptions, or constraints affecting this material plan. Supports collaborative planning.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the planning period covered by this material plan. Defines the conclusion of the demand forecast window.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the planning period covered by this material plan. Defines the beginning of the demand forecast window.']
  planning_period_type varchar [note: 'The time bucket granularity for this material plan. Indicates whether the plan is structured on a daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annual basis.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The mine site, processing plant, or operational facility for which this material requirement is planned. Four-character alphanumeric code.']
  production_schedule_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the mine production schedule or processing plant schedule that drives this material requirement. Links demand to operational plans.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'The procurement team or buyer group responsible for executing the purchase orders for this material. Three-character organizational code.']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered. Calculated based on lead time demand and safety stock requirements.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The minimum buffer stock level maintained to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Critical for operational continuity in mining operations.']
  source_of_supply varchar [note: 'Indicates how the material requirement will be fulfilled: external vendor procurement, existing contract, internal plant transfer, or in-house production.']
  stockout_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score (0-100) indicating the probability of a stockout occurring during the planning period. Based on demand variability, lead time, and supplier reliability.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the planned quantities are expressed (e.g., KG for kilograms, MT for metric tonnes, EA for each, L for litres). ISO standard unit codes.']

  Note: 'Forward-looking material requirements plan for operational inputs aligned to the mine production schedule and life-of-mine (LOM) plan. Captures plan ID, planning period (weekly/monthly/quarterly), material, plant, planned requirement quantity, planned order quantity, planned delivery date, safety stock target, reorder point, source of supply (vendor or contract), MRP controller, and plan status. Serves as the SSOT for forward demand planning of supply chain inputs. Enables proactive procurement and inventory positioning to avoid operational stoppages due to stockouts of critical consumables.'
}

Table procurement.vendor {
  vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for vendor']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Auto-generated FK linking siloed vendor to procurement_vendor']

  Note: 'Master register of all approved vendors and suppliers providing goods and services to the mining operation. Captures vendor identity, ABN/registration numbers, business classification (explosives supplier, reagent supplier, OEM, contractor, etc.), prequalification status, insurance certificates, financial standing, payment terms, and approved commodity categories. Serves as the SSOT for vendor identity within the procurement domain. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master and Pronto Xi supplier records.'
}

Table procurement.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for purchase_order']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Auto-generated FK linking siloed purchase_order to procurement_vendor']

  Note: 'Legally binding purchase order issued to a vendor for the supply of mining goods or services. Captures PO number, vendor, delivery address (mine site, ROM pad, processing plant, warehouse), line items, quantities, agreed unit prices, total value, currency, payment terms, delivery terms (FOB/CIF), INCOTERMS, tax codes, and PO status (open, partially delivered, fully delivered, invoiced, closed, cancelled). The primary commercial commitment document in the procurement lifecycle. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM and Pronto Xi.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_contract {
  procurement_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the procurement contract record. Primary key.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor party with whom the contract is established.']
  sourcing_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.sourcing_event. Business justification: Contract is awarded as result of sourcing event (RFP, RFT, tender). One sourcing event can result in multiple contracts if multi-award strategy. N:1 relationship (many contracts from one sourcing even']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of formal amendments executed against this contract since inception. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the contract received final authorization from the appropriate approval authority.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the executive or manager who provided final approval for the contract.']
  base_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Initial unit price established at contract inception, before any escalation or de-escalation adjustments.']
  commodity_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of goods or services covered by the contract (e.g., explosives, diesel fuel, mining equipment, maintenance services, PPE, laboratory consumables).']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity code under which the contract is executed for financial reporting and tax purposes.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique contract identifier used across procurement systems and vendor communications.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract: draft (being prepared), pending_approval (awaiting authorization), approved (authorized but not yet effective), active (in force), suspended (temporarily halted), expired (end date reached), terminated (ended early), closed (completed and archived). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|expired|terminated|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract structure: blanket order (quantity-based), outline agreement (value-based), schedule agreement (delivery schedule), service contract (time and materials), framework agreement (multi-year umbrella), or call-off contract (release-based).']
  contracted_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of goods or service units committed under the contract. Nullable for value-based contracts without quantity commitments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the contract record was first created in the procurement system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in the contract (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of calendar days from purchase order placement to expected goods receipt at the mine site or designated location.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or is scheduled to terminate. Nullable for open-ended contracts.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter International Commercial Terms code defining delivery responsibilities and risk transfer (e.g., FOB, CIF, EXW, DDP).']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port specified in the Incoterms clause where risk and cost transfer occurs.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the contract record was last updated in the procurement system.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was last formally reviewed for performance, pricing, and compliance.']
  latest_amendment_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent contract amendment was executed. Nullable if no amendments have been executed.']
  latest_amendment_description varchar [note: 'Summary of changes made in the most recent amendment (e.g., price adjustment, scope extension, volume change, term extension).']
  latest_amendment_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the most recent contract amendment. Nullable if no amendments have been executed.']
  maximum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Largest quantity that can be ordered under the contract in a single purchase order release. Nullable if no upper limit applies.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered under the contract in a single purchase order release.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal contract review.']
  owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the contract owner for escalations and contract-related communications.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the procurement professional or contract manager responsible for this contract.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms identifier defining due date calculation and discount conditions (e.g., NET30, 2/10NET30).']
  performance_kpi_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the performance metrics and service level agreements the vendor must meet under the contract (e.g., on-time delivery rate, quality acceptance rate, response time).']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Mine site or processing facility code to which the contract applies. Nullable for enterprise-wide contracts.']
  price_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the current pricing schedule becomes applicable.']
  price_escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Contractual terms governing automatic price increases based on index movements, cost changes, or time-based triggers.']
  price_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current pricing schedule ceases to be valid. Nullable for indefinite pricing.']
  price_review_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled interval for formal price review and renegotiation as stipulated in the contract.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'Quantity denominator for the base price (e.g., price per 1 unit, per 100 units, per 1000 units).']
  pricing_basis varchar [note: 'Mechanism governing contract pricing: fixed (locked price), CPI-indexed (Consumer Price Index escalation), commodity-indexed (linked to commodity benchmark), cost-plus (cost recovery plus margin), market-rate (prevailing market price at time of order).']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Buyer or procurement team code responsible for day-to-day contract administration and purchase order releases.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for negotiating and managing the contract (e.g., corporate procurement, regional procurement, site-level procurement).']
  renewal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the contract includes automatic renewal provisions (True) or requires explicit renegotiation (False).']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before contract expiry that renewal or termination notice must be provided to the vendor.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes effective and binding.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date when the contract was terminated early. Nullable if contract was not terminated.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required to terminate the contract before its natural expiry date.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or cause for early contract termination (e.g., vendor non-performance, scope change, mine closure, cost reduction initiative).']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value committed under the contract over its full term. Used for budget tracking and contract compliance monitoring.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit for contracted quantities (e.g., tonne, litre, each, hour, metre).']

  Note: 'Master procurement contract or framework agreement establishing commercial terms between the mining company and a vendor for the ongoing supply of goods or services. Captures contract number, contract type (blanket order, outline agreement, schedule agreement, service contract), vendor, commodity category, contracted volumes, and contract status. Includes full pricing schedule management: base price per line item, price basis (fixed, CPI-indexed, commodity-indexed), effective and expiry dates per price line, price escalation/de-escalation clauses, price review triggers, and applicable mine site or region. Includes complete amendment lifecycle: amendment number, amendment date, description of change (price adjustment, scope extension, volume change, term extension), commercial impact (value delta), approval authority, and amendment status (proposed, approved, executed). Also captures contract start and end dates, renewal options, performance KPIs, and termination conditions. Supports automated PO pricing via price condition linkage, price variance analysis, contract compliance monitoring, and full audit trail of commercial changes. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM contract management, pricing conditions, and change documents.'
}

Table procurement.contract_amendment {
  contract_amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the contract amendment record. Primary key for the contract amendment entity.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Amendments affecting budget require cost centre linkage for budget revision tracking and approval workflow. Essential for contract change management.']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Amendments affecting GL posting require account linkage for financial reporting and cost element reclassification. Required for accurate financial statements.']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original procurement contract being amended. Links to the framework agreement or purchase order contract that this amendment modifies.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Capital contract amendments link to WBS for project budget control and change order tracking. Critical for capital project contract management.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date when the contract amendment was formally created or initiated. Represents the business event timestamp for the amendment request.']
  amendment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the specific changes being made to the parent contract. Includes business rationale, scope of change, and impact summary for audit trail and governance.']
  amendment_initiator_email varchar [note: 'Corporate email address of the amendment initiator for communication and workflow notifications. Business contact information classified as confidential.']
  amendment_initiator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee or procurement professional who created and submitted this amendment request. Provides accountability for change initiation.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential amendment identifier within the parent contract lifecycle. Typically formatted as contract_number-A01, A02, etc. to maintain traceability.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract amendment. Tracks progression through approval workflow from initial draft to final execution or cancellation.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the amendment based on the nature of the contractual change being made. Determines approval workflow and commercial impact assessment requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: price_adjustment|scope_extension|volume_change|term_extension|payment_terms_revision|specification_change|delivery_schedule_revision|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  amendment_value_delta decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net change in contract value resulting from this amendment. Positive values indicate contract value increase, negative values indicate reduction. Critical for budget impact assessment and Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) or Operating Expenditure (OPEX) tracking.']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level required to approve this amendment based on delegation of authority matrix and amendment value thresholds. Ensures governance compliance.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the contract amendment received formal approval from the designated authority. Null if amendment is still pending or was rejected.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who approved this contract amendment. Provides accountability and audit trail for commercial decisions.']
  attachment_reference varchar [note: 'Document management system reference or file path to supporting documentation for this amendment. May include revised specifications, vendor quotes, or approval memos.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary business driver or trigger for the contract amendment. Used for trend analysis and procurement governance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: market_conditions|scope_creep|regulatory_requirement|vendor_request|cost_optimization|quality_issue|force_majeure|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity code in SAP Financial Accounting (FI) module that is party to this contract amendment. Required for financial reporting and intercompany reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this contract amendment record was first created in SAP S/4HANA MM. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this amendment. Ensures consistent financial reporting and foreign exchange management.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the amendment terms become legally binding and operationally active. May differ from amendment date to allow for transition planning.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date when the amendment was formally executed and signed by all parties. Represents legal commitment and triggers operational implementation.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling (CO) internal order for tracking costs related to this amendment. Used for project-based cost collection and Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to this contract amendment record. Tracks data currency and change frequency for governance and compliance.']
  legal_review_date date [note: 'Date when legal department completed their review of the amendment. Null if legal review is not required or has not yet been completed.']
  legal_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment requires review and approval by the legal department before execution. Typically true for high-value or high-risk amendments.']
  legal_reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the legal counsel who reviewed and cleared this amendment. Provides accountability for legal risk assessment.']
  original_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the parent contract before this amendment. Baseline for calculating commercial impact and value delta.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Functional team or buyer group within the purchasing organization responsible for this amendment. Used for workload distribution and performance tracking.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'SAP organizational unit responsible for negotiating and managing this contract amendment. Typically aligned to business unit or geographic region.']
  quantity_delta decimal(15,3) [note: 'Net change in contracted quantity or volume resulting from this amendment. Positive for increases, negative for reductions. Used for supply chain planning and inventory management.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation for why the amendment was rejected by approver or vendor. Null if amendment was approved or is still pending. Used for process improvement and vendor relationship management.']
  revised_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contract value after applying this amendment. Calculated as original contract value plus amendment value delta. Represents the new committed procurement spend.']
  revised_end_date date [note: 'New contract expiration date after applying term extension. Null if amendment does not modify contract duration. Critical for Life of Mine (LOM) planning and procurement forecasting.']
  term_extension_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the contract validity period is extended. Null if amendment does not affect contract duration. Used to calculate revised end date.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit for quantity measurements in this amendment. Examples include tonnes, cubic metres, litres, each, or hours depending on commodity category.']
  vendor_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor formally accepted or signed the amendment. Null if vendor acceptance is not required or has not yet been received.']
  vendor_acceptance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment requires formal acceptance or counter-signature from the vendor. True for bilateral amendments, false for unilateral variations permitted under contract terms.']

  Note: 'Record of a formal amendment or variation to an existing procurement contract. Captures amendment number, parent contract reference, amendment date, description of change (price adjustment, scope extension, volume change, term extension), commercial impact (value delta), approval authority, and amendment status (proposed, approved, executed). Maintains a complete audit trail of contract lifecycle changes for compliance and commercial governance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM contract change management.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_prequalification {
  vendor_prequalification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor prequalification assessment record. Primary key for this entity.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor being assessed for prequalification. Links to the vendor master record.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the prequalification assessment was formally approved by the authorized approver and vendor was added to or updated in the approved vendor list.']
  approved_commodity_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of commodity categories or material groups for which the vendor is prequalified to supply. Restricts vendor to specific procurement scopes.']
  approved_service_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of service categories for which the vendor is prequalified to provide. May include drilling, haulage, maintenance, engineering, consulting, etc.']
  approved_site_locations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of mine sites or plant codes where the vendor is approved to operate. Site-specific HSE inductions and access requirements may apply.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the authorized person who reviewed and approved the prequalification assessment outcome and vendor AVL inclusion.']
  assessment_completion_date date [note: 'Date when all assessment activities were completed and final scoring was determined.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the formal prequalification assessment was conducted and completed. Primary business event timestamp for this assessment.']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing additional observations, comments, or context from the prequalification assessment that do not fit structured fields.']
  assessment_outcome varchar [note: 'Final decision on the vendor prequalification application. Approved vendors are added to AVL, conditional approvals require remediation, rejected vendors cannot supply.']
  assessment_start_date date [note: 'Date when the prequalification assessment process was initiated and vendor documentation collection began.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the prequalification assessment. Tracks progression from initiation through final decision and ongoing validity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_progress|under_review|approved|rejected|conditional|expired|suspended — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of prequalification assessment being conducted. Initial assessments are for new vendors, renewals are periodic reassessments, conditional reviews are triggered by performance issues.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the procurement or supply chain professional who conducted the prequalification assessment and scoring.']
  assessor_role varchar [note: 'Job title or functional role of the person who conducted the assessment, such as Procurement Manager, Supply Chain Analyst, or Category Manager.']
  conditional_approval_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of conditions or deficiencies that resulted in conditional approval status, including specific remediation actions required before full approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this prequalification assessment record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the prequalification approval becomes valid and the vendor is eligible for procurement activities.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the prequalification approval expires and vendor must undergo reassessment to maintain approved vendor list status.']
  financial_health_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the vendor\'s financial stability, creditworthiness, and fiscal capacity to fulfill contracts. Based on financial statements, credit ratings, and liquidity analysis.']
  hse_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score evaluating the vendor\'s health, safety, and environmental compliance record including LTIFR, TRIFR, incident history, and environmental management systems. Critical for mine site access approval.']
  insurance_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assessing adequacy of vendor\'s insurance coverage including public liability, professional indemnity, workers compensation, and contract-specific insurance requirements.']
  iso_14001_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor holds valid ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification. Required for vendors with potential environmental impact.']
  iso_45001_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor holds valid ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification. Mandatory for high-risk mining service providers.']
  iso_9001_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor holds valid ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Demonstrates commitment to quality processes and continuous improvement.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this prequalification assessment record was last updated. Tracks most recent change for audit and data quality purposes.']
  licence_certification_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Assessment of vendor\'s possession of required business licences, industry certifications, trade qualifications, and regulatory permits necessary for mining operations.']
  ltifr_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Vendor\'s Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate per million hours worked. Critical HSE metric for mining industry vendor assessment and site access approval.']
  minimum_threshold_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum overall score required for vendor approval. Vendors scoring below this threshold are rejected or granted conditional approval with remediation requirements.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted composite score aggregating all assessment category scores to provide a single measure of vendor suitability for approved vendor list inclusion.']
  prequalification_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique reference number assigned to this prequalification assessment for tracking and audit purposes.']
  quality_management_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score evaluating the vendor\'s quality management systems, certifications, inspection processes, and track record of delivering conforming products and services.']
  reassessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic reassessment of vendor prequalification. Typically occurs before expiry date to ensure continuity of approval.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the vendor prequalification was rejected, including specific assessment categories that failed to meet minimum standards.']
  technical_capability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Assessment score for the vendor\'s technical competency, equipment capability, workforce skills, quality management systems, and ability to meet mining industry technical specifications.']
  trifr_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Vendor\'s Total Recordable Injury Frequency Rate per million hours worked. Broader HSE metric capturing all recordable injuries including medical treatment cases.']

  Note: 'Vendor prequalification assessment record capturing the formal evaluation of a vendor\'s capability, financial standing, HSE compliance, and technical competency before being admitted to the approved vendor list (AVL). Captures prequalification reference, vendor, assessment date, assessment categories (financial health, HSE performance, technical capability, insurance, licences), scores by category, overall outcome (approved, conditional, rejected), expiry date, and reassessment due date. Mandatory for all vendors supplying to mine sites under ICMM and ISO 45001 requirements.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_performance {
  vendor_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for vendor_performance']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Auto-generated FK linking siloed vendor_performance to procurement_vendor']

  Note: 'Periodic vendor performance evaluation record assessing a supplier\'s delivery, quality, commercial, and HSE performance against contracted KPIs. Captures evaluation period, vendor, commodity category, on-time delivery rate, quality acceptance rate, invoice accuracy rate, HSE incident count, responsiveness score, overall performance rating, and corrective action requirements. Supports supplier relationship management, contract renewal decisions, and preferred supplier designation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM vendor evaluation and IsoMetrix HSE data.'
}

Table procurement.sourcing_event {
  sourcing_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the strategic sourcing event record. Primary key.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor who was awarded the contract as a result of this sourcing event.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the sourcing event award was formally approved by the designated authority.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal approval is required before awarding this sourcing event (True) or not (False), based on delegation of authority thresholds.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status for the sourcing event award recommendation: not required, pending approval, approved, or rejected.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the approving authority who approved the sourcing event award recommendation.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date when the sourcing event was formally awarded to the successful vendor.']
  awarded_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contract value awarded to the successful vendor, representing the committed spend over the contract term.']
  baseline_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Baseline or incumbent contract value used as the reference point for calculating savings achieved through this sourcing event.']
  commodity_category varchar [note: 'Procurement commodity category or spend category that this sourcing event covers (e.g., drilling services, explosives, tyres, fuel, maintenance services, capital equipment).']
  commodity_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed subcategory within the commodity category for more granular classification of the procurement scope.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Intended duration of the resulting contract in months, defining the commitment period for the awarded vendor.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Type of contract that will result from this sourcing event (e.g., framework agreement, call-off contract, fixed-price contract, unit-rate contract).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sourcing event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this sourcing event (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).']
  estimated_annual_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total annual spend value for the commodity or service being sourced, used for vendor qualification and internal budgeting.']
  evaluation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the evaluation process was completed and a recommendation was finalized.']
  evaluation_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to evaluate vendor responses: price only (lowest price wins), weighted score (price and quality factors), best value (qualitative assessment), or technical compliance (pass/fail then price).']
  evaluation_start_date date [note: 'Date when formal evaluation of vendor responses commenced.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business reference number for the sourcing event, externally visible and used for tracking and communication with vendors.']
  event_owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the event owner for vendor communication and internal coordination.']
  event_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the procurement professional or category manager who owns and is accountable for this sourcing event.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sourcing event: draft (being prepared), published (released to vendors), open (accepting responses), closed (submission period ended), evaluation (under review), awarded (vendor selected), or cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|published|open|closed|evaluation|awarded|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of sourcing event: RFP (Request for Proposal), RFT (Request for Tender), RFQ (Request for Quotation), EOI (Expression of Interest), reverse auction, or sealed bid.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sourcing event record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, special conditions, lessons learned, or other relevant information about the sourcing event.']
  price_weighting_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to price in the evaluation scoring model (0-100). Remaining percentage is allocated to quality, technical, and other non-price factors.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date when the sourcing event was published and released to invited vendors.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Purchasing group or category team responsible for managing this sourcing event and the resulting contract.']
  purchasing_organisation varchar [note: 'Purchasing organisation or business unit responsible for conducting this sourcing event.']
  quality_weighting_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to quality and technical factors in the evaluation scoring model (0-100).']
  savings_achieved decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total savings achieved compared to the baseline value, calculated as baseline value minus awarded value. Positive values indicate cost reduction.']
  savings_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage savings achieved relative to the baseline value, calculated as (savings achieved / baseline value) * 100.']
  strategic_importance varchar [note: 'Strategic importance classification of this sourcing event based on business impact, spend value, and supply risk: critical, high, medium, or low.']
  submission_close_date date [note: 'Final date and time by which vendor submissions must be received. No late submissions are accepted after this date.']
  submission_open_date date [note: 'Date when vendor submissions are first accepted for this sourcing event.']
  vendors_invited_count varchar [note: 'Total number of vendors invited to participate in this sourcing event.']
  vendors_responded_count varchar [note: 'Total number of vendors who submitted a valid response to the sourcing event.']

  Note: 'Strategic sourcing event record representing a formal competitive tender, RFP, or auction process for a significant procurement category. Captures event reference, event type (RFP, RFT, EOI, reverse auction), commodity category, estimated annual spend, number of vendors invited, evaluation methodology (price/quality weighting), event timeline milestones, awarded vendor, awarded value, savings achieved versus baseline, and event status. Supports category management and procurement governance reporting. Distinct from operational RFQs by its strategic scope and formal evaluation process.'
}

Table procurement.sourcing_bid {
  sourcing_bid_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sourcing bid submission record.']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_contract. Business justification: Links winning sourcing bids to the resulting procurement contracts. When a sourcing_bid is awarded (award_recommendation_flag = true), a procurement_contract is created. This FK captures that relation']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor who submitted this bid.']
  sourcing_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the strategic sourcing event (RFP, RFT, EOI) for which this bid was submitted.']
  award_justification varchar [note: 'Business justification and rationale for the award recommendation decision, documenting why this bid was selected or rejected.']
  award_recommendation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bid is recommended for contract award (True = recommended, False = not recommended).']
  bid_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the bid bond or bid security submitted by the vendor, if applicable.']
  bid_bond_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the bid bond or bank guarantee submitted by the vendor.']
  bid_bond_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a bid bond or bid security was required for this sourcing event (True = required, False = not required).']
  bid_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to this bid submission by the vendor or procurement system.']
  bid_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the bid in the evaluation and award workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|under_evaluation|shortlisted|preferred|unsuccessful|withdrawn|disqualified — promote to reference product]']
  bid_validity_end_date date [note: 'Date until which the vendor\'s bid pricing and terms remain valid and binding.']
  commercial_evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score assigned to the bid based on commercial evaluation criteria (e.g., price competitiveness, payment terms, delivery terms).']
  commercial_exceptions_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of any commercial exceptions, deviations, or alternative terms proposed by the vendor that differ from the sourcing event requirements.']
  compliance_with_specifications_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bid fully complies with the technical specifications and requirements outlined in the sourcing event (True = compliant, False = non-compliant or partial compliance).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sourcing bid record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the bid value (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from purchase order placement to delivery, as proposed by the vendor.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for disqualifying the bid if it was deemed non-compliant or ineligible (e.g., late submission, non-responsive, failed prequalification).']
  evaluation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the evaluation of this bid was completed.']
  evaluation_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or observations recorded by the evaluation team during the bid assessment process.']
  evaluation_ranking varchar [note: 'Numerical ranking of this bid relative to other bids received for the same sourcing event (1 = highest ranked).']
  evaluator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the procurement or technical specialist responsible for evaluating this bid.']
  hse_evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score assigned to the bid based on the vendor\'s health, safety, and environmental management systems, certifications, and performance history.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'Incoterms code proposed by the vendor defining delivery and risk transfer responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  indigenous_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is an indigenous-owned business or includes indigenous participation in the bid, relevant for diversity and inclusion evaluation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sourcing bid record was last updated or modified.']
  local_content_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of goods, services, or labor sourced locally as declared by the vendor, relevant for local content compliance and evaluation.']
  overall_evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted overall evaluation score combining technical, commercial, and HSE scores to rank bids.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Payment terms proposed by the vendor in the bid (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, COD).']
  quality_certification varchar [note: 'Quality management certifications held by the vendor (e.g., ISO 9001, Six Sigma).']
  safety_certification varchar [note: 'Occupational health and safety certifications held by the vendor (e.g., ISO 45001, OHSAS 18001).']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vendor submitted the bid response to the sourcing event.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Sustainability or environmental certifications held by the vendor (e.g., ISO 14001, EcoVadis rating, Carbon Neutral certification).']
  technical_evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score assigned to the bid based on technical evaluation criteria (e.g., solution quality, methodology, experience, capability).']
  technical_proposal_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the vendor\'s technical approach, methodology, and solution proposed in response to the sourcing event requirements.']
  total_bid_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the bid across all line items, representing the vendor\'s proposed total contract value.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of the warranty or guarantee period offered by the vendor for goods or services.']

  Note: 'Vendor bid submission received in response to a strategic sourcing event (RFP, RFT, EOI). Captures bid reference, sourcing event, bidding vendor, submission date, total bid value, pricing breakdown by line item, technical proposal summary, compliance with specifications, commercial exceptions, evaluation scores (technical, commercial, HSE), and bid status (submitted, shortlisted, preferred, unsuccessful). Enables structured bid evaluation and award decision documentation.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_approved_vendor_material {
  procurement_approved_vendor_material_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the approved vendor-material commercial relationship record. Primary key for the Approved Vendor List (AVL) at the material level.']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Approved vendor-material relationship is often governed by a specific framework contract. Converting STRING contract_number to FK enables proper contract linkage and pricing validation. N:1 relationsh']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved vendor in this commercial relationship. Links to the vendor master record.']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or commodity category in this commercial relationship. Links to the material master record.']
  approval_expiry_date date [note: 'The date when the vendor approval for this material expires. After this date, the vendor may no longer be eligible for procurement unless re-approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the vendor-material commercial relationship. Determines whether this vendor can be used for procurement of this material.']
  blocked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor-material relationship is currently blocked for procurement. When true, purchase orders cannot be created for this combination.']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why this vendor-material relationship is blocked (e.g., quality issues, contract dispute, vendor suspension, price negotiation pending).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who created this approved vendor-material record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this approved vendor-material record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the negotiated unit price (e.g., USD, AUD, EUR).']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The standard number of days required for the vendor to deliver this material from order placement to goods receipt. Used for MRP (Material Requirements Planning) and delivery date calculation.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery terms and transfer of risk between vendor and buyer for this material (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'The named place or port specified in the Incoterms agreement (e.g., Port Hedland, Singapore, Perth). Defines the point of delivery or risk transfer.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who last modified this approved vendor-material record. Used for change accountability and audit trail.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this approved vendor-material record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent purchase order or goods receipt for this vendor-material combination. Used for vendor activity tracking.']
  last_purchase_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The most recent actual purchase price paid for this material from this vendor. Used for price variance analysis and benchmarking.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date when this vendor-material relationship was last reviewed for performance, pricing, and compliance. Part of the vendor performance evaluation cycle.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The minimum quantity that must be ordered from this vendor for this material. Enforced during purchase requisition and order creation.']
  negotiated_unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The negotiated price per unit for this vendor-material combination. Auto-populates purchase order pricing during requisition conversion.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next review of this vendor-material relationship. Used for proactive vendor management and contract renewal planning.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'The payment terms code agreed with the vendor for this material (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Determines invoice payment due dates.']
  planned_delivery_time_days varchar [note: 'The planned delivery time in calendar days from order placement to expected goods receipt at the plant. Used for MRP (Material Requirements Planning) run calculations.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The plant or site to which this vendor-material approval applies. Enables plant-specific source lists and vendor assignments.']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is the preferred supplier for this material. Used for automatic source determination during requisition conversion to purchase orders.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'The quantity unit to which the negotiated unit price applies (e.g., price per 1, per 10, per 100 units). Used in price calculation.']
  price_validity_end_date date [note: 'The date until which the negotiated unit price remains valid. After this date, price renegotiation may be required.']
  price_validity_start_date date [note: 'The date from which the negotiated unit price becomes effective. Part of the price validity period.']
  purchasing_group_code varchar [note: 'The purchasing group responsible for managing this vendor-material relationship. Identifies the buyer or procurement team.']
  purchasing_info_record_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the purchasing information record from SAP S/4HANA MM (ME11/ME12/ME13). Serves as the external reference for this vendor-material relationship.']
  purchasing_organisation_code varchar [note: 'The purchasing organization responsible for this vendor-material relationship. Determines procurement authority and organizational assignment.']
  source_list_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the source list record from SAP S/4HANA MM. Used for traceability to the originating purchasing info record.']
  source_list_validity_end_date date [note: 'The date until which this source list entry remains valid. After this date, the vendor will not be automatically proposed for this material.']
  source_list_validity_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this source list entry becomes valid for automatic source determination in MRP (Material Requirements Planning) and purchase requisition processing.']
