  standard_order_unit varchar [note: 'The standard unit of measure in which this material is ordered from this vendor (e.g., TON, KG, EA, M3). Must be compatible with the material base unit.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code applicable to purchases of this material from this vendor. Determines GST (Goods and Services Tax), VAT (Value Added Tax), or other tax treatment.']
  vendor_material_number varchar [note: 'The vendors own part number or material code for this item. Used for cross-referencing and order communication with the vendor.']

  Note: 'Master record of the approved vendor-material commercial relationship, serving as the single source of truth for all vendor-material pricing and supply terms. Captures vendor, material or commodity category, approval status, preferred vendor flag, negotiated unit price, price validity period, price unit, currency, delivery lead time, minimum order quantity, standard order unit, planned delivery time, purchasing organisation, plant assignment, last purchase price, last review date, approval expiry, and source list validity. Forms the Approved Vendor List (AVL) at the material level and provides auto-population of PO pricing during requisition conversion. Consolidates SAP S/4HANA MM source list, purchasing info records (ME11/ME12/ME13), and Pronto Xi supplier-item links into a single SSOT for vendor-material commercial terms. Replaces the need for separate purchasing info record maintenance.'
}

Table procurement.service_entry_sheet {
  service_entry_sheet_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service entry sheet record. Primary key.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the person who approved the service entry sheet. Links to workforce master data for authorization verification.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Service costs post to cost centres for opex tracking, budget variance analysis, and AISC calculation. Essential for contractor cost management in mining.']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Service posting requires GL account for financial reporting, cost classification, and opex/capex split. Required for accurate financial statements.']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Service entries are performed under contract terms and pricing. Converting STRING contract_number to FK enables proper contract compliance and pricing validation. N:1 relationship (many service entrie']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order under which the service was contracted. Links to the parent PO for service procurement.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service provider or contractor who performed the service. Links to vendor master data for contractor management and performance tracking.']
  purchase_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order_line. Business justification: Service entry sheets confirm service completion against specific purchase order line items. The SES table already has purchase_order_id (header FK) and po_line_number (INT), but no FK to the purchase_']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Capital services post to WBS for project cost tracking and asset capitalization. Common for construction and development contractor services in mining.']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the service was formally accepted and confirmed as complete by the approving supervisor or contract administrator. Triggers invoice verification workflow.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supervisor, contract administrator, or authorized person who approved and accepted the service entry. Provides accountability and audit trail.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the service entry sheet record in the system. Provides audit trail for data entry accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service entry sheet record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the service line value and unit rate. Supports multi-currency procurement operations.']
  document_date date [note: 'Date on which the service entry sheet document was created or issued. May differ from posting date for period-end adjustments.']
  invoice_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor invoice document that was verified against this service entry sheet. Links SES to accounts payable for payment processing.']
  invoice_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the two-way match process between purchase order, service entry sheet, and vendor invoice. Critical for accounts payable processing and contractor payment.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the service entry sheet record. Provides audit trail for data change accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service entry sheet record was last modified in the system. Audit trail for record updates.']
  line_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value for this service line item, calculated as quantity performed multiplied by unit rate. Forms the basis for invoice verification.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Financial posting date for the service entry in the accounting period. Determines the fiscal period for operational expenditure recognition.']
  quantity_performed decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of service units completed and accepted. Measured in hours, days, tonnes moved, metres drilled, square metres cleared, or other unit appropriate to the service type.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the service entry. Captures context not covered by structured fields.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date on which the service entry sheet was reversed or cancelled. Null if not reversed.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this service entry sheet has been reversed or cancelled. True if reversed, false if active.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for reversing or cancelling the service entry sheet, such as service not performed, incorrect quantity, or duplicate entry.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the service type performed at mine sites, processing plants, or project locations. Aligns with mining operational service categories for spend analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drilling|blasting|haulage|maintenance|engineering|consulting|laboratory|environmental|security|catering — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the service activity performed, including scope of work, location, and deliverables. Critical for contractor management and audit trail.']
  service_line_number varchar [note: 'Line item sequence number within the service entry sheet. Identifies individual service activities or deliverables within the SES document.']
  service_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the service performance period. Defines the conclusion of the timeframe during which the contracted service was delivered.']
  service_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the service performance period. Defines the beginning of the timeframe during which the contracted service was delivered.']
  ses_number varchar [note: 'Business document number for the service entry sheet, externally visible identifier used in procurement and invoice verification workflows.']
  ses_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service entry sheet. Tracks progression from creation through approval or rejection. Critical for invoice verification workflow control.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the mine site, processing plant, or project location where the service was performed. Critical for site-level cost allocation and contractor management.']
  timesheet_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to field-based timesheet system record that supports this service entry. Links to source data from contractor time tracking systems.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the service quantity is measured, such as hours, days, tonnes, metres, square metres, or other service-specific units.']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Contracted price per unit of service performed. Used to calculate line value based on quantity performed.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order or project work order under which the service was performed. Links service entry to operational execution.']

  Note: 'Service entry sheet (SES) confirming the completion and acceptance of contracted services performed at mine sites, processing plants, or project locations. Captures SES number, PO reference, service line items with activity descriptions, service period (from/to dates), quantity of service units performed (hours, days, tonnes moved, metres drilled, square metres cleared), unit rate, line value, acceptance date, approving supervisor or contract administrator, and SES status (created, approved, rejected, reversed). Critical for mining contractor management where 40-60% of operational expenditure is service-based. Triggers invoice verification for service POs via two-way match (PO-SES). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM service entry (ML81N) and field-based timesheet systems.'
}

Table procurement.price_schedule {
  price_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price schedule record. Primary key.']
  primary_price_supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for which this price schedule applies. Null if the schedule applies to a service.']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent procurement contract or framework agreement under which this price schedule is defined.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier providing the material or service under this price schedule.']
  tertiary_price_material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to procurement.material_master']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether purchase orders using this price schedule require additional approval beyond standard procurement authority limits.']
  auto_po_creation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price schedule supports automated purchase order creation from materials requirements planning (MRP) or requisitions without manual intervention.']
  base_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The contracted unit price for the material or service at the time of agreement, serving as the baseline for pricing calculations and adjustments.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or employee identifier of the person who created this price schedule record in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price schedule record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the price is denominated, such as USD, AUD, EUR, or GBP.']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The standard number of days between purchase order placement and expected delivery under this price schedule, used for materials planning and scheduling.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this price schedule becomes valid and can be applied to purchase orders and goods receipts.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date on which this price schedule expires and is no longer valid for new purchase orders. Null indicates an open-ended schedule.']
  escalation_formula varchar [note: 'The mathematical formula or calculation method used to adjust the base price based on index movements or other variables, supporting automated price variance analysis.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery terms and transfer of risk, such as FOB (Free on Board), CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight), or EXW (Ex Works).']
  index_reference varchar [note: 'The specific index or benchmark used for price adjustments when the price basis is indexed, such as Consumer Price Index (CPI), Producer Price Index (PPI), or commodity spot price reference.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or employee identifier of the person who most recently updated this price schedule record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price schedule record was most recently updated, enabling change tracking and version control.']
  maximum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The maximum quantity that can be ordered under this price schedule, used to enforce contract limits and prevent over-commitment.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The minimum quantity that must be ordered to qualify for this price schedule, supporting volume-based pricing strategies.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'The payment terms applicable to purchases under this price schedule, defining the timing and conditions of payment to the vendor.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The mine site, processing plant, or operational facility to which this price schedule applies, enabling site-specific pricing strategies.']
  price_basis varchar [note: 'The pricing mechanism or indexation method used to determine the actual price, indicating whether the price is fixed for the contract term or subject to adjustment based on external indices or formulas.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'The quantity unit to which the base price applies, typically 1 but may be 10, 100, or 1000 for bulk pricing scenarios.']
  price_variance_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable percentage deviation from the scheduled price before triggering a price variance alert or approval workflow, supporting contract compliance monitoring.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'The buyer or procurement team responsible for managing this price schedule and associated vendor relationships.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'The organizational unit responsible for procurement activities under this price schedule, typically aligned with regional or divisional procurement structures.']
  schedule_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special conditions, or instructions related to this price schedule, such as quality requirements or delivery constraints.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the price schedule, typically a human-readable code or reference number used in procurement documents and purchase orders.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price schedule indicating whether it is available for use in purchase order pricing.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the service covered by this price schedule when the schedule applies to services rather than materials.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the material or service is priced and ordered, such as tonnes, kilograms, litres, hours, or each.']
  volume_tier varchar [note: 'The volume bracket or tier to which this price schedule applies, enabling tiered pricing structures where unit price decreases with higher volumes.']

  Note: 'Reference record defining contracted or standard pricing schedules for materials and services within a procurement contract or framework agreement. Captures price schedule reference, contract reference, material or service description, unit of measure, base price, price basis (fixed, CPI-indexed, commodity-indexed), effective date, expiry date, and applicable mine site or region. Supports automated PO pricing, price variance analysis, and contract compliance monitoring. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM contract pricing conditions.'
}

Table procurement.delivery_schedule {
  delivery_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery schedule record. Primary key for the delivery schedule entity.']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Delivery schedules are call-offs against framework agreements or blanket purchase orders. Converting STRING contract_number to FK enables proper contract compliance tracking. N:1 relationship (many de']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent blanket purchase order or schedule agreement under which this delivery schedule is created. Links the schedule to the overarching procurement contract.']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier responsible for fulfilling this delivery schedule. Links to the vendor master record for the contracted supplier.']
  purchase_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order_line. Business justification: Delivery schedules are call-offs against specific purchase order line items. The delivery_schedule table already has purchase_order_id (header FK) but no FK to purchase_order_line. Adding purchase_ord']
  revised_delivery_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on delivery_schedule (revised_delivery_schedule_id)']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the item being scheduled for delivery. Identifies the specific mining consumable (explosives, reagents, fuel, tyres) covered by this schedule line.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the material was delivered and goods receipt was posted. Used for supplier performance evaluation and schedule adherence tracking.']
  call_off_date date [note: 'Date on which the call-off or release was issued to the vendor. Marks the formal request for delivery under the blanket agreement.']
  called_off_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material released or called off from the blanket purchase order for this specific delivery schedule line. Represents the amount requested from the supplier for this delivery.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Financial accounting organizational unit representing the legal entity for which this delivery schedule is recorded. Used for financial reporting and compliance.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity confirmed by the vendor for delivery on the planned delivery date. May differ from called-off quantity if supplier cannot fulfill the full request.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this delivery schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  delivered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of material delivered and received against this schedule line. Updated upon goods receipt posting.']
  delivery_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific delivery destination for this schedule line (mine site, warehouse, port facility, or storage location). Used for logistics planning and routing.']
  delivery_location_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the delivery destination. Provides human-readable context for the delivery location.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-provided delivery note or packing slip number accompanying the shipment. Used for cross-referencing and reconciliation.']
  delivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable percentage variance (over or under) from the called-off quantity that the supplier may deliver without requiring approval. Used for managing supply flexibility.']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'Material document number generated when goods are received against this delivery schedule. Links the schedule to the physical receipt transaction.']
  hazardous_material_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the scheduled material is classified as hazardous or dangerous goods. Triggers special handling and compliance requirements.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms code defining the responsibilities and risks between buyer and supplier for this delivery (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP). Critical for logistics and cost allocation.']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Specific location or port associated with the Incoterms code (e.g., FOB Perth, CIF Singapore). Defines the point at which risk and cost transfer occurs.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this delivery schedule record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  outstanding_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Remaining quantity yet to be delivered against this schedule line. Calculated as called-off quantity minus delivered quantity.']
  planned_delivery_date date [note: 'Scheduled date on which the material is expected to be delivered to the designated location. Used for planning and coordination of mining operations and inventory management.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code representing the receiving organizational unit or mine site. Used for inventory management and cost allocation.']
  priority_indicator varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this delivery schedule. Used for expediting critical materials and managing supplier attention.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Organizational unit or buyer responsible for managing this delivery schedule and supplier relationship. Used for accountability and workload distribution.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Higher-level organizational unit responsible for procurement activities. Defines the legal entity or business unit conducting the purchase.']
  schedule_line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the parent purchase order or schedule agreement identifying this specific delivery schedule line. Used for ordering and referencing individual schedule lines within a blanket agreement.']
  schedule_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this delivery schedule. Used for capturing additional context, exceptions, or coordination details.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery schedule line. Tracks progression from planning through delivery completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|called_off|confirmed|in_transit|delivered|partially_delivered|overdue|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Frequency or pattern of the delivery schedule (weekly, monthly, quarterly, or ad-hoc). Defines the cadence of call-offs under the blanket agreement.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for special handling requirements for this delivery (e.g., temperature control, hazardous materials handling, security requirements). Critical for mining consumables like explosives.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Specific storage location within the plant where the delivered material will be received and stored. Critical for warehouse management and inventory tracking.']
  transport_reference_number varchar [note: 'Shipment tracking number or transport order reference for this delivery. Used for logistics tracking and coordination.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the scheduled quantity is expressed (e.g., tonnes, litres, each, kilograms). Aligns with material master base unit or order unit.']

  Note: 'Delivery schedule and call-off record against a blanket purchase order or schedule agreement, specifying planned delivery dates, quantities, and delivery locations for periodic releases of contracted materials. Captures schedule line number, parent PO or contract reference, material, planned delivery date, called-off quantity, confirmed quantity, delivery location (mine site, warehouse, port), schedule status (planned, called-off, delivered, overdue), and actual delivery date. Critical for managing long-term supply agreements for mining consumables (explosives, reagents, fuel, tyres) where annual contracts are released in weekly or monthly call-offs. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM schedule lines and delivery schedules.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_cost_centre_spend {
  vendor_cost_centre_spend_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key identifying each vendor-cost centre spend relationship record']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to finance.cost_centre - the cost centre that procures from this vendor']
  supply_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to procurement.vendor - the vendor supplying this cost centre']
  contracted_annual_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'The contracted or budgeted annual spend limit for this cost centre with this specific vendor, used for spend control and vendor allocation planning']
  last_po_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase order issued by this cost centre to this vendor, used to identify inactive vendor relationships']
  payment_terms_override varchar [note: 'Cost-centre-specific payment terms that override the vendor\'s default payment terms, negotiated for specific sites or operational units']
  po_count_ytd varchar [note: 'Number of purchase orders issued by this cost centre to this vendor in the current fiscal year, used for vendor performance and procurement activity analysis']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is designated as a preferred supplier for this cost centre, influencing procurement decisions and sourcing strategy']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when this cost centre first began procuring from this vendor, establishing the vendor-cost centre relationship']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the procurement relationship between this cost centre and vendor: active (ongoing procurement), suspended (temporary hold), terminated (relationship ended)']
  spend_ytd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual spend posted to this cost centre from this vendor in the current fiscal year, used for budget tracking and variance analysis']
  vendor_performance_rating varchar [note: 'Performance rating assigned by this cost centre to this vendor based on delivery timeliness, quality, and service, specific to this cost centre\'s experience']

  Note: 'This association product represents the spend relationship between cost centres and vendors in mining operations. It captures vendor-specific procurement arrangements, spend tracking, and performance metrics at the cost-centre-vendor level. Each record links one cost centre to one vendor with attributes that track contracted spend limits, payment term overrides, preferred vendor status, and year-to-date actuals used for budget control and vendor performance analysis.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, each cost centre procures goods and services from multiple vendors (explosives, reagents, fuel, tyres, spare parts), and each vendor supplies multiple cost centres across different mine sites, processing plants, and operational units. The procurement function actively manages these vendor-cost centre relationships with specific contracted spend limits, payment term overrides, preferred vendor designations, and performance tracking at the cost-centre-vendor pair level for budget control and vendor performance analysis.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_site_access {
  vendor_site_access_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key uniquely identifying each vendor-tenement authorization record']
  procurement_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor authorized for site access']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the tenement where vendor access is authorized']
  access_revocation_date date [note: 'Date when site access was revoked for this vendor on this tenement, if applicable.']
  approved_activities varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of specific activities the vendor is authorized to perform on this tenement (e.g., \'explosives delivery\', \'drilling services\', \'reagent supply\', \'equipment maintenance\'). Scope of work is tenement-specific.']
  authorization_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s authorization for this tenement expires and must be renewed. Driven by insurance, certification, or contract term requirements.']
  authorization_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s authorization to operate on this tenement became effective.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor site access record was created in the system.']
  insurance_verification_date date [note: 'Date when insurance coverage was last verified for this vendor-tenement combination.']
  insurance_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor\'s insurance coverage has been verified as adequate for the activities on this specific tenement.']
  last_site_visit_date date [note: 'Date of the vendor\'s most recent visit or service delivery to this tenement. Used for activity tracking and access audit.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor site access record was last modified.']
  prequalification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vendor\'s prequalification for this specific tenement. Tracks the lifecycle of the authorization from application through approval, suspension, or revocation.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why site access was revoked for this vendor on this tenement (e.g., safety incident, contract termination, compliance breach).']
  site_access_clearance_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was granted clearance to access this specific tenement site. Critical for compliance tracking and audit purposes.']
  site_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vendor\'s designated contact person for this specific tenement site. May differ from the vendor\'s primary contact.']
  site_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the vendor\'s site-specific contact for this tenement.']
  site_induction_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has completed the mandatory site-specific safety and operational induction for this tenement. Required before physical site access is permitted.']
  site_induction_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor completed the site-specific induction training for this tenement.']
  site_induction_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the site induction expires and must be renewed. Typically annual or biennial depending on site requirements.']
  tenement_specific_conditions varchar [note: 'Special conditions, restrictions, or requirements imposed on this vendor for this specific tenement. May include environmental restrictions, heritage site protocols, or operational limitations unique to the site.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the site-specific authorization and prequalification of vendors to operate on individual mining tenements. It captures the regulatory and operational clearances required for each vendor to provide services or deliver goods to a specific tenement site. Each record links one vendor to one tenement with attributes that exist only in the context of this site-specific authorization relationship.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, vendors must be prequalified and authorized on a per-tenement (site) basis due to site-specific safety, regulatory, and operational requirements. Each tenement has multiple approved vendors for different services (explosives, reagents, fuel, equipment), and each vendor is authorized to operate across multiple tenement sites. The business actively manages these site-specific authorizations as operational records, tracking inductions, clearances, approved activities, and site-specific conditions for each vendor-tenement pairing.'
}

Table procurement.port_facility {
  port_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for port_facility']
  parent_port_facility_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on port_facility (parent_port_facility_id)']
  port_authority_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing port authority or operator organization responsible for this facility.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the port facility.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as building, suite, or terminal number.']
  annual_capacity_tonnes decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual throughput capacity of the port facility measured in metric tonnes.']
  berth_count varchar [note: 'Total number of berths or docking positions available at the port facility.']
  cargo_handling_equipment varchar [note: 'Description of major cargo handling equipment available at the facility such as cranes, conveyors, loaders, and stackers.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the port facility is located.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the port facility was officially commissioned and became operational.']
  commodity_specialization varchar [note: 'Primary commodity types the port facility is specialized to handle, such as iron ore, coal, copper concentrate, lithium, or nickel.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the port facility operations and inquiries.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the port facility operations.']
  contract_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current operating contract or lease agreement for the port facility expires.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the port facility is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the port facility record was first created in the system.']
  customs_clearance_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customs clearance services are available on-site at the port facility.']
  environmental_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental certifications or compliance standards held by the port facility such as ISO 14001 or EcoPort.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the port facility, typically assigned by port authority or international maritime organization.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Official registered name of the port facility.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the port facility based on cargo handling capability and infrastructure type.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port facility record is currently active in the system for operational use.']
  last_upgrade_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent major infrastructure upgrade or expansion of the port facility.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the port facility in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the port facility in decimal degrees.']
  max_draft_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel draft in meters that the port facility can handle, representing the depth of water required.']
  max_vessel_length_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum length of vessel in meters that the port facility can accommodate.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the port facility record was last modified in the system.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours of the port facility, indicating whether it operates 24/7 or has specific operational windows.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the port facility in its lifecycle.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ownership structure of the port facility.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the port facility location.']
  rail_connection_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port facility has direct rail connection for cargo transport.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or state/province where the port facility is located.']
  road_access_type varchar [note: 'Classification of road access available to the port facility.']
  security_certification varchar [note: 'Security certifications held by the port facility such as ISPS (International Ship and Port Facility Security) code compliance.']
  storage_capacity_m3 decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total storage capacity available at the port facility in cubic meters for bulk materials.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the port facility location used for scheduling and coordination.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the port facility for information and services.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for port_facility. Referenced by port_facility_id.'
}

Table procurement.port_authority {
  port_authority_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for port_authority']
  parent_port_authority_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on port_authority (parent_port_authority_id)']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the port authority headquarters or main administrative office.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details such as suite, building, or floor number.']
  annual_throughput_tonnes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual cargo throughput handled by the port authority measured in metric tonnes, indicating operational capacity.']
  authority_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the port authority for identification in procurement and logistics systems.']
  authority_name varchar [note: 'Official legal name of the port authority organization.']
  authority_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the port authority based on ownership and governance structure.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current certification held by the port authority expires and requires renewal.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of industry certifications held by the port authority, such as ISO certifications or environmental compliance.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the port authority is located.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code identifying the country where the port authority is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the port authority record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code representing the primary currency used for transactions with the port authority.']
  environmental_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator showing whether the port authority meets environmental compliance standards and regulations.']
  established_date date [note: 'Date when the port authority was officially established or incorporated.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or administrative jurisdiction under which the port authority operates, including state, province, or territory.']
  maximum_vessel_draft_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel draft in meters that the port can accommodate, indicating depth capacity for large vessels.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the port authority.']
  number_of_berths varchar [note: 'Total number of berths or docking positions available at the port managed by the authority.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the port authority indicating whether it is actively managing port operations.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days allowed for payment of invoices issued by the port authority.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the port authority address.']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator showing whether the port authority is designated as a preferred vendor for procurement purposes.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person at the port authority for official communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the port authority for procurement and vendor management purposes.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for contacting the port authority, including country and area codes.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official business or corporate registration number issued by the government authority.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk assessment rating assigned to the port authority based on financial stability, operational reliability, and compliance history.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the port authority is located.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number or business registration number assigned to the port authority by the relevant tax authority.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the port authority record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the port authority for accessing public information and services.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for port_authority. Referenced by port_authority_id.'
}
